# keyhog BACKLOG — the ONE ledger, the ONE plan (SOURCE OF TRUTH)

Every issue found by anyone (me, subagents, gemini) is appended here the moment it
is found. This is the ONLY task doc for keyhog — no second NOTES/FINDINGS/report/
coordination file, ever.

**ONE PLAN.** The single organizing structure is the subsystem spine `§0–§14`
(further down). Severity (`P1`–`P9`) is a TAG on a task, never a competing section.
Concrete `file:line` tasks live under their subsystem heading below and map to the
matching `§N`. Do NOT reintroduce a parallel severity-bucket or workstream plan.

Row format: `file:line | class | Pn | problem | fix | status`.

CONFIRMED NOT caused by this session's DET-0b/DET-1/gate-fixes (the 2 that reference `weak_anchor` fail at UNRELATED assertions: adjudicate email-shape + `generic_entropy_floor` source-owner). **HIGHEST PRIORITY: drive these to green — a red tree invalidates every "verified green" claim.** The 15 (test | file:line | likely class):
1. `challenging_ac_prefilter_bypass_finds_k8s_base64_secret` | adversarial/engine_cases/dead_corpus_challenging.rs:39 | recall/prefilter — **ROOT-CAUSED (recall-vs-precision judgment, not an obvious bug)**: fixture `ac_prefilter_bypass.env` = `K8S_FULL_SECRET="Y2FsaWNvLW9uLWt1YmUtYXV0aC1rZXk="` (+ an AWS key that DOES fire). Test wants the base64 value surfaced under the `SECRET` keyword. But it base64-DECODES to the readable phrase `calico-on-kube-auth-key` (dictionary words calico/kube/auth/key) → the decode-through path almost certainly suppresses it as readable-text/non-secret (a DEFENSIBLE precision call — a readable config value isn't a random key). Tension: "SECRET keyword ⇒ surface" (test/recall) vs "decodes to English ⇒ not a secret" (precision). Needs a detection-truth decision aligned with CredData precision goals + bench validation, NOT a reflex flip. Verify the exact suppressing gate via decode-trace before deciding recall-fix vs test-input-fix (Law 6/9 — don't weaken; if the fixture's planted "secret" is genuinely readable text, the FIXTURE may be the wrong test input).
2. `separator_only_run_without_keywords_is_admitted` | adversarial/round1_entropy_run_admits_base64_with_internal_punct.rs:214 | entropy admission — **ROOT-CAUSED (recall-vs-precision judgment on the keyword-free path — needs bench validation, like #1; NOT a clean fix)**: token = 60 bytes of BASE64_ALPHABET (uniform base64) with `-`/`_` at 10/24/38/52 (local `KEYWORD_FREE_MIN_LEN=56`), scanned as `cfg: {token}` (NO credential keyword) → the STRICT keyword-FREE path (`scan_keyword_free_candidates`→`collect_line_candidates`/`extract_candidates`). CORRECTED root-cause (2026-07-08, code-traced — the old "split into 10/13/13/13/7 segments" note was WRONG): the `cfg: <token>` line hits the `:`-assignment extraction (`extract_candidates_internal` keywords.rs:247-255) which pushes the FULL value after `:`; `clean_candidate_value` (keywords.rs:525) trims quotes/whitespace/`;`/`,` and cuts at whitespace/`&`/`<` but does NOT split on `-`/`_`/`.`. So the full 60-char punctuated token DOES survive to `min_len` (60≥56 ✓) and is then dropped by a PLAUSIBILITY gate (`is_candidate_plausible` at extraction OR `candidate_plausibility_rejection_stage` in `collect_line_candidates` scanner.rs:426) — NOT by separator-splitting. NEXT: a targeted diag (construct the token + call `candidate_plausibility_rejection_stage` with the keyword-free `KeywordContext`) to name the exact `EntropyShapeStage`, THEN carefully relax THAT gate for very-high-entropy (>5.8) runs only. Precision-sensitive (keyword-free path has NO anchor → admitting uniform-base64-with-separators risks FP flood) but the >5.8 bar bounds it; bench-isolate vs the B0 same-version baseline + `bench analyze` per-detector FP ([[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]]). Distinct from WITH-keyword siblings (`b64_secret_with_internal_plus_surfaces`) which PASS.

4/5/6. ✅ **DONE 2026-07-08 (VERIFIED: 3 passed, 0 failed)** `no_suppress_test_fixtures_clears_generic_fallback_haircut.rs` (:125/:102/:87). NOT a recall regression — a WRONG-DETECTOR-LABEL test. Runtime trace (transient dogfood diagnostic) proved the payload `license_key = "Zx9Kq2…46-char-high-entropy"` DOES surface at conf **0.926**, but as **`generic-api-key`**, not `generic-secret` — because `generic-api-key.toml` lists `license_key` as an explicit keyword (`generic-secret` does not). The 3 tests filtered `detector_id == "generic-secret"` → found nothing → the #6 anchor's `.expect()` panicked, cascading to #4/#5. FIX (Law-6 truthful, no weakening): repoint the filter to `generic-api-key` (the real owner) — the MC-15 `base_conf` haircut is shared by EVERY generic-* detector via `confidence::policy::generic_secret_confidence`, so generic-api-key exercises the identical fix; realistic `license_key` payload kept; comments/header corrected to "generic assignment fallback". Confirmed routing empirically: `license_key`→generic-api-key, `app_secret`/`auth_token`/`signing_secret`→generic-password (bare `secret`/`token` wins), only `webhook_url`→generic-secret.
7. ✅ **DONE (2026-07-08)** `real_credential_with_underscores_not_suppressed` | unit/a3_pipeline/word_separated_identifier_does_not_suppress_real_credential.rs:16 | suppression — **REAL RECALL BUG FIXED.** Ground-truth diag (`adjudicate_match`→`Verdict`): stripe `sk_test_4eC39HqLyjWDarjtT1zdp7dc` = SUPPRESSED(`stopwords`); mailchimp+slack already REPORTED (those asserts always passed). Root = `generic-secret.toml` stopword `test` matched as a **substring** by the per-detector stopword gate (api.rs, `contains_ignore_ascii_case`) which the Wave-3 reorder had placed FIRST (line 144) — so a real Stripe test key was killed. **FIX** (`suppression/api.rs`): split `suppress_per_detector_allowlist_stopwords`→ `suppress_per_detector_allowlist` (operator override, stays FIRST) + `suppress_per_detector_stopwords` (broad heuristic, moved to run LAST among shape gates, just before `suppression_stage_inner`) + **randomness guard**: `if randomness.is_random_token(credential) return None` — a genuine random credential BYPASSES stopword suppression (reuses the SHARED `is_random_token` model the identifier/word-separated gates wrap — ONE discriminator, no 2nd copy). `contains_stopword_here_secret` (low-entropy dict) still suppresses → existing contract `test_per_detector_allowlist_and_stopwords` STILL GREEN. Verified: #7 GREEN + blast-radius suppression/adjudicate/a3/phase2 = 336/0. ⚠ SET-CHANGING (recall+) → bench-validate. SUPERSEDES the old note below. ~~ROOT-CAUSED (real recall bug), fix non-trivial~~: test NAMES `word_separated_identifier` but that predicate (shape/mod.rs:149) is CORRECT here (24-char 3rd segment >10 → false). Actual suppressor = `suppress_per_detector_allowlist_stopwords` (api.rs:759, runs FIRST at 144): `generic-secret.toml` stopwords `["example","sample","test","dummy","placeholder","changeme","xxxxxxxx","your_key_here","000000"]` matched via **substring** `contains_ignore_ascii_case` → `sk_test_4eC39HqLyjWDarjtT1zdp7dc` contains `test` → killed. REAL recall loss for any credential containing those substrings. ⚠️ CONSTRAINED: (a) substring semantics is CONTRACTED (api.rs:888 `contains_stopword_here_secret`→stopwords), can't globally switch to word-boundary; (b) `placeholder_word_suppresses` returns TRUE unconditionally for both-`_`-bounded (`_test_`), so wouldn't save `sk_test_` either. PROPER FIX = make the stopword gate ENTROPY-AWARE (a placeholder-word token bounded inside a high-entropy value = real credential; reuse `looks_like_high_entropy_marker_collision`) OR split stopwords into substring-markers vs whole-word placeholders. Touches shared placeholder logic + Wave-3-churned pipeline → focused precision push, not a reflex edit. SHARES ROOT with #8.
8. ✅ **DONE (2026-07-08)** `named_detector_stage_reports_exact_email_shape_reason` | unit/adjudicate.rs:478 | email shape-gate reason — **FIXED by the SAME #7 reorder.** `noreply@example.test` is NOT random (dict words) so the randomness guard doesn't apply — but moving the broad stopword substring gate to run LAST (after the universal `email_address` gate at api.rs:341) means the specific `ShapeGate("email_address")` now claims the drop instead of the coincidental `stopwords` hit (`example`/`test` are generic-secret stopwords). Value still correctly suppressed; reason is now the informative one. This reorder is SET-PRESERVING (a value a stopword would drop is dropped just the same, only later, unless the randomness guard recovers it) → bench-neutral half. Verified GREEN. SUPERSEDES the old note below. ~~ROOT-CAUSED (reason-label only, NOT a correctness bug)~~: `left: ShapeGate("stopwords")` vs `right: ShapeGate("email_address")`. `noreply@example.test` is CORRECTLY suppressed either way; only the reported reason differs. Same gate as #7: `suppress_per_detector_allowlist_stopwords` runs FIRST (api.rs:144, substring) and `example`/`test` are generic-secret stopwords → "stopwords" wins before the email gate (api.rs:341, `looks_like_email_address("noreply@example.test")`=true). `adjudicate_match`→`named_detector_suppression_stage`→the SAME api.rs pipeline (one pipeline, no divergence). Judge: for explanation quality the more-specific `email_address` should win for email-shaped values → the email check would need to precede the stopwords gate (or stopwords skip email-shaped values). LOW priority (correctly dropped; only the label), and coupled to the #7 stopword-gate redesign — fix together.
9. ✅ **DONE 2026-07-08 (VERIFIED: passed; regression sweep 158 passed / only pre-existing #15 still red)** `generic_keyword_low_entropy_knob_gates_low_entropy_values` | unit/engine.rs:794 | entropy knob — REAL WIRING bug (HALF-WIRED knob, same class as #10). Runtime trace (transient dogfood diagnostic): BOTH knob states dropped `q7q4m7k4p2` (Shannon ~2.72) via `generic_entropy_below_floor`. The knob-aware check in `phase2_generic_shape.rs:70` (`generic_bridge_entropy_below_floor(..knob..)`) correctly admits under the relaxed GENERIC_KEYWORD_SECRET floor (1.5) when ON, but `phase2_generic.rs:403-428` RE-VALIDATES the floor with `owning_detector_id` (→ GENERIC_SECRET fallback, floor 2.8) IGNORING the knob → 2.72 < 2.8 → re-dropped. FIX: compute `floor_detector_id = knob ? GENERIC_KEYWORD_SECRET : owning_detector_id` once, use it in BOTH re-checks (match-arm + else-branch) so the re-validation honors the knob exactly as the shape-file gate does. Knob ON → floor 1.5 → surfaces; knob OFF → owning-detector floor → suppressed (both assertions green). Per-detector floor for knob-OFF unchanged.
10. ✅ **DONE 2026-07-08 (VERIFIED: 2 passed, broad entropy suite 528 passed / 0 new failures)** `keyword_free_uses_custom_threshold` | unit/entropy.rs:875 | entropy threshold — REAL WIRING/Law-10 bug: `entropy/scanner.rs:200-203` did a SECOND `spec.entropy_very_high.unwrap_or(param)` that UNCONDITIONALLY clobbered the explicit `keyword_free_threshold` param with the generic-secret spec value (5.8) — silently killing (a) the operator Tier-A override (test's 8.0 → ignored → value not suppressed) AND (b) the production sensitive-file recall boost (`phase2_entropy` passes `SENSITIVE_FILE_VERY_HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD`=5.5, clobbered to 5.8). The DET-0 spec-read already lives in ONE place — the no-threshold convenience entry `find_entropy_secrets` (scanner.rs:91-94) — which passes it in as this very param; the second read was a redundant duplicate. FIX = delete the clobber (honor the explicit param), documented in-code. DEDUP + WIRING + Law-10 win.
11. ✅ **DONE 2026-07-08** `entropy_plausibility_no_inline_tests` | unit/gates/entropy_plausibility_no_inline_tests.rs:7 | ORG gate — removed the inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` from `entropy/plausibility.rs`; moved `generic_detectors_declare_valid_per_detector_entropy_floors` into `tests/unit/entropy.rs` (uses public `keyhog_core::detector_spec_by_id`); confirmed `get_spec` still production-used at plausibility.rs:203. Green. **TAIL (caught by full-suite reconcile, fixed):** the GENERAL `no_inline_tests_in_src` gate has an anti-staleness check — removing plausibility's inline tests made its `INLINE_TEST_ALLOWLIST` entry STALE → hard failure. Removed `"entropy/plausibility.rs"` from the allowlist (gap/no_inline_tests_in_src.rs). This is why the targeted `entropy_plausibility_no_inline_tests` passing was NOT sufficient — the [[ci-was-red-not-green]] lesson: run the FULL gate suite, not just the named test.
12. ✅ **DONE 2026-07-08** `engine_process_early_suppression_reasons_live_in_adjudicator` | unit/gates/suppression_named_detector_ctx_owner.rs:160 | ORG gate — failing clause was `!generic_shape.contains("crate::detector_ids::GENERIC_SECRET")`: `engine/phase2_generic_shape.rs:58-61` did an O(n) `self.detectors.find(id==GENERIC_SECRET)` (identity selection in an engine leaf). Moved it to new `adjudicate::generic_secret_shape_floors(&[DetectorSpec]) -> GenericSecretShapeFloors{min_len,entropy_high}` (ONE PLACE for the generic-secret identity selection; behavior-identical — same `.find` on the active slice, same `8`/`HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD` fallbacks). Leaf now destructures the helper; no id literal remains. Sibling `phase2_generic.rs:355` does a DIFFERENT owning-detector resolution (deriving id/name/service too) and is NOT gated — left as-is (not a shared abstraction). 13/13 green.
13. ✅ **DONE 2026-07-08 (VERIFIED GREEN: 1 passed, 0 failed)** `massive_adversarial_data_driven` | adversarial/massive_adversarial/runner.rs:58 | adversarial corpus — ROOT CAUSE (both proven, NOT a detector regression): (A) **56 github failures = INVALID CRC32 checksums in the corpus.** Every dark `ghs_/gho_/ghr_/ghu_` token (digit-fabricated AND realistic) had a wrong 6-char base62 CRC32 → the detector fires, the checksum validator correctly drops it ([[checksum-wiring-invalidates-fabricated-token-fixtures]]). Verified by replicating `checksum/github.rs` crc32+base62 in python (both passing `ghp_` tokens → VALID, all dark → INVALID). FIX = regenerate valid checksums scoped per `[[case]]` block (never weaken the assertion / disable checksum). Digit tokens (entropy `123..890`) → `2PDSiF` matching the already-valid `ghp_` precedent; realistic → `9yELqD→38XESt`, `sANprk→0EBzkh`, `PR44Bp→34ae9N`, `n5Tab1→3cJfHh`. ⚠️ First attempt did a GLOBAL `123456'`→`2PDSiF'` that corrupted 174 unrelated fixtures (digit checksum not unique) — caught + fully reversed (git cross-check: only libsql prior-session work + the github fixes remain vs HEAD; ghp/hhp preserved). (B) **4 drake failures = fixture-contract mismatch.** Corpus asserted `drake_api_key = "abcde12345abcde12345"` fires, but drake only matches `account_number`/`serial_number` context + a `\d{6,}` value — no api_key pattern. FIX = rewrite suite-27 to `drake_account_number = "8402751639284057"` (real contract, non-sequential digits to dodge placeholder suppression), evasion structure preserved. RESULT: 60→4 (github cleared)→0 (verifying). ORIGINAL TRIAGE: 60/10679 assertions fail. Dark detectors (`all=[]`): `github-app-installation-token` (ghs_), `github-oauth-access-token` (gho_), `github-refresh-token` (ghr_), `github-user-to-server-token` (ghu_), `drake-api-credentials` (`abcde12345abcde12345`). The github failures come in TWO shapes: (a) obviously-fabricated `ghs_123456789012345678901234567890123456` (invalid CRC → [[checksum-wiring-invalidates-fabricated-token-fixtures]], corpus-fixture bug, regenerate with valid checksum), AND (b) realistic `ghs_Qy4gyJlDeVvhcdbDGXIrubm2bjUxGr9yELqD` ALSO `all=[]` — if THAT has a valid checksum, it's a REAL recall regression on the ghs/gho/ghr/ghu variants. MUST bisect: does `ghp_` fire in the same corpus while ghs/gho/ghr/ghu don't? If so the 4 non-ghp variants lost detection/checksum wiring. HIGH severity if real.
14. `decoded_reverse_placeholder_drop_records_adjudicator_example_suppression` | unit/root_facade/regression_decode_postprocess_suppression_trace.rs:84 | decode suppression trace — **ROOT-CAUSED PRECISELY (2026-07-08; my prior "MISSING code path" claim was WRONG).** The `decoded_reverse_placeholder` path EXISTS: `record_decoded_reverse_placeholder_suppression` (adjudicate/mod.rs:622), called from `scan_postprocess.rs:184`. Ground-truth (nocapture): the decoded value `321_TERCES_ELPMAXE_terceSedoceD` IS suppressed but by `ShapeSuppressed{reason:"hard_suppressed_context"}` in `scan_inner`, NOT the reverse-placeholder gate. MECHANISM: the custom decoder inherits path `fixtures/reverse-placeholder.txt` → context inferred TestCode → `final_emit_suppression_stage` (adjudicate/mod.rs:166-169) fires `HardSuppressedContext` because `penalize_test_paths && should_hard_suppress(conf<0.5)` (context/mod.rs:69-75). So scan_inner DROPS the match before it returns as a `decoded_match`, so the postprocess reverse-placeholder gate (which only runs on scan_inner SURVIVORS) never sees it. Unlike #8/#15 (set-preserving telemetry-attribution fixes), the match must SURVIVE scan_inner to reach postprocess — FIX = exempt decoded sub-chunks (`source_type` carries a decode marker like `/reverse`) from the context hard-suppress so the postprocess decoded-gates own decoded-match attribution, OR lift the reverse-placeholder check into the adjudication STAGES before final_emit. SET-CHANGING (decoded matches in test-paths would reach postprocess) → needs bench isolation, not a reflex edit. Value IS correctly suppressed today; this is trace-quality. **PLUMBING POINT IDENTIFIED (2026-07-08):** the named-detector decoded match hits `finalize_report_candidate` via `engine/process.rs:241` (`ReportAdjudicationPolicy.penalize_test_paths` = `self.config.penalize_test_paths`) → `final_emit_suppression_stage`. Minimal fix = thread an `is_decoded` signal (the `super::profile::set_in_decode(true)` thread-local at scan_postprocess.rs:129 is ALREADY set during decoded rescans — but reusing a PROFILER flag for suppression couples concerns; cleaner to pass a decoded flag through `ReportAdjudicationPolicy`) and force `penalize_test_paths=false` (or skip context-hard-suppress) for decoded sub-chunks, so they survive to the postprocess decoded-gates. BENCH-ISOLATE vs the B0 same-version baseline: the risk is bounded because `record_decoded_generic_entropy_suppression` (scan_postprocess.rs:171) already kills ALL generic/entropy decoded matches in postprocess, so only rare self-anchoring NAMED/vendor decoded matches in test-paths could newly surface — measure that delta before landing.
15. ✅ **DONE (2026-07-08)** `generic_gauntlet_base64_blob_drop_is_traced` | unit/root_facade/regression_dogfood_generic_gauntlet_trace.rs:68 | decode suppression trace — **TWO real bugs found via eprintln ground-truth (blob entropy=4.7999, floor=3.5, below_floor=false, blob_decoy=TRUE, gate REACHED, yet reason=`entropy_below_floor`).** (a) **entropy conflation** (uncommitted Wave-3/DET-0): `phase2_generic_shape.rs` computed `adjusted_entropy = entropy − entropy_high + HIGH_ENTROPY_BASE64_CUTOFF` and fed it to the base64 gates, tying the base64-blob boundary to GENERIC_SECRET's `entropy_high` (4.5) instead of the shape constant (4.8) — genuine marshalled-binary blobs in the 4.5–4.8 band escaped. HEAD passes RAW `entropy` (git-confirmed). FIX: reverted the 4 base64-gate args to raw `entropy`, deleted `adjusted_entropy`, dropped the now-dead `entropy_high` from `GenericSecretShapeFloors` (kept DET-0 per-detector `min_len`). (b) **telemetry keyed on keyword not value**: `phase2_generic.rs` recorded the `ValueShape` suppression against `keyword` (`secret`), so the test's `Yml0`-prefix filter never saw the base64_blob event (it saw the entropy scanner's separate `entropy_below_floor` event on the value). FIX: record against `value` (matching the sibling `BareAuthUnstructured` drop). Both SET-PRESERVING for suppression decision ((a) is precision-POSITIVE — catches more 4.5–4.8 blobs; bench-validating with rebuild). Verified GREEN + blast-radius phase2/generic/adjudicate/dogfood/suppress = 620/0 (the only fail is #14, a separate red). SUPERSEDES old note. ~~trace-attribution, base64_blob gate reordered~~
Clusters (shared cause → fix once): {4,5,6} generic-fallback-haircut suppression; {9,10} entropy-knob; {14,15} decode-suppression-trace; {3,11,12} ORG/source-shape gates ✅ ALL DONE. Standing warnings: `unused import std::path::PathBuf` (regression_detector_registry_integrity.rs:16) ✅ REMOVED 2026-07-08. STILL OPEN (ci-lean-CONDITIONAL — NOT blind-removable, need feature-gating): `unused import hex_encode` + `SCHEMA_VERSION never used` in `core/src/merkle_index/storage.rs` — both symbols ARE used in the default build (hex_encode@348,490; SCHEMA_VERSION@222,226,234,347) but their uses are cfg'd out under `ci-lean` while the import/def stay → the import (and the `super::SCHEMA_VERSION` def) must be gated to match its uses (`#[cfg(feature=…)]`), not deleted.

Status legend: `open` · `done` · `false` (verified not-a-bug) · `intentional` · `needs-review`.

Work loop: pull a disjoint slice → fix → flip `status` to `done` (with the change) →
reconcile (build+test) → repeat. Do NOT re-hunt while this pool is non-empty. Most
rows below are **verify-then-fix**: prior sessions/agents fixed ~90% of the old
severity list without flipping the row — CONFIRM against the code before spending a fix.

---

# ⚑ FLAGSHIP LOOP (2026-07-08, maintainer-ordered) — biggest-task-first; every iteration lands a SUBSTANTIAL change
Standing order: drive keyhog to **elegant + fastest + parity-correct + better-ML**. Each iteration: code → build →
tests green → GPU/CPU parity verified → documented in-code → status HERE (never scattered). Rotate axes — never
tunnel. Cadence is hook-driven: fire a real background build/bench each iteration; its completion re-invokes the
loop. A "task" is substantial (real refactor / innovation / perf win / ML gain — hundreds of lines), never a
one-liner. Stop only on explicit maintainer stop.

| # | flagship | what | maps to | status |
|---|----------|------|---------|--------|
| F1 | **DET-1 · 43rd ML feature + MoE retrain** | keyword-specificity feature: assignment key ∈ KNOWN-SERVICE vocab (codecov/equinix/…) vs GENERIC (api_key/secret/token). Separates service-keyword+UUID (catch) from generic-keyword+UUID (reject) — the split that ships creddata F1 0.37→~0.457 WITHOUT the mirror regression. `model_arch::INPUT_DIM` 42→43 (GPU shader + host buffers auto-derive — parity de-risked); mirror in `ml/feature_parity.py`+`rust_features.py`; retrain `ml/retrain_loop.sh --write --verify` (RTX 5090, fail-closed); `model_card.json`+build.rs provenance; CPU/GPU re-verify. | §DETECTION DET-1 | ✅ **DONE + VERIFIED on shipped binary 2026-07-08** (see the DET-1 detail row under THE ATTACK PLAN: INPUT_DIM=43, service_vocab feature 42 ONE-PLACE-derived from the detector corpus, weights.bin retrained, GPU shader auto-derives the dim → parity de-risked; MEASURED full-CredData F1 0.369→0.4816, P 0.376→0.7688, R preserved, FP −82%). Flagship-table status reconciled to the detail row — the remaining creddata gap is now pure RECALL, owned by DET-2. |
| F2 | **GPU/CPU parity sweep — "parity always"** | drive the known gpudecode CPU-vs-GPU decoder mismatches + the MoE scoring path to ZERO divergence; differential tests are the durable proof (permanent gate, not a one-time fix). Deepest root: §GPU-VYRE L2 (vyre literal-set under-fire). | §GPU-VYRE, §1 | open |
| F3 | **Perf: gate the always-active pool** | the 56s-vs-3s CredData gap floor = ~2700 always-active generic/entropy patterns scanned on EVERY chunk (§SPEED: 94.7% of scan). Restructure so that pool is GATED (keyword/context-admitted), not always-on — a precision AND speed double win. **FRESH FULL-CredData profile 2026-07-08 (release ci-lean, `--backend simd`): `phase2:prefilter`=84.3% of scan (836s CPU-sum), 87.7% of 388k mark-calls run the FULL per-pattern HS body (gate-skip only 12.3%), entropy leaf=7.8%, confirmed=2.9%. Decode explodes 3371 parents→347679 sub-chunks (103/parent; json=57.7%); 16.4% of ALL leaf time is inside decode rescans, prefilter's decode fraction=13.8%.** **⚠ DIAGNOSIS CORRECTED 2026-07-08 (measured, durable test `phase2_always_active_pool_family_composition`, homoglyph-split): the always-active pool is 99.9% HOMOGLYPH VARIANTS. Breakdown: REAL (non-homoglyph, runs on ASCII) = generic/entropy 0, vendor 2 (total 2); HOMOGLYPH (ASCII-skippable) = generic/entropy 7, vendor 2785 (total 2792).** So BOTH the old ledger claim ("2700 are generic/entropy") AND the intermediate "2787 real vendor" framing are wrong — the pool is homoglyph-prefix variants of vendor detectors (`AKIA`→cyrillic/greek look-alikes etc.), plus 2 real anchorless patterns. Generic/entropy DETECTION is NOT in this pool at all — it runs via the separate generic-bridge + entropy leaves (profile: generic 0.5%, entropy 7.8%). **THE LEVER (verified code fact, phase2_hs.rs:31-114): `Phase2HsEngine::build` compiles ONE HS DB over ALL always-active incl the 2792 homoglyph variants, with NO ASCII-specific DB. The RegexSet path skips homoglyph on ASCII (`homoglyph_ascii_skip`, proven recall-neutral); the HS path — which serves 99.4% of ASCII marking — does NOT. On pure-ASCII chunks the homoglyph variants are inert (unicode-lookalike prefixes absent; base ASCII prefix covered by the AC/confirmed path). So an ASCII HS sub-DB EXCLUDING homoglyph variants would be recall-neutral by the SAME invariant homoglyph_ascii_skip relies on. MAGNITUDE MEASURED (experiment `hs_homoglyph_ascii_skip_prize`, ci-lean): full-DB vs lean(non-homoglyph)-DB HS `mark` on ASCII haystacks = keyword_dense 647µs vs 3.0µs (215×), code_like 1530µs vs 11µs (139×), entropy_line 323µs vs 1.8µs (182×). HS's Teddy prefilter does NOT gate homoglyph free — their char-classes include the ASCII original (`[AА]` matches ASCII 'A'), so on ASCII they activate expensive NFAs whose only matches are already covered by the base AC pattern → inert but costly. ROOT: the recent `is_ascii` HS-engagement extension (line 72) traded the RegexSet path's `homoglyph_ascii_skip` for HS speed without realizing HS pays the FULL homoglyph cost on ASCII. FIX = give `Phase2HsEngine` a second ASCII sub-DB EXCLUDING homoglyph variants; on ASCII chunks with `homoglyph_ascii_skip` on, mark via the lean DB. Recall-neutral by the same invariant `homoglyph_ascii_skip_parity_default` already gates. Since phase2:prefilter=84.3% of scan & serves 99.4% of ASCII marking, this is a ~100× cut to the dominant cost → the flagship SPEED win. **IMPLEMENTED + UNIT-VALIDATED 2026-07-09: `Phase2HsEngine` refactored into a reusable `HsSubEngine` + `{full, ascii_lean}`; the lean ASCII sub-DB is built over the non-homoglyph subset (only when it's a strict subset — no extra memory otherwise); `mark`/`any_match` take `skip_homoglyph_ascii = chunk.is_ascii() && tuning.homoglyph_ascii_skip` (SAME predicate the RegexSet path uses — both callers in `phase2_prefilter.rs` mark_matches + any_active_match updated). Files: `engine/phase2_hs.rs` (engine), `engine/phase2_prefilter.rs` (2 callers), `engine/compiled_api.rs` (2 test diagnostics + bench), `testing.rs` (wrappers), `engine/mod.rs` (test re-export). Tests (all GREEN, in `unit/root_facade/phase2_anchor_analysis.rs` + top-level wrapper): (1) `phase2_always_active_pool_family_composition` pins the 2-real/2792-homoglyph composition; (2) `hs_homoglyph_ascii_skip_drops_only_homoglyph_variants` — RECALL-NEUTRALITY LOCK: lean DB drops ONLY homoglyph variants (⊆ full, no non-homoglyph dropped, no over-mark) across 6 credential-shaped ASCII haystacks; (3) `hs_homoglyph_ascii_skip_prize` perf experiment: production-routed full vs lean = 216×/141×/159×. Existing gates GREEN: homoglyph_ascii_skip_parity + phase2_prefix_gate_parity + prefilter_truncate_parity + confirmed_anchor_parity (8/8). **✅ FULLY VALIDATED + LANDED 2026-07-09:** (a) **SPEED — clean CredData profiled scan (release ci-lean, --backend simd): wall 71.1s→57.6s (−19%), user-CPU 988s→811s (−177s); phase2:prefilter leaf 836s→677s (−159s); hs-mark scan 63.1s→2.7s (−60s, 23×).** (b) **RECALL-NEUTRAL end-to-end: CredData bench tp=5317/fn=9784/recall=0.3521 IDENTICAL, F1 0.4816→0.4822; mirror tp=2752/fn=248 IDENTICAL. New durable gate `homoglyph_ascii_skip_parity_hs_backend` (66 ASCII inputs, HS/SimdCpu backend, skip-ON≡skip-OFF byte-identical RawMatch sets) — the definitive proof no FP added / no TP dropped; the existing gate only covered CpuFallback/RegexSet, a coverage gap now closed.** (c) **NO REGRESSIONS: full --lib reconcile = only the 3 pre-existing bench-gated reds (#1/#2/#14), ZERO new breakage.** FP vs the stale ledger baselines (CredData 1592→1634, mirror ~1043→1123) is pre-existing cross-version drift, NOT this change (findings are byte-identical, proven). **NEXT PERF LEVER (re-diagnosed via micro-bench isolation, durable in `hs_homoglyph_ascii_skip_prize`): the prefilter is STILL 83% of scan, but that is NO LONGER a wasteful sub-DB — the HS mark is now cheap (2.7s). Isolation: real `mark_matches` = 5-32µs/call (gate+anchor_present+hs_lean); the profiler leaf's ~1707µs/call is parallel-SCHEDULING CONTENTION inflating wall-time spans (CredData chunks avg ~258B, so true per-call is µs). So the 83% is the SUMMED cheap-per-call gate work over ~735k chunk-ops = 388k top-level + 347k DECODE sub-chunks (3371 parents × 103). The dominant remaining lever is the DECODE EXPLOSION (347679 sub-chunks each re-running phase-2), NOT the prefilter internals. That is deeper architectural work (decode is net-POSITIVE per the decode-guard arc, so it can't just be turned off — the lever is cutting the sub-chunk COUNT/overhead without losing the encoded-secret recall). betterleaks' 3s vs keyhog's 57s gap now traces to chunk-count × per-chunk overhead, not a single hot loop.** **F3b IN-PROGRESS 2026-07-09 (decode-homoglyph-skip — attacks the decode explosion's per-sub-chunk cost): the landed F3 skips homoglyph variants only on ASCII chunks (`is_ascii()`); NON-ASCII decoded sub-chunks (base64/hex of BINARY — a real fraction of the 347k) still pay the full 2794-pattern HS DB. RECALL ARGUMENT (rigorous): the ~2.8k homoglyph prefix variants exist to catch unicode look-alikes in SOURCE text; inside a DECODED payload a non-ASCII byte run is binary noise, and any homoglyph-variant hit there is structurally a NON-CREDENTIAL (a real secret is ASCII/UTF-8 text and is already caught by the base pattern in the lean DB; the only way a homoglyph is NEEDED on decoded content is if someone base64-encoded a homoglyph-SPOOFED secret — which is not a valid working credential). So homoglyph variants are inert on ALL decoded sub-chunks regardless of `is_ascii()`. IMPLEMENTED: (1) new single-owner `profile::in_decode()` accessor over the always-on `IN_DECODE` thread-local (set unconditionally at the decode rescan, scan_postprocess.rs:129); (2) new ONE-PLACE `homoglyph_skip_applies(match_text, homoglyph_ascii_skip)` = `homoglyph_ascii_skip && (is_ascii() || in_decode())` in phase2_prefilter.rs — REPLACES the prior 4-way inline duplication of the skip predicate (HS mark @627, RegexSet mark @704, HS any_match @796, RegexSet any_match @824), an elegance win on its own; both engines now apply the identical predicate so `backend_parity_matrix` holds. ✅ **VALIDATED + LANDED 2026-07-09:** (a) full `--lib` run = ONLY the 3 known reds (#1/#2/#14), ZERO new failures; the decode-through recall suite (`every_contract_positive_fires_through_decode_wrappers`) + `backend_parity_matrix` (HS≡RegexSet under the unified predicate) both GREEN — no real decoded secret dropped. (b) **CredData+mirror bench BYTE-IDENTICAL to the F3 baseline: CredData tp=5317/fp=1634/fn=9784/R=0.3521/F1=0.4822 and mirror tp=2752/fp=1123/fn=248/R=0.9173/F1=0.8006 — tp/fp/fn ALL unchanged on both corpora.** Empirically confirms the recall argument: the non-ASCII decoded homoglyph variants were all inert/non-credentials (zero TP, zero FP came from them). So the extension is recall-AND-precision-neutral. TWO wins: (1) ONE-PLACE — the 4-way inline duplication of the skip predicate is now one `homoglyph_skip_applies` owner (elegance, unconditional); (2) a SOUND optimization — non-ASCII decoded sub-chunks now skip the ~2.8k inert homoglyph NFAs (win magnitude is corpus-dependent — small on ASCII-heavy CredData, larger on binary/cert/CI-config-decoded corpora; a clean speed delta was NOT isolated from this single normal-scan run of 58.8s vs the F3 baseline's PROFILED 57.6s — different scan modes, single-run noise per [[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]] — but detection-neutrality makes it a strict no-regression). Never hurts (fewer patterns scanned, identical findings). REMAINING TODO (low-pri): a targeted decode-homoglyph adversarial fixture proving the perf path is TAKEN (base64(homoglyph-spoofed vendor key) skipped while a sibling ASCII secret surfaces) — the corpus byte-identity already proves the recall side.** **PROFILE CONFIRMATION 2026-07-09 (fresh --profile --backend simd on a 40-dir CredData subset, WITH F3b): `phase2:prefilter`'s DECODE fraction = 0.5% (was 13.8% in the pre-F3b full profile) — F3b + the ASCII skip together essentially ELIMINATED the decode-prefilter cost (~27× down). NEXT LEVERS RE-SCOPED off this data: (1) TOP-LEVEL `phase2:prefilter` is now 72.8% of scan (99.5% top-level, only 0.5% decode) — the biggest lever, though its 649µs/call leaf is parallel-scheduling CONTENTION-inflated (true mark_matches is µs; real aggregate cost is the summed top-level marking). (2) DECODE-RESCAN = 14.3% of all leaf time (24486 sub-chunks @ 5.95 MB/s), and json EMITS 53.3% of those sub-chunks (17486/24486; base64 8.3%, caesar 8.0%) — so a tighter JSON-decoder precondition is the tractable sub-chunk-COUNT lever (bench-gated: JSON is where encoded secrets in config/fixtures live, so tightening is recall-sensitive — needs the per-decoder precondition + differential parity, NOT a blanket gate). (3) `populate: always-active RegexSet prefilter=9725ms` is a large one-time BUILD cost (amortized, but a cold-start lever). F3b closed the decode-homoglyph sub-lever; the decode-COUNT (json) and top-level-prefilter levers remain.** **PERF-LEVER CONVERGENCE 2026-07-09 (INTROSPECTION — investigated the JSON decode-count lever to ground, verdict RECALL-BLOCKED, NOT tractable as a standalone perf fix): the json sub-chunk COUNT lever cannot be cut by a cheaper emit precondition without ML. Proof: (a) escape-class skip is recall-UNSAFE — corpus-wide escape counts on CredData are `\n`=1.22M / `\"`=546k / `\u`=271k dominant, and EACH is credential-load-bearing (`\n` = JSON-escaped PEM private keys; `\"` = double-encoded JSON `{\"token\":\"…\"}`; `\uXXXX` = arbitrary alnum) — so no escape class is inert, matching the reverted `has_decodable_payload` −7% recall result. (b) The `AlphabetScreen` (pipeline.rs:129 gate that decides which decoded chunks get SCANNED) is a coarse Layer-0 byte-presence union — any ASCII-TEXT decoded chunk shares bytes with the detector alphabet and PASSES, so it cannot distinguish inert English prose from a credential-bearing span. (c) json's own emit precondition is already tight (escaped-only, ≥4 content chars). CONCLUSION: distinguishing an inert decoded text span from a credential-bearing one requires CONTENT understanding = the SAME cheap learned "could this span be a credential" classifier that DET-2 (the top CredData PRECISION lever, generic/entropy FP flood) needs. So the top decode PERF lever and the top precision lever CONVERGE on one ML gate — build that once, both move. The remaining pure-perf levers are GPU offload of the top-level prefilter (§GPU-VYRE, separate vyre repo) and the 9.7s one-time RegexSet populate. Decode subsystem is exhaustively tested already (fan-out budget, dedup, splice window, base64 padding, screen-fail queue, truncation telemetry) — no test gap to fill there.** | §SPEED, §12 | ✅ F3 LANDED (19% wall, recall-neutral) + F3b decode-homoglyph-skip LANDED (detection byte-identical proven, 4-way predicate unified) |
| F4 | **DET-0 elegance: everything per-detector, one place** | consolidate ALL detection settings into DetectorSpec (entropy floors, min_confidence, shape rules, keywords, checksum). Migration landed 2026-07-07; `structural_password_slot` moved to per-detector TOML+verified green 2026-07-08 (DR-071b DONE); `weak_anchor`+`private_key_block` migration DET-0b in-flight (§DETECTION DET-0b bullet). Remaining: refine whole-token stopwords + drain any further scattered knobs. | §DETECTION DET-0/DET-0b, §14 | in-progress |
| F5 | **Docs as you go** | every subsystem, module boundary, non-obvious decision documented in-code at the point of change; ledger rows here, never new files. | §14 | standing |


# §DETECTION — THE ONE PLACE (goal: BEAT betterleaks on EVERY bench) — 2026-07-07

**ALL detection work lives here. One place, one plan.** Every detection section elsewhere in this
file (ACTIVE ARC below, WORKSTREAMS A/C/F, §4, RESEARCH-BETTERLEAKS, T14 CONSOLIDATE, the µ-entropy
deep-reads, FRESH BASELINE, RECALL EVIDENCE) is SUB-DETAIL of this section and is marked
`[FOLDED → §DETECTION]`. Do not start a competing detection plan.

## CURRENT STATE (2026-07-07) — where the two goals actually stand (read first)
**GOAL 1 — faster-than-Hyperscan >8MB (SPEED):** REFRAMED by 2026-07-07 `--profile` proof (see §SPEED). The >8MB bottleneck is NOT the GPU literal path — it is the **always-active anchorless RegexSet prefilter = 94.7% of scan** (2791/2807 patterns run it on every chunk; Hyperscan handles only the literal-anchored set). Session landed/found:
1. **GPU >8MB overflow FIXED + validated** (independent, landed): the GPU positioned-literal path degraded to CPU on every dense >8MB scan (122k matches > 64Ki buffer); raised cap to 1<<20 + Err-branch halving + contract test. GPU literal path now runs on >8MB.
2. **HS is 1.3–11.5× faster than the RegexSet on the always-active prefilter** (11.5× on non-ASCII/homoglyph) — but it was size-capped at 4096 so it never ran on >8MB chunks (~900KB). Simply lifting the cap SILENTLY LOST RECALL (CredData −60 real TP; HS byte-mode ≠ unicode RegexSet on non-ASCII) → reverted.
3. **is_ascii gate — LANDED + VALIDATED** (contracts gate b22ea8zuo running): engage HS on ASCII chunks of ANY size + keep RegexSet for non-ASCII large chunks. Measured recall-safe: CredData 8185/5258 EXACT parity + Mirror 975/2768 EXACT (the divergence WAS non-ASCII-only, confirmed). Speed (best-of-3): 16MB ASCII sparse 1.55s→0.75s (**2.06×**), 8MB ASCII dense 3.67s→3.34s (1.10×), non-ASCII UNCHANGED (stays on RegexSet, no regression). ONE-PLACE predicate `hs_prefilter_engages` + `hs_gate_tests` unit test lock the `|| is_ascii` clause. CPU-only, no GPU. This SUBSUMES the GPU megakernel step-2 (GPU would have to beat HS, not the RegexSet). REMAINING lever: root-cause the non-ASCII HS byte-mode-vs-unicode divergence to also unlock the 11.5× non-ASCII win (blocked on making HS recall-identical on unicode; see §SPEED root-cause hypothesis).
**GOAL 2 — beat betterleaks on EVERY bench (DETECTION):** VALIDATED session wins (correct binary, ci-lean, all bench-confirmed — supersedes the earlier "−13 FP" claim, which was an artifact of an over-broad placement that broke 15 vendor contracts):
- greptile-api-key NEW detector: homefield **+3 TP / 0 FP** (real format from betterleaks manifest).
- wandb-api-key {40}→{40}|{32} widen: homefield **+1 TP / 0 FP** + 32-hex contract lock.
- SES_PASS / *_PASS= three-part recall fix: **creddata +13 TP / +4 FP** (fn 9856→9843, F1 0.3677→0.3684), **mirror UNCHANGED (fp=975, F1=0.8210)**, contracts GREEN (7/0). Real *_PASS= credential-env recall, precision-neutral.
- google-api-key DRIVE tighten (kept, correct) + stopword whole-token REVERTED (was net +6 FP) + sequential monotonic gate scoped to entropy-only (correct, ~0 creddata effect — its benefit required the contract-breaking universal placement).
CURRENT STANDINGS: creddata F1 0.368 (P 0.391 R 0.348) vs betterleaks ~0.60; homefield ~0.48 vs ~0.61; mirror 0.821 (keyhog WINS, HOLD). The creddata/homefield gap is the entropy-token/entropy-api-key flood (68-76% of FP), which fp_hist proves is NOT threshold-separable AND the google-16-FP proves is betterleaks-curated-negative shape-INDISTINGUISHABLE. Heuristic surfacing SATURATED. THE ONE LEVER: **DET-1 — the 43rd ML feature (service-vocab keyword vs generic key), the identified clean win (ships creddata F1 0.457 w/o mirror regression)**. NOTE: the crude floor-raise lever (raise generic-keyword-secret entropy_low) is now OFF THE TABLE — it conflicts with the SES_PASS *_PASS= recall win (both route through generic-keyword-secret's low floor). Precision MUST come from DET-1's signal separation, not a blunt floor. Retrain gate (`min-real-class-support=1`) needs support-threshold calibration for n_pos<5 tails. Details: §DETECTION blocks below + [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]].

## DET-0 · ARCHITECTURE LAW (maintainer, BINDING, 2026-07-07): PER-DETECTOR, CONSOLIDATED IN THE DETECTOR.
**There is NO global/overall entropy gate, and NO global recall/precision knob applied uniformly to every
candidate. EVERY threshold, floor, min-length, entropy cut, allowlist, and stopword that affects whether a
candidate survives (i.e. affects recall or precision) is a PER-DETECTOR field OWNED IN THAT DETECTOR'S TOML
SPEC.** A reader understands a detector's full recall/precision behavior from its ONE TOML file — never from a
scattered global constant in `entropy/mod.rs` / `plausibility.rs` / `isolated.rs` / `adjudicate/` / `phase2_*`.
Pattern (already proven by `min_confidence` + `entropy_floor`): a per-detector `Option`/`Vec` spec field that
OVERRIDES a single named default; the old global const survives ONLY as that one fallback owner, documented as
"default when the detector is silent," NEVER applied as an independent uniform gate. **MIGRATION IN FLIGHT
(2026-07-07, 10 agents):** new `DetectorSpec` fields landed (`entropy_high/low/very_high`, `mixed_alnum_floor`,
`keyword_free_min_len`, `min_len`, `allowlist_paths`, `allowlist_values`, `stopwords` — `crates/core/src/spec.rs`).
Agents thread each global knob → `spec.<field>.unwrap_or(DEFAULT)` in its owning caller file + populate the
generic/entropy detector TOMLs. Docs (this law) mirrored into `spec.rs` field docs + `docs/src`. NO agent edits
`spec.rs` (I own it — the shared contract); each owns a DISJOINT caller file; TOML data is one agent's lane.

**MIGRATION LANDED + RECONCILED (2026-07-07):** all 10 agents done. Reconcile fixed 5 `E0603` (agent stripped
`pub(crate)` from `KEYWORD_FREE_LABEL`/`keyword_is_key_material`/`keyword_is_crypto_key_material`/`CREDENTIAL_CONTEXT_MIN_LEN`).
`keyhog-core` + `keyhog-scanner` + release binary all EXIT 0. Per-detector allowlist/stopword suppression wired
(`suppression/api.rs:144`, non-silent via `record_example_suppression`). MECHANISM PROVEN: `classify_keyword_to_detector_id`
(entropy/scanner.rs:31) maps entropy matches → the 4 generic specs (`generic-secret`←keyword-free, `generic-password`←pwd,
`generic-keyword-secret`←token [=entropy-token flood], `generic-api-key`←else [=entropy-api-key flood]). So the
entropy-token/entropy-api-key FP flood IS tunable via `generic-keyword-secret`/`generic-api-key` per-detector `entropy_low`/`min_len`.
**MIGRATION IS F1-NEUTRAL (as intended — consolidation, not tuning):** creddata 0.369→0.368 (fp 8238→8181 −57, fn 9821→9856 +35),
mirror 0.820→0.821, homefield-betterleaks 0.451 unchanged. The +35 FN is the substring-`contains` stopword over-suppressing
short words (test/sample) — REFINE to whole-token/word-boundary. **NOW PULL THE LEVERS (the actual win):** `--no-entropy`→F1 0.444,
so entropy-token/api-key are NET-NEGATIVE on creddata. Next A/B (rebuild-per-config, TOMLs are compile-embedded):
raise `generic-keyword-secret` entropy_low 3.0→4.5 (entropy-token P=0.062, near-pure FP) + `generic-api-key` 3.0→4.0
(entropy-api-key P=0.228); bench creddata+mirror; find the precision/recall sweet spot that beats betterleaks WITHOUT tanking mirror R=0.923.

## THE GOAL (maintainer-ordered 2026-07-07): keyhog must WIN every detection bench vs betterleaks.

|---|---|---|---|---|
| mirror | F1 ~0.82 (R 0.916) | (lower) | keyhog WINS | HOLD precision, don't regress |
| creddata | **F1 0.363** (P 0.376 R 0.350) | **F1 0.596** (P 0.864 R 0.455) | **−0.233** | THE FIGHT |
| homefield | UNMEASURED | ground-truth source | ? | MEASURE FIRST |

## WHY keyhog loses creddata (fully diagnosed — do not re-derive):
1. **PRECISION killer = generic-entropy pool.** `entropy-token` (P=0.06, 4465 FP/282 TP) + `entropy-api-key`
   (P=0.21, 2270 FP/612 TP) = 68% of all 8780 FP. `--no-entropy` → F1 **0.444** (P 0.72), i.e. these detectors
   are NET-NEGATIVE (add ~438 TP for ~6367 FP). betterleaks has NO generic-entropy detectors — tight vendor
   rules + entropy floors + allowlists only. keyhog's generic-surfacing bet is the precision loss.
2. **RECALL gap = 4324 positives betterleaks catches that keyhog misses**, dominated by **Key 3285** (bare
   `Key = <hex32/48/64>`), Secret 672, Token 106, UUID 80. Plus 3 entirely-blind categories: **AWS S3 Bucket
   (92 pos)**, Firebase Domain (38 pos) [Bearer 75 — DONE]. The Key-hex lane overfits one fixture + hex64==SHA256
   shape (generalization/precision risk keyhog declined) — but the maintainer now orders the win, so re-open it.

## THE ATTACK PLAN (ranked; each row is a task — drive to green + bench-validate BOTH corpora before landing):
- **DET-1 · 43rd keyword-specificity ML feature + retrain — ✅ DONE + VERIFIED END-TO-END ON THE SHIPPED BINARY (2026-07-08). LANDMARK WIN.** `INPUT_DIM=43` (`model_arch.rs`, flows to the GPU shader automatically via `gpu_shader::moe_shader` generation — parity de-risked); feature 42 = `SERVICE_CONTEXT_FEATURE_INDEX`, powered by `ml_scorer/service_vocab.rs` which DERIVES the service vocabulary from the embedded detector corpus (ONE-PLACE, never a hand-list; three filters: length floor, generic-family exclusion, stem-spread genericity) with a Python parity oracle (`ml/feature_parity.py` + `ml/parity_check.py`). weights.bin retrained (47928 B = 11982 f32 = exact 43-feat layout; `model_card.json` feature_count=43, model_version moe-v1-35715cc2, build.rs provenance passes). **MEASURED (release ci-lean binary, full CredData `bench run`): F1 0.369→`0.4816`, P 0.376→`0.7688` (2×!), R 0.350→0.351 (preserved), FP 8780→`1592` (−82%).** Beats the `--no-entropy` ceiling (0.444). Per-category precision transformation: Key 0.030→0.547 (FP 2564→204), UUID 0.012→0.725 (FP 563→44), Secret 0.193→0.816, Password 0.538→0.746 — the feature rejects generic-keyword-context UUIDs while keeping service-keyword-context ones, exactly as designed. Gap to betterleaks (F1 0.596) HALVED (0.227→0.114); remaining gap is now pure RECALL (→ DET-2). result: `benchmarks/results/creddata-keyhog-det1-verify.json`. Synthetic held-out F1 0.9576 (mirror precision preserved). **Perf UNCHANGED: 65.3s vs betterleaks 3s — DET-1 fixed precision not speed; perf is the next flagship.**
- **DET-2 · recover the recall categories betterleaks catches [additive, close the R gap].** (a) AWS S3 Bucket 92 pos
  entirely-blind → new detector. (b) Firebase Domain 38 pos → new detector. (c) Key-hex lane (3285) — build a
  keyword-anchored `key = <hex32/48/64>` detector gated hard enough to not flood SHA-shape FPs (needs the DET-1 feature
  or a strong-keyword requirement). status=OPEN.
  - **FRESH `bench analyze` (2026-07-08, post-#7/#8/#15 binary; artifact `scratchpad/bench_analyze_creddata.txt`) CONFIRMS the category ranking** (FN 9784: Key 3723, UUID 2290, Password 1233, Secret 762, Token 284; FP 1634: Password 409, Key 204, Key:Token 195, Secret 121, Token 118). The dominant FN lanes (short synth passwords `czplsfj`/`kovftcj101`, bare UUIDs, generic hex/base64 keys) are context-dependent → the ML/DET-1 lever, NOT gate relaxation (re-confirms why #1/#2 must not be reflex-relaxed). **INVESTIGATED the apparent "clean vendor-format wins" — REFUTED as benchmark-overfitting (2026-07-08):** (i) Mailgun `key-5as9xrzs30zd2guj9vn767bkpthbvgo9` — the detector regex `key-[a-f0-9]{32}` (mailgun-api-key.toml:29) is HEX per Mailgun's documented format; CredData's value is 32 uniform-base36 lowercase-alnum = LOOKS SYNTHETIC, not a real leaked Mailgun key. Widening `[a-f0-9]`→`[a-z0-9]` would fit CredData at real-world FP cost (a bare `key-<32 alnum>` with the low `min_confidence=0.12` anchor-trust). Law-1 no-regrets → do NOT overfit. (ii) Slack `xoxt-51285333487-…` — `xoxt` is NOT a documented Slack prefix (real: xoxb/xoxp/xoxa/xoxr/xoxs/xapp/xoxe); likely a CredData mislabel. **CONCLUSION: even the "vendor-format" misses are NOT clean heuristic wins — they're synthetic-format overfitting OR mislabels. This CEMENTS that the CredData recall gap has NO clean heuristic lever left (matches "heuristic surfacing SATURATED", [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]]); the only real lever is the DET-1/DET-2 ML retrain (blocked on the mirror-precision-vs-CredData-recall maintainer call, line ~191).** status=OPEN (ML-only).
- **DET-3 · port betterleaks precision techniques keyhog lacks [precision, low-risk].** allowlist/stopword expansion
  (example/test/sample/placeholder/changeme/your_/0000/xxxx), path-demotion (test/fixture/vendor), known-dummy tokens →
  Tier-B `rules/*.toml`. Harvest destination for the 8 betterleaks-attack agents. status=OPEN.
- **DET-4 · measure + win homefield.** Run `bench leaderboard --corpus homefield`; find keyhog's gaps vs betterleaks;
  fix detectors. status=OPEN.
- **DET-5 · tighten the loose generic detectors flooding FPs [precision].** per-detector regex tightening (harvest
  destination for the generic-detector-precision + entropy-precision agents). status=OPEN.
- **DET-FALLBACK · demote generic-entropy to opt-in (F1 0.369→0.444, 2nd place).** Only if DET-1 retrain stalls — it
  loses 438 real TP so DET-1 is strictly better. Maintainer now authorizes the product call. status=gated-on-DET-1.
