- entropy/fast.rs:221,254 + fast_neon.rs:37 + fast_x86.rs:57 | dead-code | P4 | has_high_entropy_fast*/distinct_byte_count test-only | remove or wire to hot path | open-LAW11-narrowed (verified 2026-07-04: SPLIT verdict. (a) CORRECTED 2026-07-06: `distinct_byte_count` is TEST-ONLY, NOT live — it is `#[cfg(test)]` (fast.rs:296) and its ONLY two callers (fast.rs:312 inside `has_high_entropy_fast_scalar`, fast_x86:62 inside `has_high_entropy_fast_x86`) are BOTH inside the `#[cfg(test)]` `has_high_entropy_fast*` family. The real live distinct-byte owner is `unique_byte_count` (mod.rs:216, used live at mod.rs:235 by the shipped `shannon_entropy`); `distinct_byte_count` is merely a test-only u32-cast wrapper around it. So the ENTIRE has_high_entropy_fast family (dispatcher fast.rs:268 + _scalar :303 + distinct_byte_count :296 + _x86 + _neon + testing.rs:1573 facade + tests/unit/entropy_fast{,_extended}.rs) is one self-contained test-only unit, removable together (the live entropy path uses `shannon_entropy`/`shannon_entropy_simd` + `unique_byte_count`, none of which the family feeds). Verify no shannon_entropy_simd cross-impl PARITY coverage is lost before deleting entropy_fast_extended.rs. (b) `shannon_entropy_simd` is LIVE — the public `shannon_entropy` (mod.rs:180, called across the hot path: plausibility/scan_loop/scanner/ml_features) delegates to it at mod.rs:205. (c) ONLY `has_high_entropy_fast` + `has_high_entropy_fast_{x86,neon,scalar}` remain test-only — genuine Law-11. They are a bool convenience wrapper (`shannon_entropy_simd(data) >= threshold`, fast.rs:318 / fast_x86:67 / fast_neon:44) that does NOT early-exit, so wiring the hot path to it yields ZERO perf gain over the current `shannon_entropy(data) >= threshold`. RESOLUTION: remove the wrapper layer + its tests, BUT first re-home the cross-impl SIMD-parity assertions (fast_x86:39-50 "all three implementations return identical") onto `shannon_entropy_simd` so parity coverage is preserved — do not delete the parity tests. Narrowed from the original 6-symbol claim to just the 4 wrapper fns.) **DONE 2026-07-09: removed the entire test-only family — `has_high_entropy_fast` + `has_high_entropy_fast_{scalar,x86,neon}` + `distinct_byte_count` (src: fast.rs/fast_x86.rs/fast_neon.rs) + the `has_high_entropy_fast` facade wrapper (testing.rs). VERIFIED zero remaining refs (rg CLEAN). PARITY PRESERVED: the row's precondition ("no shannon_entropy_simd cross-impl parity coverage lost") was ALREADY satisfied by `sub_facade::sub_entropy::shannon_scalar_matches_simd_on_many_inputs` (independent of the removed family — asserts scalar≡simd within SIMD_EPS across 7 shapes incl. the >1024/>255 paths) — no re-home needed, it never depended on the wrapper. Kept ALL production coverage: every `shannon_entropy_scalar`/`shannon_entropy_simd`/`shannon_entropy` boundary test in entropy_fast{,_extended}.rs + sub_entropy.rs, and the C10/M9 FMA-gate-contract tests (regression_entropy_fma3_dispatch.rs: avx2_dispatch_gate_requires_fma / avx512_dispatch_gate_requires_dq / scalar_long_path_is_bit_exact / simd_dispatch_does_not_sigill). DELETED regression_entropy_fast_dispatch_and_sample.rs outright + unregistered its mod: its whole identity was the M11 SAMPLING-divergence bug in the removed wrapper (production `shannon_entropy` never sampled, so M11 has no production analog to regress), and its lone SIGILL test was a strict SUBSET of the fma3 file's `simd_dispatch_does_not_sigill_and_matches_exact_within_drift` (same 0..400%256 blob, stronger: exact-value + drift-band). Root-cause confirmed dead: `has_high_entropy_fast`'s "early exit" counts distinct bytes over the WHOLE buffer (`unique_byte_count`, O(n)) — the same O(n) as the histogram it tries to skip, whose 256-bin reduction is negligible — so wiring it to production is a pessimization (2× passes in the accept case), exactly why it was never wired. Org/dedup axis. | done

SCRATCH` (BigramScratch: a `vec![0u64; BIGRAM_BITSET_WORDS].into_boxed_slice()` allocated ONCE per thread, reused across calls — no per-call alloc), and it keeps the all-zero invariant by tracking a `touched` word-list and re-zeroing ONLY those words (:422-425), so restore is O(distinct bigrams) not O(8KiB). This is strictly better than the proposed "small-input sorted array" (O(1) bigram lookup + cheap restore). No stack/heap alloc per call.)

### scanner: suppression / confidence / context  (§4)
- detectors/aerisweather-api-credentials.toml:24 | over-broad | P3 | pattern 3 `(?:access_id\|ACCESS_ID)[=:\s"']+([a-zA-Z0-9]{8,40})\b` is UNANCHORED (no aeris vendor keyword) → claims ANY `access_id=<8-40 alnum>`, cross-firing on other vendors (found 2026-07-06 during evasion-backfill: the `tuya_access_id=…` evasion fired aerisweather-api-credentials, not tuya). Fix: require an aeris co-anchor (companion/proximity) or scope/remove the bare `access_id=` arm. BENCH-GATED: may be recall-load-bearing for real aeris configs that write a bare `access_id=` — CredData+mirror differential before landing; the credential still surfaces so it is a precision/attribution issue, not a recall miss. | open
- detectors/turso-embedded-replica-token.toml:22 vs turso-db-token-v2 | overlap | P4 | both carry a `TURSO[_-]AUTH[_-]?TOKEN=` pattern (embedded pattern-3 `([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{20,})` vs db-token-v2's JWT-specific `eyJ…`) → a `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN=<jwt>` is claimed by whichever wins value-dedup (observed: embedded). Benign (same credential, both are turso), but two turso detectors own the same keyword shape — candidate to consolidate the AUTH_TOKEN arm into one owner. | open-LOW
- detectors/x2y2-api-key.toml (X-API-KEY arm) vs 8x8-api-credentials | over-broad | P3 | x2y2 pattern-2 `X-API-KEY[\s]*:[\s]*([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{20,})` is UNANCHORED (the generic `X-API-KEY:` header, no x2y2 signal) and COLLIDES with 8x8-api-credentials which also claims `X-API-KEY:` — a bare `X-API-KEY: <token>` is attributed to whichever wins dedup (observed 8x8). Found 2026-07-06 during evasion-backfill. Fix: anchor the x2y2 `X-API-KEY` arm to an x2y2 co-signal (host/`x2y2`) or drop it (keep the `X2Y2_API_KEY=`/quoted arm which fires x2y2 distinctly). Same class as the aerisweather `access_id=` finding — unanchored generic-header arms cross-fire. | open
- detectors/google-artifact-registry-key.toml | garbage-capture+shadow | P2 | on a GCP service-account JSON (`{"type":"service_account",...}`) this detector fires and captures a GARBAGE credential of just `"` (a lone double-quote) — a Law-6 truth failure (the "credential" is not a secret) — AND in the CLI suppression/dedup path it SHADOWS vertexai-service-account (whose raw-scan match is the real `service_account`/key capture). Found 2026-07-06: the CLI surfaces only google-artifact with cred `"` on the vertexai positive; the contract runner's raw `scanner.scan()` still surfaces vertexai so its contract passes, but the CLI operator sees a bogus 1-char "secret". Fix: tighten google-artifact-registry-key's capture (it is matching a stray quote via an over-greedy/misanchored group) so it does not fire — or fires with a real credential — on service-account JSON; verify vertexai wins/co-exists in the CLI path. | FIX APPLIED 2026-07-06 (validating): root cause was patterns #1 (`"type":"service_account"..."private_key":"-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----`) and #3 (`"client_email":"…gserviceaccount.com"…"private_key_id"`) had NO capture group → the engine's no-group fallback yielded a lone `"`. Added a recall-NEUTRAL capture group to each (pattern #1 captures `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----`, matching its own contract's expected credential which previously only passed via the co-firing PEM detector; pattern #3 captures the service-account email). Firing is unchanged (the literals were already required, so match boundaries are identical) → no bench needed for recall, and CredData impact is neutral-to-positive (a 1-char `"` never matched a labeled secret). BONUS: because google-artifact now captures the SAME `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` as the co-firing PEM detector, value-dedup merges them — the operator no longer sees a bogus 1-char `"` finding alongside the real one. Detector TOML was clean (not in the concurrent diff). **DONE 2026-07-06 — contracts_runner 7/7 green: google-artifact positive still fires (now self-captures the meaningful credential), all 129 evasions intact.** SYSTEMATIC HUNT (since the contract runner is BLIND to garbage-capture — `any_credential_contains` passes via a co-firing detector): probed all 78 clean-TOML detectors whose PRIMARY pattern lacks a capture group, comparing each detector's OWN self-captured credential to its contract's expected value. Result: **google-artifact was the ONLY real tested garbage-capture** — the surface is clean (the 2 "garbage" + 3 "prefix" flags were false positives: PEM/PuTTY keys redact to `----...----` and legitimately capture a superset containing the expected body). RESIDUAL (separate class, NOT this finding): UNTESTED secondary patterns of the same buried-JSON shape (e.g. google-classroom-api-credentials pattern #1 `"type":"service_account"[^\n]{0,128}"classroom"`, no group, no positive exercises it) — these are over-broad cross-file-JSON CONTEXT anchors (kimi-4 audit already flagged the classroom one) with no clear credential to capture; tightening/removing them is a precision change that is bench-gated — blocked by the concurrent whole-tree refactor (a stable A/B can't isolate it while the adjudicate/suppression src is churning); revisit once that lands.
- detectors/woocommerce-consumer-key.toml vs woocommerce-rest-api-credentials.toml | dup | P3 | BOTH detectors' primary regex is the SAME `ck_[a-f0-9]{40}` (found 2026-07-06 during evasion-backfill: a `ck_<40hex>` fires whichever wins value-dedup, observed woocommerce-rest-api-credentials). Two detector ids for one identical shape = redundant (ONE PLACE) + confusing attribution (a real WooCommerce consumer key is reported under an arbitrary one of the two). Consolidate: one detector owns `ck_` (consumer key), the other should anchor on its DISTINCT signal (rest-api = the `cs_` consumer-secret companion or the `/wp-json/wc/` endpoint), not re-declare `ck_`. Bench-check both mirror+CredData before merging (some corpus rows may attribute to the specific id). | open
- detectors/clerk-api-key.toml vs clerk-frontend-api-key.toml | dup + recall-gap | P2 | BOTH have the BYTE-IDENTICAL regex `pk_(?:live|test)_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}` (found 2026-07-06 via the garbage-capture hunt's no-self-finding bucket: clerk-api-key's own positive fires clerk-frontend-api-key after value-dedup). TWO problems: (1) ONE-PLACE dup — two detector ids for one identical shape. (2) **RECALL GAP** — a Clerk `pk_` is the PUBLISHABLE key (public by design, low sensitivity), yet clerk-api-key is NAMED "Clerk Publishable API Key" and clerk-frontend "Clerk Frontend API Key" — so BOTH flag the harmless public key while Clerk's actual SECRET key `sk_(live|test)_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}` is detected by NEITHER. Fix: clerk-api-key (the "API key") should own the SECRET `sk_` pattern (the real credential); clerk-frontend keeps `pk_`. That ADDS sk_ recall (bench-check FP) + removes the dup. Bench-gated (new firing) → blocked by the concurrent whole-tree refactor; revisit on a stable tree. | open
- detectors/notion-api-key.toml vs notion-integration-token.toml | subset-dup | P3 | notion-api-key = `secret_[a-zA-Z0-9]{43}`; notion-integration-token = `secret_[a-zA-Z0-9]{43,50}` + `ntn_[a-zA-Z0-9]{43,50}` (found 2026-07-06, same hunt bucket: notion-api-key's positive fires notion-integration-token). notion-api-key's `{43}` is a STRICT SUBSET of the token detector's `{43,50}` → fully redundant, and it lacks the newer `ntn_` prefix. notion-integration-token is the correct broader owner. Fix: retire notion-api-key (repoint any registry refs) OR give it a distinct notion secret shape it uniquely owns; never two ids for a subsumed regex. Bench-gated (attribution) + registry-integrity → stable-tree consolidation with the clerk/woocommerce/turso dups. | open
- suppression/decode.rs (decoded generic/entropy guard) vs tests/adversarial_explosion_runner.rs `every_contract_positive_fires_through_decode_wrappers` | conflict | P1 | **CONCURRENT IN-FLIGHT SRC WORK, not the evasion pass** — the decode-guard suppresses generic/entropy detectors on DECODED content (a precision feature: avoid FP on random base64/hex/url payloads), but the strict decode-through RECALL gate (added committed-green at 5c6471a87 with an EMPTY `allowed` allowlist) asserts every contract positive survives a base64/hex/url round-trip. The two now CONFLICT: **9 detectors lose their credential through decode-through** — the 3 generic-family (generic-api-key, generic-keyword-secret, generic-password) + 6 vendor detectors whose positive is actually caught by the generic/entropy pool not their own vendor pattern (aws-ses-smtp-credentials, bigcommerce-store-api-credentials, canva-api-token, flipside-api-key, kafka-connect-credentials, looksrare-api-key). Found 2026-07-06 in the evasion-pass FINAL RECONCILE. **Two runner symptoms, one root cause:** (i) `adversarial_explosion_runner::every_contract_positive_fires_through_decode_wrappers` — all 9 lose the credential through base64/hex/url; (ii) `encoding_explosion_runner::every_positive_swept_through_every_encoding` — `base64-url decode-hit 87.7% dropped below floor 88.0%` (the same suppressed hits). (Cross-checked clean: companion_contracts_runner + path_shape_runner GREEN — my companion/twilio-iot evasions don't break them; chunk_ad_runner's line-98 panic is only a missing `KEYHOG_CHUNK_IDS` env var when run standalone, not a regression.) `adjudicate/generic.rs`, `decode/pipeline.rs`, AND the test file itself are all uncommitted-modified by the concurrent worker (+ 70 other detector TOMLs mid vendor-anchoring pass) → this is their active decode-guard-vs-recall reconciliation, NOT mine (the evasion pass touched only contract `.toml` `[[evasion]]` sections + the ceiling gate + 2 guard-test helpers + a GPU source string the CPU decode path never runs; `contracts_runner` 7/7 + validation-129 green). **Resolution is a genuine architecture call the guard's owner must make** (do NOT silently allowlist all 9 — the test's own comment calls these "real recall regressions"): either (a) make the decode-guard NOT suppress a decoded generic/entropy hit that carries a strong keyword anchor (recover recall while keeping the anchorless-random-base64 FP suppression), OR (b) if a specific positive's re-encoded form is genuinely too short to be recognised as a base64/hex run, document THAT one in the `allowed` allowlist with its length reason. The 6 vendor detectors are the higher-value half — they SHOULD fire on their own vendor pattern (which would survive decode-through) rather than lean on the generic pool; anchoring them is the real fix. | open (owner: decode-guard concurrent work)

- context/inference.rs:343,352 | divergence/hardcoded | P2 | is_in_test_function vs is_rust_test_attribute divergent; lang markers hardcoded | unify + Tier-B | done-STALE (verified 2026-07-04: divergence resolved by a single-owner const. `CFG_TEST_ATTR` (inference.rs:57, `concat!("#[cfg(", "test)]")`) is documented as the "Single owner for the two match sites (`is_in_test_function` current-line check and `is_rust_test_attribute`)... keeping it in one place stops the two sites from silently drifting apart" (:52-56). `is_in_test_function` (:335) now COMPOSES `is_rust_test_attribute` (:344 `|| is_rust_test_attribute(trimmed)`) rather than re-implementing recognition, so they cannot diverge. - context/false_positive.rs:334 | perf | P4 | configmap walkback up to 4096 lines O(n^2) | cache indentation | open-LOW (verified 2026-07-04: the walkback (:351 `lines[start..line_idx].iter().rev()`) is a SINGLE backward pass that early-stops at the first less-indented line (the structural parent, :358) — for a well-formed ConfigMap it stops within a few lines at the `binaryData:` header. The 4096 cap (:337 doc) only bounds a pathological long same-indent run. Worst case is O(matches·4096·avg_indent), bounded — NOT true O(n²). "cache indentation" would only speed the pathological case (same line re-scored across multiple matches' walkbacks), marginal. Bounded-by-design; low priority.)

No double-iteration remains.)

Behavior is covered by real-value integration tests, which is the correct pattern here. Only `decode.rs` (context/decode) wasn't matched by name — if a genuine gap exists it's narrow; the P3 "add in-file tests" as written is unactionable (would fail the org gate).)

### verifier  (§5)
- verifier/cache.rs:209 | cache-bug | P3 | updated entry not moved to queue back → premature eviction | move key to back on update | done 2026-07-04 (generation-stamped lazy LRU: CacheEntry + queue markers carry a monotonic generation; put ALWAYS enqueues a fresh (key,gen) marker; evict_one_oldest remove_if-skips stale markers so a refreshed credential survives capacity eviction; reconcile retains only current-generation markers; amortized stale sweep when queue > 2x max (no O(N) reposition per update — avoids the cache.rs:286 P2 class). Contract test updated truthfully (overwrite re-enqueues, map does not grow) + NEW regression capacity_eviction_prefers_stale_slot_over_refreshed_entry proving the fixed eviction order)
- verifier/cache.rs:286,354 | perf | P2 | O(N) queue reconcile/evict under global Mutex | sampled/lock-free eviction | done-STALE 2026-07-04 (superseded by the cache.rs:208 generation-stamped lazy-LRU fix, done same day): per-op eviction is now AMORTIZED — `evict_expired` (the O(N) reconcile) runs only every `EVICTION_INTERVAL`-th insert (cache.rs:200-206); `reconcile_queue_with_entries` fires on insert ONLY when the queue doubles past `max_entries` (needs_stale_sweep, amortized O(1)); `enforce_max_entries_bound` is amortized O(1) (`evict_one_oldest` pops stale markers, bounded by the sweep). The residual O(N) lives only in the periodic `evict_expired` reconcile — inherent to expired-scanning (no sub-O(N) way to find TTL-expired keys without a separate priority index whose upkeep would cost more) AND amortized 1/`EVICTION_INTERVAL`. For a network-verification result cache (not a hot inner loop), the periodic Mutex hold is negligible. No further fix warranted; a lock-free/sampled rewrite would add complexity for no measurable win at this call frequency.
- verifier/verify/aws.rs:94 | wiring | P3 | region not resolved via resolve_field | resolve_field(region) | done 2026-07-03 (build_aws_probe now `let region = resolve(region)` where resolve = sanitize_raw_value∘resolve_field; `companion.region` refs resolve instead of failing the region screen verbatim)
- verifier/verify/aws.rs:40 | correctness | P3 | access/secret key not sanitized → trailing newline marks Dead | sanitize_raw_value | done 2026-07-03 (access/secret/session_token/region all go through `sanitize_raw_value(&resolve_field(…))` — mirrors the AuthSpec::Query arm; a live `AKIA…\n` no longer misreported Dead. NEW test crates/verifier/tests/regression_aws_field_sanitize.rs, 3 tests incl. real build_aws_probe preflight, all green) | §5
- verifier/verify/response.rs:224 | dos | P3 | JSON metadata inserted without length cap | truncate to MAX_METADATA_VALUE_BYTES | done (STALE 2026-07-03: cap EXISTS — cache.rs MAX_METADATA_{ENTRIES=16,KEY_BYTES=64,VALUE_BYTES=256} + truncate_to_char_boundary applied at the cache boundary cache.rs:404-408; response.rs:224 is now json_indicates_error, no unbounded insert)
- verifier/interpolate.rs:238 | correctness | P3 | single-companion fast path skips URL percent-encoding | url_encode on Url context | intentional/analysis-COMPLETE 2026-07-04 — DEFENSIBLE, no bug. The `{{match}}`/`{{companion.X}}` fast path (interpolate.rs:249-262) fires ONLY when `matches("{{").count()==1` AND the token spans the WHOLE template → the value IS the entire field. For the URL field that means the whole URL, which must NOT be %-encoded (encoding `https://…`→`https%3A%2F%2F…` would corrupt it). Embedded tokens (`…/{{companion.X}}/…`) carry literal text so they can't be whole-template → they take the SLOW path (resolve_placeholder→`interpolate_placeholder_value`) which DOES %-encode per-component. It is structurally impossible for a whole-template to be a "component". Safety is not lost: the fast path `sanitize_raw_value` control-strips (CRLF/injection), and a malicious whole-URL companion is caught by the SSRF gate (`is_private_url` on the final URL), not by %-encoding. Detector-usage audit (the open question): `grep` finds ZERO detectors using `{{companion.X}}` in a URL template (whole or embedded) — so the path is both correct-by-construction AND effectively unexercised in URL context. No divergence.
- verifier/oob/client.rs:592 | correctness | P3 | OOB URL normalization keeps path → malformed host | keep scheme/host/port only | done (STALE 2026-07-03: collector_host_and_port at oob/client.rs:589 extracts host_str()+port_or_known_default() and returns "host:port" — path is already discarded, no malformed host)
- verifier lib.rs:227 DEFAULT_HTTPS_PORT | dup+dead | P3 | const created to own `.unwrap_or(443)` but NEVER wired → dead const + 2 magic 443s (oob/client.rs:596, verify/request.rs:218) | done 2026-07-03 (wired both call sites to `crate::DEFAULT_HTTPS_PORT`; const now live, dead-code warning gone, ONE owner) | §7-ONE-PLACE
- verifier/oob/client.rs:135 | weak-crypto | P4 | test constructor 1024-bit RSA | 2048-bit | done 2026-07-04 (bumped 1024→2048, NIST-minimum modulus — a security tool must not ship a 1024-bit RSA key even in a test-only constructor. The naive fix would 4-8x the keygen cost across the **221** `for_test` call sites (~15-30s added to the suite, Law 7); instead added a shared `static TEST_RSA_KEY: LazyLock<Result<RsaPrivateKey,String>>` generated ONCE and `.clone()`d into each instance — full 2048 hygiene, single keygen, Result contract preserved (no panic-gate violation), zero cost in production builds. Production keygen (client.rs:248 `register`) was already 2048.)

### sources  (§7)
- sources/filesystem.rs:524,737 | traversal | P3 | canonicalize().unwrap_or(root) bypasses containment on failure | skip/refuse uncanonicalizable | done (MISDIAGNOSIS 2026-07-03: neither site is a containment check on untrusted input. :524 canonicalizes the scan ROOT itself (the boundary, operator-chosen — no parent to escape). :737's `allowed` is the operator's EXPLICIT --include paths (operator intent), and a canonicalize-failing path is handled LOUDLY downstream — is_file/is_dir both false → else branch filesystem.rs:823 emits tracing::warn! + record_skip_event(Unreadable) + SourceError, NOT a silent drop. No traversal/fail-open.)
- sources/cloud/mod.rs:122 | perf/hang | P5 | blocking ToSocketAddrs on caller thread | async/threadpool DNS | wontfix-BY-DESIGN (verified 2026-07-04: the cloud source path is intentionally BLOCKING I/O — cloud/mod.rs:21 exposes `blocking_client()` (reqwest blocking), and every caller of `validate_cloud_endpoint`/`screen_resolved_endpoint_host` is a SYNC fn invoked with `?` not `.await` (s3/mod.rs:472 in `fn build_base_url`, gcs.rs:145 in `fn collect_gcs_chunks`). There is NO async executor thread to stall, so the blocking `(host,port).to_socket_addrs()` (:147, the SSRF resolve-and-refuse screen) is CORRECT and consistent with the module's blocking design. "async/threadpool DNS" would wrongly inject async into a sync blocking-I/O module. Also runs once per source-setup, not per-object. Not a bug.)
- sources/docker/layer.rs:179 | perf | P6 | re-canonicalizes layer_root per chunk | cache once | done 2026-07-04 (rewrite_layer_chunks canonicalizes the invariant layer_root ONCE before the chunk loop and threads the canonical Path into rewrite_chunk/layer_relative_path; per-chunk path now pays only its own unavoidable canonicalize — was 2 syscalls/chunk walking the same dir thousands of times on a big layer)
- sources/filesystem/extract/archive/zip_scan/duplicates.rs:459 | unbounded-alloc | P4 | eager central-dir alloc from user zip header | bounded reads / validate vs file size | done (STALE 2026-07-04: duplicates.rs:453 rejects central_offset+central_size > file_len BEFORE `vec![0u8; central_len]` — the alloc is already file-size-bounded, plus the u32::MAX sentinel guard at :439)
- sources/filesystem/extract/archive.rs:54 | dup | P2 | archive extension list dup rules/openpack-extensions.toml | done (STALE 2026-07-04: OPENPACK_EXTS is already a LazyLock over include_str!("rules/openpack-extensions.toml") — single Tier-B owner, verified archive.rs:48)
- sources/tests/regression_git_blob_drops_counted.rs:45 (+ every git-spawning sources test: git_source.rs, regression_git_commit_scan.rs, regression_gitignore_nesting.rs, …) | test-hygiene/host-dep | P5 | the `git(repo,args)` helper runs `Command::new("git")` with NO env isolation — no `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null`, `GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null`, `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, or `-c commit.gpgsign=false`. On a host WITH global `commit.gpgsign=true` / a credential.helper / core.hooksPath, `git commit`/`git add` BLOCKS on a passphrase/credential/hook prompt → the test hangs indefinitely (Testing-Contract "HOST-INDEPENDENCE" violation; a latent CI hang). Secondary: 15 tests serialize on one global `COUNTER_LOCK` mutex + write a 10 MiB blob, so they crawl under I/O/CPU load (observed **721s @ 14% CPU** under concurrent builds during the Arc<str> validation — NOT a deadlock, NOT the Arc<str> change; this machine's git config is clean so it didn't truly hang, just contended). | isolate the git env inside the shared `git()` helper: `.env("GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL","/dev/null").env("GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM","/dev/null").env("GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT","0")` + `-c commit.gpgsign=false` so every git-based test is host-independent (ONE-PLACE: fix the shared helper, not each test). | open — NOT fixed now: sources git code + these tests sit in the concurrent worker's ACTIVE restructuring zone (137 sources mods, git/source.rs in flux); fix on a settled tree to avoid clobbering their WIP.

- core/allowlist/glob.rs:200 | perf | P8 | segment_match allocs Vec<char> for non-ascii | iterate chars | open-LOW (verified 2026-07-04: the `Vec<char>` is only built on the NON-ASCII branch (glob.rs:211-213); ASCII globs — the overwhelming majority of allowlist patterns/paths — take the zero-alloc `segment_match_units(as_bytes())` fast path (:207-208). The `Vec<char>` feeds `segment_match_units`, which needs O(1) INDEXED access for `*`-backtracking (star_pi/star_ti reset) — a plain char iterator can't backtrack, so removing the Vec would require re-slicing `&str` at saved char-boundary offsets AND breaking the generic `<T: Copy+Eq>` matcher shared with the ASCII path. Cold-path alloc + shared-matcher coupling → not worth the rewrite. Low priority.)

- core/config.rs:67,73 | wiring-gap | P7 | ScanConfig.max_file_size + dedup never read on live path | remove or wire | done-DOCUMENTED (verified 2026-07-06: NOT a dead/unwired KNOB — the operator-facing caps ARE live, they just flow through a DIFFERENT owner than ScannerConfig. `max_file_size`: `ScanArgs.max_file_size → FilesystemSource::with_max_file_size` (grep-confirmed live: filesystem.rs:596-597 setter, :633-635 applied in the walker; hosted_git.rs:885, docker layer/metadata all feed it) — the effective cap works. `dedup`: `ScanArgs.dedup → DedupScope` applied by the verifier. The two ScannerConfig fields (config.rs:67,73) are DOCUMENTED surface-only mirrors — each carries an explicit NOTE: "not read here on the live path... retained for the canonical config surface but is not carried into ScannerConfig." So it is honest+documented, not a silent wiring lie. Mild ONE-PLACE nit (a second surface home for a value the source/verifier layer owns), but removing them breaks the public `ScannerConfig` API (Law-3, needs a migration path) and they are labeled — low priority, defensible as-is. Not a bug.)

- benchmarks/Makefile:125 | portability | P3 | ** glob not POSIX sh | use find | done-STALE (verified 2026-07-06: the `clean` recipe already uses POSIX `find bench -type d -name __pycache__ -prune -exec rm -rf {} +` with the rationale documented in-recipe (":`bench/**/__pycache__` is a bash globstar, but make runs recipes with /bin/sh where `**` collapses to a single-level `*`... `find` is POSIX and recurses to every depth"). No `**` glob remains.)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml:197,260 | missing-gate | P2-P3 | verifier/sources --lib not tested | add cargo test -p keyhog-verifier/-sources --lib | done 2026-07-04 (added `cargo test -p keyhog-verifier --lib` + `-p keyhog-sources --lib` to the core test job; YAML validated. Directly relevant: the verifier cache eviction contracts I just added would otherwise be CI-invisible on the default build)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml:511,563 + bench/differential | unpinned-dep | P2-P3 | tools unpinned | pin versions/SHAs | open
- .github/workflows/ci.yml:550 | missing-gate | P2 | continuous fuzzing not wired | nightly fuzz job | needs-review
- .github/workflows/release.yml:309,369 | gate | P2 | docker+major-tag lack needs:build | add needs:build | done 2026-07-04 (both jobs had NO `needs:` → could publish the GHCR container / move the floating major tag `v1` in parallel with (or before) `build`, shipping an empty/stale image or pointing `@v1` at a half-published release. Added `needs: build` to both — release DAG is now build → {sign, docker} → (each gated on build), and major-tag waits for build. Used bare `needs: build` (not `[build,sign,docker]`) deliberately: sign/docker may carry `if:` conditions and a skipped needed-job would skip major-tag too. YAML validated via yaml.safe_load; all 4 jobs' `needs:` resolve. File was CLEAN/non-concurrent so no collision.)
- .github/workflows/integration-smoke.yml:54,65 | coherence | P2 | downloads install.sh from main not checked-out ref | use github.sha | wontfix-BY-DESIGN (verified 2026-07-06: the workflow header (:3-10) states this is the "End-to-end smoke test of the actual install paths a stranger hits ... Manual only. Pre-launch sanity gate" — it INTENTIONALLY `curl`s the LIVE published install.sh/install.ps1 from `main` + installs a PUBLISHED release (`--version`, default v0.5.37), which is exactly what a real stranger gets. There is NO checkout step (by design). `github.sha` would test an UNPUBLISHED script against a published release — breaking the "real stranger path" intent. OPTIONAL enhancement (a maintainer CI-design call, NOT a bug): replace hardcoded `main` with `${{ github.ref_name }}` so a maintainer can dispatch on a branch/tag to smoke a proposed install-script change pre-merge, while main-dispatch keeps testing main. Not a defect; the row's "use github.sha" fix is a misdiagnosis.)

### vyre (separate repo — santhsecurity/vyre)  (its own § at end)
- vyre L2 region-presence under-fire | correctness | P1 | not prefilter/region-bsearch (both correct); under-fire is DFA-walk emit or byte-class mask build | GPU differential on reproducing literal set | open

# ============================================================================
# SUB-EFFORT: SETTINGS CONSOLIDATION — ONE-PLACE detector TOML
# (a focused cross-cutting effort under this plan; its tasks map to §4/§7/§8/§14)
# ============================================================================

## Settings Consolidation — the detector TOML owns every knob

## The law (non-negotiable)
A detector's behavior is owned **entirely** by its `detectors/<id>.toml` file.
Confidence floor, entropy floor, keywords, suppression rules — all of it lives in
the TOML. The Rust code is a **dumb engine**: it reads the merged `DetectorSpec`
and executes it exactly. There is:
- **NO** second file that also sets a floor (`rules/entropy-floors.toml` dies).
- **NO** hardcoded per-detector map in Rust (`SHIPPED_DETECTOR_FLOORS` dies).
- **NO** global default that wins over a detector (`min_confidence 0.40`, `entropy 4.5/3.5`, `4.8` cutoff — die).
- **NO** silent override / clamp / fallback anywhere (Law 10).
To change a detector you edit its file, and **nothing else in the tree can override it.**

Non-detector data (source/parser/infra lists) that is genuinely global gets **exactly one**
Tier-B data file owner under `rules/` — never inline, never duplicated.

Success test: `grep -rInE '<any threshold/keyword list>' crates/*/src/` returns the
*schema/default* in one place and every value comes from a TOML.

---

## RECALL EVIDENCE (why this matters — real CredData, results-fullfield 2026-06-21)
keyhog: R=0.288 P=0.328 F1=0.307 (TP 4008 / FP 8209 / FN 9909). Rank 2/6 (betterleaks
0.530 leads; keyhog beats noseyparker/titus/kingfisher/trufflehog). Misses concentrate in
the SHAPELESS/generic buckets — Key FN 3710, UUID FN 2262, Password FN 1275, Secret FN 803 —
i.e. the values handled by the **synthetic generic detectors + entropy/ML fallback**, NOT the
service-specific TOML detectors (those score F1 0.83–1.0). Recall is bound by the generic path,
and the generic path's knobs are the scattered settings below. THIS is why "tune recall" fails
today: the floors/thresholds/keywords that decide whether a shapeless candidate survives live
in ~6 code locations, not one place you can edit.

### FRESH BASELINE (2026-07-04, live `bench run keyhog creddata` on THIS churned tree)
Build: `--no-default-features --features ci-lean --profile release-fast` (CPU/SIMD, **no GPU**). Result:
**R=0.295 P=0.293 F1=0.294** (TP 4109 / FP 9907 / FN 9808; 16490 findings; 14.4 MB/s, 67.6s, 2.5GB RSS).
Consistent with the 2026-06-21 full-field F1=0.307 — the small precision delta (FP 9907 vs 8209) is most
likely the ci-lean build lacking GPU-ML FP-suppression, NOT a regression. **This confirms the current real
number is ~0.30 (rank ~2/6), which means the stale note "F1 0.15 / 5th-of-6" is WRONG — corrected.**
Per-category LEVER MAP (biggest drags, actionable):
- **`Key`**: FP 2537 + FN 3719 (P=0.029 R=0.020) — the #1 drag on BOTH axes (generic key material).
- **`API`**: FP 1335 / TP 1 (P=0.001) — a PURE PRECISION FLOOD; the generic-API path over-matches. Killing
  these 1335 FP is the single biggest precision win available. → concrete precision task.
- **`UUID`**: FP 563, R=0.003 (7 TP / 2267) — keyhog barely detects UUID-shaped secrets + floods FP.
- **`Secret`** FP 1732 / **`Password`** FP 988 FN 1220 (best generic, P=0.54) / **`Auth`** FP 488 / **`Token`** FP 428.
- Specific vendor detectors are near-perfect (Anthropic/Google API key F1=1.0, AWS Client ID P=0.54) — the
  entire drag is the GENERIC path, confirming the 2026-06-21 diagnosis. Bench artifact: scratchpad/creddata-baseline-churned.json.
  NOTE: any generic-path precision/recall change MUST re-run this A/B (churn cancels in both arms) before landing.
- PER-DETECTOR FP (fresh 2026-07-04): entropy-token fp4465/tp285 (94%) + entropy-api-key fp2270/tp609 (79%) =
  the ML-ONLY b64/uuid pool (KH-L-0405/0406 proved NOT heuristic-tunable — leave to model authority). The
  TUNABLE precision lever, distinct from that pool: **`private-key` fp135/tp0 (100% FP)** and
  **`google-cloud-storage-hmac-key` fp78/tp0 (100% FP)** — pure-FP detectors (never correct on CredData). Plus
  `ssh-private-key` fp815/tp30 (96%) + `kubernetes-bootstrap-token` fp77/tp8 (91%, loose `[a-z0-9]{6}\.[a-z0-9]{16}`).
  → INVESTIGATED 2026-07-04: `private-key` (135 FP/0 TP) is a CredData LABELING ARTIFACT, NOT an over-match —
  `detectors/private-key.toml` matches the STRONG unambiguous PEM marker (`-----BEGIN … PRIVATE KEY-----`,
  keywords `["BEGIN","PRIVATE KEY"]`); a match IS a real private key, so 135 FP/0 TP means CredData labels those
  real test/example PEM keys as negatives. Tightening would LOSE real detection → DO NOT touch it (matches the
  memory's "might be labeling artifacts" caveat). Same likely for `gcs-hmac`/`ssh-private-key` (strong-prefix
  detectors on test keys). CONCLUSION: the pure-FP strong detectors are bench-scoring artifacts, not precision
  bugs; the genuine FP drag is the ML-ONLY entropy-token/api-key b64/uuid pool (heuristic-tunable levers are
  documented-saturated). Remaining F1 gain = model-authoritative ML surfacing, the known hard arc. [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]]

## GROUND TRUTH (2026-07-02, supersedes earlier assumptions)
- **Four** phase2-generic detectors ship as first-class `detectors/*.toml` specs:
  `generic-api-key`, `generic-secret`, `generic-password`, `generic-keyword-secret`.
  `generic-database-url` was **REMOVED** (user-approved): nothing emitted or floor-keyed it;
  scheme://user:pass@host coverage overlaps `url-credentials` + connection-string detectors
  (0 dogfood events). Embedded corpus: **919** detectors; **344** carry `[detector.verify]`.
- (Historical) The five generics were originally **SYNTHETIC**: code-only ids in
  `crates/scanner/src/detector_ids.rs`, emitted by the phase-2 path, with knobs scattered
  across code until promotion to TOML specs (A0/A1).
- Confidence floor is ALREADY single-owner: global default `0.40` lives once at
  `core/config.rs:155`; `SHIPPED_DETECTOR_FLOORS` is already empty `&[]`; per-detector
  `min_confidence` is already a `DetectorSpec` field (57 detectors set it). A2 is mostly DONE.

## WORKSTREAM A — promote the synthetic generic detectors to TOML specs that own every knob
- [x] **A0 schema foundation.** `DetectorSpec` now has `kind: DetectorKind` (regex | phase2-generic)
  + `entropy_floor: Vec<EntropyFloorBucket>`; `patterns` is `#[serde(default)]` so a phase2-generic
  detector can omit regex. `validate.rs` is kind-aware (regex⇒patterns required; phase2-generic⇒
  patterns forbidden, keywords required). VERIFIED compiling.
- [x] **A1 ENTROPY FLOOR fully migrated + VERIFIED GREEN.** The four generic detectors are now
  first-class `detectors/generic-*.toml` phase2-generic specs; each owns its `entropy_floor`
  in its own TOML. `entropy_floors.rs` builds the runtime table from the embedded corpus specs
  (not a file); `rules/entropy-floors.toml` has been removed. Ripple fixed: count now 919
  after generic-database-url removal (registry + coherence_verify_count + verification.md),
  corpus-integrity patterns-exemption, ~29 files' DetectorSpec literals. TESTS GREEN: core
  detector_corpus_integrity 16/16, scanner regression_entropy_floor_config 19/19 (byte-for-byte
  legacy floor parity), regression_detector_registry_integrity 15/15 (919 count + phase2-generic).
- [ ] **A1b keyword/suppression knobs — the phase-2 EMITTER still reads `detector_ids.rs` constants +
  `phase2_generic.rs` keyword lists, NOT the new specs' `keywords`.** So keywords are still dual-homed
  (TOML metadata vs engine lists). NEXT: rewire the phase-2 emitter to read keywords/suppression from
  the generic specs, making the TOML the sole home for EVERY generic knob (not just the floor).
- [x] **A1c DELETE `rules/entropy-floors.toml`** — DONE (file gone; no code loads it).
- [x] **A1d docs count** — DONE: all doc/README/SVG detector counts reconciled to the live 919
  (registry-pinned `EXPECTED_EMBEDDED_DETECTOR_COUNT`).
- [~] **A2 confidence floor — already single-owner (see Ground Truth).** Remaining: assert with a
  gate test that no second confidence default can appear; keep `core/config.rs:155` the sole owner.
- [ ] **A3 suppression thresholds → TOML.** Inline entropy cutoff `4.8`+len floor
  (`suppression/decision.rs:112,114`), confidence penalties (`confidence/penalties.rs:180`),
  confidence policy magic (`confidence/policy.rs:399`) → per-detector TOML or single schema owner.
- [~] **A4 detection keyword lists → TOML keywords / one Tier-B file.**
  - [x] `BLOCKCHAIN_ADDR_KEYWORDS` (`phase2_entropy/gates.rs`) → `rules/blockchain-address-keywords.toml`
    (single-quoted TOML literals preserve the significant trailing `=`/space/`"` boundary bytes). DONE.
  - [ ] `CREDENTIAL_COMPACT_KEYWORDS`, `STRONG_HEX_KEY_COMPACT_EXACT` (`phase2_generic/keywords.rs`) —
    these drive the phase-2 EMITTER classification; they belong in the generic-detector spec `keywords`
    (the A1b emitter rewire), NOT a flat rules file. Do them WITH A1b, gated on a recall bench.
  - [ ] `keyword_is_crypto_key_material`/`keyword_is_key_material` (`entropy/scanner.rs`),
    `entropy/keywords.rs` → detector TOML `keywords` or one `rules/*.toml`.

## WORKSTREAM A5 (2026-07-04, design decision w/ user) — EVERYTHING per-detector, compile-time resolved
Direction confirmed: the detector TOML owns EVERY per-detector behavior knob (the A-law), resolved
at scanner-compile time into dense indexed tables so per-detector tuning has ZERO hot-path cost.
- [x] A5a DONE 2026-07-04 (first step): EntropyFloorTable.families is now HashMap<detector_id, buckets> — O(1) keyed lookup replacing the O(families) string-compare scan; from_specs + family_floor + the exactly-the-generic-ids test updated; scanner entropy_floors tests green. (The further dense-detector-index resolution at CompiledScanner build remains open for when floors generalize corpus-wide.) Original row:
  `entropy_floors.rs:44 family_floor` does a LINEAR `families.iter().find(|f| f.detector == detector_id)`
  STRING-compare per candidate. Fine at 4 families; a Law-7 bug at 919. Fix: resolve detector_id → dense
  detector index once at CompiledScanner build; table = `Vec<&[EntropyFloorBucket]>` indexed load
  (same cost as a global const). Entropy itself is computed once per CANDIDATE (O(len)) — per-detector
  floors never multiply it; only the comparison is per-detector, and that's one array load. So YES:
  per-detector entropy everywhere, no perf loss, PROVIDED the lookup is index-not-string.
- [ ] A5b promote per-detector SUPPRESSION/SHAPE exemptions from code to spec fields: the KH-L-0110
  strong-keyword hex-key exemption, the url-credentials/sql/cli dictionary-gate scoping
  (`detector_ids::is_url_userinfo_detector` / `is_structural_password_slot_detector`), and A3's inline
  4.8 cutoff/penalties are all per-detector policy currently hardcoded as id-match functions in Rust —
  exactly the pre-A1 floor pattern. Become optional DetectorSpec fields (e.g. `suppression.allow_hex_key_lens`,
  `suppression.dictionary_gate`, `suppression.entropy_cutoff`) with compiled defaults, resolved into the
  same dense per-detector table at scanner build. Engine keeps ZERO id-string matches on the hot path.
- [ ] A5c OPTIONAL build-time codegen: corpus is already `include_str!` (compile-time embed) + one-time
  LazyLock parse (~ms startup, zero per-scan cost). A build.rs spec→static-table codegen would remove the
  startup parse + the corpus-load panic path, and could thread per-detector constants into WGSL at GPU
  pipeline build. Startup/robustness win only — scan throughput is unaffected either way; do AFTER A1b/A5a/A5b.
- Boundary (do NOT move into detector specs): host-dependent knobs (autoroute cache), engine-wide
  mechanics (chunk size, prefilter engine, decode depth — Tier-A), and shared non-detector data lists
  (Tier-B rules/*.toml). Per-detector = behavior of ONE detector; everything else keeps its current tier.

## WORKSTREAM C — silent overrides → DELETE (Law 10, recall-critical)
- [~] RESOLVED (verified 2026-07-04): every `.ok()`/`unwrap_or`/`Err(_)=>` in `sources/src/filesystem.rs` now carries an explicit `// LAW10:` justification (e.g. canonicalize-failure→original-path best-effort, missing-ext→generic-symlink-wording with the symlink still REFUSED, prefix-mismatch→default-exclude-only with target bytes never opened — all recall-safe). Law-10 allows loud/recorded/recall-preserving fallbacks; these are audited, not silent.
- [~] RESOLVED (verified 2026-07-04): `scanner/src/structured/parsers/yaml.rs` — the `k.as_str().unwrap_or_default()` sites (yaml.rs:65/333) carry `// LAW10:` notes (missing/non-string key → empty → value fails downstream shape/length checks, recall-safe), and tail-loss is made visible via `documents_parsed` (yaml.rs:151/231) rather than dropped silently. Audited, not silent.
- [x] `rules/entropy-floors.toml:56-58` dead-config-silent-noop (generic-database-url family): RESOLVED 2026-07-02: rules/entropy-floors.toml was already removed (floors moved to per-detector TOMLs), and the generic-database-url detector itself was removed as a redundant phantom (user-approved). Family + floor both gone.

# ============================================================================
# THE §-SPINE — the canonical strategic subsystem plan (§0-§14) + PART II µ-tasks.
# This is the ONE organizing structure; the OPEN rows above map into these §N.
# ============================================================================

## §-spine — hand-authored, code-grounded, exhaustive

Every task below was written by reading the actual source it names — file and
line, real function, real cap, real branch, real flag. There is **no template
and no generator**. Each entry is a *bunch* of work, never a single assertion:
a testing task means an entire new test file/suite (often hundreds of cases) PLUS
the dogfood run that verifies it on real machines; a fix task means the code
change PLUS its regression suite PLUS the dogfood proof.

## How to work this file
- Work top-to-bottom. The diff is the report. Never weaken a test to pass (Law 6/9).
- **One test case is NOT one test.** A test task ships a whole suite/file. Assert
  real values — exact file, line, rule id, redacted value, CWE, severity, exit
  code, output bytes, SARIF partialFingerprint — never `!is_empty()`.
- **No task is ever skipped for being too big.** A "massive" tag means *do it in
  installments across turns*, not defer it. The §0 MASSIVE tasks are listed FIRST
  precisely so they can never be quietly skipped. If a task is too big for one
  turn, land the first installment and keep going — never write "later".
- Dogfooding is verification: install the SHIPPED binary (install.sh / installer /
  released artifact — never `cargo run`), then drive the real workflow. A finding
  with no reproducing test does not count.
- This file grows in installments. It is authored top-to-bottom by reading the
  codebase module by module; new sections are appended as more of the tree is read.

## Shared shorthand (defined once; every task is still specific about which axes apply)
- **INPUT-MATRIX** (8): (a) UTF-8 file; (b) UTF-16LE/BE via mmap window seam;
  (c) base64 + percent-encoded decode-through; (d) gzip/zip/tar nested-archive
  member; (e) git-history blob from a prior/deleted commit; (f) stdin pipe;
  (g) multiline/continuation (YAML block scalar, shell `\`, dotenv quote);
  (h) structured decode-derived buffer (k8s `data:` / compose / tfstate / ipynb).
- **MACHINE-MATRIX** (6): linux-x86_64 desktop; macOS-arm64 CI runner;
  RTX 5090 host; RTX 4090 host; santhserver (multi-GPU); Windows ThinkPad.
- **ASSERT-SPINE**: exact file, line, rule id, redacted value, CWE, severity,
  exit code, and (where emitted) output bytes + SARIF partialFingerprint.
- **DIFFERENTIAL-PEERS**: trufflehog, gitleaks, noseyparker, detect-secrets,
  on the shared CredData corpus — assert keyhog recall ≥ peer or document the
  exact FP/FN delta and the precision tradeoff.

---

# §0 — MASSIVE TASKS (listed first; NEVER skipped, only done in installments)

These are the big-ticket, multi-day items. Each is decomposed into concrete
installments so progress is always a landable diff, never a deferral.

- **M-01 — Ship-time pattern compilation: precompile + embed the matching engine.**
  Today `CompiledScanner::compile` rebuilds the AC map, phase-2 RegexSet, bloom,
  alphabet screen, and `detector_digest` on every process start (see
  `engine/compiled_api.rs::warm`, `compiler/compiler_compile.rs`). Installments:
  (1) serialize the compiled AC automaton + phase-2 prefilter + bigram bloom +
  alphabet screen into a versioned blob keyed by `detector_digest()`; (2) embed
  the default + portable detector-set blobs at build time via `build.rs`;
  (3) load-or-rebuild with a LOUD rebuild reason if the embedded digest mismatches
  the loaded detectors (Law 10 — never silently rebuild); (4) a criterion bench
  proving cold-start `compile()` wall-clock drops to the deserialize cost on all
  MACHINE-MATRIX hosts; (5) a regression suite asserting the deserialized scanner
  emits byte-identical findings to a freshly-compiled one over the contracts
  corpus. (Task system #14.)
- **M-02 — Full GPU↔CPU decoder + scan parity (the ~30 known parity gaps).**
  Drive every detector through `ScanBackend::Gpu`, `MegaScan`, `SimdCpu`, and
  `CpuFallback` on real GPU hosts and assert identical `RawMatch` sets (value,
  offset, confidence within the documented epsilon). Installments: one parity
  test file per detector family; a `backend_parity_matrix` extension that runs on
  RTX 5090/4090/santhserver (Law 8 — "skipped: no GPU" is a config bug on those
  hosts); fix each divergence at its WGSL-vs-CPU source, not by loosening epsilon.
  **VERIFIED GREEN on RTX 5090 (2026-07-06, `--features gpu` default build, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=…/cargo-target-kgpu`): all 7 scanner backend-parity tests PASS with the GPU genuinely engaged (idle baseline 6% → peak 13% util during the run, NOT pure CPU fallback; Law-8 satisfied — the GPU host did not skip the GPU) — `backend_parity_matrix` (2, forces Gpu/MegaScan/SimdCpu/CpuFallback per fixture), `backend_parity_determinism_fixed_corpus` (3), `pathless_chunk_cpu_recall_parity` (1), and the heavy `worst_case_backend_parity` (1, 18.57s on large/adversarial inputs). So keyhog's GPU↔CPU SCAN parity holds on real GPU hardware. The "~30 known parity gaps" are NOT in keyhog's scanner backend — they are the gpudecode/vyre CPU/GPU DECODER parity failures ([[gpudecode-resolve-and-parity]], a separate repo/lane), not this M-02 scan-backend surface. **REAL-WORKLOAD DIVERGENCE FOUND (2026-07-06, the gap the unit fixtures MISS).** The unit parity fixtures only drove the GPU to 13% util (correctness-parity on non-borderline inputs, NOT heavy-load parity). A real-workload `keyhog scan --backend gpu` vs `--backend simd` differential on CredData/data (11390 real files, GPU hit **56% util** = genuine megakernel engagement) DIVERGES: **gpu 9392 unique findings vs simd 10200 — GPU misses 1499, SIMD misses 691** (default CLI confidence floor, so ~10k findings not the bench's ~13.5k). The divergence is **ALMOST ENTIRELY the generic/entropy family**: SIMD-only = entropy-api-key 1225 + generic-secret 258 + generic-password 5 + entropy-token 3 + entropy-password 2; GPU-only = entropy-api-key 404 + generic-secret 133 + entropy-token 132 + entropy-password 11 + a few `:reassembled`. VENDOR detectors are near-identical (the only vendor deltas: mongodb-connection-string 5, resend 1 SIMD-only; azure-storage-account-key 1, magento-integration-token 1, url-credentials 2 GPU-only — single-digit, likely decode/reassembly-order). **ROOT (connects to [[generic-entropy-detectors-are-net-negative-on-f1]]): the generic/entropy detectors sit at BORDERLINE confidence (entropy-threshold-gated), and the GPU-megakernel vs CPU-SIMD entropy/confidence computation differs by more than the parity epsilon on those borderline values, pushing ~1900 near-threshold findings across the confidence floor inconsistently. So the generic/entropy pool is unreliable on a THIRD axis — GPU↔CPU parity — not just F1 (net-negative) and perf (the always-active floor). The vendor detectors (clear-cut confidence) are parity-stable. The unit `backend_parity_matrix` misses this because its fixtures aren't confidence-borderline.** **DETERMINISM CONFIRMED (2026-07-06): gpu=9392 and simd=10200 on BOTH runs (gpu run1-vs-run2 = 0 diff) → the divergence is a REAL, SYSTEMATIC GPU-vs-CPU difference, NOT run-variance.** So it is a genuine M-02 parity gap. **ROOT-CAUSE RESULT (`--min-confidence 0` on both backends, 2026-07-06): CANDIDATE-ADMISSION, not confidence-float.** At floor 0 the backends STILL diverge — and MORE (gpu 23463 vs simd 25798; SIMD-only 5091, GPU-only 2756, still ~all entropy-api-key/generic-secret/entropy-token). So the GPU and SIMD backends genuinely SURFACE DIFFERENT CANDIDATE SETS for the anchorless generic/entropy detectors — the conf=0.5-exactly findings seen at the default floor were only the tip visible above the floor; the real divergence is in candidate admission and is even larger below it. This is a DEEPER, more-serious class than confidence-float: a GPU-vs-CPU RECALL divergence (which secrets are even considered), directly matching [[simd-trigger-union-recall-load-bearing]] — the anchorless detectors (~49 no-literal: entropy-*, twilio-auth-token, …) fire ONLY via the Hyperscan-hit trigger, and the GPU region-presence producer's trigger union differs from the SIMD trigger-bitmap, so the two backends admit different anchorless candidates. SIMD surfaces MORE (25798 vs 23463) → net the GPU under-admits anchorless candidates, but it is asymmetric (GPU-only 2756 too), so both miss some the other finds. Vendor/literal-anchored detectors are parity-STABLE (they gate on a required literal via the AC, which both backends share) — only the anchorless generic/entropy pool diverges. **CONFIRMED CODE ROOT (backend_triggered.rs, 2026-07-06) — and it is a LAW-10 SILENT RECALL LOSS on the GPU backend, broader than generic/entropy: `collect_triggered_patterns_simd` = `collect_triggered_patterns_cpu` (AC literals) + HYPERSCAN hits (`scanner.scan_matches_result`, regex-capable); `collect_triggered_patterns_cpu` = AC literals ONLY (`ac.find_overlapping_iter`, no HS); `collect_triggered_patterns_gpu` (:249-293) = `collect_triggered_patterns_cpu` (AC) + GPU-LITERAL-presence — i.e. AC + GPU literal matcher, MISSING the Hyperscan regex union the SIMD path has.** The GPU literal matcher structurally CANNOT trigger a no-literal (regex-only) detector, so on the GPU backend the ~49 HS-only detectors [[simd-trigger-union-recall-load-bearing]] are silently under-triggered — and that set includes VENDOR detectors (twilio-auth-token, datadog-api-key, …), NOT just entropy. On CredData/data it surfaced as the entropy divergence (that corpus's HS-only content is mostly entropy), but any twilio/datadog/etc. secret in a no-literal position is SILENTLY MISSED on `--backend gpu` while `--backend simd` finds it — a Law-10 in-file recall loss on the NORMAL GPU success path (the `deny_silent_gpu_degrade` guard only covers GPU FAILURE, not this normal-path trigger gap). The SIMD trigger-union bug was fixed @3ccad545 but the GPU path was NOT brought to parity. FULL-FLOW CONFIRMED: `scan_coalesced_phase2` (:356) consumes the passed phase-1 `triggers` bitmap and EXTRACTS on hardcoded `ScanBackend::SimdCpu` (:371, CPU on BOTH backends) — so the extraction is identical and the divergence is PURELY the phase-1 trigger bitmap, NOT a megakernel-float issue (the conf=0.5 confidence-float reading was a RED HERRING — the borderline findings were a secondary effect; the primary is candidate admission via the trigger). **FIX IS DEEP, NOT a trigger-union (that idea is ARCHITECTURALLY WRONG): HS is ~77.5% of scan and the GPU backend exists to OFFLOAD it, so adding a CPU Hyperscan pass into `collect_triggered_patterns_gpu` would make `--backend gpu` SLOWER than `--backend simd` — self-defeating. The real options: (a) the GPU megakernel/region-presence producer must itself cover the no-literal detectors' REGEX triggers on-GPU (deep WGSL work — the true "fix at the WGSL source"); or (b) the tractable LAW-10 fix — the GPU backend must LOUDLY surface that it structurally under-covers the ~49 HS-only detectors (a startup/scan warning, a coverage-FAIL line, or a bounded loud degrade for exactly that detector subset) instead of the current SILENT miss on the normal success path. "Mark them always-active" does NOT work — they are HS-triggered, and forcing them unconditional would run them on every chunk (perf).** Verify any fix by re-running the `--backend gpu` vs `--backend simd` CredData/data differential to convergence. OR the product-demote of generic/entropy shrinks (but does NOT eliminate — the vendor HS-only detectors twilio-auth-token/datadog-api-key remain) this gap. This is the highest-value M-02 installment surfaced this session: a Law-10 GPU silent-recall-loss found only by real-hardware dogfooding, root-caused end-to-end (phase-1 trigger `backend_triggered.rs:249-293` lacks the HS union `collect_triggered_patterns_simd` has → phase-2 `scan_coalesced.rs:356` consumes it → HS-only detectors never extracted on GPU). **MAJOR REFRAME (2026-07-06): the fix mechanism ALREADY EXISTS — `gpu_recall_floor` (`gpu_region_dispatch.rs:275` `gpu_recall_floor_enabled()`; `[tuning].gpu_recall_floor`, docs/reference/configuration.md:288 "forces the GPU region-presence path to compute the full CPU prefilter"; :384 reports "(chunk,detector) pair(s) recovered by gpu_recall_floor coverage"). It DEFAULTS TO FALSE (configuration.md:278), so the default `--backend gpu` under-covers the HS-only detectors BY DESIGN — my differential ran with the default (floor off) → the ~1900 gap. So this is NOT an unknown bug but a KNOWN config-gated perf/recall tradeoff DEFAULTING to reduced recall, documented in the config reference but NOT loudly surfaced at scan time. That makes it a Law-10-BY-DEFAULT concern (silent-by-default recall reduction on a security tool's GPU path) with a TRACTABLE fix — NOT deep WGSL: (a) default `gpu_recall_floor = true` (correctness-first: accept the CPU-prefilter cost so `--backend gpu` never silently under-scans; autoroute can still pick the fast path where it's proven safe), or (b) keep the default off but LOUDLY warn at scan time when the GPU path runs floor-off (coverage line: "N HS-only detectors reduced-coverage; set gpu_recall_floor=true or use --backend simd for full recall"). CONFIRMATION: CODE-confirmed (gpu_region_dispatch.rs:275-287 — `full_recall_floor = gpu_recall_floor_enabled()`; floor-on → `cpu_triggers = Some(compute_coalesced_triggers(chunks, simd_prefilter))` = the full CPU/SIMD HS trigger set → recovers the HS-only detectors; floor-off → `cpu_triggers = None` → GPU-presence only → the gap). EMPIRICAL floor-on confirmation was INCONCLUSIVE (honest): my attempt used `--config <path>`, but that flag is HERMETIC ("ignore ambient .keyhog.toml, run shipped defaults") — it does NOT load a file — so the run used the shipped floor-OFF default (23466 ≈ the 23463 floor-off, not simd's 25798). The floor is set via ambient `.keyhog.toml` walk-up (`[tuning].gpu_recall_floor = true`, cli/config/schema.rs:241 → sections.rs:269 → tuning.rs:141), which I did not place to avoid polluting the tracked CredData corpus. To empirically confirm: scan a temp copy with a parent `.keyhog.toml` floor-on, expect convergence toward the simd count. Code path is unambiguous regardless. NOTE: this only bites `--backend gpu`/`gpu-region-presence` (opt-in); the default autoroute/CPU path is unaffected — bounding the blast radius to opt-in GPU users. Vendor-detector parity is SOLID under real load (only single-digit vendor deltas). LANDABLE test-depth gap regardless: `backend_parity_matrix`'s 6 fixtures are all CLEAR-CUT vendor creds (AKIA/ghp_/sk_live_) — NONE is confidence-borderline, which is exactly why the unit matrix passes while real-workload parity fails. Add a confidence-BORDERLINE generic/entropy fixture (ideally one of the concrete divergent CredData cases) so CI catches this class. Fix is deep (GPU-vs-CPU float/trigger parity) OR the generic/entropy product-demote (which fixes F1 + perf + THIS in one move). **SHIPPED 2026-07-06 — option (b), the tractable Law-10 loud surface, IMPLEMENTED + DOGFOOD-VERIFIED on the 5090.** **CRITICAL EMPIRICAL CORRECTION (real ambient `.keyhog.toml` floor-on scan of a 3054-file CredData subset): `gpu_recall_floor` does NOT recover the anchorless gap — floor-on total == floor-off total == 4435 findings (entropy-api-key 877 vs 879), both ≪ simd 5155. The floor's recovery loop (`gpu_region_dispatch.rs:386` `if det >= ac_map.len() {continue}`) only recovers AC-LITERAL region under-fires (vyre literal-set bugs, `det < ac_map.len()`), NEVER the anchorless phase-2 set. So the earlier "floor-on → compute_coalesced_triggers recovers the HS-only detectors" framing in this row (the MAJOR REFRAME above) is REFUTED: the anchorless GPU↔SIMD divergence is FLOOR-INDEPENDENT and the ONLY remediation is `--backend simd`. This is itself a Law-10 lesson — the fix's FIRST cut wrongly advised "set gpu_recall_floor=true" and wrongly gated the warning on floor-off (which would have silently under-scanned floor-ON `--backend gpu` users); the empirical caught it before it was trusted.** The corrected fix: a once-per-process `note_gpu_region_presence_anchorless_gap_once()` (`OnceLock` + `eprintln!`, modeled on `deny_silent_gpu_degrade_with_reason` — deliberately NOT `record_gpu_degrade`, which increments `gpu_degrade_count`; this is not a dispatch FAILURE). On EVERY region-presence dispatch (regardless of floor) it LOUDLY prints once: `keyhog: --backend gpu (region-presence) DIVERGES from --backend simd on anchorless detectors … Use --backend simd for complete anchorless-detector recall. NOTE: [tuning].gpu_recall_floor does NOT recover this (it only recovers AC-literal region under-fires).` DOGFOOD CONFIRMED: fires EXACTLY ONCE on stderr on BOTH floor-off AND floor-on `--backend gpu` scans, and NOT on `--backend simd`. NO clean per-scanner predicate exists (the phase-2 structures are homoglyph-polluted: `phase2_patterns` is never empty because every detector gets a homoglyph variant; `phase2_always_active_indices` counts any phase-2 pattern with no >=4-char keyword — including those per-detector homoglyphs — so even a 1-detector AKIA scanner trips both), so the notice fires UNCONDITIONALLY on the region-presence path (an opt-in `--backend gpu` path that always diverges for the real detector set). TEST: `tests/gap/gpu_region_presence_anchorless_gap_surfaced.rs` (source-string contract, NOT gpu-gated so it also guards the ci-lean lane) locks the Law-10 surface — the notice must exist, state the `--backend simd` remedy, DISCLAIM the floor, be process-once guarded, and be invoked UNCONDITIONALLY (asserts the removed floor-gated forms `gpu_region_presence_undercovers_anchorless`/`note_gpu_recall_floor_off_once` are gone). ci-lean + gpu builds green; existing `gpu_region_cpu_floor_is_explicit_not_default` contract still passes. GPU build green. Does NOT change the default (no perf regression, no default flip — the `gpu_region_cpu_floor_is_explicit_not_default` contract still holds); it makes the silent GPU-vs-SIMD anchorless divergence LOUD at scan time, satisfying Law-10. The DEEP fix (option a: on-GPU no-literal regex triggers) and the product-demote remain the recall-RECOVERING options; this ships the honest surface now. Verified: ci-lean (default CI) + gpu builds both green, existing source-string contracts (`gpu_region_cpu_floor_is_explicit_not_default`, `gpu_region_dispatch_uses_one_coalesced_region_presence_batch`) still pass. COMPLEMENTS µ-tun-14: that item is the floor-*ON* recovery-report assertion (the `:384` "(chunk,detector) pair(s) recovered" loud report when floor is enabled); THIS is the floor-*OFF* reduced-recall warning — the two together make BOTH floor states loud (on = report what was recovered, off = warn what is reduced). µ-tun-14's floor-ON recovery-report test remains open. **LANDABLE TEST-DEPTH GAP CLOSED (vendor case) + EMPIRICAL REFINEMENT OF THE HYPOTHESIS (2026-07-09, RTX 5090, default `--features gpu`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=…/santh-archive/cargo-target`): added a 7th fixture `hs_only_twilio_companion` to `backend_parity_matrix::build_fixtures()` — a `twilio-auth-token` companion pair (`AC`+32-hex account_sid + 32-hex auth token), the CONFIDENCE-CLEAR-CUT vendor member of the ~49 no-literal/HS-only class. `backend_parity_matrix_all_fixtures_all_backends` + the determinism test now run 28 cells (7 fixtures × 4 backends incl `Gpu`/`MegaScan`) and BOTH PASS on the 5090 (`cells=28 failed=0`, RC=0). So GPU/MegaScan surface `twilio-auth-token` BYTE-IDENTICALLY to the SimdCpu reference. **This REFUTES this row's earlier hypothesis that "any twilio/datadog/etc. secret in a no-literal position is SILENTLY MISSED on --backend gpu"** — it is NOT: the anchorless VENDOR detectors are covered on GPU because the phase-2 confirm pass (`scan_phase2_patterns`, seeded from `phase2_always_active_indices` for keyword-less detectors) runs backend-INDEPENDENTLY after trigger collection — it does not depend on the phase-1 HS-trigger union that the GPU path lacks. So the genuine GPU↔SIMD divergence is NARROWER than the row feared: it is confined to the BORDERLINE-CONFIDENCE entropy family (entropy-api-key/generic-secret/entropy-token), whose near-threshold candidate ADMISSION differs — NOT the clear-cut vendor HS-only detectors. The twilio fixture LOCKS the vendor-HS-only parity where the matrix actually runs: the GPU nightly job (`runners-nightly.yml`, `--features gpu`, real-GPU host → all 28 cells green as measured; on the no-GPU hosted runner the forced-`Gpu` cells fail-CLOSED with exit 12 by design — "you requested `--backend gpu` on a no-GPU binary" — and are absorbed by that job's `continue-on-error`). It is NOT in the ci-lean `all_tests` aggregate, so the ci-lean CI job never runs it (a bare local `--features ci-lean` run of it exits 12 on the first forced-`Gpu` cell, which is the intended fail-closed behavior, NOT a regression — the matrix is a gpu-build test). So the fixture is CI-safe: it exercises + locks the vendor-HS-only GPU parity exactly on the real-GPU nightly job and is inert to the ci-lean lane. Verified alongside a full parity re-run on the 5090 (2026-07-09): `backend_parity_matrix` 2✓, `detector_corpus_backend_parity` 1✓, `gpu_parity` 2✓, `ml_forward_parity` 3✓ (43-feature FC), `ml_model_arch_wgsl_parity` 4✓ (INPUT_DIM=43 + WORKGROUP_SIZE WGSL), `regression_backend_trigger_parity` 14✓, `regression_gpu_region_presence_batch_parity` 2✓ (bug-6314 region batch borrow/scratch byte-identity) — the whole GPU/ML parity surface GREEN on real hardware. REMAINING deep M-02 work unchanged: the entropy-family borderline-admission divergence (option a on-GPU regex triggers OR the generic/entropy product-demote); the loud anchorless notice already ships for it. **MECHANISM FINDING 2026-07-09 (code-read, informs the deep fix): `entropy-api-key`/`entropy-token`/`entropy-generic`/`entropy-password` are NOT TOML detectors — they are synthetic labels emitted by `scan_entropy_fallback` (`engine/phase2_entropy.rs:18` + `phase2_entropy/helpers.rs`), whose CANDIDATE DISCOVERY (whole-chunk Shannon sweep over `preprocessed.text.lines()`) is BACKEND-INDEPENDENT. But its OUTPUT is coupled to backend state via the dedup guard (phase2_entropy.rs:69-75): it builds `skip_lines` from `scan_state.matches` and skips entropy emission on any line already claimed by a NON-generic/entropy named detector. `scan_state.matches` at that point includes the CONFIRMED pass, which IS backend-dependent (the GPU trigger bitmap = AC ∪ GPU-literal-presence lacks the SIMD HS-regex union), so the two backends claim different lines → the entropy-fallback dedup-skips differently → entropy-label finding sets diverge even though the entropy sweep itself is identical. So the entropy divergence is a SECOND-ORDER effect of the phase-1 trigger gap (via the dedup coupling), not a float/threshold issue. Classification confirmed: `phase2_always_active_indices` (gpu_artifacts.rs:98) = phase-2 patterns with NO ≥4-char keyword → run every chunk regardless of backend (this is why companion-based twilio-auth-token, keyword-less, is covered on GPU); keyword-bearing patterns (generic-secret, entropy keywords) are keyword-gated. Empirical GPU-INDEPENDENT half now MEASURED (2026-07-10, debug binary, 627-file CredData slice incl. 72 files >32 KiB up to 1.2 MB): `--backend simd` FUSED (per-chunk) vs `--backend simd --batch-pipeline` (coalesced batch producer — `should_use_fused_pipeline`→false forces the non-fused path, fused.rs:38, VERIFIED non-vacuous) produce BYTE-IDENTICAL finding sets (713≡713, ZERO symmetric difference by detector×location×credential_hash). So the SHARED coalesced admission gate (`scan_coalesced_phase2_with_admission` scan_coalesced.rs:383-403 → `should_scan_no_hit_chunk`/`no_hit_text_admits`'s 32 KiB `small_chunk` gate + entropy prefilter) is PARITY-CLEAN — it drops/adds nothing vs the per-chunk entropy sweep, EXONERATING it as a divergence source. This DATA-REFUTES an in-flight hypothesis (that the coalesced size/entropy admission gate caused the entropy gap) before it was written as fact — the divergence is confined to the GPU-SPECIFIC phase-1 trigger gap (GPU = AC ∪ GPU-literal-presence, lacking the SIMD HS-regex union), confirming this row's framing; the coalesced admission path is identical on both backends and cannot be the cause. Existing parity locks already cover the SIMD coalesced≡per-chunk contract (`lane6_full_corpus_property_invariants::coalesced_equals_per_chunk_for_single_chunk` 10k cases, `scanner_invariants_full_corpus_proptest` ⊇, `phase2_no_hit_branch_recall`), but all use SMALL proptest inputs (≤3072 B) — the >32 KiB `small_chunk`-gate BOUNDARY was uncovered. NOW LOCKED: new ci-lean boundary test `regression_coalesced_large_chunk_entropy_parity` (a 40 KiB no-trigger chunk carrying one anchorless entropy token, runtime-generated so it's self-scan-safe) asserts per-chunk `scan` ≡ `scan_coalesced` finding-for-finding AND non-vacuously that the token IS found — EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED both pipelines surface the same `entropy-token` (the coalesced admission gate admits the large chunk via chunk-windowing / the always-active phase-2 pre-check BEFORE the 32 KiB size gate, so the gate is never authoritative for the whole chunk). So the coalesced admission path is parity-clean at the boundary too. **GPU-vs-SIMD magnitude NOW MEASURED (2026-07-10, existing debug `--features gpu` binary — the divergence warning fired, confirming the region-presence path engaged, `--min-confidence 0`, SAME 627-file slice): SIMD per-chunk 713 vs GPU 619 (net GPU −94); by set-diff (detector×location×hash): SIMD-only=388 (of which entropy-api-key 387 + generic-secret 1), GPU-only=294 (entropy-api-key 293 + generic-secret 1) — 100% of the ~682 divergent findings are the ANCHORLESS ENTROPY FAMILY, ZERO vendor-detector divergence (confirms the twilio-fixture refutation: literal-anchored + always-active-phase-2 detectors are byte-identical on both backends).** The divergence is ASYMMETRIC/CHURN (each backend both misses AND finds entropy-api-key the other doesn't), which is the fingerprint of the SECOND-ORDER dedup coupling (phase2_entropy.rs:69-75: the backend-dependent confirmed-pass trigger set claims different lines → the backend-independent entropy sweep dedup-skips differently), NOT a clean one-directional under-fire. CRUCIAL PRECISION CONNECTION: entropy-api-key is the P≈0.06 FP flood ([[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]], "API 1335 FP/1 TP"), so the ~682 divergent findings are ~94% FALSE POSITIVES — the GPU↔SIMD divergence is entropy-NOISE churn on both sides, not lost real secrets. So M-02's entropy divergence is a SYMPTOM of the entropy FP flood: the real remediation CONVERGES with DET-2 (the entropy-precision retrain/demote) — shrinking the borderline entropy candidate pool shrinks the dedup-coupling divergence at the same time. A backend-trigger "fix" that made GPU admit SIMD's extra 388 would ADD ~364 FP to recover ~23 TP (precision-negative, the wrong direction), so the loud `--backend simd` notice + the entropy-precision arc remain the correct resolution; on-GPU regex triggers only matter once the entropy family is precision-fixed. **OPERATOR-VISIBLE IMPACT NOW QUANTIFIED (2026-07-10, SAME slice+binary at the DEFAULT confidence floor, i.e. what a real user sees — NOT `--min-confidence 0`): SIMD 230 vs GPU 230 (identical count), divergence = just 9 SIMD-only + 9 GPU-only, ALL `entropy-api-key`, SYMMETRIC — 18 of 230 findings (~8%) churn, and at P≈0.06 that's ~17 FP / ~1 TP. So the floor-0 682-finding divergence collapses to ~18 sub-floor-adjacent entropy-FP churn at the operator floor: M-02's REAL user impact is tiny and is almost entirely false-positive noise the confidence floor already suppresses. CONCLUSION: M-02 is LOW-priority as a recall bug (no meaningful real-secret loss GPU-vs-SIMD; the loud `--backend simd` notice already covers the honest surface) — its remaining value is entirely SUBSUMED by DET-2 (fix entropy precision → the borderline candidate pool shrinks → both the FP flood AND this backend churn shrink together). Do NOT spend the deep on-GPU-regex-trigger effort on M-02; spend it on the entropy-precision retrain.** **GPU DECODE/ENTROPY PARITY RE-VERIFIED GREEN on RTX 5090 (2026-07-09, post-foreign-refactor): after the heavy in-flight foreign refactors to decode/* + entropy/* + checksum/*, re-ran the GPU parity suites that exercise those paths through all backends — `backend_parity_chunk_boundary_all_backends` (2, 292s), `backend_parity_coalesced_vs_individual` (2, 291s), `backend_parity_entropy_fallback` (2), `regression_cpu_decoder_parity` (5), `regression_coalesced_reassembly_parity` (1), `decode_backend_matrix` (18) = 30 tests, ALL PASS (RC=0). So the foreign decode/entropy/checksum churn did NOT regress GPU↔CPU decode/entropy parity on real hardware (Law 8 satisfied — the 5090 genuinely engaged, not skipped).
- **M-03 — 10k+ hand-written test cases across the codebase.** Every subsystem
  section below ships entire suites. This is the running total target; it is met
  by completing the per-module suites in §1–§9, not by one mega-file.
- **M-04 — Full 6-machine dogfood matrix as CI-encoded verification.** Each
  subcommand × each output format × each source × each backend, installed from the
  SHIPPED artifact and PTY-driven, on every MACHINE-MATRIX host. Installments per
  surface; each lands a recorded transcript + an asserting e2e test.
- **M-05 — Multi-root scanning** (`keyhog scan a/ b/ c/`): accept multiple
  positional paths, dedup overlap, merge coverage-gap counters, stable ordering.
  Suite: overlapping roots, symlink cross-root, per-root gitignore, exit-code
  aggregation. (Task system #50.)
- **M-06 — Daemon: fixed-location socket reachable by scans + cold-start progress.**
  (Task system #47.) Installments: socket path resolution + permissions; cold-start
  progress line; reconnect/stale-socket handling; PTY e2e proving a daemon-served
  scan streams progress and findings and exits clean.
- **M-07 — Publish runway**: version bump + pinned-dep bump (vyre/simdsieve) +
  release gates green on all platforms incl. macOS-arm64. (Task system #29/#48.)
- **M-08 — Real-world CredData recall**: keyhog is precision/recall-bound on real
  CredData. **CORRECTED baseline (2026-07-06, ci-lean, corpus-fix + bearer + decode-guard): F1 0.3690, P 0.391, R 0.350, tp 5280, fp 8237** (the old "F1 ~0.15" was a scan-CRASH artifact — long fixed; ALWAYS bench CredData not just mirror where recall is ~0.92). keyhog ranks 3rd on the corrected 6-scanner leaderboard (betterleaks 0.596 » titus 0.367 » keyhog 0.369 » noseyparker 0.357). Installments: per-family recall audit vs DIFFERENTIAL-PEERS; surface the gap each family loses; raise recall without regressing precision. SHIPPED this arc: corpus-slicer measurement fix (+21.6% F1), bearer-authorization detector (recall), decode generic/entropy anchor guard (precision). CHARACTERIZED-as-gated (not heuristic-landable): (a) the generic/entropy pool is net-negative on F1 (0.369 vs 0.444 w/o) AND the perf floor [[generic-entropy-detectors-are-net-negative-on-f1]] — no confidence threshold recovers it, so it needs the RETRAIN or a product demote-decision; (b) the biggest catchable recall gap (Key 3285, betterleaks-diff) is the DELIBERATELY-DECLINED bare-`Key = <hex>` lane (overfit one fixture + hash-digest FP + `key`-substring perf + mirror-can't-gate) — catching it = Law-9/generalization evasion. This is open-ended — do it family by family, forever, never declare "done"; the residual needs the model-authoritative surfacing (retrain), not more heuristics.

---

# §1 — SCANNER: backend routing & scan dispatch
Grounded in `crates/scanner/src/engine/compiled_api.rs` and
`crates/scanner/src/hw_probe/select.rs` (read in full).

- **SR-001 — Fail-closed backend-resolution matrix.** New suite
  `crates/scanner/tests/backend_failclosed_matrix.rs` (≥160 cases). For the full
  cross-product of {each label in `select::BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES` (10 values)} ×
  {simd prefilter present/absent} × {gpu stack present/absent} × {`gpu_required_by_policy`
  on/off}, assert the EXACT outcome of `resolve_backend_for_scan` +
  `deny_silent_selected_backend_degrade` (compiled_api.rs:72-101): explicit
  `simd-regex` with no live prefilter → `process_exit::backend_unavailable` with
  the exact stderr text and nonzero exit; explicit GPU under required policy with
  no stack → `require_gpu_unmet` with the `gpu_literals/gpu_backend/gpu_matcher`
  detail string; auto-selected GPU without stack → relabel to `live_cpu_backend()`.
  Never a silent degrade. Dogfood: SHIPPED portable binary (`--features portable`,
  no simd) `--backend simd` on linux-x86_64 + macOS-arm64 → assert fail-closed.
- **SR-002 — `warn_*_auto_degrade` single-fire semantics.** The `SIMD_AUTO_DEGRADE_WARNED`
  / `GPU_AUTO_DEGRADE_WARNED` OnceLocks (compiled_api.rs:4-5,40-70) must emit the
  operator stderr line EXACTLY once per process and the `tracing::warn!` on EVERY
  occurrence. Suite asserting: first call prints to stderr + logs; second call only
  logs; the printed text matches `live_cpu_backend().label()` and the `context`.
  Concurrency case: 64 threads racing the first degrade → exactly one stderr line.
- **SR-003 — `parse_backend_str` ↔ `BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES` ↔ clap coherence.**
  Suite asserting every advertised clap value (incl. the no-hyphen `megascan`,
  `gpu-zero-copy` legacy alias, `literal-set`, `regex-nfa`, `rule-pipeline`,
  `hyperscan`, `scalar`) maps to the right `ScanBackend`; every value in
  `BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES` is parseable; no value parses to `None`; case/whitespace
  insensitivity holds. Regression-pins the historical `megascan`→`None` silent
  fall-through-to-auto bug named in the source comment (select.rs:450-456).
- **SR-004 — `gpu_could_engage` tier-threshold truth table.** Pure-function suite
  (host-independent, per the select_backend host-dependency memo): for each GPU
  tier from `classify_gpu_tier`, assert the exact `workload_bytes >= solo ||
  (workload_bytes >= min && pattern_count >= pattern_floor)` boundary (select.rs:395-404)
  at solo-1/solo/solo+1, min-1/min/min+1 × pattern_floor-1/floor/floor+1, plus
  software-renderer and `!gpu_available` → false. ≥120 boundary cases.
- **SR-005 — Batch dominance guard (`select_backend_for_batch`).** Suite encoding
  the Linux-kernel swarm scenario from the select.rs:308-354 doc: 94k tiny files
  summing past the GPU floor with only 55 ≥2 MiB files must NOT route GPU
  (`gpu_dominates_dispatch_cost` false → `GpuBatchNotDominant`); a batch that IS
  mostly big-file data must route GPU. Assert `BackendRoutingVerdict.reason` +
  `reason_detail()` string for every `BackendRoutingReason` variant (7 variants).
- **SR-006 — `BackendRoutingVerdict.reason_detail()` exhaustive string contract.**
  One assertion per `BackendRoutingReason` variant on the EXACT formatted string,
  including the `GpuBatchNotDominant` `large_chunk_bytes == None` branch
  (select.rs:160-170) and the threshold-numbers interpolation. These strings reach
  `keyhog backend --autoroute --show`; pin them so the operator-facing reason text
  cannot drift.
- **SR-007 — Decode-through prefilter-bypass recall (compiled_api.rs:457-522).**
  The crux Law-10 path: a FULLY-ENCODED secret carries none of any detector's
