  literal bytes/bigrams, so `AlphabetScreen::screen` + `bigram_bloom.maybe_overlaps`
  reject it, and only the `should_scan_no_hit_chunk` / `chunk_needs_decode_postprocess`
  fall-through recovers it. Suite (≥80 cases across INPUT-MATRIX c+h): for each
  encoding (base64, base64url, percent, gzip-in-archive, k8s `data:`), plant a real
  detectable secret that survives ONLY through decode-through, assert it is found;
  plant a non-secret encoded blob and assert `record_file_skipped()` fires and no
  finding; assert the `warn_simd_auto_degrade("decode-shaped no-hit chunk…")` path
  on a portable build. Differential: same inputs on `--backend cpu-fallback` vs
  `simd` produce identical finding sets.
- **SR-008 — Windowed vs inner dispatch seam (`MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES`,
  compiled_api.rs:533-537).** Suite planting secrets straddling the
  `scan_windowed`↔`scan_inner` boundary at len = cap-1/cap/cap+1 and at every
  window seam, across UTF-8 and UTF-16; assert no secret is split or lost and the
  reassembly preserves exact offsets. Pairs with the UTF-16 mmap recall fix (#26).
- **SR-009 — `detector_digest()` stability + sensitivity (compiled_api.rs:298-310).**
  Suite: digest is byte-identical across two compiles of the same detector set and
  across process restarts (load order independence); digest CHANGES when any
  pattern source changes; digest domain-separation tags (`b"domain"`, `b"regex"`,
  `b"pattern_count"`) prevent collision between a pattern-count change and a regex
  change. This digest keys M-01's embedded-blob invalidation, so its contract is
  load-bearing.
- **SR-010 — `warm()` covers every lazy regex (compiled_api.rs:229-251).** Suite
  proving `warm()` first-touches ac_map, phase2_patterns, shared_regexes,
  phase2_generic, multiline, AND checksum runtime regexes (the 6 warm calls), and
  that a post-`warm()` scan compiles zero new regexes (instrument the `OnceLock`
  hit/miss). Perf: criterion showing first-batch latency after `warm()` is within
  noise of steady-state on a daemon-sized scan, on all MACHINE-MATRIX hosts.
- **SR-011 — `select_backend_for_file` GPU-required-policy abort (compiled_api.rs:314-354).**
  Suite: under `gpu_required_by_policy`, an auto-selected GPU/MegaScan with no live
  stack must `require_gpu_unmet`-exit (not degrade); without the policy it must
  relabel to `live_cpu_backend()` and fire `warn_gpu_auto_degrade` once; a
  host-capability SimdCpu pick with no built prefilter must auto-route to
  `CpuFallback` (the `[a-z]{16}`-only detector-set case named at :341-352), NOT
  fatal-abort (that abort is reserved for explicit `--backend simd-regex`).
- **SR-012 — `warm_backend` GPU/MegaScan unification (compiled_api.rs:386-403).**
  Suite asserting `Gpu` and `MegaScan` warm to the same `gpu_stack_usable()` result
  (they are the same live engine now), `SimdCpu`→`simd_backend_usable()`,
  `CpuFallback`→always true; and that a not-ready GPU warm triggers
  `gpu_forced::deny_silent_gpu_degrade` (fail-closed), never returns a quiet false.
- **SR-013 — `detector_pattern_weak_anchor` index-parity (compiled_api.rs:131-142).**
  Suite asserting the `WeakAnchorBase::{Always,Never,PerPattern}` decision indexed
  by `entry.detector_index` matches the per-pattern `has_broad_identifier_capture()`
  memo for every compiled pattern; an index-parallel construction bug must make a
  test loud (build a scanner, walk all patterns, assert no panic + correct anchor).
- **SR-014 — `runtime_status()` / `CompiledScannerRuntime` reporting contract.**
  Suite asserting `detector_count`, `pattern_count`, `detector_digest`,
  `preferred_backend`, `gpu_backend`, `gpu_degrade_count` all reflect the real
  compiled scanner and feed the startup banner + autoroute cache invalidation
  correctly; `gpu_backend_label()` distinguishes "cuda" vs "wgpu" (compiled_api.rs:356-366).

---

# §2 — SCANNER: structured preprocessor & decode-through
Grounded in `crates/scanner/src/structured/mod.rs`,
`crates/scanner/src/structured/parsers/` and `crates/scanner/src/telemetry.rs`.

- **ST-001 — Finish & land the 2MB decode-through cap surfacing (in progress).**
  `preprocess()` now records `record_structured_oversize_skip()` + warns when an
  oversized k8s/compose/tfstate/ipynb file is skipped (mod.rs), with a new
  `STRUCTURED_OVERSIZE_SKIPS` counter + reporter line + run.rs gap-sum wiring.
  Remaining: write the differential suite
  `crates/scanner/tests/regression_structured_oversize_skip_counted.rs` (own
  binary for global-counter isolation, mirroring
  `regression_structured_parse_failure_counted.rs`): a >2MB k8s Secret whose only
  secret is inside a base64 `data:` value → assert oversize count == 1 AND the
  decoded secret is NOT found via structured decode (recall gap is real); the same
  shape small → secret IS found, count unchanged; an oversize `.env` (not
  decode-through) → count stays 0 (env extracts plain values the regular scan
  sees); a decode-derived oversize buffer → not counted (no false-loud). Then
  build `-p keyhog-scanner --no-default-features --features ci-lean` + commit.
  (Task system #52.)
- **ST-002 — `detect_format` sniffing truth table (mod.rs).** New suite over the
  refactored `detect_format()` asserting EXACT `StructuredFormat` for every
  path/content combination: `.env`/`*.env` → Env; `*.yaml`/`*.yml` + `kind: Secret`
  → K8sSecret; `docker-compose`/`compose` + yaml → DockerCompose; `.tfstate` →
  Tfstate; `.tf`/`.tfvars`/`.hcl` → Hcl; `.ipynb` → Jupyter; and the negative
  twins (a `*.yaml` WITHOUT `kind: Secret` → None; a `compose.txt` → None). Pin
  the precedence order (env before k8s before compose) and the case-insensitive
  byte compares (`ends_ci`, `file_contains_ci`). ≥90 cases.
- **ST-003 — `uses_decode_through` recall-vs-context contract (mod.rs).** Suite
  proving the four decode-through formats (K8sSecret, DockerCompose, Tfstate,
  Jupyter) base64-decode their values and the regular scan does NOT recover them
  (so skipping them loses a secret), while Env/Hcl extract only plain scalars the
  regular scan still finds (so skipping them is lossless). This contract is what
  keeps the oversize counter honest (Law 10 — no false-loud on Env/Hcl).
- **ST-004 — Per-parser exhaustive suite: `parse_k8s_secret` (parsers/yaml.rs).**
  Entire suite (≥120 cases): every base64 `data:` value decoded and scanned;
  `stringData:` plain values; multi-doc YAML (`---` separators); the depth-0 gate
  that prevents JWT-decode false coverage-gap alarms (the
  `decode_derived_gate::depth0_extracts_decoded_jwt` path); malformed YAML →
  `report_yaml_parse_failure` counts + warns; nested `data:` maps; non-Secret kinds
  with a `data:` key. Assert decoded credential value + line mapping back to the
  original document. INPUT-MATRIX a,c,e,g,h.
- **ST-005 — Per-parser exhaustive suite: `parse_docker_compose` (parsers/yaml.rs).**
  Entire suite: `environment:` list form (`- KEY=val`) and map form (`KEY: val`);
  `secrets:`/`x-` extension blocks; `env_file:` references; quoted/unquoted values;
  interpolation `${VAR:-default}`; malformed → counted parse failure. Assert the
  keyword context (`KEY`) sits adjacent to the value so named detectors fire.
- **ST-006 — Per-parser exhaustive suite: `parse_tfstate` (parsers/json.rs).** Entire
  suite: `resources[].instances[].attributes` walk; sensitive value extraction;
  the `MAX_TFSTATE_DEPTH = 256` DoS bound (assert a 256-deep degenerate state is
  bounded, a 255-deep real secret is found); base64-encoded attribute values
  decoded; malformed JSON → counted. INPUT-MATRIX a,c,d,h.
- **ST-007 — Per-parser exhaustive suite: `parse_hcl` (parsers/hcl.rs).** Entire
  suite: `variable "x" { default = "<secret>" }` extraction so the keyword `x`
  becomes adjacent context; the `MAX_VARIABLE_BLOCK_LINES = 16` and
  `MAX_HEREDOC_LINES = 512` caps (context-only — assert the plain value is STILL
  found by the regular scan past the cap, and that NO coverage-gap counter fires,
  proving these stay silent per the §52 distinction); heredoc `<<EOF` bodies;
  nested blocks; `.tfvars` `key = "val"` form. ≥80 cases.
- **ST-008 — Per-parser exhaustive suite: `parse_jupyter` (parsers/ipynb.rs).** Entire
  suite: `cells[].source` as string AND as array-of-lines; the mixed-type source
  array that loses one decode-through fragment and is counted; `outputs[]` text;
  base64 image-data is NOT mis-scanned as a secret; malformed notebook JSON →
  counted. INPUT-MATRIX a,c,g,h.
- **ST-009 — `build_preprocessed_text` line-mapping invariant (mod.rs, multiline
  feature).** Suite asserting every synthesized `(context, value, line)` triple
  maps the finding back to the ORIGINAL file line, not the appended synthetic line;
  property test (10k) that for any extracted pair set the reported line is within
  the original document and round-trips. Pairs with the
  `LINE_OFFSET_MAPPING_MISMATCHES` telemetry counter.
- **ST-010 — Telemetry coverage-gap counter family completeness (telemetry.rs).**
  Suite asserting each `ScannerCoverageGapEvent` variant
  ({StructuredParseFailure, StructuredOversizeSkip, DecodeTruncation,
  InvalidPatternIndexSkip, BoundaryResultCardinalityMismatch,
  LineOffsetMappingMismatch}) increments its own counter, is summed into the
  not-clean determination in `orchestrator/run.rs`, and is surfaced by a distinct
  `report_skip_summary` WARN line with the right wording. Regression: a scan with
  ONLY one gap type set must report not-clean and print exactly that one line.

---

# §3 — SCANNER: checksum validation
Grounded in `crates/scanner/src/checksum/` (mod.rs read in full; submodules
github/gitlab/npm/slack/stripe).

- **CK-001 — `validate_checksum` first-claim-wins ordering (mod.rs:44-68).** Suite
  asserting the `VALIDATORS` vector order is deterministic and that the FIRST
  validator to return a non-`NotApplicable` verdict wins; construct a token that
  two validators could both claim and pin which one does (ordering is a real
  contract — GithubClassic before FineGrained before npm/slack/pypi/stripe/gitlab).
- **CK-002 — `ChecksumResult::Invalid` → match DROPPED (mod.rs:118-146).** Suite
  driving `checksum_adjusted_confidence`: `Invalid` → `None` (caller drops);
  `Valid` → confidence floored at `CHECKSUM_VALID_FLOOR = 0.9`; `StructurallyValid`
  and `NotApplicable` → pass-through unchanged. Cross-check the floor stays above
  `ScannerConfig::HIGH_PRECISION_MIN_CONFIDENCE` (a property test that fails if a
  future edit lowers the floor below the precision bar). This is the
  fabricated-token guard the memory note `checksum-wiring-invalidates-fabricated-token-fixtures`
  warns about — encode it so any test fixture with a bad checksum is provably dropped.
- **CK-003 — `extension_downgrades_checksum` boundary-revert (mod.rs:80-83).** Suite:
  a checksum-`Valid` token that, extended by one adjacent byte, becomes non-`Valid`
  → assert the boundary extender in `engine::scan_filters` reverts to the original
  so the canonical token still surfaces with its valid checksum; a token that stays
  valid when extended → no revert; a token invalid both ways → no spurious revert.
- **CK-004 — GitHub CRC32 validator deep suite (checksum/github.rs).** Entire suite
  for `GithubClassicPatValidator` + `GithubFineGrainedPatValidator`: real `ghp_`/
  `github_pat_` shapes with VALID `crc32_base62_suffix` (use `standard_crc32` +
  `base62_encode_u32` to construct), one-bit-flipped suffix → `Invalid`, wrong
  length → `NotApplicable`, every `gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_` prefix variant. Assert
  `base62_encode_u32(value, width)` width-padding is exact. ≥60 cases. (Never paste
  a real-shaped token in a comment — the self-scan would flag it; construct in code.)
- **CK-005 — gitlab/npm/pypi/slack/stripe validator suites** (one file each):
  per family, construct valid + corrupted-checksum + wrong-family tokens and assert
  the exact `ChecksumResult`; assert `NotApplicable` for a foreign family so the
  first-claim-wins ordering is never short-circuited by a greedy validator.
- **CK-006 — `warm_runtime_regexes` chain (mod.rs:148-150 → slack).** Suite proving
  `checksum::warm_runtime_regexes()` (called from `CompiledScanner::warm`)
  first-touches the slack runtime regex so no checksum regex compiles inside the
  first hot scan batch; pairs with SR-010.
- **CK-007 — Checksum on the fast/hot-pattern path (Law-10 parity).** Per the memory
  note `keyhog-hot-pattern-path-bypasses-process-match`: write a differential suite
  proving a checksum-`Invalid` token planted on the HOT-pattern fast path is dropped
  identically to the slow path; a checksum-`Valid` token is floored identically.
  Any divergence is a finding (the fast path must replicate per-match checksum policy).

---

# §4 — SCANNER: confidence policy & ML scoring
Grounded in `crates/scanner/src/confidence/policy.rs` (read in full),
`confidence/penalties.rs`, `ml_scorer.rs`, `ml_scorer/ml_weights.rs`.

- **CF-001 — `compute_confidence` normalized-weighted-sum truth table.** Entire
  suite over `ConfidenceSignals` (has_literal_prefix, has_context_anchor, entropy,
  keyword_nearby, sensitive_file, match_length, has_companion): assert the exact
  normalized score for every single-signal-only input and representative
  combinations; pin that an anchor-ONLY named detector lands below 0.40 BEFORE the
  named-detector floor lift (the structural under-credit the doc at policy.rs:122-154
  describes), proving CF-002's lift is load-bearing. Property test (10k): score is
  monotonic non-decreasing in each positive signal.
- **CF-002 — `apply_named_detector_anchor_floor` lift (policy.rs:155-165).** Suite:
  `is_named_detector && has_anchor` → `max(confidence, NAMED_DETECTOR_ANCHOR_FLOOR=0.55)`;
  not-named OR no-anchor → unchanged; the three anchor forms (`has_context_anchor`,
  `has_literal_prefix`, `has_distinctive_inner_literal` — terraform `\.atlasv1\.`)
  each independently trigger the lift; assert FP-safety: a weak-anchor / generic /
  entropy / private-key-fallback detector is NOT named so never lifted. Build the
  CROWDIN_API_TOKEN (`<40hex>` context anchor) and bare `cs_<34 alnum>` cloudsmith
  cases from the doc and assert they now clear 0.40.
- **CF-003 — `apply_checksum_decision_confidence` 4-way (policy.rs:34-47).** Suite:
  `Invalid`→`None` (drop), `Valid`→floor 0.9, `StructurallyValid`/`NotApplicable`→
  pass-through. Differential against CK-002 to prove the checksum module wrapper and
  the confidence policy owner agree byte-for-byte.
- **CF-004 — `pre_ml_heuristic_confidence` context multiplier (policy.rs:57-71).**
  Suite: `TestCode`/`Documentation` with `!penalize_test_paths` → ×1.0 (no haircut);
  with `penalize_test_paths` → `code_context.confidence_multiplier()`; ordinary
  source → multiplier; assert the multiplier values from `CodeContext` are applied
  exactly. INPUT: synthesize matches in test files, doc files, and prod source.
- **CF-005 — `ml_pending_confidence` blend + authority (policy.rs:281-304, ml
  feature).** Suite: `model_authoritative` → model score wins outright; else
  `max(blend, heuristic, model)` where `blend = w*model + (1-w)*heuristic`; the
  comment/test/doc context penalty applies only when `confidence < 0.95` and the
  relevant flag is set. Pin against the memory note
  `entropy-unification-gated-off-pending-better-model`: assert authority is gated
  by `KEYHOG_ENTROPY_ML_AUTHORITATIVE` and defaults OFF. ≥80 cases.
- **CF-006 — `finalize_report_confidence` penalty pipeline order (policy.rs:245-267).**
  Suite asserting the EXACT order: post-ML penalties (+ encoded-text lift) → path
  penalties → known-prefix floor → calibration multiplier → checksum confidence
  (which can still drop to `None`). Reorder-sensitivity test: a synthetic match
  where two orderings give different finals, proving the order is contractual.
  **PARTIAL DONE 2026-07-09 (terminal-behavior + veto leg; ci-lean GREEN 6/6):** added a
  `pub fn finalize_report_confidence` primitive-arg facade to `testing::confidence`
  (`ReportConfidencePolicy` is `pub(crate)`, so the facade takes primitives, `calibration=None`)
  + new top-level `tests/regression_finalize_report_confidence_pipeline.rs` (wired into
  `all_tests.rs` in sorted order, so the `recall_locks_wired.py` orphan gate passes). Pins the
  SECURITY-CRITICAL terminal contract the old `engine_fallback_confidence_owner` source-shape gate
  never checked behaviorally: (1) `bad_checksum_vetoes_to_none_even_at_max_confidence` — a `ghp_`
  PAT with a FORGED CRC is dropped to `None` even entering at 0.99 on a named detector (proves the
  checksum decision runs LAST and vetoes past the known-prefix floor — a reorder would emit a
  provably-forged credential as high-confidence FP); (2) `valid_checksum_floors_a_low_entry_up` —
  a checksum-VALID PAT entering at 0.30 is floored up to ≥0.9; (3) `no_checksum_prefix…passes_through`
  — NotApplicable branch neither vetoes nor lifts (score ≤ entry, Some not None); (4)
  `nan_entry_never_leaks_as_some_nan` — end-to-end NaN barrier (never `Some(NaN)`); (5)
  `test_path_penalty_does_not_raise_confidence` — the test-path haircut is inside the tail; (6)
  `encoded_text_lift_flag_never_lowers_confidence_vs_disabled` — the `allow_encoded_text_lift` flag
  is wired through the tail and can only ADD (monotonic `≥`, Review Vector 9).
  Chose the STABLE surface deliberately: the in-flight foreign `confidence/policy.rs` diff (32 lines)
  touches NEITHER `finalize_report_confidence` NOR the checksum stage (verified via `git diff`), and
  the ghp verdicts are deterministic CRC32 — so these assertions are robust to the surrounding
  confidence refactor. REMAINING for full CF-006: the calibration-MULTIPLIER-vs-known-prefix-floor
  reorder-sensitivity case (needs a `Calibration` store with a <1 multiplier via
  `apply_calibration_multiplier_with_store`) — BLOCKED: `confidence/penalties.rs` (which owns
  `apply_calibration_multiplier`) is foreign-in-flight (`git status`=M), so a value-exact
  multiplier-composition assertion would pin a moving target; unblocks the moment that refactor lands.
  **PROPERTY DIMENSION ADDED 2026-07-09 (ci-lean GREEN 2/2, 8000 cases):** new top-level
  `tests/regression_finalize_confidence_proptest.rs` (wired into `all_tests.rs`) sweeps random
  (entry∈[0,1] × credential∈{valid-PAT, forged-PAT, plain} × is_named × penalize × lift × path)
  tuples: (1) `finalized_score_is_finite_unit_interval` (4000 cases) — a present score is always
  finite ∈ [0,1] (NO confidence > 1.0 across the whole flag space — the pipeline bounds it) AND a
  NaN entry never leaks `Some(non-finite)`; (2) `bad_checksum_is_always_vetoed` (4000 cases) — the
  forged-CRC PAT is dropped to `None` for EVERY entry/flag tuple (the terminal veto is unbypassable).
  Complements the example suite with the Testing-Contract proptest dimension.
- **CF-001-monotonicity (compute_confidence signal-monotonicity property) — BLOCKED, recorded.** CF-001
  wants a "score monotonic non-decreasing in each POSITIVE signal (10k)" proptest. Current coverage:
  `property/compute_confidence_always_in_unit_interval.rs` (unit-interval only), `confidence_floor_monotonic.rs`
  (min-confidence FLOOR monotonicity — a different property). The signal-monotonicity property is UNCOVERED,
  but writing it now is BLOCKED: `confidence/mod.rs` is foreign-in-flight refactoring the normalization
  (`MAX_POSSIBLE_SCORE` compile-time denominator replacing per-call accumulation), so `compute_confidence`'s
  exact output is a moving target. NOTE the property is only monotone in the BOOLEAN signals — `match_length`
  is NON-monotone (the low-entropy-long-match penalty, `sub_confidence::low_entropy_long_match_applies_penalty`
  out=0.21), so the proptest must hold entropy+length fixed and vary only booleans. Unblocks when the
  `MAX_POSSIBLE_SCORE` refactor lands. | status=blocked-on-foreign-normalization-refactor
- **CF-007 — `generic_secret_confidence` base/boosts (policy.rs:380-402).** Suite:
  base per context (TestCode+penalize→0.25, Comment+scan→0.60, Doc+penalize→0.30,
  Comment→0.30, else→0.60); `entropy_boost=min((entropy-3.5)*0.1, 0.25)`;
  `length_boost=clamp((len-16)*0.005, 0, 0.15)`; final `min(0.95)`. Boundary cases
  at entropy 3.5 and len 16. This is the generic-keyword low-entropy floor from the
  CredData recall memo — pin it so recall work doesn't silently regress it.
- **CF-008 — `entropy_fallback_confidence` tiers (policy.rs:361-378, entropy feat).**
  Suite: `>= VERY_HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD`→0.75, `>= HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD`→0.65,
  else→`min(0.55, entropy/8.0)`; the `+0.1` keyword lift (capped 0.90) applies
  unless keyword == "none (high-entropy)". Boundary at each threshold.
- **CF-009 — `probabilistic_promise_confidence_override` (policy.rs:347-359, ml).**
  Suite: a `ProbabilisticGate::looks_promising` credential → `None` (no override,
  goes to model); an identifier-shaped (`looks_like_word_separated_identifier` /
  `looks_like_pure_identifier`) OR not-named credential → `Some(0.1)` suppression;
  a named non-identifier credential → `None`. This is a recall-affecting suppression
  path — assert real high-entropy secrets are NOT suppressed.
- **CF-010 — `MlScoreResult` lifetime placeholder safety (policy.rs:3-20).** Suite +
  compile gate proving the `_Lifetime(PhantomData)` variant is NEVER constructed in
  the no-ml build and the `unreachable!` is provably unreachable; the no-ml build
  emits `Final(heuristic_conf)` for every candidate (recall-safe, Law 10). Cross-build
  test: ml and no-ml builds agree on the heuristic-only score for the same input.
- **CF-011 — `apply_known_prefix_floor` applied twice (policy.rs:224, 260).** Suite
  proving the known-prefix floor is idempotent (applying it in `candidate_match_score`
  AND again in `finalize_report_confidence` yields the same result as once); pin the
  prefix→floor table from `known_prefix_confidence_floor`.

---

# §5 — VERIFIER: SSRF guard, DNS, live verification
Grounded in `crates/verifier/src/ssrf.rs` (read in full) and `verifier/src/bogon.rs`,
`verifier/src/lib.rs`. Security-critical — fail-closed is contractual.

- **SSRF-001 — Integer-encoded IP bypass matrix (`is_private_url`, ssrf.rs:120-192).**
  Entire suite (≥200 cases) covering EVERY radix a permissive resolver canonicalizes:
  decimal `http://2130706433/`, hex `http://0x7f000001/` (and `0X`), octal
  `http://017700000001/`, dotted `http://127.0.0.1/` — each → blocked. Add the
  full private/loopback/link-local/metadata set (127/8, 10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16,
  169.254/16, 100.64/10 CGNAT, ::1, fc00::/7, 169.254.169.254 cloud metadata). This
  regression-pins VRF-001 (the hex-form bypass the kimi review found when only
  decimal was blocked). Property test: any integer in 127.0.0.0/8 in any radix blocks.
- **SSRF-002 — inet_aton short-form canonicalization (`canonicalize_short_form_ipv4`
  + `parse_ip_field`, ssrf.rs:205-278).** Suite: 2-part `127.1`→127.0.0.1,
  3-part `172.16.1`→172.16.0.1, with each field in decimal/hex(`0x`)/octal(leading
  `0`); out-of-range field → `None` (treated as domain, still DNS-screened);
  the `https://127.1/` proxy-path case (which SKIPS the post-resolution veto, so the
  string gate is the ONLY guard — assert it blocks). Boundary: field > max_last,
  field > 0xFF in a leading position, empty field.
- **SSRF-003 — `looks_like_malformed_ip` octet/octal evasion (ssrf.rs:280-315).**
  Suite: 4+ dot parts all octet-shaped (incl. `0x7f.0.0.-1` — the `f` hexdigit the
  pre-fix `digit|-|x|X` set missed, an SSRF bypass) → blocked; 4-part all-octal
  `0177.0.0.1` → blocked; a legitimate 4-label domain (`a.b.c.example`) → NOT
  mis-blocked. Differential against SSRF-001 to ensure no double-count gap.
- **SSRF-004 — Fail-closed parse + scheme + host gates (ssrf.rs:92-129).** Suite:
  unparseable URL → blocked (true); non-http(s) scheme (`file:`, `gopher:`, `ftp:`,
  `dict:`, `ldap:`) → blocked; hostless URL → blocked; `localhost`, `*.localhost`,
  `*.local`, `*.internal`, `*.localdomain`, single-label (no dot) host → blocked.
  Every `return true` branch is a security decision — assert each fires with the
  exact offending input. Law 10: no input reaches the network that should be blocked.
- **SSRF-005 — DNS-rebinding post-resolution veto (`is_private_ip_addr`,
  `resolve_dns_cached`, ssrf.rs:31-89).** Suite (needs a controllable resolver/mock):
  a public-looking domain whose A record resolves to 127.0.0.1 / 169.254.169.254 /
  a bogon → the post-resolution `is_private_ip_addr` veto blocks it even though the
  string gate passed; assert the verifier never connects. Pin that `is_private_ip_addr`,
  `is_private_ip_addr_fast`, and the URL-string path all route through the SINGLE
  `verifier_blocks_ip_addr` policy (no drift).
- **SSRF-006 — `verifier_blocks_ip_addr` = bogon ∪ {v4 multicast, Class-E ≥240}
  (ssrf.rs:64-73).** Suite asserting the verifier blocks everything `bogon` blocks
  PLUS IPv4 multicast (224/4) and Class-E (240/4); a property test feeding random
  IPs and cross-checking `bogon::ip_addr_is_bogon ⟹ verifier_blocks_ip_addr`.
  Pairs with the existing NAT64/6to4-wrapped-bogon tests added this session.
- **SSRF-007 — DNS cache bounds (`DNS_CACHE_TTL=60s`, `DNS_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES=4096`,
  ssrf.rs:14-55).** Suite: an entry older than 60s is re-resolved (not pinned);
  inserting the 4097th distinct host clears the cache rather than growing unbounded;
  an attacker-influenced wildcard host (`*.evil.example` resolving many subdomains)
  cannot keep memory growing. Concurrency: parallel `resolve_dns_cached` on the same
  host does not deadlock (the stale-entry `drop(entry)` before `remove`).
- **SSRF-008 — IPv6 wrapping coverage.** Suite: IPv4-mapped (`::ffff:127.0.0.1`),
  IPv4-compatible, NAT64 (`64:ff9b::/96` wrapping a bogon), 6to4 (`2002::/16`),
  Teredo (`2001::/32`) — each wrapping a private/bogon v4 → blocked. Extends the
  ssrf.rs IPv6 arm (currently `IpAddr::V6(_) => false` in `verifier_blocks_ip_addr`,
  so the wrapping must be caught by `bogon` — assert it is, and ADD any uncaught
  wrapper as a fix to the bogon table source, not a local patch).
- **SSRF-009 — Live verifier fail-closed UNKNOWN (verifier/src/lib.rs).** Suite per
  verifiable detector family: a network error / timeout / non-2xx during live
  validation → verdict UNKNOWN (never silent-VALID); a blocked-SSRF target →
  refused before any socket; `apply_proxy_config` honored. Dogfood: run `keyhog`
  with `--verify` against a localhost honeypot and assert no connection to internal
  ranges, on linux-x86_64 + macOS-arm64.

---

# §6 — CLI: `keyhog scan` flag surface
Grounded in `crates/cli/src/args/scan.rs` (read in full, ~90 flags). Each task is
an entire e2e suite that drives the SHIPPED binary, asserts behavior + exit code +
output bytes, and dogfoods on the MACHINE-MATRIX. Pairs with the CLI-08 long-flag
snapshot gate so no flag is silently added/removed.

- **CLI-001 — Preset base semantics (`--fast`/`--deep`/`--precision`/`--lockdown`).**
  Entire suite asserting each preset SEEDS defaults then yields to compatible
  explicit knobs: `--fast --decode-depth 2` re-enables shallow decode on the fast
  base; `--deep --min-confidence 0.9` raises the floor on the deep base;
  `--precision` raises the confidence floor to 0.85 and drops entropy-only/ML-
  speculative findings; `--lockdown` enables `apply_protections(true)` (mlock,
  refuse-on-coredump-leak, refuse-on-disk-cache), forces HTTPS-only verifier, and
  HARD-ABORTS if any protection fails to take. Assert every `conflicts_with_all`
  pair in the source actually rejects (e.g. `--fast --no-entropy` → clap error,
  not a no-op flag). Dogfood `--lockdown` and assert it refuses to write any cache.
- **CLI-002 — Source-selection flag matrix.** Entire suite per source flag:
  `--path`/positional `[PATH]`, `--stdin`, `--binary`, `--git-blobs`, `--git-diff`,
  `--git-history`, `--git-staged`, `--git-diff-path`, `--github-org`, `--gitlab-group`,
  `--bitbucket-workspace`, `--s3-bucket`, `--gcs-bucket`, `--azure-container-url`,
  `--docker-image`, `--url`. Assert each `requires`/`requires_all` relationship
  (e.g. `--github-org` requires `--github-token`); each routes to the right Source
  impl; mutually-exclusive sources error clearly. Plant a real secret reachable
  ONLY through each source and assert it is found with the right `source_type`.
- **CLI-003 — Multi-root rejection → real support (`extra_paths`, scan.rs:95-104).**
  Suite proving `keyhog scan a/ b/` today routes to
  `reject_multiple_scan_roots` with actionable guidance (common-parent / per-path /
  `--exclude-paths`), NOT a bare clap "unexpected argument"; then the M-05
  installment that turns `extra_paths` into real multi-root scanning. Assert the
  `hide=true` extra-paths arg carries no `long` so the CLI-08 snapshot never sees it.
- **CLI-004 — Output-format fan (`--format`, OutputFormat 9 variants).** Entire
  suite scanning ONE fixed corpus and asserting byte-stable output for Text, Json,
  Jsonl, Sarif, Csv, GithubAnnotations, GitlabSast, Html, Junit; redaction holds in
  ALL nine; SARIF `partialFingerprint = sha256(value)` is byte-identical across two
  runs (dedup stability); `--output <file>` writes the same bytes as stdout; the
  `--stream` stderr preview line matches the report exactly (never previews a
  dropped match). Pairs with §8 reporter suites. Dogfood all nine on MACHINE-MATRIX.
- **CLI-005 — Security/network flags fail-safe (`--proxy`/`--insecure`/
  `--allow-s3-credential-forward`/`--allow-gcs-token-forward`/`--allow-script-verify`).**
  Suite proving: `--proxy` is the ONLY proxy source (ambient `HTTPS_PROXY`/`HTTP_PROXY`/
  `ALL_PROXY` are IGNORED — set them and assert no reroute); `--proxy off` is explicit
  no-proxy; `--insecure` is the ONLY TLS-skip source (no env can disable verification);
  `--allow-s3-credential-forward`/`--allow-gcs-token-forward` are OFF by default and
  print an explicit warning when on; `--allow-script-verify` prints a warning and is
  off by default (scripts run verifier-supplied code). These are exfil-risk surfaces —
  every default must be the safe one. AUDIT: assert no env var silently flips any.
- **CLI-006 — Daemon tri-state resolution (`--daemon[=auto|on|off]`/`--no-daemon`/
  `--daemon-socket`, scan.rs:62-81,473-517).** Suite: bare `--daemon`→On,
  `--daemon=auto`→Auto (default when absent), `--daemon=off`→Off, `--no-daemon`→Off;
  `daemon_mode()` folds the alias (assert clap `conflicts_with` makes both-supplied
  impossible); `--daemon-socket` reaches a non-default socket; `--daemon=on` with no
  live daemon → fail (not silent in-process). Pairs with M-06. PTY e2e proving the
  daemon route streams progress + findings + exits clean.
- **CLI-007 — Verify-flag suite (`--verify`/`--verify-oob`/`--oob-server`/
  `--oob-timeout`/`--verify-rate`/`--verify-batch`/`--timeout`/`--rate`).** Entire
  suite: `--verify-oob` requires `--verify` (clap rejects standalone, not silent-ignore);
  `--oob-server`/`--oob-timeout` require `--verify-oob`; `--verify-rate` default 5.0 RPS
  per service is honored even with `--verify-batch`; `--verify-batch` serializes
  per-service (max-concurrent=1); a network failure → UNKNOWN not silent-VALID (pairs
  SSRF-009). `parse_verify_rate`/`parse_ml_threshold` boundary cases.
- **CLI-008 — Decode/entropy knob suite (`--no-decode`/`--decode-depth`/
  `--decode-size-limit`/`--no-entropy`/`--entropy-threshold`/`--entropy-source-files`/
  `--min-secret-len`/`--no-entropy-ml-scoring`/`--no-keyword-low-entropy`/`--ml-threshold`/
  `--ml-weight`/`--no-ml`).** Entire suite: `--decode-depth` 1-10 boundary via
  `parse_decode_depth`; `--decode-size-limit` default 512KB; `--entropy-threshold`
  default 4.5 bits/byte; `--min-secret-len` default 16; `--no-keyword-low-entropy`
  restores the high-entropy-only generic gate (assert recall drops on a low-entropy
  config-password corpus — the CredData recall lever); `--ml-threshold` composes via
  `.max()` with the floor. Differential: each knob's recall/precision delta vs default.
- **CLI-009 — Baseline lifecycle (`--baseline`/`--create-baseline`/`--update-baseline`).**
  Suite: `--create-baseline` writes a baseline of current findings and exits;
  `--baseline` suppresses matching findings on a later scan; `--update-baseline` adds
  new findings; the three `conflicts_with_all` each other. Assert a baselined finding
  is suppressed by stable fingerprint and a NEW finding still surfaces + sets exit code.
- **CLI-010 — Config discovery & hermeticity (`--config`/`--no-config`).** Suite:
  `--no-config` skips walk-up `.keyhog.toml` discovery AND rejects `--config` (clap
  conflict), running on compiled-in `SHIPPED_*` defaults; a stray ancestor
  `.keyhog.toml` cannot silently change measured behavior under `--no-config` (the
  hermetic-config contract the benchmark harness relies on); `--config <path>` loads
  exactly that file; CLI knobs override TOML which overrides compiled defaults (Tier-A).
- **CLI-011 — Performance-tuning knobs (`--threads`/`--reader-threads`/`--fused-batch`/
  `--fused-depth`/`--per-chunk-timeout-ms`/`--regex-dfa-limit`/`--max-file-size`/
  `--batch-pipeline`/`--no-batch-pipeline`/`--incremental`/`--incremental-cache`/
  `--cache-dir`).** Suite: each `parse_positive_*` value-parser rejects 0/negative;
  `--max-file-size` default 100 MiB (the FilesystemSource ceiling — pin the
  kimi-dogfood-3 #135 regression where help said "10MB" but 100 MiB was de facto);
  files above the 1 MiB window read in overlapping windows; `--incremental` merkle
  skip-cache gives the documented re-run speedup and updates the index; `--per-chunk-timeout-ms`
  enforces a hard deadline while decode keeps its internal bomb guard. Criterion perf
  per knob on MACHINE-MATRIX.
- **CLI-012 — Reporting/filter flags (`--severity`/`--min-confidence`/`--dedup`/
  `--show-secrets`/`--hide-client-safe`/`--scan-comments`/`--no-suppress-test-fixtures`/
  `--no-default-excludes`/`--exclude-paths`/`--dogfood`/`--progress`/`--profile`/
  `--perf-trace`).** Entire suite: `--severity` filters at each tier; `--min-confidence`
  default 0.40 floor; `--dedup credential|file|none` (CliDedupScope→DedupScope);
  `--show-secrets` unredacts (default redacted); `--hide-client-safe` drops the
  CLIENT-SAFE tier (default keeps them below LOW); `--scan-comments` removes the
  comment haircut; `--no-suppress-test-fixtures` surfaces the bundled demo creds
  (for differential vs gitleaks/trufflehog which don't suppress); `--dogfood` JSON
  trace names the exact suppression gate per silenced match. Assert `mark_cli_value_sources`
  correctly distinguishes CLI-explicit from default for detectors/format/dedup.

---

# §7 — SOURCES: walkers, extraction, the 16 source impls
Grounded in `crates/sources/src/filesystem.rs` (+ submodules extract/filter/path/
read/reader) and the `impl Source` set (filesystem, stdin, git{,diff,history},
docker{,oci,layer}, s3, gcs, azure_blob, web, slack, github_org, gitlab_group,
bitbucket_workspace, binary).

- **SRC-001 — Filesystem windowing seam (`reader::DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE`/
  `DEFAULT_WINDOW_OVERLAP`, filesystem.rs:70-75).** Entire suite: a file above the
  ~1 MiB window is read in overlapping windows so memory stays bounded regardless of
  size; a secret straddling EVERY window seam (at offset window-1, window, window+1,
  and at the overlap edges) is recovered exactly once (no duplicate, no loss);
  assert the overlap is ≥ the longest detectable credential so nothing splits. Pairs
  SR-008 and the UTF-16 mmap fix (#26). Scale: a 100 MiB file with secrets sprinkled
  at every seam, peak RSS asserted bounded, on MACHINE-MATRIX.
- **SRC-002 — `--max-file-size` skip + counter honesty (`DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES`
  100 MiB).** Suite: a file at cap-1/cap/cap+1; the over-cap file is SKIPPED and
  listed in the end-of-scan "files skipped: exceeded --max-file-size" summary
  (Law 10 — never a silent skip); the skip is counted and reaches the coverage-gap
  determination; a custom `--max-file-size` re-bounds it. Uses
  `process_entry_with_recorded_size_for_test` to drive `process_entry` directly and
  assert the Unreadable/Skipped row.
- **SRC-003 — Symlink + TOCTOU + unreadable-entry safety (`process_entry`,
  `open_file_safe`, filesystem.rs:79-115).** Suite: a symlink to outside the root is
  refused and recorded as an Unreadable skip (not followed); a symlink loop does not
  hang; a file deleted between walk and read (TOCTOU) records a skip, not a crash; an
  unreadable (perm-denied) file records a counted skip. Assert the
  `acquire_scan_read_lease` serializes counter recording. AUDIT vector: traversal +
  symlink race.
- **SRC-004 — Reader-pool panic recovery (`spawn_chunk_producer`,
  `reader_panic_rows_for_test`, `process_entry_panic_rows_for_test`).** Suite: a
  panicking entry iterator does NOT take down the scan — the panic is contained, the
  row surfaces as an error, and remaining files still scan; `reader_thread_count`
  derivation from scanner threads (with and without explicit `--reader-threads`
  config) is correct at 1, N, and pathological inputs. Law 10: a swallowed reader
  panic that drops files silently is a finding — assert the error is surfaced.
- **SRC-005 — Default-excludes + gitignore + extension skip (filter submodule:
  `default_exclude_dirs`, `is_default_excluded`, `is_skip_extension`).** Suite: lock
  files / minified / build outputs / `node_modules` are excluded by default;
  `--no-default-excludes` re-includes them; `.gitignore` is honored by `CodeWalker`;
  `--exclude-paths <glob>` adds explicit excludes; a SECRET inside an excluded dir is
  NOT found by default but IS found with `--no-default-excludes` (proving the exclude
  is the only reason). Tier-B: the exclude list is data-driven (per the consolidated
  skip-dir memo), not hardcoded — assert it loads from the Tier-B file.
- **SRC-006 — Merkle incremental skip-cache (`MerkleIndex`,
  `process_entry_with_merkle_for_test`, `--incremental`).** Suite: first scan builds
  `~/.cache/keyhog/merkle.idx`; a re-scan with no content change skips unchanged
  files (assert the documented 10-100x speedup on a mostly-unchanged tree) and still
  updates the index; a changed file is re-scanned and its new secret found; a
  corrupted index fails loudly, not silent-rescan-everything-as-if-fresh (verify it's
  not a silent fallback). `--incremental-cache <path>` overrides location.
- **SRC-007 — Nested-archive extraction depth + budget (extract submodule:
  `extraction_total_budget`, `MAX_NESTED_ARCHIVE_DEPTH`).** Suite (extends the
  unified-depth-cap test added this session): zip-in-tar-in-gz to the unified depth
  cap is bounded across ALL families (zip/tar/gz/bz2/xz/zst); a decompression bomb is
  refused via the total budget (Law 7 — algorithmic-DoS guard); a real secret at the
  max legal depth IS found; the over-budget refusal is counted + surfaced, never
  silent. AUDIT vector: decompression bomb.
- **SRC-008 — Per-source suite: stdin (`StdinSource`/`ConfiguredStdinSource`).**
  Suite: piped content scanned with `source_type="stdin"`; empty stdin → clean exit;
  huge stdin streamed in windows; binary-on-stdin handling; a secret on stdin found
  with correct (synthetic) path/line. INPUT-MATRIX f.
- **SRC-009 — Per-source suite: git history/diff/blobs/staged (`GitSource`,
  `GitHistorySource`, `GitDiffSource`).** Suite: a secret committed then deleted in a
  later commit is recalled from history with correct commit/author/date (INPUT-MATRIX
  e); `--git-diff main` scans only added lines between refs; `--git-blobs` dedups by
  blob id; `--git-staged` scans only staged files; `--max-commits` bounds traversal.
- **SRC-010 — Per-source suite: cloud (s3/gcs/azure_blob).** Suite per provider:
  `ListObjectsV2`/JSON-API/container enumeration; `--*-prefix` limits; `--*-endpoint`
  override; the credential-forward flags gate ambient-credential leakage to custom
  endpoints (pairs CLI-005); skip-extension filtering on object keys; a secret in a
  bucket object is found with the right `source_type`. Mock the API; assert no real
  network in tests.
- **SRC-011 — Per-source suite: docker image (`DockerImageSource`, docker/oci/layer/
  metadata).** Suite: `docker image save` unpack; per-layer chunk attribution
  (`source_type="docker"`); secret in an image layer found; metadata/config blobs
  scanned; a malformed/oversized layer is bounded + counted.
- **SRC-012 — Per-source suite: web/slack/github-org/gitlab-group/bitbucket-workspace.**
  Suite per remote source: JS/source-map/WASM fetch (`WebSource`, `--url` multi-arg);
  org/group/workspace repo enumeration with the required token; pagination; rate-limit
  handling → backoff not silent-truncate; every fetch routes through the SSRF gate +
  the `--proxy` (no ambient env) + `--insecure` policy. Mock transports; assert
  fail-closed UNKNOWN on transport error.
- **SRC-013 — Per-source suite: binary string extraction (`BinarySource`,
  `--binary`).** Suite: ASCII/UTF-8/UTF-16 string extraction from ELF/PE/Mach-O;
  a hardcoded secret in a binary's `.rodata` found; the min-string-length gate; a
  non-binary file under `--binary` handled; the 256-byte-pattern cap (the ebpfkit
  memo) respected. Pairs the binary-source isolation gate.

---

# §8 — DETECTOR FAMILY HARDENING (904 detectors, as bunches not atoms)
This REPLACES the old per-detector × 20-template mess. The unit is a provider
FAMILY; each task is ONE bunch that hardens EVERY member end-to-end. Per member,
the bunch ships an entire suite: valid-checksum positive; revoked/example
negative-twin; split/interpolated/concatenated evasion recall; base64+percent +
nested-archive + git-history + UTF-16-seam INPUT-MATRIX recall; entropy-floor
straddle; keyword-bridge low-context surface; 10k-case proptest on the canonical
shape; differential vs DIFFERENTIAL-PEERS on CredData; redaction in all 9 output
formats + SARIF fingerprint; verifier live-check provider-endpoint mapping with
fail-closed UNKNOWN; CWE/severity propagation into every reporter. Each member's
contract lives at `crates/scanner/tests/contracts/<id>.toml`; the suite asserts
ASSERT-SPINE values, never `!is_empty`.

- **DF-google — 12 detectors**: google-ads-api-developer-token, google-api-key,
  google-artifact-registry-key, google-classroom-api-credentials,
  google-cloud-iot-credentials, google-cloud-source-repos-credentials,
  google-cloud-sovereign-credentials, google-cloud-storage-hmac-key,
  google-forms-api-credentials, google-meet-api, google-oauth-client-secret,
  google-secret-manager-credentials. Special care: `AIza` prefix shape vs the
  HMAC-key shape; OAuth client-secret entropy floor; the govcloud/sovereign variants
  must not collide with the base api-key. Differential: Google keys are a known
  trufflehog/gitleaks strength — match or beat recall.
- **DF-azure — 11 detectors**: azure-blob-sas-token, azure-container-registry-token,
  azure-devops-pat, azure-functions-key, azure-government-credentials,
  azure-iot-connection-string, azure-key-vault-credentials, azure-openai-api-key,
  azure-service-bus-connection-string, azure-storage-account-key,
  azure-subscription-key. Special care: connection-string parsing (the `;`-delimited
  `AccountKey=`/`SharedAccessKey=` extraction), SAS-token query-string shape, the
  base64 storage-account-key vs subscription-key GUID distinction.
- **DF-aws — 11 detectors**: aws-access-key, aws-bedrock-api-key,
  aws-codecommit-credentials, aws-cognito-client-secret, aws-ecr-token,
  aws-govcloud-access-key, aws-lambda-function-url-secret, aws-secret-access-key,
  aws-secrets-manager-arn, aws-session-token, aws-ses-smtp-credentials. Special care:
  the `AKIA`/`ASIA`/govcloud `AGPA…` prefix family; the access-key↔secret-key
  COMPANION signal (has_companion confidence boost); session-token length; the
  AKIA…EXAMPLE placeholder suppression (pairs the entropy-placeholder-marker memo).
- **DF-github — 9 detectors**: github-app-installation-token, github-app-private-key,
  github-classic-pat, github-oauth-access-token, github-oauth-secret,
  github-pat-fine-grained, github-refresh-token, github-user-to-server-token,
  github-webhook-secret. Special care: the CRC32 checksum on `ghp_`/`github_pat_`
  (pairs CK-004) — a valid-checksum positive AND a one-bit-flip negative per shape;
  the `gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_` prefix split across detectors; app-private-key PEM block.
- **DF-vercel — 7 detectors**: vercel-api-token-v2, vercel-blob-credentials,
  vercel-edge-config-token, vercel-edge-function-credentials, vercel-kv-credentials,
  vercel-postgres-credentials, vercel-token. Special care: the v2 token shape vs the
  legacy token; the connection-string-bearing credentials (kv/postgres) decode-through.
- **DF-supabase — 6 detectors**: supabase-anon-key, supabase-jwt-secret,
  supabase-management-api-key, supabase-realtime-credentials, supabase-service-key,
  supabase-storage-credentials. Special care: anon-key is a JWT and is CLIENT-SAFE by
  design (pairs `--hide-client-safe`); service-key vs anon-key role distinction; the
  jwt-secret must not be confused with the JWTs it signs.
- **DF-slack — 6 detectors**: slack-app-token, slack-bot-token, slack-config-token,
  slack-oauth-secret, slack-user-token, slack-webhook-url. Special care: the
  `xoxb/xoxp/xoxa/xoxs` prefix split + the Slack checksum validator (pairs CK-005);
  webhook-url OOB-verifiable (pairs CLI-007 `--verify-oob`).
- **DF-gitlab — 6 detectors**: gitlab-deploy-token, gitlab-package-registry-token,
  gitlab-personal-access-token, gitlab-pipeline-trigger-token,
  gitlab-runner-authentication-token, gitlab-webhook-secret. Special care: the
  `glpat-` checksum (pairs CK-005 + the checksum-fabricated-token memo); the
  `gldt-`/`glrt-`/`glptt-` prefix family; self-hosted `--gitlab-endpoint` parity.
- **DF-cloudflare — 6 detectors**: cloudflare-api-token, cloudflare-d1-credentials,
  cloudflare-global-api-key, cloudflare-kv-credentials, cloudflare-workers-api-token,
  cloudflare-zero-trust-service-token. Special care: global-api-key (37 hex) vs the
  scoped api-token (40 base64url) precision split; zero-trust service-token shape.
- **DF-twilio — 5 detectors**: twilio-api-key, twilio-auth-token, twilio-flex-token,
  twilio-iot-credentials, twilio-webhook-signing-secret. Special care:
  twilio-auth-token is a NO-LITERAL-PREFIX detector that fires ONLY via Hyperscan
  (the SIMD-trigger-union memo) — its parity test MUST cover the HS-only path; the
  `SK`/`AC` SID companion signal.
- **DF-shopify — 4 detectors**: shopify-access-token, shopify-admin-api-token,
  shopify-storefront-api-token, shopify-webhook-secret. Special care: the
  `shpat_/shpca_/shppa_/shpss_` prefix split; storefront token is client-safe.
- **DF-redis — 4 detectors**: redis-cloud-v2-api-key, redis-connection-string,
  redis-labs-api-key, redis-sentinel-credentials. Special care: connection-string
  `redis://user:pass@host` password extraction (decode-through + URL parsing).
- **DF-remaining — the other ~820 detectors, batched by provider.** Work the rest of
  `detectors/*.toml` in alphabetical provider runs of ~12, ONE bunch per run, each
  applying the full per-member matrix above. Never an atomic "add one test for X";
  every run hardens its whole batch. Track completion against the contracts dir so
  `detector_contract_coverage_meets_floor` stays at 1.0. Priority order: the families
  CredData proves keyhog loses recall on first (the M-08 audit names them), then the
  no-literal-prefix Hyperscan-only detectors (the SIMD-trigger-union memo lists
  twilio-auth-token, datadog-api-key, … ~49 of them), then the rest.

---

# §9 — CLI: the other 16 subcommands
Grounded in `crates/cli/src/args.rs` (Subcommand enum) + `subcommands/`. Each task
is an entire e2e suite that PTY-drives the SHIPPED binary, asserts output bytes +
exit codes, covers every flag + every error path, and dogfoods on MACHINE-MATRIX.

- **SUB-doctor — `keyhog doctor`.** Suite: reports HEALTHY/UNHEALTHY (not warning) on
  GPU self-test FAIL (pairs #15/#16); the GPU self-test is suppressed-probe-safe (no
  false FAIL); reports autoroute calibration coverage (which presets/configs are
  calibrated, #37); machine-readable failure causes via `last_gpu_degrade_reason` (no
  stderr scraping); on a GPU host (RTX 5090/4090/santhserver) doctor must PASS GPU (Law
  8 — "skipped: no GPU" is a config bug there); on portable/macOS it exits 0 with GPU
  honestly absent (the install-flow memo). Assert exact exit code per health state.
- **SUB-backend — `keyhog backend` / `--autoroute` / `--self-test` / `--show`.** Suite:
  prints the deterministic `select_backend_for_batch` verdict + `reason_detail()` for
  the current host (pairs SR-005/SR-006); `--autoroute --show` surfaces the persisted
  calibrated decision + the decision inputs (#38); `--self-test` exercises each
  available backend and fails closed on a backend it can't honor; `BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES`
  all accepted (pairs SR-003).
- **SUB-calibrate / SUB-calibrate-autoroute.** Suite: `keyhog calibrate-autoroute`
  benchmarks parity-checked candidates and persists the fastest-correct decision per
  workload bucket (#32); a near-miss workload bucket does NOT exit-2 (the generalizing
  bucket model, #34); measurement rigor gives a stable fastest-backend decision not
  noise (#35); `keyhog calibrate` writes the per-detector Bayesian confidence cache;
  every documented preset × workload class resolves a decision after `install --calibrate`
  (#39); `--show` is inspectable. Below-smallest-bucket + between-rung single files
  fail closed exit-2 (#44/#46).
- **SUB-detectors — `keyhog detectors`.** Suite: lists all 904 detectors with id +
  service + severity + CWE; `--explain <id>` shows the pattern/keywords/checksum;
  filtering by service/severity; the output is coherent with the loaded TOML
  (every listed id resolves, no orphan); machine-readable (json) form.
- **SUB-explain — `keyhog explain`.** Suite: explains why a given value would/would not
  be flagged (which detector, which confidence signals, which suppression gate); pairs
  the `--dogfood` trace; detector-quality advisories are silenced on this user-facing
  path (#24 — explain must not spew advisory noise).
- **SUB-diff — `keyhog diff`.** Suite: diffs two scan result sets (new/fixed/persisting
  findings); stable ordering; exit code reflects new findings; output formats.
- **SUB-watch — `keyhog watch`.** Suite (PTY/e2e — a watch surface must run the real
  workflow, not a shell): start a watch, modify a file to introduce a secret, assert
  the finding streams; debounce; clean exit on SIGINT; the warmed scanner is reused
  (no per-event recompile). Pairs the `warm()` daemon-sized path (SR-010).
- **SUB-daemon — `keyhog daemon start|stop|status`.** Suite: socket at
  `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/keyhog.sock` (+ `--socket` override → fixed location, M-06); a
  `scan --daemon=on` reaches it (pairs CLI-006); cold-start progress line (#47); stale
  socket handling; `status` reports liveness; the daemon holds a compiled scanner so
  each scan is sub-ms IPC. PTY e2e proving stream + clean shutdown.
- **SUB-hook — `keyhog hook`.** Suite: pre-commit/pre-push hook install + invocation;
  scans staged files; blocks the commit on a finding with the right exit code; respects
  `--no-daemon`; the hook glue passes `--no-daemon` compatibility alias (pairs CLI-006).
- **SUB-completion — `keyhog completion <shell>`.** Suite: bash/zsh/fish/powershell
  completions generated; every subcommand + long flag appears (coherence with the
  CLI-08 snapshot); the script is valid for each shell (source it in a subshell).
- **SUB-config — `keyhog config`.** Suite: show/validate/init `.keyhog.toml`; Tier-A
  precedence (CLI > TOML > compiled `SHIPPED_*`); a malformed config fails loudly with
  context+fix (not silent default); `--no-config` hermeticity (pairs CLI-010).
- **SUB-update / SUB-repair / SUB-uninstall.** Suite: `update` checks + applies a new
  release with checksum/signature verification (no silent unverified swap); `repair`
  restores PATH/completions/binary from a damaged install; `uninstall` removes binary +
  cache + PATH entry + completions leaving a clean tree (the dogfood-doctrine clean
  uninstall). Dogfood these as the REAL install flow (install.sh / installer), never
  cargo. Pairs §13.
- **SUB-scan-system — `keyhog scan-system`.** Suite: scans system-wide credential
  locations; respects the same SSRF/redaction/suppression policy; bounded + does not
  hang on special files (/proc, /sys, devices, sockets, FIFOs); the long-lived scan
  warms the scanner once (SR-010).

---

# §10 — CORE: reporters, redaction, dedup, calibration
Grounded in `crates/core/src/report/` (banner/csv/escape/github_annotations/
gitlab_sast/html/json/junit/sarif{,_taxonomies,_types,_uri}/text) and core
redaction/dedup/MerkleIndex/Severity/Calibration.

- **RPT-001 — Per-reporter golden suite (one file per format).** Entire suite per
  reporter: scan a FIXED multi-finding corpus, assert byte-exact output (golden file)
  for text/json/jsonl/sarif/csv/junit/github_annotations/gitlab_sast/html; redaction
  holds in every field of every format; special chars are escaped per format
  (`escape.rs` — JSON `\"`, CSV quoting, HTML entity, SARIF). A new detector or field
  must update the golden, never silently change bytes.
- **RPT-002 — SARIF correctness deep suite (sarif.rs + sarif_types/taxonomies/uri).**
  Suite: schema-valid SARIF 2.1.0; `partialFingerprints` = sha256(value) byte-stable
  across runs (dedup/`.keyhogignore` stability, the self-scan memo); CWE taxonomy
  references resolve; `render_relative_uri` prefixes `./` when the first segment has a
  colon (the leading-colon-segment fix added this session) — assert a `a:b/c.rs` path
  is disambiguated and a normal path is not; absolute/relative/UNC/Windows paths all
  render valid URIs.
- **RPT-003 — Redaction is total (core redaction).** Suite + AUDIT: NO output format,
  NO log line, NO SARIF fingerprint, NO `--stream` preview, NO `--dogfood` trace ever
  emits a full plaintext credential unless `--show-secrets` is set; property test (10k)
  feeding random secrets through every reporter asserting the plaintext never appears.
  Law: never log secrets.
- **RPT-004 — Dedup scope semantics (`DedupScope::{Credential,File,None}`).** Suite:
  Credential collapses identical values across files (assert N copies → 1 finding +
  the count); File dedups within a file; None reports every occurrence; the dedup key
  is stable across runs and matches the SARIF fingerprint. Scale: 5,000 copies of one
  secret across a 50k-file tree collapse with bounded finding count + wall-clock.
- **RPT-005 — `-o`/`--output` atomic write to any target (the #23 fix).** Suite:
  writing to a regular file is atomic (temp+rename); writing to `/dev/null`, a FIFO, a
  pipe, or a non-regular target does NOT fail the atomic-write path; the same bytes as
  stdout; a write error surfaces loudly (not silent-empty-report).
- **RPT-006 — Severity + CWE propagation (core Severity, detector TOML).** Suite: every
  detector's declared severity + CWE propagates UNCHANGED into every reporter and the
  verifier verdict; `--severity <tier>` filters correctly at each level; the
  CLIENT-SAFE tier sits below LOW and is droppable via `--hide-client-safe`.
- **RPT-007 — Calibration multiplier (core Calibration, `apply_calibration_multiplier`).**
  Suite: a per-detector calibration entry scales confidence; an absent entry is
  identity; a damaged explicit calibration cache fails before scanning (reproducible
  scores, not silent-ignore, pairs CLI-001 `--calibration-cache`); the GPU/CPU MoE
  score divergence that flipped a floor-straddle finding (#18) stays fixed under a
  differential GPU-vs-CPU calibration test.

---

# §11 — AUDIT HUNTS (the 15 adversarial vectors, codebase-wide)
Each is a deep bunch: find the sink + all callers, drive under a sanitizer, add a
proptest + a hostile corpus, fix at the source. Never declare a vector "clean".

- **AUD-001 — Decompression/zip bombs & OOM**: every archive family (zip/tar/gz/bz2/
  xz/zst) + nested-archive depth + the structured 2MB cap + decode-through budget;
  assert a bomb is refused via a counted, surfaced budget (Law 7), never a silent OOM.
- **AUD-002 — Panics / unwraps / expects**: a gate that greps non-test code for
  `unwrap()`/`expect()`/`panic!` on attacker-reachable paths (the
  `structured_parsers_no_unwrap_expect` gate generalized crate-wide); each becomes a
  fix to fail-closed or handle, plus a fuzz case that would have hit it.
- **AUD-003 — TOCTOU / symlink races / traversal**: the filesystem walk + extract
  paths (pairs SRC-003); a symlink swapped between stat and open; `../` traversal in
  archive member names + remote object keys; assert fail-closed.
- **AUD-004 — SSRF / DNS-rebinding / proxy bypass**: pairs §5; extend to every remote
  source (web/github/gitlab/bitbucket/s3/gcs/azure) + the verifier; assert the
  `--proxy`-only (no ambient env) policy holds on every outbound path.
- **AUD-005 — Arg injection / shell quoting / CRLF / control-char injection**: every
  place keyhog shells out (git, docker save, completion install) or emits to a terminal/
  CI annotation; assert no value can inject an arg, a CRLF log-forge, or an ANSI/control
  escape into output (the GitHub/GitLab annotation + text reporters).
- **AUD-006 — Weak crypto / RNG / constant-time**: the checksum/CRC paths are
  non-secret (fine), but any token comparison, OOB correlation-id minting, or signature
  verification must use a CSPRNG + constant-time compare where it matters; audit + pin.
- **AUD-007 — Algorithmic DoS / catastrophic regex / O(n²)**: every detector regex is
  fuzzed for catastrophic backtracking (the lazy-DFA limit `--regex-dfa-limit` bounds
  it — assert a pathological input stays bounded); any O(n²) in chunk reassembly /
  dedup / structured parse is a Law-7 fix.
- **AUD-008 — Concurrent races / data races**: the DashMap caches (fragment, DNS),
  the global telemetry atomics, the reader pool, the OnceLock degrade warns — run under
  ThreadSanitizer + loom where applicable; assert deterministic counters under parallel
  scan (pairs the skip-counter isolation gate #51).

---

# §12 — PERFORMANCE (measured, before/after, on MACHINE-MATRIX)
- **PERF-001 — Per-hot-path criterion suite**: phase-1 prefilter (alphabet screen +
  bigram bloom), phase-2 RegexSet (`mark_matches` gate, the 30931 ns/call baseline at
  compiled_api.rs:182), AC map, decode-through, structured parse, confidence scoring.
  ns/byte before/after, gate regressions.
- **PERF-002 — Whole-scan throughput on the standard corpus** (Django + kubernetes/
  kubernetes + linux/linux): wall-clock + peak RSS per backend; assert the swarm
  routing fix (SR-005) keeps the kernel scan at ~96s not ~204s; no fallback path is
  more than a rounding error slower than the primary (the SPEED-BOUND law).
- **PERF-003 — GPU throughput on RTX 5090/4090/santhserver**: the GPU region-presence
  path vs SIMD on a big-file-dominated batch; assert GPU wins where calibration says
  it should and the measured router never picks a slower path (autoroute truth).
- **PERF-004 — Cold-start**: `compile()` + first-batch latency; the M-01 embedded
  precompiled blob drops cold-start to deserialize cost; the daemon holds it warm.

---

# §13 — INSTALL FLOW & DOGFOODING (the shipped product, never cargo)
Per the dogfooding doctrine: uninstall everything first, then run the REAL install
flow (install.sh / PowerShell installer / released artifact) interactively via tmux,
judge it as a premium product, then dogfood the produced binary.

- **DOG-001 — Clean install/uninstall round-trip on MACHINE-MATRIX**: uninstall →
  install.sh → doctor self-test exits 0 → scan a real repo → uninstall leaves a clean
  tree (binary, cache, PATH entry, completions all gone). macOS needs `--features
  portable` (no gpu) — the shipped portable binary is SAFE (doctor exits 0); the wgpu
  PIPELINE_CACHE crash only hits `--features gpu` opt-in on Apple Silicon (the
  install-flow memo). Record the tmux transcript + an asserting e2e test.
- **DOG-002 — Route install.sh + install.ps1 core sweep through `calibrate-autoroute`
  (release-gated, #45)**: the installer calibrates the documented presets so a
  post-install scan resolves a decision for every workload class (#39/#33).
- **DOG-003 — Install UX as a finding source**: progress clarity, honest doctor
  self-test, no scary errors, speed, reversibility, checksum/signature verification on
  `update`. Every friction is a finding + a fix.
- **DOG-004 — Heavy real-corpus dogfood**: run the shipped binary over large real
  trees, every subcommand, every output format + flag, every error path, at scale;
  every issue → a failing regression test (the durable artifact) + a fix. The self-scan
  workflow uses the PINNED release with value-dedup suppression (the self-scan memo).

---

# §14 — ARCHITECTURE, DEDUP, COHERENCE, WIRING (standing gates)
- **ARCH-001 — One-way layers**: domain logic (core/scanner) must not import CLI/
  transport/UI; a gate asserting the dependency direction; the
  `engine_match_policy_checksum_owner` gate generalized (confidence + validity
  primitives stay out of `src/engine/`).
- **ARCH-002 — No duplication**: one primitive/schema/parser/constant source; before
  any helper, grep repo-wide; the divergent skip-dir lists (#10), the two index-coupled
  hot-pattern tables (#11), the nested-archive depth caps (unified this session) are the
  model — find and collapse the next duplications.
- **ARCH-003 — Wiring completeness**: every parsed flag / config field / env var /
  computed result reaches operator-visible behavior AND an e2e test; a gate that fails
  on a `ScanArgs` field with no consuming code path (the UTILIZATION vector); the
  CLI-08 long-flag snapshot stays coherent.
- **ARCH-004 — Coherence**: README + `--help` + error messages + JSON fields + exit
  codes + tests + code agree at every commit; a gate cross-checking help text against
  real defaults (pairs the `--max-file-size` "10MB"-vs-100MiB regression #135).
- **ARCH-005 — No silent fallbacks (Law 10 sweep)**: a recurring audit for any
  `.ok()`/`unwrap_or`/`Err(_)=>`/`Option→None→other` that degrades without surfacing;
  each becomes fail-closed or loud+counted. The validator-bypass-on-fast-path bug class
  (the memo) is the template — checksum/hot-pattern/merge/gpudeflate all need the rich
  path on the fast path too.
- **ARCH-006 — Testing contract enforcement**: every shipped rule/feature has positive,
  negative, adversarial, cross-file, proptest-10k, differential, criterion, scale, and
  e2e coverage; a gate flags any contract with `!is_empty`-only assertions (Law 6);
  failing contract tests are findings, never weakened to pass (Law 9).

---

# ===========================================================================
# PART II — GRANULAR, PER-FUNCTION MICRO-POLISH (read-grounded, 1000s of items)
# ===========================================================================
# §1–§14 above are the strategic spine. PART II is the deep pass: every module
# read in full, every public fn / cap / branch / magic constant / SIMD body /
# error string / silent-`.ok()` turned into its own specific micro-task. IDs are
# `µ-<module>-NN`. These are MICRO (one fn, one constant, one branch) but each
# still ships a real test or a real code change — never "look at X". Worked in
# loops: read a module → append its block → next module, until all 421 are done.

## µ-bigram_bloom (`crates/scanner/src/bigram_bloom.rs`, read in full)
- µ-bigram-01: Boundary test for the saturation threshold `popcount*5 >= SLOTS*3`
  (`recompute_saturation`, line 162-166): build tables at popcount = floor(3/5·65536)-1,
  ==, +1 and assert `is_saturated()` flips exactly at 39322 set bits, integer-math (no
  float) so no rounding drift.
- µ-bigram-02: Differential proptest (10k) `maybe_overlaps` vs `scalar_overlaps_reference`
  on NON-saturated random tables + random chunks — assert byte-identical verdict; this
  pins the 4-wide unroll (line 208-224) against the naive walk.
- µ-bigram-03: Soundness invariant test: `maybe_overlaps` NEVER returns false when
  `scalar_overlaps_reference` returns true (no false negatives — the recall guarantee at
  line 51-53); property over random literal sets + chunks.
- µ-bigram-04: `from_literal_prefixes` 1-byte-literal path (line 135-142): assert a
  1-byte prefix sets the whole 256-slot row (4 contiguous `u64::MAX` words via
  `insert_row`) and that `maybe_overlaps` then admits every chunk containing that byte.
- µ-bigram-05: Terminal-extension widening (line 144-153): assert `ghp` populates `gh`,
  `hp`, AND every `p_X` row so `ghp_AB…` is admitted under a truncated prefix; negative:
  a chunk with none of those bigrams is rejected.
- µ-bigram-06: `insert_row` word-alignment proof (line 112-121): assert `(a as usize)<<2`
  indexes exactly the 4 u64 words covering rows `[a<<8 .. a<<8+256)`; boundary at a=0 and
  a=255 (words 1020-1023).
- µ-bigram-07: `chunk.len() < 2` early-admit (line 188-189): a 0- or 1-byte chunk returns
  true (cannot prove clean) — assert, with the smallest possible secret straddling a 1-byte
  window so recall is preserved.
- µ-bigram-08: `saturated_for_test` count check (line 266-274): assert 158 full rows =
  40448 set bits really crosses the 39322 threshold (off-by-one guard on the test helper).
- µ-bigram-09: Perf — criterion `maybe_overlaps` ns/byte on a 1 MiB non-matching buffer;
  assert the ~1 cycle/byte claim (line 32) holds on Zen4 + Apple-M; regression-gate it.
  VERIFY the doc's "L1d-resident 8 KB" claim by asserting `bits` stays `Box<[u64;1024]>`.
- µ-bigram-10: Clone fidelity (line 67-74): assert a cloned bloom has identical `bits` AND
  `saturated`, so a per-thread clone never diverges in the short-circuit.
- µ-bigram-11: VERIFY the "SIMD gather offers no win" claim (line 184-186) with an actual
  AVX2/NEON gather micro-bench; if it DOES win on M-series, that's a perf task, not a
  comment — escalate to §12. (Capability vector: every "no win" is a hypothesis to test.)
- µ-bigram-12: `popcount` (line 232) is called by the PRODUCTION `recompute_saturation`
  (line 164) — it is correctly non-test. Test it directly (all-zero→0, all-set→65536, a
  known bit set→exact count) and assert `recompute_saturation` consumes it. [Gemini Iter-3
  corrected the earlier mis-claim that popcount was test-only — it is NOT.]

## µ-alphabet_filter (`crates/scanner/src/alphabet_filter.rs`, read in full)
- µ-alpha-01: Prefilter-robustness differential proptest (10k): `from_bytes_scalar` vs
  `from_bytes_avx2` vs `from_bytes_sse2` (x86) and vs `from_bytes_neon` (aarch64) — assert
  byte-identical `[u64;4]` mask for random inputs incl. all-0x00..0xFF, empty, 1-byte,
  31/32/33-byte (the chunks_exact(32) seam). The SIMD bodies are retained ONLY for this
  test (line 24-27) — so the test must exist or the bodies are dead (UTILIZATION).
- µ-alpha-02: `screen` AVX2 vs scalar differential (10k): `screen_avx2` vs the scalar
  `any(contains_byte)` on random (target-set, chunk) pairs incl. the 32-byte-chunk +
  remainder seam and the 0x80-0xFF high-half (the `is_128_255` blend, line 248-253).
- µ-alpha-03: INCONSISTENCY polish — `from_bytes` dropped its `is_x86_feature_detected!`
  runtime dispatch (calls scalar directly, line 28-30) but `screen` STILL runtime-dispatches
  to `screen_avx2` (line 182-188). Task: bench whether `screen_avx2` actually beats the
  auto-vectorized scalar `any`; if not, drop the dispatch too (same cleanup that was done to
  `from_bytes`) — symmetry + one less CPUID branch. If it does win, document why screen
  differs from from_bytes. Either way, resolve the asymmetry.
- µ-alpha-04: Case-fold mask correctness (`AlphabetScreen::new`, line 160-174): assert for
  every ASCII alphabetic byte BOTH `b` and `b ^ 0x20` bits are set; a screen built from
  `"GHP"` admits a chunk containing `ghp`; non-alphabetic bytes are NOT case-flipped.
- µ-alpha-05: `screen` empty-chunk returns false (line 178-180) — a genuinely empty chunk
  is "clean" (opposite of bigram's len<2 admit, because screen has no minimum). Pin the
  divergence with a comment-test so the two prefilters' empty-handling is intentional.
- µ-alpha-06: `intersects` / `union` / `contains_byte` truth tables (line 122-141): per-lane
  (4×u64) boundary at byte 63/64/127/128/191/192/255 (lane edges); assert union is
  commutative + idempotent and intersects is symmetric.
- µ-alpha-07: AVX2 `bit_selector` LUT correctness (line 221-225): assert the
  `1,2,4,8,16,32,64,-128` shuffle table maps `v&7` to the right bit; a unit test feeding
  each of 0..8 low-bits.
- µ-alpha-08: NEON body chunks_exact(16) seam (line 49-66): aarch64-only test at 15/16/17
  bytes; assert remainder handling matches scalar. Law 8: this MUST run on the macOS-arm64
  runner, not be `#[ignore]`d.
- µ-alpha-09: SAFETY-doc audit — every `unsafe fn` (from_bytes_{avx2,sse2,neon}, screen_avx2)
  states its target-feature precondition; assert the only callers are behind the matching
  `is_x86_feature_detected!`/`cfg(target_arch)` gate (no unguarded UB call site). Pairs
  AUD-002. The kimi-wave1 findings 6.LOW.*.162/180 referenced in comments — verify closed.
- µ-alpha-10: `from_text` → `from_bytes` UTF-8 byte-equivalence (line 118-120): a multibyte
  string's mask equals its UTF-8 bytes' mask (no char-vs-byte confusion, the hashkit-style
  multibyte bug class from the memo).
- µ-alpha-11: Perf — criterion `screen` ns/byte on a 1 MiB no-target chunk, AVX2 vs scalar,
  on MACHINE-MATRIX; gate regression. Confirms Layer-0 stays cheaper than bigram Layer-0.5.
- µ-alpha-12: Integration — alphabet_screen ∪ bigram_bloom ∪ decode-through fall-through
  (pairs SR-007): a fully-encoded secret rejected by BOTH prefilters must still reach
  decode-through; assert no Layer-0/0.5 short-circuit silently drops it.

## µ-ascii_ci (`crates/scanner/src/ascii_ci.rs`, read in full)
- µ-ci-01: `ascii_is_uppercase` branchless correctness (line 232-234): proptest all 256
  bytes assert `byte.wrapping_sub(b'A') <= 25` ⟺ `byte.is_ascii_uppercase()`; pin the
  wrapping-sub trick can't misclassify control bytes / 0x00 / 0xFF.
- µ-ci-02: `ascii_lower_branchless` (line 225-228) all-256-byte truth table vs
  `byte.to_ascii_lowercase()`; assert non-alpha bytes are untouched and the `<<5` (0x20)
  only flips on uppercase.
- µ-ci-03: AVX2 `write_ascii_lowercase_avx2` vs scalar differential (10k) at len
  127/128/129/160/161 (the `>=128` SIMD gate + 32-byte stride + scalar tail, line 60-65,
  133-146); assert byte-identical lowercase output, tail uninitialized-then-written
  contract holds (MaybeUninit safety).
- µ-ci-04: NEON `write_ascii_lowercase_neon` vs scalar differential at len 63/64/65/80/81
  (the `>=64` gate + 16-byte stride, line 72-77, 184-197); MUST run on macOS-arm64 (Law 8).
- µ-ci-05: `has_ascii_uppercase` AVX2/NEON vs scalar `any` differential (line 50-55,
  151-172, 201-221); the early-`movemask`/`vmaxvq` return must match the scalar `any` incl.
  a single uppercase byte at the very end (scalar tail) and at chunk boundary.
- µ-ci-06: `extend_ascii_lowercase_from` MaybeUninit safety (line 25-36): proptest that
  after the call `dst[old_len..]` equals `src.to_ascii_lowercase()` and `dst.len()` grew by
  exactly `src.len()`; Miri/ASan run to prove no uninit read (set_len soundness).
- µ-ci-07: `ci_find` vs `ci_find_nonempty` empty-needle DIVERGENCE (line 264-296): assert
  `ci_find("", …)`→true (empty matches every offset) but `ci_find_nonempty("", …)`→false;
  this is the user/configured-keyword-list guard (a `""` keyword must NOT match everything).
  Pin the divergence with a comment-test so a future "unify" doesn't reintroduce the bug.
- µ-ci-08: `ci_find_nonempty` memchr2 first-byte skim correctness (line 285-295): a needle
  whose first byte appears in BOTH cases (e.g. `Aa…`) — assert both `memchr2(lower,upper)`
  candidate positions are checked; a haystack with the needle only at the last possible
  offset (`start+n == haystack.len()`) is found; `start+n > len` breaks correctly.
- µ-ci-09: `ends_with_ignore_ascii_case` / `starts_with_ignore_ascii_case` boundary (line
  239-253): suffix/prefix longer than haystack → false; exact-length equal → true; the
  `get(..prefix.len())` None-safety on a short haystack.
- µ-ci-10: `contains_path_segment` trailing-separator SEMANTICS (line 308-329): matches
  `/seg/` and `\seg\` but the `body_end >= bytes.len()` guard (line 318) means a path
  ENDING in the segment with no trailing separator (`/node_modules`) does NOT match. Task:
  decide+test whether a final path segment (a FILE named exactly the segment, or a dir at
  path end) SHOULD match the vendored-tree suppression; if yes, fix the boundary; either
  way pin the chosen semantics with positive+negative tests (this gates vendored-tree
  suppression recall — a real precision/recall lever).
- µ-ci-11: `contains_path_segment` mixed-separator path (line 315): a Windows-on-POSIX path
  `a/b\seg\c` is walked over BOTH `/` and `\` — assert a segment bounded by mismatched
  separators (`/seg\`) matches; case-insensitivity (`/NODE_MODULES/` matches `node_modules`).
- µ-ci-12: `contains_path_segment_two` adjacency (line 333-369): `/a/b/` matches, `/a/x/b/`
  (non-adjacent) does NOT, `/a/b` (no trailing sep) does not; the `total + 3` length
  pre-guard correctness at the minimum matching path.
- µ-ci-13: Allocation-free proof (the whole module's reason for existing, line 5-12): a
  test/bench asserting `ci_find`/`contains_path_segment` allocate ZERO bytes (e.g. via a
  counting allocator) on a hot-path call — the regression these primitives prevent (tens of
  thousands of transient `to_ascii_lowercase()` Strings per chunk).
- µ-ci-14: `#[cfg(any(feature="gpu", test))]` gating audit: the lowercase-materialization
  helpers are GPU-only (the GPU path needs a lowercased buffer); assert a non-gpu non-test
  build still compiles (the path-segment + ci_find primitives are unconditionally
  available) and the gpu-gated ones aren't referenced outside gpu/test (UTILIZATION).

## µ-entropy_fast (`crates/scanner/src/entropy/fast.rs`, read in full)
- µ-entf-01: SIMD↔scalar bit-for-bit agreement (the module's core contract, line 86-90,
  155-159): differential proptest (10k) `shannon_entropy_simd` vs `shannon_entropy_scalar`
  on random buffers — assert agreement to ≤ a few ULPs across AVX512/AVX2/SSE2 (x86) and
  NEON (aarch64, on the macOS-arm64 runner, Law 8). The whole "single histogram + single
  reduction" design exists for this — pin it.
- µ-entf-02: 8-byte null-padding contract (`histogram_8way`, line 77-150): a fully-null
  8-byte chunk subtracts 8 from `active_len` and is skipped; a chunk that merely CONTAINS
  nulls is counted in full; a lone trailing null in the <8 remainder drops out. Truth-table
  test at lengths 7/8/9/15/16 with nulls at each position; assert `active_len` exact.
- µ-entf-03: `entropy_from_histogram` dual-form boundary (line 173-200, KH-28): at
  `active_len` = 254/255/256 assert the table form (`count·log2(count)`, ≤255) and the
  direct form (`-Σ p·log2 p`, >255) yield the SAME entropy to ULPs (the branch must be
  seamless); `active_len==0`→0.0.
- µ-entf-04: `get_log2_table` correctness (line 12-21): assert `table[i] == i·log2(i)` for
  i in 1..256 and `table[0]==0`; OnceLock init is idempotent + thread-safe (race 64 threads).
- µ-entf-05: Distinct-byte ceiling early-exit soundness (`has_high_entropy_fast_scalar`,
  line 272-288, KH-26): a buffer whose FULL distinct-byte count `u` has `log2(u)<threshold`
  returns false WITHOUT the reduction; a buffer at exactly the ceiling does the full calc.
  Adversarial: a long constant run hiding a high-entropy tail must NOT false-negative
  (the reason it counts the whole buffer, not a sample) — pin with a crafted input.
- µ-entf-06: `distinct_byte_count` 256-bit bitset (line 261-267): all-256-distinct → 256;
  single byte → 1; boundary bytes 63/64/127/128/191/192/255 land in the right u64 lane.
- µ-entf-07: AVX2 dispatch requires `avx2 AND fma` (line 54-55): a test/doc-gate asserting
  the runtime probe is a SUPERSET of `shannon_entropy_avx2`'s `#[target_feature(avx2,fma)]`
  — an AVX2-without-FMA VM must fall to SSE2, never SIGILL. Same for AVX512f+bw+dq (line
  48-50) — assert the `dq` over-gate is intentional headroom (KH C10/M9), documented.
- µ-entf-08: REGRESSION guard for the removed polynomial log2 (line 161-166): a test that
  would FAIL if a future edit reintroduces a vectorized minimax-poly reduction diverging
  >1e-4 bits/byte from `entropy_from_histogram` near a gate threshold (the 5e-3 drift that
  flipped a gate). Encodes "soundness over micro-opt that was never on the hot path".
- µ-entf-09: `shannon_entropy_simd` empty/1-byte (line 27-29, 208-210): empty→0.0; a single
  byte→0.0 (one symbol, log2(1)=0); two distinct bytes→1.0 bit/byte exactly.
- µ-entf-10: Perf — criterion entropy ns/byte on a 64 KiB high-entropy window per ISA
  (AVX512/AVX2/SSE2/NEON/scalar) on MACHINE-MATRIX; assert the SIMD path beats scalar and
  gate regressions; VERIFY the "reduction is 0.0004% of work" claim (line 165).
- µ-entf-11: `histogram_8way` 8-accumulator correctness (line 99-147): assert the 8 partial
  histograms sum to the true per-byte counts (no double-count, no drop at the chunk seam);
  proptest random data; the memory-bound-8-chain rationale (KH-27) — bench 8-way vs 1-way.
- µ-entf-12: Architecture-fallback parity (line 66-75): the non-x86/non-aarch64
  `shannon_entropy_simd` == `shannon_entropy_scalar` (it IS scalar); a CI matrix row on a
  third arch (or a forced-scalar build) proving the generic path is exercised, not just x86.
- µ-entf-13: `#[cfg(test)]` gating audit: `has_high_entropy_fast`, `distinct_byte_count`,
  `has_high_entropy_fast_scalar` are test-only — confirm the PRODUCTION early-exit lives
  elsewhere (entropy/scanner.rs) and these are genuinely test-mirrors, or promote them if a
  prod path needs the cheap ceiling check (UTILIZATION — a test-only sound early-exit that
  prod doesn't use is a left-on-the-table perf win, escalate to §12).
- µ-entf-14: Cross-check `shannon_entropy_*` against the `--entropy-threshold` default 4.5
  bits/byte (pairs CLI-008): a value at 4.49/4.50/4.51 bits straddles the gate — assert the
  entropy fn's output drives the gate decision identically on every ISA (no ISA-dependent
  flip near 4.5, the exact failure the bit-for-bit contract prevents).

## µ-detector_ids (`crates/scanner/src/detector_ids.rs`, read in full)
- µ-did-01: WHOLE-CORPUS classification test (the load-bearing one): load all 904 detector
  TOMLs and assert each id classifies EXACTLY once across `is_generic_detector`,
  `is_entropy_detector`, `is_private_key_fallback`, `is_service_anchored_detector` (line
  44-74) — no service detector accidentally reads as generic/entropy (which would wrongly
  exclude it from the CF-002 named-detector anchor floor) and vice versa. This directly
  gates recall + precision; a misclassification is a silent confidence bug.
- µ-did-02: `is_service_anchored_detector` == `!generic && !entropy && !private_key_fallback`
  (line 70-74) is the `is_named_detector` predicate from confidence/policy.rs — assert the
  two definitions agree (one source of truth, no drift); a detector named `generic-foo` is
  NOT service-anchored, `aws-access-key` IS.
- µ-did-03: Prefix predicates (line 44-52): `is_generic_detector` keys on `generic-` prefix,
  `is_entropy_detector` on exact `entropy` OR `entropy-` prefix; adversarial ids
  (`generic`, `entropyfoo`, `my-generic-x`, `entropy-`) classify per the exact rule; pin
  that a detector literally named `entropy` (no dash) IS an entropy detector (line 51).
- µ-did-04: `#[cfg(feature="simdsieve")]`-gated id consts (line 30-40: OPENAI_API_KEY,
