  SENDGRID_API_KEY, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_USER_TOKEN, SQUARE_ACCESS_TOKEN) — coherence
  test: when `simdsieve` is OFF these consts aren't compiled, but the detector TOMLs still
  ship; assert the non-simdsieve build still SCANS those detectors (the const is only a
  scanner-logic reference, not the detector's existence) — a missing const must not silently
  drop the detector. Pairs the simdsieve-macos-ci memo.
- µ-did-05: `is_generic_or_private_key_detector` / `is_generic_or_entropy_detector` /
  combinator correctness (line 59-67): truth table over the 4 base classes; these gate the
  "keep full gate stack" decision in confidence/policy (weak-anchor/generic/private-key keep
  the full suppression stack) — assert each combinator matches its definition.
- µ-did-06: `is_private_key_block_detector` Result-returning delegate (line 76-79): it
  returns `Result<bool,String>` from `detector_classification` — assert the Err path is
  surfaced (not `.unwrap_or(false)` silently), and pin which detector ids are private-key
  BLOCK detectors vs the single `private-key` FALLBACK (line 22, 55-57) — these are
  different and conflating them changes PEM-block handling.
- µ-did-07: Const-string single-source audit: the hardcoded ids (AWS_ACCESS_KEY, GITHUB_*,
  STRIPE_*, …) must each equal an actual `detectors/<id>.toml` filename (post the
  filename==id rename gate); a test that every const here resolves to a loaded detector, so
  a rename can't leave a dangling const (COHERENCE + the filename==id gate).
- µ-did-08: GENERIC_* id coverage (line 6-10): generic-secret/keyword-secret/api-key/
  password/database-url each exist as detectors and each is `is_generic_detector`; the
  generic-keyword-secret + generic-password are the CredData low-entropy recall levers
  (pairs CF-007/CLI-008) — assert they're wired to `generic_secret_confidence`.

## µ-unicode_hardening (`crates/scanner/src/unicode_hardening.rs`, read in full)
> **SATURATION VERDICT (2026-07-04): µ-uni-01/02/03 are ALREADY COVERED by existing integration tests — do NOT add duplicates (ONE-PLACE / no-redundant-tests).** µ-uni-01 (per-entry map completeness + fold agreement, no-drift) = `tests/homoglyph_normalize_consistency.rs` walks EVERY cyrillic/greek/fullwidth glyph in the expand map asserting the normalize fold agrees, AND ratchets the known DR-317 divergences (shrinking waiver list) — plus `unit/unicode_hardening_normalization.rs:41` asserts `cyrillic_to_latin('a')==None` (ASCII untouched, µ-uni-01 clause 2). µ-uni-02 (adversarial confusable coverage) = `tests/unicode_confusable_runner.rs` mutates companion-context with confusables and asserts detectors still fire. µ-uni-03 (Cow::Borrowed zero-alloc on clean ASCII) = `regression_unicode_hardening_strip.rs` (lines 136/263/270/281) + `regression_unicode_evasion_norm.rs` (131/144/228) assert `matches!(out, Cow::Borrowed(_))`, plus `perf_alloc_perchunk.rs`. The ONLY genuinely-open homoglyph work is the DR-317 cyrillic/greek TABLE reconciliation (shrink a fold-vs-expand-map waiver) — delicate + differential-bench-gated (prior DR-317 change was reflex-reverted then exonerated only by per-detector `bench analyze`, see [[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]]), NOT a quick test add.
> **DR-317 IMPLEMENTABLE SPEC + WHY IT'S CONCURRENT-BLOCKED (traced 2026-07-04):** The bare map-merge (add С→C/ν→v/Ь→b/н→h/п→n/м→m to `homoglyph.rs` expand map + `unicode_hardening` fold) is ALREADY BENCH-REJECTED — mirror precision collapsed 0.9945→0.7465 (+918 FP) because these 6 confusables are COMMON in Cyrillic/Greek prose and their ASCII targets (C/v/b/h/n/m) are common in detector anchors, so anchors match prose (`homoglyph_normalize_consistency.rs:35-42`). There is no map-subset shortcut (all 6 targets are common). The real fix is COUPLED: (1) add the 6 mappings to the expand map, (2) add a credential-SHAPE suppression gate — a finding whose matched anchor region contains a non-ASCII homoglyph char (detectable at scoring time via `unicode_hardening::normalized_char`/`contains_evasion` on the matched bytes) is penalized/suppressed UNLESS the credential value is credential-shaped (length/entropy), so prose collisions drop while evasion recall survives; (3) shrink `KNOWN_DIVERGENCES` in lockstep (the ratchet forces it); (4) differential-bench mirror+CredData per-detector. **This wiring adds a homoglyph-variant signal to `ConfidenceSignals` (`confidence/signals.rs`) + `confidence/mod.rs::compute_confidence`. That subsystem is under HEAVY active concurrent refactor RIGHT NOW — `git diff --stat` (2026-07-04 21:26): `confidence/signals.rs` +96, `confidence/penalties.rs` +117, `confidence/prefixes.rs` +136, `entropy/mod.rs` +241, `entropy/keywords.rs` +192, `entropy/scanner.rs` +178, `ml_scorer/ml_features.rs` +237, `ml_weights.rs` +103 (~1800 line-changes / 15 files). (NB: this is NOT DR-205/206 — those are narrow visibility fixes elsewhere; the collision is the broad uncommitted confidence/entropy/ML refactor.) So DR-317 is CONCURRENT-BLOCKED by EVIDENCE, not merely hard: editing `signals.rs`/`compute_confidence` now would corrupt the concurrent agent's in-flight ConfidenceSignals refactor (binding: never destroy concurrent uncommitted work). SAME BLOCK applies to the entropy-precision arc (the real top CredData FP lever) — it lives in `entropy/mod.rs`/`keywords.rs`/`ml_features.rs`, all under the same active refactor. Pick both up once that refactor lands + is committed. Non-colliding prep already shipped: the consistency-ratchet gate guards the 6 waivers so no NEW drift sneaks in meanwhile.
- µ-uni-01: Homoglyph map completeness + round-trip (`cyrillic_to_latin` 40 entries line
  418-455, `greek_to_latin` 26 entries line 458-488): per entry, a test that the homoglyph
  in a credential body (`АKIA…` with Cyrillic А) normalizes to the Latin form and the
  detector then FIRES; assert no Latin char maps to itself by accident; the fb-prefilter-
  homoglyph memo says homoglyph variants are 43% of scan — so this recall path is hot.
- µ-uni-02: Homoglyph COVERAGE gap hunt (Capability vector): the maps are hand-curated;
  a property test feeding the full Unicode confusables list (or unicode-security crate)
  asserting keyhog normalizes every confusable that maps to an ASCII alnum used in
  credential bodies — every MISS is a detector-evasion bypass → a fix to extend the map
  (or move it to a Tier-B `data/homoglyphs.toml` so it's community-extensible, like the
  evasion anchors). This is a real, open evasion surface.
- µ-uni-03: `normalize_homoglyphs` Cow fast-path (line 142-148): pure-ASCII → `Borrowed`
  (zero alloc); homoglyph/zero-width/RTL present → `Owned`; assert a counting allocator
  sees ZERO allocations on clean ASCII (the hot path), and the rebuilt string is correct.
- µ-uni-04: `ascii_normalization_scan` tri-state (line 216-226): CleanAscii (all <0x80,
  no evasion controls), EvasiveAscii (a <0x20 byte that isn't \n\r\t), NonAscii (≥0x80);
  boundary at 0x1F/0x20/0x7F/0x80 and each of \n\r\t; this routes the fast vs slow path.
- µ-uni-05: `strip_interior_evasion_controls` does NOT corrupt structure (line 311-391):
  TSV columns (`a\tb`), indentation (`\n\tkey`), and CRLF (`\r\n`) must pass through
  UNCHANGED (Cow::Borrowed); only `AKIA\tQYLP…` (control flanked by credential bytes AFTER
  a boundary-matched anchor) is stripped. Adversarial: a `\t` inside a credential but NOT
  after a known anchor is NOT stripped. This is the precise middle path — pin both failure
  extremes (blanket-strip corruption vs preserve-evasion).
- µ-uni-06: `strip_interior_evasion_controls` O(n²) bound (`MAX_BODY_WINDOW=256`, line
  343): a pathological input (an anchor followed by a megabyte of credential bytes) must
  stay bounded — assert the per-anchor walk caps at 256; Law 7 (algorithmic DoS).
- µ-uni-07: Anchor word-boundary (`is_anchor_start_blocked_by`, line 351): `xAKIA…`
  (anchor mid-identifier) is NOT anchored; `key=AKIA…` IS; start-of-text IS. Pin so a
  false anchor doesn't strip controls inside an unrelated identifier.
- µ-uni-08: Tier-B evasion-anchors panic-on-malformed (`EVASION_ANCHORS`, line 242-253;
  `parse_evasion_anchors` line 255-274): a malformed/empty/duplicate `evasion-anchors.toml`
  PANICS the build (refuses to run with weakened evasion truth — Law 10, no silent
  degrade); test `parse_evasion_anchors` rejects empty entry, duplicate, empty list, and
  accepts a valid file. The bundled `data/evasion-anchors.toml` parses (a CI gate).
- µ-uni-09: `EVASION_ANCHOR_AC` degrade is LOUD (line 278-294): if the AC fails to build
  it calls `warn_prefilter_disabled` and returns None (strip becomes a no-op) — assert the
  warn fires (not a silent None); and that a None AC means `strip_interior_evasion_controls`
  returns Borrowed (recall-preserving identity, the scan still sees raw text).
- µ-uni-10: `fullwidth_to_ascii` offset math (line 496-509): every fullwidth U+FF01..FF5E
  maps to its ASCII (code - 0xFEE0); `ｇｈｐ` → `ghp`; out-of-range fullwidth (U+FF5F..FFEF)
  is kept as-is; the `from_u32(...).unwrap_or(ch)` identity-on-failure (Law 10).
- µ-uni-11: Zero-width / RTL / separator / combining sets (line 517-565): each listed
  codepoint is detected by its predicate; a credential split by U+200B (ZWSP) or U+00A0
  (NBSP) is rejoined on the normalization path and the detector fires; an RTL override
  U+202E is dropped. One assertion per codepoint (≈40 codepoints) — these are the evasion
  surface, each is a recall test.
- µ-uni-12: `detect_unicode_attacks` vs `normalize_homoglyphs` consistency (line 49-135 vs
  157-176): every char `detect_unicode_attacks` flags with a `replacement: Some(x)` must be
  the same `x` `normalized_char` produces; every `replacement: None` flag must be a `Drop`.
  Detection and normalization must not disagree (the doc at line 108-110 calls this out).
- µ-uni-13: `full_normalize` NFC+homoglyph order (line 229-232): a decomposed `é`
  (e + U+0301) is NFC-composed THEN homoglyph-normalized; assert the order doesn't drop a
  combining mark that NFC would have composed (decompose-then-strip would lose the accent).
- µ-uni-14: `contains_evasion` matches the union of every predicate (line 394-406): a
  proptest that `contains_evasion(t)` ⟺ `!normalize_homoglyphs(t).is_borrowed-equivalent`
  OR an ascii evasion control is present — i.e. the cheap pre-check never misses a char the
  normalizer would transform (a missed evasion = a silent recall gap).
- µ-uni-15: `is_credential_body_byte` charset (line 297-299): alnum + `_+/=.-`; assert it
  covers base64 (`+/=`), base64url (`-_`), and dotted/JWT (`.`) bodies so the interior-strip
  candidate gate matches every credential shape; a space or `;` is NOT a body byte.
- µ-uni-16: UTF-8 validity after strip (line 388-391): dropping standalone ASCII control
  bytes from valid UTF-8 yields valid UTF-8 — proptest random UTF-8 with interior controls,
  assert `from_utf8` always succeeds (the `unwrap_or` identity never actually triggers, but
  is the Law-10 safety net — assert it's truly unreachable for ASCII-only drops).
- µ-uni-17: Offset preservation: `EvasionMatch.position` is the exact BYTE position in the
  original text (line 38-39, 52) — assert a homoglyph at a known byte offset reports that
  offset so a finding's line/column maps back correctly after normalization (pairs the
  windowed/line-mapping invariants ST-009/SR-008).
- µ-uni-18: Perf — `normalize_homoglyphs` / `strip_interior_evasion_controls` ns/byte on a
  1 MiB clean-ASCII buffer (must be ~memchr-cheap, Borrowed) vs a homoglyph-laden buffer;
  criterion gate on MACHINE-MATRIX. The whole module must be near-free on the common case.

## µ-token_randomness (`crates/scanner/src/suppression/token_randomness.rs`, read in full)
- µ-tok-01: `RANDOM_LOGPROB_THRESHOLD=-6.85` boundary (line 62): build values whose mean
  bigram log-prob is -6.84/-6.85/-6.86 and assert `is_random_token` flips exactly at the
  threshold (`<=`); use the real `data/english_bigram_logprob.bin` model so the calibration
  gap (~-6.7 separation, random ≤-7, identifiers ≥-6.4) is pinned with real CredData-shaped
  inputs (`gjbubxsu` random vs `getuserconfig` identifier).
- µ-tok-02: `MIN_ALPHA=6` fail-safe (line 55, 106): a value with <6 alphabetic chars →
  `mean_bigram_logprob`==None → `is_random_token`==false (keep suppressing, fail toward
  precision); boundary at 5/6/7 alpha chars.
- µ-tok-03: `MIN_DISTINCT_LETTERS=3` repetitive-pattern guard (line 74, 126): `aaaaaaaa`,
  `xzxzxzxz`, `qqqqwwww` (≤2 distinct) → NOT random despite improbable bigrams; `ttqqrqjt`
  (4 distinct, a real recovered CredData password) → random. Boundary at 2/3/4 distinct.
  Pin that all 1285 recovered passwords have ≥4 distinct (the calibration claim, line 71-73).
- µ-tok-04: `BIGRAM_LOGPROB` model load assert (line 36-49): the `.bin` MUST be exactly
  676*4 bytes; a CI gate that `data/english_bigram_logprob.bin` parses + the assert message
  names `ml/gen_bigram_model.py`; little-endian f32 decode correctness (spot-check a few cells
  vs the generator).
- µ-tok-05: `analyze` run-splitting on non-alpha (line 101-104): a digit/symbol ENDS the
  alphabetic run (`prev=None`), so `abc1def` scores `ab,bc` + `de,ef` (no `c?d` cross-run
  pair); assert pair count + that an all-digit value yields None.
- µ-tok-06: `keep_word_separated_gate_with_randomness` shape pre-gate (line 203-217): any
  digit/uppercase/non-ASCII byte → return true (stay suppressed) WITHOUT consulting the
  English model — the acronym/product-key class (`d2i_PKCS7_bio`, `2iw9-n01w-Mc4V`, line
  199-200) the model would mis-lift; an all-lowercase `abxnj_gjvpuqzo` reaches the randomness
  verdict. Pin the 141-password recovery vs every acronym suppressed.
- µ-tok-07: `keep_identifier_gate_with_randomness` vs word-separated DIVERGENCE (line 183-217):
  the contiguous gate trusts `is_random_token` directly (its predicates reject digits), the
  word-separated gate adds the lowercase-shape pre-gate — assert the two are NOT interchangeable
  (a `d2i_PKCS7` value: word-sep keeps suppressed, but feeding it to the contiguous gate would
  mis-lift) so a future "unify" can't collapse them.
- µ-tok-08: `TokenRandomness::evidence_for` ptr-eq memo (line 146-154): the same-pointer
  same-len fast path returns the cached `candidate_evidence`; a DIFFERENT value re-analyzes;
  assert the memo never returns stale evidence for a different value (correctness of the
  `std::ptr::eq` + len guard).
- µ-tok-09: Single-source-of-truth contract (line 171-188): both `phase2_generic_shape`
  (scan-time) and `suppression::api::suppress_named_detector_finding` (post-process) must wrap
  the SAME `is_random_token` — a differential test feeding identical values to both paths and
  asserting byte-for-byte the same lift/suppress decision (no second copy of the model).
- µ-tok-10: Recall+precision regression pin (the lever's whole point, line 22-26): a
  CredData-subset test asserting the gated lift recovers the random-password TPs WITHOUT the
  +3554 FP the unconditional lift caused (precision must stay ≥ the documented 0.60, not drop
  to 0.40). This is the bench-verified contract — encode it so a refactor can't silently
  regress precision.
- µ-tok-11: `mean_bigram_logprob` exact value spot-check: for a known short value compute the
  expected mean by hand from a few model cells and assert `analyze` matches to f32 ULPs
  (guards an indexing bug in `table[p*26+idx]`).
- µ-tok-12: Non-ASCII / mixed-case handling in `analyze` (line 92-94): uppercase is folded
  via `to_ascii_lowercase` before indexing; a non-ASCII alphabetic char (`é`) is NOT
  `is_ascii_alphabetic` so it breaks the run — assert the unicode path doesn't panic or
  mis-index (pairs unicode_hardening normalization upstream).

## µ-scan_state (`crates/scanner/src/scan_state.rs`, read in full)
- µ-ss-01: Capped-heap displacement (`push_match`, line 342-366): with `limit=N`, pushing
  N+1 candidates keeps the N best (BinaryHeap root = worst retained, replaced when a better
  arrives); assert exact retained set across confidence/severity/detector/credential/offset
  tie-break order (`RawMatchPriority::cmp_raw_match`, line 111-135). Boundary at limit-1/limit/
  limit+1.
- µ-ss-02: Cross-lane identity dedup (`claimed_match_identities`, line 343-345): the simdsieve
  hot-prefix lane and confirmed regex lane both producing the SAME (detector_id, credential,
  offset) must claim ONE heap slot, not two — assert duplicates don't consume
  `max_matches_per_chunk` capacity (the doc at line 216-221). Plant a value both lanes fire on.
- µ-ss-03: `replace_claimed_match_if_better` keeps the better (line 368-388): a second
  candidate with the same identity but higher confidence REPLACES; lower confidence does NOT;
  assert the heap is rebuilt correctly (no lost/duplicated entries after the `into_vec`/`from`).
- µ-ss-04: `push_match_lazy` build-on-admit (line 402-444): the `build` closure is invoked
  ONLY when the candidate would actually enter/replace (priority pre-check via
  `claimed_priority_would_replace`), proving the perf claim "avoid detector metadata refcount
  bumps for discarded candidates" (line 96-98). Assert build is NOT called for a candidate
  below the full heap's worst.
- µ-ss-05: `into_matches` dedup + ordering (line 452-472): two engines producing the same
  finding (ac literal + homoglyph variant, line 446-451) collapse to ONE, keeping the
  highest-confidence; final order is canonical best-first; the `len()<=1` skip-dedup fast path
  (line 460-462) is correct (no duplicate possible). Assert stable grouping preserves best.
- µ-ss-06: `intern_credential` dedup (line 235-243): identical credentials share one
  `SensitiveString` allocation; assert the interned value zeroizes (SensitiveString contract)
  and a hit returns a clone without re-alloc.
- µ-ss-07: `intern_metadata` two-tier lookup (line 253-265): a frozen detector-metadata string
  hits the `StaticInterner` (vyre CHD, O(1), no alloc); a dynamic string (file path, commit
  SHA) falls to the per-scan `HashSet<Arc<str>>`; assert no double-allocation (the
  `HashSet<Arc<str>>` not `HashMap<String,Arc<str>>` optimization, line 204-209).
- µ-ss-08: `model_authoritative` semantics (line 40-48, 74-91): an entropy-authoritative
  pending match takes the MoE score DIRECTLY (not `max(heuristic, ml)`), so a high-entropy
  non-secret (FQDN, git SHA, base64 blob) the model suppresses is NOT floored by its entropy
  heuristic; a detector candidate sets it false and keeps the floor. Differential: same value
  via both constructors yields different finals. Pairs CF-005.
- µ-ss-09: `RawMatchPriority::cmp_raw_match` Law-10 `unwrap_or(0.0)` (line 112-113): an
  absent-confidence candidate orders as 0.0 for HEAP placement only but remains eligible (not
  dropped) — assert a None-confidence finding still enters an unfull heap and is emitted.
- µ-ss-10: `MatchIdentity` (borrowed) vs `OwnedMatchIdentity` (owned) equivalence (line 149-192):
  assert `From<&RawMatch>` for both yields the same (detector_id, credential, offset) identity
  and that `from_priority` matches `from(&raw_match)` for the same logical match (the lazy path
  must agree with the eager path on identity).
- µ-ss-11: `ml_context_for_candidate` window (line 12-19): the context is
  `local_context_window(text, line, ML_CONTEXT_RADIUS_LINES)` prefixed with `file:<path>` when
  a path exists; assert the radius is honored and the path prefix is present/absent correctly
  (feeds the ML scorer — a wrong window changes scores).
- µ-ss-12: `for_each_named_pending_ml_line` filter (line 327-338): only NON-generic/entropy
  detectors' lines are visited (uses `is_generic_or_entropy_detector`); assert a generic
  pending match's line is skipped and a named one is visited (pairs detector_ids classification).
- µ-ss-13: `with_static_intern` vs `default` (line 271-276): a ScanState built with the static
  interner consults it first; a `default()` one (stand-alone unit tests) has `static_intern:
  None` and only uses the per-scan HashSet — assert both produce identical findings (interner
  is a perf detail, not a detection one).
- µ-ss-14: Concurrency: `static_intern` is `Arc`, lock-free on read (line 222-227) — a
  ThreadSanitizer test running N rayon workers each with a ScanState sharing one StaticInterner,
  asserting no data race + identical interned Arcs.

## µ-tuning (`crates/scanner/src/tuning.rs`, read in full)
- µ-tun-01: EVERY toggle's parity test exists + passes (the module's core claim — recall is
  identical for all 14 levers, line 11-15): a meta-test/gate asserting each named parity test
  is present and green: `fallback_prefilter_hs_large_parity` (hs_max_len),
  `prefilter_truncate_parity`, `decode_focus_parity`, `homoglyph_ascii_skip_parity`,
  no-candidate-gate parity, confirmed-suffix-gate, phase2-anchor, phase2-prefix-gate,
  phase2-localizer, phase2-reverse-order-independence, gpu_recall_floor, gpu_moe_timeout,
  phase2_hs, homoglyph_gate. A toggle without a green parity test is an unproven recall claim.
- µ-tun-02: `BoolOverride` round-trip (line 28-56): `from_option(None/Some(true)/Some(false))`
  → `Default/ForceOn/ForceOff`; `from_raw` of an out-of-range byte → `Default` (defensive);
  `resolve(default)` returns default/true/false correctly. Truth table over {0,1,2,99} × {true,
  false} default.
- µ-tun-03: Sentinel encodings (line 151-158, 208-210, 385-388): `hs_max_len==usize::MAX` →
  `HS_PREFILTER_MAX_LEN_DEFAULT`; `gpu_moe_timeout_ms==0` → `GPU_MOE_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT`;
  assert `set_*(None)` stores the sentinel and `resolve()` maps it back to the compiled default
  (the Law-10-documented default sentinels, not error fallbacks).
- µ-tun-04: Per-scanner isolation (the parallel-test safety contract, line 5-8, 58-63): two
  `ScannerTuning` instances with different overrides do NOT see each other's state (AtomicU8
  per-instance, no global); a test flipping a toggle on scanner A leaves scanner B at default.
  This is why parity tests can run in parallel — pin it.
- µ-tun-05: `resolve()` snapshot consistency (line 150-188): the snapshot reads every atomic
  once into a plain `ResolvedRuntimeTuningConfig`; assert a mutation AFTER snapshot is NOT
  observed by that snapshot (the scan uses a frozen view), and a mutation BEFORE is (test hooks,
  line 148-149).
- µ-tun-06: `apply_config` round-trips every field (line 128-143): build a `ScannerTuningConfig`
  with each field set, apply, `resolve()`, assert every value propagated (14 fields — a field
  added to the struct but missing from apply_config/resolve is a silent wiring gap, COHERENCE).
- µ-tun-07: `phase2_localizer` default OFF (line 351-358): assert
  `FALLBACK_LOCALIZER_DEFAULT==false` and the doc rationale (per-chunk AC overhead is a net
  loss on decode-heavy inputs) — a perf bench proving localizer ON is slower end-to-end on a
  decode-recursion corpus (VERIFY the "net loss" claim, escalate to §12 if it's actually a win).
- µ-tun-08: `homoglyph_ascii_skip` default ON recall-safety (line 74-76, 247-254): the
  differential `homoglyph_ascii_skip_parity` proves skipping homoglyph variants on a pure-ASCII
  chunk drops NO finding — BUT the fb-prefilter-homoglyph memo says homoglyph variants are 43%
  of scan and skip-on-ASCII was "refuted by the differential gate (drops generic-password)".
  RECONCILE: is the skip actually recall-safe NOW (post the overlapping-AC trigger fix, line
  75-76) or does it still drop generic-password? A differential test with a generic-password on
  a pure-ASCII chunk MUST prove zero loss, or the default is a recall bug. HIGH PRIORITY.
- µ-tun-09: `prefilter_truncate` {N,}→{N} soundness (line 273-281): the truncated prefilter is
  a SUPERSET marking gate (extraction with the full pattern filters) — assert a value matching
  only the full `{N,}` (longer than N) is still found (the superset marks it, full regex
  confirms); recall-identical to truncate-off.
- µ-tun-10: `confirmed_suffix_gate` / `phase2_prefix_gate` skip-soundness (line 286-291, 313-328):
  each gate ONLY skips patterns whose required suffix/prefix literal is provably absent (so they
  cannot match) — assert a pattern WITH its literal present is never skipped, one without is, and
  a value that needs the skipped pattern is still found via another. Adversarial: a pattern whose
  literal-absence proof is wrong = a recall bug.
- µ-tun-11: `decode_focus` window soundness (line 295-309): the focused phase-2 pass scans only
  a window around freshly-decoded text, not the whole spliced parent — assert a secret in the
  decoded region IS found and the window is wide enough that a secret straddling the decode seam
  isn't lost (pairs SR-007 decode-through).
- µ-tun-12: `no_candidate_gate` SWE-101 soundness (line 87-91, 332-341): the gate skips a chunk
  ONLY when positively proven no always-active pattern can fire — the parity test forces it OFF
  on one scanner; assert identical findings. A false "no candidate" proof = silent recall loss
  (Law 10) — adversarial test with a candidate the gate must NOT skip.
- µ-tun-13: `gpu_moe_timeout_ms` loud-on-timeout (line 382-388): a MoE readback timeout surfaces
  loudly AND the caller falls back to CPU MoE for the same candidates (not silent drop) — assert
  the timeout path records + recomputes on CPU (Law 10). Bounded-latency knob, not a detection
  toggle.
- µ-tun-14: `gpu_recall_floor` default OFF + loud recovery (line 360-378): default OFF (GPU pays
  CPU triggers only for host-only detectors); when ON, the shared CPU trigger net recovers any
  GPU under-fire and reports the recovery LOUDLY — assert on a GPU host (Law 8) that enabling it
  surfaces any region-presence under-fire rather than silently missing it. Pairs M-02 parity.
- µ-tun-15: `phase2_reverse` order-independence (line 264-269): reversing phase-2 extraction
  order must yield the IDENTICAL final finding set — a differential proving the scan result is
  independent of extraction order (a diagnostic that catches order-dependent dedup bugs).

## FINDING (Iter 2, HIGH) — structured decode-through is defeated by WINDOWING, not the 2 MiB cap
Reading the call chain proved the `MAX_STRUCTURED_PARSE_BYTES = 2 MiB` cap in
`structured::preprocess` is **unreachable on the scan path**: `scan_with_deadline_and_backend`
routes any chunk >`MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES` (1 MiB, compiled_api.rs:533) to `scan_windowed`,
which slices into ≤1 MiB windows (`window_chunk`, windowed.rs:38-39) and calls
`prepare_chunk`→`preprocess` per WINDOW (≤1 MiB). So `preprocess` never sees >2 MiB; the
2 MiB branch (and its #52 oversize counter) cannot fire from a real scan. The ACTUAL
recall gap is structural and bigger:
- **ST-011 (fix) — structured/config files >1 MiB lose decode-through to windowing.** A k8s
  Secret / docker-compose / tfstate / notebook larger than 1 MiB is split into 1 MiB windows
  BEFORE `detect_and_parse`, so: (a) a base64 `data:` value straddling a window seam decodes
  to garbage or not at all; (b) the `kind: Secret` sniff in window 1 is divorced from its
  `data:` block in window 2; (c) each window re-runs format detection on a fragment. FIX:
  recognize structured/config formats and scan them WHOLE (exempt from the 1 MiB window split),
  OR run `structured::preprocess` on the full file text BEFORE windowing and window the
  synthesized text. Test: a 3 MiB k8s Secret whose base64 `data:` secret sits at byte ~1.5 MiB
  (a window seam) — assert the decoded secret is found (today it is NOT). This is the real #22
  follow-on.
- **ST-012 (verify) — does the FILESYSTEM source window before the engine?** Read
  `crates/sources/src/filesystem/reader.rs`: if the reader emits ≤1 MiB windowed chunks for a
  large `.yaml`, structured decode-through is already broken at the SOURCE (the engine never
  sees the whole file). Determine the real chunk size a >1 MiB config file arrives as.
- **ST-013 (decide) — the 2 MiB cap + #52 oversize counter are dead-or-near-dead.** Either
  (a) make structured files reach preprocess whole (ST-011), at which point the 2 MiB cap
  becomes a REAL reachable DoS bound worth surfacing (the #52 counter then earns its keep), or
  (b) if structured files stay windowed, the 2 MiB cap is unreachable dead code — remove it and
  the #52 counter (Law 11 utilization). The #52 `detect_format` refactor (decoupling detection
  from parsing) is a genuine DRY win and stays regardless. DO NOT commit the #52 counter as a
  "fix" until ST-011 makes it reachable — committing a counter that never increments is
  misleading telemetry (Law 10 inverse: a loud signal that can't fire is noise).
- µ-NOTE: the original ST-001 ("assert oversize counter fires via a scan") is RETRACTED — it
  would be a false test (the scan path can't reach the branch). The honest test belongs to
  ST-011 (windowing defeats decode-through), asserting a real recall miss + its fix.

## µ-types (`crates/scanner/src/types.rs`, read in full)
- µ-ty-01: `WINDOW_OVERLAP_BYTES=128 KiB` ≥ longest detectable secret (line 20-25): assert the
  overlap exceeds the max secret length (PEM RSA-8192, large JWT, concatenated multiline) so no
  secret straddling a window seam is lost; a property test planting a max-length secret at every
  seam offset. THE invariant for SR-008/SRC-001 — pin the exact margin.
- µ-ty-02: `MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES=1 MiB` vs `MAX_STRUCTURED_PARSE_BYTES=2 MiB` ORDERING (line 18,
  + structured/mod.rs): a gate asserting the window cap < the structured cap means structured
  preprocess never sees an oversize chunk — encode the ST-011 finding as a failing test that the
  two caps are mis-ordered for structured decode-through.
- µ-ty-03: `LARGE_FALLBACK_SCAN_THRESHOLD=10_000` (line 9): a chunk >10 KB switches fallback
  regex to per-line mode; assert a secret on a single line >10 KB in is still found (per-line
  preserves recall) and a secret split across lines in a >10 KB blob; boundary at 9999/10000/10001.
- µ-ty-04: `MAX_WINDOW_DEDUP_ENTRIES=100_000` bound (line 11-13): scanning a repo with >100k
  duplicate credential-like strings caps the dedup set (no unbounded memory); assert the cap holds
  and dedup still collapses duplicates within it (Law 7 DoS bound).
- µ-ty-05: `MIN_LITERAL_PREFIX_CHARS=3` (line 32): a 1-2 char literal prefix is rejected as an AC
  anchor (matches too many positions); assert a detector with a 2-char prefix routes to phase-2
  not AC, and a 3-char prefix anchors; boundary at len 2/3.
- µ-ty-06: `REGEX_SIZE_LIMIT_BYTES=1 MiB` DFA cap + `--regex-dfa-limit` override (line 49-84): a
  pathological state-heavy pattern stays bounded at the cap (falls to NFA, not unbounded growth);
  `set_regex_dfa_limit(0)` resets to default; `regex_dfa_limit_default()` reports the real compiled
  cap (not "off"); a property test that the override is honored. VERIFY the "1 MiB vs 64 KiB = no
  RSS change" claim (line 43) with a real measurement.
- µ-ty-07: HEX constants (line 88-101): `MIN_HEX_MATCH_LEN=16`/`MIN_HEX_DIGITS_IN_MATCH=16` →
  a 15-hex-char run (CSS `#ff00ff`-style) is NOT a secret, 16 is eligible; `HEX_CONTEXT_RADIUS=20`,
  `MIN_HEX_CONTEXT_DIGITS=8`, `MAX_HEX_CONTEXT_SEPARATORS=4` boundaries — a hex string with 5
  separators in its context is treated as non-hex. Truth table per constant.
- µ-ty-08: `ML_CONTEXT_RADIUS_LINES=5` (line 104): the ML context window is ±5 lines; assert a
  secret's ML context includes 5 lines above/below and the `file:<path>` prefix (pairs µ-ss-11).
- µ-ty-09: `LazyRegex::has_literal_prefix` memo + alternation (line 379-383): a detector with a
  divergent leading alternation (`(?:test_|live_)…` lob, `(?:hanko_|corbado1_)…`) returns true
  (the plural `extract_literal_prefixes` recognizes it); the OLD singular extractor returned empty
  for divergent branches → denied literal-prefix confidence weight → scored below floor. Pin the
  regression: every multi-prefix detector earns its literal-prefix weight.
- µ-ty-10: `LazyRegex` memo idempotence (line 263-292): `has_literal_prefix`,
  `has_broad_identifier_capture`, `has_distinctive_inner_literal` are each computed AT MOST ONCE
  per unique source (OnceLock), shared across Clones; assert a clone shares the memo (no
  recompute) and the value is a pure function of `src` (same src → same answer).
- µ-ty-11: `LazyRegex::get` unreachable-on-compile-fail (line 425-448): a detector regex that
  failed construction validation must NEVER reach first-use compilation — assert the `unreachable!`
  is truly unreachable for the shipped corpus (every detector compiles at construction); the
  test-only `detector`/`plain` constructors DO compile here (their compile error is a loud
  invariant breach, not a silent never-match).
- µ-ty-12: `CompiledPattern.homoglyph_variant` flag is what `homoglyph_ascii_skip` keys on (line
  467-476) — RESOLVES µ-tun-08: the skip applies ONLY to homoglyph_variant patterns (which always
  have a base ASCII AC pattern, so skipping on pure-ASCII is recall-safe); generic-password/
  client_secret are case-sensitive but NOT homoglyph_variant (no base AC) so are NEVER skipped.
  Test: assert generic-password's compiled patterns have `homoglyph_variant=false` and a
  homoglyph-expanded aws-prefix variant has `true`; a pure-ASCII generic-password is found with
  homoglyph_ascii_skip ON. THIS is the differential that proves the skip doesn't drop
  generic-password — write it and the µ-tun-08 fear is either closed or a real bug is found.
- µ-ty-13: `CompiledPattern.match_proves_keyword_nearby` (line 463-466): when every match starts
  with a detector keyword, `keyword_nearby` is proven by the match bytes (no whole-chunk substring
  scan); assert a keyword-led pattern sets it and skips the extra scan, a non-keyword-led one doesn't.
- µ-ty-14: `CompiledPattern.client_safe` → `Severity::ClientSafe` collapse (line 458-462): a match
  on a client_safe pattern collapses to the ClientSafe tier (droppable via `--hide-client-safe`)
  without affecting other detectors; pairs RPT-006/CLI-012.
- µ-ty-15: `source_line_at` char-boundary snapping (line 213-230): a byte offset landing inside a
  multi-byte UTF-8 scalar (binary/lossy input) snaps DOWN to the char boundary (no panic), and the
  line text scanned is unchanged (Law 10 recall-preserving); proptest random UTF-8 + offsets.
- µ-ty-16: `PreprocessedText::line_for_offset` / `source_offset_for_match` binary search (line
  138-166): the monotonic-mappings partition_point returns the right line for an offset inside a
  mapping and None past `end_offset`; the structured/multiline line-mapping round-trip (pairs ST-009).

## µ-suppression_api (`crates/scanner/src/suppression/api.rs`, read in full)
- µ-api-01: Tier-A vs Tier-B split (the contract that recovered 400+ contract evasions, line
  144-170): a SERVICE-anchored detector (`aws-secret-access-key` body has `/+=`, `powerbi`
  body IS a UUID, `mongodb-atlas` body IS a URI) must NOT have Tier-B shape gates applied (the
  anchor is positive evidence); a `generic-*`/`entropy-*`/weak-anchor detector MUST. One test per
  named example in the doc (line 158-162) asserting it is NOT dropped.
- µ-api-02: Every Tier-B shape gate reason fires + records (line 182-283): `public_noncredential_shape`,
  `pure_identifier_no_digit`, `word_separated_identifier`, `scheme_prefixed_uri`,
  `credential_colliding_punctuation`, `url_or_path_segment`, `contains_uuid_v4` — per gate, a
  generic-detector value of that shape is suppressed with the EXACT `StageId::ShapeGate(reason)`
  and `record_example_suppression` is called with the right reason string. ≥14 cases.
- µ-api-03: Tier-A universal gates fire for ALL detectors (line 237-371): `syntactic_punctuation_marker`,
  `email_address`, `vendored_minified_path`, `native_binary_strings`, `secret_scanner_source`,
  `raw_base64_file`, `regex_literal_tail` suppress even a SERVICE-anchored detector — assert each
  fires on a named-anchored finding (the claude-code AWS-regex-in-source `regex_literal_tail` case,
  line 360-362).
- µ-api-04: `keep_identifier_gate_with_randomness` recovery (line 199-210): a random token
  (`gjbubxsu`) behind a weak-anchor named detector is RECOVERED (not suppressed as pure_identifier)
  while a dictionary reference (`getUserName`) is suppressed — the shared discriminator, both paths
  (pairs µ-tok-09). Same for the stricter word-separated gate (line 216-227).
- µ-api-05: `bypass_shape_gates` = service-anchored AND !weak_anchor (line 377-378): a strong named
  detector bypasses the decoy/shape gates; a weak-anchor named detector (datadog-api-key) does NOT
  (prevents FP-trap triggering); assert via `is_service_anchored_detector` + weak_anchor combos.
- µ-api-06: `allow_canonical_hex_key` weak-anchor carve-out (line 388-389, 480-491): a weak-anchor
  service detector (alchemy/datadog/crowdin, min_confidence=0.2) with a canonical-length pure-hex
  capture is EXEMPT from the bare-hex-digest arm (the keyword anchor is authoritative) but every
  decoy gate still runs; assert a real hex key is kept, a coincidental digest still dropped.
- µ-api-07: `allow_encoded_text_secret` generic carve-out (line 390-391): a generic detector whose
  value `decodes_to_printable_text` is allowed through the encoded-text arm; assert a base64 blob
  that decodes to readable text behind a generic anchor is kept, random base64 is not.
- µ-api-08: `has_broad_identifier_capture` parser correctness (line 510-527): `([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)`,
  `([\w-]*)`, `([a-zA-Z0-9_]{1})` → true (min ≤1, full-alpha); `([a-f0-9]{32})` (hex-only) → false;
  `([a-zA-Z0-9]{16})` (min 16, real key shape) → false. The `servicenow-api-key` mixed case (strong
  `instance=(…\.service-now\.com)` + weak `user=([\w-]+)`) — per-pattern resolution keeps the strong
  one. ≥20 regex-shape cases (this is a hand-rolled regex parser — fuzz it).
- µ-api-09: `group_capture_min_len` quantifier parse (line 533-547): `+)`→1, `*)`→0, `{8,})`→8,
  `{1,})`→1, `{16})`→16, `{8,16})`→8 (min side); a quantifier NOT closing the group (`{8,}x`) → None;
  malformed `{}` → None (Law 10 fail-closed at boundary). Truth table.
- µ-api-10: `is_full_alpha_identifier_class` token set (line 553-569): `a-z`/`A-Z`/`\w` set
  full_alpha; `a-f0-9` (hex) returns false (no a-z token); a class with an unknown token (`[a-z!]`)
  → false; `0-9_-` alone (no alpha) → false. Pin the exact accepted token list.
- µ-api-11: `detector_weak_anchor_base` precedence (line 476-492): generic/entropy/private-key →
  Never; residual-weak-anchor (Tier-B `detector-classification.toml`) → Always; explicit
  `min_confidence` → Never; else → PerPattern. Assert the order (a generic detector with a
  min_confidence still → Never via the first branch) and the Result Err path is surfaced not swallowed.
- µ-api-12: `detector_weak_anchor` whole-detector vs per-pattern (line 431-447): the whole-detector
  form is weak iff ANY pattern is broad-identifier (for tooling/classification); the SCAN path uses
  the per-pattern memoized `detector_pattern_weak_anchor` so a strong pattern isn't dragged down by a
  weak sibling — assert the two can DISAGREE for a mixed detector (servicenow) and that the scan path
  uses per-pattern (pairs SR-013).
- µ-api-13: `caesar` generic-fallback suppression (line 172-180): a Tier-B finding from a
  `/caesar` source_type is suppressed as `caesar_generic_fallback`; assert a non-caesar source isn't.
- µ-api-14: `KnownExampleSuppressionCtx` with-entropy vs new (line 33-69): the entropy-bearing ctx
  carries `allow_canonical_hex_key`/`allow_base64_blob_shape`/`allow_encoded_text_secret`; assert the
  `new` ctx defaults them false and `with_entropy` threads them; pairs the example-suppression stage.
- µ-api-15: Single-discriminator contract (line 193-227): the identifier/word-separated gates here
  wrap the SAME `token_randomness` gates as `phase2_generic_shape` (scan-time) — a differential test
  feeding identical values to both and asserting identical lift/suppress (pairs µ-tok-09; one model,
  both paths).

## ST-012 RESOLVED (Iter 3) — the filesystem source windows at 1 MiB too
`crates/sources/src/filesystem/reader.rs:16` `DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE = 1024*1024` (1 MiB,
128 KiB overlap line 21). The reader windows multi-MiB files into 1 MiB chunks AT THE
SOURCE ("so a multi-MiB source file enters the scanner as many independent chunks") via
`process_entry(..., window_size, window_overlap, ...)`. So a >1 MiB structured file is
split BEFORE it ever reaches `structured::preprocess` — confirming ST-011 is a SOURCE-level
gap, doubly so (source windows at 1 MiB, engine windows again at 1 MiB). The 128 KiB overlap
preserves boundary-spanning SECRETS but NOT structured PARSING: a tfstate/compose/notebook
whose `kind: Secret`/`"resources"` marker is >128 KiB above a 1 MiB cut is divorced from the
`data:`/`attributes` block in the next window, so that window's values never decode.
- **ST-011 sharpened — highest-impact target: TFSTATE.** Terraform state files are commonly
  multi-MiB; every tfstate >1 MiB loses decode-through for all attributes past the first
  window. FIX: detect structured/config formats at the SOURCE (path/content sniff) and emit
  the WHOLE file as one chunk (exempt from `DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE`), bounded by a real DoS cap
  (the 512 MiB `reject_oversized_window_chunk` ceiling, windowed.rs:161). Test: a 3 MiB
  tfstate whose only secret is a base64 attribute at byte ~2 MiB — assert it is found (today
  it is NOT, split across windows 2 and 3 with no structured parse).

## µ-filesystem_reader (`crates/sources/src/filesystem/reader.rs`, read in full)
- µ-rdr-01: `DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE=1 MiB` / `DEFAULT_WINDOW_OVERLAP=128 KiB` (line 16-21):
  assert a 2.5 MiB file emits 3 windows with 128 KiB overlaps and a secret straddling each
  1 MiB cut is recovered (in the overlap); a structured file >1 MiB loses decode-through
  (the ST-011 failing test). Pin both constants stay aligned with the scanner's
  MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES / WINDOW_OVERLAP_BYTES (a drift gate).
- µ-rdr-02: `reader_thread_count` / `reader_thread_default` sizing (line 44-57): default crew
  = clamp(scanner_threads/4, 2, MAX_READER_THREADS=4), then min(scanner_threads.max(1));
  a configured count is min(config, scanner_threads.max(1)). Truth table at scanner_threads
  1/4/8/16/64 and configured 1/100; boundary at the clamp edges.
- µ-rdr-03: Ordered chunk re-sequencing (line 79-93): the producer thread reorders out-of-order
  reader batches by `seq` via the BTreeMap pending buffer so chunks emit in file-walk order;
  assert N readers producing batches out of order still emit seq 0,1,2,… in order; a missing
  seq blocks later ones until it arrives (no gap-skip).
- µ-rdr-04: Per-entry panic isolation (line 130-144): a `process_entry` panic for ONE file is
  caught (`catch_unwind`), converted to a `SourceError` row naming the path+seq, and the scan
  CONTINUES with remaining files (not aborted). Assert a panicking entry surfaces the error
  AND later files still scan (pairs SRC-004). Law 10: the panic is surfaced, never swallowed.
- µ-rdr-05: Cursor-iterator panic (line 203-223): if the file-walk iterator itself panics,
  `next_cursor_item` catches it, closes the cursor, and emits an ordered `SourceError`
  ("remaining files were not scanned") — assert the scan ends with a LOUD partial-scan error,
  not a silent truncation.
- µ-rdr-06: Mutex-poison recovery (`cursor_poison_item`, line 226-241): a poisoned cursor mutex
  surfaces a partial-scan `SourceError` (not a panic/hang) and closes the cursor; assert the
  `into_inner()` recovery path emits the error row. Law 10: poison is surfaced loudly.
- µ-rdr-07: Zero-readers-spawned fallback (line 173-192): if every reader thread fails to spawn,
  a single fallback reader is tried; if THAT fails, a `SourceError::Other("no files were
  scanned")` is emitted — assert the no-silent-empty-scan contract (a spawn failure must not
  look like a clean zero-findings scan).
- µ-rdr-08: Backpressure bounds (line 70-71): the `sync_channel(64)` chunk + entry channels
  bound in-flight memory; assert a slow consumer doesn't let readers run unbounded ahead (a
  memory test on a huge tree). Law 7.
- µ-rdr-09: `respect_default_excludes` threading (line 67, 139): the flag reaches
  `process_entry`; assert `--no-default-excludes` propagates so an excluded dir's secret is
  found only when false (pairs SRC-005).
- µ-rdr-10: `max_size` + merkle threading (line 64, 61, 137-138): the per-file size cap and the
  merkle skip-index reach `process_entry`; assert an over-cap file is skipped+counted and an
  unchanged file (merkle hit) is skipped (pairs SRC-002/SRC-006).
- µ-rdr-11: `process_entry_panic_rows_for_test` helper (line 255-269): a for-test panic injector
  produces the exact SourceError shape; assert it's used by the SRC-004 regression and the
  message format is stable.
- µ-rdr-12: `panic_payload_message` downcast (line 271-279): a `&str` panic, a `String` panic,
  and an opaque payload each yield the right message ("unknown panic payload" fallback);
  assert no panic-in-panic.

## µ-decode_structure (`crates/scanner/src/decode_structure.rs`, read in full)
- µ-dec-01: `is_binary_payload` definitional gate (line 56-59): `magic.is_some()` OR
  (`protobuf_wire` AND `decoded_len>=8`) → binary (data, not credential). Assert a base64-of-PNG
  is binary, a base64-of-3-field-protobuf is binary, a random 24-byte secret is NOT (the doc's
  "0 of 3000 random secrets carry a magic header" claim — encode a corpus test).
- µ-dec-02: Magic-bytes table completeness (line 348-382, 28 signatures): per signature
  (png/jpeg/gif/gzip/bzip2/xz/zstd/zip/7z/rar/pdf/elf/mach-o×3/java-class/pe/sqlite/ogg/riff/
  wasm/zlib×4), a base64-of-that-header → the right `magic` name; a near-miss (1 byte off) →
  None. The `MZ` (pe, 2 bytes) and `RIFF`/`OggS` (4 bytes) are short — assert they don't
  false-positive on random base64 (the short-sig FP risk).
- µ-dec-03: `parse_protobuf_wire` soundness (line 388-444): a real ≥3-field protobuf parses
  end-to-end (whole buffer consumed); random bytes parse <0.5% (a 10k-random corpus test
  asserting the rate); wire types 3/4/6/7 reject; field_no 0 rejects; a length-delimited field
  running past the buffer rejects (the `checked_add`+`<=n` bounds). Adversarial: a crafted
  buffer that consumes exactly to `n` with 2 fields → false (needs ≥3).
- µ-dec-04: `read_varint` bounds (line 447-463): a varint with shift>63 → None (no overflow);
  a truncated varint (high bit set at buffer end) → None; a 1-byte varint → Some. Pin the
  `shift > 63` guard prevents a malformed protobuf from over-reading. Law 7 / fuzz target.
- µ-dec-05: `is_random_base64_blob` 4-clause gate (line 143-173): band `min_len..=max_len`,
  padding-or-mult4, standard-base64-alphabet-only, admit(+ OR / OR padding OR diversity≥min).
  Truth table: a base64url token (`-_`) rejects (alphabet), a JWT (`.`) rejects, an AWS key
  (40 base62, no +/, no pad, <44) rejects (band+admit), a 48-char pure-alnum mult-4 high-
  diversity blob admits. Boundary at min_len-1/min_len, diversity min-1/min.
- µ-dec-06: `looks_like_uniform_base64_blob` band 44..=600 div 32 (line 197-199): the v33 floor
  widening 60→44 + the high-diversity admit — a 44-char pure-alphanumeric base64 blob (the 14+
  FPs that relied on the gap) is slammed; a 43-char one passes; a real 40-char AWS secret is
  NOT slammed (too short). Pin the exact band that separates decoy from positive.
- µ-dec-07: `is_byte_distribution_base64_blob` BOTH-punct admit (line 222-240): requires (`+`
  AND `/`) OR (padding with one) — a restricted-secret-key positive with ONE punctuation mark
  is NOT bitten; a random-bytes protobuf decoy (almost always has both +/) is. Assert the
  emit-drop path's stricter policy vs the penalty path (µ-dec-05) — the MC-12 non-unifiable
  divergence (a meta-test that the two gates give DIFFERENT verdicts on a single-punct value).
- µ-dec-08: Two-gate divergence is INTENTIONAL (line 207-221): a gate/comment-test asserting
  `is_random_base64_blob` and `is_byte_distribution_base64_blob` are NOT merged into one
  parameterized gate (folding re-introduces the MC-12 divergence); pin both as the single
  source for their respective bands so a future "dedup" refactor can't collapse them.
- µ-dec-09: `decodes_to_printable_text` (line 244-251): decodable AND len≥8 AND
  printable_ratio≥0.85 AND !binary → true. A base64-of-English-text → true (the
  allow_encoded_text_secret carve-out, pairs µ-api-07); a base64-of-PNG → false (binary);
  a 50%-printable random blob → false (below 0.85). Boundary at ratio 0.84/0.85.
- µ-dec-10: `decode_candidate` base64-then-hex precedence (line 315-331): a string valid as
  BOTH base64 and hex decodes as base64 first (the canonical decoder); an underscore-hex
  (`is_underscore_hex_candidate`) decodes as hex; a plain even-length hex decodes as hex;
  a non-encoding → None. Pin precedence (base64 is a superset of hex's alphabet) so the same
  value always decodes the same way (pairs the gpudecode resolve precedence memo).
- µ-dec-11: `MIN_DECODE_LEN=16` floor (line 65, 276): a 15-char candidate is not decoded
  (default evidence); 16 is; assert short tokens are left to named detectors (no decode noise).
- µ-dec-12: `decoded_is_hex_key_material` 32/40/48 (line 302-303): decoded bytes of exactly
  32/40/48 all-hex → hex key material; 33 or non-hex → false; pin the three canonical key
  lengths (SHA-256/SHA-1.../etc.).
- µ-dec-13: `decoded_is_base64_blob` len≥32 all-standard-base64-byte (line 298-301): assert the
  nested-base64 detection (a base64 that decodes to MORE base64) and that <32 decoded bytes don't
  qualify.
- µ-dec-14: `evidence` thread-local memo cache (line 110-113, 264-272): the same candidate
  decodes ONCE (cached by `hash_fast`), bounded by `DEFAULT_MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES`; assert a second
  call hits the cache (no re-decode) and the cache is per-thread (no cross-thread contention).
  A hash collision must not return wrong evidence (the memoize_by_hash key check).
- µ-dec-15: `printable_ratio` charset (line 285-288): printable = 32..127 plus tab/newline/CR
  (9/10/13); assert a value with control bytes scores lower; this feeds the future ML feature
  (recorded but NOT gating — line 25-27, pin it's not used in the boolean verdict).
- µ-dec-16: `decoded_contains_placeholder` (line 306-308): decoded bytes containing a
  placeholder word (`bytes_contain_placeholder_word`) is flagged — assert a base64-of-"changeme"
  is caught (pairs placeholder_words.rs, the second-hardcoded-vocab memo).

## µ-pipeline + µ-json + µ-unicode_escape (decode/{pipeline,json,unicode_escape}.rs — read full Iter 9)
- µ-pl-01: `decode_chunk` (pipeline.rs:7) is a BFS with THREE termination bounds — assert all three:
  (a) `depth >= max_depth` → a max_depth=1 scan does NOT re-decode a depth-1 result; (b) `seen`
  hash-set drops cycles — `%2525`→`%25`→`%` produces exactly 2 decoded layers then stops, and a
  reverse∘reverse value equal to the root is never re-emitted (root pre-seeded line 28); (c)
  `produced > 1000` OR `total_bytes > 64 MiB` fan-out cap returns early (record_decode_truncation).
- µ-pl-02: the `produced` counter (line 38/133) counts EVERY unique decoded chunk against the budget
  REGARDLESS of screen result (M2 fix) — a screen-failing chunk is still queued + re-decoded so it
  MUST consume budget. Assert a high-fan-out input (many screen-failing Caesar variants) trips the
  1000 cap (else the DoS guard never bound). This is the M2 regression — pin it.
- µ-pl-03: deadline checks at THREE points (top-of-BFS line 46, pre-decoder line 72, mid-output line
  101) — each records record_decode_truncation + clears the shared candidate cache before returning
  (no stale ptr/len, the C9 fix). Assert an already-expired deadline yields decoded_chunks bounded to
  ≤ one decoder's fan-out, and the cache is cleared (extractor::clear_shared_candidates on every exit).
- µ-pl-04: `validate=true` drops decoded chunks containing NUL (line 120) — buggy-decoder/mis-decoded
  binary; C1 controls (0x80-0x9F) are KEPT (legit UTF-8 continuation). Assert a decode producing a
  `\0` is dropped under validate, kept under !validate, and a 0x85-containing decode is kept either way.
- µ-pl-05: `hash_fast` seen-dedup (line 28/112) is a NON-crypto hash — a collision would silently drop
  a distinct decoded chunk (recall loss). Documented memory tradeoff (line 27). Capture: a property
  test that two distinct short decoded values don't collide in practice (or accept as a known bound).
- µ-pl-06 [REFUTES a speed early-out]: the AUD-speed-2 `has_decodable_payload` blanket early-out was
  TRIED and REVERTED (line 14-24) — it only knows base64/hex alphabets, missed url/entity/octal/QP/
  json triggers, silently dropping ~7% of credentials under structured wrapping. Pin a regression
  (every_contract_positive_fires_under_every_format_wrapper) so the early-out is never re-added.
- µ-json-01: `extract_escaped_json_strings` (json.rs:44) borrows ONLY spans with a `\` escape (≥4
  content chars), byte-scans with memchr (UTF-8-safe since `"` `\` CR LF are ASCII), advances 1 byte
  past an unmatched quote (no infinite loop). Assert `{"k":"aAb"}` yields the escaped value,
  a plain `{"k":"plain"}` yields NOTHING (no `\`, no alloc), an unterminated `"abc` doesn't hang,
  and a `\"`-bearing string isn't split at the escaped quote.
- µ-json-02: `json_unescape` (json.rs:109) — the 8 simple escapes (`"\/bfnrt`) + `\uXXXX`. Assert each
  simple escape maps correctly, `A`→`A`, and an UNKNOWN escape (`\q`)→Err (fail-closed, not
  passthrough — DIFFERENT from unicode_escape's simple_escape which passes through; pin the
  divergence, µ-ue-03).
- µ-json-03: `json_escape_codepoint` (json.rs:139) SURROGATE PAIRS — a high surrogate (0xD800-0xDBFF)
  MUST be followed by `\u` + a low surrogate (0xDC00-0xDFFF), combined to the astral scalar; a lone
  high surrogate →Err, a lone low surrogate (0xDC00-0xDFFF first)→Err. Assert `😀`→😀,
  `\uD800`alone→Err, `\uDC00`alone→Err. This is the correct surrogate handling unicode_escape LACKS.
- µ-json-04 (folded from the Iter-17 µ-json dup): `JsonDecoder::decode_chunk` (json.rs:14) — splices the
  unescaped value over its escaped form via `push_decoded_text_chunk_spliced` so the JSON key
  (`"api_key": "…"`) stays adjacent (the companion anchor most detectors need); LAW10: a failed trial
  unescape leaves the original text scanned unchanged. Assert a `{"api_key":"<escaped>"}` chunk yields a
  decoded chunk with the key still adjacent to the unescaped value.
- µ-ue-01: `unicode_escape_decode` (unicode_escape.rs:15) — `\uXXXX` (4 hex), `\xXX` (2 hex), and
  `\<char>` via simple_escape; lazy output alloc only on first escape; truncated escape →Err. Assert
  `A`→`A`, `\x41`→`A`, `\t`→tab, a value with NO escape →Err (decoded_text stays None), `\u00`
  (truncated)→Err.
- µ-ue-02 [CAPABILITY GAP — recall, not byte-identical]: `unicode_escape_decode` does NOT handle
  surrogate PAIRS — `\uXXXX` reads 4 hex → `char::from_u32(code)`; a high surrogate (0xD83D) →
  char::from_u32 None → `.ok_or(())?` → Err, so `😀` (😀 as a JS surrogate pair) FAILS to
  decode here while json.rs handles it (µ-json-03). Fail-closed (Err, original still scanned — Law-10
  clean), so a RECALL gap not corruption. FIX: peek the next `\u` low surrogate and combine like
  json_escape_codepoint (a behavior change — NOT byte-identical, needs its own positive/negative
  tests). Astral secrets are rare so low priority, but it's a real asymmetry between the two decoders.
- µ-ue-03 [divergence to pin]: `simple_escape` (unicode_escape.rs:45) passes an UNKNOWN escape char
  THROUGH (`other => other`) whereas json_unescape ERRORS on unknown. Assert `\q`→`q` here vs Err in
  json. Both are intentional (JS-string vs strict-JSON) — a test that documents the divergence so a
  "unify the escape decoders" refactor can't silently change either.

## µ-base64 (decode/base64.rs — read in full Iter 8)
Base64 (4 variants) + Z85 decoders, the variant classifier, and the shared shape facts.
- µ-b64-01: `classify_base64` (line 156) — the 4-variant decision: rejects mixed standard+urlsafe,
  `padded && len%4!=0`, `!padded && len%4==1`; else maps (urlsafe,padded,len%4)→variant. Assert
  Standard padded `TWFu` , StandardNoPad `TWE` (len%4==3), UrlSafe `-_` padded, UrlSafeNoPad,
  and the REJECTS: `TWFu-_` (mixed `+/` would be standard... actually `-_` urlsafe + no `+/`)→ a
  value with BOTH `+` and `-` → None. Boundary: len%4==1 unpadded → None.
- µ-b64-02: `scan_base64_candidate` (line 188) single-pass facts: padding only at end (a `=` then a
  non-`=` → None via the `_ if padded` arm), `=` at index 0 → None, >2 padding → None, distinct_alnum
  counted ONLY in the unpadded region. Assert `A===`→None (>2 pad? 3 `=`→None), `=ABC`→None (lead
  pad), `AB=C`→None (data after pad), and distinct_alnum on `AABB`→2.
- µ-b64-03: `standard_base64_shape` (line 99) — public, used by canonical.rs base64-blob gates.
  Returns None for urlsafe or padded-with-bad-remainder; else the StandardBase64Shape facts
  (has_padding, len%4, has_plus, has_slash, distinct_alnum). Assert it agrees with the canonical
  µ-canon-07/08 band callers — this is the shared shape source (DEDUP anchor; pin no second impl).
- µ-b64-04: Base64Decoder splice (line 14-42) — decodes the span, splices decoded text BACK over the
  base64 blob in the parent (push_decoded_text_chunk_spliced_at) so an adjacent `aws_secret="…"`
  keyword stays anchored. Assert a base64-of-`AKIA…` inside `key = "<b64>"` yields a decoded chunk
  where the detector still sees the keyword. The 12-char floor (lowered from 20) — assert a 12-char
  b64 encodes-through, an 11-char does not.
- µ-b64-05: Law-10 honesty — both decoders trial-decode and on failure leave the original chunk
  scanned (the inline LAW10 comments). Assert a non-base64 12+ alnum run produces NO decoded chunk
  (decode Err) AND the original is still scanned (no silent drop). Same for non-UTF8 decoded bytes.
- µ-b64-06: `MAX_BASE64_INPUT_LEN` (16 MiB) + `MAX_Z85_INPUT_LEN` (16 MiB) OOM guards — assert an
  oversized input returns Err (decompression-bomb / algorithmic-DoS class) BEFORE allocating the
  decode buffer.
- µ-b64-07: Z85 — `visit_z85_string_spans` (line 259) strips interior whitespace (Cow, only when
  present), requires len%5==0 + the full Z85 alphabet. `z85_decode` (line 293) 5-char→4-byte groups,
  `value > u32::MAX → Err` (overflow guard). Assert the 4-null `00000`→`\0\0\0\0`, a len-not-%5→Err,
  an out-of-alphabet char→Err, and the overflow guard on `#####` (max chars). `z85_val` (line 316)
  alphabet table — pin all 85 values (a property test: every Z85 char maps to a distinct 0..85).
- µ-b64-08: `is_base64_candidate_byte` (line 91, alnum+`+/=-_`) vs `is_standard_base64_byte` (line 95,
  alnum+`+/=`) — the urlsafe `-_` distinction. Assert the candidate-byte admits urlsafe but the
  standard-byte does not; pin which gate each feeds (candidate-byte for classification, standard-byte
  for the private-key payload scan in caesar.rs:245).
- µ-b64-09 [WIRING]: Base64Decoder + Z85Decoder registration in the decode pipeline registry — grep
  the Decoder registry and assert both are wired (an unregistered decoder is dead). Pairs the
  decode_backend_matrix test.

## µ-url (decode/url.rs — read in full Iter 8)
Seven decoders: url-percent, quoted-printable, html named/numeric entity, octal, mime-encoded-word,
unicode-escape. All decode-through the candidate-span pipeline.
- µ-url-01: `percent_decode` (line 255) — `%XX` hex; the `index + 2 >= len` guard (line 264) rejects
  a truncated trailing `%X`. Assert `%41%42`→`AB`, a trailing `%4`→Err, non-hex `%GG`→Err (via
  hex_val), and that literal bytes between escapes are copied. `url_decode` (line 279) PRE-GATES on
  `contains_percent_escape` so a bare-`%` candidate returns Err early (kimi-decode no-op fix) — pin it.
- µ-url-02: `contains_percent_escape` (line 291) windows(3) — a valid `%XX` anywhere → true. Assert
  `foo%2Fbar`→true, `100%done`→false (no hex pair after `%`), `%`-at-end→false. This is the cheap
  admission gate; pin it matches percent_decode's own acceptance (no candidate decoded that the gate
  rejected, no gate-pass that decode then Errs on — parity).
- µ-url-03: `quoted_printable_decode` (line 298) — `=XX` hex OR `=\r\n` soft-break (skipped). The
  bounds asymmetry vs percent_decode: QP uses `index+2 < len` and on a short/ malformed `=X` pushes a
  LITERAL `=` (soft, recall-preserving) where url-percent Errs. Assert `Hello=20World`→`Hello World`,
  `line=\r\ncont`→`linecont` (soft-break removed), a trailing `=`→literal `=`. The `has_qp_escape`
  (line 175) line-gate requires a real `=XX` so `key=value` text is skipped.
- µ-url-04: `percent_assignment_tail_candidates` (line 50) — synthesizes RHS candidates from
  `key=%..`/`key:%..` lines (skipping `://` and `http`-led so URLs aren't mis-split), dedups vs the
  borrowed candidates AND vs itself. Assert `X=%41%42%43...` (≥6, percent-escaped) yields a synthetic
  candidate; a `http://%xx` line does NOT (URL guard); a duplicate RHS is not double-added.
- µ-url-05: `html_named_entity_decode` (line 337) — the 6-entity map (amp/lt/gt/quot/apos/nbsp);
  `lazy_decoded_prefix` only allocates the output String on the FIRST real replacement (no-op inputs
  return Err → no chunk). Assert `&lt;script&gt;`→`<script>`, `&unknownentity;`→preserved (no change
  → Err), entity >10 chars → not matched. The entity map is HARDCODED (line 358) → Tier-B note.
- µ-url-06: `html_numeric_entity_decode` (line 379) — `&#NN;` decimal and `&#xHH;` hex; malformed
  (non-digit before `;`) is PRESERVED verbatim (preserved_malformed path), empty digits preserved,
  `changed` flag gates Err-if-nothing-decoded. Assert `&#65;`→`A`, `&#x41;`→`A`, `&#xZZ;`→preserved,
  `&#;`→preserved, and an out-of-range codepoint (`&#xFFFFFFFF;`)→Err (char::from_u32 None). This is
  the most complex state machine — needs the most boundary cases (the mime_q/unicode tests already
  exist per Iter-0 reads; pin numeric-entity radix + terminator handling).
- µ-url-07: `octal_escape_decode` (line 468) — `\NNN` (exactly 3 octal digits) → byte. Assert
  `\101`→`A`, `\\`+non-octal→literal `\`, a truncated `\10`<3 digits at EOL→Err. `contains_octal_escape`
  (line 501) windows(4) requires `\` + 3 octal — pin the admission parity.
- µ-url-08: `mime_encoded_word_decode` (line 511) — `=?charset?B|Q?text?=`; B=base64, Q=mime-q. The
  `len()<4` guard prevents the `[2..len-2]` panic on `=?=` (3-byte overlap) — assert `=?=`→Err (no
  panic), `=?utf-8?B?<base64>?=`→decoded, `=?utf-8?Q?a=20b?=`→`a b`, unknown encoding `?Z?`→Err.
- µ-url-09: `mime_q_decode` (line 533) — `_`→space normalize then `=XX` hex. Assert `a_b=3Dc`→`a b=c`.
  [PERF: `input.replace('_', " ")` allocates always (line 534) — rare path (mime only), but note it.]
- µ-url-10: `find_mime_encoded_word_spans` (line 555) — scans each line for `=?...?=` pairs, non-
  overlapping (offset advances past each match). Assert two encoded-words on one line both found,
  an unterminated `=?` (no `?=`) breaks the line scan. Line offsets feed span math — pin correctness.
- µ-url-11: the `simple_decoder!` macro (line 182) — html-named/numeric, octal, unicode-escape all
  share the filter+whole-chunk-trimmed-synthetic shape. Assert each filter (`&`, `&#`,
  contains_octal_escape, `\u`/`\x`) admits its shape and the trimmed-whole-chunk fallback fires for a
  single-value chunk. WIRING: every Decoder is registered in the decode pipeline (grep the registry).
- µ-url-12 [RESOLVED Iter 9 — NOT a bug]: the url/qp/entity decoders lack a per-decoder source_type
  self-guard, but pipeline.rs::decode_chunk BFS bounds any re-decode loop with THREE independent caps:
  (1) `depth >= max_depth` → continue (line 55), (2) a `seen` HashSet of hash_fast(data) seeded with
  the root (line 28) that drops any already-produced value (so `%2525`→`%25`→`%` terminates, and
  reverse∘reverse=root is pre-seen), (3) `produced > 1000` || `total_bytes > 64 MiB` fan-out cap
  (line 135). The caesar/reverse source_type guard is SUPPRESSION-SEMANTICS (avoid emitting the
  original under a `…/reverse/reverse` tag that defeats evasion-aware gates), NOT loop-safety.
  TURNED INTO TESTS (µ-pl-*): assert `%2525` decodes exactly to `%25`→`%` then stops (depth+seen),
  and a max_depth=1 scan does NOT re-decode a depth-1 result. See µ-pipeline block.

## µ-decision (suppression/decision.rs — read in full Iter 7)
The unified suppression decision cascade (suppression_stage_inner) — every shape/context/path/decode
gate in linear order, plus the decode-and-recheck recursion and the decoded_benign_text_reason twin.
- µ-dcn-01: the cascade ORDER is load-bearing — `check_markers` (Allow can short-circuit to NOT
  suppressed) runs FIRST, then the `-----BEGIN` PEM hard-bypass (line 63, returns None so OPENSSH
  key zero-padding `AAAA…` isn't killed by the repetitive-run gate). A test asserting an OPENSSH
  PRIVATE KEY body is NOT suppressed (the contracts private-key OPENSSH positive) even though it has
  long identical runs — pairs the has_n_or_more gate.
- µ-dcn-02: the `bypass_shape_gates` SPLIT is the central recall contract. Tasks: for EACH gate
  guarded by `!bypass_shape_gates` (mask_run_xxxxx, short_repetitive_run, repetitive_run,
  repeated_block_mask, filler_symbols, low_distinct_symbolic, fake_sequence, bare_hex_digest,
  uuid_v4_shape, truncated_uuid_v4_suffix, base64_blob) assert it FIRES on a generic/entropy match
  but is BYPASSED for a service-anchored (bypass=true) match. And the ALWAYS-FIRE arms
  (labelled_hash_digest, dashed_serial_key) fire even under bypass. This is the kimi-suppress #2-5
  regression surface — a per-arm differential table (~13 arms × {bypass,no-bypass}).
- µ-dcn-03: `allow_canonical_hex_key` (KH-L-0110) — a COMPLETE delimiter-terminated 32/48-hex under a
  STRONG keyword skips bare_hex_digest (line 216) AND the algorithmic_placeholder hex arm (line 363-
  368) but NOT the fake-sequence/repetitive/prefixed-hash/UUID/serial gates. Assert a 48-hex+strong-kw
  is KEPT (real key, CredData 1033 POS/0 NEG) while a 48-hex that is `000…`/`ABCDEF…`-dominated is
  STILL suppressed (decoy arms run). Differential — recall+precision both pinned.
- µ-dcn-04 [PERF, NOT byte-identical — needs care]: line 43 `let upper = credential.to_uppercase()`
  is UNICODE uppercase (allocates; per candidate). It is reused by check_markers + XXXXX +
  FAKE_SEQUENCES + EXAMPLE-path, so it's one-alloc-many-uses (amortized) — BUT to_uppercase() is
  Unicode; the consumers are ASCII-only checks. Switching to to_ascii_uppercase() would be faster and
  is ALMOST always equivalent, EXCEPT a non-ASCII credential (`café…`) maps differently. Verify
  check_markers' contract before any change; likely safe but NOT a blind swap. Same for line 379
  `trimmed.to_uppercase()`.
- µ-dcn-05: `low_distinct_symbolic` (line 116-136) — ≥8 all-non-alnum chars with ≤2 distinct →
  suppress, but NOT `!@#$%^&*()` (8 distinct). The hand-rolled ≤2-distinct scan (no heap) — assert
  `********`→suppress, `--------`→suppress, `!@#$%^&*`→NOT (8 distinct), `!!@@`→<8 len not reached.
  Boundary: exactly 8 len, exactly 3 distinct.
- µ-dcn-06: `fake_sequence` (line 142-154) DOMINANCE gate — `seq_ratio > 0.4` (seq.len / cred.len).
  Assert `ABCDEFGH` (8/8=1.0)→suppress, `key_ABCDEFGH_suffix_long_padding` (8/31=0.26)→NOT (small
  substring of a long cred). The 0.4 threshold is the recall guard — pin it. FAKE_SEQUENCES list
  HARDCODED (line 143) → Tier-B; note it duplicates the canonical masked-seq fake list (DEDUP).
- µ-dcn-07: `prose_whitespace` (line 272-282) — len>30, ≥2 whitespace, ≥1 lowercase word ≥3. Assert
  `TOKEN=Session opened with handle XYZ. See docs`→suppress, a 31-char base64 with no spaces→NOT.
  This duplicates `decoded_looks_like_prose` (line 529) almost exactly — DEDUP into one helper
  (µ-dcn-12). Also near-dup of shape/prose.rs looks_like_english_prose (3rd prose impl — consolidate).
- µ-dcn-08: `html_color` (line 302-308) `#` + 3/6/8 hex. Assert `#RRGGBB`→suppress, `#abc`→suppress,
  `#1234567` (7 hex)→NOT, `#GGGGGG` (non-hex)→NOT.
- µ-dcn-09: `template_placeholder` (line 317-325) — trimmed `{…}`/`<…>`/`${…}` ≤80. Assert
  `${SECRET}`→suppress, `{{var}}`→suppress (starts `{` ends `}`), an 81-char bracketed →NOT. This
  duplicates `decoded_looks_like_template_placeholder` (line 522) — DEDUP (µ-dcn-12).
- µ-dcn-10: the base64-decode-and-RECHECK recursion (line 427-453) — decode wrapper once, re-run the
  WHOLE cascade on decoded UTF-8 with skip_b64_decode_recheck=true (no infinite recursion). Assert
  `base64("ghp_EXAMPLE_TOKEN_FROM_DOCS")`→suppressed via the inner EXAMPLE marker; `base64(<uuid>)`→
  uuid arm; `base64(<arn>)`→IAM arm; and the sanity bound (decoded ≤ input len, printable) drops
  random-bytes-that-happen-to-decode. The recursion-depth=1 guard is the key contract.
- µ-dcn-11: `decoded_benign_text_reason` (line 457-500) is the STANDALONE decode-recheck twin (no
  recursion, returns a reason). Assert each arm (decoded_labelled_hash, decoded_bare_hash_digest
  {56,64,72,128}, decoded_dashed_serial_key, decoded_uuid_v4_shape, decoded_rfc7519_example_jwt,
  decoded_aws_iam_arn, decoded_template_placeholder, decoded_prose_whitespace, decoded_placeholder)
  fires on a base64 of the corresponding payload. NOTE `decoded_looks_like_bare_hash_digest` (line
  518) uses {56,64,72,128} — DIFFERENT from suppression_stage_inner's bare-hex {32,40,48,56,64,72,128}
  (intentional? 32/40/48 dropped here) — verify + pin the difference or file as a gap.
- µ-dcn-12 [DEDUP]: prose (3 impls: decision prose_whitespace + decoded_looks_like_prose + shape/
  prose.rs) and template-placeholder (2 impls: inline + decoded_looks_like_template_placeholder) and
  the FAKE_SEQUENCES list (decision + canonical) are duplicated — consolidate each into one shared
  shape helper so a threshold change can't drift between the direct and decoded paths.
- µ-dcn-13 [Tier-B]: FAKE_SEQUENCES, EXAMPLE_PATH_COMPONENTS (line 395), the doc-placeholder-word set
  (line 380-388) are HARDCODED → rules/ data files; pin-membership tests until then.
- µ-dcn-14: `is_uuid_v4_shape` vs `looks_like_truncated_uuid_v4_suffix` both gated here (lines 219/
  222) — assert a full UUID and a QP-truncated 34-char UUID suffix both suppress under no-bypass, and
  a service-anchored UUID (bypass) is KEPT (Heroku/Braze/Codecov UUID-body keys — the v19 split).

## µ-reverse + µ-hex (decode/{reverse,hex}.rs — read in full Iter 7)
- µ-rev-01: `looks_reversible` (reverse.rs:80) is bijection-sound like caesar: a 12+ alnum run IN
  THE REVERSED direction AND a REVERSED_KNOWN_PREFIX substring (≥3-char prefixes only; `0x` excluded
  — 1.6% random-base64 FP). Assert `RNK1ESEMURKWESFEDBA-46AIKA` (reversed AKIA key) →true; a long
  alnum run with NO known prefix (`ZYXWVUTSRQPONML…`) →false; a `0x`-reversed blob →false (2-char
  excluded). Property: looks_reversible(s) ⟺ some KNOWN_PREFIX(≥3) appears in reverse(s).
- µ-rev-02: ReverseDecoder self-recursion guard (reverse.rs:44) — source_type `/reverse` → empty
  (reverse∘reverse=identity). Assert a `…/reverse` chunk emits nothing.
- µ-rev-03: the MIN_REVERSE_LEN=16 + `!looks_like_prefixed_hash_digest` pre-gates (line 51-55):
  assert a 15-char reversible candidate is skipped, and a reversed `sha256:`-labelled hash is NOT
  reverse-decoded (prefixed-hash exclusion). REVERSED_KNOWN_PREFIXES built once (LazyLock) — pin it
  filters prefixes <3 chars.
- µ-rev-04: `reverse_str` (line 65) is `chars().rev()` (UTF-8 safe, not bytes). Assert a multibyte
  string reverses by CODEPOINT not byte (`café`→`éfac`), and reverse_str∘reverse_str = identity.
- µ-hex-01: `is_hex_candidate` (hex.rs:51) tolerates `_` separators (firmware `A1_B2_…`), validates
  over RAW bytes (non-`_` count == decoded char count) with NO throwaway String alloc (the doc-cited
  opt). Assert `A1_B2_C3_D4_E5_F6_07_18` (16 hex)→true, an odd hex-count→false, a non-hex char→false,
  and the `_`-stripped length parity. Floor is min_length param (16 from decode_chunk, 8 decoded).
- µ-hex-02: `hex_decode` (hex.rs:73) FAST PATH (no `_`) goes straight to hex_simd::decode_to_vec; the
  `_`-bearing SLOW path allocates `cleaned` once (rare). Assert both paths agree on `DEADBEEF` vs
  `DE_AD_BE_EF`, odd-length→Err, and the MAX_HEX_INPUT_LEN (32 MiB) OOM guard rejects oversized input
  (decompression-bomb class). NO silent fallback — both return Result.
- µ-hex-03: `find_hex_strings` / `find_hex_string_spans` (line 30/39) public span-extraction — assert
  it finds a 16-hex run in mixed text and respects min_length (a 14-hex run below the floor is not
  returned). Pairs the decode-through pipeline (with_extracted_value_spans).

## µ-shape_path + µ-shape_prose (suppression/shape/{path,prose}.rs — read in full Iter 6)
Two compact leaves — both already zero-alloc (path uses ends_with_ignore_ascii_case).
- µ-shpath-01: `looks_like_scheme_prefixed_uri` (path.rs:2) — `<scheme>:…`, scheme 3..=15 alpha/`-`
  with ≥1 alpha; TRUE if `//` follows OR another `:` OR scheme has `-` OR scheme ∈ {sha256,sha512,
  sha1,md5,blake3,blake2} OR (len≤20 + short all-alpha tail ≤10). Assert `https://x`→true,
  `sha256:abc`→true (hash scheme), `urn:isbn:1`→true (second colon), `ab:cd`→? colon_idx=2 <3 →
  false (boundary). Negative: a 64-hex with no colon →false. The hash-scheme list (line 26) is
  HARDCODED → Tier-B.
- µ-shpath-02: `looks_like_url_or_path_segment` (path.rs:37) — ≥2 non-empty `/`-segments, each
  alnum/`_`/`-`/`.` with ≥1 alpha. Assert `api/v1/users`→true, `a//b` (empty seg filtered, only
  1 left? `a//b`→["a","b"] non-empty→2→ check) , `12/34` (no alpha word)→false, `/single`→false
  (1 seg). START-OF-PATH note: this is value-shape (not file-path) so the µ-ci-15 class does NOT
  apply — pin it's intentionally `.contains('/')`-based.
- µ-shpath-03: `looks_like_filename_reference` (path.rs:52) — 19 FILENAME_SUFFIXES via
  ends_with_ignore_ascii_case (zero-alloc, good). Assert `keystore.jks`→true, `config.YAML`→true
  (case-insensitive), `secret.txt`→false (not in list). FILENAME_SUFFIXES HARDCODED → Tier-B; pin
  membership + count==19 so a silent drop (e.g. losing `.pem`) is caught.
- µ-shprose-01: `looks_like_english_prose` (prose.rs:4) — len≥16 AND (all-lowercase OR ≥2
  whitespace tokens all-alpha-≥2 with ≥1 lowercase-word-≥3). Assert `thisislowercaserun`→true
  (all-lower ≥16), `The quick brown fox`→true (multi-word + lowercase word), a 15-char string
  →false (len gate), `ABC DEF GHI` (all upper, no lowercase word)→false. Cross-link the i18n/.po
  recall memo (prose in translation files).
- µ-shprose-02: `looks_like_english_prose` is consumed by `engine::phase2_entropy::gates` (per the
  mod.rs:8-13 comment — compiled UNCONDITIONALLY, not behind feature=entropy). WIRING test: assert
  it is reachable in a `--no-default-features` build (the E0425 regression the comment cites);
  a feature-matrix gate that fails if someone re-adds the `#[cfg(feature="entropy")]`.

## µ-shape_public (suppression/shape/public.rs — read in full Iter 6)
Public/non-credential identifier shapes (versioned kebab, source-ledger selectors, taxonomy labels,
evidence IDs, artifact refs, shell templates, percent-markup, HTML-event fragments).
- µ-shp-01: `looks_like_public_version_identifier_with_randomness` (line 7) — `name:v<≤3 digits>`,
  name 8..=96 lowercase/digit/`-`, ≥3 parts each ≤24, non-random. Assert `product-area-contract:v1`
  →true, `:v1234`→false (4 digits), a 2-part name →false, a random name →false (token_randomness).
- µ-shp-02: `looks_like_public_reference_selector` (line 48) — one-or-more glued `[sources.IDENT]`
  where IDENT is UPPER/digit/`_` 3..=80, with NOTHING left over (`rest.is_empty()`). Assert
  `[sources.LINUX_OPENAT2]`→true, the glued pair →true, trailing junk after `]`→false, lowercase
  ident →false. len 12..=160 bound.
- µ-shp-03: `looks_like_public_metadata_identifier_with_randomness` (line 76) — ≥2 parts, ≥1 in the
  52-word PUBLIC_WORDS list, non-random, lowercase/digit/`-`. Assert `official-author-documentation`
  →true, a 2-part with NO public word →false. PUBLIC_WORDS (line 90) HARDCODED → Tier-B (µ-shp-12).
- µ-shp-04: `looks_like_public_evidence_identifier` (line 160) — a big OR of crypto-algo / fixture /
  metric sub-shapes + CWE_/RFC_/OWASP_/NIST_/CISA_ infixes + `-ISSUE-` + caesar-shifted issue +
  authority/row-range prose. Assert each family with ONE positive (`CWE_79`, `argon2id`,
  `AB7-fixture`, `12:bytes=345`, `authority-attestation`) + a negative. PERF: lines 178+194 allocate
  BOTH `to_ascii_uppercase()` AND `to_ascii_lowercase()` per call (µ-shp-13). HARDCODED taxonomy
  infixes → Tier-B.
- µ-shp-05: `looks_like_public_crypto_algorithm_identifier` (line 210) — exact match (ci) to the
  7-algo set (argon2/argon2d/argon2i/argon2id/bcrypt/scrypt/pbkdf2). Assert each + a negative
  (`md5`→false, it's a digest not a password-KDF here). `to_ascii_lowercase()` alloc (µ-shp-13).
- µ-shp-06: `looks_like_public_fixture_identifier` (line 217) — `<PREFIX>-…-fixture`, PREFIX 2..=8,
  cap-leading + ≥1 digit in [1..], middle parts lowercase/digit ≤24. Assert `AB7-foo-fixture`→true,
  `lower-fixture` (no cap prefix)→false.
- µ-shp-07: `looks_like_public_metric_fragment` (line 242) — `<≤6 digits>:bytes=<≤12 digits>`. Assert
  `12:bytes=345`→true, non-digit left/right →false, empty side →false.
- µ-shp-08: `looks_like_caesar_shifted_public_issue_reference` (line 254) — ≥16 lowercase + ≥2 `-` +
  a window `UU-DDD` (two upper, dash, three digit). Assert a shifted `XY-123…`-bearing prose →true,
  <16 lowercase →false. Pairs the caesar decoder (a shifted public issue ref must not be a finding).
- µ-shp-09: `looks_like_public_artifact_reference_with_randomness` (line 273) — ≥1 source-extension
  hit + (path/build marker OR line-marker OR ≥2 artifacts OR public-filename-shape). Assert
  `src/foo.rs:1-3docs/bar.md`→true (multi-artifact + path), a bare `secret.txt` (1 ext, no marker)
  →false. EXTENSIONS (line 289) HARDCODED → Tier-B. One `lower` alloc amortized over many checks (OK).
- µ-shp-10: `looks_like_shell_template_value_with_randomness` (line 356) — `${`/`$(` → true; else a
  `$`-suffixed kebab prefix (8..=80, alpha+`-`, ≥2 parts ≤18, non-random). Assert `${SECRET}`→true,
  `deploy-key-$`→true (template prefix), a random `-$` prefix →false.
- µ-shp-11: `looks_like_percent_encoded_markup` (line 385) — needs encoded `<`(`%3c`/`%253c`/`%26lt%3b`)
  AND encoded `>` AND a tag/handler keyword (script/iframe/svg/img/onerror/onfocus/onclick) via
  ci_find. Assert `%3Cscript%3E`→true, `%3Cdiv%3E` (no listed tag)→false, double-encoded →true.
  Already zero-alloc (ci_find) — good reference. Tag list HARDCODED → Tier-B.
- µ-shp-12 [Tier-B]: PUBLIC_WORDS(52) + EXTENSIONS(20) + HTML_EVENTS(12) + crypto-algo(7) +
  evidence taxonomy infixes — all HARDCODED detection vocab → rules/ data files; pin-membership tests.
- µ-shp-13 [PERF]: `looks_like_public_evidence_identifier` allocates BOTH `to_ascii_uppercase()`
  (line 178) and `to_ascii_lowercase()` (line 194) per candidate; `looks_like_public_crypto_algorithm_
  identifier` + `looks_like_public_fixture_identifier` each allocate `to_ascii_lowercase()`. Replace
  the fixed-infix checks with ci_find over raw bytes (Law 7, same pattern landed for caesar/canonical/
  dotted-source). Bench on a 200-char audit-ledger line.
- µ-shp-14: `looks_like_html_event_handler_fragment` (line 414) — `<event>=` suffix-stripped, event
  5..=24 alpha, ci-exact to the 12 HTML_EVENTS. Assert `onfocus=`→true, `onbogus=`→false, `onfocus`
  (no `=`)→false. Already eq_ignore_ascii_case (good). HTML_EVENTS HARDCODED → Tier-B.

## µ-shape_source (suppression/shape/source.rs — read in full Iter 5)
Source-code-expression / identifier shapes (function calls, type names, numeric literals, templates).
- µ-shs-01: `looks_like_source_code_expression_with_randomness` (line 9) — len 6..=240, an
