  early-return on 5 sub-shapes (numeric/const/string-terminator/escaped/format-template), then a
  source-alphabet `.all()` + ≥3 alpha + (`::`|`.`lead|`:`lead|`->`|`=>`|call/index syntax). Assert
  `TokenizationScratch::default()`→true, `vec![...]`→true (call/index), `.unwrap_or(u32::MAX)`→true,
  and a real base64 secret (contains chars outside the source alphabet like `+`)→false. Boundary:
  exactly 5 len →false, alpha=2 →false.
- µ-shs-02: `looks_like_source_numeric_literal` (line 75) — `0x` prefix, underscored hex groups
  (≤8 each, ≥6 hex digits TOTAL) with optional Rust int suffix (`_u32` etc.). Assert
  `0xDEAD_BEEF`→true (2 groups, 8 hex), `0xDEAD_BE_u8`→true (suffix + 6 hex), and the CORRECTED
  negatives: `0x00_u32`→FALSE [Gemini Iter-6: only 2 hex digits, fails `hex_digits >= 6` — my earlier
  "true" was wrong], `0xDEADBEEF` (no `_`)→false, a group >8 chars →false. The suffix list (line 84)
  is HARDCODED Rust int types — pin membership (Tier-B-ish but language-intrinsic).
- µ-shs-03: `looks_like_source_const_label` (line 112) `const:` prefix + UPPER/digit/`_` body 3..=80
  with ≥1 `_`. Assert `const:MAX_RETRY_COUNT`→true, `const:lowercase`→false (not upper), `const:NODASH`
  (no `_`)→false.
- µ-shs-04: `looks_like_source_string_terminator_fragment` (line 124) — strips `")` suffix then
  checks version/short-version/symbol-identifier on the prefix. Module-pair (source→public version
  predicate + token_randomness). Assert `v1.2.3")`-style and a symbol `someIdentifier")` →true; a
  random 30-char base64 + `")` →false (random token).
- µ-shs-05: `looks_like_short_version_literal` (line 139) — `v<≤3 digits>:<≤24 alnum/_/-/>`. Assert
  `v12:abc-1`→true, `v1234:x`→false (4 digits), `v1:`+25-char →false.
- µ-shs-06: `looks_like_source_escaped_string_fragment` (line 156) — `\"`-led, trim trailing `\`,
  inner 6..=40 non-random alnum/_/-. Assert `\"stable_token_name`→true (non-random), `\"a8Xk2…`
  random →false.
- µ-shs-07: `looks_like_source_format_template_fragment` (line 173) — `NAME=template` where NAME is
  UPPER/digit/`_` (≥1 alpha, 3..=48) and template has `%` + one of `\n`/`%X`/`%x`/`%d`/`%s`. Assert
  `LOG_FMT=%s\n`→true, `NAME=plainvalue` (no `%`)→false. The printf-verb set is HARDCODED.
- µ-shs-08: `looks_like_source_symbol_identifier_with_randomness` (line 194) — len 8..=96, ident
  alphabet, leading alpha/`_`; snake → every part ≤24 non-empty; else camel needs ≥2 upper + ≥3
  lower AND (a SOURCE_TYPE_TERM substring OR non-random). Assert `HashMapInputKey`→true (term),
  `my_long_config_name`→true (snake), `A8kXz9Qw…`→false (random, no term). SOURCE_TYPE_TERMS (line
  221) is HARDCODED → Tier-B (µ-shs-12).
- µ-shs-09: `looks_like_program_identifier` (line 238) — all `[A-Za-z_]`; snake-lower OR ≥1 camel
  transition. Assert `getUserName`→true, `snake_case_name`→true, `ALLCAPS` (no transition, has
  upper but not lower-snake)→false, `abc123`→false (digit). Distinguish from µ-shm-01/03 (those
  allow digits/length bounds) — pin they are NOT duplicates.
- µ-shs-10: `looks_like_kebab_config_identifier` (line 258) — ≤24 len, ≥1 `-`, ≥half lowercase, no
  `+`/`/`/`=`. Assert `read-only-token`→true, `A-B` (not enough lowercase)→false, `a-b/c`→false.
- µ-shs-11: `looks_like_dotted_source_identifier` (line 277) — 2..=5 non-empty alnum/_ segments;
  TRUE if segment[0] ∈ {this,self,window,process,global,config,client,service} OR (a camel segment
  AND a credential-word segment). Assert `this.apiToken`→true (receiver), `svc.getSecretKey`→true
  (camel+word), `a.b.c`→false. [PERF µ-shs-13: line 291 `segments[0].to_ascii_lowercase()` + line
  305 per-segment `to_ascii_lowercase()` ALLOCATE — replace with eq_ignore_ascii_case / ci_find,
  same as the canonical/caesar Law-7 fixes.]
- µ-shs-12 [Tier-B]: SOURCE_TYPE_TERMS, the dotted-receiver list, the credential-word list, the
  printf-verb set, the Rust-int-suffix set — all HARDCODED vocab → rules/ data files; pin-membership
  tests until then.
- µ-shs-13 [PERF]: kill the `to_ascii_lowercase()` per-segment allocations in
  `looks_like_dotted_source_identifier` (use eq_ignore_ascii_case for the receiver set + ci_find for
  the credential words) — per-candidate alloc in a suppression predicate (Law 7).
- µ-shs-14: `looks_like_source_type_identifier_with_randomness` (#[cfg(feature="entropy")], line
  342) — leading-UPPER, all-alnum, 8..=120, ≥2 upper + ≥3 lower + ≥1 digit + non-random. Assert a
  `MyClass2Name`-style →true, a random `Xk9Qm2Za…` →false; under no-entropy the fn is cfg-absent
  (gate-coherence). The #[cfg(feature="entropy")] re-export at mod.rs:39-40 must match.
- µ-shs-15: `has_call_or_index_syntax` (line 313) — an open bracket `([{` at idx 0 OR preceded
  (skipping ws) by alnum/`_!)]>+-*/%`. Assert `foo(bar)`→true, `key = [1,2]`→true (`[` after `=`?
  NO — `=` not in the prev set, so `key =[` would be... check: prev non-ws before `[` is `=`, NOT in
  set → false; but `arr[0]` prev `arr`→alnum→true). Pin the exact prev-char set; a real secret with
  a stray `(` mid-base64 must not trip it (precision).

## µ-shape_mod (suppression/shape/mod.rs — read in full Iter 5)
The shape-predicate hub: identifier/prose/punctuation/email/uuid shapes + the public-shape dispatch.
- µ-shm-01: `looks_like_pure_identifier` (line 60) — snake_case (≥2 `_`, no digit) OR single-sep
  alpha 8..=40. Assert `sk_SRP_user_pwd_new_null`→true, `getParameter`→true, `user-password`→true,
  and NEGATIVES: any digit (`abc123`)→false, any non-`[A-Za-z0-9_-]` byte→false, 7-alpha→false,
  41-alpha→false. The 40-char upper bound protects rare long alpha secrets — pin it.
- µ-shm-02: `looks_like_word_separated_identifier` (line 147) — len 8..=50, alnum+`_`+`-`, ≥1 sep,
  no empty word, every word has a letter, max word ≤10. Assert `s3_secret_access_key`→true,
  `X-Shopify-Access-Token`→true, and the discriminator: `sk_live_4eC39HqLyjWDarjtT1zdp7dc`→FALSE
  (24-char random suffix >10). Boundary: a value with an 11-char word →false; `foo_`/`_foo`/`a--b`
  (empty word) →false; `12_34` (no-letter word) →false. [This pairs µ-api-15 — the SSOT contract.]
- µ-shm-03: `looks_like_camel_case_no_digit` (line 121) needs ≥2 lower→upper transitions, no digit.
  Assert `getUserName`→true (2 transitions), `getName`→false (1), `getName2`→false (digit). The
  `.take(2)` short-circuit — proptest it never over/undercounts.
- µ-shm-04: `looks_like_train_case_prose_identifier` (line 193) — len 24..=160, alpha+`-`, ≥4 parts
  each ≤18, ≥3 lowercase parts, ≥1 PROSE_CONNECTOR. Assert the systemd example string →true; a
  4-part train-case with NO connector →false; a part >18 chars →false. PROSE_CONNECTORS is a
  HARDCODED list (line 201) — Tier-B candidate (µ-shm-12).
- µ-shm-05: `public_noncredential_shape_with_randomness` (line 247) is the scope-aware dispatch
  (Full vs WeakAnchor). CRITICAL: WeakAnchor returns EARLY after a narrow set (train-case, version,
  shell-template, percent-markup, html-handler) and must NOT reach the broad artifact/domain/
  reference suppressors (those erase real service URLs + tenant hostnames — the doc warning). TABLE
  TEST: a value that is `public_reference_selector` is suppressed under Full but NOT under WeakAnchor
  (returns None). Pin every reason-code string the reporter renders (12 distinct codes).
- µ-shm-06: `public_noncredential_shape` (line 238) is `#[cfg(test)]`-only (the non-randomness
  wrapper) — assert it agrees with the _with_randomness form for the same value (the randomness is
  computed internally), and that NO non-test caller exists (UTILIZATION; it is a test convenience).
- µ-shm-07: `looks_like_high_entropy_punctuation_payload` (line 298) — entropy≥4.8 AND len≥40 AND
  (`+`/`/` present OR bang-led). Assert a 40-char base64 with `+` at entropy 4.9 →true, a 40-char
  pure-alnum at 4.9 (no punct, not bang-led) →false, and `looks_like_bang_led_opaque_secret`:
  `!`-led (not `!!`), all graphic, has alpha+digit, ≥4 punct, alnum≥half →true. Shared by generic+
  entropy paths — a parity test that both call it (no drift).
- µ-shm-08: `looks_like_regex_literal_tail` (line 344) — 24 REGEX_SIGIL_SUFFIXES (`)/g`, `]+`, `)?$`
  …) [Gemini Iter-6: the array has exactly 24 elements, not 25]. Assert a JS regex literal `…)/g`
  →true, a real cred ending `==` →false. HARDCODED list → Tier-B (µ-shm-12). Pin the exact set
  (membership AND count==24) so a silent drop is caught.
- µ-shm-09: `looks_like_email_address` (line 358) — len 5..=64, exactly one `@`, non-empty local
  (alnum+`_-.+`) + domain (alnum+`-.`, ≥1 dot). Assert `noreply@gogs.localhost`→true, `a@b`→false
  (no dot, too short), two-`@`→false, `@host.com`/`user@`→false (empty side). Boundary: 64 vs 65 len.
- µ-shm-10: `contains_uuid_v4_substring` (line 401) — O(n·36) sliding window finding a UUID ANYWHERE.
  Assert `TOKEN_LIST=<uuid>` →true (embedded), a bare 35-char string →false, and a value with the
  dashes-at-8/13/18/23 but a non-hex body →false. PERF: it re-scans 36 bytes per offset; consider a
  memchr on `-` to jump to candidate dash positions (note as perf µ-shm-13).
- µ-shm-11: `looks_like_syntactic_punctuation_marker` (Tier-A, line 435) vs
  `looks_like_credential_colliding_punctuation` (Tier-B, line 497) vs the combined
  `looks_like_punctuation_decorated_identifier` (line 540). The SPLIT is recall-load-bearing: `--api`
  (double-dash, not `---` PEM) Tier-A-suppressed; `!t1c!_…` / `/ZM9…` (cred-colliding) only Tier-B.
  Assert `--api-secret`→syntactic true, `-----BEGIN`→syntactic FALSE (5-dash PEM TP), `&password`→
  true but `@gAdtFo%B!tcnSl`→false (symbol tail, real secret), `Password:`→true, `/ZM9base64`→
  colliding true but syntactic false. The combined = OR of both. This split is why named detectors
  use the halves — a test that the combined is NEVER used on a named-anchored path (WIRING).
- µ-shm-12 [Tier-B]: PROSE_CONNECTORS + REGEX_SIGIL_SUFFIXES + the TS non-null keyword list (line
  522) are HARDCODED detection vocab → move to rules/ data files; until then pin-membership tests.
- µ-shm-13 [PERF]: `contains_uuid_v4_substring` byte-by-byte offset loop → memchr2 the dash anchor
  or only test offsets where bytes[i+8]==`-`; bench on a 4KB env-dump line.
- µ-shm-14: `looks_like_ts_non_null_identifier` (line 505) — trailing `!`, ≥9 len, alnum+`_` body,
  no digit, ≥1 camel transition, AND a credential keyword (token/secret/key/password/passwd/auth/
  credential via ci_find). Assert `privateAccessToken!`→true, `SnowFlakePass123!`→false (digit),
  `randomThing!`→false (no keyword). Pairs the snowflake-positive recall guard.

## µ-caesar (decode/caesar.rs — read in full Iter 5)
The ROT-N decoder: rotated-prefix AC shift-selection, shift-invariant precondition, and
span-based skips (credential URLs, private-key blocks). Sound by construction (bijection proof).
- µ-caesar-01 [LANDED Iter 5 — PERF]: `source_path_matches` + `is_program_source_code_path` +
  `is_source_code_path` allocated `p.replace('\\',"/").to_ascii_lowercase()` (TWO heaps) per call,
  run once per chunk per decoder. Rewrote zero-alloc with `ends_with_ignore_ascii_case` (extensions)
  + `path_basename_bytes` + `eq_ignore_ascii_case` (filenames). Exposed is_program_source_code_path
  in the testing facade; added caesar_source_path_zero_alloc_parity (case-insensitive ext, both-sep
  filename, program-vs-text split, negatives, None). (Done.)
- µ-caesar-02 [SOUNDNESS — the load-bearing proof]: `matched_caesar_shifts` + `ROTATED_PREFIX_AC`
  claim recall-EXACT shift selection via the bijection `caesar_shift(c,k).contains(P) ⟺
  c.contains(caesar_shift(P,26-k))`. PROPERTY TEST (10k): for a random candidate, the set of shifts
  that `looks_credential_shaped` after `caesar_shift` is EXACTLY the set returned by
  `matched_caesar_shifts` intersected with `candidate_shape_invariant` — i.e. the optimized
  emitted-chunk set is byte-identical to the all-25-shifts reference. The facade already exposes the
  pieces (decode_caesar::{caesar_shift,candidate_shape_invariant,looks_credential_shaped,
  matched_caesar_shifts}); perf_decode_caesar.rs is the migrated parity test — assert it covers the
  needle-index→k mapping `k=(i%25)+1` and the `find_overlapping_iter` (not find_iter) requirement
  (a non-overlapping walk drops a nested needle's k → lost shift).
- µ-caesar-03: `candidate_shape_invariant` (line 448) is the shift-invariant superset gate: ≥1 digit
  AND ≥1 letter AND an 8+ alnum run. Assert it short-circuits (returns false) a no-digit value, a
  no-letter value, and a value whose longest alnum run is 7; and returns true for an AWS-shaped 16+
  alnum-with-digit value. Pin it NEVER pre-checks KNOWN_PREFIXES (the one gate a shift can newly
  satisfy must stay in the loop — a regression that adds a prefix check here would drop real
  rotations like `BLJB`+25→`AKIA`).
- µ-caesar-04: `ROTATED_PREFIX_AC` build-failure path (line 64) is Law-10 LOUD: it calls
  `warn_prefilter_disabled` then falls back to all-25-shifts (recall-preserving). Assert (a) the AC
  builds from KNOWN_PREFIXES (non-None in a normal build), (b) the None fallback in
  `matched_caesar_shifts` sets ALL 25 shift slots true (recall-preserving), (c) the warn fires (not
  silent). This is the canonical loud-recall-preserving-fallback shape — pin it as the reference.
- µ-caesar-05: `decode_chunk` self-recursion guard (line 305): a chunk whose source_type contains
  `/caesar` returns empty (one pass per input). Assert a `filesystem/caesar` chunk yields no further
  caesar output (else 25× re-shift would round-trip back to the original and trip evasion logic).
- µ-caesar-06: `credential_url_line_spans` + `line_has_credential_url` (line 130): per-LINE skip of
  `scheme://user:pass@host`. Assert `postgres://u:p@h/db` line → candidates inside it are NOT
  caesar-decoded (the resolution-loss FN fix), but `https://example.com` (no `:pass@`) line is NOT
  skipped; and a chunk MIXING a cred-URL line with a caesar-encoded cred elsewhere still decodes the
  latter (per-line granularity, not whole-chunk). Boundary: scheme <2 alpha bytes rejected; `@`
  without `:` before it rejected.
- µ-caesar-07: `private_key_material_spans` = `private_key_block_spans` (PEM `-----BEGIN…PRIVATE
  KEY…-----END`) + `encoded_private_key_payload_spans` (base64 run ≥128 that decodes to a PEM).
  Assert a candidate inside a PEM private-key block is NOT caesar-decoded (the key body is the
  secret, already detected); a base64-wrapped PEM ≥128 chars is span-skipped; and a base64 run that
  decodes to non-PEM bytes is NOT skipped. The `flush` <128 floor + utf8 + triple-marker contract.
- µ-caesar-08: `caesar_shift` (line 471) correctness: A-Z and a-z rotate mod 26, digits/punct fixed.
  Property: `caesar_shift(caesar_shift(s,k),26-k) == s` for all k∈1..25 (round-trip), and
  `caesar_shift(s,0)==s` and `caesar_shift(s,26)==s` (identity). Unicode passthrough: a `é` is
  unchanged. The facade exposes caesar_shift.
- µ-caesar-09: `looks_credential_shaped` (line 492) is the per-shift final gate: ≥1 digit + 8+ alnum
  run + a KNOWN_PREFIXES substring. Assert a shifted value that gains an `AKIA`/`sk_live_`/`ghp_`
  prefix passes; a high-alnum value with NO known prefix fails (the `sb_…` letter-coincidence FP the
  comment cites). Cross-link the suppress_named_detector_finding `/caesar` example-marker bypass.
- µ-caesar-10 [Tier-B]: PROGRAM_SOURCE_CODE_EXTENSIONS (40+), SOURCE_CODE_FILENAMES (3),
  CAESAR_TEXT_NOISE_EXTENSIONS (4) are HARDCODED lists (Tier-B violation). Move to a rules/ data file
  the operator can extend; assert the loaded set still classifies `.rs`/`Kconfig`/`.md`. Until then,
  a test pinning the exact membership so a silent drop (e.g. losing `.tbl`) is caught.
- µ-caesar-11: `base64ish_line_value` (line 268) strips an optional `key:` prefix + trims whitespace,
  returning the absolute start offset. Assert `  token: ABC123==  ` → value `ABC123==` at the correct
  offset (the offset feeds span math; an off-by-one mis-skips the private-key payload). Boundary:
  no-colon line returns the whole trimmed line; colon-at-end returns empty.
- µ-caesar-12: `candidate_inside_spans` (line 283): a candidate span fully contained in any skip span
  → true. Assert exact-boundary containment (start==span_start, end==span_end) is inside, and a
  candidate straddling a span edge is NOT (partial overlap must decode, not skip — else a cred
  adjacent to a URL is lost).
- µ-caesar-13: MIN_CAESAR_LEN (16) + MIN_ALNUM_RUN (8) thresholds: assert a 15-char candidate is
  skipped, 16 admitted; and the run gate at 7 vs 8. These bound the fan-out; pin them as the
  documented contract so a tuning change is deliberate.

## µ-path_filter (suppression/path_filter.rs — read in full Iter 4)
The path-only suppression predicates. µ-ci-15 fixed the segment-fn start-of-path miss; reading
the callers surfaced the SAME bug class in the `.contains()`-based predicates here.
- µ-pf-01: `looks_like_secret_scanner_source` (line 17-38): ten-needle `ci_find` over raw bytes,
  zero-alloc. Assert each needle (secretscanner / secret-scanner / trufflehog / gitleaks /
  detect-secrets …) matches case-insensitively in a path AND a basename, and a near-miss
  (`secretscanners_notes.md`? — it WOULD match via substring) — pin the substring semantics
  (it is a deliberate broad whack-a-mole skip, not segment-anchored). Negative: an unrelated
  path (`src/auth/login.rs`) does NOT match.
- µ-pf-02: `looks_like_vendored_minified_path` (line 53-134) — the µ-ci-15 caller. NOW that the
  segment fns handle offset-0, assert `node_modules/foo/index.js` (bare relative, non-.min.js)
  IS suppressed (was the leak). Plus each vendored shape: bower_components, jspm_packages,
  site-packages, wp-includes, public/plugins, wp-content/themes, dist/assets, vendor/assets,
  /dist/vendor literal. Negative twin: `my_node_modules/foo.js` NOT suppressed.
- µ-pf-03: `looks_like_vendored_minified_path` Rails branch (line 91-132): `app/assets/
  javascripts/<prefix>.js` where prefix ∈ VENDORED_JS_PREFIXES (bootstrap/jquery/react./vue./
  lodash/…). Assert `app/assets/javascripts/jquery.min.js` suppressed, `app/assets/javascripts/
  my_app.js` (first-party, no vendored prefix) NOT suppressed. This branch uses `p.contains(
  "/app/assets/javascripts/")` — START-OF-PATH GAP: a relative `app/assets/javascripts/jquery.js`
  (no leading `/`) MISSES. Capture as fix-task µ-pf-07.
- µ-pf-04: `path_is_ci_workflow_file` (line 136-157): assert each CI shape (.github/workflows/,
  .github/actions/, .gitlab-ci.yml, .circleci/, azure-pipelines, bitbucket-pipelines, .travis.yml,
  Jenkinsfile basename ci-exact) matches POSIX + Windows. Negative: `src/Jenkinsfile.txt` (the
  existing test) NOT matched. START-OF-PATH GAP (fix-task µ-pf-07): relative `.github/workflows/
  ci.yml` (from `keyhog scan .github`) MISSES `/.github/workflows/` — no leading sep.
- µ-pf-05: `path_is_i18n_file` (line 159-191): assert locale/ locales/ i18n/ l10n/ translations/
  lang/ langs/ dir shapes + .po/.pot ext + the `messages_*.properties`/`strings_*.json` basename
  rule. Negative: `messages_en.rs` (the existing test). START-OF-PATH GAP for the `/locale/`
  forms (relative `locale/x.po` survives ONLY via the `.po` ext fallback, NOT the dir match) —
  note it; the ext fallback masks it here but not for `lang/foo.json`.
- µ-pf-06: `raw_base64_path_match` (line 201-215) + the two wrappers (line 193-199): `.b64`/
  `.base64` ext, `base64_` basename prefix, `base64_string` infix, and the
  `include_plain_base64_txt` flag toggling whether bare `base64.txt` matches. Assert the flag
  asymmetry: `looks_like_raw_base64_file_path("base64.txt")` TRUE but
  `looks_like_entropy_raw_base64_file_path("base64.txt")` FALSE (the existing test pins this) —
  one boolean, two call-site contracts; a refactor must not collapse them.
- µ-pf-07 [FIX — start-of-path bug class, sibling of µ-ci-15]: the `.contains("/dir/")` predicates
  (`path_is_ci_workflow_file`, the Rails `app/assets/javascripts/` branch, the `/locale/`-family
  in `path_is_i18n_file`) all REQUIRE a leading separator, so a relative path that BEGINS with the
  suppressed dir (`.github/workflows/x.yml`, `app/assets/javascripts/jquery.js`, `lang/foo.json`)
  is silently not suppressed → FP leak on relative roots. FIX OPTIONS: (a) per-predicate add a
  `starts_with("dir/")` twin (mechanical, many sites), or (b) ARCH-PF: normalize the path to a
  canonical leading-separator form ONCE at the suppression entry (api.rs) so every `.contains
  ("/dir/")` and segment check sees `/<relpath>`; (b) is the clean single-point fix — pursue it.
  Tests: relative-root positive per family + absolute-path twin + negative substring twin.
- ARCH-PF (architecture): introduce a single `normalize_suppression_path(path) -> Cow<str>` at the
  suppression boundary that guarantees a leading `/` (or `\` on Windows shapes) so ALL path
  predicates (segment fns AND `.contains` fns) get start-of-path coverage for free, killing the
  µ-ci-15 / µ-pf-07 bug class at the source instead of per-predicate. Wire it in api.rs before any
  predicate runs; assert every predicate is start-of-path-correct via one shared table test.

## µ-canonical (suppression/shape/canonical.rs — read in full Iter 4)
Canonical non-secret body shapes (digests, UUIDs, serials, JWT/discord, base64 blobs, masks).
- µ-canon-01 [LANDED Iter 4 — PERF]: `looks_like_prefixed_masked_sequence` allocated
  `body.to_ascii_uppercase()` PER candidate in the suppression hot path (Law 7). Rewrote with
  `starts_with_ignore_ascii_case` + pre-lowered `ci_find` (zero-alloc, same primitives path_filter
  uses); dropped the redundant "ABCDEFGHIJ" needle (subsumed by "abcdefgh"). 3 inline regression
  tests (mask+fake-run case-insensitive, trailing-ellipsis, partial-signal negatives). (Done.)
- µ-canon-02: `looks_like_entropy_uuid_shape` (line 31) vs `is_uuid_v4_shape` (line 386) vs
  `looks_like_truncated_uuid_v4_suffix` (line 405) — THREE UUID predicates with different
  strictness (entropy one ignores version nibble; v4 one runs uniform-case; truncated one requires
  v4+variant nibbles at 34 len). TABLE TEST asserting each accepts its intended shape and REJECTS
  the others' edge cases (e.g. a mixed-case UUID passes entropy-uuid byte-`.all` but FAILS
  is_uuid_v4_shape's uniform-case gate — pin that asymmetry, it is load-bearing).
- µ-canon-03: `hex_bytes_are_uniform_case` (line 365) MiXeD-case rejection is recall load-bearing
  (the comment: mixed-case saves recall on Base16-ish secrets). Assert all-lower 64-hex → digest
  (suppressed), all-upper 64-hex → digest, MiXeD 64-hex → NOT a digest (survives as candidate).
  Proptest: any uniform-case hex of len ∈ {32,40,48,56,64,72,128} is uniform; flipping one char's
  case breaks it.
- µ-canon-04: `looks_like_bare_hex_digest` (line 146) lengths {32,40,48,56,64,72,128} vs
  `is_canonical_service_hex_key` (line 183) lengths {32,40,48,64} — the 56/72/128 EXCLUSION from the
  service-key arm is deliberate (SHA-224/384/512 bodies no detector requests). Assert a 56-hex under
  a service anchor STAYS suppressed (not lifted as a key) while a 64-hex under a service anchor is
  lifted — the KH-L-0110 escape-hatch contract. Differential test, recall+precision both pinned.
- µ-canon-05: `strip_hash_algo_prefix` (line 228) substring (not prefix) match + LABEL ORDER:
  LABELS checked sha256:/sha512:/sha512-/sha256-/sha1:/md5: in order, returns body after the FIRST
  label that `.find`s anywhere. Assert `nginx@sha256:<64hex>` strips at the `@sha256:` (docker), and
  a value containing BOTH `md5:` early and `sha256:` later strips at sha256 (label-priority, not
  position) — pin the order-vs-position semantics so a refactor to position-first doesn't regress.
- µ-canon-06: `is_structured_dotted_token` (line 49) JWT-like (eyJ prefix, 3 segs ≥4 base64url) vs
  discord-style (len bands 23-28 / 6-8 / 27-38). Assert each positive (the two inline tests cover
  shape) PLUS boundary: a 2-segment or 4-segment value rejected (exactly 3 via the None 4th-split),
  a property chain `this.svc.token` rejected, and a discord value one char outside each band rejected
  (off-by-one on all six bounds — 22/29 first, 5/9 second, 26/39 third).
- µ-canon-07: `looks_like_standard_base64_blob` (line 290) delegates to `decode_structure::
  is_random_base64_blob(value, 40, 80, 32)` — pin the [40,80] band + 32-diversity floor as a CONTRACT
  (a test that fails if the magic args change) and assert the recall-safety claims from the doc:
  86/88-char Azure storage key OUTSIDE the band survives; a 40-char no-pad random base64 IS caught;
  AWS 40-char base62 (diversity <32) survives. This is the base64-protobuf-precision memo's surface —
  cross-link [[base64-protobuf-precision-is-inseparable-from-recall]].
- µ-canon-08: `is_random_base64_blob` vs `looks_like_uniform_base64_blob` vs
  `is_byte_distribution_base64_blob` (the three decode_structure delegates, lines 298/302/311) all
  take (len_lo, len_hi[, diversity]) bands. ANTI-DRIFT TABLE: one test listing every caller's exact
  band [(40,80,32),(50,300),(40,300),(44,600,32)] so a silent band change in one caller is caught
  (the org/dedup finding the doc cites — they were byte-identical loops before reconciliation).
- µ-canon-09: `generic_base64_candidate_is_ambiguous` (line 314) HIGH_ENTROPY_BASE64_CUTOFF 4.8 +
  MIN_DISTINCT_ALNUM 32: assert entropy <4.8 → NOT ambiguous (returns false early), entropy ≥4.8 with
  ≥32 distinct alnum → ambiguous, ≥4.8 with <32 → not. The 4.8 cutoff is duplicated in
  `looks_like_generic_random_base64_blob_decoy` (line 306) — DEDUP into one const.
- µ-canon-10: `has_three_or_more_consecutive_identical` / `has_n_or_more_consecutive_identical`
  (line 423/503) — the n-variant excludes `-` (PEM/UUID/JWT delimiter) but the 3-variant does NOT.
  ASYMMETRY: assert `aaa` → both true, `---` → 3-variant TRUE but n-variant(3) FALSE. Decide if the
  3-variant should also exclude `-` (likely yes — a finding: `key=---...` would trip the 3-variant
  but is delimiter noise). Capture as a potential fix.
- µ-canon-11: `has_repeated_block_mask` (line 456) + `has_repeated_full_block` (line 480): ≥3 long
  alnum runs (≥4) OR a full value that is N repeats of a 3..=64-byte block. Assert `ABCDABCDABCD`
  (3× "ABCD") → true via full-block, `aaaabbbbcccc` → true via 3 long-runs, and a random 24+ char
  token → false. Boundary: exactly 2 long runs → false, block_len just over len/3 → no match.
- µ-canon-12: `is_dash_segmented_alnum_decoy` (line 526) two arms: fixed-width 5-char upper/digit
  groups (≥3) OR ≥3 all-alphabetic groups that are NOT random (`token_randomness.is_random_token`).
  Assert `ABC12-DEF34-GHI56` (5×upper) → true, `alpha-beta-gamma` (alpha, non-random) → true,
  `Xk9-aB2c-Zz` (random-ish) → false, and a 2-group value → false. The randomness call makes this a
  module-pair test (canonical→token_randomness) per the testing contract.
- µ-canon-13: `looks_like_random_byte_base64_blob` (line 334) rejects +,/ and requires
  decode_structure::analyze decodable + (magic OR printable_ratio<0.85). The 3 inline tests cover
  alnum-accept / slash-reject / urlsafe-`ghp_`-reject; ADD: a base64-of-printable-text (ratio≥0.85,
  no magic) → false (don't suppress decodable English), and a base64-of-PNG (magic) → true.
- µ-canon-14: `looks_like_aws_iam_arn` / `looks_like_trimmed_aws_iam_arn` (line 187/197) +
  `aws_iam_arn_body_has_resource_target` (resource ∈ role/user/group/policy/instance-profile). The
  inline tests cover role/instance-profile/user accept + secretsmanager/server-certificate reject.
  ADD: aws-cn + aws-us-gov partitions on BOTH the full and trimmed forms (partition matrix), and the
  trimmed form must NOT match a full `arn:aws:iam::` (the inline negative pins this — keep it).
- µ-canon-15: `looks_like_entropy_integrity_digest` (line 78) sha512-/sha384-/sha256- + base64 body
  vs `looks_like_prefixed_hash_digest` (line 118) sha256:/sha512-/… + hex-or-base64 body. Assert
  the entropy variant requires the `-` (not `:`) labels and a non-empty base64 body; the prefixed
  variant accepts both `:`/`-` and hex bodies of 40/64/128. Cross-check they agree on a real npm
  `sha512-<base64>` integrity string (both suppress) — no gap between the two arms.
- µ-canon-16: `RFC7519_EXAMPLE_JWT_PREFIX` const (line 4) — assert it is actually USED (grep its
  callers; UTILIZATION) and that a credential beginning with it is suppressed as the canonical
  RFC example JWT. If it has no non-test caller, that is an insufficiency finding (wire it or drop).

## µ-scanner_config (scanner_config.rs — read in full Iter 4)
The Tier-A scanner tuning + the ScannerConfig newtype over ScanConfig.
- µ-sc-01 [LANDED Iter 4]: `sanitise` entropy_threshold NaN/negative scrub forked a hardcoded
  4.5 instead of reading `canon.entropy_threshold` like ml_weight/min_confidence. Fixed to read
  canon (single source). Regression: scanner_config_sanitise_nan_reads_canonical_defaults asserts
  all three scrubs == ScanConfig::default() by identity + the 8.0 ceiling clamp. (Done.)
- µ-sc-02 [DRIFT HAZARD]: `ResolvedScannerTuningConfig` (15 fields, line 145) and
  `ResolvedRuntimeTuningConfig` (14 fields — no `hs_shard_target`, line 164) are TWO hand-listed
  resolved structs. There is no conversion binding them, so a new tuning knob added to one can
  silently miss the other. ADD a `From<&ResolvedScannerTuningConfig> for ResolvedRuntimeTuningConfig`
  (or a field-parity test) so the 14 shared fields cannot drift; document WHY runtime omits
  hs_shard_target (compile-time-only). Pin both lists against ScannerTuningConfig's field set.
- µ-sc-03: `effective()` / the 15 `*_effective()` resolvers — TABLE TEST: for EVERY knob, assert
  (a) None → the documented `*_DEFAULT` const, (b) Some(x) → x verbatim, (c) the const matches the
  shipped value the docs claim. 15 knobs × 3 = 45 assertions; a single proptest over Option<bool>/
  Option<usize> per field is cleaner. Pin that `effective()` reads EVERY field (no knob silently
  dropped from the resolved struct — wire-completeness).
- µ-sc-04: `ScannerConfig` Deref/DerefMut to ScanConfig (line 237-248): assert `config.min_confidence`
  reads/writes through to `self.scan.min_confidence` (the MC-01 single-definition contract — no
  parallel field list). A proptest mutating a deref'd field then reading `.scan` directly proves
  the wrap is structural, not a copy.
- µ-sc-05: `From<ScanConfig> for ScannerConfig` (line 384-413): assert the move-wrap preserves
  EVERY ScanConfig field byte-for-byte (no rename/drop/invent — the MC-01 regression) AND that
  `sanitise()` runs (a NaN-bearing ScanConfig.into() comes out clean). The defaults for the two
  added knobs: multiline=default, penalize_test_paths=true.
- µ-sc-06: presets `fast()`/`thorough()`/`high_precision()` (line 262-313): assert each sets EXACTLY
  the documented knobs and inherits the rest from default (fast: decode_depth 0, ml off, entropy off;
  thorough: decode_depth 10, ml on, entropy on, min_confidence INHERITED not forked; high_precision:
  decode_depth 1, entropy off, generic_keyword_low_entropy off, min_confidence 0.85). Negative:
  high_precision must NOT disable ml (the doc-stated contract — disabling would crater the 0.85 bar).
- µ-sc-07: `high_precision()` ml-stays-on is recall/precision load-bearing — a differential test:
  high_precision keeps a checksum-valid token (floored 0.9 > 0.85) and drops a checksum-fail token
  (capped 0.1 < 0.85); pairs the install-flow checksum policy memo.
- µ-sc-08: `sanitise` caps `max_matches_per_chunk` to 1_000_000 and floors 0→1000, and maps
  per_chunk_timeout_ms Some(0)→None: assert each boundary (0, 1, 1_000_000, 1_000_001) and the
  timeout zero-normalisation. These are Tier-A-ish magic constants — consider hoisting to named
  consts (DRY with CLI/TOML validation that enforces the same caps — µ-sc-09).
- µ-sc-09 [DEDUP]: the decode-depth ceiling reads `keyhog_core::max_decode_depth_limit()` (good,
  shared) but the 1_000_000 / 1000 / 8.0 caps are local literals. Verify CLI parsing + TOML
  validation enforce the SAME caps from the SAME source (grep max_matches_per_chunk across cli/);
  if they fork, hoist to one `keyhog_core` contract fn like max_decode_depth_limit.
- µ-sc-10: `per_chunk_deadline()` (line 320-323): maps per_chunk_timeout_ms → Instant::now()+dur;
  assert None→None, Some(ms)→a deadline ~ms in the future (monotonic, not wall-clock). Pin it's
  computed per-call (fresh now()), not cached.
- µ-sc-11: `ResolvedRuntimeTuningConfig::gpu_moe_timeout()` (#[cfg(feature="ml")], line 184): asserts
  Duration::from_millis(gpu_moe_timeout_ms); test under the ml feature that 30_000 default → 30s and
  an override propagates. Under no-ml the method is absent (cfg-gated) — gate-coherence check.
- µ-sc-12: the GPU_MOE_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT (30_000) and gpu_recall_floor default (false) — assert the
  shipped defaults match the doctor/backend reporting that surfaces them (COHERENCE across crates).
- µ-sc-13: HIGH_PRECISION_MIN_CONFIDENCE (0.85) is distinct from ScanConfig default floor (0.40) BY
  DESIGN — assert they are intentionally different (a test that fails loudly if someone "unifies"
  them, with a comment pointing at the precision-vs-recall tradeoff).
- µ-sc-14: ScannerTuningConfig derives Hash/Eq and is part of the autoroute cache identity (doc
  line 14-16). Assert two configs differing in ONE knob hash differently (cache-key correctness —
  a recall-equivalent route change must invalidate the autoroute cache, else a stale decision ships).
- µ-sc-15: `min_confidence(self, f64)` builder (line 315-318) sets the field and returns self;
  assert it composes (`ScannerConfig::high_precision().min_confidence(0.95)` yields 0.95, overriding
  the preset) — the doc claims "--min-confidence override layers on top of the preset".

1dec29dc, ~76s). url / base64 / reverse / hex: behaviors all CORRECT. The bulk of the report was

µ-blocks were authored. The µ-tasks are FUNCTION-NAME-anchored (stable); line numbers are
approximate-at-authoring and intentionally NOT churn-updated each edit. Actionable folds:

  allocation is GONE — `looks_like_public_evidence_identifier` now uses `ci_find` (it saw it at the
  new line 186). That was landed in µ-shp-13 (commit e9f1fa4d5). The PERF half of µ-shp-04 is done;
  the TEST half (assert each evidence sub-shape family) remains open.
- µ-dcn-07 [RESOLVED — dedup half]: Gemini confirms the prose predicate is now the single shared
  `looks_like_prose_whitespace_run` (no more decoded_looks_like_prose duplicate) — landed in µ-dcn-12
  (commit e0245a5b4). The remaining µ-dcn-07/12 work is the THIRD impl (shape/prose.rs, intentionally

- µ-b64-10 [MISSED — added]: `find_base64_strings` (base64.rs, public) has no µ-task. Assert it
  extracts valid standard/urlsafe base64 ≥min_length wrapped as EncodedString, respects the floor
  (a sub-min run is not returned), and pairs find_base64_string_spans (the span-returning twin) —

## Gemini review (Iter 6) — folded notes
Reviewed µ-caesar / µ-shm / µ-shs / µ-canonical / µ-scanner_config / µ-path_filter against the
source (Gemini 3.5 Flash, job eaf5efd6, ~95s). µ-caesar / µ-scanner_config / µ-path_filter: clean
(no grounding errors, no missed tasks, no dups). Two grounding errors fixed inline above (µ-shm-08
count 25→24; µ-shs-02 `0x00_u32` is FALSE not true — only 2 hex digits). Three genuinely-missed
canonical predicates added (the functions exist + have inline tests, but my µ-block had no task):
- µ-canon-17: `looks_like_dashed_serial_key` (canonical.rs:12) — the case-INSENSITIVE 5×5 wrapper
  (`is_five_by_five_dash_shape` with `is_ascii_alphanumeric`). Assert it matches BOTH
  `JQQJN-VBWHG-XBC8R-2MV9F-CD7P9` and its lowercase twin, while the entropy sibling
  `looks_like_entropy_upper_license_serial` (line 89) accepts ONLY uppercase/digit — the exact
  29-length, dashes-at-5/11/17/23 contract. The inline `dashed_serial_key_*` tests cover the base;
  add the upper-vs-any case-asymmetry assertion explicitly (pairs µ-canon-02's strictness theme).
- µ-canon-18: `looks_like_entropy_canonical_non_secret_shape` (canonical.rs:24) — the AGGREGATE of
  the 4 entropy decoy predicates (uuid / canonical-hex / integrity-digest / upper-license-serial);
  assert it returns true iff ANY arm matches, and that it is STRICTER than the report-time helpers
  (the doc at line 16-23: it does NOT accept lowercase serials or 48/56/72 truncated digests). A
  table test: one positive per arm + a value each arm rejects.
- µ-canon-19: `looks_like_entropy_canonical_hex_digest` (canonical.rs:41) — pure-hex of EXACTLY
  {32,40,64,128} (NOTE: NOT 48/56/72 — distinct from `looks_like_bare_hex_digest`'s wider set in
  µ-canon-04). Assert 32/40/64/128 hex →true, a 48-hex →FALSE here (but true in bare_hex), a 33-hex
  →false, a non-hex char →false. This length-set DIFFERENCE between the two functions is the
  load-bearing contract — pin it so a "unify the hex lengths" refactor can't silently widen entropy.

## Gemini 3.5 Flash review (Iter 3) — folded notes
Reviewed PART II µ-blocks + the #52 finding. Outcome: mostly GROUNDING OK; one grounding
error fixed inline (µ-bigram-12 — `popcount` IS called by prod `recompute_saturation:164`,
NOT test-only); one duplicate confirmed; the windowing finding INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED with
a THIRD layer; and the contains_path_segment start-of-path miss CONFIRMED as a real bug.

- DEDUP confirmed: µ-tok-09 ≡ µ-api-15 (both the single-discriminator/SSOT contract for the
  identifier + word-separated gates). Kept µ-api-15 as the canonical contract task; µ-tok-09
  stays as the token_randomness-local pointer ("pairs µ-api-15"). One test, not two.
- WINDOWING FINDING CONFIRMED (third layer): Gemini independently traced a THIRD 1 MiB cap at
  `crates/sources/src/filesystem/extract.rs:573` (DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE) on top of reader.rs:16
  and engine windowed.rs — so a >1 MiB tfstate/k8s-Secret is windowed at the SOURCE, again on
  re-extract, and again in the engine, all before structured preprocess. ST-011 is the real
  fix (preprocess-before-window / scan structured files whole under the 512 MiB ceiling);
  the #52 2 MiB cap + counter remain dead until ST-011 makes them reachable (ST-013).

MISSED tasks Gemini surfaced (added here, code-grounded):
- µ-bigram-13: `from_literal_prefixes` with an EMPTY prefix slice must yield an all-zero bloom
  (no bits set) and `contains` must return false for every probe — assert the empty-input edge
  doesn't accidentally mark the bloom saturated (recompute_saturation on an empty set).
- µ-alpha-13: `intersects`/`union` of the alphabet mask against an all-zero mask (empty result)
  and an all-ones mask (identity) — boundary algebra; assert idempotence union(m,m)==m and
  intersects(m, !m)==∅.
- µ-ci-15 [REAL BUG — fix + test]: `contains_path_segment` / `contains_path_segment_two`
  MISS a target segment at the absolute START of a RELATIVE path (e.g. `node_modules/foo/bar`,
  `vendor/x`) — the scan loop only begins AFTER the first separator, so a segment at offset 0
  with no leading `/` is never compared. Effect: vendored-tree suppression silently fails to
  fire on relative paths that begin with the ignore segment (recall/precision bug — unwanted
  vendored hits leak, or worse, an intended suppression is skipped). FIX: also test the
  leading segment (`bytes[..seg.len()]` bounded by a following separator or end). Add positive
  (`node_modules/p` suppressed), negative (`mynode_modules/p` NOT suppressed — no false
  substring match), and the absolute-path twin (`/r/node_modules/p` still suppressed) tests.
  Pairs the earlier µ-ci-10 trailing-sep observation — same function, the other boundary.
- µ-entf-15: `shannon_entropy_scalar` of a 2-symbol perfectly-balanced sequence (`"abab…"`,
  256 bytes) must equal exactly 1.0 bit/byte — assert the canonical entropy anchor (and that a
  single-symbol run is 0.0), pinning the scalar reference the SIMD path is differential-tested
  against.
- µ-did-09: `is_residual_weak_anchor` has no `detector_ids`-style wrapper symmetric with
  `is_private_key_block_detector` — either add the wrapper for call-site symmetry or document
  why the bare predicate is the boundary (UTILIZATION + COHERENCE).
- µ-uni-19: `is_evasion_char` must return true for BOTH a zero-width char (U+200B/U+FEFF) and
  an RTL/bidi override (U+202E) — assert each evasion class independently (the two families
  have different code points; one test per class, not a single OR).
- µ-ss-15: `extend_lines_with_pending_ml_matches` line-extraction — a pending ML match whose
  span crosses a synthetic structured line must map back to the correct ORIGINAL line via the
  mapping table; assert the reported line is the source line, not the synthetic offset.
- µ-tun-16: `phase2_anchor_enabled` default resolution — assert the compiled default, the
  tool.toml override, and the CLI override resolve in CLI>TOML>compiled order (Tier-A contract)
  and that the resolved value actually reaches the phase-2 anchor gate (WIRING).
- µ-ty-17: `PreprocessedText::passthrough` constructor — a text with no structured pairs must
  produce a passthrough whose mappings are identity (every original line maps to itself, no
  synthetic lines) and `original_end == text.len()`; assert the no-op path allocates no
  synthetic mappings.
- µ-api-16: `suppress_known_example_credential_stage` wrapper — feed a known-example credential
  (AWS docs `AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE`) and assert it is suppressed at THIS stage with the correct
  reason code, and a real-shaped twin is NOT suppressed (positive + negative twin).
- µ-api-17: `suppress_named_detector_finding` wrapper — assert a finding from a named detector
  on the suppression list is dropped with the named-detector reason, and an off-list detector's
  finding survives; pin the reason-code string the reporter renders.

## µ-dm — suppression/doc_markers.rs (read in full, Iter 10)
`upper_contains_token(upper, token)` + `check_markers(credential, upper, from_evasion_decoder, _path, entropy_hint) -> MarkerVerdict {Suppress(&'static str) | Allow | KeepChecking}`.
- µ-dm-01: `upper_contains_token` word-boundary truth. Assert `upper_contains_token("FOO_EXAMPLE_BAR", "EXAMPLE") == true` (token bracketed by `_`, non-alphanumeric both sides) AND `upper_contains_token("FOOEXAMPLEBAR", "EXAMPLE") == false` (alphanumeric both sides → substring, not a token). Positive + negative twin on the exact predicate.
- µ-dm-02: `upper_contains_token` end-anchored boundaries. `upper_contains_token("EXAMPLE_KEY","EXAMPLE")==true` (before = None) AND `upper_contains_token("KEY_EXAMPLE","EXAMPLE")==true` (after = None). Both `is_none_or` arms exercised.
- µ-dm-03: `upper_contains_token` byte/char boundary correctness (the bug the module header documents). Assert `upper_contains_token("™EXAMPLE™","EXAMPLE")==true`: the `™` (U+2122, 3 UTF-8 bytes, non-alphanumeric) before/after the match proves `upper[..idx].chars().next_back()` reads the boundary on a BYTE-sliced prefix correctly — the old `chars().nth(idx-1)` (char index against a byte offset) would mis-read it. This is the load-bearing regression for the whole module's correctness claim.
- µ-dm-04: gate ORDER — placeholder-word wins first. With a credential carrying a known non-example placeholder word (from `placeholder_words`), assert `check_markers` returns `Suppress("placeholder_word")` even when the same string also embeds a later-gate marker (e.g. a known prefix). Pin the exact reason string. (Needs a real placeholder word from the Tier-B list — read placeholder_words.rs first to pick one with a valid checksum-free shape.)
- µ-dm-05: EXAMPLE-token vs reserved-domain carve-out. `check_markers("AKIA…EXAMPLE", upper, false, None, None)` → `Suppress("contains_EXAMPLE_token")`; but a value containing `example.com` (e.g. `https://example.com/cb?x=…EXAMPLE…`) is NOT suppressed by that gate (the `!credential.contains("example.com")` guard) — assert it does not return `Suppress("contains_EXAMPLE_token")`. RFC 2606 reserved-domain false-positive guard.
- µ-dm-06: evasion-decoder bypass. The SAME EXAMPLE-suffixed AWS key with `from_evasion_decoder=true` must NOT return `Suppress("contains_EXAMPLE_token")` NOR `Suppress("doc_marker_substring")` (both gates guard `!from_evasion_decoder`) — an EXAMPLE marker emerging from `/reverse` or `/caesar` IS evidence of a real leak. Assert the verdict is Allow/KeepChecking, not those two Suppress reasons. Negative twin of µ-dm-05.
- µ-dm-07: INSTRUCTIONAL_FRAGMENTS (`YOUR_ YOUR- INSERT CHANGE REPLACE`) leading-boundary only. `check_markers("YOUR_API_KEY",…)` → `Suppress("instructional_fragment")`. Negative: `"abcCHANGEdef"` (CHANGE preceded by alphanumeric `c`) does NOT suppress via instructional_fragment — the leading-boundary `match_indices`+`chars().next_back()` filter rejects the embedded substring. Assert both; pin reason.
- µ-dm-08: dev-marker overrides prefix trust. `check_markers("ghp_TODO_REPLACE_THIS",…)` → `Suppress("dev_marker_todo_fixme")` (via `upper_contains_token(upper,"TODO")`), proving it fires BEFORE the known-prefix Allow fast-path. Also assert FIXME path. Negative: `"ghp_methodically…"` where TODO/FIXME appear only as substrings inside words → not suppressed (token boundary).
- µ-dm-09: RFC7519 specimen JWT — `credential.contains(RFC7519_EXAMPLE_JWT_PREFIX)` → `Suppress("rfc7519_example_jwt")`, and it must fire even when the specimen is embedded with leading context (`auth_token=eyJ…` — `contains`, not `starts_with`). Assert both bare and `auth_token=`-prefixed forms suppress; this is the gate that fixed 349+142 leaked FPs (per code comment). Must run before the `eyJ` known-prefix Allow.
- µ-dm-10: DOC_MARKER_SUBSTRINGS (16 entries) run BEFORE known-prefix Allow. `check_markers("ghp_PLACEHOLDER_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",…)` → `Suppress("doc_marker_substring")` NOT `Allow`. Assert at least PLACEHOLDER, NOT_A_REAL, REDACTED, FAKE_KEY each on a service-prefixed body suppress (parametrize). Ordering regression: the comment notes 145 leaked FPs were this exact ordering bug.
- µ-dm-11: TESTKEY_ carve-out (two-part). `check_markers("TESTKEY_aaaaaaaaaaaa",…)` — the `TESTKEY`/`TEST_KEY` markers are skipped (the `credential.starts_with("TESTKEY_")` `continue`), AND the known-prefix Allow is skipped (`!credential.starts_with("TESTKEY_")` false) → verdict is `KeepChecking` (falls through to repetitive-mask gates). Assert it is neither Suppress("doc_marker_substring") nor Allow. The adversarial fixtures depend on this exact fall-through.
- µ-dm-12: known-prefix Allow vs masked body. With `known_prefix_body(credential) = Some(body)`: `ghp_` + an all-`x`/masked body → `looks_like_prefixed_masked_sequence(body)` true → `Suppress("prefixed_masked_sequence")`; `ghp_` + a real-entropy body → `Allow`. Assert both branches; this is the positive-evidence early-return that skips shape gates.
- µ-dm-13: WIRING — `MarkerVerdict::Allow` ⇒ the decision tree returns `false` (NOT suppressed) immediately and skips shape gates. Cross to `decision.rs::suppression_stage_inner`: feed a clean `ghp_<real-body>` and assert it is NOT suppressed AND the downstream shape gates did not run (observable via no `Suppress(shape…)` reason). Real-A→real-B module-pair test.

## µ-util-surr — decode/util.rs `surrogate_pair_to_char` (added Iter 10)
- µ-util-01: exact astral scalars. `surrogate_pair_to_char(0xD83D,0xDE00)==Some('\u{1F600}')`; `(0xD801,0xDC37)==Some('\u{10437}')`; range floor `(0xD800,0xDC00)==Some('\u{10000}')`; range ceil `(0xDBFF,0xDFFF)==Some('\u{10FFFF}')`. Assert the decoded `char`, not just `is_some`.
- µ-util-02: proptest 10k — for all `high∈0xD800..=0xDBFF`, `low∈0xDC00..=0xDFFF`: result is `Some(c)` AND `c as u32 == 0x10000 + (((high-0xD800)<<10)|(low-0xDC00))` AND `(0x10000..=0x10FFFF).contains(&(c as u32))`. The bit-math invariant — guards the one shared definition both decoders trust.
- µ-util-03: DEDUP differential (anti-drift). Feed the SAME surrogate-pair escape text (built `format!("\\u{:04X}\\u{:04X}",h,l)`) through BOTH `testing::unicode_escape_decode` and the json path (a `"…"` JSON value run through the json decoder/testing facade) and assert the decoded scalar is byte-identical. If json grows a divergent surrogate handling later, this fails. (If no json testing facade exists, add one — the two callers MUST stay observably identical since the comment promises it.)

## µ-sd — suppression/decode.rs `try_decode_b64_to_utf8` (read in full, Iter 10)
- µ-sd-01: length floor. `try_decode_b64_to_utf8("YWJj")` (len 4, valid b64 for "abc") → `None` (the `< 8` suppression-specific floor short-circuits before `base64_decode`). Boundary twin: an 8-char valid b64 string DOES proceed to decode (assert `Some`). Pins the floor at exactly 8.
- µ-sd-02: happy path. `try_decode_b64_to_utf8(<base64 of "PLACEHOLDER">)` → `Some("PLACEHOLDER")`. Assert the decoded text exactly so the inner doc-marker gate can fire on it (the whole reason this helper exists). Use a ≥8-char input.
- µ-sd-03: non-UTF-8 payload → None (LAW10, recall-preserving, loud-comment path). Feed base64 of raw bytes `[0xFF,0xFE,0xFF,0xFE,0xFF,0xFE]` (valid base64, invalid UTF-8) → `String::from_utf8` Err arm → `None`. Assert `None`, no panic. The `// LAW10` comment must match: miss keeps the original candidate unsuppressed.
- µ-sd-04: decode failure → None. Feed a ≥8-char string `base64_decode` rejects (e.g. contains `!@#` or a non-multiple length that the strict variant refuses) → `None`. Assert the `Err(_) => None` arm; pin that it does not propagate the error.
- µ-sd-05: WIRING (real-A→real-B). Cross to `decision.rs`: a k8s-Secret-style generic candidate whose VALUE is base64 of `"REDACTED"` is suppressed because `try_decode_b64_to_utf8` surfaces the marker for the inner gate; a base64-wrapped REAL secret of identical outer shape is NOT suppressed. Positive + negative twin proving the peek actually changes the suppression decision (not just decodes).

## µ-tr → REMOVED (Iter-12 review dedup)
token_randomness.rs was ALREADY deep-read (canonical `## µ-token_randomness`, µ-tok-01..11 above) — the Iter-11 NEXT pointer wrongly re-sent it. The Iter-11 µ-tr-01..10 added nothing the canonical µ-tok section lacks (µ-tok is more thorough: it also has µ-tok-07 divergence, µ-tok-10 precision-regression pin, µ-tok-11 exact-value spot-check). Removed in the Iter-12 Gemini review. Canonical home: µ-tok-*.

## µ-pw — placeholder_words.rs (read in full, Iter 11)
Tier-B placeholder vocabulary (`rules/placeholder_words.toml`), one source for surface/decoded/doc-marker suppression. `parse_placeholder_words`, `placeholder_word_suppresses`, `looks_like_high_entropy_marker_collision`, `bytes_contain_*`.
- µ-pw-01: `parse_placeholder_words` validation — assert the exact Err substring for each: empty entry ("must not be empty"), non-lowercase ("must be lowercase ASCII"), non-alphanumeric ("must be ASCII alphanumeric"), duplicate ("duplicate placeholder word"), empty list ("at least one entry"). Positive: a valid 2-word TOML → 2 `PlaceholderWord` with correct `lower`/`upper`. Tier-B contract.
- µ-pw-02: `PlaceholderWord::is_example` true ONLY for `"example"`. Assert true for example, false for "placeholder"/"fake". AND `example_word()` returns `Some(w)` with `w.is_example()` when the Tier-B vocab contains "example" (it must — doc_markers' EXAMPLE gate depends on it), `None` only if a future vocab drops it; assert the loaded `rules/placeholder_words.toml` yields `Some`. (Gemini Iter-12: example_word had no direct coverage.)
- µ-pw-03: `placeholder_word_suppresses` boundary. BOTH-boundary token suppresses: "FAKE_VALUE" + word fake → left None, right `_` → `true`. Embedded "MYFAKEKEY" → before `Y`, after `K` both alnum → neither boundary → `false`. Assert.
- µ-pw-04: one-sided boundary + entropy-collision carve-out (line 127-133). When exactly ONE boundary holds → suppress UNLESS `looks_like_high_entropy_marker_collision`. A short one-sided match → suppressed; a 40+ char value with `+`/`/` and entropy ≥4.8 and a one-sided placeholder-word boundary → NOT suppressed. Assert both.
- µ-pw-05: `looks_like_high_entropy_marker_collision` gates — `false` unless `len≥40 AND (contains '+' or '/')`; then `entropy(hint or shannon) ≥ 4.8`. Assert: 39-char → false; 40-char without `+`/`/` → false; 40-char with `/` entropy 4.9 → true; passing `entropy_hint=Some(5.0)` short-circuits the `shannon_entropy` recompute (Law-7 — assert no recompute path by the hint branch).
- µ-pw-06: `bytes_contain_placeholder_word` is zero-alloc via `ascii_ci::ci_find` (NO `to_ascii_uppercase`) and is SUBSTRING (no word boundary). Assert it finds "fake" in `b"MY_FAKE_TOKEN"` case-insensitively. Document the intentional split: boundary-aware (`contains_placeholder_word`, alloc) vs substring fast-path (`bytes_*`, zero-alloc).
- µ-pw-07: `bytes_contain_entropy_placeholder_marker` — assert representative positives across the hardcoded marker set (`your_`, `replace_me`, `change_me`, `insert_here`, `fake_`, `dummy_`, `mock_`, `secret_key`+len<20, `AKIA…EXAMPLE`, `AKIA…1234567890`, `<`/`>`, exact `null|none|undefined|empty|default|secret|password`) + a negative. CAPTURE (Tier-B violation): this hardcoded list must move to `rules/` data (Law: hardcoded lists banned) — generated from a Tier-B file, not source consts.
- µ-pw-08: Law-7 CAPTURE — `contains_placeholder_word_with_entropy_hint` allocates `credential.to_ascii_uppercase()` per call (hot: `confidence::penalties`, URL userinfo, decoded-form via `suppression::decision`). Verified the callers hold NO precomputed upper, so a byte-identical removal is blocked: the boundary check uses Unicode `char::is_alphanumeric` over the uppercased string; a zero-alloc version needs a case-insensitive substring search RETURNING positions + a Unicode-correct boundary on the original bytes. Captured; needs a `ci_find_indices` primitive first.
- µ-pw-09: DEDUP CAPTURE (cross-module) — the word-edge snippet (`upper[..idx].chars().next_back()` / `upper[idx+token.len()..].chars().next()` + `is_none_or(|c| !c.is_alphanumeric())`) is duplicated in `placeholder_words::placeholder_word_suppresses` AND `suppression::doc_markers::upper_contains_token` (+ doc_markers instructional block, left-only). Extract `left_word_edge(s,idx)` / `right_word_edge(s,idx_after)` to a NEW shared `word_boundary.rs` (NOT `ascii_ci` — that is ASCII-byte; this is Unicode `char`). Byte-identical extraction; blocked only on the home-module choice. doc_markers already depends on placeholder_words so the dependency direction is established. (Deferred this iter in favor of the contained caesar DEDUP; pick up when the home module is created.)
- µ-pw-10: WIRING — `contains_non_example_placeholder_word_with_entropy_hint` filters `!is_example`, so "example" alone does NOT trip the placeholder gate (doc_markers handles EXAMPLE separately). Assert `contains_non_example_placeholder_word_with_entropy_hint("example_key", upper, None)==false` BUT `contains_placeholder_word("example_key")==true`. Pins the example carve-out that prevents double-suppression.
