## µ-iso — entropy/isolated.rs (read in full, Iter 12 — GENUINELY NEW, not previously deep-read)
Isolated bare-secret entropy detection: a high-entropy token on a line with NO keyword anchor (`KEYWORD_FREE_ISOLATED_MIN_LEN=16`). Threshold selection + 3 sub-threshold "floor" shapes + the candidate-extraction grammar.
- µ-iso-01: `isolated_bare_entropy_threshold(t)` (line 42-48) — returns `t` iff `t.is_finite() && t > HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD`, else `MIXED_ALNUM_TOKEN_THRESHOLD`. Assert: a finite `t` above HIGH → passes through; `t==NaN` / `t==-inf` / `t <= HIGH` → falls back to MIXED. Pin both imported constants (read entropy/mod.rs) so the fallback floor is exact.
- µ-iso-02: `isolated_bare_entropy_floor_met` (line 50-60) — `entropy>=threshold` short-circuits true; ELSE only when `threshold <= MIXED_ALNUM_TOKEN_THRESHOLD` may one of the 3 sub-floors rescue it; when `threshold > MIXED`, sub-floors are NOT consulted (returns false). Assert a value below an elevated threshold is rejected even if it would pass a sub-floor (the elevated-threshold path disables the rescues).
- µ-iso-03: `mixed_separator_token_floor_met` (line 72-93) — const `MIXED_SEPARATOR_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=3.65`; requires `entropy>=3.65 AND len>=20 AND contains('_') AND every non-`_` byte is ASCII-alnum AND has_upper AND has_lower AND has_digit`. Positive: a 20+ char `Mixed_Case_99_Token...`; negatives: <20 len, no `_`, a non-alnum non-`_` byte, missing any of upper/lower/digit. Assert each rejection arm.
- µ-iso-04: `lower_dash_app_password_floor_met` (line 95-124) — const `3.9`; requires `entropy>=3.9 AND len==19 AND exactly 4 dash-groups of EXACTLY 4 chars, each `[a-z0-9]` with has_alpha AND has_digit, AND at least one non-hex alpha overall (has_non_hex)`. Positive: `abcd-12gh-9xk3-mn45` (19 chars, group shape, a non-hex letter like `g`/`h`/`x`/`k`/`m`/`n`). Negatives: len≠19, a group ≠4, an uppercase byte, an all-hex value (`abcd-1234-...` with only a-f digits → has_non_hex false → rejected — pins the "not just a UUID-ish hex" intent).
- µ-iso-05: `mixed_contiguous_token_floor_met` (line 126-149) — const `3.65`; `entropy>=3.65 AND len>=20 AND all bytes alnum AND upper+lower+digit AND !all_hex AND is_random_token(candidate)`. The `is_random_token` AND-gate is load-bearing: a pronounceable-but-mixed 20-char value fails. Assert: a random mixed token passes; an all-hex 20-char fails (all_hex); a dictionary-ish mixed token fails (is_random_token false). Cross to token_randomness (µ-tok-*).
- µ-iso-06: `isolated_bare_secret_entropy_decision` (line 208-228) ordering — (1) `is_canonical_non_secret_shape` → Err(CanonicalNonSecretShape); (2) entropy floor not met → Err(EntropyBelowFloor); (3) `!is_isolated_bare_secret_plausible` → Err(SecretPlausibilityRejected); else Ok(entropy). Assert each typed `StageId` Err in order (a value failing gate 1 never reaches gate 2). This is the Result-returning production path (the `_entropy` wrapper is a LAW10 test-only adapter, line 194-206).
- µ-iso-07: `collect_isolated_bare_candidates` dedup (line 180-191) — a value appearing twice on a line is inserted into `seen` once → exactly ONE `EntropyMatch`; assert the match's `line == line_idx + 1` (FIRST_SOURCE_LINE_NUMBER) and `offset == line_offset + candidate_offset` (absolute offset arithmetic). Pins 1-based line numbering + offset correctness.
- µ-iso-08: `is_likely_innocuous_line` short-circuit (line 30-32, 160-162) — an innocuous line yields NO candidates from both `has_isolated_bare_secret_candidate_with_lines` and `collect_isolated_bare_candidates`. Assert a line `is_likely_innocuous_line` returns true for produces zero matches (cross to entropy/keywords.rs).
- µ-iso-09: `isolated_bare_candidate(line, min_len)` core grammar (line 295-343) — trims whitespace + leading/trailing `;`/`,`; rejects `len<min_len` OR any internal whitespace OR no alpha OR (no digit AND not symbolic-alpha-only). Then accepts iff ANY of: standard_token / colon_separated_opaque / no_digit_symbolic / (no-`=` AND symbolic) / bang_led_symbolic. Assert: a clean 16+ alnum-with-digit token → Some; a token with an internal space → None; a 16+ all-letter token that is NOT symbolic-alpha-only → None (no digit).
- µ-iso-10: `colon_separated_opaque_candidate` (line 345-367) — exactly ONE `:`, no `://`, `left.len()>=20 AND right.len()>=16`, both halves all-alnum with alpha+digit. Assert: a `20+chars:16+chars` opaque pair → true; `://` present → false; 2 colons → false; left 19 → false; right 15 → false.
- µ-iso-11: `symbolic_alpha_only_opaque_candidate` (line 369-397) — `len>=18 AND no '://' AND all graphic non-quote AND NO digit AND has_upper AND has_lower AND punctuation>=3 AND alpha*2>=len AND is_random_token`. This is the no-digit symbolic path. Assert the `alpha*2>=len` ratio gate and `punctuation>=3` minimum; a quote byte (`"`/`'`/`` ` ``) → false; a digit → false (it routes to the digit path instead).
- µ-iso-12: `symbolic_isolated_bare_candidate` (line 399-413) — no `://`, no `:`/`,`, all graphic non-quote, `symbol_count>=2` (non-alnum bytes). Assert `symbol_count` exactly counts non-alnum and `>=2` is the floor (1 symbol → false).
- µ-iso-13: `has_non_padding_equals` (line 415-421) — trailing `=` run >2 → true (more than base64 padding); else any `=` BEFORE the trailing padding → true. Assert: `abc=def` (mid `=`) → true; `abcd==` (exactly 2 trailing pad) → false; `abc===` (3 trailing) → true; `abcdef` → false. Pins the base64-padding-vs-assignment distinction used by `bang_led_symbolic` (line 330-333).
- µ-iso-14: `visit_isolated_bare_candidates` two-mode walk (line 230-265) — if the WHOLE trimmed line is one candidate → visit it and RETURN (no token split); else, only if the line has whitespace, split on whitespace and test each token via `isolated_bare_candidate_in_span` (which strips leading/trailing `;`/`,`). Assert: a single-token line yields the whole-line candidate (offset 0 after trim); a multi-token line yields per-token candidates with correct offsets; a no-whitespace non-candidate line yields nothing.

## µ-cpfx — confidence/prefixes.rs (read in full, Iter 13)
KNOWN_PREFIXES (the single source of truth, 38 entries) + the 0.8 known-prefix confidence floor.
- µ-cpfx-01: KNOWN_PREFIXES single-source-of-truth — assert `KNOWN_PREFIXES.len()==38` AND that confidence/prefixes + `context::inference`/`context::placeholder`'s prefix-strip both consume THIS const (no second copy). The header documents the pre-2026-05-24 triple-duplication + drift (missing glcbt-/glrt-/xoxs-/vercel_/sbp_/0x/rk_test_/sk-; the inference copies missed PRIVATE KEY/-----BEGIN/TESTKEY_). A grep-gate test: no other `&[&str]` literal in scanner re-lists these prefixes.
- µ-cpfx-02: `known_prefix_body` longer-prefix-first ORDERING (load-bearing). `find_map` strips the FIRST matching prefix in list order; `sk-proj-` (line 43) precedes `sk-` (line 45), so `known_prefix_body("sk-proj-XYZ")==Some("XYZ")` NOT `Some("proj-XYZ")`. Assert this AND `known_prefix_body("sk-ant-XYZ")==Some("XYZ")` and a bare `known_prefix_body("sk-XYZ")==Some("XYZ")`; a no-prefix value → None. If the list were reordered shortest-first the body would be wrong — pin the ordering.
- µ-cpfx-03: `known_prefix_confidence_floor` → `Some(0.8)` for a clean known-prefix credential; `None` when `contains_placeholder_word` OR `decoded_contains_placeholder` OR `is_degenerate_repeat`. Assert `ghp_<realbody>`→Some(0.8); `ghp_EXAMPLE_token`→None; `AKIAXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX` (16 `X`)→None (degenerate). The 154 docs-example-FP fix (line 86-92) + the degenerate-repeat hole (line 94-98).
- µ-cpfx-04: body-placeholder guard (line 107-110) — even a known prefix returns None if `bytes_contain_placeholder_word(body)`. Assert `ghp_this_is_fake_token`→None (fake in body) but `ghp_<random>`→Some(0.8).
- µ-cpfx-05: no-prefix → None (line 113). A random non-prefixed high-entropy value → None.
- µ-cpfx-06: WIRING — the 0.8 floor reaches `scan_postprocess` `final_score.max(0.8)`; assert a clean known-prefix credential's final confidence ≥0.8, AND that a placeholder known-prefix is NOT re-lifted (the lift-back bug the guards close). Real-A→real-B to scan_postprocess.

## µ-agen — adjudicate/generic.rs (read in full, Iter 13)
Generic-bridge keyword/value-shape signals + the GenericValueShapeStage reason-string table.
- µ-agen-01: `GenericBridgeSignal::stage_id` (line 13-20) — KeywordBoundary→GenericKeywordBoundary, NamedDetectorOwnedKeyword→GenericNamedDetectorOwnedKeyword, BareAuthUnstructured→BareAuthUnstructured, ValueShape(s)→GenericValueShape(s). Assert all 4.
- µ-agen-02: `generic_bridge_keyword_boundary_rejected` — only `pass`/`auth` require a word boundary (`generic_bridge_keyword_requires_word_boundary`, line 49-51). Assert `"pass"` inside `bypass` (prev `y` lowercase, keyword_first `p` lowercase → no boundary) → rejected true; `"pass"` at word start → not rejected; a non-pass/auth keyword (`token`) → never rejected regardless of boundary.
- µ-agen-03: `keyword_has_word_boundary` (line 55-66) — `keyword_start==0`→true; prev non-alphabetic→true; prev lowercase + keyword_first UPPERCASE (camelCase hinge `apiPass`)→true; prev lowercase + keyword_first lowercase (`bypass`)→false. Assert each of the 4 arms.
- µ-agen-04: `generic_bridge_bare_auth_rejected` = `keyword.eq_ignore_ascii_case("auth") && !bare_auth_value_allowed(value)`. `bare_auth_value_allowed` = `is_structured_dotted_token(value)` OR (`!contains('.')` AND has a non-alnum byte AND `passes_secret_strength_checks`). Assert: keyword "auth" + a JWT-dotted value → not rejected; "auth" + a plain dictionary word → rejected; "Auth" (case) treated same.
- µ-agen-05: `generic_bridge_canonical_hex_placeholder_stage` — `allow_canonical_hex_key && is_known_example_credential(value)` → `Some(SharedSuppression("algorithmic_placeholder"))`; else None. Assert with allow=true + an EXAMPLE value → Some; allow=false → None; allow=true + real value → None.
- µ-agen-06: `GenericValueShapeStage::as_str` COHERENCE (line 106-134) — the 25 variants each map to their EXACT reason string the reporter renders; SharedShape/SharedSuppression/DecodedBenignText pass their `&'static str` through. Assert the full table (e.g. PureIdentifierNoDigit→"pure_identifier_no_digit", Base64Blob→"base64_blob", TrimmedAwsArn→"trimmed_aws_arn", RandomByteBlob→"random_byte_blob"); a drift here mislabels a suppression in the SARIF/JSON output.

## µ-cdoc — context/documentation.rs (read in full, Iter 13)
`documentation_line_flags` (markdown-fence + Python docstring state machine) and its quote-aware helpers.
- µ-cdoc-01: markdown fence — a ```` ``` ```` line toggles `in_markdown_code_block`; lines INSIDE the fenced block are flagged true (incl. the opening fence line). Assert a 4-line text ```` ```\nSECRET\n``` \nx ```` flags lines 0,1,2 and not 3.
- µ-cdoc-02: Python docstring toggle — a self-contained `"""x"""` (even delimiter count, opens at a string-open position) flags that line; an opener-only `"""` flags every subsequent line until a closing `"""`. Assert both (single-line and multi-line spans), and `'''` triple-quote behaves identically.
- µ-cdoc-03: `before_line_comment` quote-awareness (line 46-85) — strips a `//` line comment ONLY when not inside a string; a `//` inside a regular `"…"` is NOT a comment; a `"""` triple-quote is skipped 3 bytes; backslash-escape inside a quote is honored. Assert `before_line_comment` on `x = "a//b" // c` returns the slice up to the REAL `//` (not the in-string one).
- µ-cdoc-04: `opens_docstring` recall-preserving gates (line 111-120) — returns false if `has_assignment_operator(before)` (`x = """data` is runtime string data → code, NOT doc) OR the bytes before the delimiter have unbalanced regular quotes. Assert `x = """data`→false (assignment), `"""data`→true, `key " : """ : ' ": " SECRET` (quote noise, unbalanced)→false. This is the fix that stopped a stray `"""` from suppressing every credential below it.
- µ-cdoc-05: `has_balanced_regular_quotes` — even count of BOTH `"` and `'` → true. Assert `a"b"c`→true (2 dquotes), `a"b`→false (1).
- µ-cdoc-06: `is_triple_quote_at` — exactly 3 identical quote bytes. Assert `"""`→true, `'''`→true, `""`(2)→false, `"'"`→false, `""x`→false.

## µ-cph — context/placeholder.rs (read in full, Iter 13)
Value-shape example/placeholder heuristics (no hardcoded credential lists). `is_known_example_credential` + hex/sequential sub-checks.
- µ-cph-01 (LANDED Iter 13, this commit): `is_known_example_credential` EXAMPLE/EXAMPLEKEY suffix is zero-alloc `ascii_ci::ends_with_ignore_ascii_case` (NOT per-candidate Unicode `to_uppercase()`); byte-identical (the suffixes are ASCII; no non-ASCII char's uppercase is a bare ASCII letter). DONE in `tests/gap/context_example_suffix_ascii_ci.rs` (5 hot callers: adjudicate generic/entropy/mod + suppression::decision x2).
- µ-cph-02: x/X-masking filler — `len>=16 AND x_count > len*3/4`. Assert `XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX` (16 X)→true; `xXxXxXxXxXxXxXxX`→true; a 16-char value with exactly 12 `x` (==3/4, not `>`)→false (strict `>` boundary); `<16` chars all-x→false.
- µ-cph-03: `is_empty_input_hash` — EXACT MD5/SHA1/SHA256-of-empty at exact lengths 32/40/64. Assert `d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e`→true, `da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709`→true, the SHA256 empty digest→true; a 32-char NON-empty-hash hex→false; a 31/33-char value→false (length-exact guard).
- µ-cph-04: `is_sequential_placeholder` — strips known prefix first (line 60), body<8→false, all-same-byte body→true, repeated-2-byte-pair body (len>=8)→true. Assert `ghp_aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa`→true (body all-`a` after strip), `abababababab`→true (pair), `xy`→false (<8), `realbody1234`→false.
- µ-cph-05: `is_hex_sequential_placeholder` — body all-hexdigit AND len>=16; single-byte monotonic ascending/descending with steps `> (len-1)*9/10`, OR pair-column sequential. Assert `0123456789abcdef`→true, `fedcba9876543210`→true, `ghp_0123456789abcdef`→true (strip prefix), a 16-hex random non-monotonic→false, a non-hex body→false (early return).
- µ-cph-06: `hex_forward_step`/`hex_reverse_step` wrap + case — forward 9→a and f→0; reverse a→9 and 0→f; both `to_ascii_lowercase` first. Assert via crafted bodies (`89abcdef...`, `FEDCBA...` uppercase still descending).
- µ-cph-07: `credential_body_without_known_prefix` LAW10 (line 119-121) — unknown prefix → `unwrap_or(credential)` inspects the FULL credential (over-suppresses LESS, recall-safe). Assert a no-prefix value's body is itself; a `ghp_`-prefixed value's body is the stripped tail.

## µ-cinf — context/inference.rs (read in full, Iter 13)
Line-context classifier (`infer_context`) + Tier-B test-path rules + the 2KiB-capped context window.
- µ-cinf-01: `infer_context_with_documentation` PRECEDENCE cascade (line 65-98) — order: idx-out-of-range→Unknown; test_file→TestCode; encrypted_block→Encrypted; commented_assignment→Assignment; comment→Comment; documentation flag→Documentation; test_function→TestCode; assignment→Assignment; else `infer_default_context`. Assert each earlier branch WINS over a later-eligible one (e.g. a `tests/` path on an assignment line → TestCode, not Assignment; a commented assignment `# k=v` → Assignment, not Comment).
- µ-cinf-02: `is_test_file` Tier-B (`data/test-path-rules.toml`) — filename_prefixes (stem startswith, `stem.len()>prefix.len()`, ci), filename_suffixes (filename `ends_with`), path_components (`path_has_any_component`). Assert `test_foo.py`→true, `foo_test.go`→true, `src/tests/x.rs`→true, `production.rs`→false. `parse_test_path_rules` rejects: `schema_version!=1`, an empty list ("must contain at least one entry"), a `/` or `\` in an entry ("must not contain path separators"), a `.` in a PathComponent ("must be a path segment"), a duplicate ("duplicate"). Assert each Err substring.
- µ-cinf-03: `has_assignment_operator` — matches `:=`/`->`/`=` but NOT a comparison via `is_comparison_operator`. Assert `a = b`→true, `a := b`→true, `a -> b`→true, `a == b`→false, `a != b`→false, `a >= b`→false, `a <= b`→false.
- µ-cinf-04: `has_yaml_mapping` — `": "` present AND NOT `starts_with("- ")`. Assert `key: value`→true, `- item: x`→false (list item), `nospace:value`→false (needs the space).
- µ-cinf-05: `is_comment_line` — `//`,`#`,`--`(but NOT `---`),`/*`,`<!--`,`<#`,`"* "`,`"*/"`,`"rem "`,`"REM "`. Assert each prefix true; `---` (YAML doc separator)→false; `-- sql comment`→true.
- µ-cinf-06: `is_commented_assignment_line` — strip a comment prefix (`strip_comment_prefix`), trim trailing `*/`/`-->`, then `has_assignment_operator || has_yaml_mapping`. Assert `# key = val`→true (→ Assignment context, line 78), `// foo: bar`→true (yaml), `<!-- k=v -->`→true, `# plain prose`→false.
- µ-cinf-07: `is_in_test_function` 100-line lookback (line 285-350) — openers `def test_`/`class Test`/`it(`/`describe(`/`test(`/`#[test]`/`#[cfg(test)]`/`#[tokio::test`/`func Test`/`@Test` → true; a NON-test boundary (`class `, non-test `def `/`async def `, non-test `func `, non-test `function `, or a rust `fn` WITHOUT a `#[test]`-class attr in the preceding ≤3 lines) STOPS the scan → false. Assert: a credential 5 lines into `def test_x` → TestCode; a rust `fn helper()` with no test attr → boundary-stop false; a rust `fn t()` preceded by `#[tokio::test]` → true.
- µ-cinf-08: `is_in_encrypted_block` 10-line lookback — `$ANSIBLE_VAULT`/`ENC[`/`sops:`/`sealed-secrets`/`-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----`/`-----BEGIN AGE ENCRYPTED` within 10 lines above (inclusive) → Encrypted. Assert a marker 9 lines up → Encrypted, 11 lines up → not.
- µ-cinf-09: `surrounding_line_window` 2KiB cap (line 352-402, Law-7 anti-quadratic) — for ordinary short lines the window is byte-identical to an uncapped ±radius walk; for a pathological no-newline 164KiB line the per-match window is capped at MAX_WINDOW_BYTES (2KiB) each side → O(1) not O(line_len) (the 164KiB/8K-match 18s→fast fix). Assert: small multi-line text radius=1 → the exact ±1-line window; a giant single line → window ≤ ~4KiB centered on the offset; char-boundary safety (`ceil_char_boundary`/`floor_char_boundary`) on a multibyte cut.
- µ-cinf-10: `infer_default_context` — a `"` or `'` present → StringLiteral, else Unknown. Assert `foo "bar"`→StringLiteral, `foo bar`→Unknown.
- µ-cinf-11: `is_comparison_operator` (line 266-274) — only relevant for `=`; comparison iff `before ∈ {=,!,>,<}` OR `after == '='`. Assert `a==b`→comparison (after first `=` is `=`), `a>=b`→comparison (before is `>`), `a!=b`→comparison, `a=b`→NOT; `:=`/`->` never treated as comparison (early `operator != "="` → false).
- µ-cinf-12 (Iter-15 Gemini-missed public entry; thin): `infer_context` (L44, pub — the only `pub` fn here) = `documentation_line_flags(lines)` precompute → `infer_context_with_documentation` (µ-cinf-01). Its distinct job is OWNING the doc-flag precompute that the `_with_documentation` form takes as input (so callers with the flags reuse them). Assert it produces the SAME CodeContext as calling `_with_documentation` with freshly-computed flags (differential, low-value — covered transitively by µ-cinf-01).

## µ-sig — confidence/signals.rs (read in full, Iter 14)
`ConfidenceSignals` struct + `is_sensitive_path` (cached AC over sensitive filenames/extensions).
- µ-sig-01: `ConfidenceSignals` field contract — `entropy` is bits/byte (0.0..=8.0) NOT normalized (use `entropy::normalized_entropy` for 0..1); `match_length` is byte length. COHERENCE pin: a constructed signals carries the RAW entropy; cross-check the normalized form is a separate rescale.
- µ-sig-02: `is_sensitive_path` AC positives — `.env`/`.env.local`/`credentials`/`secrets`/`api_keys`/`.npmrc`/`.pem`/`.key`/`.p12`/`.github/workflows`/`Jenkinsfile`/`helm/values`/`config.toml` etc match case-INsensitively as SUBSTRINGS. Assert a representative true set + `src/main.rs`→false + uppercase `.ENV`→true. The AC is built ONCE (OnceLock cache).
- µ-sig-03: Law-10 loud recall-preserving fallback (line 77-103) — if the AC fails to BUILD (an invalid marker added), it `eprintln!`s a loud `keyhog: BUG —` message AND returns `true` for EVERY path (`ac.as_ref().is_none_or(|ac| ac.is_match(path))`: None→true), failing toward recall, never silently dropping the sensitive-file boost. Assert the `is_none_or` semantics (None→true). The old `.ok()` silently returned false fleet-wide — that's the bug this guards. `confidence_signals_no_unwrap_expect` gate forbids panics here.
- µ-sig-04: CAPTURE Tier-B violation — the 40+ sensitive markers (lines 33-74) are HARDCODED in source; per CLAUDE.md Tier-B (hardcoded lists banned) they should live in `rules/sensitive-paths.toml` like the already-Tier-B disclaimer-phrases.toml / test-path-rules.toml. Move + parse with a schema_version + validation (lowercase-ascii, non-empty, dedup).

## µ-aent — adjudicate/entropy.rs (read in full, Iter 14)
EntropyShapeStage reason table + the entropy-fallback example/placeholder gate.
- µ-aent-01: `EntropyShapeStage::as_str` COHERENCE (line 44-83) — 36 variants each map to their EXACT `entropy_*` reason string; `SuppressionStage(reason)` passes through. Assert the full table (EnglishProse→"entropy_english_prose", UuidV4OrSubstring→"entropy_uuid_v4_or_substring", CanonicalNonSecretShape→"entropy_canonical_non_secret_shape", SecretPlausibilityRejected→"entropy_secret_plausibility_rejected", …). A drift mislabels the SARIF/JSON suppression reason the operator reads.
- µ-aent-02: `EntropyFallbackSignal::stage_id` / `EntropyGenerationSignal::stage_id` — NamedDetectorOwnedAssignment→`StageId::EntropyNamedDetectorOwnedAssignment`, ValueShape(s)→`EntropyValueShape(s)`; SuppressionStage(id)→id passthrough. Assert.
- µ-aent-03: `entropy_fallback_example_suppression_stage` OFF-lift (line 127-143) — builds `KnownExampleSuppressionCtx::with_entropy(path, Unknown, source, entropy, allow_canonical_hex_key=false, allow_base64_blob_shape=isolated_bare_token, allow_encoded_text_secret=false)` where `isolated_bare_token = keyword==ISOLATED_BARE_ENTROPY_LABEL`, then delegates to `suppress_known_example_credential_stage`. Assert an EXAMPLE value off-lift → `Some(SuppressionStage(reason))`; pin the isolated-bare-token base64 flag wiring.
- µ-aent-04: ON-lift content-marker retention (line 145-171) — when `canonical_lift`, the canonical SHAPE is released but CONTENT markers still suppress: `is_known_example_credential`→"algorithmic_placeholder", `contains_placeholder_word`→"placeholder_word", `has_three_or_more_consecutive_identical`→"repetitive_run"; a `is_uuid_v4_shape` value → `None` (RELEASED for MoE arbitration, line 157-159); else delegate with `allow_canonical_hex_key=true`. Assert: a UUID under lift → None (recovered, the CredData UUID miss class), an EXAMPLE/placeholder under lift → STILL suppressed. The "shape released, content retained" contract.

## µ-plaus — entropy/plausibility.rs (read in full, Iter 14)
Candidate/secret plausibility (Lenient vs Strict) + the known-non-secret + strength gates.
- µ-plaus-01: `PlausibilityContext::new(is_credential_context, allow_canonical_shapes)` + Default (both false). The two flags drive the `is_known_non_secret` releases. Assert const ctor + Default.
- µ-plaus-02: `is_known_non_secret` UUID release (line 31-35) — `!allow_canonical_shapes && looks_like_entropy_uuid_shape` → true (non-secret) OFF lift; ON lift (allow_canonical_shapes) the whole-value UUID is RELEASED (LaunchDarkly/Heroku/PowerBI CredData miss class). Assert both.
- µ-plaus-03: `is_known_non_secret` canonical-hex (line 42-53) — `looks_like_entropy_canonical_hex_digest` AND (`!is_credential_context`→non-secret; OR len∈{40,128}→non-secret EVEN in cred context (sha1/sha512); OR len==64 && !allow_canonical_shapes→non-secret). Plus `data:image/`→non-secret. Assert: 40-hex always non-secret; 64-hex in cred ctx ON lift → released; 32-hex in cred ctx → NOT non-secret via this gate.
- µ-plaus-04: `matches_universal_rejection` (line 78-106) — the reject set: `://`, leading `/`/`./`/`../`, `${{`/`{{`/`${`, `(?`, `^`, `ssh-`/`ecdsa-`, `eyJ`+exactly-2-dots, `$ANSIBLE_VAULT`, `ENC[`, `-----BEGIN`, `Ag`+len>40, `age1`, `vault:`, `AQI`, `CiQ`, Windows drive `X:\`/`X:/`, ```` ``` ````, `---`, `===`. Assert a representative true per class + a normal secret false.
- µ-plaus-05: `has_low_alnum_ratio` — `alnum/len < 0.5`. Boundary: `!!!!abcd` (4/8==0.5, NOT <0.5)→false; `!!!!!abc` (3/8<0.5)→true (Unicode `is_alphanumeric`).
- µ-plaus-06: `passes_secret_strength_checks` symbolic relaxation (line 114-152) — `passes_secret_shape_checks` first; entropy≥HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD(4.5)→true; in credential context: `has_symbol && entropy≥3.5`→true (the `1E1B3b4Ho$U4kYBi`/`Y6NPMwS*...` band) OR `!has_symbol && has_alpha && has_digit && len≥20 && entropy≥MIXED_ALNUM_TOKEN_THRESHOLD`→true. Assert a symbolic ~3.95-entropy value in cred ctx → passes; a pure-alnum ~3.95 → fails (keeps the 4.5 floor).
- µ-plaus-07: `passes_secret_shape_checks` (line 221-273) — outside cred ctx: all-hex len>10→reject (digest); all-same-char len>4→reject; len>16 && unique_char_count<8→reject; len>16 && second_half_entropy<2.5→reject; `looks_like_program_identifier`→reject (the ApiKeyService.java 149-FP fix); `is_dash_segmented_alnum_decoy`→reject. Assert each gate with a positive + the carve-out (hex IN cred ctx is allowed).
- µ-plaus-08: `is_isolated_bare_secret_plausible` (line 154-181) — leading-slash base64 → true; dotted: `len≥40 && !placeholder && is_structured_dotted_token`→true, `eyJ`-dotted→false, `looks_like_dotted_source_identifier`→false; else Lenient plausibility AND shape checks. Assert the branches.
- µ-plaus-09: `is_isolated_leading_slash_base64_secret` (line 183-219) — `/`-prefixed, len≥40, not placeholder, alnum-ratio ok, trailing padding≤2 and no interior `=`, base64 charset, has upper+lower+digit, `shannon_entropy≥4.8`, shape checks. Assert a `/`-led high-entropy base64 blob → true; a short or low-entropy one → false.
- µ-plaus-10: `unique_char_count` ASCII fast-path (line 275-294) — 256-bool array for ASCII, HashSet for non-ASCII; assert identical counts on an ASCII and a non-ASCII value. `second_half_entropy` uses `floor_char_boundary` (multibyte-safe split).
- µ-plaus-12 (Iter-15 Gemini-missed public entries; thin): `is_candidate_plausible` (L302) = `passes_plausibility_checks(Lenient)`, `is_secret_plausible` (L315) = `passes_plausibility_checks(Strict)` — the two pub(crate) entries the keyword extractor (µ-kw-09) calls. The ONLY difference is the `PlausibilityMode`: Strict additionally runs `passes_secret_strength_checks` (µ-plaus-06). Assert a symbolic ~3.95-entropy cred-ctx value passes BOTH; a borderline-strength value passes is_candidate_plausible (Lenient) but FAILS is_secret_plausible (Strict) — the lenient-vs-strict split the quoted-span (strict) vs `=`-RHS (lenient) sources rely on.
- µ-plaus-11: `is_placeholder_ci` (line 328-341) — `placeholder_keywords` via `ci_find_nonempty` OR `contains_placeholder_word_with_entropy_hint(value, Some(shannon_entropy(bytes)))` OR `bytes_contain_entropy_placeholder_marker`. CAPTURE Law-7 NON-fix: the eager `Some(shannon_entropy(bytes))` is a deliberate single-compute MEMO shared across the word loop — passing `None` would RECOMPUTE shannon per colliding word (pathological 40+char multi-marker value). Do NOT "optimize" to None; it would regress the pathological case. (Documented so a future pass doesn't mistake it for a wasteful eager compute.)

## µ-fp — context/false_positive.rs (read in full, Iter 14)
Match-context + line-context false-positive detectors, all on raw bytes via ci_find (the documented per-match-String-alloc Law-7 fix).
- µ-fp-01: `is_false_positive_match_context_with_path` dispatch (line 16-42) — go.sum / integrity-hash / git-lfs-pointer / renovate-digest / CORS / http-cache / disclaimer-comment, NO per-match String alloc (the earlier shape allocated a window copy + 8 substring searches per match). Assert one positive per detector + a normal credential line → false.
- µ-fp-02: `has_disclaimer_comment_bytes` (line 109-135) — a comment marker (`//`,`#`,`--`,`/*`,`<!--`,`rem `) with a whitespace/start boundary AND NOT inside ASCII quotes, whose tail contains a Tier-B disclaimer phrase → true. Assert `KEY=sk_live_... // not a real key`→true; a `//` inside a quoted string is skipped (`is_inside_ascii_quotes`); a disclaimer phrase with NO comment marker → false (anchored to the marker). `parse_disclaimer_phrases` Tier-B Err substrings: schema_version!=1, empty entry, non-lowercase-ASCII, duplicate, empty list.
- µ-fp-03: `is_go_sum_checksum_bytes` (line 203-248) — finds a token-boundaried `h1:`; suppresses if the path `ends_with_ignore_ascii_case("go.sum")` OR strict shape (≥2 preceding whitespace fields + a 44-char base64 digest + a trailing whitespace/EOL boundary). Assert a real go.sum line by PATH and by STRICT SHAPE; a bare `h1:` substring in prose → not suppressed.
- µ-fp-04: `is_integrity_hash_bytes` (line 268-300) — `integrity` present AND a `sha256-`/`sha512-` SRI value that is a base64-scalar (`is_base64_scalar`: len≥8, %4==0, base64 charset). Assert an npm lockfile integrity line → true; `integrity` alone → false.
- µ-fp-05: `is_configmap_binary_data_context` (line 302-349) — a base64-scalar value line (`key: <b64>`, yaml-scalar key) indented MORE than a `binaryData:` header found within 8 lines above at LOWER indent. Assert a k8s configmap binaryData block value → true; the same value not under binaryData → false.
- µ-fp-06: git-lfs pointer (line 376-427) — `is_git_lfs_oid_line` (`oid sha256:` + exactly 64 hex) + a version line nearby (±3, `nearby_lines_contain`) + a size line following (±3, `following_lines_contain`). The two orchestrators (`_with_lines` line-array, `_bytes` byte-split) SHARE the 3 leaf predicates (DEDUP'd). Assert a full LFS pointer block → true; an oid line alone → false; oid with 63/65 hex → false (exact 64).
- µ-fp-07: `is_renovate_digest_match_context` (line 449-509) — a `renovate/<branch>` where the MATCH OFFSET falls inside an 8+-hex run within the branch name. Assert a renovate branch digest with the offset inside the hex run → true; offset outside → false. CAPTURE: `is_renovate_digest_context_with_lines` (line 441-447) returns `false` unconditionally — renovate needs the match OFFSET the lines-path lacks, so that `|| false` term in `is_false_positive_context_with_path` is intentionally dead (document; not a stub to "implement", it's not-applicable-in-this-path).
- µ-fp-08: `is_cors_header_bytes`/`is_http_cache_header_bytes` (line 533-553) — header NAME (before `:`, `[alnum-]` only) eq_ignore_ascii_case one of 8 CORS headers, or `etag`. Assert `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`→true, `ETag: "abc"`→true, `Authorization: x`→false, a non-header line→false.
- µ-fp-09: `header_name_matches` boundary (line 555-573) — name must be non-empty, `[alnum-]` only, and an EXACT ci match; a value mentioning a header name mid-line (not at the name position) → false. Assert.
- µ-fp-11 (Iter-15 Gemini-missed public entries; thin): `is_false_positive_match_context` (L6, pub(crate)) = `is_false_positive_match_context_with_path(.., None)` — the no-pre-lowered-path entry over µ-fp-01 (callers without a cached lowered path). `is_false_positive_context` (L168, pub(crate)) = thin alias for `is_false_positive_context_with_path` (the line-context dispatch over µ-fp-02..09); the `_with_path` name is now a vestigial alias since the body went byte-wise CI (`ends_with_ignore_ascii_case`) and no longer needs a pre-lowered path (doc comment L162-167). Assert the wrapper == the `_with_path` form with `None`; low-value (covered transitively).
- µ-fp-10: `line_at_offset` (line 511-531) vs `surrounding_line_window(.,0)` — `line_at_offset` returns `(line, relative_offset)` UNCAPPED (renovate needs the offset), while the radius-0 window caps at 2KiB; they intentionally differ on a pathological no-newline long line. Both use `ceil/floor_char_boundary` (multibyte-safe). Assert the relative offset is correct across a multi-line text.

## µ-kw — entropy/keywords.rs (read in full, Iter 15 — supersedes the Iter-14 PARTIAL stub)
Per-line credential-keyword surface detection + candidate value extraction (the entropy keyword-scan front door). `normalize_assignment_keyword` is IMPORTED from `engine::phase2_generic::keywords` (not defined here — see that module for its µ-block).
- µ-kw-01 (LANDED Iter 14, c102cb27d): `authorization_header_value` (L418, pub(crate)) matches `bearer `/`basic ` via `ascii_ci::starts_with_ignore_ascii_case` on raw bytes (NOT a per-line `rhs.to_ascii_lowercase()` alloc); byte-identical (schemes ASCII, token sliced from the ORIGINAL `rhs`, 7/6-byte offsets are char boundaries). Returns the FIRST whitespace-delimited field after the scheme; `Digest`/non-`Authorization`/schemeless → None. Test `tests/gap/entropy_authorization_scheme_ci.rs`.
- µ-kw-02 (LANDED Iter 15): `xml_assignment_tag` (L435) + `xml_assignment_value` (L453, now pub(crate)) — replaced TWO `format!("</{tag}>")` per XML-shaped line with one zero-alloc `find_xml_close_tag` (memchr over `<`, exact `</`+name+`>` match). Byte-identical to `haystack.find(&format!("</{tag}>"))` (tag is a `>`-terminated whitespace-split name, no `<`/`>`/space); the close offset is reused by value (was a 2nd format!). Test `tests/gap/entropy_xml_close_tag_search.rs` (incl. stray-`<` skip `<password>a<b>c</password>`→`a<b>c`, mismatched-close `</token>` for `<password>` → None) + facade `testing::entropy_keywords::xml_assignment_value`.
- µ-kw-03: `line_has_credential_assignment_surface` (L269, pub(crate)) — true iff `authorization_header_value.is_some()` OR `assignment_keyword_for_line` normalizes to a credential keyword. `is_keyword_assignment_line` (L44) calls it BEFORE the Tier-B `secret_keywords` AC scan (short-circuit). Assert `Authorization: Bearer x`→true, `api_key = v`→true, `count = 5`→false, `import os`→false (import-like rejected upstream at L46).
- µ-kw-04: `assignment_keyword_for_line` (L276, pub(crate)) RIGHT-to-LEFT separator scan — XML tag wins first; else iterate `=`/`:` separators in REVERSE (`char_indices().rev()`), for each take `line[..sep]`, `rsplit` on non-`[alnum _ - .]` → first non-empty = key, normalize; return the FIRST (rightmost) key that `normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential`; else the first normalized non-credential key seen (fallback). Assert: `host: x; api_key: secretval` → credential (rightmost credential wins over the leftmost `host` non-credential); pin the rightmost-credential-beats-leftmost-noncredential order.
- µ-kw-05: `normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential` (L306, pub(crate)) — (a) `separated_secret_suffix`: contains `_` AND last `_`-segment ∈ {key,secret,token,password,passwd,pwd} → true (`db_secret`/`foo_token`/`my_key`→true); (b) else compact path: strip `_`, lowercase into a 128-byte STACK buffer (no heap), match `compact_assignment_keyword_bytes_are_credential`; >128 non-`_` bytes → `_slow` byte-iterator path. Assert `accessKey`→true (compact "accesskey"), `clientSecret`→true, `username`→false, a >128-char non-keyword → slow-path false.
- µ-kw-06: `CREDENTIAL_COMPACT_KEYWORDS` (L328) — EXACTLY 26 entries (password,passwd,pwd,passphrase,token,secret,credential,bearer,authorization,apikey,accesskey,authkey,privatekey,signingkey,encryptionkey,masterkey,secretkey,sessionkey,clientsecret,appsecret,salt,nonce,seed,hmacsalt,hmacseed,passwordsalt). `compact_assignment_keyword_bytes_are_credential` (L357) = exact-match-any OR `ends_with` salt/nonce/seed (so `hmacSalt`/`appSeed`/`xNonce` match via suffix). Assert the count==26, a representative exact hit, a suffix-only hit (`foononce`→true, `food`→false).
- µ-kw-07: compact slow-path TRIO `compact_normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential_slow`/`_eq`/`_ends_with` (L366-405) — byte-iterators that skip `_`+lowercase on the fly (no buffer), used only when normalized has >128 non-`_` bytes. Differential pin: for ANY normalized key, slow-path verdict == fast-path verdict (assert equality on a <128 key forced through both AND a >128 key). `_ends_with` reverse-iterates matching the suffix backwards (boundary: `suffix_index==0` after full consume → true).
- µ-kw-08: `clean_candidate_value` (L407) returns `&str` (no alloc) — trim, `trim_matches` `" ' \` ; ,`, cut at first whitespace/`&`/`<`, re-trim the same quote/`;`/`,` set. Assert `"sk_live_abc";`→`sk_live_abc`, `tok & more`→`tok` (cut at `&`), `val<tag`→`val` (cut at `<`), quotes stripped both ends.
- µ-kw-09: `extract_candidates_internal` (L147) source ORDER + dedup — `is_likely_concatenation_fragment` short-circuits to empty (traces `ConcatenationFragmentLine`); else push from (1) auth-header value (lenient), (2) xml value (lenient, allow_structured_dotted=true), (3) first `=`-or-`:` RHS (lenient, dotted=true), (4) each `"`/`'`-quoted span (STRICT). `push_candidate` closure: `clean_candidate_value` → reject `<min_length` (traces CredentialContextTooShort/KeywordFreeTooShort) → `is_structured_dotted_token` bypass OR strict→`is_secret_plausible`/lenient→`is_candidate_plausible` with `PlausibilityContext::new(is_credential_context, allow_canonical_shapes)` → dedup by value before push. Assert the 4-source order + that a quoted span uses STRICT while the `=`-RHS uses LENIENT.
- µ-kw-10: `extract_candidates` (L95) vs `extract_candidates_with_rejections` (L130) — both delegate to `_internal` with `trace_rejections` false/true; public `extract_candidates` returns `.candidates` only. `_internal` final `rejections.retain` (L236) drops any rejection whose value was ALSO accepted (a value reachable by two sources, rejected strict but accepted lenient, is NOT reported rejected). Assert a value one source rejects + another accepts appears ONLY in candidates.
- µ-kw-11: `is_likely_innocuous_line` (L60, pub(super)) — a URI-scheme-prefixed line WITH a credential-assignment surface returns FALSE (the `https://user:pass@` carve-out, L68); else import/from/require/use/package/include lines → true; `sha256:`/`sha512:`/`sha1:`/`md5:`/`git-sha:` (after quote-trim) → true; a 40-char all-hex (after quote-trim) → true (git-SHA decoy). Assert each, plus `https://u:p@h`→false (credential survives).
- µ-kw-12: `find_keyword_assignment_lines` (L31, pub(super)) collects `(index, line)` for every `is_keyword_assignment_line`; `is_keyword_assignment_line` rejects `is_import_like` (import/package/`use `/`from `/`require(`), accepts on `line_has_credential_assignment_surface`, else requires a Tier-B `secret_keywords` substring (`ci_find_nonempty`) AND a `=`/`:`. Assert `import secret_key` → NOT collected (import-like beats keyword), `api_key=v` → collected.
- µ-kw-13: `KeywordContext.allow_canonical_shapes` (L28) CredData recall lift — set ONLY when `ml_enabled && entropy_ml_authoritative && is_credential_context`; releases the extraction-time UUID + hex32/40/64/128 canonical-shape drop so the MoE arbitrates (the ~83% never-generate-a-candidate miss class: UUID + hex64/AES-256). Keyword-FREE path keeps it false (no anchor ⇒ no lift) → SecretBench mirror precision byte-identical. COHERENCE pin with µ-plaus-02/03 (the same flag threads into PlausibilityContext via L188).

## µ-pen — confidence/penalties.rs (read in full, Iter 15)
Post-ML confidence shaping: NaN-safety, shape/placeholder penalties, calibration multiplier, path penalty. Every public entry funnels through `finalize_confidence`.
- µ-pen-01: `finalize_confidence` (L20) NaN sentinel — `score.is_nan()` → `CONFIDENCE_MIN` (NOT clamp; `f64::NAN.clamp(0,1)==NaN`), else `clamp(CONFIDENCE_MIN, CONFIDENCE_MAX)`. Barrier guards a GPU/shader/adversarial-weights NaN from serializing as `confidence: NaN`. Assert NaN→CONFIDENCE_MIN, +Inf→CONFIDENCE_MAX, -Inf→CONFIDENCE_MIN, 0.5→0.5. EVERY other public fn here calls it on every return path (calibration bypass, path no-path/no-penalize, post-ML end).
- µ-pen-02: `char_diversity` (L64) — unique-byte / len over a 256-bool array (by byte, ASCII+non-ASCII); empty→1.0. Assert `aaaa`→0.25, `abcd`→1.0, ``→1.0. Drives the named (<0.1) and generic (<0.3) shape gates.
- µ-pen-03: `longest_repeat_run_len` (L88) + `max_repeat_run` (L109) + `is_degenerate_repeat`/`DEGENERATE_RUN_LEN=10` (L124/133) — absolute longest identical-byte run; `max_repeat_run`=run/len; `is_degenerate_repeat`=run≥10. The ABSOLUTE arm is load-bearing: `AKIAXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX` has a 16-X run but ratio EXACTLY 0.8 (NOT >0.8), so only `is_degenerate_repeat` catches it. Assert run len on `AKIAXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX`==16, ratio==0.8, is_degenerate==true; a real 64-hex body's run ~2-3 → is_degenerate false. SINGLE-SOURCE pin: `prefixes::known_prefix_confidence_floor` consumes `is_degenerate_repeat` so a known-prefix placeholder is NOT re-floored to 0.8 (parallel to the placeholder-word skip).
- µ-pen-04: `apply_post_ml_penalties_with_encoded_text_lift` (L157) named-vs-generic branch — empty cred → score unchanged. Placeholder (surface `contains_placeholder_word` OR decode-through `decode_structure::evidence().decoded_contains_placeholder`) → ×0.05, EXCEPT `placeholder_is_only_url_host` (is_named && surface-placeholder && !decoded && `has_credential_url_userinfo_without_placeholder`) which is exempted. NAMED: char_diversity<0.1→×0.1; (max_repeat_run>0.8 || is_degenerate_repeat)→×0.1 (skips the small-alphabet hex/UUID legit-secret penalty the generic path applies). GENERIC: diversity<0.3→×0.1; (run>0.5 || degenerate)→×0.1; `is_binary_payload`→×0.02; `looks_like_uniform_base64_blob` (unless allow_encoded_text_secret)→×0.02; `decoded_is_base64_blob` (unless allow)→×0.02. Assert: a 64-hex NAMED token survives (no diversity penalty), the SAME body under a generic detector gets diversity<0.3 ×0.1; an `AKIAXXXX…` named token → degenerate ×0.1; a base64-PNG generic → ×0.02.
- µ-pen-05: `has_credential_url_userinfo_without_placeholder` (L35) — `scheme://`, scheme non-empty `[alnum + . -]`, authority up to `/?#"'<>() `/ws, `rfind('@')` userinfo, a `:` with a non-empty password segment, and `!contains_placeholder_word(userinfo)`. The L51 `unwrap_or(authority.len())` is a LAW10-annotated recall-safe boundary default (no terminator ⇒ whole remainder). Assert `https://user:realpass@host`→true (rescues a named cred whose host literally contains "example"), `https://user:@host`→false (empty pass), `https://user:placeholder@host`→false.
- µ-pen-06: `apply_calibration_multiplier` (L258) — None calibration → finalize_confidence(score) passthrough (still NaN-sanitized); `calibration_observations==0` (Beta(1,1) prior; obs = (α-1)+(β-1) via saturating_sub) → passthrough (no halving of fresh detectors); else ×`calibration_posterior_mean`=α/(α+β). Assert no-calibration→unchanged-but-sanitized, Beta(1,1)→unchanged, Beta(9,1)→×0.9, Beta(1,9)→×0.1.
- µ-pen-07: `apply_path_confidence_penalties` (L303) — None path OR `!penalize` → passthrough(sanitized); else `platform_compat::path_component_matches` against FIXTURE_COMPONENTS {test,tests,example,examples,sample,samples} (ci) OR any placeholder-word component → ×0.5. Assert `src/tests/x.rs`→×0.5, `src/main.rs`→unchanged, `penalize=false` (`--no-suppress-test-fixtures`) → unchanged even under tests/. CAPTURE: FIXTURE_COMPONENTS is a hardcoded 6-entry list (Tier-B candidate — could join placeholder-words/sensitive-paths data files; low priority, tiny stable set).
- µ-pen-08: `is_service_anchored_detector` (L144) + `contains_placeholder_word` (L31) are thin single-line DELEGATING shims to `detector_ids::`/`placeholder_words::` (DEDUP already single-source — the doc comments make the "keep in one place" contract explicit). NON-task: do not duplicate the predicate here.

## µ-cmod — confidence/mod.rs (read in full, Iter 16)
Heuristic (non-ML) confidence scorer: additive weighted signals normalized to 0..1. The ML path is separate (penalties.rs / ml_scorer).
- µ-cmod-01: weight constants (L24-35) — LITERAL_PREFIX_WEIGHT=0.35, CONTEXT_ANCHOR_WEIGHT=0.20, ENTROPY_WEIGHT=0.20, HIGH_ENTROPY_PARTIAL_WEIGHT=0.12, MODERATE_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=3.0, MODERATE_ENTROPY_WEIGHT=0.05, LOW_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=2.0, LOW_ENTROPY_MIN_MATCH_LENGTH=10, LOW_ENTROPY_PENALTY=0.6, KEYWORD_NEARBY_WEIGHT=0.10, SENSITIVE_FILE_WEIGHT=0.10, COMPANION_WEIGHT=0.05. CAPTURE Tier-A: these scoring weights are hardcoded; document whether they should be config-tunable (likely compiled, but pin the values as a coherence contract).
- µ-cmod-02: `compute_confidence` (L51) = `compute_confidence_with_threshold(signals, HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=4.5)` — the no-config wrapper; the named-detector hot path uses the threshold form so the resolved `ScannerConfig.entropy_threshold` drives the scoring floor (not a hardcoded const).
- µ-cmod-03: `compute_confidence_with_threshold` (L60) tiered entropy — high_tier=entropy_threshold, very_high_tier=entropy_threshold+VERY_HIGH_ENTROPY_MARGIN where MARGIN=VERY_HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD(5.8)−HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD(4.5)=1.3 (L43). So a tuned `--entropy-threshold` slides BOTH tiers together; at the default 4.5 the very-high tier reproduces 5.8 EXACTLY. Assert: entropy≥very_high→+0.20; ≥high→+0.12; ≥3.0→+0.05; else 0.
- µ-cmod-04: low-entropy penalty (L87) — entropy<LOW_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD(2.0) AND match_length>LOW_ENTROPY_MIN_MATCH_LENGTH(10) → final ×LOW_ENTROPY_PENALTY(0.6); else ×1.0. Assert a long low-entropy match is 0.6×; a short low-entropy one is not penalized.
- µ-cmod-05: normalization (L110-114) — `(score/max_possible)*low_entropy_penalty` clamped [0,1]. max_possible is summed at runtime but is ALWAYS exactly 1.00 (0.35+0.20+0.20+0.10+0.10+0.05), so the division is effectively identity. Assert all-signals + very-high-entropy + no-low-penalty → score==sum-of-fired-weights==1.0. CAPTURE minor: max_possible could be a `const 1.0` (the runtime sum is defensive against weight edits — document, low priority).

## µ-escan — entropy/scanner.rs (read in full, Iter 16)
The entropy secret-scan driver: keyword-context window scan + keyword-free scan + isolated-bare, per-candidate plausibility staging, and the CredData canonical-shape generation lift.
- µ-escan-01: constants (L17-21) — CREDENTIAL_CONTEXT_MIN_LEN=8, KEYWORD_FREE_MIN_LEN=20, MIN_PASSWORD_LEN=8, FIRST_SOURCE_LINE_NUMBER=1, KEYWORD_FREE_LABEL="none (high-entropy)".
- µ-escan-02: public entry CASCADE (L53-151) — find_entropy_secrets (9-arg; keyword_free_threshold defaults VERY_HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD, skip_lines=None) → find_entropy_secrets_with_threshold (STABLE signature, allow_canonical_lift=false → byte-identical non-ML behaviour) → find_entropy_secrets_with_canonical_lift (adds the lift bool) → _and_lines (splits text ONCE: lines + pipeline::compute_line_offsets). Assert the default 9-arg path is byte-identical to lift=false.
- µ-escan-03: `find_entropy_secrets_with_canonical_lift_and_lines` (L154) — two phases sharing ONE `seen: HashSet<String>` (cross-phase dedup) + one matches Vec: scan_keyword_contexts THEN scan_keyword_free_candidates. debug_assert line_offsets covers every split line.
- µ-escan-04: `scan_keyword_contexts` (L204) — for each keyword-assignment line, build keyword_context(lift), scan a ±context_lines window (start=saturating_sub, end=min(len)), skip skip_lines, collect_line_candidates per line.
- µ-escan-05: `scan_keyword_free_candidates` (L248) — effective_keyword_free_threshold = `max(keyword_free_threshold, entropy_threshold+1.0)` (keyword-free floor is ALWAYS ≥ entropy_threshold+1.0 — stricter with no anchor); KEYWORD_FREE context is_credential_context=false AND allow_canonical_shapes=false UNCONDITIONALLY (anchor-gated lift never engages keyword-free). Also runs collect_isolated_bare_candidates. Assert the +1.0 floor and the forced-false lift.
- µ-escan-06: `collect_line_candidates` (L336) — is_likely_innocuous_line short-circuit; dogfood split: telemetry::is_dogfood_enabled → extract_candidates_with_rejections (records each rejection as an EntropyGeneration SuppressionStage) else extract_candidates; per candidate: candidate_plausibility_rejection_stage gate (records suppression in dogfood), seen-dedup, push EntropyMatch{value, entropy, keyword:ctx.keyword, line:idx+1, offset:line_offset}.
- µ-escan-07: `candidate_plausibility_rejection_stage` (L414) gate CASCADE — structured_dotted_token → StructuredDottedTooShort if len<min(KEYWORD_FREE_MIN_LEN, ctx.min_len); entropy<ctx.threshold → EntropyBelowFloor; IF credential_context: canonical_lift = ctx.allow_canonical_shapes && canonical_shape_lift_allowed(value,keyword); `!lift && is_canonical_non_secret_shape` → CanonicalNonSecretShape; len<MIN_PASSWORD_LEN(8) → CredentialContextTooShort; ELSE (keyword-free): len<min(20,ctx.min_len) → KeywordFreeTooShort; `!is_secret_plausible(Strict, default ctx)` → SecretPlausibilityRejected; else None. `candidate_is_plausible` (L404) = stage.is_none(). Assert each stage with a real value.
- µ-escan-08: `is_canonical_non_secret_shape` (L484) = DEDUP shim to suppression::shape::looks_like_entropy_canonical_non_secret_shape.
- µ-escan-09: `canonical_shape_lift_allowed` (L494) CredData lift truth table — UUID shape (looks_like_entropy_uuid_shape) → true (ANY keyword); else must be all-hex; len 32 → keyword_is_key_material; len 64 → keyword_is_crypto_key_material; 40(sha1/git)/128(sha512)/other → false. NARROWER than credential context: mirror negatives wrap sha1 in `api_key=`, so hex40 NEVER lifts; hex64 only under explicit crypto-key anchors. Test tests/gap/entropy_canonical_lift_keyword_material.rs.
- µ-escan-10 (LANDED Iter 16, byte-identical Law-7 + local DEDUP): replaced `compact_keyword` (per-canonical-lift-candidate `String` alloc) with zero-alloc `compact_keyword_contains` — a skip-aware substring matcher over the `_`/`-`/`.`-stripped ASCII-lowercased keyword; byte-identical to `compact_keyword(keyword).contains(needle)`. Callers: keyword_is_key_material (15 needles: apikey/accesskey/authkey/privatekey/signingkey/encryptionkey/masterkey/secretkey/sessionkey/hmacsalt/hmacseed/passwordsalt/salt/nonce/seed) + keyword_is_crypto_key_material (13: encryptionkey/masterkey/signingkey/privatekey/secretkey/sessionkey/hmacsecret/hmacsalt/hmacseed/passwordsalt/salt/nonce/seed). CAPTURE cross-layer DEDUP: engine::phase2_generic::keywords already has compact_keyword_eq/_ends_with + is_assignment_compact_separator (the SAME `_-.` strip set); HOIST that matcher family (eq/ends_with/contains + the separator predicates) to a shared LOW layer (a compact_keyword module beside ascii_ci) so entropy/scanner.rs and engine share ONE impl — entropy must NOT import engine (would invert the entropy→engine layering). CAPTURE Tier-B: the two key-material needle lists are hardcoded (rules/ candidate).
- µ-escan-11: `keyword_context` (L603 — shifted from L567 by the Iter-16 compact_keyword_contains insertion; L567 now points at compact_keyword_contains) — keyword = assignment_keyword_for_line (exact) ?? first secret_keyword ci_find ?? "unknown" (LAW10 reporting placeholder); is_credential_context = exact-normalized-credential OR any of the 19-entry CREDENTIAL_KEYWORDS (hardcoded, Tier-B capture: password/passwd/pwd/db_pass/db_password/api_key/apikey/api-key/auth/authorization/bearer/_key/-key/token/_token/-token/secret/_secret/-secret) ci_find; base_threshold: finite & >HIGH(4.5) → use it; finite → min(it, LOW(2.0)); else LOW(2.0). min_len = credential?8:min_length. allow_canonical_shapes = allow_canonical_lift AND is_credential_context (BOTH). Assert the threshold tiers + the AND-gate.
- µ-escan-12: test constructors credential_keyword_context / _with_lift (L29/39, doc-hidden via testing::entropy_scanner) build the production credential anchor (threshold=LOW_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD, min_len=CREDENTIAL_CONTEXT_MIN_LEN, is_credential_context=true). has_lower_dash_app_password_candidate_with_lines (L298, cfg simd/gpu/entropy) = a presence-probe reusing extract→floor→plausible.

## µ-adj — adjudicate/mod.rs (read in full, Iter 16)
The single candidate-adjudication funnel: an ordered STAGES pipeline over a sum-of-optional-signals MatchCtx; first Suppress wins.
- µ-adj-01: `STAGES` (L416) ordered [explicit, process_signal, generic_bridge, entropy_fallback, hot_pattern, entropy_generation, named_detector_suppression, final_emit]. `adjudicate_match` (L427) runs in order; first Suppress → Verdict::Suppressed(stage_id); all Pass → Verdict::Reported(final_emit.confidence). Assert an explicit_stage suppression beats a later final_emit (first-wins ordering).
- µ-adj-02: `MatchCtx` (L292) sum-of-optionals — each `for_*` const ctor sets exactly ONE signal field (explicit/process/generic_bridge/entropy_fallback/hot_pattern/entropy_generation/named_detector/final_emit), rest None; each stage fires only on its Some field. COHERENCE: 8 ctors ↔ 8 stages 1:1.
- µ-adj-03: `ProcessCandidateSignals::from_match` (L53) suppression order — DetectorCredentialShapeInvalid (shape.allows false) → WithinHexContext (pipeline::is_within_hex_context) → HexDigestFragment (is_hex_digest_fragment) → ProbabilisticGateNotPromising (is_generic_detector && known_prefix_confidence_floor None && !ProbabilisticGate::looks_promising). Assert each with a real input + a clean credential → pass.
- µ-adj-04: `from_process_entropy_shape` (L122) — only gates is_generic||is_weakly_anchored; floor_id = GENERIC_API_KEY if weakly_anchored else detector_id; → EntropyBelowFloor (generic_entropy_below_floor) or CamelCaseNoDigit (shape::looks_like_camel_case_no_digit). 
- µ-adj-05: `generic_entropy_floor` (L244) per-detector floors — GENERIC_API_KEY: len≤24→3.0, ≤40→2.8, else→3.5; GENERIC_PASSWORD→2.5; GENERIC_DATABASE_URL→2.0; GENERIC_SECRET: ≤24→2.8, ≤40→3.2, else→3.5; GENERIC_KEYWORD_SECRET→1.5; default→3.5. If entropy_threshold finite & >DEFAULT_GENERIC_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD(4.5) → base.max(threshold). Assert each detector's floor at boundary lengths (24/25/40/41). CAPTURE Tier-A: per-detector floors + the 4.5 default are tuning constants.
- µ-adj-06: `final_emit_suppression_stage` (L158) — context_hard_suppression (penalize_test_paths OR CodeContext::Comment) && should_hard_suppress(confidence) → HardSuppressedContext; else confidence<floor: generic detector with known_prefix_floor Some && !looks_promising → ProbabilisticGateNotPromising, else GenericBelowMinConfidence; non-generic → BelowMinConfidence. Assert each branch.
- µ-adj-07: `finalize_report_candidate` (L469) — policy::finalize_report_confidence (None → record_checksum_invalid_suppression + return None) THEN the final_emit adjudication. finalize_report_raw_match (L521) wraps onto a RawMatch (sets confidence). Assert a checksum-invalid credential records ChecksumInvalid + drops.
- µ-adj-08: `record_suppression_telemetry` (L506) — reason=="contains_EXAMPLE_token" → record_example_suppression (kind example_suppressed CLAIMS the per-credential dogfood-dedup slot first; KH-GAP-091: recording a shape event would claim the slot and mislabel the trace shape_suppressed); else record_shape_suppression. Assert the EXAMPLE-token reason routes to the example kind.
- µ-adj-09 CAPTURE (Law-7 NOT-safe — declined): `record_decoded_reverse_placeholder_suppression` (L571, cfg decode) does `reverse_str(&credential).to_uppercase()` (a SECOND alloc after reverse_str) then 4 ASCII-marker `.contains()` (EXAMPLE/PLACEHOLDER/SAMPLE/YOUR_). NOT safely replaceable with ascii_ci::ci_find on raw bytes: Unicode full-uppercase expands non-ASCII (ß→"SS", ﬁ→"FI", ﬅ→"ST"), which can FABRICATE a marker substring a raw case-insensitive byte search misses (e.g. `"ßample".to_uppercase()=="SSAMPLE"` contains "SAMPLE", but ci_find("ßample","sample")==false). The soundness oracle can't prove safety → DECLINE (Screwdriver: soundness over reach). Only runs when decoded_source_type contains "/reverse" (rare). Documented so a future pass doesn't "optimize" it into a behaviour change.
- µ-adj-10: `is_hex_digest_fragment` (L684) — credential len≥16 & all-hex & part of a contiguous hex run (rev byte-walk before + fwd byte-walk after) reaching ≥40 (sha1/git/sha256). before==0 && after==0 (delimiter-bounded) → false (genuine fixed-length hex API key). Assert a 16-hex slice inside a 40-hex run → true; the same delimiter-bounded → false; 15-hex → false (len gate).
- µ-adj-11 (Iter-18 Gemini-missed entry points; the trivial-const-ctor + telemetry-wrapper surface):
  `ProcessCandidateSignals::from_false_positive_context`/`from_missing_required_companion`/`from_checksum_invalid` are const bool→Self ctors that suppress FalsePositiveContext / MissingRequiredCompanion / ChecksumInvalid respectively (else pass()); `from_checksum_policy` runs `confidence::policy::checksum_policy_for(credential).is_invalid()` then delegates to from_checksum_invalid. `detector_min_confidence_floor(detector_floor, default)` = `detector_floor.unwrap_or(default)` (LAW10: absent → explicit Tier-A scan default), `detectorless_min_confidence_floor(default)` = the None form. `generic_entropy_below_floor`/`generic_bridge_entropy_below_floor` are the bool `entropy < generic_entropy_floor(...)` wrappers around µ-adj-05 (the bridge form picks GENERIC_KEYWORD_SECRET vs GENERIC_SECRET by the generic_keyword_low_entropy flag). `record_suppression` (L437) = adjudicate_match → suppressed_stage → record_suppression_telemetry; `record_checksum_invalid_suppression`/`record_missing_required_companion_suppression` are the for_process_signals wrappers. `record_decoded_parent_example_suppression` (L555, cfg decode) — sibling of µ-adj-09: is_known_example_credential(credential) && parent_data.contains(credential) → record "decoded_parent_example" (this one is ASCII-clean, no uppercase trap). Assert the ctor→stage mapping + the floor unwrap_or default + the parent-example suppression. (IGNORED as already-covered: the 8 `MatchCtx::for_*` ctors → µ-adj-02; MIN_PREFIX_BYTES → µ-p2-04; CandidateMatch::new/credential trivial accessors; finalize_report_raw_match → µ-adj-07.)

## µ-pgate — probabilistic_gate.rs (read in full, Iter 16)
`ProbabilisticGate::looks_promising` — fast statistical reject of obvious non-secrets (UUID/hash/base64 pad) before heavy ML. FULLY allocation-free + UTF-8-iteration-free (byte scanners).
- µ-pgate-01: len<16 → true (too short to gate reliably). Assert.
- µ-pgate-02: diversity gate (L17) — 256-bool array, count distinct bytes, break at 5; later `count<5 → false` (e.g. "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa" → 1 distinct → rejected). Assert.
- µ-pgate-03: UUID detection (L31) — len 32..=40, exactly 4 dashes, validated 5 hex parts (no empty part, every non-dash byte ascii_hexdigit) → false (reject). Alloc-free byte scan. Assert a canonical 36-char UUID → false; a same-length string with a non-hex char → NOT rejected here (falls through).
- µ-pgate-04: bigram density gate (L89) — len≥32: a 512-bit bitset (8×u64) over bigram_slot_512 of every adjacent byte pair; distinct = popcount; reject if distinct<8 OR distinct<len/4. Rejects pure-base64 UUID-without-dashes pads; 32-hex SHAs (~28 distinct bigrams) PASS. Lossy screen (~0.6% FP at 28 bigrams). `bigram_slot_512(a,b)` (L108) = `(a*33 ^ b*17 ^ a<<1 ^ b>>3) & 0x1ff`. Assert a 32-hex passes; a 32-char low-bigram-diversity string rejects.
- µ-pgate-05 NON-fix: the module is already alloc-free + byte-optimized (docstring: "Allocation-free, UTF-8 iteration-free optimized byte scanner"); the bitset is the intended cheap proxy (the comment explicitly rejects 2 FNV rounds per pair as unnecessary hot-path work). No Law-7 fix to make here.

## µ-cshape — credential_shapes.rs (read in full, Iter 16)
Detector-owned Tier-B credential shape rules (rules/detector-credential-shapes.toml) — the source of the DetectorCredentialShapeInvalid suppression.
- µ-cshape-01: `CredentialShapeRule::allows` (L19) — exact_length mismatch → false; if prefix set and credential lacks it (strip_prefix None) → true (rule does NOT own this shape); if prefix matches → body_min/max enforced on the body. KEY quirk: body min/max apply ONLY when the prefix matches; a value with the right exact_length but wrong prefix passes. Assert: exact_length 20 allows 20-char, rejects 19; prefix "sk-ant-api03-" body 80..120 rejects "sk-ant-api03-short" but ALLOWS "sk-ant-modern-…" (prefix absent ⇒ not owned).
- µ-cshape-02: `validate_shape_entries` (L126) Tier-B Err substrings — empty detector id; duplicate ("more than once"); no shape constraints; "prefix but no length constraint"; exact_length=0; empty prefix; "body_min_length greater than body_max_length"; "body length without a prefix"; "exact_length below prefix plus body_min_length"; "exact_length above prefix plus body_max_length"; overflow ("overflows prefix plus body_*"). Assert each (most are in the in-file tests).
- µ-cshape-03: `build_detector_shape_rules` (L90) — parsed_shape_rules (OnceLock-cached Result, .map_err(Clone::clone)) → detector_catalog::validate_rule_detector_ids vs bundled_detector_ids (unknown id → "unknown detector '<id>'") → maps each DetectorSpec to its Option<rule> by id. Both structs `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`. Assert an unknown-detector rule errors; a known one maps.
- µ-cshape-04: live rules (L310) — rules/detector-credential-shapes.toml parses and contains aws-access-key + anthropic-api-key entries; the AWS rule allows the 20-char `AKIA…EXAMPLE` and rejects 19; anthropic rejects `sk-ant-api03-short`. (in-file tests).

## µ-emod — entropy/mod.rs (read in full, Iter 17)
Entropy thresholds + the cached shannon primitive + the path-appropriateness gate + EntropyMatch.
- µ-emod-01: thresholds (L26-37) — LOW_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=3.0 (pub), HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=4.5 (pub), MIXED_ALNUM_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=4.0, ISOLATED_BARE_ENTROPY_LABEL="none (isolated-token)", VERY_HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=5.8 (pub), SENSITIVE_FILE_VERY_HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=5.5. COHERENCE CAPTURE (real footgun): `entropy::LOW_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=3.0` COLLIDES BY NAME with the SEPARATE private `confidence::mod::LOW_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD=2.0` (µ-cmod-01) — same identifier, different value, different module. Rename one (e.g. confidence's → LOW_ENTROPY_PENALTY_THRESHOLD) so a reader can't conflate them.
- µ-emod-02: `shannon_entropy` (L42) — thread-local cache for data ≤1024 bytes (FNV-1a key via util_hash::hash_fast, memoize_by_hash, DEFAULT_MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES=4096, wholesale evict); >1024 → shannon_entropy_uncached → fast::shannon_entropy_simd. Assert: same ≤1KB input twice → cache hit (identical value); a >1024-byte buffer bypasses the cache.
- µ-emod-03 (LANDED Iter 17, byte-identical DEDUP): `unique_byte_count` (new pub(crate)) — single-pass 256-bool distinct-byte count; the shared home for THREE byte-identical copies (normalized_entropy's denominator, confidence::penalties::char_diversity, ml_scorer::ml_features::unique_byte_count). Test tests/gap/entropy_unique_byte_count_dedup.rs.
- µ-emod-04: `normalized_entropy` (L71) — shannon/log2(unique_byte_count); empty→0; unique≤1→0; max_entropy==0→0. Assert `aaaa`→0 (unique 1), a diverse string → shannon/log2(unique).
- µ-emod-05: `is_entropy_appropriate` / `_with_content` / `_inner` (L120-349) — path gate; the content variant lifts two hard-OFFs when a secret-keyword assignment line is present: (a) `.json` (the single biggest FN wrapper), (b) source-code files (caesar::is_program_source_code_path) when !allow_source_files — source lift needs a SAME-LINE credential surface (line_has_credential_assignment_surface); config lift uses find_keyword_assignment_lines non-empty. Unconditional hard-OFF: `.lock`/`.map`/`.min.js`/`.min.css`. ALREADY Law-7-clean (byte eq_ignore_ascii_case, no whole-path lowercase, byte rposition for the filename). Assert: `.json` with a keyword line → true, without → false; `secrets.rs` source → false unless allow_source_files; `secrets.yaml` → true; `Cargo.toml` → false (manifest exclusion).
- µ-emod-06 CAPTURE Tier-B: `is_entropy_appropriate_inner` hardcodes manifest stems (Cargo.toml/package.json/pyproject.toml/composer.json/Pipfile/Gemfile/pom.xml/build.gradle{,.kts}/build.sbt/mix.exs), config extensions (.env/.yaml/.yml/.toml/.properties/.cfg/.conf/.ini/.config/.secrets/.pem/.key/.tfvars/.hcl), PREFIX_MATCH_NAMES (.env/.npmrc/.pypirc/.netrc), EXACT_OR_CONFIG_EXT_NAMES (credentials/secrets/apikeys/docker-compose), CONFIG_EXTENSIONS_AFTER_STEM (+.enc/.vault/.prod/.txt). Move to rules/entropy-path-rules.toml (Tier-B).
- µ-emod-07: `EntropyMatch` (L98) — {value:String, entropy:f64, keyword:String, line:usize (1-based), offset:usize}. COHERENCE pin: offset = containing line start for line candidates, token start for isolated candidates (the doc contract).

## µ-decmod — decode/mod.rs (read in full, Iter 17)
Decode-through dispatch: re-export surface + the has_decodable_payload routing gate + the Decoder trait.
- µ-decmod-01: `has_decodable_payload` (L62, cfg decode) — O(n) alloc-free gate that routes a fully-encoded chunk (one the literal-byte/bigram prefilters would drop, since its keyword only appears AFTER decoding) into a decode-only pass. Returns true on: ≥MIN_PERCENT_ESCAPES(4) `%XX` hex escapes, OR ≥MIN_BACKSLASH_ESCAPES(2) `\uXXXX`/`\xXX` escapes, OR a contiguous base64-candidate run ≥MIN_DECODABLE_RUN(24). Assert each trigger + a plain-text chunk → false; bounded to encoded-looking chunks so normal traffic keeps the fast skip.
- µ-decmod-02: `Decoder` trait (L133) {name()->&'static str, decode_chunk(&Chunk)->Vec<Chunk>}; `EncodedString`{value:String}. The pipeline registers Decoders; decode_chunk is the per-decoder entry.
- µ-decmod-03: MIN_DECODABLE_RUN=24 rationale — a base64 of a ~16-byte secret is ~24 chars; shorter runs can't hide a credential and only add prefilter-bypass cost. Pin the constant + the MIN_PERCENT_ESCAPES(4)/MIN_BACKSLASH_ESCAPES(2) trio.

## µ-rev (Iter 17) → REMOVED (Iter-18 review dedup)
decode/reverse.rs was ALREADY deep-read Iter 7 under `## µ-reverse + µ-hex (decode/{reverse,hex}.rs)` —
see µ-rev-01..04 there (looks_reversible, self-recursion guard, MIN_REVERSE_LEN+prefixed-hash+LazyLock,
reverse_str codepoint), which strictly supersedes the Iter-17 re-read (added nothing new). The brace-glob
header `decode/{reverse,hex}.rs` is why the Iter-17 grep-verify (`grep -F decode/reverse.rs`) missed it.
LESSON: grep-verify by the basename STEM as a word (`grep -iE '\breverse\b'` over `## µ-` headers), not the
full `name.rs` path — headers list multiple files as brace-globs.

## µ-uhash — util_hash.rs (read in full, Iter 17)
The DEDUP'd hash+cache home (collapsed 6 FNV-1a loops + 5 verbatim thread-local caches into one place).
- µ-uhash-01: FNV-1a single source — FNV_OFFSET_BASIS=0xcbf29ce484222325, FNV_PRIME=0x100000001b3 (the ONE seed/prime; every content-keyed cache depends on identity, so a seed change re-keys ALL caches together). `hash_fast(data)` (L32) = FnvHasher::new().write(data).finish(). Assert a known FNV-1a vector.
- µ-uhash-02: `FnvHasher` (L46) incremental — write folds per-byte `hash ^= byte; hash *= PRIME`; for multi-slice keys (ML scorer: text+sep+context) folding slices is byte-identical to hashing the concatenation WITHOUT allocating the joined buffer (MC-12: a single-slice FnvHasher == hash_fast). Assert `FnvHasher::new().write(a); .write(b)` == `hash_fast(&[a,b].concat())`.
- µ-uhash-03: `memoize_by_hash` (L103) — thread-local HashMap<u64,T:Copy>; hit returns cached; miss computes, wholesale-clears at max_entries (DEFAULT_MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES=4096), inserts. The immutable borrow is dropped before compute() (no re-entrancy panic for non-recursive compute). Each call site MUST pass a DISTINCT thread-local so verdict kinds never collide. Assert hit/miss + eviction at the cap.

## µ-p2 — engine/phase2.rs (read in full, Iter 17)
The always-active phase-2 prefilter + SWE-101 no-candidate gate architecture (the measured #1 scan cost: `phase2:prefilter`).
- µ-p2-01: `ActivePatternsScratch` (L109) — a SPARSE active-index list + a versioned `stamp`/`generation` "seen" set. begin() bumps generation (no O(F) per-chunk clear), grows stamp once, clears the sparse active (retains cap); mark() dedups by stamp==generation; is_active() is O(1). Replaced a dense Vec<bool> of ~1000 patterns zero-filled + fully iterated per chunk → O(active) not O(F). Generation wraparound (==0) resets all stamps so a stale u32::MAX can't alias. Assert mark dedups within a generation; begin invalidates the prior generation.
- µ-p2-02: `PrefilterBatch` (L186) — a compiled RegexSet over always-active patterns + ascii_set (ASCII-folded homoglyph variant, match-equiv on pure-ASCII → identical entry ids) + set_trunc/ascii_set_trunc (truncate_for_prefilter, sound SUPERSET on the fast lazy-DFA) + phase2_indices map + gateable (every pattern prefix-anchorable: finite non-empty pure-ASCII ≥3-byte prefix set) + homoglyph_skippable. SOUNDNESS: each set entry built with the pattern's EXACT flags so the set reports a pattern iff its own regex matches — recall-preserving, only dead work removed; MUST run over the same preprocessed.text the extraction uses.
- µ-p2-03: `CombinedNoCandidateGate` (L265, SWE-101) — anchor_ac (ascii_case_insensitive AC over anchorable patterns' ≥3-byte ASCII prefix literals) + non_anchorable (no-required-literal patterns as (idx, LazyRegex)) + anchor_first_bigram (65536-bit FirstBigramSet, 8KB L1d, all 4 case combos inserted). `anchor_present` (L310) = first_bigram.may_have_match && anchor_ac.is_match (the bigram prescreen is O(N/4) 4-wide, ~10-30x cheaper than AC, a SOUND subset of AC's non-hit). `mark_non_anchorable` checks each non-anchorable's OWN regex → byte-identical marking on the skip path. Validated by phase2_prefilter_hs_findings_parity + phase2_no_candidate_zero_work. Assert: a chunk with no anchor literal + no non-anchorable match → zero marks, recall-identical to the full body.
- µ-p2-04: `gate_prefix_literals` (L399) — extracts a pattern's required-prefix literals IF gate-eligible (prefix Seq finite, non-empty, every member ≥MIN_PREFIX_BYTES(3) AND pure ASCII); else None. LAW10 fail-OPEN: a regex parse failure → None → the pattern runs UNCONDITIONALLY (a parse failure can only cost a missed optimization, never a missed match). Assert a literal-prefixed regex → Some(literals); an unanchored `.*` → None; a 2-char or non-ASCII prefix → None.
- µ-p2-05: `DECODE_FOCUS_MARGIN`=64 (L378) — bytes of parent context kept each side of a decoded span when focus-restricting phase-2; validated by decode_focus_parity. CONTRACT (L380, do-NOT-re-add): there is intentionally NO confirmed-pass focus equivalent — splicing decoded text changes byte adjacencies + creates new token boundaries, so the "outside-the-span is a parent duplicate" theorem fails for confirmed/companion detectors (a symmetric M=256 window still dropped real cloudflare-api-token + mysql-connection-string findings; the only safe M = full splice context = zero savings). Pin the constant AND the do-not-re-add note.
- µ-p2-06: profiling counters (L31-95) — PHASE2_PATTERN_NS/RUNS (per-pattern wall time, lazy Vec<AtomicU64>), POPULATE_PREFILTER_NS/KEYWORD_NS, GATE_BATCH_SKIPS/RUNS/CALLS; phase2_gate_stats_dump prints skip%. Zero-cost when super::profile::enabled() is false. (measurement-only; pin that the counters never affect findings.)
- µ-p2-07: `PortablePrefilter` (L228) + `Phase2AlwaysActivePrefilter` (L344) — lazy OnceLock RegexSet batches + ci_gate/plain_gate AC + (cfg simd) hs OnceLock Phase2HsEngine. HS (when enabled) replaces the ~2,679-pattern whole-chunk RegexSet pass with ONE SIMD multi-pattern SINGLEMATCH scan (~1000x faster per phase2_prefilter_hs_vs_regexset, findings-identical per phase2_prefilter_hs_findings_parity); ungated_indices (compile-failed batches) run UNCONDITIONALLY (Law 10: a compile failure costs performance, never recall).

## µ-p2gk — engine/phase2_generic/keywords.rs (read in full, Iter 18)
Generic-assignment-bridge keyword classification + the compact-keyword matcher FAMILY (the cross-layer DEDUP home µ-escan-10 flagged).
- µ-p2gk-01: `normalize_assignment_keyword` (L160, pub(crate)) — THE canonical normalizer (imported by entropy/keywords.rs + the adjudicate bridge): keeps ASCII-alnum (lowercased), collapses any `_`/`-`/`.` run to a SINGLE `_` (only when not leading/after a sep), trims a trailing `_`; None if empty. Allocates String::with_capacity (owned, inherent). Assert `API_KEY`→"api_key", `segment.write.key`→"segment_write_key", `a__b`/`a-.-b`→"a_b" (run collapse), `--x--`→"x".
- µ-p2gk-02: the compact matcher FAMILY — `compact_keyword_eq` (L268, strip-sep+lowercase byte-iter == needle exactly) / `compact_keyword_ends_with` (L281, reverse byte-iter suffix) + `is_assignment_compact_separator` (`_`/`-`/`.`, L260) / `is_normalized_compact_separator` (`_` only, L264). This IS the family scanner.rs reinvented locally as `compact_keyword_contains` (µ-escan-10) because entropy must NOT import engine; the cross-layer DEDUP task = hoist eq/ends_with/contains+separators to a shared LOW layer. Assert `API_KEY` eq "apikey", `my_api_key` ends_with "key".
- µ-p2gk-03: `is_strong_keyword_anchored_hex_key` (L192, pub(crate)) — value len 32 OR 48 & all-hex & (STRONG_HEX_KEY_COMPACT_EXACT 9-entry exact: secret/apikey/privatekey/encryptionkey/signingkey/accesskey/clientsecret/appsecret/masterkey) OR (compact_keyword_ends_with key/secret EXCEPT licensekey→false). DELIBERATELY excludes token/pass*/auth*/credential/passphrase (weaker on CredData). Assert 32-hex under `encryption_key`→true, under `license_key`→false, under `token`→false, 33-hex→false.
- µ-p2gk-04: `is_strong_keyword_anchored_encoded_text_secret` (L236, pub(crate)) — value no `.` & len≥24 & strong anchor (normalized_assignment_keyword_has_secret_suffix OR ENCODED_TEXT_SECRET_ANCHORS 7-entry: password/passwd/pwd/passphrase/token/secret/credential via compact_keyword_eq) AND decode_structure::decodes_to_printable_text. Lets `password: <base64("SuperSecret…")>` reach the scorer; suppresses random base64 blobs. Assert.
- µ-p2gk-05: `normalized_assignment_keyword_has_secret_suffix` (L180, pub(crate)) — last `_`-segment ∈ {key,secret,token,password,passwd,pwd} OR ends_with key/secret/token/password. Assert `db_secret`→true, `segment`→false.
- µ-p2gk-06: `collect_generic_keyword_lines` (L69, pub(crate)) hot-path stem prefilter — one byte-walk replacing a whole-chunk AC; GenericKeywordStemSet{stems, by_first[256], has_first[256]} dispatch on has_first[byte] → generic_stem_matches_at (eq_ignore_ascii_case) → memchr to next line (stops after the FIRST stem hit per line). `collect_generic_keyword_lines_from_positions` (L101) = the GPU-region mapping/dedup variant (partition_point + sort_unstable + dedup). Assert a `password=x` line collected, `import os` not.
- µ-p2gk-07: `generic_keyword_prefilter_stem` (L135) / `generic_keyword_prefilter_stems` (L45, pub(crate)) — maps each GENERIC_ASSIGNMENT_KEYWORDS entry (+ a bare "key") to a minimal recall-preserving stem (secret/pass/pwd/token/webhook/key/auth/credential, else the EXACT spelling so a keyword-list expansion can't become invisible to the prefilter). Assert `clientsecret`→"secret", `webhook_url`→"webhook", an unknown keyword→its exact spelling.

## µ-ml — ml_scorer.rs (read in full, Iter 18)
The MoE secret scorer: gate(D,6)→softmax + 6 experts(D→32→16→1) → gate-weighted logit sum → sigmoid; D=NUM_FEATURES=42.
- µ-ml-01: architecture consts (L41-58) — EXPERT_COUNT=6, EXPERT_HIDDEN_LAYER_1=32, EXPERT_HIDDEN_LAYER_2=16; FOUR compile-time `const _: () = assert!(...)` guards mirror NUM_FEATURES/EXPERT_COUNT/FC1_OUT/FC2_OUT against ml_weights so an architecture drift FAILS THE BUILD (the strides slice weights.bin; a mismatch = silent wrong scores in release where debug_asserts vanish). GPU_BATCH_THRESHOLD=64 (single-source serial/parallel + GPU-engage boundary). Pin the const-assert guard exists.
- µ-ml-02 (LANDED Iter 18, byte-identical DEDUP): `score_with_config` (L66) cache — migrated the hand-rolled thread-local bounded cache (manual get / compute-outside / clear-at-256 / insert) to `util_hash::memoize_by_hash(&SCORE_CACHE, key, 256, ||…)`, COMPLETING the migration util_hash.rs's doc already claimed (it lists ml_scorer::score_with_config as a memoize_by_hash consumer, but it still hand-rolled — µ-uhash-03). Byte-identical: same FNV key (text + `[0]` sep + context via FnvHasher, MC-12), same f64 value, same cap-256 wholesale evict. Test tests/gap/ml_score_cache_memoized.rs.
- µ-ml-03: `forward_pass` (L237) two numerically-inert opts — (1) weight HOIST (ml_weights::model() OnceLock acquired ONCE vs 37 per-candidate accessor re-slices); (2) output-stationary `dense_relu_layer_t` over COLUMN-major weights vectorizes across output lanes BUT is BIT-IDENTICAL to row-major dense_row (each output reduces inputs in index order, separate round on mul/add, NO FMA). CONTRACT (do-NOT): an AVX2+FMA reduction was TRIED + REVERTED — `_mm256_fmadd_ps` fuses+reassociates → sub-ULP divergence pushed twilio-auth-token/africastalking-api-key/appsmith across min_confidence, regressed 30+ contracts_runner; proven bit-identical in tests/ml_forward_parity.rs. Do NOT reintroduce FMA/lane-reassoc without recalibrating every contract + the GPU parity ref (DET-11).
- µ-ml-04: dense kernels — `dense_relu_layer_t<INPUT,OUTPUT>` (L302, output-stationary + ReLU max(0)), `dense_row<INPUT>` (L337, sequential dot+bias; used by gate+fc3), `softmax` (L358, max-shift numerical stability), `sigmoid` (L345, fast rational `0.5+0.5x/(1+|x|)` clamped at ±6, avoids exp). expert_logit (L268) = fc1(relu)→fc2(relu)→fc3; forward_pass_impl (L244) = softmax(gate)·Σ gate_prob·expert_logit → sigmoid. Assert dense_row vs dense_relu_layer_t parity (ml_forward_parity.rs), sigmoid clamps ±6.
- µ-ml-05: score entry points — `score` (L61, empty heuristic lists) / `score_with_config` (L66) / `score_pending_matches_with_config` (L123, cfg ml, per-pending map) / `pending_match_score_inputs` (L148, borrows the (cred,ctx) pairs) / `complete_pending_match_scores_with_config` (L161, CARDINALITY guard — a backend score-count != pending recomputes CPU MoE rather than drop queued findings, Law-10 loud tracing::warn) / `score_features` (L188, cfg ml, precomputed features). Assert the cardinality-guard recompute path (a short score vec → recompute, never truncate).

## µ-p2a — engine/phase2_anchor.rs (read in full, Iter 19)
Shared-anchor localization: replaces ~82 per-pattern memchr passes (77-85% of phase-2 time) with ONE Aho-Corasick pass over every eligible pattern's required-prefix literals; each AC hit is a candidate START verified by a `\A`-anchored copy of the pattern's regex. Soundness: for eligible P with literal set L(P), every match starts with l∈L(P), so verifying at every AC-reported pos finds every whole-chunk match — `phase2_anchor_parity` asserts byte-identical `RawMatch` sets.
- µ-p2a-01: caps — `MAX_LITERALS_PER_PATTERN`=32 (L48, was an 8-floor; `mx[_-]?api[_-]?key` alone yields 29 ci variants), `CONFIRMED_MAX_LITERALS_PER_PATTERN`=8 (pub(crate)). A pattern over the cap (homoglyph cross-products, giant alternations) returns None → stays whole-chunk. Assert required_prefix_literals_with_cap returns None when literal count > cap.
- µ-p2a-02: `Phase2AnchorIndex` (L53) — THREE parallel localizers: (1) ci `anchor_ac` (ASCII-case-insensitive, `find_overlapping_iter` so `sk-`/`sk-ant-` both report) + `literal_patterns: Vec<Vec<u32>>` lit_id→phase2 idxs; (2) `always_anchor_ac` over only always-active-eligible literals (these LEAVE the RegexSet prefilter — THE win, gated purely by AC presence); (3) case-SENSITIVE `plain_anchor_ac` over FOLDED homoglyph leading literals (ASCII-chunk path). Each AC has a `FirstBigramSet` prescreen. `anchored: Vec<Option<AnchoredRegex>>` per idx; `eligible`/`always_active_eligible: Vec<bool>`.
- µ-p2a-03: `build` (L140) — per pattern: `required_prefix_literals(regex)` Some → eligible, register literals, `anchored[idx]=AnchoredRegex::new(regex, ci)`; None & !ci → fold non-ASCII out ONCE (`chars().filter(char::is_ascii)`), `leading_literals_of_folded` → plain path with `AnchoredRegex::new(&folded_src, false)` (folded ASCII DFA is far cheaper to quick-fail than the unicode one), else `plain_always_mark.push(idx)`. always_active_eligible = eligible ∩ always_active_indices.
