Returns None only when eligible_count==0 AND plain empty. AC build failure → tracing::warn + None (recall preserved via whole-chunk).
- µ-p2a-04: `collect_candidates` (L338) — `out.clear()`; first_bigram prescreen `!may_have_match → return`; `find_overlapping_iter`, for each lit_id map `literal_patterns[lit_id]`, push `(pat,pos)` iff `is_always_active_eligible(p) || is_active(p)` (always-active gated by AC presence; keyword-triggered keeps its keyword gate — EXACT current active-set semantics); `out.sort_unstable(); out.dedup()` so each (pat,pos) verified once. `out` is caller-owned reused scratch (Law-7 clean — no per-chunk alloc). Siblings `collect_always_active_candidates` (L371), `collect_plain_candidates` (L427) same shape, plain/always paths have NO is_active gate.
- µ-p2a-05: `leading_literals_of_folded` (L459) — `MAX_VARIANTS`=64; `Extractor::kind(ExtractKind::Prefix)`, must be finite, EVERY member ≥ MIN_PREFIX_BYTES (returns None if ANY below — whole-set reject), UTF-8, sort+dedup; case-SENSITIVE parse (fold's ASCII members carry case). `required_prefix_literals_with_cap` (L501) — uses `gate_prefix_literals` (same primitive as the no-candidate gate), ASCII-lowercases each + dedups, None if > cap; debug_assert lit≥MIN_PREFIX_BYTES && is_ascii. DEDUP-CANDIDATE (CAPTURE, do-NOT-land): both re-do the regex_syntax Prefix-extract dance, but soundness contracts DIVERGE (case-sensitivity, gate_prefix_literals vs raw Extractor, cap-before vs cap-after) — a merge would be unsound; keep separate (Screwdriver: soundness over reach).
- µ-p2a-06: `has_plain_localizer` (L411) — gated by `tuning.phase2_localizer_enabled()`; the per-chunk plain-AC is a net end-to-end LOSS on decode-recursion-heavy inputs (many tiny sub-chunks) so the single-RegexSet fold is the default, localizer is A/B-tunable. Tier-A capture: phase2_localizer_enabled is a real tuning knob with a perf-justified default-off.
- µ-p2a-07 (accessors, folded from Iter-21 Gemini review): `eligible_count` (L109, diag count of eligible patterns), `is_eligible` (L114, false when `anchor_ac.is_none()` else `eligible[idx]==Some(true)`; Law-10 marker: not-eligible ⇒ caller whole-chunk), `is_always_active_eligible` (L122, same guard on `always_anchor_ac`), `always_anchor_literals` (L129, `&[String]` — the GPU producer appends these after detector literals + phase-2 keywords so an all-zero tail row proves the small AC can't match), `anchored_regex` (L399, the `\A`-anchored verifier owner per idx, None if not eligible/empty), `plain_always_mark` (L419, plain patterns with no folded leading literal — run whole-chunk on ASCII). All accessors over build-time state; no hot-path alloc.

## µ-p2hs — engine/phase2_hs.rs (read in full, Iter 19)
`Phase2HsEngine` — Hyperscan-backed always-active phase-2 prefilter; `#[cfg(feature="simd")]` at the `mod` in engine/mod.rs. Holds the compiled HS DB; marks matching always-active patterns into the caller scratch. "Pure move from phase2.rs, no behaviour change."
- µ-p2hs-01: struct (L13) — `scanner: HsScanner`, `hs_to_phase2: Vec<usize>` (HS id→phase2 det_idx), `dropped: Vec<(usize, LazyRegex)>` (HS-incompatible patterns on the LOUD host path).
- µ-p2hs-02: `build` (L30) — per always-active pattern: `hs_prefilter_requires_host_regex(regex)` true → `dropped.push((idx, regex.clone()))`; else `refs.push((idx,0,regex,false))` + `caseless.push(is_case_insensitive())`. `HsCompileOpts { singlematch:true, caseless:Some(&caseless), shard_target:Some(usize::MAX) (ONE DB — a sharded scan pays per-shard overhead N× per chunk + loses to RegexSet on tiny files), utf8:false }`. utf8:false is LOAD-BEARING: HS_FLAG_UTF8 REJECTS many homoglyph classes at compile (→ silent RegexSet fallback); byte mode keeps them on the fast path, findings parity holds. Compile fail → tracing::warn + None (RegexSet path). Returns None if `refs.is_empty()`.
- µ-p2hs-03: Law-10 `dropped` handling (L84-107) — `unsupported` ids (HS returned, indexes `refs`) map back to phase2 idxs onto the host path; a `refs.get(i)` miss PANICS ("compiled scanner invariant violation … refusing to disable the prefilter") rather than silently shrink coverage. The non-empty `dropped` log is `tracing::debug!` NOT warn — it's a STATIC capability fact (same count every build), recall is IDENTICAL (host regex), so a per-scan WARN was stderr noise that also masked real errors in the installer's first-line reason capture. Assert: dropped patterns are marked, not dropped (recall parity).
- µ-p2hs-04: `mark` (L120) — one `scanner.scan_each_result(bytes, |hs_id| scratch.mark(hs_to_phase2[hs_id]))` + host loop `dropped.iter(): re.is_match → scratch.mark(idx)`. `any_match` (L146) — boolean companion: `scanner.any_match_result` early-exits at first hit (HS native termination) + host loop; recall-identical to `mark` then non-empty without building the set. `hs_prefilter_requires_host_regex` (L159) — char-scan tracking `escaped`/`in_class`, returns true on `^`|`$` OUTSIDE a `[...]` class (HS anchoring semantics differ from per-line regex). No hot-path alloc (refs/caseless/dropped are build-time Vecs).

## µ-fbg — engine/phase2/first_bigram.rs (read in full, Iter 19)
`FirstBigramSet` — exact first-bigram prescreen for literal-set AC scans; a 1024-u64 bitset (65536 bits = one bit per adjacent byte pair). A miss proves the AC cannot match → skip the state machine; a hit is only "maybe" (caller runs the exact AC). Recall-load-bearing prescreen, not a filter.
- µ-fbg-01: `bits: Box<[u64;1024]>` + `fail_open: bool` (L9). `from_literals` (L18, build-time) — any literal `len < 2` SATURATES the set (`Box::new([u64::MAX;1024])`, fail_open=true): a <2-byte literal has no bigram to gate on, so absence is unprovable → every text falls through to the AC, NEVER a silent skip (Law 10). When `ascii_case_insensitive`, inserts all ASCII case variants of the first pair (mirrors an ascii-case-insensitive AC). idx = `(a<<8)|b`, set `bits[idx>>6] |= 1<<(idx&63)`.
- µ-fbg-02: `may_have_match` (L62, hot-path, zero-alloc) — fail_open→true; len<2→false; 4-wide UNROLLED probe loop (`probe(i)|probe(i+1)|probe(i+2)|probe(i+3)` — bitwise `|` not `||` so all four evaluate branchlessly) then a scalar tail to `last_start=len-2`. `#[inline(always)] probe` reads `(bytes[i]<<8)|bytes[i+1]` against the bitset. Already optimal — no fix.

## µ-ctx — context/mod.rs (read in full, Iter 19)
`CodeContext` — the 7-variant credential-context classifier driving confidence multipliers + hard-suppression (the enum is the public boundary; documentation/placeholder/inference/false_positive submodules feed it — see µ-cdoc/µ-cph/µ-cinf/µ-fp).
- µ-ctx-01: variants — Assignment, Comment, TestCode, Encrypted, Documentation, StringLiteral, Unknown. `confidence_multiplier` table (Tier-A capture): Assignment 1.0, StringLiteral 0.9, Unknown 0.8, Comment 0.4, Documentation 0.3, TestCode 0.3, Encrypted 0.05. Pin EXACT multipliers (any drift changes every contextual finding's score).
- µ-ctx-02: `should_hard_suppress` thresholds — Documentation|TestCode|Comment hard-suppress when multiplier-adjusted confidence < 0.5; Encrypted < 0.8 (already-encrypted material is near-worthless as a leaked secret). Assert the threshold-per-variant table (a positive in a doc-block below 0.5 is dropped; an encrypted value below 0.8 is dropped). No alloc fix (pure enum + match).

## µ-struct — structured/mod.rs (read in full, Iter 19)
Structured-format preprocessor: detect known config formats, extract (context,value) pairs, APPEND them as synthetic `"{context}: {value}"` lines so the regex pipeline sees the key adjacent to the value while original line mappings stay intact.
- µ-struct-01: `MAX_STRUCTURED_PARSE_BYTES`=2 MiB (L13). `StructuredFormat` enum (L25): Env, K8sSecret, DockerCompose, Tfstate, Hcl, Jupyter. `uses_decode_through` (L41) — K8sSecret|DockerCompose|Tfstate|Jupyter TRUE (their structured pass DECODES base64 `data:` blocks the byte scan can't recover); Env|Hcl FALSE (plain scalars the byte scan already sees). LOAD-BEARING: oversize-skip telemetry only fires for decode-through formats (else false-loud).
- µ-struct-02: `preprocess` (L60) — `decode_derived` param: true when `text` is a decode-pipeline-synthesised buffer (carries `ChunkMetadata::decoded_span`). Oversize path (L65): a NON-decode-derived decode-through format over 2 MiB calls `record_structured_oversize_skip()` + `tracing::warn!` (was a bare silent `return None` — Law-10 fix); decode_derived buffers NOT counted (already decoded upstream); Env/HCL oversize stays silent (lossless). Assert: a >2 MiB k8s Secret records the skip; a >2 MiB .env does not.
- µ-struct-03: `detect_format` (L100, hot — every chunk) — byte-level ASCII-ci sniff (was building a fully-lowercased path copy EVERY call; now `eq_ignore_ascii_case` on slices, `rposition` for last sep). Env: file starts/ends `.env`; K8sSecret: `.yaml|.yml` && `text.contains("kind: Secret")` (content scan short-circuited behind the cheap suffix check); DockerCompose: name contains `docker-compose|compose` && yaml/yml; Tfstate `.tfstate`; Hcl `.tf|.tfvars|.hcl`; Jupyter `.ipynb`.
- µ-struct-04: `build_preprocessed_text` (L182 multiline / L244 non-multiline) — PRE-SIZES output (`original + 1 + Σ(ctx+2+val+1)`) + pushes parts directly (NO `format!` per pair). DEDUP-CANDIDATE (CAPTURE, do-NOT-land byte-identical): the two cfg-gated bodies are near-identical but the LineMapping + PreprocessedText structs differ (multiline's PreprocessedText carries an extra `original_end` field; last-line end_offset is `(end+1).min(original_end)` inline vs a post-loop `last.end_offset = text.len()` fixup — equivalent result) — a clean merge needs a trait/macro, not byte-identical; capture only.

## µ-sline — structured/parsers/line.rs (read in full, Iter 19)
Shared batched line-attribution for structured parsers: ONE multi-pattern AC pass locates every extracted value's anchor instead of one full-document search per value (gated by `structured_json_line_lookup_batched`).
- µ-sline-01: `PendingExtractedPair` (L11) + `LineAnchor::{Value, Owned(String)}` — value/owned/owned-with-fallback constructors; the anchor is the substring whose position gives the source line (Value = the value itself; Owned = a distinct anchor string, e.g. a JSON key path).
- µ-sline-02: `resolve_line_number_options` (L73) — dedups needles into an `AhoCorasick::new(patterns.iter().copied())` (pattern_ids HashMap → pattern_slots), `find_overlapping_iter(text)` ONE pass marking first-hit per pattern with a `remaining` early-break; `line_number_for_offset` = `line_starts.partition_point(|&s| s <= offset)` (1-based). AC build failure → tracing::warn + all line-1 attribution (Law-10 loud, recall-safe — value still emitted). `finalize_pending_pairs` (L136) batches primary+fallback anchors into ONE resolve call.
- µ-sline-03 (LANDED Iter 19, byte-identical DEDUP): deleted the local `build_line_starts(text)` — it was a CHARACTER-FOR-CHARACTER copy of `pipeline::compute_line_offsets` (same `bytes.len()/40 + 1` capacity, same leading `push(0)`, same `memchr::memchr_iter(b'\n') → push(pos+1)` loop). Routed `resolve_line_number_options` through `crate::compute_line_offsets` (re-exported at lib.rs:168 `pub(crate) use pipeline::compute_line_offsets`, already used 5× incl. structured/mod.rs ×2). Output Vec is identical (values + capacity) so line attribution is unchanged; NO-DUPLICATION law. Updated the source-shape gate `structured_json_line_lookup_batched.rs` (asserted `build_line_starts(text)` → `compute_line_offsets(text)`, same intent: one line-start index). Test tests/gap/structured_line_offsets_shared_builder.rs pins the exact offset table `[0,6,11,24,30]` for `"alpha\nbeta\nGAMMA=secret\ndelta\n"` + the resolver's partition_point lookup (offset 0→line 1, 11→3, 17→3, 24→4, 30→5).

## µ-wsup — engine/windowed_support.rs (read in full, Iter 20)
Window slicing + `(detector, credential, source_offset)` raw-hit dedup for the windowed scan path.
- µ-wsup-01: `window_end_offset`/`next_window_offset` (L7/L11) — `ceil_char_boundary`-snapped overlap slicing (start+max_len, end−overlap); `window_ranges` (L15) loops, breaks at `end>=text.len()`, guards `next>offset` to avoid a zero-progress window on a degenerate overlap. `window_chunk` (L30) — `chunk.data[start..end].to_string().into()` owns one sub-chunk String per window (inherent: a Chunk owns its bytes).
- µ-wsup-02: `record_window_match` (L37) — re-maps `m.location.offset` from window-local to absolute (`source_base_offset + window_offset + window_local_offset`, saturating); rejects `window_local_offset >= window_len`; line via `source_base_line + line_offsets.partition_point(|&lo| lo <= chunk_local_offset)` (O(log L), the FAST path the doc-comment contrasts with the "slow way"). Dedup: `HashSet` membership + `VecDeque` FIFO eviction bounded by `MAX_WINDOW_DEDUP_ENTRIES` (pop_front oldest when full) — bounded memory (Law 7), key `(detector_id, credential, offset)`.
- µ-wsup-03: `line_number_for_offset(text, offset)` (L88) — SLOW O(offset) `text[..safe_offset].chars().filter(== '\n').count()+1`; grep-proven TEST-ONLY (prod uses µ-wsup-02's partition_point + structured's local slice version — NO src/ caller); a legit test oracle (unit/engine.rs, property dedup, audit_speed compute the obvious way + compare), so NOT removed/Law-11. `floor_char_boundary`/`ceil_char_boundary` (L93/L104) — boundary walk helpers (engine boundary owners). No prod-path alloc fix.

## µ-btrig — engine/backend_triggered.rs (read in full, Iter 20)
`CompiledScanner::scan_prepared_with_triggered` — the per-chunk multi-stage scan orchestrator + the 3 per-backend trigger producers.
- µ-btrig-01: `scan_prepared_with_triggered` (L7) — stages under `profile::span` P::{Hot,Confirmed,Generic,Entropy,Ml}, each preceded by a `crate::deadline::expired` early-return (returns partial `scan_state.into_matches()` — deadline is loud upstream). No-trigger fast path: confirmed-pattern Vec built ONLY when `expanded_patterns.iter().any(|&w| w!=0)`, pre-sized by `Σ count_ones` then `for_each_set_bit` (skip the dead extraction + its alloc when no AC fired).
- µ-btrig-02: decode-FOCUS (L128) — a decode sub-chunk's `metadata.decoded_span` windows the expensive phase-2 pass to span+margin (rest of the splice already scanned+deduped by the parent), GUARDED by `tuning.decode_focus_enabled()` && `std::ptr::eq(preprocessed.text, chunk.data)` && equal len (so the span in chunk.data coords indexes preprocessed.text). KEY ASYMMETRY: the CONFIRMED pass is deliberately NOT focus-restricted — a splice creates new byte adjacencies + token boundaries so a confirmed/companion detector (cloudflare-api-token, mysql-connection-string) can fire arbitrarily far from the decoded span where the parent (encoded bytes) did not; the decode-focus theorem ("outside span = parent dup") fails for confirmed detectors (`confirmed_focus_parity` rejected M=256), holds for phase-2 capture (self-contained at the credential). phase2_always_anchor_present/confirmed_anchor_literal_matches/generic_keyword_positions all `.filter(|_| preprocessed.text.as_bytes()==chunk.data.as_bytes())` — a GPU raw-byte presence row is a sound skip proof ONLY when phase-2 scans the same bytes (unicode/multiline preprocessing can add ASCII anchor text the raw pass never saw).
- µ-btrig-03: `collect_triggered_patterns_cpu` (L354) — `find_OVERLAPPING_iter` NOT leftmost: a non-overlapping sweep reports the longest literal + skips past, shadowing a nested shorter literal (`client_secret` swallows `secret` → generic-password pattern never AC-confirmed, the exact base-AC gap that blocked the homoglyph ASCII-skip). Triggers are position-independent bits (confirmed pass re-scans+filters by full regex) so over-marking overlaps only ADDS sound work, never an FP; ~1.7% of scan, recall-neutral by `homoglyph_ascii_skip_parity_default`. `collect_triggered_patterns_simd` (L300) — AC∪HS union; HS scan failure LOUDLY over-marks all SIMD-covered patterns (tracing::warn). `collect_triggered_patterns_gpu` (L249) — GPU presence readback validated (length == `gpu_presence_literal_count().div_ceil(32).max(1)`, `gpu_presence_stray_tail_bits` rejects out-of-range bits) then unioned with the CPU AC set (GPU literal matcher never the sole gate). `degrade` closure routes every failure through `record_gpu_degrade` + `deny_silent_gpu_degrade_with_reason` → `degraded_backend_after_gpu_failure` = `live_cpu_backend` (Law 10: never claim SimdCpu while running weaker AC).
- µ-btrig-04: `mark_triggered_pattern` (L464) — sets `triggered[idx/64] |= 1<<(idx%64)` + propagates `prefix_propagation[idx]` (a prefix-shared detector lights its dependents). `triggered_patterns_from_gpu_presence`/`phase2_keyword_hints_from_gpu_presence`/`phase2_always_anchor_present_from_gpu_presence` — bit-walk the GPU presence words via `trailing_zeros`+`bits&=bits-1`; literal layout is `[ac_map | phase2_keyword | phase2_always_anchor]` (`gpu_presence_literal_count` = sum of the three counts).
- µ-btrig-05 (folded from Iter-21 Gemini review): `collect_triggered_patterns_for_backend` (L232) — the per-backend trigger-producer DISPATCHER: Gpu|MegaScan→`_gpu`, SimdCpu→`_simd`, CpuFallback→`_cpu`, under `profile::span(P::Phase1Triggers)`. `debug_scan_phase2_only` (L212, `#[cfg(test)]`) — runs ONLY `scan_phase2_patterns` (no triggered/generic/entropy/ml/postprocess) so the anchored-vs-whole-chunk differential isolates exactly that pass; assert it bypasses reassembly.

## µ-p2pf — engine/phase2_prefilter.rs (read in full, Iter 20)
`Phase2AlwaysActivePrefilter` — always-active phase-2 prefilter build + the per-chunk `mark_matches`/`any_active_match` hot paths (the SWE-101 "phase-2 must never eat runtime" lever).
- µ-p2pf-01: consts — `BATCH_SIZE`=512 (one RegexSet over all ~2.7k always-active patterns blows the compiled-program limit; batching bounds each NFA), `BATCH_SIZE_LIMIT_BYTES`=64<<20 (size/DFA limits affect only compile success+cache, never which matches report → larger stays match-equivalent), `MAX_NON_ANCHORABLE_FRACTION`=1/2, `MAX_NON_ANCHORABLE_ABS`=256. `build` (L27) always Some for non-empty input (batches that fail to compile fall to `ungated_indices` = run unconditionally → recall-equivalent). Lazy OnceLock: `portable`/`combined_gate`/`hs`.
- µ-p2pf-02: `build_combined_gate` (L155) — anchorable patterns contribute `to_ascii_lowercase` required-prefix literals to one AC; non-anchorable carried as `(index, LazyRegex.clone())` (Arc-shared compile cache — no recompile) checked each with its OWN exact regex (no over/under-mark). DECLINES the gate (`None` = full body runs) LOUDLY (tracing::warn, NOT silent — Law 10, cost is per-chunk not a rounding error) only when non_anchorable > 256 AND > 1/2 of always-active. `build_partition`/`build_batches` (L259/L324) split each case/homoglyph partition into gateable (all provably require a prefix literal → AC no-hit = sound batch skip) vs ungateable; a plain gateable batch downgrades to always-run if its ASCII-fold (`build_ascii_alternate`) fails to compile (else the folded-literal gate is unsound on ASCII).
- µ-p2pf-03 (LANDED Iter 20, byte-identical DEDUP): `ascii_fold_regex_src` — THREE inlined copies of `regex.as_str().chars().filter(char::is_ascii).collect::<String>()` (`pattern_gate_literals` L249, `ascii_folded_sources` L523, AND `phase2_anchor::build` L181) collapsed into ONE `pub(crate) fn` in engine/phase2.rs. The fold is a SOUNDNESS contract (the folded gate literals must equal the folded matcher that runs on ASCII chunks — the code comments at all three sites explicitly worried about drift); now it is one definition. Byte-identical output. Test tests/gap/phase2_ascii_fold_shared.rs (via testing::ascii_fold_regex_src_for_test) pins `[sѕ]k_live_[0-9]`→`[s]k_live_[0-9]`, `aαbβcγ`→`abc`, `ｓk-token`→`k-token`, all-non-ASCII→empty.
- µ-p2pf-04: `mark_matches` (L547) hot path (reused `scratch`, no per-chunk alloc) — (1) `tuning.no_candidate_gate`: on a pure-ASCII chunk where `combined_gate.anchor_present` (first-bigram prescreen + ci-AC) finds nothing, mark ONLY non-anchorable matches + `return` (`record_mark_gate_skip`); (2) `#[cfg(simd)]` HS fast path when `fallback_hs && len <= hs_prefilter_max_len` (size-dispatch: HS wins small chunks, RegexSet wins large); (3) RegexSet batch loop with `truncate`/`use_ascii`/gateable-skip/homoglyph-ASCII-skip/localizer-skip selection of `{set,set_trunc,ascii_set,ascii_set_trunc}`; (4) `ungated_indices` always marked. `any_active_match` (L735, cfg simd|gpu) — boolean companion for the no-phase-1-hit admission gate, same dispatch with early-exit `is_match`, never applies measurement-only prunes (sound superset, can't reject a chunk the scan would mark).
- µ-p2pf-05 (folded from Iter-21 Gemini review): `compile_truncated_or_full_set` (L415, pub(crate)) — builds the TRUNCATED RegexSet (`trunc_srcs` via truncate_for_prefilter, BATCH_SIZE_LIMIT, crlf=ci) and on compile failure `.or_else` LOUDLY (tracing::warn) recompiles the FULL set (Law-10 recall-preserving, perf-only impact). The truncated set is a sound SUPERSET (keeps the lazy-DFA off PikeVM on `{N,}` bodies); extraction with the full pattern filters any over-marks.

## µ-tbm — engine/trigger_bitmap.rs (read in full, Iter 21)
Shared dense trigger-bitmap primitives (a `Vec<u64>`, bit i = "pattern i may match this chunk, run extraction"). Itself a prior DEDUP: the allocate/set/walk ops were open-coded across backend_triggered + scan_postprocess and funneled here so the word width + `div_ceil(64)` sizing live in ONE place. All `#[inline(always)]` so it compiles to the open-coded loops (DEDUP, not abstraction cost).
- µ-tbm-01 (LANDED Iter 21, byte-identical DEDUP): `words_for(n_patterns)` (NEW) = `n_patterns.div_ceil(64)` — the single sizing source the module doc PROMISED ("div_ceil(64) in exactly one place") but didn't deliver: `new_trigger_bitmap` open-coded it AND `scan_coalesced::compute_coalesced_triggers` open-coded `ac_len.div_ceil(64)` for its pooled `with_trigger_buffer` scratch. Routed both through `words_for`. Byte-identical (same usize). Made `mod trigger_bitmap` → `pub(crate)` + facades `testing::{trigger_bitmap_words_for_test,new_trigger_bitmap_for_test}`. Test tests/gap/trigger_bitmap_words_for_single_source.rs pins words_for {0→0,1→1,63→1,64→1,65→2,128→2,129→3} + new_trigger_bitmap(n).len()==words_for(n) all-zero for n∈{0,1,64,65,200,2700}. NOTE (CAPTURE, NOT landed — gpu-gated, unverifiable under ci-lean): `gpu_region_dispatch.rs:160` uses `div_ceil(64).max(1)` (the `.max(1)` differs → would need `words_for(n).max(1)`); fold on a `--features gpu` build.
- µ-tbm-02: `new_trigger_bitmap(n)` (L29, shifted down by the Iter-21 words_for insertion) = `vec![0u64; words_for(n)]`. `for_each_set_bit(words, f)` (L40, `#[inline(always)]`) — ascending bit-walk `f(word_idx*64 + bits.trailing_zeros())`, `bits &= bits-1` clears lowest; trailing padding bits always zero (bitmap sized to exact pattern count) so f never sees an out-of-range idx, but fallible-indexing callers still guard. Callers: scan_postprocess.rs:232, backend_triggered.rs:89. CAPTURE (gpu-gated DEDUP): `gpu_region_dispatch.rs:274-279` open-codes this exact u64 bit-walk (`w*64+lo`, trailing_zeros, `rest&=rest-1`) with a `continue` skip — a clean `for_each_set_bit(cpu_bits, |det| {…})` conversion (closure captures `&mut triggers`/`&mut gpu_underfire_recovered`); land on a gpu build. backend_triggered's GPU-presence walk is u32 (`w*32`), NOT poolable here.

## µ-trunc — engine/phase2_truncate.rs (read in full, Iter 21)
Prefilter input-truncation + UTF-8 boundary helpers (pure `&str`, no engine-state coupling). Build-time (truncation done ONCE at scanner construction in build_batches), not a hot path.
- µ-trunc-01: `truncate_for_prefilter` (L44, re-exported at crate root) — truncates a pattern at its FIRST top-level unbounded repetition + bounds it to the minimum so the always-active RegexSet stays on the lazy-DFA off PikeVM on `{N,}`/`+`/`*` (measured ~793ms dominant cost). SOUNDNESS: any match of full `A B{n,} <rest>` starts with `A B{n}`, so a truncated no-match ⇒ full no-match (sound SUPERSET presence gate; over-marks filtered by full-pattern extraction; EXACT for the common `prefix charclass{n,}` shape). Per RepetitionKind: `ZeroOrMore`→drop B (`src[..b_start]`), `OneOrMore`→`src[..op_start]` (B{1}), `Range(AtLeast(n))`→`format!("{}{{{}}}", src[..op_start], n)`; ZeroOrOne/Exactly/Bounded already finite→continue. Returns None (use full, sound just slow) on no-unbounded-rep / non-simple-concat / parse-fail (LOUD warn) / truncated-compile-fail (LOUD warn). Tests in tests/fallback_truncate_contract.rs (src forbids inline tests, KH-GAP-004).
- µ-trunc-02: `regex_prefix_anchorable` (L11) — true iff finite enumerable required-prefix set, every member ≥3 bytes (`seq.is_finite() && len>0 && min_literal_len>=3`) — the precondition for anchor-positions vs whole-chunk. `truncate_src(s,n)` (L112) — display truncation `format!("{}…", &s[..floor_char_boundary(n)])` (logging helper). `focus_floor_boundary`/`focus_ceil_boundary` (L104/L108) — thin semantic-alias forwarders to `super::{floor,ceil}_char_boundary` for the decode-focus window (CAPTURE: removable indirection, but they name the focus-window intent; low value to inline).

## µ-bprep — engine/backend_prepared.rs (read in full, Iter 22)
`PreparedChunk<'a>` — per-chunk prepared scan state (already heavily Law-7-optimized) + `code_lines_from_offsets` + the SIMD HS-scanner builder.
- µ-bprep-01: `PreparedChunk` (L4) — `chunk: &'a Chunk` (BORROWED, was owned — drops one full ChunkMetadata clone = 5+ String allocs per chunk), `preprocessed: ScannerPreprocessedText<'a>` (`Cow::Borrowed(chunk.data)` on the passthrough common path — no per-chunk body copy; owns a String only on structured/multiline-join), `line_offsets: OnceLock<Vec<usize>>` (Task #93 — both the triggered-pattern + pattern-hits paths used to call compute_line_offsets separately, walking the text twice; cached once). `line_offsets()` (L30) = `get_or_init(|| compute_line_offsets(&preprocessed.text))`. `code_lines(line_offsets)` (L35) — passthrough (`preprocessed==chunk.data` bytes) → `code_lines_from_offsets`, else `chunk.data.lines().collect()`.
- µ-bprep-02: `code_lines_from_offsets` (L44, pub(crate), re-exported engine root) — pre-sized `Vec::with_capacity(line_offsets.len())`; per line `end = has_next ? line_offsets[idx+1]-1 : text.len()`, strips a trailing `\r` when has_next. Returns `Vec<&str>` borrowing text (no copy). Hot-ish (per chunk) but inherent alloc, already pre-sized.
- µ-bprep-03: `build_simd_scanner` (L74, cfg simd, build-time) — dedups ac_map regexes into HS (`regex_to_hs_id` HashMap; `index_map[hs_id]` = the ac indices sharing each regex), returns `(HsScanner, index_map, unsupported_ac)`; `unsupported_ac` = ac indices whose regex HS couldn't compile (over-long/`{100,200}`) so the caller reroutes them to the phase-2 keyword lane (else ~10 context-anchored detectors line/paloalto/tower/keystonejs are SILENTLY DEAD — contracts_runner). A `unsupported` hs_id outside index_map PANICS (invariant, refuses to disable the prefilter). CAPTURE (build-time Law-7, NOT landed — hard to isolate-test, contracts_runner covers dedup): `regex_to_hs_id.entry(regex_str.to_string())` clones the key on EVERY ac entry even on a dedup HIT; a `get`-then-`insert` clones only on miss (where hs_patterns needs the owned String anyway). ~902 build-time allocs, many redundant; byte-identical output.

## µ-glm — engine/phase2_generic/line_mapping.rs (read in full, Iter 22)
- µ-glm-01: `line_at_index(text, line_offsets, line_idx)` (L3, pub(super)) — slices ONE line: `start = line_offsets[idx]`, `end = line_offsets[idx+1]` (or text.len() for the last line), then strips a trailing `\n` THEN a trailing `\r` (CRLF), returns `text.get(start..end)`. SIMILAR to µ-bprep-02 code_lines_from_offsets but single-line-by-index vs build-all, and a different end mechanism (full next-offset then strip `\n`+`\r`, vs next-offset−1 then strip `\r`) — equivalent result, NOT a clean DEDUP (different shapes/contracts). No alloc (returns a borrow).

## µ-gmet — engine/phase2_generic/metrics.rs (read in full, Iter 22)
- µ-gmet-01: generic-assignment-bridge profile counters — 6 `static AtomicU64` (GENERIC_PREFILTER_NS/EXTRACT_NS/PREFILTER_CALLS/KEYWORD_LINES/REGEX_CAPTURES/EMITS). `generic_profile_dump` (L13, swap-to-0 read, early-return if prefilter+extract+calls all 0, eprintln ms), `generic_profile_reset` (L31, store 0). `record_{prefilter,extract}_ns` → shared `record_elapsed(slot, Option<Instant>)` (L62, `#[inline]`, adds `start.elapsed().as_nanos()` only if Some — the shared timing helper, no dup). `record_prefilter_call(keyword_lines)`/`record_regex_capture`/`record_emit` fetch_add. Pure profiling, Relaxed ordering. No fix.

## µ-frag — engine/scan_postprocess/fragments.rs (read in full, Iter 22)
Cross-chunk SECRET-FRAGMENT reassembly (`scan_cross_chunk_fragments`): records each `var = "value"` fragment into `FragmentCache`, rescans any reassembled same-path candidate, stamps the trigger fragment's REAL source line+offset.
- µ-frag-01 (LANDED Iter 22, byte-identical Law-7): `has_fragment_assignment_syntax` (L169) — the per-chunk prefilter gate that must see BOTH an assignment op (`=`/`:`) AND a quote (`"`/`'`/`` ` ``). Was FIVE `memchr(..).is_some()` passes OR'd; replaced with `memchr2(b'=',b':',data).is_some()` + `memchr3(b'"',b'\'',b'`',data).is_some()` — TWO SIMD passes, byte-identical boolean (first-occurrence-of-any ⟺ any-present). Made pub(crate) + `testing::has_fragment_assignment_syntax_for_test`. Test tests/gap/fragment_assignment_syntax_prefilter.rs (truth table: `=`/`:` AND quote→true, op-only→false, quote-only→false, neither/empty→false). 20th landing.
- µ-frag-02: `scan_cross_chunk_fragments` (L11) — deadline-checked; ASSIGN_RE captures (var, value); `fragment_assignment_name_is_credential_like` gate; `fragment_value_offset` via ptr-arithmetic (`line.as_ptr()-chunk.data.as_ptr()+value_match.start()`) for a REAL source offset (synthetic ~19-byte offsets broke the chunk-boundary recall invariant, proptest gpu P3). `record_and_reassemble` returns candidates; each filtered by `match_entropy < 3.0 || len < 16`; a synthetic `reassembled_key = "…"` chunk (pre-sized `candidate.len()+24`) is rescanned via `scan_inner` forced SimdCpu/CpuFallback (NEVER GPU — ~10-100ms dispatch ≫ ~50-byte CPU scan; a leak into select_backend_for_file once turned a 64 MiB scan into ~60s of synthetic GPU launches). Reassembled matches stamped `:reassembled` detector_id + fragment_path (same-path-only attribution) + absolute line (`fragment_line + base_line`) + `offset.saturating_add(base_offset)` (saturating: custom sources can synthesize base_offset near usize::MAX → unchecked add panics debug/wraps release).

## µ-cext — engine/scan_postprocess/confirmed_extract.rs (read in full, Iter 23)
`extract_confirmed_patterns` — the confirmed-pass extraction tail (owns suffix gating + shared-anchor localization + the Stripe direct-prefix dup filter).
- µ-cext-01: `extract_confirmed_patterns` (L14) — `total = ac_map.len() + phase2_patterns.len()`. SUFFIX GATE (L33): engages iff `tuning.confirmed_suffix_gate_enabled()` AND some confirmed pattern is NOT anchor-eligible but HAS a non-empty `ac_suffix_gate`; when on, ONE `suffix_gate_ac.find_overlapping_iter` builds a `HashSet<usize>` of present suffix-literal ids (per chunk, gated alloc), and `suffix_allows(pat_idx)` skips a pattern whose required-suffix literals are ALL absent (recall-identical — every match ends with one). Capture: the per-chunk suffix-present HashSet + the stripe-offsets HashSet are gated-path allocs (reusable scratch candidates, not always-hot).
- µ-cext-02: anchored path (L72) — when `confirmed_anchor_index` has an active-eligible pattern, uses the thread-local `CONFIRMED_ANCHOR_CANDIDATES` scratch; collects via `collect_candidates_from_literal_matches` (GPU literal-match reuse) ELSE `collect_candidates` (whole-text AC); groups candidates by pat_idx (the inner `while j` run), STRIPE-hot patterns filter out already-emitted absolute offsets (`pos + base_offset` ∈ stripe_hot_offsets) into `filtered_group`, then `anchored_regex(pat_idx)` Some→`extract_anchored(group)` / None→`extract_matches`. `is_active = binary_search(confirmed_patterns) && suffix_allows`. Non-anchored loop (L188): skip if `!suffix_allows` or anchor-eligible, else `extract_matches` over ac_map[pat_idx] or phase2_patterns[pat_idx−ac_len].
- µ-cext-03: `stripe_direct_emitted_offsets` (L246) — None unless a stripe-hot confirmed pattern is present; collects `scan_state.matches` offsets whose detector_id == `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` (dedup the direct stripe-key prefix). `is_stripe_hot_confirmed_pattern` (L266) — `stripe_hot_confirmed_by_pattern[pat_idx]`, PANICS on a missing classification (invariant). Profiling via `confirmed_prof_*` (per-pattern ns+runs AtomicU64 vecs).

## µ-sgate — engine/scan_postprocess/suffix_gate.rs (read in full, Iter 23)
Confirmed-pass SUFFIX-GATE builder: one ASCII-case-insensitive AC over every ac_map pattern's required TRAILING literals so the confirmed pass skips a pattern whose suffix is absent (recall-identical). Runtime toggle on ScannerTuning, not here.
- µ-sgate-01: `suffix_gate_literals` (L21, now pub(crate)) — `ExtractKind::Suffix`, case-SENSITIVE parse; returns `Vec::new()` unless the suffix is a FINITE set of ≤`MAX_LITS`(4) literals each ≥`MIN_LEN`(6) bytes (selective enough to gate — skips the O(chunk) `.*<sitename>` scans of site-key detectors that trigger on "key" but require a rare trailing literal); UTF-8, ASCII-lowercased, sort+dedup. Any member <6 bytes ⇒ whole-set reject (empty). Test tests/gap/suffix_gate_literals_contract.rs pins `secrettoken`→[secrettoken], `SECRETTOKEN`→[secrettoken], `short1`(6)→[short1], `short`(5)/`abc`/`[0-9]+`/``→[].
- µ-sgate-02 (LANDED Iter 23, byte-identical Law-7): `build_confirmed_suffix_gate` (L59) — per-source extraction CACHED in `src_cache: HashMap<&str, Vec<String>>` (~6-15% dup regexes parsed once). FIX: `for lit in lits.clone()` (deep-cloned the whole `Vec<String>` per ac_map entry just to iterate) → `for lit in lits.iter()` — the inner `lit.clone()` calls that the HashMap/`literals` Vec actually OWN stay; only the redundant per-pattern Vec clone is removed (~902 build-time deep clones). Byte-identical output (same literals/ids/per_pattern). Made `mod` reachable via engine-root `pub(crate) use ...suffix_gate_literals` + `testing::suffix_gate_literals_for_test`. 21st landing.

## µ-canch — engine/scan_postprocess/confirmed_anchor.rs (read in full, Iter 23)
`ConfirmedAnchorIndex` — shared-anchor localization for the confirmed pass (the confirmed twin of phase2_anchor's ci-eligible path): one AC over finite required-prefix literals collects candidate starts, each verified by the anchored regex; non-prefix patterns keep whole-chunk.
- µ-canch-01: `build` (L57) — per ac_map pattern: `required_prefix_literals_with_cap(regex, CONFIRMED_MAX_LITERALS_PER_PATTERN=8)` Some→register literals (iterate `&pattern_literals` by ref — already clean, no Vec clone, contrast µ-sgate-02) + `eligible[idx]=true` + `anchored[idx]=AnchoredRegex::new(regex, ci)`. `anchor_ac` built with `AhoCorasickKind::DFA` explicitly (the confirmed pass wants the DFA, asserted by confirmed_anchor_kind_for_test) + ascii_case_insensitive. Returns None if eligible_count==0 or AC build fails (LOUD warn, whole-chunk preserved).
- µ-canch-02: `collect_candidates` (L145) / `collect_candidates_from_literal_matches` (L171) — hot, `out: &mut Vec` reused (the CONFIRMED_ANCHOR_CANDIDATES thread-local from confirmed_extract); first-bigram prescreen gate, `find_overlapping_iter`, map literal→patterns, push `(pat,pos)` if `is_active`, sort+dedup. The literal-matches variant skips the AC scan entirely (reuses GPU literal positions). Accessors: `eligible_count`, `anchor_literals`, `is_eligible` (`eligible.get(idx)==Some(true)`), `anchored_regex` (None if not eligible/empty), `anchor_kind` (#[cfg(test)]). `CONFIRMED_ANCHOR_CANDIDATES` thread-local `RefCell<Vec<(u32,u32)>>` (const-init, reused scratch — no per-chunk alloc).

## µ-nhr — engine/scan_no_hit_reassembly.rs (read in full, Iter 24)
Cross-FILE no-hit fragment reassembly (`record_and_reassemble_for_no_hit_chunk`, cfg simd): records each no-hit-branch match into the cross-file FragmentCache + rescans reassembled candidates, so a monorepo scan can pair an access-key fragment in file A with a secret fragment in file B.
- µ-nhr-01 (LANDED Iter 24, byte-identical Law-7 + DEDUP): the prefilter was the SAME 5-memchr open-coding (`=`/`:` assignment + `"`/`'`/`` ` `` quote) as µ-frag-01's `has_fragment_assignment_syntax` — TWO copies of one predicate, a drift hazard. Routed through `Self::has_fragment_assignment_syntax(chunk.data.as_bytes())` (now pub(crate) from Iter 22; itself memchr2/memchr3 = 2 SIMD passes). Byte-identical boolean. Test tests/gap/fragment_prefilter_single_source.rs (source-shape: no-hit routes through the shared predicate + no re-open-coded `memchr(b'='`; fragments.rs owns it + uses memchr2/3; + behavioral truth-table tie via the facade). 22nd landing.
- µ-nhr-02: `record_and_reassemble_for_no_hit_chunk` (L8) — early-return if `matches.is_empty()`; per match builds a `SecretFragment {prefix=detector_id, var_name=detector_name, value=Zeroizing(credential), line=location.line.unwrap_or(0) (Law-10 metadata-only), path=Arc::clone(path)}` and `record_and_reassemble_stamped`; each reassembled candidate filtered by `match_entropy < 3.0 || value.len() < 16`, then a synthetic `reassembled_key = "…"` chunk (pre-sized `value.len()+24`) rescanned via `scan_inner` on `live_cpu_backend()` (NEVER GPU), stamping `location.line = candidate.line`. Pre-reserves `matches` +16 + `reassembled_candidates` cap 16. Note vs µ-frag: this path stamps line only (no offset rewrite); the in-chunk fragments.rs path does the full real-source-offset stamp.

## µ-segattr — engine/segment_attribution.rs (read in full, Iter 24)
Attribute coalesced-buffer matches back to logical input segments (keyhog-local mirror of vyre's `match.map_offsets_to_segments`, kept until that primitive is on crates.io). Pure, self-contained, well-structured — no fix.
- µ-segattr-01: types `Segment {id,start,len: u32}` (+ `checked_end` → SegmentEndOverflow on `start+len` > u32::MAX), `GlobalMatch {pattern_id,start,end}`, `AttributedMatch {segment_id,pattern_id,local_start,local_end}` — all `#[derive(Copy, Ord, Hash)]` + `const fn new`. `SegmentAttributionError` (thiserror): SegmentEndOverflow / SegmentsNotSorted / SegmentsOverlap / InvalidMatchRange (each carries the offending indices+offsets in its `#[error]`).
- µ-segattr-02: `map_offsets_to_segments` (L152) — validates+normalizes segments, sorts match indices by `(start,end,pattern_id,index)`, single forward `segment_cursor` sweep (advance while `normalized[cursor].end <= item.start`), a match fully inside `[seg.start, seg.end]` is rewritten to segment-local offsets into `mapped_by_input_order` (preserves INPUT order via flatten); a match in a gap or crossing a boundary is OMITTED; empty range (`end<=start`) → InvalidMatchRange err. `validate_segments` (L197) — `checked_end` per segment + enforces sorted-by-start + non-overlap (touching OK: `start < previous_end` is the overlap test). Pre-sized `Vec::with_capacity`. CAPTURE: the `Hash`/`Ord` derives + const constructors are a clean public contract — no alloc/dedup fix.

## µ-sip — engine/scan_inner_profile.rs (read in full, Iter 25)
- µ-sip-01: `scan_inner` prepare/phase-1 overhead profiler (split from scan.rs for the 500-LOC cap). 3 `static AtomicU64` (SCAN_PREPARE_NS/PHASE1_NS/INNER_CALLS, pub(super)). `scan_inner_prof_enabled` = `profile::enabled()`. `scan_inner_profile_dump` (pub(crate)) — swap-to-0 read, early-return if all 0, eprintln `prepare/phase1 ms + µs/call`. `scan_inner_profile_reset`. Zero-cost unset. No fix.

## µ-spp — engine/scan_postprocess.rs (parent, read in full, Iter 25)
The postprocess orchestrator: cross-chunk fragment scan + decode-through recursion + same-prefix trigger expansion. Re-exports the satellite interface (`decode_profile_dump/reset`, `ml_batch_profile_dump/reset`, `build_confirmed_suffix_gate`).
- µ-spp-01: `post_process_matches` (L33, pub(crate) entry) thinly delegates to `post_process_matches_inner` (L42) — deadline-checked; `scan_cross_chunk_fragments` (µ-frag); then `#[cfg(feature = "decode")]` (L57) when `chunk.data.len() <= config.max_decode_bytes`: seeds `seen: HashSet<(Arc<str>, SensitiveString)>` from existing matches REUSING the zeroizing credential (no String clone — saves ~10-30µs/chunk for 50+ matches), `decode_chunk` (depth/validate/alphabet_screen), per decoded sub-chunk over-cap → `record_decode_truncation` + debug (Law-10 counted-before-logged), `> MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES` → `scan_windowed` else `scan_inner` on `live_cpu_backend()` (NEVER GPU — 10-100ms dispatch ≫ tiny payload; a 64MiB→1000-subchunk scan = 50s GPU tax), example/reverse-placeholder suppression, then `decoded_candidates.sort_by_key(|m| m.location.offset)` (L189, STABLE so equal-offset entries keep deterministic scan order) + first-wins `seen` dedup (Law-10: lowest REAL source offset wins, not scan-order; a synthetic-append alias offset > original_end+1 is an invalid coordinate). [Iter-27 review: was `#[cfg(decode)]`/`sort_by_key(offset)` shorthand; corrected to real feature-gate + key expr.]
- µ-spp-02: `expand_triggered_patterns` (L206, param `triggered_patterns: &[u64]`) — NO-TRIGGER fast path: `if !triggered_patterns.iter().any(|&w| w!=0)` (L228) `return Vec::new()` BEFORE the `to_vec()` clone (L231) + bit-scan (dominant no-hit chunk). Else `to_vec()` + `for_each_set_bit` propagates ONLY via `same_prefix_patterns` (NOT detector_to_patterns — that forced full-regex passes on guaranteed-no-match patterns); out-of-range idx → `record_invalid_pattern_index_skip` telemetry. Open-codes a set-bit (`expanded[other/64] |= 1<<(other%64)`) — a `trigger_bitmap::set_bit` helper could DEDUP it + mark_triggered_pattern, but each site differs (telemetry / prefix_propagation) — CAPTURE, not a clean single-helper.

## µ-sfilt — engine/scan_filters.rs (read in full, Iter 25)
Phase-2 no-hit admission prefilters + credential boundary extension + per-pattern confidence signals.
- µ-sfilt-01: `has_secret_keyword_fast` (L19, cfg simd|gpu) — LazyLock `Option<AhoCorasick>` over exactly 25 distinctive split-prone secret PREFIXES (Iter-27 review: was "~27"; counted L40-73 = sk-proj-/sk-svcacct-/sk-admin-/sk_live_/sk_test_/rk_live_/pk_live_/ghp_/ghs_/gho_/ghu_/ghr_/github_pat_/xoxb-/xoxp-/xoxa-/xoxr-/xoxs-/xapp-/sk-ant-/hf_/.iam.gserviceaccount.com/glpat-/npm_/HRKU-); single AC pass (was N memmem). FAIL-CLOSED: `None`→`true` (scan unconditionally), LOUD one-shot `warn_prefilter_disabled` (Law 10, a static-init panic would poison the LazyLock fleet-wide). `has_generic_assignment_keyword` (L158) — same shape, `ascii_case_insensitive(true)` AC over `GENERIC_ASSIGNMENT_KEYWORDS`.
- µ-sfilt-02: `GENERIC_ASSIGNMENT_KEYWORDS` (L90, pub(super) const, exactly 46 entries (Iter-27 review: was "~48"; counted L91-142): secret/password/passwd/pwd/pass/token/webhook_{url,-url,.url}/api{key,_key,-key,.key}/auth/authorization/auth_{token,key}/credential/private_key/signing_key/encryption_key/access_key/client_secret/app_secret/master_key/license_key with _-. variants). Bare `pass` covers `*_PASS=` (dominant CredData env shape); whole-word left boundary in GENERIC_RE prevents bypass=/compass= promotion. CAPTURE (Tier-B): a hardcoded list — the "no hardcoded lists" law wants it generated from a Tier-B data file; big migration, not byte-identical.
- µ-sfilt-03: entropy-run gate — `has_high_entropy_run_fast` = `_at_least(data, DEFAULT_ENTROPY_RUN_BYTES=32)`; single forward pass counting consecutive `is_entropy_candidate_byte` (alnum + `-_+/=.:!@#$%^&*`), early-true at min_run. 32 chars chosen so it rarely trips natural code (fn names ~24, UUID base62-run ~12, longest English word 28) — admits pure-entropy no-keyword chunks (was recall 0.36 on mirror). `looks_like_variable_name` (L254) — bytes ≤64, all `alnum|_` (byte ops 4x faster than chars().all()).
- µ-sfilt-04: `extend_known_prefix_credential` (L266, pub(crate)) — for a known-prefix token, extends right over `is_provider_token_byte` (alnum + `_-.`) then `extend_base64_padding` (swallow ≤2 trailing `=` if base64-shaped); reslices from the credential's OWN start (ptr-delta, not match_start, so a grouped `KEYWORD[=:]VALUE` detector doesn't prepend the keyword). NEVER downgrades a valid checksum: if extension changed the credential AND `checksum::extension_downgrades_checksum`, revert to the original (the pypi `…MNH="` separator-`=` case). CAPTURE: extend_known_prefix + extend_base64_padding share a `cred_start ptr-delta → data[cred_start..end]` reslice with DIFFERENT guards — borderline DEDUP, not cleanly byte-identical.
- µ-sfilt-05 (LANDED Iter 25, byte-identical Law-7): `compute_pattern_signals` (L373, pub(super)) keyword-nearby probe HOISTED the loop-invariant `preprocessed.text.as_bytes() != chunk.data.as_bytes()` out of the per-keyword `.any()` closure into one `let text_differs` — on the passthrough path (same Cow::Borrowed bytes) the slice `!=` is an O(len) memcmp, so per-keyword it was O(keywords×len) for an invariant. Byte-identical result (text_differs is constant across keywords; `∃kw:(A∨(C∧B))` with C invariant). Test tests/gap/pattern_signals_keyword_invariant_hoisted.rs (source-shape: comparison appears EXACTLY once + closure consumes `text_differs`). 23rd landing. `sf` signal = `is_sensitive_path(path)`.

## µ-bnd — engine/boundary.rs (read in full, Iter 25)
Cross-chunk WINDOW-boundary secret reassembly: for gapless-contiguous same-file chunk pairs, synthesize a thin tail-of-A+head-of-B buffer, scan it, report straddle matches the in-chunk scan missed.
- µ-bnd-01: `BoundaryContextBytes::{Bounded(usize), FullAdjacentChunks}`; `derive_pattern_boundary_context` — max over `regex_match_byte_upper_bound`, ANY unbounded→FullAdjacentChunks. `ast_match_byte_upper_bound` (L55) — recursive AST max-width: Empty/Flags/Assertion=0, Literal=len_utf8, Dot/Class=4, Concat=Σ(saturating), Alternation=max, Repetition: ZeroOrOne=×1 / Exactly(n)/Bounded(_,n)=×n / ZeroOrMore|OneOrMore|AtLeast=None (unbounded). Parse fail → None (Law-10 full-pair seam scan).
- µ-bnd-02: `scan_chunk_boundaries` (L103, the cross-seam entry) — `<2 chunks`→noop; cardinality-mismatch→telemetry+return; group chunk indices by `(source_type, path)`, sort by base_offset, `windows(2)` → `scan_one_pair`. `scan_one_pair` (L146) — only `a_end == b.metadata.base_offset` (L167; gapless no-overlap; overlap already covered, gap unavailable; Iter-27 review: the offset/line fields live under `.metadata`, was shorthand `b.base_offset`); tail=last `context_bytes` of A (floor_char_boundary), head=first of B; `boundary_base_offset = a.metadata.base_offset.checked_add(tail_start)` (L210, saturating-audit, near-usize::MAX→return), `boundary_base_line = a.metadata.base_line.saturating_add(memchr_iter(b'\n', a_bytes[..tail_start]).count())` (L223-226), scan synthetic buffer; a match with `end <= seam_file_offset` is entirely in A's tail (skip); `already_seen` dedup keyed `(offset, detector_id, credential_hash)` — detector_id REQUIRED (shpat_ generic+specific both claim same span; offset+hash-only key would drop the 2nd, order-dependent recall loss). `boundary_context_for_pair` — FullAdjacentChunks if scanner-level unbounded OR entropy-appropriate. `scan_boundary_chunk_whole` — select_backend + deny_silent_gpu_degrade + scan_inner + post_process.

## µ-spml — engine/scan_postprocess/ml.rs (read in full, Iter 25)
- µ-spml-01: `apply_ml_batch_scores` (L38, pub(crate), NOT cfg-gated — Iter-27 review: was "cfg ml" but `mod scan_postprocess_ml` at mod.rs:118 has no `#[cfg]`, so the fn compiles unconditionally; the ML *blend* is gated downstream at the gpu/model layer, not here) — panics if ml_pending non-empty while `!ml_enabled` (invariant); `pending_match_score_inputs(&ml_pending)` borrows candidates, `gpu::batch_ml_inference_with_timeout(gpu_moe_timeout)`, `ml_pending.drain(..).collect()` owns the pending, `complete_pending_match_scores_with_config` (CARDINALITY-guard recompute, µ-ml-05), zip scores → `policy::ml_pending_match_confidence(ml_weight, scan_comments, penalize_test_paths)` → `emit_finalized_pending_match`. `emit_finalized_pending_match` (L11) — `adjudicate::finalize_report_raw_match` with ReportAdjudicationPolicy (allow_encoded_text_lift:false, calibration), `None`→drop, else `push_match(max_matches_per_chunk)`. No alloc fix (drain/collect inherent).

## µ-wnd — engine/windowed.rs (read in full, Iter 26)
Windowing over a single oversized chunk: slide MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES windows (WINDOW_OVERLAP_BYTES overlap), scan each, re-attribute match offset/line back to the parent chunk, dedup seam-duplicated matches.
- µ-wnd-01: `scan_windowed` (L10, pub(super)) — SEQUENTIAL path. `reject_oversized_window_chunk` gate first. `all_matches = Vec::with_capacity((chunk_text.len()/4096).max(16))` (≈1 match/4KiB est). `seen: HashSet` + `seen_order: VecDeque` bounded-LRU dedup (shared mutable → why this path stays single-threaded). KEY: `line_offsets = compute_line_offsets(chunk_text)` computed ONCE up front (L30) so `record_window_match` binary-searches (O(log L)/match) instead of re-counting `\n` from buffer start per match (O(offset)/match → O(n²) on a match-dense multi-MiB window). Loop: `window_end_offset(text, offset, MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES)` → `window_chunk` → `select_backend_for_file(window_len)` → `scan_inner` → `record_window_match`; advance `next_window_offset(text, end, WINDOW_OVERLAP_BYTES)`; `end >= len` breaks. deadline checked per window.
- µ-wnd-02: `scan_windowed_with_triggered` (L65, pub(crate), cfg simd|gpu) — PARALLEL path. `window_ranges(text, MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES, WINDOW_OVERLAP_BYTES)` precomputes `(offset,end)` pairs → `.par_iter().map()` (rayon) scans each window independently into `(offset, window_len, Vec<RawMatch>)`; the `record_window_match` merge stays SEQUENTIAL after the join (seen/seen_order are not thread-safe). Per window it RE-SLICES the phase-2 GPU hint rows into window-local coords: `confirmed_anchor_literal_matches` and `generic_keyword_positions` are filtered to `pos∈[offset,end)` then rebased `(pos-offset)` (a window-local owned Vec held in `window_confirmed_anchor_matches`/`window_generic_keyword_positions`, sliced via `.as_slice()` — borrow outlives the call). `prepare_chunk` per window (own line_offsets/code_lines for the sub-buffer).
- µ-wnd-03 (LANDED Iter 26, byte-identical Law-7): per-window `triggered_patterns.to_vec()` clone REMOVED. `scan_prepared_with_triggered` (backend_triggered.rs:7) signature `triggered_patterns: Vec<u64>` → `&[u64]` — it only BORROWS (sole use `expand_triggered_patterns(triggered_patterns)` at backend_triggered.rs:48, and `expand_triggered_patterns(&self, &[u64])` at scan_postprocess.rs:206). Old windowed.rs:126 did `triggered_patterns.to_vec()` INSIDE the rayon `.par_iter().map()` → one owned `Vec<u64>` allocation PER WINDOW (N windows on a multi-MiB chunk); now passes the `&[u64]` slice straight through, zero per-window alloc. 5 call sites updated to pass `&triggered`: scan_coalesced.rs:358 & :403, windowed.rs:126, scan.rs:62, compiled_api.rs:491 (all hold an owned `triggered` Vec in a `let` and borrow it for the call). Byte-identical: `expand_triggered_patterns` consumes `&[u64]` identically either way; deleting the owned copy changes no value. 24th landing. Test tests/gap/windowed_triggered_slice_no_per_window_clone.rs (source-shape: windowed map passes the slice not `.to_vec()`, signature takes `&[u64]`).
- µ-wnd-04: `reject_oversized_window_chunk` (L161, free fn) — `len ≤ 512MiB`→false (scan); else LOUD `tracing::warn!` (path or `"unknown"` placeholder, LAW10 reporting-only) + true (skip, OOM guard). Hard 512MiB ceiling above MAX_SCAN_CHUNK_BYTES — protects even the windowing setup (`with_capacity`, `compute_line_offsets`) from an absurd allocation before any window runs.

## µ-csr — engine/csr.rs (read in full, Iter 28)
`CsrU32` compressed-sparse-row index table: flattened `u32`-narrowed replacement for `Vec<Vec<usize>>` detector-side maps (prefix_propagation / same_prefix_patterns / phase2_keyword_to_patterns / hs_index_map), each ~1000+ rows. Two Vecs: `data` (all rows concatenated) + `offsets` (n+1, `row(i)=&data[offsets[i]..offsets[i+1]]`). Empty rows cost 0 data bytes.
- µ-csr-01 (LANDED Iter 28, byte-identical Law-7 + coherence): the type doc claims CSR "collapses each table to exactly two allocations" — TRUE of the stored form but FALSE of the BUILD: `from_rows` grew `data` from `Vec::new()` and `offsets` from `vec![0u32]`, reallocating ~log(n) times as 1000+ rows pushed. All 4 builders construct via `From<Vec<Vec<usize>>>` (compile.rs:130/131/155/169) which knows row count AND total elem count up front. Restructured: single build loop `from_rows_sized(rows, data_cap, offsets_cap)` (the ONE concatenation owner — NO DUPLICATION); generic `from_rows` reserves `offsets` via `rows.size_hint().0+1` (exact for Vec<Vec> into_iter `(n,Some(n))`), data_cap 0 (unknowable generically, strictly ≥ old); `From<Vec<Vec<usize>>>` reserves BOTH exactly (`data_cap = rows.iter().map(Vec::len).sum()`, `offsets_cap = rows.len()+1`) → build is now literally 2 allocations. Byte-identical: data/offsets CONTENTS unchanged (same values/order); only capacity differs, unobservable through CsrU32's API (get/Index, no PartialEq, Debug shows contents). Test tests/gap/csr_build_exact_capacity.rs (behavioral roundtrip incl. empty/interior/trailing empty rows via `csr_from_rows_roundtrip_for_test` facade + source-shape: single loop owner, exact data-cap sum, both with_capacity, old grow-from-empty seeds gone). 25th landing.
- µ-csr-02: `get(i)` (L83, #[inline], pub(crate); Iter-30 review: was L63, drifted +20 by the Iter-28 from_rows_sized/doc additions) — `offsets.get(i)?`/`get(i+1)?` bounds-checked → `Some(&data[start..end])`, replaces `Vec::get -> Option<&Vec<usize>>` on the hot lookup path. `Index<usize>` (impl L99 / `fn index` L106, panicking; was L76) — `&data[offsets[i]..offsets[i+1]]` for infallible callers. `#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]`; `Default` = empty data + empty offsets (NOTE: a Default CsrU32 has EMPTY offsets, so `get(0)` returns None — the `n+1` invariant only holds once built; compile.rs:157 `CsrU32::default()` for the no-Hyperscan case relies on get→None never being indexed).

## µ-hot — engine/hot_patterns.rs (read in full, Iter 28)
- µ-hot-01: `scan_hot_patterns_fast` (L6, pub(crate), cfg simdsieve) — the SimdSieve hot-literal accelerator. `SimdSieve::new(text_bytes, HOT_PATTERNS)` (HOT_PATTERNS is already `&[&[u8]]` — passed through, NOT `.to_vec()`'d per chunk, a prior fix); Err→LOUD `tracing::warn` + return (Law-10 standard scanner stays active, recall-safe — phase-2 still runs). For each sieve offset: `hot_pattern_index_at` resolves the table slot, confirm full literal (`&text_bytes[offset..end] == pattern`, direct `HOT_PATTERNS[pattern_idx]` panic-on-OOB = corrupt-build-loud), `self.hot_pattern_slots[pattern_idx]` (direct index, ditto), `slot.ac_map_index` None→skip.
- µ-hot-02: credential boundary scan — `candidate = &text_bytes[offset..(offset+100).min(len)]` then `.position(|b| 14-way stop-set + control: b' '/\n/\r/"/'/\\/;/,/()/[]/{}/ then byte<0x20)` (Iter-30 review: 14 explicit stop bytes — counted L65-81 — plus the `<0x20` control test, was mislabeled "13-way") → cred_end (Law-10 unwrap_or(len) = whole remainder, recall-safe). Then `slot.validator.find(credential)` (^-anchored precise regex): trailing-alnum after `m.end()` → `record_hot_drop(ShapeGate("hot_regex_validation_rejected"))`+skip; None→same drop; else trim to `&credential[..m.end()]`. CAPTURE: the 14-way stop-set `position` closure (L63-82) is UNIQUE (only site with this shape; NOT the complement of `is_provider_token_byte` which stops at `=`/`:`/`/`/`+` that hot keeps) — no clean memchr/dedup, >3 needles. 
- µ-hot-03: emits via `compute_pattern_signals` (keyword_nearby+sensitive_file) → `process_match` (FULL per-match policy: suppression/checksum/confidence/ML/report) — NOT a bypass (the old hot-pattern-bypasses-process_match bug is fixed). One sieve offset → at most one hot literal (mutually-exclusive prefixes), fall through to next offset (no per-offset pattern loop).

## µ-ext — engine/extract.rs (read in full, Iter 28)
Per-pattern regex extraction loops (4 entries): `extract_matches` (L22, pub(crate) legacy no-cursor wrapper) → `extract_matches_inner` (L55, pub(super), dispatches group vs plain) → `extract_grouped_matches` (L106) / `extract_plain_matches` (L270). Both inner loops → `process_match` per surviving candidate.
- µ-ext-01: `extract_grouped_matches` — `signals = OnceCell<(bool,bool)>` lazy per-pattern (keyword_nearby O(K×|chunk|) + sensitive_file AC-over-path computed at MOST once, on FIRST match; zero-match AC-triggered patterns pay nothing — eager compute regressed entropy_noise bench -36%). REUSES one `locs = rx.capture_locations()` buffer across all iters (not a fresh `Captures`/match — tens of thousands of avoided allocs on a 100k-file scan). cursor_range = GPU-anchored window (`start past cursor_end` → break, off the literal hit). MAX_INNER_LOOP_ITERS=1_000_000 hard cap + `loop_expired_on_cadence(.., 64)` deadline cadence (defends --timeout on false_prefix_storm / catastrophic-backtrack). Zero-width match → cursor+1 (ceil_char_boundary) + `continue` (never surfaced — replicates legacy captures_iter silent-skip). Var-name heuristic: if captured group `looks_like_variable_name` && groups_total>2, scan other groups for a non-varname value-shaped (`len>=8`) candidate.
- µ-ext-02: `extract_plain_matches` — same OnceCell signals + MAX_INNER_LOOP_ITERS + cadence; walks `rx.find_at(text, cursor)` manually (find_iter has no start-pos) so the anchored window stays cheap; legacy (0..bytes_total) behaves identically to the old find_iter loop. NOTE asymmetry: grouped increments match_count BEFORE the regex read (L181), plain AFTER the cursor advance (L342) — both gate identically, behavior-equivalent. CAPTURE: grouped/plain inner loops are structurally near-identical — a DEDUP candidate BUT differ at the core (captures_read_at+group-resolution+varname-fallback vs find_at+as_str) — not cleanly byte-identical; the divergent middle is the whole point.

## µ-proc — engine/process.rs (read in full, Iter 28)
`process_match` (L46, pub(super)): the per-match policy chain (suppression → companion gate → entropy/shape → checksum → ML/heuristic score → emit Final or queue MlPendingMatch). `match_companions` (L18, pub(crate)).
- µ-proc-01: `match_companions` — `self.companions.get(detector_index)` None or empty → `Some(HashMap::new())` (alloc-free until first insert, no bucket sizing); else `HashMap::with_capacity(len)`, per companion `find_companion`; a missing `required` companion → `None` (hard skip signal). Caller (L149) fast-paths `self.companions.is_empty()` to skip the call entirely.
- µ-proc-02: suppression chain — FIVE `record_suppression` gates each `.is_some()→return` (Iter-30 review: was "FOUR" — the checksum gate was described under µ-proc-03 but it is the 5th early-return gate; all traced through adjudicator, Law-10 no silent return, KH-L-0412): process-signals (L80; extend_known_prefix_credential first, then shape/checksum-shape via ProcessCandidateSignals::from_match), false-positive-context (L103; is_false_positive_context line-window OR is_false_positive_match_context), named-detector (L133; weak_anchor + inferred_context + source_type + example/context), entropy-shape (L177; is_generic + weak_anchor + entropy vs config.entropy_threshold), checksum (L194; from_checksum_policy — checksum fail = NOT a real token, skip ML before scoring). `match_entropy(credential.as_bytes())` computed ONCE (L163), reused at score/build (L219/270/300).
- µ-proc-03: scoring — `candidate_match_score(CandidateMatchScorePolicy{...})` uses MEMOIZED-on-LazyRegex `entry.regex.has_literal_prefix()` (L217; prior inline `extract_literal_prefix(regex.as_str()).is_some()` re-ran the allocating prefix parser EVERY surviving candidate — identical value, now ≤1×) + `has_distinctive_inner_literal()` (L232, terraform `\.atlasv1\.` infix). `is_named_detector = is_service_anchored_detector && !weak_anchor`. `MlScoreResult::Final`→`finalize_report_candidate` (None→drop) → `build_raw_match` (source_offset+base_offset, line+base_line) → `push_match(max_matches_per_chunk)`; `Pending` (cfg ml)→`push_detector_ml_pending` (pending_credential.into_owned, ml_context); `_Lifetime` (cfg not ml)→`unreachable!`. No clean byte-identical fix — mature.

## µ-bdisp — engine/backend_dispatch.rs (read in full, Iter 29)
Backend fan-out + per-chunk preprocessing entry.
- µ-bdisp-01 (LANDED Iter 29, byte-identical DEDUP, Vector 7): `scan_chunks_with_backend_internal` (L6, pub(crate)) had the parallel-CPU-scan + boundary-reassembly block COPIED twice — the `!gpu_path || chunks.is_empty()` branch AND the `#[cfg(not(feature="gpu"))]` degrade branch — byte-identical but for `return results;` vs `results`. Both run `chunks.par_iter().map(|c| scan_with_backend(c, backend)).collect()` then `boundary::scan_chunk_boundaries(self, chunks, &mut results)` (the seam pass is LOAD-BEARING recall — a secret straddling two gapless same-file chunks is invisible to the per-chunk scan; a drift between the two copies would silently lose findings on one path). Extracted to ONE owner `fn scan_chunks_cpu_parallel(&self, chunks, backend)`; both branches delegate. Byte-identical behavior. Test tests/gap/backend_dispatch_cpu_path_single_owner.rs (source-shape: helper exists, the par_iter scan-map appears EXACTLY once, boundary pass invoked from owner, both branches delegate). gpu branch unchanged (`scan_coalesced_gpu_region_presence`, cfg gpu). 26th landing.
- µ-bdisp-02: `prepare_chunk` (L59, pub(crate); Iter-30 review: was L52, drifted +7 by the Iter-29 scan_chunks_cpu_parallel dedup helper inserted above it) — zero-alloc Cow pipeline. `profile::span(Preprocess)`. If `config.unicode_normalization`: chain `unicode_hardening::normalize_homoglyphs(&chunk.data)` → on Owned, `strip_interior_evasion_controls(&normalized)` (Owned→stripped else keep normalized); on Borrowed (no-op, the 99% pure-ASCII case), strip against `chunk.data` ITSELF so a no-op there preserves the `&'a chunk.data` borrow — the Cow MUST borrow chunk.data (lifetime 'a) so the passthrough text outlives the call inside `PreparedChunk<'a>`. Else `Cow::Borrowed(&chunk.data)`. Then `structured::preprocess(&data_to_pp, path, decode_derived)` (decode_derived = `chunk.metadata.decoded_span.is_some()` — a structured-parse fail on a decode-produced buffer is EXPECTED, must not count as a lost decode surface, Law-10); else multiline (cfg multiline, `has_concatenation_indicators` gate) else `passthrough(data_to_pp)`. `line_offsets: OnceLock::new()` (lazy). CAPTURE: clean borrow design, no fix.

## µ-rpipe — engine/rule_pipeline.rs (read in full, Iter 29)
VRAM-adaptive MegaScan input sizing (the old vyre RulePipeline NFA engine is dead — `--backend mega-scan` collapses onto the GPU region-presence route; this module owns only the live byte-budget selector for routing + cache-key stability).
- µ-rpipe-01: `megascan_input_len` (L30, pub) — `OnceLock` cached for process lifetime (cache-key stability across batches); probes `hw_probe::probe_hardware().gpu_vram_mb` → `megascan_input_len_for_vram_mb` + debug-logs the sizing. `megascan_input_len_for_vram_mb` (L46, pub(crate)) — pure tier map: ≥24GiB→1GiB, ≥12→512MiB, ≥8→256MiB, `Some(_)|None`→128MiB (`MEGASCAN_INPUT_LEN_UNKNOWN`; UNKNOWN deliberately shares the low-mem safety class, NOT the 8-11 tier — bigger buffer = fewer ~50-300µs dispatches but must fit input+transition-tables+output in VRAM, +1GiB upper bound to bound pre-compile time). CAPTURE (Tier-B): the 24/12/8-GiB → 1024/512/256/128-MiB tier thresholds are hardcoded magic constants — a small Tier-B/data-file candidate, but host-routing not detection, low value; not byte-identical.

## µ-p2anc — engine/phase2_anchor_scan.rs (read in full, Iter 31)
`extract_anchored` (L30, pub(crate); Iter-33 review: was L31, drifted -1 by the Iter-31 unused-import removal): the consumer side of the shared-anchor phase-2 localizer — replays the whole-chunk find_iter walk for ONE eligible pattern at its candidate anchor positions via the `\A`-anchored regex, byte-identical to extract_matches on the same pattern (every real match starts at a required-prefix literal, so anchoring at every AC-reported start finds every whole-chunk match).
- µ-p2anc-01: soundness = non-overlapping/leftmost/zero-width-skipping mirror of the whole-chunk cursor. `next_allowed` tracks the whole-chunk cursor (after match `[s,e)` resume at `e`, or `s+1` zero-width), so candidate positions inside an already-consumed match are skipped exactly as the cursor-advance loop skips them. `positions` sorted ascending; `pos < next_allowed`→skip. OnceCell signals (compute at most once on first match, same as extract.rs). MAX_INNER_LOOP_ITERS=1_000_000 + `loop_expired_on_cadence(..,64)` (adversarial-bound parity with extract.rs).
- µ-p2anc-02: left-context handling — for `pos>0`, build `anchored_re.get_with_left_context()` (consumes exactly ONE real preceding char so `\b`/multiline-`^` see real context, not a fabricated haystack start); on None→fall back to whole-chunk `extract_matches` + return (Law-10 no silent recall loss). `context_start = floor_char_boundary(text, pos-1)`; match must `whole.start()==0` (the `\A` guarantee); `full_end = context_start + whole.end()`. Reuses `no_context_locs`/`left_context_locs` capture buffers (no per-candidate alloc).
- µ-p2anc-03 (LANDED Iter 31, byte-identical cross-file DEDUP, Vector 7): the grouped-credential variable-name fallback (`looks_like_variable_name(group) → scan siblings for first non-varname value-shaped len≥8`) was OPEN-CODED identically here AND in extract.rs `extract_grouped_matches` — two copies of a detection-load-bearing heuristic (a drift in one path silently diverges whole-chunk vs anchored recall). Extracted to `scan_filters::resolve_value_shaped_group(locs, text, group, groups_total, current) -> (usize,usize)` (pub(crate); returns the value-shaped sibling range or `current` unchanged). Both sites call it then reslice from their own base (`search_text` / `slice`). Byte-identical: helper returns the same (s,e) the inline loop set, or current (reslicing the same range = same &str). Removed phase2's now-unused `looks_like_variable_name` import. Test tests/gap/value_shaped_group_shared_heuristic.rs — BEHAVIORAL (Law 6) via `resolve_value_shaped_group_for_test` facade driving a real compiled regex: varname→sibling fallback fires, non-varname unchanged, sub-8 sibling rejected, ≤2 groups short-circuits. 27th landing.

## µ-chelp — engine/compile_helpers.rs (read in full, Iter 31)
Scanner-build helpers (setup path).
- µ-chelp-01: `validate_compiled_pattern_detector_indices` (L5, pub(super)) — every ac_map + phase2_patterns entry's `detector_index < detectors_len` else `ScanError::Config` with a rebuild-fix message (loud construction invariant, no silent OOB).
- µ-chelp-02: `build_hot_pattern_slots` (L65, pub(super), cfg simdsieve) — zips `build_hot_pattern_validators(detectors)` × `build_hot_ac_map_index_by_index(detectors, ac_map)` into one `HotPatternSlot{validator: ac_map_index.and(validator), ac_map_index}`; `validate_hot_pattern_runtime_table_lengths` asserts equal lengths BEFORE the zip (Law-10, else zip silently truncates to shorter). Lockstep invariant `validator.is_some() == ac_map_index.is_some()` for ANY detector set (unbacked slot = (None,None), hot path skips it, confirmed AC still covers — recall-safe).
- µ-chelp-03: `build_hot_ac_map_index_by_index` (L112, cfg simdsieve) — per HOT_PATTERN_DETECTOR_IDS slot, `ac_map.iter().position(|e| detectors.get(e.detector_index).id == detector_id && extract_literal_prefixes(e.regex).any(== hot_literal))`. CAPTURE: looks O(slots×ac_map) with a per-iter `extract_literal_prefixes` alloc, BUT the `detector_id ==` check short-circuits (`&&`) BEFORE extract_literal_prefixes, so extraction runs only for entries whose detector matches the slot's id (few). Naive precompute-all-prefixes would REGRESS the common case (extracts for all ~1000 entries vs the matched handful) — NOT a clean byte-identical win; leave. `surface_cuda_acquisition_failure`/`nvidia_userland_present` (cfg linux+gpu) = CUDA→wgpu degrade warning, one-shot OnceLock, --require-gpu hard-exit on NVIDIA host (gpu-gated, not ci-lean-verifiable).

## µ-prof — engine/profile.rs (read in full, Iter 31)
Unified scan profiler (one switch, one hierarchical dump; replaced scattered per-pass atomic hacks). OFF-hot-path telemetry only — no detection logic, no fix.
- µ-prof-01: `enum P` (13 leaf timing points, `#[repr(usize)]`), `const N=13`, `NAMES[N]`, `zeros!` macro (13 AtomicU64) — the enum/N/NAMES/macro must stay in lockstep (drift hazard, but no clean byte-identical FIX — Rust const arrays can't derive the count from the enum; a test gate asserts NAMES.len()==N). `span(P)→Guard` RAII (inert when `!enabled()`, no Instant taken); `Drop` fetch_adds NS+CALLS, and NS_DECODE iff thread-local `IN_DECODE` (decode-rescan attribution). LEAF-only timing; parents (scan/phase2/phase2-capture) are SUMS in `dump` (no double-count, no per-span stack).
- µ-prof-accessors (Iter-33 review, completeness): pub/pub(crate) surface — `enabled()` (L118 pub(crate)), `perf_trace_enabled()` (L123 pub(crate), cfg simd|gpu), `set_in_decode(on)->prev` (L136 pub(crate), cfg decode, decode-rescan nesting), `add_bytes`/`add_files` (L176/L183 pub(crate), throughput line, gated on enabled()), `Guard` (L141 pub(crate) RAII), `span` (L150 pub(crate)).
- µ-prof-02: `set_profile_enabled`/`set_perf_trace_enabled` (pub) — explicit replacements for the old ambient env hooks. `dump(label)` reads-and-resets (swap-to-0), prints the tree (ms/parent%/scan%/calls/ns-call/decode%), folds in 7 auxiliary sub-histograms (scan_inner/decode/extract/decoder/generic/ml_batch/ml_split profile dumps, each early-returns when empty). `reset()` discards counters + resets all 8 sub-profilers (Law-10 telemetry-only discard).

## µ-comp — engine/compile.rs (read in part, Iter 32: the post-build artifact-assembly tail L260-440)
The `CompiledScanner::compile` tail: after AC/phase2 tables are built, assemble the prefilters + interning + final struct.
- µ-comp-01 (LANDED Iter 32, byte-identical Law-7): `alphabet_targets` (L282, the literal/keyword set for AlphabetScreen + BigramBloom) = `ac_literals.clone()` then per phase2-pattern `.extend(keywords.clone())`. The `keywords.clone()` materialized a THROWAWAY `Vec<String>` per pattern (hundreds of patterns) then moved it in, AND alphabet_targets grew by repeated reallocation. Fixed: `reserve(Σ keywords.len())` once + `.extend(keywords.iter().cloned())` (clones each String straight in, no intermediate Vec). Byte-identical (same Strings, same order). Test tests/gap/alphabet_targets_no_per_pattern_clone.rs (source-shape: iter().cloned() present, keywords.clone() gone, reserve present; behavioral correctness covered by the full scan suite which builds the screen/bloom from this set). 28th landing.
- µ-comp-02: `static_intern` (L320; Iter-33 review: was L305, drifted +15 by the Iter-32 reserve/comment additions) — `detectors.iter().flat_map(|d| [id,name,service])` → `StaticInterner::from_detector_strings` (CHD perfect hash, built once, lock-free read, per-scan intern hands out shared Arc<str> w/o the global allocator). `alphabet_screen` None iff alphabet_targets empty; `bigram_bloom = BigramBloom::from_literal_prefixes(&alphabet_targets)`. `confirmed_anchor_index.eligible_count()` logged. `log_quality_warnings(&state.quality_warnings)`.
- µ-comp-03 (LANDED Iter 33, coherence fix, Vector 10): the compile.rs `bigram bloom built` debug log claimed `(4096 bits)` — STALE: BigramBloom is a 65536-bit / 8 KB / 1024-u64 DIRECT lookup table (`TABLE_SLOTS=65536`, `bits: Box<[u64;1024]>`, "60% = 39322 of 65536 slots"); bigram_bloom.rs L21 even says "The previous implementation used a 4096-bit bloom" — the log message was never updated when the impl moved from a 4096-bit hashed bloom to the 65536-slot direct table. Surfaced by the Iter-33 Gemini review (my µ-block had copied the wrong "4096 bits" from the log). Fixed the log → `(65536 slots / 8 KB direct table, …)`. Test tests/gap/bigram_bloom_log_bit_count_coherence.rs (source-shape: compile.rs log states 65536/8KB and NOT "4096 bits"; bigram_bloom.rs TABLE_SLOTS==65536). 29th landing.
- µ-comp-04: entry points (read head L1-130, Iter 33) — `compile` (L9, pub) → `compile_with_gpu_policy` (L13, pub) → `compile_with_gpu_policy_and_tuning` (L24, pub); the GPU-backend cascade (cfg gpu) CUDA `vyre_driver_cuda::cuda_factory()` → wgpu `WgpuBackend::shared()` → None, each failure LOUD (tracing::warn/surface_cuda_acquisition_failure, Law-10 no silent regress); `gpu_disabled` from `gpu_disabled_by_policy()` / Force{Enabled,Disabled}. `with_config` (L491, pub), `with_tuning_config` (L499, pub). CAPTURE: rest of compile.rs (L130-260 AC/regex/phase2 table BUILD) not yet read.

## µ-p2gs — engine/phase2_generic_shape.rs (read in full, Iter 32)
`generic_value_shape_rejected` (L42, pub(crate)): the PURE per-candidate generic-secret value-shape rejection gauntlet — `Some(GenericValueShapeStage)` iff a precision shape gate would reject (names the FIRING gate for the `--dogfood` suppression trace, KH-L-0412: each former silent `continue` became `return Some(stage)` so every generic-bridge drop is loud+attributable), else None. Pure: reads value/entropy/chunk-meta/config only; the caller `scan_generic_assignments` records telemetry + emits.
- µ-p2gs-01: gate order (each `if predicate { return Some(stage) }`): caesar-source-type → entropy-below-floor (generic_bridge_entropy_below_floor w/ generic_keyword_low_entropy) → len<8 → public_noncredential_shape_with_randomness → contains(' ')=CodeExpressionChars → source_code_expression → source_symbol_identifier (caesar source-path) → `starts_with(':')||contains("::")`=ScopeResolution (Rust/C++ scope FP family) → TypeNameShape (upper-start, ≥2 upper, has-lower, 8..=40, all-alnum, keep_identifier_gate randomness-lifted) → NonJwtDotted (contains '.' && !is_structured_dotted_token; real JWT begins eyJ) → PureIdentifierNoDigit → PureIdentifier → WordSeparatedIdentifier → SchemePrefixedUri → PunctuationDecoratedIdentifier → UrlOrPathSegment → VendoredMinifiedPath → RegexLiteralTail → Base64Blob (gated !allow_canonical_hex_key && !allow_encoded_text_secret) → TrimmedAwsArn → canonical-hex-placeholder → known-example → DecodedPlaceholder → DecodedBenignText → EncodedBinary → RandomByteBlob.
- µ-p2gs-02: `TokenRandomness::for_candidate(value)` computed ONCE (L71), threaded into every keep_*_gate_with_randomness — the SHARED context-aware gate (English-bigram model: LIFTS the identifier/word-separated gate, recovering ~1114 real random lowercase passwords like `GRAPHITE_PASS=gjbubxsu` that are shape-identical to code identifiers, while a dictionary identifier stays suppressed; scan-time + post-process weak-anchor paths agree exactly). `high_entropy_punctuation_payload` + `allow_ambiguous_base64_candidate` also computed once + reused as gate exemptions. No alloc/dedup fix — mature, recall-bench-guarded; each gate carries its real-world FP-family provenance (llama-cpp jinja, malachite ecdsa, gogs templates, SecretBench negatives.py).

## µ-p2c — engine/phase2_compiled.rs (read in full, Iter 34)
Phase-2 pattern execution: route a chunk through the shared-anchor / small / large / decode-focus paths, scanning only the SPARSE active phase-2 set.
- µ-p2c-01: `scan_phase2_patterns` (L16, pub(crate)) — deadline early-return; SHARED-ANCHOR fast path first (if phase2_patterns nonempty && tuning.phase2_anchor_enabled && phase2_anchor_index present → `scan_phase2_with_anchors`, supersedes size split, recall-identical); else `text.len() > LARGE_FALLBACK_SCAN_THRESHOLD` → `scan_large_phase2_patterns`; else small whole-chunk path. `scan_phase2_patterns_focused` (L116, pub(crate); Iter-36 review: all line refs in µ-p2c drifted up ~17 from the Iter-34 helper extraction shrinking the small-path closure) — decode-recursion variant: cursor-bounded to the focus window `[start,end)` (everything outside was scanned+deduped at parent), keeps full-splice signals so focus matches stay byte-identical to whole-splice (`decode_focus_parity`); PRECONDITION preprocessed.text byte-aligned with chunk.data (passthrough). `has_active_phase2_patterns_for_chunk` (L294), `phase2_profile_dump`/`reset` (L552/591).
- µ-p2c-02 (LANDED Iter 34, byte-identical DEDUP, Vector 7): the per-pattern extraction loop (sparse active set, `is_multiple_of(16)` deadline cadence, per-pattern `phase2_pattern_prof_record`, `extract_matches`) was OPEN-CODED IDENTICALLY in the small-chunk (`scan_phase2_patterns`) AND large-chunk (`scan_large_phase2_patterns`) whole-chunk paths — a drift hazard (e.g. changing the abort cadence on one path but not the other makes --timeout behave differently BY CHUNK SIZE). Extracted to `extract_active_phase2_patterns(active_patterns, preprocessed, line_offsets, code_lines, documentation_lines, chunk, scan_state, deadline, prof)`; both `with_active_phase2_patterns` closures delegate. The decode-focus copy (L208) is CORRECTLY left separate — it calls `extract_matches_inner(..., cursor, ...)` (cursor-bounded), a real difference not duplication. Byte-identical: `extract_matches(..., deadline)` ≡ `extract_matches_inner(..., None, deadline)`, the focus path keeps its cursor. Test tests/gap/phase2_extract_loop_single_owner.rs (source-shape: helper exists, 2 delegations, the `is_multiple_of(16)` cadence drops 3→2 sites = the two whole-chunk copies collapsed, focus extract_matches_inner kept). 30th landing.
- µ-p2c-03: `populate_active_phase2` (L366) — keyword_ac present: phase2_always_active_prefilter `mark_matches` (RegexSet over anchorless always-active detectors; ASCII-folded alternate on pure-ASCII = identical marking, far faster, the ~90%-of-phase2 prefilter cost; `None`→mark all always-active indices except anchor-eligible under anchor_mode) THEN keyword marking (from phase2_keyword_hints fast path, else `keyword_ac.find_iter(data)` → phase2_keyword_to_patterns CSR → scratch.mark); no keyword_ac → mark ALL. `with_active_phase2_patterns` (L243) — ACTIVE_PATTERNS_POOL thread-local scratch (reset not freed, reused per chunk per worker), passes the SPARSE `&[usize]` to the closure (no Vec<&CompiledPattern> collect). CAPTURE: mature, no further byte-identical fix.

## µ-p2ca — engine/phase2_compiled_anchored.rs (read in full, Iter 35)
`scan_phase2_with_anchors` (L121, pub(crate); Iter-36 review: was L50, drifted +71 by the Iter-35 verify_anchored_candidates helper added before it): the shared-anchor localized phase-2 scan — ONE AC pass over eligible patterns' required-prefix literals → anchored verification, recall-identical to the legacy active-set loop (`phase2_anchor_parity`).
- µ-p2ca-01: `with_active_phase2_scratch` (L18) — like `with_active_phase2_patterns` but hands the closure the FULL `ActivePatternsScratch` (not just the sparse list) so it can `is_active(idx)` in O(1) to gate candidate positions; anchor_mode=true; `phase2_reverse_enabled` reverses active order. `KEYWORD_ANCHOR_WHOLE_CHUNK_CUTOFF=2`: `localize_keyword_anchors` only when >2 active eligible keyword-anchors (else the AC-localization overhead isn't worth it vs whole-chunk).
