Three candidate-collection modes: `collect_candidates` (gated by is_active) when localizing; `cands.clear()` when `phase2_always_anchor_present==Some(false)` (GPU proved no anchor); else `collect_always_active_candidates`. Focus-window shift: candidates relative to `scan_text[fs..fe]` get `c.1 += shift` to lift back into full-text coords.
- µ-p2ca-02 (LANDED Iter 35, byte-identical DEDUP, Vector 7): the anchored-candidate VERIFY loop (group a pattern's contiguous `(pat,pos)` run → `extract_anchored` if `anchored_regex(pat)` compiled, else LOUD fallback to cursor-bounded `extract_matches_inner`; per-pattern `phase2_pattern_prof_record`) was OPEN-CODED byte-identically in TWO passes: the main shared-anchor candidates AND the localized-homoglyph plain candidates (Iter-36 review: the two open-coded loops are now both delegation calls to `verify_anchored_candidates` at L48 — main delegation ~L190, homoglyph ~L223). Drift hazard: a change to one pass's anchored-vs-fallback dispatch (or profiling) silently diverges recall between them. Extracted `verify_anchored_candidates(anchor_idx, cands: &[(u32,u32)], preprocessed, line_offsets, code_lines, documentation_lines, chunk, scan_state, cursor, deadline, prof)`; both passes delegate. The `Phase2AnchoredVerify` profile span stays at the MAIN call site (outside the loop, behavior-preserving). Byte-identical. Test tests/gap/anchored_candidate_verify_single_owner.rs (source-shape: helper exists, 2 delegations, `match anchor_idx.anchored_regex(pat)` dispatch drops to 1 site). 31st landing.
- µ-p2ca-03: after the two anchored passes — `plain_always_mark()` patterns (no folded literal, run whole-chunk) + the non-eligible active patterns (`scratch.active`, skip eligible under localize, `expired_on_cadence(.., 16)`) both run `extract_matches_inner` cursor-bounded. The union of anchored-verified + whole-chunk = exactly the legacy active set (recall-identical). CAPTURE: these two trailing loops also share the extract_matches_inner+prof shape with each other and with phase2_compiled's focus loop — a deeper cross-file DEDUP candidate (cursor-bounded variant of µ-p2c-02's helper), but the gating differs (plain_always_mark list vs active-with-eligible-skip) — borderline, not cleanly one helper.

## µ-p2en — engine/phase2_entropy{.rs, /helpers.rs, /line_context.rs} (read in full, Iter 37)
Entropy-fallback driver + its shape/line-ownership helpers (all cfg entropy). The LAST detection lane: high-Shannon-entropy values with no service prefix and no generic-keyword anchor.
- µ-p2en-01: `scan_entropy_fallback` (phase2_entropy.rs L18, pub(crate)) — gated: `entropy_enabled`, not caesar source-type, `entropy_lines = preprocessed.text.lines().collect()` (collected ONCE, reused by all consumers). Admission = `path_entropy_appropriate` OR `isolated_bare_candidate`; cfg simd adds a `has_high_entropy_run_at_least(min_secret_len)` precheck before the full Shannon sweep + `lower_dash_app_password_candidate`. Builds `skip_lines` HashSet from non-generic/entropy named matches + (cfg ml) named-pending-ml lines so entropy doesn't dup a line a named detector claimed. `keyword_free_threshold` = SENSITIVE_FILE_VERY_HIGH vs VERY_HIGH by `is_sensitive_path`. `find_entropy_secrets_with_canonical_lift_and_lines` → per match: `entropy_match_suppression_stage` gauntlet (cfg, gates.rs) → `entropy_candidate_owned_by_named_assignment` → UNIFIED SCORING: cfg ml + entropy_ml_authoritative routes to `push_entropy_authoritative_ml_pending` (MoE authoritative, no entropy-floor; apply_ml_batch_scores runs the ONE penalty/path/calibration/checksum/floor pipeline — no bespoke tail), else `finalize_report_candidate` + `push_match_lazy`. `build_raw_match` closure Arc::clones the pre-interned (id,name,service) triple ONLY for emitted candidates. `entropy_skip_line_index` = `absolute_line?.checked_sub(base_line+1)`.
- µ-p2en-02 (LANDED Iter 37, byte-identical Law-7 lazy-alloc): `keyword_is_credential_anchor` (helpers.rs L49, pub(crate)) computed `let lower = keyword.to_ascii_lowercase()` UP FRONT, but the `normalize_assignment_keyword(keyword)`→`normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential` path is checked FIRST (reads `keyword` directly) and returns early for credential keywords — so `lower` was allocated+discarded on the common positive path. Moved the `to_ascii_lowercase()` BELOW the early-return; only the GENERIC_ASSIGNMENT_KEYWORDS-substring + `bearer` tail (the false-leaning path) allocates it. Byte-identical (same value, same outputs). Live callers: line_context.rs:78, gates.rs:109 (per entropy candidate). Test tests/gap/entropy_keyword_anchor_lazy_lowercase.rs (BEHAVIORAL Law-6 via new plain `pub fn keyword_is_credential_anchor_for_test` facade: credential keywords admit / sentinel + plain words reject / + source-order pin that `return true;` precedes `let lower`). 32nd landing.
- µ-p2en-03: helpers.rs `ENTROPY_DETECTOR_METADATA` (L12, const [(&str,&str,&str);4], index-parallel with `classify_entropy_detector_index` L26: "none (high-entropy)"→0, contains password/pwd→1, token→2, else→3) — pre-interned into `entropy_metadata_by_index` so emit clones an Arc by index, not re-intern per finding (PERF-locality_intern-1). line_context.rs has TWO pub(super) entry points: `value_line_has_same_line_credential_keyword` (L12, the canonical-lift surface = `line_has_credential_assignment_surface(line) || value_owned_by_local_credential_key(line, value)`) and `value_line_has_random_byte_blob_owner` (L24). `value_owned_by_local_key_matching` (L82) is the SHARED key-ownership scan (`line.find(value)` → rfind `=`/`:` separators before it, rsplit+`normalize_assignment_keyword` the LHS key, `accepts(normalized)` closure). Its TWO DIRECT callers pass different predicates: `value_owned_by_local_credential_key` (L75, accepts = `normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential || keyword_is_credential_anchor`) and `value_line_has_random_byte_blob_owner` (L24→L32, accepts = `random_byte_assignment_key_is_high_signal`). So L12 reaches the owner indirectly via L75; L24 reaches it directly. A clean closure-parameterized one-owner; no fix. `entropy_value_line` (L37, pub(crate)) — line-offset lookup then newline-scan fallback, both LAW10 boundary-default; requires `line_text.contains(value)`. `random_byte_assignment_key_is_high_signal` (L114) builds a compact lowercased key once. No further byte-identical fix — mature.

## µ-aci — ascii_ci.rs (read in full, Iter 38)
Zero-alloc ASCII case-insensitive byte primitives (the module exists to kill `text.to_ascii_lowercase().contains(needle)` per-match alloc storms; tens of thousands of transient String allocs/chunk).
- µ-aci-01: `ci_find` (L264, pub(crate)) vs `ci_find_nonempty` (L277, pub(crate)) — TWO entry points with a deliberate empty-needle split: ci_find treats `""` as MATCH (true; for pre-lowercased static needles), ci_find_nonempty treats `""` as NO-MATCH (false; for user/configured keyword lists where empty must not hit every offset). ci_find delegates to ci_find_nonempty for the non-empty case (already one-owner; no dup). Skim = ONE `memchr::memchr2_iter(first_lower, first_upper, haystack)` SIMD pass, full `eq_ignore_ascii_case` only at candidate first-byte positions. For a non-letter first byte first_lower==first_upper (memchr2 of two equal bytes — degenerate but correct).
- µ-aci-02 (LANDED CANDIDATE — captured, NOT byte-identical, NOT landed): `contains_path_segment` (L308) + `contains_path_segment_two` (L342) share the leading-segment-at-offset-0 special case + the `memchr2_iter(b'/', b'\\')` separator-anchored loop with `eq_ignore_ascii_case` body + trailing-separator gate. A merge to one owner (`contains_path_segment_n(&[&str])`) would change the inner-loop shape (slice-of-segments vs fixed 1/2) — NOT provably byte-identical (loop unroll + bounds differ), and the 2-segment requires BOTH inner separators. Left as-is; both already alloc-free.
- µ-aci-03: SIMD writers (`write_ascii_lowercase_avx2` L123 32-byte, `write_ascii_lowercase_neon` L175 16-byte; `has_ascii_uppercase_avx2`/`_neon`) + scalar `ascii_lower_branchless` (L225, body `byte | ((uppercase as u8) << 5)` where `uppercase = ascii_is_uppercase(byte)`; Iter39-fold: was mis-noted `is_upper`) + `ascii_is_uppercase` (L233, `byte.wrapping_sub(b'A') <= b'Z'-b'A'`) are all `#[cfg(any(feature="gpu", test))]` — NOT in the ci-lean shipped scan path (only the GPU lowercase-materialization path + tests). The three cfg-gated PUBLIC entry points over them: `extend_ascii_lowercase_from` (L25, `#[cfg(test)]`, Vec lowercase-append via spare_capacity_mut + set_len), `write_ascii_lowercase_into` (L40, MaybeUninit slice writer = SIMD prefix + scalar tail), `has_ascii_uppercase` (L50, SIMD-or-scalar-any). `ends_with_ignore_ascii_case`/`starts_with_ignore_ascii_case` (L239/L249) are unconditional. The avx2/neon prefix returns the scalar-tail offset; the loop bound `offset + 32 <= len` is monotonic so the `has_*` early `return true` is correct.

## µ-uha — unicode_hardening.rs (read in full, Iter 38)
Unicode-evasion detection + normalization (homoglyph/zero-width/RTL/fullwidth/decomposed/separator). Detect (`detect_unicode_attacks` L49 → Vec<EvasionMatch>) and normalize (`normalize_homoglyphs` L142 → Cow) are the two surfaces.
- µ-uha-01 (LANDED Iter 38, byte-identical DEDUP / drift-hazard kill): `contains_evasion` (L394, pub(crate)) inlined the SAME 8-predicate disjunction (`cyrillic_to_latin(ch).is_some() || greek_to_latin(ch).is_some() || is_fullwidth || is_zero_width || is_rtl_override || is_unicode_separator_evasion || is_combining_mark || is_ascii_evasion_control`) that `normalized_char` (L157) already owns (Replace = the 3 homoglyph families, Drop = the 5-way OR, Keep = none). Two copies of "which chars are evasive" drift: a category added to normalized_char would silently desync the detector. Replaced the inline closure with `!matches!(normalized_char(ch), NormalizedChar::Keep)`. Byte-identical (Replace∪Drop == the 8-way OR; order-independent boolean). Kept the `contains_ascii_evasion(bytes)` byte fast-path short-circuit ahead of the char loop. Test tests/gap/contains_evasion_single_owner_classification.rs (BEHAVIORAL via existing testing::unicode_hardening::contains_evasion facade: each category detected — incl. DEL 0x7F routed through the char/Drop path since the byte path only catches <0x20 — clean ASCII + \n\t\r rejected; + single-owner source pin that the inline disjunction is gone). 33rd landing.
- µ-uha-02: `normalize_homoglyphs` (L142) FAST PATH = `ascii_normalization_scan` (L216) returns CleanAscii → `Cow::Borrowed` zero-alloc (the vast majority of source). EvasiveAscii (sub-0x20 control ≠ \n\r\t) OR NonAscii falls to `normalize_evasive_chars` (L178) which lazily allocates the output String ONLY on first Replace/Drop via `get_or_insert_with(|| { String::with_capacity(text.len()); push_str(&text[..byte_pos]); })` — a clean lazy-materialization (LAW10 identity Cow::Borrowed when no transform). `full_normalize` (L229) = NFC-collect then normalize_homoglyphs. Mature, no fix.
- µ-uha-03: Tier-B `EVASION_ANCHORS` (L242, LazyLock, `include_str!("../data/evasion-anchors.toml")`, PANICS on malformed bundled data — fail-closed, refuses to run with weakened split-credential evasion truth) parsed by `parse_evasion_anchors` (L255, pub(crate): toml deserialize → trim + reject-empty + BTreeSet dup-reject + reject-all-empty, returns Result not panic — the LazyLock is what converts Err→panic) → single `EVASION_ANCHOR_AC` Aho-Corasick (L278, one O(n) pass over all anchors, NOT one search/anchor; degrade is LOUD via prefilter_degrade::warn_prefilter_disabled → None). `strip_interior_evasion_controls` (L322) cheap-gate = `memchr2_iter(\t,\r)` flanked by `is_credential_body_byte` both sides → else Cow::Borrowed; per AC anchor walks ≤`MAX_BODY_WINDOW=256` body bytes dropping prefix-anchored interior `\t`/`\r`; word-boundary `is_anchor_start_blocked_by` (alnum|_) before anchor. `is_evasion_char` (L510, pub(crate)) = `is_zero_width(ch) || is_rtl_override(ch)` — the narrow public predicate (NOT the full evasion set; that's contains_evasion/normalized_char). Captures: `is_credential_body_byte`/`is_anchor_start_blocked_by`/`is_interior_control` are tight Tier-A-ish predicates. Homoglyph tables `cyrillic_to_latin`(L416, 31 chars)/`greek_to_latin`(L456, 26 chars) are hardcoded match arms (Iter39-fold: line/count were 418/32 & 458/25 pre the Iter-38 contains_evasion −2-line shift; verified arm counts via grep `=> Some(`) — Tier-B-migration candidate (CAPTURED; not landed: would need a data-file + LazyLock map, behavior-preserving but not byte-identical to a match).

## µ-senv — structured/parsers/env.rs (read in full, Iter 40)
.env KEY=VALUE extraction → Vec<ExtractedPair>.
- µ-senv-01: `parse_env` (L15, pub(crate)) — per `text.lines()`: trim, skip empty/`#`-comment, `strip_prefix("export ")` (LAW10 unchanged-if-absent), `split_once('=')`, trim key+value, skip empty key, `unquote_env_value` → push ExtractedPair{context=key, value=unquoted, line=idx+1}. The ONLY parser whose line is the literal source line index (no anchor resolution) — it parses line-oriented so the line is exact.
- µ-senv-02: `unquote_env_value` (L43) — if first byte ∈ `"`/`'`/backtick AND `closing_quote_idx` found AND trailing after close is empty-or-`#`: return inner unquoted; else if `find_inline_comment` (L78, `windows(2)` ws-then-`#`, returns `i+1`): strip trailing comment trim_end; else verbatim. `closing_quote_idx` (L60) honors `\\` escape (idx+=2). Inline comments stripped on UNQUOTED values only (quoted bodies keep `#` — explicit opt-in). No fix (per-short-line strings; the windows(2) inline-comment scan is not a hot-path alloc).

## µ-syaml — structured/parsers/yaml.rs (read in full, Iter 40)
k8s Secret (`data:` base64-decode + `stringData:` raw) + docker-compose (`environment:` mapping OR `KEY=val` sequence) extraction. decode_derived flag gates the Law-10 structured-gap accounting (depth0 = real lost surface → counted+warned; depth>0 = re-decode of an already-scanned splice → debug-only, NOT counted, prevents false telemetry inflation).
- µ-syaml-01 (LANDED Iter 40, byte-identical DEDUP one-owner): the `stringData:` loop (was L106-123) and `extract_environment_block`'s `Value::Mapping` arm (was L290-307) were BYTE-IDENTICAL scalar-mapping extraction (read each scalar via `yaml_scalar_value_text`, skip empty key, build `yaml_mapping_anchors` = (`"{key}: {value}"`, `"{key}:"`) primary+fallback, push `owned_anchor_with_fallback`). Extracted `push_scalar_mapping_pairs(map: &serde_yaml::Mapping, pending)` as the single owner; both surfaces now delegate `push_scalar_mapping_pairs(map, pending)`. Byte-identical: serde_yaml::Mapping iterates insertion-order (deterministic), same skip/anchor/push; both already route to the SHARED `finalize_pending_pairs` (line.rs) for AC-anchored 1-based line resolution. The `data:` loop stays separate (it base64-decodes via `decode_standard_base64`, not raw). Test tests/gap/yaml_scalar_mapping_single_owner.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW plain `pub fn parse_docker_compose_tuples`/`parse_k8s_secret_tuples` facades returning (ctx,val,line): exact tuples for compose `environment:` mapping [(API_KEY,sk_live…,L4),(REGION,us-east-1,L5)] + k8s `stringData:` [(password,…,L6),(token,…,L7)]; + 2-delegation source pin). 34th landing.
- µ-syaml-02: parse entry points `parse_k8s_secret` (L21, pub(crate), multi-doc via `parse_yaml_documents`) / `parse_docker_compose` (L118, pub(crate), single via `parse_yaml_value`; Iter42-fold: was L133 pre the Iter-40 stringData dedup −15-line shift). `report_yaml_parse_failure` (L153; Iter42-fold: was L168) 3-arm surface match (k8s-secret/docker-compose/_) — each a near-identical `tracing::warn!` differing only in static message → NOT byte-identical-mergeable (tracing treats the message literal specially; runtime-var message changes log shape). CAPTURED. Caps: `SERDE_YAML_PARSE_RECURSION_LIMIT=128` (parse-time, serde_yaml-enforced), `MAX_YAML_TRAVERSAL_DEPTH=256` (post-parse compose/list traversal, `extract_k8s_secret`/`find_environment_pairs`). `yaml_scalar_value_text` (String/Number/Bool→Some, else None). `yaml_kind` reads `.kind`. Sequence-form environment (`KEY=val` strings) uses `owned_anchor` (no fallback) — distinct shape, not merged.
- µ-syaml-03: dispatcher structured/parsers.rs (37 lines, wc-verified — Iter42 rejected a Gemini "38" flag) routes by format to these + line.rs/json.rs/hcl.rs. `ExtractedPair` (structured/mod.rs L15) is the shared output struct: the STRUCT is `pub(crate)` but its three fields `context`/`value`/`line` are declared `pub` (Iter42-fold: was mis-noted "fields pub(crate)"; effective external visibility is still capped by the pub(crate) struct). NOTE for future review: the Iter-40 dedup REMOVED ~30 lines from yaml.rs so any later µ-block line refs into this file shift up — anchor on fn name.

## µ-shcl — structured/parsers/hcl.rs (read in full, Iter 41)
Terraform/HCL extractor: `variable "<name>" { default = ... }` blocks, flat `.tfvars`/`locals` assignments, heredocs → Vec<ExtractedPair>. A hand-rolled line-state machine (no serde — HCL has no stable Rust deserializer for this).
- µ-shcl-01 (LANDED Iter 41, byte-identical DEDUP one-owner): the HCL-identifier char-class reject `name.is_empty() || !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '-')` was inlined VERBATIM in 3 fns — `parse_variable_header` (var name), `parse_hcl_assignment` (LHS key), `parse_heredoc_marker` (marker). Extracted `fn is_hcl_identifier(s) -> bool { !s.is_empty() && s.chars().all(|c| …alphanumeric||'_'||'-') }`; the 3 sites became `if !is_hcl_identifier(x) { return None; }`. Byte-identical by De Morgan (`!(!empty && all)` == `empty || !all`); `chars().all` on empty is true but `!is_empty()` short-circuits identically. Test tests/gap/hcl_identifier_single_owner.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW plain `pub fn parse_hcl_tuples`: all 3 id-paths extract exact tuples [(my_key,secret-val-0123,L2),(api-token,…,L5),(config,heredoc-secret-body,L8)] + reject path `bad.key=` drops while `good_key=` extracts + source pin is_hcl_identifier refs==4, inline id-class==1). NOT folded: the boundary check in `parse_hcl_default_in_fragment` uses the NEGATED single-char form `!c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() && c != '_' && c != '-'` (word-boundary, not all-chars) — distinct predicate, left alone. 35th landing.
- µ-shcl-02: caps `MAX_VARIABLE_BLOCK_LINES=16` (lookahead inside a variable block before giving up), `MAX_HEREDOC_LINES=512` (`collect_heredoc` body window). `HclValue` enum = Scalar|HeredocStart(marker)|CompositeStart. Composite (`{`/`[`) defaults recurse one level via `default_composite_depth` tracking, emitting `{var}.{name}` contexts. Brace depth tracked by `brace_delta_outside_strings_and_comments` (strips line comment first, then counts `{`/`}` outside quotes w/ `\\` escape). `find_unescaped_quote` is interpolation-aware (`${…}` depth + nested quote tracking) — the one genuinely subtle scanner. parse_hcl_default_in_fragment handles inline `default=` on the same line as the `variable {` header.
- µ-shcl-03: comment stripping has TWO owners by necessity: `strip_hcl_line_comment` (returns `&str`, single-line `#`/`//`/`/*` truncate, quote-aware) for value/brace scans, and `strip_hcl_comments` (returns String, STATEFUL `*in_block_comment` across lines for `/* … */`) for the main line walk. Different return types + block-comment state = not mergeable; both are quote+escape state machines but the line one can't carry block state. CAPTURED (no byte-identical merge).

## µ-pdisp — structured/parsers.rs dispatcher (read in full, Iter 41)
37-line module boundary. `mod {env,hcl,json,line,yaml}` private; re-exports the 6 parse entry points (`parse_env`/`parse_hcl`/`parse_jupyter`/`parse_tfstate`/`parse_docker_compose`/`parse_k8s_secret`) as `pub(crate) use`. `structured_gap_is_real` (L31, pub(super), `#[must_use]`) is the SHARED Law-10 decode-gap arbiter: decode_derived=false → `telemetry::record_structured_parse_failure()` + return true (real lost surface, caller warns loud); decode_derived=true → return false (already-decoded splice, caller stays debug-quiet, no telemetry inflation). Single source of truth consumed by yaml.rs + json.rs. `ExtractedPair{context,value,line}` is the one shared output struct (`super::ExtractedPair`). Clean one re-export point — no fix.

Deleted the index_key twin; its sole caller `tfstate_instance_context` (L187, `instance.get("index_key").and_then(...)`) now routes to `json_scalar_literal`. Byte-identical (same match arms ⇒ same Option<String> for all inputs) AND semantically coherent (both = "render a JSON scalar as a literal"). NOTE the DISTINCT third fn `scalar_value_text` (L478) is NOT merged: its String arm returns RAW `s.clone()` (no JSON-quoting) vs json_scalar_literal's `json_string_literal(s)` (quoted) — different output, kept separate. Cap `MAX_TFSTATE_DEPTH=256` checked in all 4 recursive fns.
- µ-sjson-03: jupyter traversal — `parse_jupyter` filters `cell_type=="code"`, `extract_jupyter_source` (L304, String|Array-of-fragments via `jupyter_join_text_fragments` L450 which sets `malformed` on any non-string fragment → LOUD warn@depth0), `extract_jupyter_outputs`→`extract_jupyter_output` (L366, reads `text` / `data."text/plain"` / `traceback`) → `extract_jupyter_output_text` (L410, `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`). `push_jupyter_text_pair` (L463) skips `value.trim().is_empty()`, owned_anchor if a fragment anchor else value_anchor. `first_nonempty_fragment_anchor` (L513; Iter45-fold: was L518, −5 from the twin deletion) trims trailing `\n`/`\r`. `json_string_literal` (L509; Iter45-fold: was L514) = `serde_json::Value::String(s).to_string()` (the canonical JSON-escape owner). All warns are Law-10-honest (gap counted once, debug-only on derived).

## µ-spprof — engine/scan_postprocess/profile.rs (read in full, Iter 44)
Phase-2 post-process PROFILERS (measurement only, extracted from scan_postprocess.rs per Law 5). THREE independent profilers, all `keyhog scan --profile`-gated via `super::profile::enabled()` (super=engine; this file is `mod scan_postprocess_profile` included at engine level via `#[path]`, so `super::profile` = the unified engine/profile.rs, NOT itself — that's why super::profile::enabled() resolves). Module is PRIVATE `mod scan_postprocess_profile` (engine/mod.rs:120, no pub(crate)) → fns not reachable from integration tests.
- µ-spprof-01 (LANDED Iter 44, byte-identical coherence/dedup): the always-compiled confirmed-pass counters spelled `std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed` in full (11 occurrences) while the ml/decode-gated code used the imported `Relaxed` alias whose `use` was `#[cfg(any(feature="decode", feature="ml"))]`. Made the import UNCONDITIONAL (the always-compiled confirmed_* code uses it too, so no unused-import risk under no-ml-no-decode) and replaced all 11 full-paths with the `Relaxed` alias. Byte-identical (alias resolves to the same enum variant; only Relaxed is ever used — verified no Acquire/Release/SeqCst). One ordering spelling for the whole module (Vector-7 dedup + Vector-10 coherence). Test tests/gap/profile_relaxed_ordering_single_reference.rs (SOURCE-SHAPE gate — module unreachable behaviorally: 0 full-path occurrences remain, import unconditional/un-cfg-gated, confirmed_prof_record uses `fetch_add(1, Relaxed)`; + ml histogram coherence pin: `[AtomicU64; 10]` array ⇔ `_ => 9` catch-all so the bucket index is always in-bounds). 37th landing.
- µ-spprof-02: confirmed-pass profiler (always compiled): `confirmed_prof_enabled` (L19)/`confirmed_prof_record(ConfirmedStage, Duration)`/`confirmed_prof_stage_take`→[(u64,u64);3]/`confirmed_prof_vecs(len)` (OnceLock-lazy per-pattern AtomicU64 vecs)/`confirmed_prof_reset`. The parallel per-profiler gate-check fns: `ml_batch_prof_enabled` (L149, cfg ml) + `decode_prof_enabled` (L256, cfg decode) — all three just delegate to `super::profile::enabled()` (Iter45-fold: named for exhaustiveness). `ConfirmedStage` enum (SuffixGate=0/AnchorCollect=1/Extract=2) is INDEX-COUPLED with the dump's `labels = ["suffix-gate","anchor-collect","extract"]` (L92; Iter45-fold: was L101, −9 from the Iter-44 reflows) — coherent drift hazard (CAPTURED, currently aligned). `confirmed_profile_dump`/`confirmed_profile_reset` are `impl super::CompiledScanner` methods; dump sorts top-30 by ns, indexes `ac_map` then `phase2_patterns` (total = ac_map.len()+phase2_patterns.len()).
- µ-spprof-03: ML batch-size histogram (`#[cfg(feature="ml")]`): `ml_batch_bucket(n)` 10 buckets (0/1/2-7/8-15/16-31/32-63/64-127/128-255/256-1023/1024+), `ml_batch_record` increments bucket + ML_BATCH_CALLS/CANDIDATES + the >=64 GPU-eligible counters (64 = GPU MoE dispatch threshold), `ml_batch_profile_dump`/`_reset` (+ `#[cfg(not(ml))]` no-op twins). Decode-recursion profiler (`#[cfg(feature="decode")]`): pub(crate) `DECODE_PARENTS/SUBCHUNKS/SUBCHUNK_BYTES/GEN_NS/SCAN_NS` AtomicU64 statics poked inline by scan_postprocess.rs; `decode_profile_dump`→(parents,subchunks,bytes,gen_ms,scan_ms) returns the tuple so a measurement test can assert (the only profiler that returns data vs eprintln-only), `decode_profile_reset` (+ not(decode) twins returning zeros). All-zero early-return guards keep an unrun profiler silent.

## µ-homo — homoglyph.rs (read in full, Iter 46)
Forward homoglyph expansion: turns a detector regex prefix into a homoglyph-aware character-class regex so Cyrillic/Greek/fullwidth lookalikes match. (Distinct from unicode_hardening.rs's REVERSE cyrillic_to_latin/greek_to_latin normalization tables — same glyph universe, opposite direction; a drift hazard but not byte-identical mergeable, CAPTURED.)
- µ-homo-01 (LANDED Iter 46, byte-identical Law-7 alloc + Vector-10 doc coherence): `expand_homoglyphs` (L56, pub(crate)) built its output via `String::new()` (zero capacity) then per-char push — reallocating as it grows over every detector prefix at build. Added `String::with_capacity(pattern.len() * 8)` (~`[<ascii><glyphs>]`≈8B/mapped char). Byte-identical (capacity is unobservable through the returned String). ALSO fixed the stale doc example (L55): was `"ghp_" -> "[gｇ][hнｈ][pрｐ]_"` (one glyph/class) but the real map emits MORE — corrected to the true output `"[gɡｇ][hнһｈ][pрρｐ]_"`. Test tests/gap/homoglyph_expand_exact_and_reserved.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW plain `pub fn expand_homoglyphs_str`: EXACT expansion of "ghp_"→`[gɡｇ][hнһｈ][pрρｐ]_`, "a."→`[aаαａ]\.` (regex-special escape), "z"→"z" (unmapped passthrough), "."→`\.`, ""→""; + source pin with_capacity present & String::new absent). 38th landing.
- µ-homo-02: `homoglyph_map` (L10) is a OnceLock<HashMap<char, Vec<char>>> — 38 ASCII keys (a-y lowercase minus a few + select uppercase) → lookalike glyph vecs (the 'l' entry is the fattest: 10 glyphs incl. digit-zero lookalikes O/o). `push_regex_literal_char` (L77) escapes the regex metacharacters `\ . + * ? ( ) | [ ] { } ^ $ # & -` before emitting a non-mapped char. HARDCODED map = Tier-B-migration candidate (CAPTURED; the reverse tables in unicode_hardening are the same candidate — a single bidirectional glyph data file would own both). Mature otherwise.

## µ-jwt — jwt.rs (read in full, Iter 47)
JWT structural validation (`pub mod jwt`, public API). Decodes header+payload base64url, validates shape, surfaces alg/iss/sub/aud/exp + anomalies. NOT crypto verification (no issuer key) — high-recall structural layer; verifier crate does sigs.
- µ-jwt-01 (LANDED Iter 47, byte-identical Law-7 alloc + Vector-10 doc coherence): (a) `finding_metadata` (L107, pub fn — the SINGLE shared bridge called by in-process finalize + verify-skip + daemon-route, so jwt.alg_none can't diverge by route) built its metadata map via `HashMap::new()` (0 cap) then ≤8 inserts (jwt.alg + iss/sub/aud/exp + ≤3 anomaly keys) — added `HashMap::with_capacity(8)`, byte-identical (capacity unobservable). (b) `JwtAnomaly::NonStandardTyp` doc (L58-59) listed only 4 standard typs but `is_standard_typ` (L286) accepts FIVE (`JWT|at+jwt|id+jwt|dpop+jwt|logout+jwt`) — added `logout+jwt` to the doc. Test tests/gap/jwt_finding_metadata_reserve_and_typ_coherence.rs (BEHAVIORAL via the public `keyhog_scanner::jwt::finding_metadata`, precomputed JWTs: HS256 real JWT → exact {jwt.alg,iss,sub,exp} len==4; logout+jwt → NO jwt.non_standard_typ key (coherence pin) len==2; alg=none → jwt.alg_none="true (unsigned token: RFC 7519 §6 risk)" len==3; + source pin with_capacity(8) + doc lists logout+jwt). 39th landing.
- µ-jwt-02: `analyze` (L186, pub(crate)) → jwt_segments (L147, 3 non-empty base64url parts, `MAX_JWT_SEGMENT_LEN=16*1024` anti-DoS) → decode_b64url (L257, URL_SAFE_NO_PAD, strips `=`) → `check_nesting_depth(&json, 15)` (L328, hand-rolled brace/bracket depth counter, string+escape aware, anti-stack-overflow) → serde JwtHeader/JwtPayload. alg absent → `"<missing>"` placeholder (LAW10 reporting-only). alg=="none" (ci) → AlgNone; else-if !is_known_alg → UnknownAlg (mutually exclusive). exp via `json_i64` (L245), expired = `SystemTime::now >= exp` (pushes Expired anomaly inside the map closure). `JwtPayload::take_aud` (L306) String→Some / Array→`join_audience_strings` (L315, comma-join string members) / else None.
- µ-jwt-03: `anomalies_to_metadata` (L67, pub(crate)) BTreeMap (no cap concept — tree); `is_known_alg` (L265, 14 algs: RS256/384/512 + HS256/384/512 + ES256/384/512 + ES256K + PS256/384/512 + EdDSA — Iter48-fold: was mis-COUNTED as 13, grep `"…"`==14) + `is_standard_typ` (L286, 5 typs) are HARDCODED registries (Tier-B-migration candidates, CAPTURED — RFC 7518/7519 algorithm + media-type registries). `is_base64url_byte` (L253) the shared char-class (alnum|-|_|=). `looks_like_jwt` (L143, cfg test) = jwt_segments().is_some(). `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` module-level.

## µ-ptrie — prefix_trie.rs (read in full, Iter 49)
Prefix-superstring propagation table for the phase-1 AC prefilter: given N literal detector prefixes, entry `i` = the indices whose prefix is a strict SUPERSTRING of `prefixes[i]` (so a broad prefix cheaply activates its more-specific children). O(N·L) trie build vs O(N²) pairwise.
- µ-ptrie-01 (LANDED Iter 49, byte-identical Law-7 alloc — move-on-last): `collect_propagation` (L51) assigned `propagation[idx] = descendant_indices.clone()` for EVERY pattern index ending at a node, but `descendant_indices` is unused after that loop (the fn returns `subtree_indices`). Switched to `split_last()`: clone for all-but-last, MOVE `descendant_indices` into the last slot. In a real prefix trie almost every node holds exactly ONE pattern → the common path now does ZERO clones (was 1 Vec clone/node over ~900 detector prefixes at build). Byte-identical (propagation[idx] content identical, same assignment order). Test tests/gap/prefix_trie_propagation_move_on_last.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW plain `pub fn build_propagation_table_for_test`: LINEAR chains for deterministic descendant order — ["a","ab","abc"]→[[1,2],[2],[]] (single-pattern move path), ["a","a","ab"]→[[2],[2],[]] (dup-prefix node = clone idx0 + move idx1), ["foo","bar"]→[[],[]] disjoint). 40th landing.
- µ-ptrie-02: `build_propagation_table` (L36, pub(crate)) builds the trie (`TrieNode{children: HashMap<char,TrieNode>, pattern_indices: Vec<usize>}`) then `vec![Vec::new(); prefixes.len()]` + collect_propagation. NOTE: `children.values()` HashMap iteration is NON-DETERMINISTIC, so a node's descendant ORDER varies per run — the propagation lists are sets-in-arbitrary-order (callers treat them as sets; tests must use linear chains or sort). CAPTURED (a BTreeMap would make it deterministic but changes perf/behavior — not byte-identical). The doc-comment doctest (L30) references `keyhog_scanner::testing::build_propagation_table` but the fn is pub(crate) in a private mod so rustdoc never runs it (dead doctest — CAPTURED, harmless). Doc also has a DUPLICATE summary line (L23 "Build a propagation table using a trie." + L26 "Build a prefix propagation table…") — minor.

## µ-aregex — anchored_regex.rs (read in full, Iter 49)
Lazily-compiled anchored copies of a detector regex for the verifier's whole-chunk-equivalent left-context match.
- µ-aregex-01: `AnchoredRegex` (L7, pub(crate)) = `{src: Arc<str>, case_insensitive: bool, cell: OnceLock<Option<Arc<Regex>>>, left_context_cell: OnceLock<…>}`. `get` (L24) lazily compiles with prefix `\A(?:` + suffix `)`; `get_with_left_context` (L30) with `\A(?s:.)(?:` + `)` (one leading char of left context). Both delegate to the one-owner `compile` (L36, format the anchored src + RegexBuilder with REGEX_SIZE_LIMIT_BYTES + regex_dfa_limit + case_insensitive). LOUD degrade: compile failure → `tracing::error!` "anchored-regex failed to compile; pattern keeps the whole-chunk path" → None (recall-safe, the whole-chunk path still runs). CURIOSITY (CAPTURED, not touched — not byte-identical): L42 `.crlf(self.case_insensitive)` ties CRLF mode to the case-insensitive flag, which look semantically unrelated; could be intentional (the two are set together for the same detector class) or a latent coupling — needs a behavioral oracle, not a blind flip. Already minimal; no byte-identical fix.

## µ-mpri — resolution.rs (read in full, Iter 50)
Match resolution: when multiple detectors hit the same credential region, keep only the most specific / highest-confidence match; suppress entropy findings near a named detector on the same line; suppress matches nested inside a private-key block. pub fns `resolve_matches` (L23, panics on rule error) + `try_resolve_matches` (L36). Priority weights are all named consts: ENTROPY_MATCH_PRIORITY=0.0, NAMED_DETECTOR_PRIORITY=10.0, CONFIDENCE_WEIGHT=5.0, DETECTOR_ID_LENGTH_WEIGHT=0.1, MAX_CREDENTIAL_PRIORITY_LENGTH=200, CREDENTIAL_LENGTH_WEIGHT=0.01; ADJACENT_LINE_DISTANCE=2, SINGLE_MATCH_COUNT=1, PRIORITY_EPSILON=1e-9. (Iter 51 line refs reconciled post-Iter-50 const insert.)
- µ-mpri-01 (LANDED Iter 50, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): `match_priority` (L211) had a bare `priority += 5.0;` (now L239 `priority += KNOWN_PREFIX_SERVICE_BONUS;`) — the lone un-named weight in a fn where EVERY other component already used a named const. Extracted `const KNOWN_PREFIX_SERVICE_BONUS: f64 = 5.0;` (next to CREDENTIAL_LENGTH_WEIGHT) and use it. Byte-identical (same f64 value). Made `match_priority` `pub(crate)` so the testing facade can reach it. The bonus fires iff `known_prefix_confidence_floor(&credential).is_some() && is_service_anchored_detector(detector_id)`. Test tests/gap/resolution_known_prefix_service_bonus_named.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW plain `pub fn match_priority_for_test(detector_id, credential, confidence)` building a RawMatch): two matches IDENTICAL in detector_id="aws-access-key"/conf=0.5/len=16 differing only in known prefix ("AKIArxq7n2mk4p8w" vs "Wprxq7n2mk4p8w01") differ in priority by EXACTLY 5.0 (every other weighted term cancels in the diff — robust, decoupled from the other consts' values); SECOND pair pins the `&&` guard: with a generic detector ("generic-password", not service-anchored) the known prefix adds EXACTLY 0.0. 41st landing.
- µ-mpri-02 (LANDED Iter 51, byte-identical Vector-7 DEDUP one-owner — CORRECTS my Iter-50 over-claim, caught by the Iter-51 Gemini review): `is_service_specific_detector` (L155) was `!is_entropy_detector(id) && !is_generic_detector(id)` where the LOCAL `is_generic_detector` wrapper = `detector_ids::is_generic_or_private_key_detector` = `generic || private_key_fallback`. So it expanded to `!entropy && !(generic || pk_fallback)` = `!entropy && !generic && !pk_fallback` — ALGEBRAICALLY IDENTICAL to `detector_ids::is_service_anchored_detector` = `!generic && !entropy && !pk_fallback` (pk_fallback == the single id "private-key", NOT a broader block set — my Iter-50 "NEAR-twins but NOT identical" was WRONG; the private-key excluded by BOTH is the same `is_private_key_fallback` id). FIX: `is_service_specific_detector` now delegates to the canonical `is_service_anchored_detector` (one owner, drift hazard removed); deleted the now-dead local `is_entropy_detector` + `is_generic_detector` wrappers AND the now-orphaned `detector_ids::is_generic_or_private_key_detector` (its ONLY caller was that wrapper — confirmed zero refs by grep, deletion fixed a dead_code warning). Byte-identical (predicate output identical for all detector_ids; deleting unused fns is unobservable). Made `is_service_specific_detector` `pub(crate)` for the facade. Test tests/gap/resolution_service_detector_predicate_dedup.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `pub fn is_service_specific_detector_for_test` + `is_service_anchored_detector_for_test`): pins the EXACT truth table {aws-access-key:T, stripe-secret-key:T, generic-password:F, entropy-token:F, entropy:F, private-key:F} AND that the two predicates agree on every case. 42nd landing.
- µ-mpri-03 (Law-7 candidate, CAPTURED not landed; count corrected by the Iter-51 Gemini review): `suppress_matches_nested_in_private_key_blocks` (L45) is actually THREE passes over `matches` / FOUR Vec allocs, not two: (pass 1, L48-58) build `private_key_spans` = `.collect::<Result<Vec<_>,_>>()?` (intermediate Vec of &RawMatch, alloc 1) `.into_iter().filter_map(match_span).collect()` (the spans Vec, alloc 2); (pass 2, L63-82) build `retain: Vec<bool>` (alloc 3, fallible — `is_private_key_block_detector` returns Result, `?` propagates); (pass 3, L83-88) drain `matches` into `retained: Vec` (alloc 4) filtered by the bools. KEY nuance I'd omitted: L59-61 early-returns `Ok(())` when `private_key_spans.is_empty()`, so the COMMON no-private-key-block case runs ONLY pass 1 (+allocs 1-2) then returns — the 2-extra-pass cost is rare (only when a private-key block coexists with other matches). Can't collapse to `Vec::retain` (predicate is fallible). NOT byte-identical to rewrite — left as-is; low priority given the early return.
- µ-mpri-04: `resolve_match_groups` (L159) groups by (file_path, line) via HashMap then for each group keeps `best_matches_for_group` (L192): sorts by `match_priority` desc, keeps all within PRIORITY_EPSILON of the top. `suppress_entropy_matches_near_named_detectors` (L100) drops entropy matches within ADJACENT_LINE_DISTANCE lines of a named (service-specific) detector. CAPTURED — already minimal.

## µ-frcache — fragment_cache.rs (read in full, Iter 52)
Cross-chunk fragment cache: reassembles secrets split across chunk seams / large-file windows. SHARD_COUNT=64, MAX_FRAGMENTS_PER_SCOPE=8. All credential text in `Zeroizing<String>` (scrubbed on evict/drop); custom Debug redacts `value` as `<redacted N bytes>`.
- µ-frcache-01 (LANDED Iter 52, byte-identical Law-7 single-alloc + secret-residue hardening): the glued `joined` in BOTH reassembly loops (record_and_reassemble + record_and_reassemble_stamped) was `Zeroizing::new(String::new())` then two `push_str(f1.value)`/`push_str(f2.value)`. Growing 0→f1→f1+f2 forces an intermediate REALLOC that copies f1.value's plaintext to a fresh buffer and frees the old one WITHOUT zeroing — a stray secret-fragment copy left on the heap, defeating the module's whole Zeroizing intent. Changed both to `Zeroizing::new(String::with_capacity(f1.value.len() + f2.value.len()))`: exactly ONE allocation, no intermediate realloc → no un-zeroed plaintext copy, and zero grow-realloc cost in the O(n²) ≤56-join loop. Byte-identical (capacity is unobservable; glued content + sort order identical). Both target lines were textually identical so one replace_all covered both fns (`f1`/`f2` in scope at each). Test tests/gap/fragment_cache_reassemble_join.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `pub fn fragment_reassemble_for_test`): two near same-path fragments ("AKIA1111"@l1, "AKIA2222"@l2, path a.env) → EXACTLY ["AKIA1111AKIA2222","AKIA2222AKIA1111"] (both i≠j orderings, sorted by glued bytes); a Δline=200 pair → EXACTLY [] (the near window is strictly `< 100`). 43rd landing.
- µ-frcache structure: `SecretFragment` (L27, pub(crate)) {pub prefix:String, var_name:String, value:Zeroizing<String>, line:usize, path:Option<Arc<str>>}. `ReassembledCandidate` (L59, cfg(any(simd,test))) {value, path, line} — anchor provenance is the PREFIX fragment f1 (fixes cross-file mis-attribution). `FragmentCache` (L109): `shards: [Mutex<LruCache<String,Vec<SecretFragment>>>; 64]`; `new(capacity)` (L116) per_shard=(capacity/64).max(1), NonZeroUsize floor MIN.
- µ-frcache-02: `record_and_reassemble` (L128, pub(crate)) → Vec<Zeroizing<String>>; `record_and_reassemble_stamped` (L218, cfg(any(simd,test)) attr at L217) → Vec<ReassembledCandidate>. Both: shard via `shard_index_of(prefix,scope)` (no key String), lock, `with_scoped_key` → `get_or_insert_mut_ref` (thread-local SCOPED_KEY_SCRATCH `prefix\0scope`, borrowed on hit, to_owned only on miss), dedup-push (path+line+value eq), `evict_one` if >8, then O(n²) i≠j pair loop with `near = same path && |Δline|<100`. Plain sorts joins by glued bytes; stamped stamps f1's path/line and sorts by (bytes, line). CAPTURED DEDUP candidate: the two fns are near-twins (differ only in return type + sort key + cfg gate; NOT byte-identical to merge); the dedup-push closure (`.any(|f| {…}` path+line+value, L143 + L232) is duplicated (small one-owner helper candidate). [Iter-53 review: L218/L232 line refs reconciled post-Iter-52 with_capacity 1→2-line split.]
- µ-frcache-03: `evict_one` (L93) removes the `min_by_key` of `fragment_eviction_key=(line, value.as_bytes())` — content/position-derived, so deterministic under rayon (Law-7 determinism; ≡ old remove(0)/"drop oldest" in a sequential scan). `with_scoped_key` (L318); `shard_fold` (L333) is the single hash recurrence `h*31+b` (`wrapping_mul(31)` at L334); `shard_index_of` (L342) folds prefix,0,scope %64 with NO String; `shard_index_drift_probe` (L363, #[doc(hidden)] pub(crate)) returns (slice_shard, joined_shard) for the equivalence invariant (drift ⇒ a fragment recorded under one shard is unfindable under the other → silent reassembly break). [Iter-53 review: +2 line shift folded; Gemini's "shard_fold L334" was an off-by-one — grep proves the fn header is L333.]

## µ-deadln — deadline.rs (read in full, Iter 52)
Cooperative scan-deadline gates (no background timer; checked on a cadence). `expired(Option<Instant>)` (L4) = `deadline.is_some_and(|deadline| Instant::now() >= deadline)`. `LoopDeadline` (Clone+Copy) {start:Instant, budget:Duration}: `from_deadline` budget = checked_duration_since(now) or Duration::ZERO; `expired` = `budget.is_zero() || start.elapsed() >= budget`. [post-µ-deadln-01-landing the LoopDeadline/from_deadline/expired line refs shift +9 from the inserted cadence_tick fn; anchor by NAME.]
- µ-deadln-01 (LANDED Iter 53, byte-identical Vector-7 DEDUP one-owner — confirmed by the Iter-53 Gemini review): the cadence-gate `iteration > 0 && iteration.is_multiple_of(cadence)` was duplicated VERBATIM in `expired_on_cadence` and `loop_expired_on_cadence`. Extracted `pub(crate) fn cadence_tick(iteration, cadence) -> bool` (single owner); both wrappers now `cadence_tick(i,c) && <…expired…>`. Byte-identical (`&&` associativity preserves short-circuit + result). Test tests/gap/deadline_cadence_tick_dedup.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `pub fn cadence_tick_for_test` + `expired_on_cadence_now_for_test` + `loop_expired_on_cadence_now_for_test`): pins the EXACT 9-row truth table {(0,3)F,(1,3)F,(2,3)F,(3,3)T,(6,3)T,(4,2)T,(5,2)F,(1,1)T,(0,1)F} for the helper AND — driving each wrapper with an already-reached deadline (`Some(Instant::now())` → expired/zero-budget always true) — that BOTH wrappers' results equal the gate, so neither can drift from cadence_tick. 44th landing. [Iter-53 review also: cfg-shorthand `cfg(any(simd,test))` flagged by Gemini is intentional abbreviation of `cfg(any(feature="simd",test))` used across all µ-blocks — REJECTED as non-error.]

## µ-cksumgl — checksum/gitlab.rs (read in full, Iter 54) + µ-cksumnpm/µ-cksumcat/µ-cksumdeg captures
`GitlabTokenValidator` (L19+, pub(crate)) — STRUCTURAL-only GitLab PAT checks (never recomputes the routable CRC, so it returns StructurallyValid not Valid, and must NOT false-`Invalid` a plausible-length modern token or it drops every `glpat-`/`glrt-`/`glcbt-` routable token → the atlantis-credentials / gitlab-personal-access-token contract regressions). Bounds: GITLAB_BODY_MIN=20 (classic PAT body), GITLAB_BODY_MAX=64 (generous ceiling). `gitlab_body_charset_ok` (L31) = all chars in base64url ∪ {`.`} (the routable body↔CRC-suffix separator; classic regex forbids `.` so admitting it can't make a classic token Valid). `validate` (L44): `glpat-` body → charset-fail Invalid; len 20..=64 StructurallyValid; <20 Invalid (fabricated/truncated); else NotApplicable. `glcbt-`/`glrt-` body → charset-fail Invalid; len 16..=64 StructurallyValid; <16 Invalid; else NotApplicable. NotApplicable if no gitlab prefix.
- µ-cksumgl-01 (LANDED Iter 54, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): the routable-token arm used a BARE `16` at TWO sites (`16..=GITLAB_BODY_MAX` band + `n if n < 16` too-short guard) — the lone un-named bound in a module that already names GITLAB_BODY_MIN/GITLAB_BODY_MAX. Extracted `const GITLAB_ROUTABLE_BODY_MIN: usize = 16;` and use it at both sites. Byte-identical (16 == the const) AND removes a two-site drift hazard (the band floor and the guard must agree). Test tests/gap/gitlab_checksum_body_length_floors.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `pub fn gitlab_checksum_verdict_for_test` → "valid"/"structurally-valid"/"invalid"/"not-applicable"): pins EXACT verdicts — glpat-+20 SV / +19 Invalid (classic floor), glrt-+16 SV / +15 Invalid + glcbt-+16 SV (routable floor = the named 16), glpat-+64 SV / +65 NotApplicable (ceiling), glrt-+(15+'!') Invalid (charset gate fires before length), ghp_… NotApplicable. 45th landing.
- µ-cksumnpm — checksum/npm.rs (read in full): `NpmTokenValidator` (L10) `npm_`+36 (=30 entropy+6 base62-CRC32): len!=NPM_BODY_LEN NotApplicable; non-alnum Invalid; `expected = base62_encode_u32(crc32(entropy.as_bytes()), NPM_CHECKSUM_LEN)` (both imported from super::github) → Valid iff matches else Invalid. `PypiTokenValidator` (L41) `pypi-`+macaroon: <20 Invalid; `decode_pypi_payload` → Ok(≥32 bytes) Valid / Ok(<32) Invalid / Err Invalid (LAW10 fail-closed). `decode_pypi_payload` (L60) sniffs url-safe vs standard vs padding bytes, rejects mixed-alphabet, picks the right base64 engine.
  - µ-cksumnpm-01 (LANDED Iter 55, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const + coherence): the npm body layout was bare `36`/`[..30]`/`[30..]`/`, 6)` magic literals. Named `const NPM_ENTROPY_LEN=30; NPM_CHECKSUM_LEN=6; NPM_BODY_LEN = NPM_ENTROPY_LEN + NPM_CHECKSUM_LEN;` (36 DERIVED so the length gate and the slice boundary can't disagree) and use at all 4 sites. Byte-identical (same values). Also widened `checksum::github::{crc32, base62_encode_u32}` pub(super)→pub(crate) (benign reach widen for the test facade; both stay used by production paths — no warning). Test tests/gap/npm_checksum_token_structure.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `pub fn npm_checksum_verdict_for_test` + `npm_expected_checksum_for_test`): builds a GENUINELY-valid token (entropy="a"*30 + the real 6-char base62-CRC32) → "valid" (pins the 30/6 split through the Valid path), one flipped checksum char → "invalid", 35/37-char body → "not-applicable" (pins NPM_BODY_LEN=36), 36-char body w/ '!' → "invalid" (charset), ghp_… → "not-applicable". 46th landing.
- µ-cksumcat — detector_catalog.rs (read in full): `bundled_detector_ids()` (L6, pub(crate)) OnceLock<Result<HashSet<String>,String>> from load_embedded_detectors_or_fail → map ids → HashSet (collect already reserves via the map iterator size_hint, so NO with_capacity win). `validate_rule_detector_ids(rule_name, detector_ids: impl IntoIterator<Item=&str>, valid_detector_ids: &HashSet<String>)` (L23) errors on first id not in the set (`.contains(&str)` via Borrow<str>, no String alloc). No byte-identical landing — both clean. Good behavioral-test target (validate → Ok / "rule references unknown detector 'X'").
- µ-cksumdeg — prefilter_degrade.rs (read in full): `warn_prefilter_disabled(site, err: &dyn Display)` (L29, pub(crate)) emits ONE loud `keyhog: PREFILTER DISABLED [site]: …` stderr line from a failing static-prefilter LazyLock init (Law-10 loud-but-recall-preserving; LazyLock runs once → fires once/process). Single eprintln, constant format — no landing.

## µ-cksumslk — checksum/slack.rs (read in full, Iter 56) + µ-sintern capture
`SlackTokenValidator` (L10, pub(crate)) — STRUCTURAL-only Slack-token gate (no public checksum algo; strict regex shape match, routed through `checksum_adjusted_confidence` where `ChecksumResult::Invalid` DROPS the finding). Two compile-time-constant LazyLock<Option<Regex>>: SLACK_BOT_RE = `^xoxb-[0-9]{10,15}(?:-[0-9]{10,15})?-[a-zA-Z0-9]{15,40}$` (the optional 2nd numeric segment is the contract-load-bearing fix — accepts BOTH the 3-segment canonical AND the older 2-segment `xoxb-{digits}-{secret}` shape; a prior mandatory-2nd-segment regex false-dropped every 2-segment bot token), SLACK_USER_RE = `^xoxp-[0-9]{10,15}-[0-9]{10,15}(?:-[0-9]{10,13})?-[a-zA-Z0-9]{24,40}$`. `warm_runtime_regexes` (L54+) forces both LazyLock inits. `slack_bot_match`/`slack_user_match` → Option<bool> (None = regex never compiled = NotApplicable, NOT Invalid — a missing-regex must not DROP real tokens on infra failure). `validate`: xoxb-→bot, xoxp-→user, else NotApplicable; Some(true)→Valid, Some(false)→Invalid, None→NotApplicable.
- µ-cksumslk-01 (LANDED Iter 56, byte-identical DEDUP one-owner): SLACK_BOT_RE + SLACK_USER_RE were two LazyLock blocks with a STRUCTURALLY IDENTICAL `match regex::Regex::new(pat) { Ok(re)=>Some(re), Err(error)=>{ warn_prefilter_disabled(site,&error); None } }` body (differ only in pattern + site-label). Extracted `fn compile_slack_re(pattern: &str, site: &str) -> Option<regex::Regex>` single owner; both LazyLocks now `compile_slack_re(PAT, "…(SLACK_*_RE)")`. Byte-identical (patterns + site labels passed verbatim; same compile + warn + Option). Test tests/gap/slack_checksum_token_shapes.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `pub fn slack_checksum_verdict_for_test`): bot 3-seg `xoxb-1234567890-0987654321-abcdefghijklmnop` → "valid", bot 2-seg `xoxb-1234567890-abcdefghijklmnop` → "valid" (the both-shapes contract), bot `xoxb-12345-…` (5-digit 1st seg) → "invalid", user 3-seg → "valid" / user missing-2nd-numeric → "invalid", ghp_… → "not-applicable". 47th landing.
- µ-sintern — static_intern.rs (read in full): frozen detector-metadata interner. `SEED_SOURCE_TYPES` (L41, pub(crate) const, 17 entries: filesystem/git/git/head/git/history/git/tag/git/unreachable/git/diff/git/staged/git-diff/git-history/stdin/s3/docker/web/github/slack/binary). `StaticInterner` {arena: Vec<Arc<str>>, index: HashMap<Arc<str>,u32,ahash::RandomState>} (L70, pub(crate)). `from_detector_strings` (L79): BTreeSet dedup (stable arena order for index-keyed caches) + SEED, `arena = all.iter().map(Arc::from).collect()` (BTreeSet iter size_hint → collect reserves, NO with_capacity win), `index = HashMap::with_capacity_and_hasher(arena.len(), ahash)` (already reserved). `lookup` (L116, #[inline]): index.get(s)? → arena.get(idx).cloned() (Arc<str>:Borrow<str> → alloc-free hit). `len`/`seed_source_type_count` cfg(test). No byte-identical Law-7 landing (collects already reserve).

## µ-cksumgh — checksum/github.rs (read in full, Iter 58) + µ-adjstage capture
The CRC32/base62 checksum PRIMITIVES (shared fleet-wide): `crc32` (L6, pub(crate)) standard CRC32 with a const-block-built `TABLE: [u32;256]` (const-evaluated via a `while` loop inside `const TABLE = { … }`, NOT a `const fn`; poly 0xEDB88320, init/final 0xFFFFFFFF); `base62_encode_u32(value, width)` (L35+, pub(crate)) left-pads with '0' to `width`, `Vec::with_capacity(width.max(6))`, BASE62_DIGITS 0-9A-Za-z. (Both widened pub(super)→pub(crate) in Iter 55 for the npm test facade.) Two validators: `GithubClassicPatValidator` (L55+) `ghp_`+30 entropy+6 base62-CRC32 (CRC over the 30 entropy only); `GithubFineGrainedPatValidator` (L90+) `github_pat_`+22 alnum+`_`+59 alnum.
- µ-cksumgh-01 (LANDED Iter 58, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const + cross-validator DEDUP): named `const CHECKSUM_LEN = 6` (the 6-char base62 CRC32 checksum width — SINGLE OWNER shared by BOTH validators, was bare `6` at 4 sites: classic L74 `,6`, fine-grained try_payload L100/L101 `len()-6`, L102 `,6`; plus `<7` floor → `< CHECKSUM_LEN + 1`), `const GITHUB_CLASSIC_ENTROPY_LEN = 30`, `const GITHUB_CLASSIC_BODY_LEN = ENTROPY + CHECKSUM_LEN` (36 DERIVED; was bare `36` at classic L63 `>36`→Invalid + L66 `!=36`→NotApplicable, `30` at L72/L73 slices). All byte-identical (same values). Test tests/gap/github_checksum_token_structure.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `github_classic_checksum_verdict_for_test` + `github_fine_grained_checksum_verdict_for_test`, reusing the Iter-55 `npm_expected_checksum_for_test` = the SAME base62(crc32,6)): classic ghp_+30+real-checksum → "valid", flipped → "invalid", ghp_+37 → "invalid" (>36) vs ghp_+35 → "not-applicable" (<36, the verdict SPLIT), '!'-body → "invalid", glpat- → "not-applicable"; fine-grained github_pat_+22_`{53 entropy+6 checksum}` (right-segment CRC) → "valid", 21-char left → "invalid", ghp_ → "not-applicable". 49th landing.
- µ-cksumgh-02 (LANDED Iter 59, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): `GithubFineGrainedPatValidator` segment lengths were bare `22`/`59` at the length check. Named `const GITHUB_FINE_GRAINED_LEFT_LEN = 22` + `GITHUB_FINE_GRAINED_RIGHT_LEN = 59` (L13/L14) and use at the check (now L138). Byte-identical (values unchanged). Test (extended tests/gap/github_checksum_token_structure.rs): a 23-char left → "invalid", a 58-char right → "invalid" (pins the named boundaries; the valid 22/59 already covered by fine_grained_right_segment_checksum_validates). 50th landing. `split_fine_grained_payload` (now L122) split_once('_'), rejects a 2nd '_'. `try_payload(s)` (assoc fn) validates the SINGLE string `s` it is given — CRC over `s[..len-CHECKSUM_LEN]` vs its trailing CHECKSUM_LEN chars — returning `ChecksumResult::Valid`/`Invalid` (no Option). It is `validate` (L130+) that orchestrates the two tries: `try_payload(payload) == Valid` then `try_payload(right) == Valid` (the whole payload, then the right segment). [Iter-61 review: this corrected an earlier over-claim that try_payload itself tried both / returned Some(Valid).] [Iter-59 review: µ-cksumgh-01's "was bare 6 at L74/L100/L101/L102" + classic L63/L66/L72/L73 are PRE-landing line refs; current state uses CHECKSUM_LEN at L85/L108/L111/L112/L113 and the classic body consts at L74/L77/L83/L84 — anchor by NAME. crc32 L17, base62_encode_u32 L46, GithubClassicPatValidator L66, GithubFineGrainedPatValidator L104.]
- µ-adjstage — adjudicate/stage.rs (read in full): pure adjudication enums. `StageId` (L4+, pub(crate)) 19 variants incl 3 data-carrying (ShapeGate(&'static str), GenericValueShape(GenericValueShapeStage), EntropyValueShape(EntropyShapeStage)); `StageId::as_str` (L27, const fn) exhaustive match → snake_case strs (ShapeGate/GenericValueShape/EntropyValueShape delegate to the carried reason/stage). `StageOutcome` {Pass, Suppress(StageId)}. `Verdict` {Suppressed(StageId), Reported(Option<f64>)} + `suppressed_stage` (L65, const fn). No magic-const/alloc/dup → NO landing (pure type defs); good behavioral-test target (as_str truth table) for a future iter.

## µ-gkwown — generic_keyword_owner.rs (read in full, Iter 60)
Named-detector ownership for generic assignment-key anchors: the broad generic `KEY=value` bridge must NOT second-guess service-specific assignment names already owned by a loaded named detector (`aws_secret_access_key`, `segment_write_key`). `pub(crate) mod generic_keyword_owner` (lib.rs L99). Imports `is_generic_detector` (detector_ids), `normalize_assignment_keyword`+`normalized_assignment_keyword_has_secret_suffix` (engine::phase2_generic::keywords). Owned set is built once at scanner build as a SORTED `Vec<Arc<str>>` (BTreeSet→into_iter→Arc::from preserves order) so the lookup can `binary_search`.
- build_generic_named_assignment_keywords (L23, pub(crate)): for each DetectorSpec, skip if `is_generic_detector(&id)` (id starts with GENERIC_PREFIX) OR `service == "generic"`; normalize the service (skip on None); skip if `service.len() < MIN_SERVICE_NAME_LEN`; for each keyword: normalize (skip None), require `normalized_assignment_keyword_has_secret_suffix` (rsplit('_').next() ∈ {key,secret,token,password,passwd,pwd} OR ends_with key/secret/token/password), require `normalized.contains(service)`, then `owned.insert(normalized)`. Returns `owned.into_iter().map(Arc::from).collect()` (sorted).
- assignment_keyword_owned_by_named_detector (L49, pub(crate)): empty-guard → false; normalize → has_secret_suffix → `normalized_assignment_keyword_owned_by_named_detector` (binary_search).
- line_assignment_owned_by_named_detector (L65): empty-guard → false; `entropy::keywords::assignment_keyword_for_line(line)` then binary_search.
- candidate_embeds_owned_assignment_key (L79): empty-guard; `leading_assignment_key` (None → `candidate_starts_with_owned_assignment_key`); else owned-by-named OR starts-with.
- entropy_candidate_owned_by_named_assignment (L93): candidate_embeds OR same_line line_assignment.
- keyword_span_owned_by_named_detector (L103): bounds (start>end || end>len → false); direct slice owned; else widen [start,end] over `is_assignment_key_byte` and re-check only if widened.
- private: `normalized_assignment_keyword_owned_by_named_detector` (L129, binary_search_by cmp) — REQUIRES sorted owned (invariant held by build). `leading_assignment_key` (L138) scans is_assignment_key_byte then `matches!(bytes[end], b'=' | b':' | b'~').then_some`. `candidate_starts_with_owned_assignment_key` (L150) LINEAR `.iter().any` prefix-match (`normalized.len() > owned.len() && starts_with(owned) && has_secret_suffix(owned)`) — prefix-range, NOT binary-searchable; inherent. `is_assignment_key_byte` (L164) `is_ascii_alphanumeric() || matches!(byte, b'_'|b'-'|b'.')` — single owner of the key-byte class WITHIN THIS MODULE, used at L112/L116/L135.
- µ-gkwown-CAPTURE (Iter 63 review, NOT landed — Law-1 regret risk): `engine/scan_filters.rs::is_provider_token_byte` (private, ~L400) has a BYTE-IDENTICAL body (`byte.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || matches!(byte, b'_'|b'-'|b'.')`) to `is_assignment_key_byte`. COINCIDENTAL identity, NOT semantic: one names assignment-key chars (KEY=value anchors), the other provider-token chars (engine token-boundary widening); a future token format accepting `+`/`/` would force a re-split. A 3rd nearby predicate at `suppression/shape/public.rs:166` is DIFFERENT (negated + also `:`/`/`/`=`) so NOT part of any merge. Deferring a cross-module consolidation to a deliberate decision about the right shared owner+name (e.g. an `is_identifier_token_byte` in a shared char-class module) rather than a blind 1-min merge that couples two independent predicates.
- µ-gkwown-01 (LANDED Iter 60, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): the service-name floor was a bare `3` at L25 (`if service.len() < 3`). Named `const MIN_SERVICE_NAME_LEN: usize = 3` (the floor below which a service marker — `ci`/`db`/`io` — is too generic to claim ownership) and use at the gate. Byte-identical (value unchanged). Test tests/gap/generic_keyword_owner_service_name_floor.rs (BEHAVIORAL via NEW `generic_named_owned_keywords_for_test(service, keywords)` facade building one synthetic DetectorSpec): service "aws"+`["aws_secret_access_key"]` → owned == `["aws_secret_access_key"]`; the floor is EXACTLY 3 — service "ab"+`["ab_secret_key"]` → `[]` (empty) vs service "abc"+`["abc_secret_key"]` → `["abc_secret_key"]` (same anchor shape, only length differs); "segment"+`["segment_write_key","segment"]` → `["segment_write_key"]` (bare "segment" lacks a secret suffix → not owned). 51st landing.

## µ-tier — hw_probe/tier.rs (read in full, Iter 62)
GPU performance-tier classifier + per-tier threshold lookups. PURE-FN (module doc: "unit-testable without a real GPU") — NOT cfg-gated, compiles+runs under ci-lean (no gpu feature). `mod tier` is PRIVATE in hw_probe/mod.rs (L24) but `pub use tier::{gpu_routing_profile, gpu_routing_profiles, GpuRoutingProfile}` (hw_probe/mod.rs L34) re-exports the table API at `keyhog_scanner::hw_probe::`; `classify_gpu_tier`+`GpuTier` are ALSO surfaced via the `hw_probe::testing` wrapper module (from_inner/to_inner). `GpuTier` (pub(crate) enum, 3 variants: High/Mid/Low — High keeps a higher heuristic floor until calibration, Low = most conservative for unknown/iGPU).
- classify_gpu_tier (pub(crate), #[must_use]): None → Low; else `to_ascii_lowercase()` then ORDERED substring scan — High wins first (contains any of: "rtx 40","rtx 50","a100","h100","h200","rx 7900 xtx","rx 7900 xt","m{1,2,3,4} max","m{1,2,3,4} ultra"), then Mid (contains any of: "rtx 20","rtx 30","gtx 16","arc","rx 6","rx 7","apple m{1,2,3,4}","m{1,2,3,4} pro"), else Low. So "RTX 4090"→High, "Apple M1 Max"→High (m1 max matched before mid's apple m1), "RTX 3080"→Mid, "Intel Arc A770"→Mid (arc), "Intel UHD Graphics"→Low, None→Low.
- gpu_min_bytes_for_tier / gpu_solo_bytes_for_tier / gpu_pattern_breakeven_for_tier (each pub(crate), #[must_use]): match tier → thresholds:: const. min_bytes: High GPU_MIN_BYTES_HIGH_TIER=128 MiB, Mid GPU_MIN_BYTES_MID_TIER=256 MiB, Low GPU_MIN_BYTES=512 MiB (Low has the HIGHEST floor — most conservative). solo High GPU_BYTES_BREAKEVEN_SOLO_HIGH_TIER=256 MiB. pattern High GPU_PATTERN_BREAKEVEN_HIGH_TIER=100.
- GpuRoutingProfile (pub struct): {tier:&'static str, description:&'static str, min_bytes:u64, solo_bytes:u64, pattern_breakeven:usize}. gpu_routing_profile(adapter) (pub, #[must_use]) = profile_for_tier(classify_gpu_tier(adapter)). gpu_routing_profiles() (pub, #[must_use]) → array [High, Mid, Low] profiles in that order. profile_for_tier (private) maps tier→(label,description): High ("high","RTX 40/50, A100/H100, M-Max"), Mid ("mid","RTX 20/30, GTX 16, Arc, M-Pro/base"), Low ("low","low / unknown") + the 3 threshold lookups.
- µ-tier-01 (LANDED Iter 62, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): `gpu_routing_profiles` return type was a bare `[GpuRoutingProfile; 3]`. Named `const GPU_TIER_COUNT: usize = 3` (the GpuTier-variant count = the table width) and used it in the return type `[GpuRoutingProfile; GPU_TIER_COUNT]`. Byte-identical: 3==3, the array literal still enumerates the 3 explicit tiers. Test tests/gap/gpu_routing_profile_tier_table.rs (BEHAVIORAL via the already-pub `keyhog_scanner::hw_probe::{gpu_routing_profile, gpu_routing_profiles}` — no facade needed): profiles len==3 (pins GPU_TIER_COUNT) + ordering [0]"high"/[1]"mid"/[2]"low"; per-tier thresholds high min 128 MiB/solo 256 MiB/pattern 100, mid min 256 MiB, low min 512 MiB; classifier "RTX 4090"→high, "Apple M1 Max"→high, "RTX 3080"→mid, "Intel Arc A770"→mid, "Intel UHD Graphics"→low, None→low. 52nd landing.

## µ-thresh — hw_probe/thresholds.rs (read in full, Iter 64)
Routing crossover threshold consts (pure data, NOT cfg-gated, ci-lean). `pub(crate) mod thresholds` (hw_probe/mod.rs L26). The tier lookup fns (gpu_min_bytes_for_tier etc., in tier.rs) consume these; CLI/reporting consumes the FNS, never the raw consts (module doc). Tier→threshold map is deliberately conservative (last RTX 5090 sweep 2026-06-19 did not beat CPU/SIMD through 64 MiB, so heuristic GPU routing starts beyond that). 9 consts:
- min_bytes (total-buffer floor before GPU dispatch): GPU_MIN_BYTES=512 MiB (Low/conservative default), GPU_MIN_BYTES_MID_TIER=256 MiB, GPU_MIN_BYTES_HIGH_TIER=128 MiB. Lower tier-number = lower floor (faster GPU engages sooner). Low has the HIGHEST floor.
- pattern_breakeven (pattern count above which GPU literal-matching wins regardless of size): GPU_PATTERN_BREAKEVEN=2_000 (Low default), GPU_PATTERN_BREAKEVEN_HIGH_TIER=100, GPU_PATTERN_BREAKEVEN_MID_TIER=500.
- solo (single-file size justifying GPU at low pattern counts): GPU_BYTES_BREAKEVEN_SOLO=1024 MiB=1 GiB (Low default), GPU_BYTES_BREAKEVEN_SOLO_HIGH_TIER=256 MiB, GPU_BYTES_BREAKEVEN_SOLO_MID_TIER=512 MiB.
- µ-thresh-01 (LANDED Iter 64, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const + DEDUP one-owner): the `1024 * 1024` MiB multiplier was repeated inline across 6 byte-size consts (`512 * 1024 * 1024`, `256 * 1024 * 1024`, `128 * 1024 * 1024`, `1024 * 1024 * 1024`, `256 * 1024 * 1024`, `512 * 1024 * 1024`). Introduced `const MIB: u64 = 1024 * 1024;` (module-private, single owner — no pre-existing MIB/bytesize in the crate, grep-confirmed) and rewrote all 6: `512 * MIB`, `256 * MIB`, `128 * MIB`, `1024 * MIB` (=1 GiB), `256 * MIB`, `512 * MIB`. Byte-identical: each const-folds to the same u64 (e.g. 512*MIB = 512*1048576 = 536870912 = 512*1024*1024). The 3 pattern-breakeven consts (2000/100/500) are counts, untouched. Test tests/gap/gpu_threshold_mib_units.rs (BEHAVIORAL via already-pub `gpu_routing_profiles()` — the consts surface through the tier lookup fns): mid solo==512*MIB + pattern==500; low min==512*MIB + solo==1024*MIB (also asserted ==1024*1024*1024) + pattern==2000. This pins the 3 refactored consts the Iter-62 High-only table test did NOT cover (GPU_BYTES_BREAKEVEN_SOLO=1024*MIB, _SOLO_MID_TIER=512*MIB, GPU_MIN_BYTES=512*MIB) plus the 2 previously-untested Mid/Low pattern-breakevens. 53rd landing.

## µ-banner — hw_probe/banner.rs (read in full, Iter 66)
One-line startup-banner formatter. PURE-FN, ci-lean. Module doc: "Stable text shape is part of the CLI contract (parsed by the `keyhog backend` text-vs-JSON diffing tests)". Single `pub fn startup_banner(caps: &HardwareCaps, detector_count: usize, pattern_count: usize) -> String` (re-exported `pub use banner::startup_banner` at hw_probe/mod.rs L28; `HardwareCaps` is a `pub struct` in `pub mod hw_probe` so the integration-test-reachable path is `keyhog_scanner::hw_probe::HardwareCaps` directly — all 13 fields pub, NOT non_exhaustive; the non_exhaustive at mod.rs L38 is on ScanBackend. NB the `hw_probe::testing` module that ALSO re-exports them is `#[cfg(test)]` = in-crate unit tests only, INVISIBLE to integration tests, so a gap test must use the non-gated paths). Format: `"{cores} cores | {gpu} | SIMD: {simd} | {hs} | {det} detectors ({pat} patterns){uring}"`. Branches: gpu = `GPU: {name}` (gpu_name=Some, !software) | `GPU: {name} (software, ignored)` (software renderer — llvmpipe/lavapipe/swiftshader, select_backend rejects them) | `GPU: none` (None); simd = AVX-512 (has_avx512) ELSE AVX2 (has_avx2) ELSE NEON (has_neon) ELSE scalar — strict priority, avx512 wins over avx2; hs = Hyperscan (hyperscan_available) | AC; uring = ` io_uring` (leading space, io_uring_available) | empty. No magic-const/alloc/dedup → NO code landing (pure contract-string formatter).
- µ-banner-01 (LANDED Iter 66, BEHAVIORAL gap test — the test IS the deliverable; module has no byte-identical code fix): the only pre-existing startup_banner tests (tests/unit/gpu_cases/startup_banner_no_gpu + _software_gpu_ignored) assert WEAK substrings (`banner.contains("GPU: none")`) — the Testing-Contract-banned !is_empty/contains anti-pattern. Added tests/gap/startup_banner_exact_shape.rs (4 tests, EXACT full-string assert_eq via the already-reachable `hw_probe::testing::{startup_banner, HardwareCaps}` + a local `caps(...)` builder) pinning the WHOLE contract string across every branch: high-end (16 cores, RTX 4090, AVX-512, Hyperscan, io_uring) → "16 cores | GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 | SIMD: AVX-512 | Hyperscan | 42 detectors (1234 patterns) io_uring"; software-GPU+AVX2+AC+no-uring → "8 cores | GPU: llvmpipe (software, ignored) | SIMD: AVX2 | AC | 10 detectors (200 patterns)"; no-GPU+NEON → "4 cores | GPU: none | SIMD: NEON | AC | 5 detectors (50 patterns)"; scalar fallback → "2 cores | GPU: none | SIMD: scalar | AC | 1 detectors (1 patterns)". Covers all 4 SIMD tiers, all 3 GPU states, both HS engines, both uring states. 54th landing.

## µ-err — error.rs (read in full, Iter 68)
Scanner-engine error types. PURE type defs (thiserror), ci-lean. `pub(crate) mod error` (lib.rs L95) but `ScanError`+`Result` reach the crate root via `pub use api::*` (lib.rs L177) → integration tests use `keyhog_scanner::ScanError`. `#[derive(Debug, Error)] pub enum ScanError` — 6 variants, exact `#[error(...)]` Display text:
- RegexCompile {detector_id:String, index:usize, source:regex::Error} → "failed to compile regex for detector {detector_id} pattern {index}: {source}. Fix: correct the detector regex or capture group configuration".
- RegexSetCompile(#[from] regex::Error) → "failed to compile scanner regex set: {0}. Fix: simplify the detector regex set or remove the invalid pattern".
- AhoCorasick(#[from] aho_corasick::BuildError) → "failed to build Aho-Corasick literal matcher: {0}. Fix: check for empty or invalid detector keywords".
- Gpu(String) → "GPU scanner failure: {0}" (NO Fix:). Simd(String) → "SIMD scanner failure: {0}" (NO Fix:). Config(String) → "scanner configuration failure: {0}. Fix: correct the bundled scanner rules".
`pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ScanError>;`. 4 of 6 variants carry the engineering-standard "Fix:" guidance (all but Gpu/Simd, which wrap an already-actionable lower-layer message). No magic-const/alloc/dedup → NO code landing (pure thiserror enum).
- µ-err-01 (LANDED Iter 68, BEHAVIORAL gap test as deliverable — no byte-identical code fix in a pure type-def module): the only pre-existing ScanError tests (file_gate.rs error_happy/error_error_path, scanner_misc.rs scan_error_display_includes_detector_id) assert WEAK substrings (`to_string().contains("probe")`/`contains("d")`/`contains("demo")`) — none pin the full message OR the "Fix:" contract. Added tests/gap/scan_error_display_messages.rs (3 tests via the already-pub `keyhog_scanner::ScanError`): Gpu("probe failed")→EXACT "GPU scanner failure: probe failed"; Simd("init blew up")→EXACT "SIMD scanner failure: init blew up"; Config("rules file missing")→EXACT "scanner configuration failure: rules file missing. Fix: correct the bundled scanner rules" (pins the full Fix: contract); RegexCompile{detector_id:"aws-key",index:2,source:regex::Error::Syntax("oops")} → starts_with EXACT "failed to compile regex for detector aws-key pattern 2: " AND ends_with EXACT ". Fix: correct the detector regex or capture group configuration" (pins the source-independent template + Fix: suffix without depending on regex's internal source render). regex::Error::Syntax(String) is constructible here (mirrors the existing file_gate/scanner_misc tests). 55th landing.

## µ-scanloop — pipeline/scan_loop.rs (read in full, Iter 70)
Hex-context suppression predicate + entropy dispatch. CPU/portable, ci-lean. `pub(crate) use scan_loop::{is_within_hex_context, match_entropy}` (pipeline/mod.rs L14). Consts from types.rs: HEX_CONTEXT_RADIUS_CHARS=20, MIN_HEX_MATCH_LEN=16, MIN_HEX_DIGITS_IN_MATCH=16, MIN_HEX_CONTEXT_DIGITS=8, MAX_HEX_CONTEXT_SEPARATORS=4.
- is_within_hex_context (pub(crate)): true iff a match sits inside a hex-digest run (→ suppressed as a hash substring FP). Steps: valid_match_bounds (end>start + both char-boundaries) else false; matched=data[start..end], if len<MIN_HEX_MATCH_LEN(16) false (cheap reject FIRST — reordered before the hex-digit count to skip the walk on sub-16B AC matches); if !has_at_least_n_hex_digits(matched,16) false; then surrounding_hex_context (±20 chars, floor/ceil_char_boundary) → formatted_hex_run(before reversed)+formatted_hex_run(after); true iff hex_before≥8 AND hex_after≥8.
- has_at_least_n_hex_digits (private): early-exit — n==0→true; counts ascii hexdigits, returns true the instant seen≥n (no full walk).
- formatted_hex_run (private): counts leading hex digits, TOLERATES separators ' '/'\t'/':'/'-' up to MAX_HEX_CONTEXT_SEPARATORS(4) once a hex digit seen (so colon/dash-separated hex like a SHA-with-dashes still reads as a hex run); breaks on any other char.
- match_entropy (pub(crate)): cfg(entropy)→crate::entropy::shannon_entropy; cfg(not entropy)→phase2_entropy→crate::entropy::fast::shannon_entropy_scalar (no stale fork).
NO landing here — already EXTENSIVELY covered (testing.rs L914 `pub fn is_within_hex_context` facade + 8 a3_pipeline tests incl. hex_line_true `deadbeef-cafef00d-...` exercising separator tolerance, suppresses_sha256_hex_digest, named_detector_keeps_algolia_hex, short_match_false, prose_false) AND no magic-const/dedup (consts named, reorder-opt done). Captured; pivoted to select.rs for the landing.

## µ-select — hw_probe/select.rs (read in full, Iter 70)
Workload-aware backend routing — ONE private selector so all entry points share the GPU/CPU ladder. CPU/portable, ci-lean. `pub use select::{gpu_could_engage, parse_backend_str, select_backend, select_backend_verdict, BackendRoutingReason, BackendRoutingVerdict, BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES}` (hw_probe/mod.rs L30) + `pub(crate) use select::select_backend_for_file`.
- cpu_tier_backend (pub(crate), #[must_use]): SINGLE source of truth for "no GPU" — hyperscan_available ? SimdCpu : CpuFallback.
- BackendWorkload (priv struct): {bytes, pattern_count, large_chunk_bytes:Option<u64>}. gpu_dominates_dispatch_cost: None→true (file), Some(lc)→ lc>0 && lc*2≥bytes (batch dominance ≥50%).
- BackendRoutingReason (pub enum, 7 variants: TestOverride/GpuDisabledByPolicy/GpuProbeMiss/GpuSoftwareRenderer/GpuBatchNotDominant/GpuThresholdNotMet/GpuSelected) + label()→snake_case.
- BackendRoutingVerdict (pub struct, 11 fields incl backend/reason/gpu_tier/thresholds) + reason_detail()→human string.
- select_backend_for_workload (priv): the ladder — test_override → gpu_disabled_by_policy → !gpu_available(GpuProbeMiss) → gpu_is_software → !gpu_dominates(GpuBatchNotDominant) → gpu_could_engage?(GpuSelected) → GpuThresholdNotMet (all CPU branches via cpu_tier_backend).
- select_backend/select_backend_verdict (pub) + select_backend_for_file (pub(crate)) + select_backend_for_batch/_verdict (cfg(test)).
- gpu_could_engage (pub, #[must_use]): !gpu_available||software→false; else tier=classify_gpu_tier(name), `bytes>=solo || (bytes>=min && patterns>=pattern_floor)`.
- BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES (pub const [&str;10]): auto/gpu/gpu-region-presence/mega-scan/megascan/gpu-mega-scan/simd/simd-regex/cpu/cpu-fallback.
- parse_backend_str (pub): trim+lowercase → gpu|gpu-region-presence|gpu-zero-copy|literal-set=Gpu; mega-scan|megascan|gpu-mega-scan|regex-nfa|rule-pipeline=MegaScan; simd|simd-regex|hyperscan=SimdCpu; cpu|cpu-fallback|scalar=CpuFallback; else None.
- µ-select-01 (LANDED Iter 70, byte-identical Law-7/clarity DEDUP-vs-stdlib): `test_backend_override` (pub(super)) hand-rolled `match {Some(b)=>b, None=>None}` over the `Option<Option<ScanBackend>>` thread-local — that IS `Option::flatten`. Replaced with `.flatten()` (flatten is #[inline] to that exact match → byte-identical codegen). Test tests/gap/backend_override_values_parse_coherence.rs (3 tests, Vector-10 coherence via already-pub `keyhog_scanner::hw_probe::{parse_backend_str, BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES, ScanBackend}` — FIRST integration-test (non-cfg-test) coverage; existing parser tests use the cfg(test) testing path + hardcoded alias lists, NONE iterate the actual const): iterate BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES → every value except "auto" parses to Some (auto→None, the autoroute sentinel) — pins the const↔parser drift the module doc warns of; exact canonical maps (gpu→Gpu, mega-scan & no-hyphen megascan→MegaScan [the documented past silent-fallthrough bug], simd→SimdCpu, cpu-fallback→CpuFallback); trim+lowercase ("  SIMD  "→SimdCpu) + ""/"gibberish"→None. 56th landing.

## µ-cpolicy — confidence/policy.rs (read in full, Iter 72)
The confidence-policy owner: heuristic scoring, ML routing, post-ML finalize, checksum/prefix floors. CPU/portable (ml/entropy/gpu branches all cfg-gated; the non-cfg core is ci-lean). `pub(crate) mod policy` (confidence/mod.rs L5).
- MlScoreResult<'a> (pub(crate) enum): Final(f64) | cfg(ml) Pending{heuristic_conf,code_context,credential:Cow} | cfg(not ml) _Lifetime(PhantomData) — never-constructed lifetime placeholder (unreachable! if matched).
- checksum_policy_for / apply_checksum_confidence / apply_checksum_decision_confidence: ChecksumResult → Option<f64>: Invalid→None (drop), Valid→Some(conf.max(CHECKSUM_VALID_FLOOR)), StructurallyValid/NotApplicable→Some(conf unchanged).
- apply_known_prefix_floor: max(conf, known_prefix_confidence_floor(cred)) if Some, else conf.
- pre_ml_heuristic_confidence: raw * context_multiplier (TestCode/Documentation with !penalize → 1.0 multiplier; else code_context.confidence_multiplier()).
- NAMED_DETECTOR_ANCHOR_FLOOR (pub(crate) const = 0.55): clears default min_confidence 0.40 with headroom, below the high-confidence band. apply_named_detector_anchor_floor(conf, is_named, has_anchor) = is_named && has_anchor ? conf.max(0.55) : conf (a FLOOR/max, never a cap). [ALREADY covered: testing.rs facade + regression_named_detector_anchor_floor_recall.rs exact-value test.]
- candidate_match_score: heuristic → anchor-floor lift (anchor = context_anchor||literal_prefix||distinctive_inner_literal) → cfg(ml) ml-enabled?probabilistic_promise_override:Pending else Final → apply_known_prefix_floor on Final.
- finalize_report_confidence: post-ml penalties (+encoded-text lift) → path penalties → known-prefix floor → calibration multiplier → checksum confidence.
- cfg(ml) ml_pending_confidence: model_authoritative?model_conf : blend(ml_weight*model + (1-ml_weight)*heuristic).max(heuristic).max(model); then context penalty applies (Comment&&!scan_comments, Test/Doc&&penalize) ONLY if conf<0.95 → *= multiplier.
- cfg(entropy) entropy_fallback_confidence: ≥VERY_HIGH→0.75, ≥HIGH→0.65, else 0.55.min(entropy/8.0); +0.1 (cap 0.90) unless keyword=="none (high-entropy)".
- generic_secret_confidence(context,scan_comments,penalize_test_paths,entropy,value_len): base = TestCode&&penalize→0.25, Comment&&scan_comments→0.60, Documentation&&penalize→0.30, Comment→0.30, _→0.60; entropy_boost=((entropy-3.5)*0.1).min(0.25) [NO lower clamp — can be NEGATIVE]; length_boost=((value_len-16.0)*0.005).clamp(0.0,0.15) [clamped ≥0]; result=(base+entropy_boost+length_boost).min(0.95).
- µ-cpolicy-01 (LANDED Iter 72, BEHAVIORAL gap test as deliverable — NO code landing: the only repeated literal `0.95` at ml_pending_confidence's penalty-skip threshold vs generic_secret_confidence's cap is two INDEPENDENTLY-tunable policy thresholds; coupling them via one const = Law-1 coincidental-value coupling [same caution as Iter-63 is_provider_token_byte] → declined; the rest are tuned-formula magics not cleanly single-nameable). `generic_secret_confidence` had ONLY a source-shape gate (engine_fallback_confidence_owner.rs checks the fn STRING exists) — its computed VALUES were never pinned. Added NEW `generic_secret_confidence_for_test` facade (testing.rs, maps a context label→CodeContext) + tests/gap/generic_secret_confidence_formula.rs (4 tests, EXACT assert_eq using entropy=3.5+len=16 to zero both boosts → off float fragility): context base+gating (source→0.60, comment→0.30 / scan_comments→0.60, test+penalize→0.25 / no-penalize→0.60, doc+penalize→0.30 / no-penalize→0.60); high-entropy+long-value saturates the 0.95 ceiling; short value (len 10<16) → 0.60 (pins length clamp ≥0, no negative penalty); low entropy (1.5<3.5) → strictly < 0.60 (pins the entropy term's missing lower clamp, relational to avoid float fragility). 57th landing.

## µ-mlgate — multiline/config.rs has_concatenation_indicators large-file keyword gate (Iter 74)
- has_concatenation_indicators(text)->bool [cfg(multiline), pub(crate); re-exported multiline/mod.rs:15; callers engine/scan_coalesced.rs:227 + engine/backend_dispatch.rs:124]: structural pre-scan deciding whether a chunk is worth multiline concat-recovery preprocessing.
- EARLY-OUT 1 (structured-doc skip): trim_start then starts_with `{` / `[` / `<?xml` / `<` → false (JSON/XML/HTML never get the concat pass).
