- GATE (large-file keyword gate): if bytes.len() > LARGE_FILE_KEYWORD_GATE_BYTES (=4096, NOW NAMED) AND no secret keyword present → false. Keyword set = memmem `ecret`|`oken`|`assword`|`api_key`|`API_KEY`|`redential` (substring needles, so `Secret`/`secret`/`Token`/`Password`/`credential` all hit via the case-distinct stems; note `api_key`/`API_KEY` are the only case-paired full needles). Below the gate the cheap structural scan runs unconditionally.
- CHEAP-INDICATOR union (any → continue, none → false): has_explicit_concat (`" +` | `' +`); has_backslash_cont (`" \` | `' \`); has_template (memchr backtick); has_paste (`paste0(`|`paste(`|`concat!(`); has_implicit (markers `" "`,`' '`,`"\n"`,`"\n `,`"\n\t`,`'\n'`,`'\n `,`'\n\t`); has_var_ref_concat (memchr `+` AND var-ref-concat regex line).
- LINE CONFIRM (final pass): per line.trim() returns true on ends_with `+` / starts_with `+`|`+ ` / contains paste/concat / parenthesized-implicit-block / `" +`|`' +`|`+ "`|`+ '` / odd trailing `\` / `" "`|`' '` / var-ref-concat-line / single-backtick trailing / `${"`|`${'` (literal spliced into interpolation) / `}${` (adjacent interpolations). Else false.
- µ-mlgate-01 (LANDED Iter 74, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): the bare `4096` at the keyword-gate compare was the ONLY 4096 in config.rs (verified single-use) → hoisted to `#[cfg(feature="multiline")] const LARGE_FILE_KEYWORD_GATE_BYTES: usize = 4096;` (doc-commented, gated to match its sole gated user → no dead_code when multiline off), compare now `bytes.len() > LARGE_FILE_KEYWORD_GATE_BYTES`. Unobservable through any API (same compiled compare). Added `has_concatenation_indicators_for_test` facade (testing.rs multiline mod) + tests/gap/multiline_large_file_keyword_gate.rs (3 tests, #![cfg(multiline)]): SAME concat shape `x = "abc" +\n    "def"` is an indicator when short (≤4096, no keyword → true); is GATED OFF when padded >4096 with keyword-free 'a' filler (→ false); is back ON when the padded text's assignment name carries `secret` (→ true). Pins the gate threshold + the keyword-presence requirement above it. 58th landing.

## µ-mlpre — multiline/preprocessor.rs preprocess_multiline join pass (Iter 76)
- preprocess_multiline<'a>(text: impl Into<Cow<'a,str>>, config: &MultilineConfig, fragment_cache: &FragmentCache) -> PreprocessedText<'a> [cfg(multiline), pub(crate); re-exported multiline/mod.rs:20]: joins adjacent string fragments + continuations before scanning so a credential split across `"abc" +`/`"def"` lines surfaces as one token. Cow-discipline: returns Cow::Borrowed on passthrough/no-append (no body copy), Cow::Owned ONLY when a real concat join or structural fragment appends NEW bytes.
- EARLY-OUTS (both passthrough, carry text_owned Cow unchanged): should_passthrough(text) → passthrough_text; OR lines empty → PreprocessedText{Borrowed(""),0,[]}; OR first non-white char is `{`|`[` → passthrough_text. first_nonwhite uses text.trim_start().chars().next().unwrap_or(' ') [LAW10 documented sentinel default].
- JOIN WALK: lines = text.lines().collect(); source_line_offsets = compute_line_offsets; while index<lines.len() { process_line_chain(lines, offsets, index, config, current_offset) → (joined_line, lines_consumed, line_mappings); push joined_line; index += lines_consumed.max(1) }. result_lines holds ≤ lines.len() entries (one push/iter, index advances ≥1/iter).
- APPEND DECISION: joined_text = result_lines.join("\n"); is_real_concatenation = joined_text.trim() != text.trim(); will_append = is_real_concatenation && !joined_text.is_empty(). structural_base computed ARITHMETICALLY (will_append ? original_end+1+joined_text.len() : original_end+1) so collect_structural_fragments runs BEFORE materializing the extended buffer. KEY no-copy short-circuit: if !will_append && structural_joined.is_empty() → return text_owned (the original Cow) unchanged with identity_line_mappings — the common indicator-tripped-but-nothing-to-join case never builds a fresh String. original_end ALWAYS == text.len() on every path.
- BUFFER BUILD (only when appending): final_text = text.to_string(); if will_append { push '\n' + joined_text; shift mappings by original_end+1; appended_any=true }; if !structural_joined.is_empty() { if !appended_any push '\n'; push structural_joined.join("\n"); extend structural_mappings }. Returns Cow::Owned(final_text). identity_line_mappings(text, original_end): one LineMapping per '\n'-split segment, end_offset = (offset+line.len()+1).min(original_end).
- process_line_chain: joined_parts walk bounded by join_limit = config.max_join_lines.max(1); first line pushes part if non-empty; subsequent lines push when continuation_type ∈ {Backslash,PlusOperator,Implicit} OR part non-empty; breaks when !continues. joined = joined_parts.join(""); single LineMapping spanning [base_offset, base_offset+joined.len()] when non-empty.
- µ-mlpre-01 (LANDED Iter 76, byte-identical Law-7 capacity reserve): result_lines was `Vec::new()` then push'd once per outer-loop iteration (loop advances index ≥1/iter so ≤ lines.len() entries) → `Vec::with_capacity(lines.len())` to skip the doubling reallocs as the join walk fills it. Capacity is unobservable in the returned PreprocessedText (which exposes only text/original_end/mappings) → byte-identical. mappings left as Vec::new() (extend count not cleanly upper-boundable). Added preprocess_multiline_for_test facade (testing.rs multiline mod → (String, usize) of result.text.into_owned() + original_end, builds MultilineConfig::default() + FragmentCache::new(1024)) + tests/gap/multiline_preprocess_join_contract.rs (2 tests, #![cfg(multiline)]): passthrough JSON `{...}` → text==input exactly + original_end==input.len(); concat `secret = "abc" +\n    "def"` → original_end==input.len(), text.starts_with(input) (original preserved as prefix), text.contains("abcdef") (split reassembled), text.len()>input.len(). 59th landing.
- µ-mlpre-CAPTURE (Iter 77 review, TWO own-imprecision corrections — verified vs source, NO code change, the SOURCE was correct, my top-bullet was over-simplified): (1) Cow-discipline phrasing "returns Cow::Borrowed on passthrough/no-append" is the COMMON-CASE shorthand (the scan hot path hands in an already-Borrowed chunk), NOT the literal contract. PRECISE: the passthrough/no-append paths carry `text_owned` THROUGH UNCHANGED — the input variant is PRESERVED (Borrowed-in→Borrowed-out; an already-Owned normalization buffer is MOVED, not re-copied — source L117 comment "A borrowed chunk stays borrowed; a normalization-owned buffer is moved, not re-copied"). The real invariant is "no NEW body copy", not "always Borrowed". (2) the no-copy short-circuit return is NOT `identity_line_mappings` ALONE: it is `identity_line_mappings(text, original_end)` EXTENDED with the loop's accumulated chain `mappings` UNSHIFTED (source L119-120 `original_mappings.extend(mappings)`; comment "The chain mappings are still emitted unshifted ... so the offset→line map is byte-identical to the eager-copy result"). The chain mappings may be non-empty even when !will_append. Both folds tighten the µ-block to source fidelity; the LANDED capacity-reserve fix + the gap test are unaffected/correct.

## µ-mlsx — multiline/string_extract.rs per-line string-literal extraction (Iter 78)
- Module: cohesive family of pure per-line string-literal extractors feeding the multiline preprocessor (it owns orchestration/offsets; this owns pulling the literal out of ONE line across quote styles, `\`-continuation, `+`-concat, Python implicit adjacency, paste0()/paste()/concat!() joins, JS template literals). Two non-cfg-gated pub(crate) entry points (extract_prefix, fragment_assignment_name_is_credential_like, re-exported mod.rs:21) + a cfg(multiline) family (extract_string_part, extract_quoted_content [pub(crate)], etc.).
- extract_prefix(var_name:&str)->String [NOT cfg-gated, pub(crate)]: collapses a split-credential fragment NAME to a shared base. Byte walk: skip `_`/`-`; if bytes[i..].get(..4) eq_ignore_ascii_case `part` → skip 4 (the `part` segment); else push ch.to_ascii_lowercase(), advance ch.len_utf8(). FINALLY truncate to prefix.trim_end_matches(ascii_digit).len() (drop a trailing numeric run). String::with_capacity(var_name.len()). E.g. api_key→apikey, token_part1→token, Auth-Token→authtoken.
- fragment_assignment_name_is_credential_like(var_name)->bool [NOT cfg-gated, pub(crate)]: normalize_assignment_keyword first (None→false); public-metadata owners excluded (digest/hash/checksum/version/lines + *_digest/_hash/_checksum/_version/_lines/_dedup_key → false); else normalized_or_fragment_base_is_credential_like. is_bare_ambiguous_fragment_owner (key/token/secret/password/passwd/pwd/pass/credential/auth/authorization) is rejected ONLY when from_fragment_suffix=false (a bare ambiguous owner isn't credential-like on its own, but IS once a fragment suffix was stripped).
- extract_string_part(line, config, is_continuation) [cfg(multiline), pub(super)] -> (String, bool, ContinuationType{None,Backslash,PlusOperator,Implicit,TemplateLiteral}): ORDER = backslash-cont (trimmed ends `\` not `\\`, with embedded `+`→plus-concat else string-content) → function-concat (paste0/paste/concat!) → plus-concat (config-gated) → python-implicit (config-gated) → template-literal (config-gated) → fallback (is_continuation? extract_string_content : line.to_string()).
- extract_quoted_content(s, open, close) [cfg(multiline), pub(crate)]: f-string detection is ADJACENCY-ONLY — tracks only the char immediately before the open quote, is_fstring = open!='`' && prev∈{f,F} (fixes the old bug where any preceding `f` in `prefix`/`config`/`final`/`ref` wrongly flagged f-string and dropped brace spans). Inside an f-string: `{{`/`}}`→literal `{`/`}` (consume the twin); a real `{expr}`→skipped with brace-depth nesting (runtime value, not literal). `\`-escape pushes both chars. Returns Some(content) at close, None if unterminated/no open.
- extract_plus_concatenation: owns QUOTED literal concat only — bails if the post-`=` content has no `"`/`'`/backtick (unquoted `+` is base64/arithmetic, not a join; var-ref `head + tail` is the structural resolver's job). split('+'), extract_string_content each part, join; continues = ends_with('+'). extract_python_implicit_concatenation: adjacency requires NO non-whitespace gap between closed literals; <2 parts→None. extract_template_literal_continuation: continues = backtick count is ODD; inside `${...}`, only string-literal bytes are appended (bare idents/operators skipped); brace-depth + in_str nesting.
- µ-mlsx-01 (LANDED Iter 78, contained one-owner DEDUP): strip_separated_fragment_suffix (matches! over 16 literals) and strip_compact_fragment_suffix (inline `[…;16]` array of the SAME 16 literals) duplicated the fragment-suffix list — a drift hazard. Hoisted to one `const FRAGMENT_SUFFIXES: [&str; 16]` (prefix/suffix/head/tail/left/right/chunk/piece/frag/fragment/part/chunks/pieces/frags/fragments/parts); separated now `FRAGMENT_SUFFIXES.contains(&suffix)` (membership-equivalent to the matches!), compact now `for suffix in FRAGMENT_SUFFIXES`. The `part<digits>` numeric form stays per-stripper (a pattern, not a literal). Behaviorally identical for every input → unobservable through any API. const is non-cfg-gated to match its non-gated users. Added extract_prefix_for_test facade (testing.rs multiline mod) + tests/gap/multiline_fragment_prefix_extraction.rs (3 tests, #![cfg(multiline)], EXACT assert_eq): api_key→apikey (`_` sep dropped), token_part1→token (`part` skip + trailing-digit trim), Auth-Token→authtoken (`-` sep + lowercase). 60th landing.

## µ-ctxwin — pipeline/context_window.rs per-finding context windowing (Iter 80)
- Module: PORTABLE (no top-level cfg; only monotonic_prefix_len is cfg-split multiline vs not). Per-finding/per-match context extraction + line bookkeeping. Heavily perf-tuned already (memchr newline scans, partition_point binary search, capacity reserves, find_iter vs captures_iter fast path, reused CaptureLocations buffer).
- local_context_window(text, line/*1-based*/, radius) -> &str [pub(crate); re-exported pipeline/mod.rs:10]: BORROWS the [line-radius, line+radius] window directly out of text — no Vec<&str> collect, no join, no O(file) lines().skip(). start = walk forward over (line-radius-1) newlines via memchr (returns "" if fewer lines than asked); const MAX_WINDOW_BYTES = 8*1024 hard byte cap (cap = (start+8KiB).min(len)) — byte-identical to uncapped for ordinary source, only bites a pathological multi-KiB no-`\n` line (was quadratic: 164KiB single line × 8K matches = tens of seconds). window_lines = radius*2+1; walk that many `\n` from start; the FINAL window line's terminator is EXCLUDED (end+pos) while earlier lines keep it (end+pos+1) so neighbours stay `\n`-joined; None-before-cap → end=cap. end snapped via engine::floor_char_boundary (only the byte-cap path can land mid-codepoint). Returns &text[start..end]. Pairs with context::inference::surrounding_line_window's same cap.
- compute_line_offsets(text) -> Vec<usize> [pub, re-exported pipeline/mod.rs:7 + crate root lib.rs:168 pub(crate)]: offsets[0]=0 then pos+1 for each `\n` via ONE memchr_iter pass (carries search state across matches, ~4x str::match_indices on >1KiB). Pre-size Vec::with_capacity(bytes.len()/40 + 1) (~40-char avg line).
- match_line_number(preprocessed, line_offsets, offset) -> usize: preprocessed.line_for_offset(offset) first; fallback line_offsets.partition_point(|&lo| lo <= offset) — the first offset strictly > offset is the 1-based line index (O(log L), was O(L) position() walk).
- find_companion(preprocessed, primary_line/*1-based*/, companion) -> Option<String>: window = [primary_line-within_lines (clamped ≥ FIRST_LINE_NUMBER), primary_line+within_lines]; haystack = preprocessed.text.get(window_start..window_end)? (`.get` not `[..]` — windowed/decoded text may be shorter, must not panic a worker). NO-capture-group fast path uses find_iter (no Captures alloc); capture-group path reuses ONE CaptureLocations via captures_read_at + manual cursor (zero-width match → cursor+1 then ceil_char_boundary). Both paths skip a match/group longer than the cap and require line_for_offset ∈ line_range before returning m/group .to_string().
- line_window_offsets(preprocessed, start_line, end_line) -> Option<(usize,usize)>: byte-for-byte preserved from the original full-vec linear scan — start_offset = FIRST mapping (vec order) with line_number ≥ start_line; end_offset = LAST mapping (vec order) with line_number ≤ end_line. mappings is start_offset-sorted but line_number is NOT globally monotonic (multiline appends structural/joined segments carrying ORIGINAL source line + huge SYNTHETIC_BASE_LINE), so: binary-search the monotonic identity PREFIX (two partition_point calls, O(log L)) then linear-fold the tiny structural TAIL in vec order (start-side tail wins only if prefix had none; end-side tail always supersedes). monotonic_prefix_len: cfg(multiline) = partition_point(start_offset < original_end); not(multiline) = mappings.len() (no tail).
- µ-ctxwin-01 (LANDED Iter 80, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): find_companion had the SAME `4096` companion-match-length cap as TWO bare literals — no-group path `m.len() > 4096` (skip) and capture-group path `e.saturating_sub(s) <= 4096` (accept) — both meaning "max acceptable companion match/group length". Hoisted to one fn-local `const MAX_COMPANION_MATCH_BYTES: usize = 4096;` used at both sites (the only two 4096 in the file; MAX_WINDOW_BYTES=8*1024 is the separate window cap). Removes the drift hazard (one path could change without the other) — same compiled comparison, unobservable through any API. Added local_context_window_for_test facade (testing.rs ROOT level → .to_string()) + tests/gap/context_window_local_slice.rs (3 tests, NOT cfg-gated, EXACT assert_eq) on "aaa\nbbb\nccc\nddd\neee": (3,1)→"bbb\nccc\nddd" (centred, final `\n` excluded, interiors kept), (3,0)→"ccc" (bare line), (1,1)→"aaa\nbbb\nccc" (lower bound clamps to file start). 61st landing.

## µ-splice — decode/pipeline/splice.rs decoded-payload splice (Iter 82)
- Module: PORTABLE (no cfg). After a decoder turns an encoded blob → plaintext, this splices the decoded text BACK into the parent at the blob's position so the parent's companion anchors (aws_secret=, Authorization: Bearer, api_key:) stay ADJACENT to the decoded credential — which is what detector regexes need to fire. Functions are pub(in crate::decode), re-exported pub(super) in pipeline.rs:179 (now incl. bytecount_newlines + splice_decoded_payload_at) then pub(crate) in decode/mod.rs:22.
- push_decoded_text_chunk(decoded_chunks, chunk, text, decoder_name): legacy no-blob entry → forwards to _spliced with original_encoded="" (decoded-text-alone shape). push_decoded_text_chunk_spliced → _spliced_at with original_span=None.
- push_decoded_text_chunk_spliced_at(decoded_chunks, chunk, original_span: Option<(usize,usize)>, original_encoded, text, decoder_name): FAST control-char reject FIRST — return if text empty OR any byte <0x20 except \n/\r/\t (byte-level, no UTF-8 decode). Then if original_encoded non-empty: splice_decoded_payload (find blob) or _at (known span); on Some((win_start, spliced, decoded_at)) → base_offset = chunk.base_offset+win_start, base_line = chunk.base_line + bytecount_newlines(parent[..win_start]), payload=spliced, decoded_span=Some((decoded_at, decoded_at+text_len)); on None → (base_offset, base_line, text, Some((0,text_len))). Empty original_encoded → same text-alone fallback. Pushes Chunk{data: payload.into(), metadata{... source_type=format!("{}/{}", parent.source_type, decoder_name), decoded_span ...}}.
- splice_decoded_payload(parent, original_encoded, decoded_text, decoder_name): start=parent.find(original_encoded)?; end=start+len; → _at. splice_decoded_payload_at(parent, start, end, decoded_text, decoder_name) -> Option<(usize win_start, String payload, usize decoded_at)>: bail None if start>end || end>parent.len() || !is_char_boundary(start|end). If decoder_name=="base64", end = consume_adjacent_base64_padding(end). win_start=floor_char_boundary(parent, start.saturating_sub(SPLICE_CONTEXT_WINDOW)); win_end=ceil_char_boundary(parent, end.saturating_add(SPLICE_CONTEXT_WINDOW)). payload = String::with_capacity((win_end-win_start)-(end-start)+decoded_text.len()) [EXACT reserve]; push parent[win_start..start] + decoded_text + parent[end..win_end]; decoded_at = start-win_start.
- const SPLICE_CONTEXT_WINDOW = 512 (bytes of parent kept each side — already named). consume_adjacent_base64_padding(parent, start): consume up to 2 trailing `=` (end-start<2 guard) ONLY if the byte AFTER the padding is EOF or a delimiter (\n \r \t space ; , " ' ` } ] &); else return start (don't consume). decode_candidate_refs_exact / decode_candidate_spans_exact: iterate candidates, decode each (Ok→splice via _spliced_at with candidate.span(), Err→skip [LAW10 recall-preserving — original chunk stays]), collect Vec<Chunk>.
- µ-splice-01 (LANDED Iter 82, byte-identical Law-7 scalar→SIMD): bytecount_newlines was `bytes.iter().filter(|&&b| b == b'\n').count()` (scalar) → `memchr::memchr_iter(b'\n', bytes).count()` — the SAME idiom compute_line_offsets uses (~4x on >1KiB). Runs on the parent prefix up to the splice point per decoded candidate (can be large). Identical count for every input → unobservable through any API. Widened bytecount_newlines + splice_decoded_payload_at to pub(in crate::decode) + re-export chain (pipeline.rs pub(super) use, decode/mod.rs pub(crate) use) for test reach. Added bytecount_newlines_for_test + splice_decoded_payload_at_for_test facades (testing.rs ROOT) + tests/gap/decode_splice_payload_window.rs (4 tests, NOT cfg-gated, EXACT assert_eq): splice("x=AAAA;",2,6,"SECRET","raw")→Some((0,"x=SECRET;",2)) (span swapped, context kept); ("k=QUJD==;",2,6,"DEC","base64")→Some((0,"k=DEC;",2)) (`==` padding consumed before `;`); ("abc",1,99,"X","raw")→None (end>len, no panic); count_newlines b"a\nb\nc"=2, b"no newlines here"=0, b""=0, b"\n\n\n"=3. 62nd landing.
- µ-splice-CAPTURE (Iter 89 GEMINI REVIEW, INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED — visibility correction, no code change): the µ-splice header + µ-splice-01 said bytecount_newlines + splice_decoded_payload_at are `pub(in crate::decode)` re-exported `pub(super)` in pipeline.rs. SOURCE contradicts: both are declared `pub(crate) fn` at the DEFINITION site (splice.rs:124 bytecount_newlines, :149 splice_decoded_payload_at), re-exported via a SEPARATE `pub(crate) use splice::{bytecount_newlines, splice_decoded_payload_at};` line (pipeline.rs:184 — NOT inside the `pub(super) use splice::{...}` block at :180) then `pub(crate) use pipeline::{bytecount_newlines, …, splice_decoded_payload_at, …}` in decode/mod.rs:22-24. They are `pub(crate)` END-TO-END — which is precisely the Iter-82 E0364 fix (a `pub(in crate::decode)` item on a `pub(super) use` line cannot be re-exported wider at pub(crate)). The OTHER splice fns (push_decoded_text_chunk*, decode_candidate_{refs,spans}_exact) ARE `pub(in crate::decode)` and DO sit in the pub(super) block. Gemini missed this (it only checked the memchr idiom, not the visibility); found by independent visibility read. The earlier header/µ-splice-01 wording is the pre-fix/imprecise description; the pub(crate)-end-to-end state is correct and intended.

## µ-xtract — decode/pipeline/extractor.rs pre-decode encoded-value extraction (Iter 84)
- Module: PORTABLE (no cfg). Single char-level pass pulling candidate encoded values (base64/hex/url/quoted/assigned) out of chunk text BEFORE decoders run, with byte spans so a decoded result can be spliced back (µ-splice). ExtractedValue{value:String, start:Option<usize>, end:Option<usize>} (pub(crate)); ::new(v,s,e) sets Some spans, ::synthetic(v) sets None/None, .span()->Option<(usize,usize)> = (start?, end?). hash_fast re-exported from crate::util_hash (single FNV-1a owner; comment notes it was copy-pasted into entropy/ml_scorer/decode_structure before unification).
- SHARED_CANDIDATES thread_local cache: prime_shared_candidates(text) computes extract_encoded_value_spans_raw once per BFS item (the ~5 whole-chunk decoders base64/hex/url/caesar/reverse reused ONE extraction instead of 5-6× recompute — was ~67% of decode-gen), keyed by (text.as_ptr(), text.len()); clear_shared_candidates() drops it. with_extracted_value_spans(text, f) [pub(crate), now re-exported pub(crate) via pipeline.rs + decode/mod.rs]: reads the cache iff (ptr,len) match else computes fresh extract_encoded_value_spans_raw(text), passes &[ExtractedValue] to f.
- extract_encoded_value_spans_raw(text)->Vec<ExtractedValue>: single peekable char_indices pass with FOUR accumulators. (1) QUOTED ("/'/`): flush b64+pct, read until matching quote, \-escape keeps both bytes, ws-skipped, push iff cleaned.len() >= MIN_EXTRACTED_VALUE_LEN. (2) ASSIGNMENT (`:`/`=`): flush b64+pct, skip ws after delim, read value until ws/;/,/quote, push iff len >= MIN_EXTRACTED_VALUE_LEN. (3) PERCENT-run (is_pct_run_char = '%' || ascii_hexdigit; a run must START with '%' — bare hex with empty block falls through to b64): accumulate, DON'T also feed b64 (avoids `%41%57`→`4157` spurious b64 candidate). (4) BASE64 (is_b64_char = ascii && is_base64_candidate_byte): accumulate; non-ws non-b64 flushes; ws inside a b64 block is allowed (line continuations). flush_b64: push iff b64_block.len() >= 16 (else clear). flush_pct: push iff len >= MIN_PCT_TRIPLETS*3 && '%'-count >= MIN_PCT_TRIPLETS (const MIN_PCT_TRIPLETS=1). Trailing flush of both after the loop.
- EXTRACT_CALLS/BYTES/NS atomics + ExtractTimer Drop = `keyhog scan --profile` measurement (gated on engine::profile::enabled()); extract_profile_dump/reset (pub(crate)).
- CAPTURE (stale-doc) → CLEARED Iter 86 (µ-xtract-02): (a) the doc comment directly above `flush_b64` described a "percent run / 2 triplets / printf noise" — it belonged to flush_pct, not the base64 flush (flush_b64 gates on b64_block.len() >= 16). (b) flush_pct's inner comment said "Two triplets (6 decoded bytes) ... Three triplets remains the default bar" but the const is MIN_PCT_TRIPLETS=1 — comment contradicted the value. Both were real doc-code coherence drifts (confirmed by Iter-85 Gemini review + grep); fixed in Iter 86.
- µ-xtract-01 (LANDED Iter 84, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): `cleaned.len() >= 4` appeared at BOTH the quoted-string push gate AND the assignment-value push gate — the SAME minimum-extracted-value-length floor as two bare literals (drift hazard: one could be bumped without the other). Hoisted to fn-local `const MIN_EXTRACTED_VALUE_LEN: usize = 4;` at both sites (the only two `4` floors; b64's 16 + pct's triplet math are distinct). Same compiled comparison → unobservable through any API. Re-exported with_extracted_value_spans at pub(crate) end-to-end (moved from pipeline.rs pub(super) block to a `pub(crate) use extractor::with_extracted_value_spans;` + decode/mod.rs pub(crate) use — the Iter-82 E0364 lesson). Added extract_encoded_value_spans_for_test facade (testing.rs ROOT → (value,start,end) tuples via .span()) + tests/gap/decode_encoded_value_extraction.rs (3 tests, NOT cfg-gated, EXACT assert_eq): "key = secretvalue"→[("secretvalue",6,17)] (assignment span); "x=\"hunter2pass\""→[("hunter2pass",3,14)] (quoted span, `x=`+quotes excluded); "a = abc"→[] (3<4 dropped) + "a = abcd"→[("abcd",4,8)] (pins the floor boundary). 63rd landing.
- µ-xtract-02 (LANDED Iter 86, Vector-10 stale-doc PAIRED with a test, comment-only code change = trivially unobservable through any API): rewrote BOTH confirmed stale comments to match the code — flush_b64's doc now describes flushing a BASE64 block (push iff len >= 16, else discard short alphanumeric words), flush_pct's now describes MIN_PCT_TRIPLETS=1 (one `%41`-style triplet floor) instead of the wrong "two/three triplets". PAIRED 3 EXACT-value tests appended to tests/gap/decode_encoded_value_extraction.rs pinning the REAL thresholds the corrected comments now state: extract("abcdefghijklmno")→[] (15-char b64 run < 16 dropped) + extract("abcdefghijklmnop")→[("abcdefghijklmnop",0,16)] (16 >= 16 kept, pins flush_b64's 16); extract("%41")→[("%41",0,3)] (a SINGLE percent-triplet is kept — pins MIN_PCT_TRIPLETS=1, the exact behavior the old "two/three triplets" comment denied; the `41` hex is NOT also accumulated as a b64 candidate). 64th landing.
- µ-xtract-CAPTURE (Iter 89 GEMINI REVIEW, INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED — terminology precision, no code change): the block described extract_encoded_value_spans_raw as "a single peekable char_indices pass with FOUR accumulators" and listed QUOTED/ASSIGNMENT/PERCENT/BASE64 as if symmetric. PRECISE: only TWO are cross-iteration accumulators — b64_block (L137-139) and pct_block (L148-150), persistent buffers flushed across main-loop iterations via flush_b64/flush_pct. QUOTED (L217-254) and ASSIGNMENT (L257-290) are INLINE single-iteration sub-scanners: each builds a LOCAL `cleaned` String in a nested inner `while chars.peek()` loop and pushes + `continue`s within the SAME outer iteration, carrying NO state forward. The four-BRANCH dispatch + each branch's identity/gate are correct; "accumulator" simply over-applies to the two inline branches. Confirmed by reading the loop body (Gemini-flagged + independently verified). Re-state as "FOUR branches: 2 persistent accumulators (b64/pct) + 2 inline sub-scanners (quoted/assignment)".

## µ-struct — multiline/structural.rs cross-line structural reassembly (Iter 88)
- Module: cfg(feature="multiline"). collect_structural_fragments(lines, source_line_offsets, initial_offset, fragment_cache) -> (Vec<String> joined, Vec<LineMapping>) [pub(super)]: the cross-line pass the per-line chain (preprocessor) can't do — reassembles credentials split across MULTIPLE lines. Early-return ([],[]) if ASSIGN_RE is None. Appends to a running current_struct_offset (each push advances offset by joined.len()+1). FIVE reassembly shapes:
  1. INLINE ARRAY (per-line, gate len>=16): inline_array_assignment_name(line) credential-like → join_inline_array_strings(line) (concatenate every quoted run's contents) → push if >=16.
  2. CLUSTER build (main loop): ASSIGN_RE captures (var_name, value); record var_values[name]=(index,value); skip if !fragment_assignment_name_is_credential_like(var_name); group by extract_prefix(var_name) into active_clusters; append to an existing cluster only if is_related_variable (same name or same prefix) AND within 10 lines of its last member, else start a new cluster.
  3. PARENTHESIZED IMPLICIT blocks (gate >=12): collect_parenthesized_implicit_blocks scans up to PARENTHESIZED_IMPLICIT_SCAN_LINES=16 lines after a `(`-opening assignment, joining quoted_literal_line parts until `)`; needs >=2 literal parts.
  4. CONCAT-REFERENCE (gate >=12): resolve_concat_reference(line, var_values) matches CONCAT_RE `lhs = id1 + id2 [+ ...]`, resolves each ident to its recorded literal, joins; kept only if concat_target_name_is_credential_like(target) OR known_prefix_confidence_floor(joined).is_some(). Uses line_number = SYNTHETIC_BASE_LINE(=1_000_000_000)+offset_idx so these don't merge with real line ranges in line_window_offsets.
  5. TEMPLATE INTERPOLATION (gate >=12, ONLY if any line contains `}${`): collect_template_vars (TVAR_RE, admits 1-char + `$`-prefixed names) then resolve_template_reference per line; line_number=SYNTHETIC_BASE_LINE.
  Finally: cluster pass — clusters with >=2 members join their values (gate >=12) AND every member is record_and_reassemble'd into fragment_cache (cross-chunk).
- resolve_template_reference(line, vars: &HashMap<String,String>) -> Option<String> [NOW pub(crate), re-exported multiline/mod.rs]: finds the backtick-delimited template RHS; for each `${...}` interpolation: a quoted inner (`${"AB"}`/`${'AB'}`, len>=2) contributes the inner bytes, else a bare ident resolves via vars (None on ANY unresolved ref → no partial candidate); plain template text kept verbatim. Returns Some(joined) iff >=1 interpolation resolved.
- inline_array_assignment_name(line)->Option<&str>: last identifier-ish token (alphanumeric/_/-/.) before the first `=`/`:`. join_inline_array_strings(line)->String: concatenate the contents of every quoted run in the line. is_related_variable(v1,v2): v1==v2 || extract_prefix(v1)==extract_prefix(v2). resolve_concat_reference requires >=2 RHS idents all resolving. CONCAT_RE/TVAR_RE are LazyLock<Option<Regex>> (warn_prefilter_disabled on compile error → None, fail-safe); warm_runtime_regexes() force-inits both.
- µ-struct-01 (LANDED Iter 88, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): the SAME min-reassembled-fragment-length floor `12` appeared as FOUR bare literals across the structural passes — parenthesized-block filter, concat-reference filter, template filter, and the cluster-join gate (4-way drift hazard). Hoisted to fn-local `const MIN_STRUCTURAL_FRAGMENT_LEN: usize = 12;` at all four sites. The inline-ARRAY path's separate `16` cutoff is intentionally NOT folded in (different value/policy). Same compiled comparison at all four → unobservable through any API. Widened resolve_template_reference to pub(crate) + re-export for test reach. Added resolve_template_reference_for_test facade (testing.rs multiline mod, builds the vars HashMap from &[(&str,&str)]) + tests/gap/multiline_structural_template_resolve.rs (3 tests, #![cfg(multiline)], EXACT assert_eq): "token = `${a}${b}`;" {a:xoxb-,b:SECRET}→Some("xoxb-SECRET") (adjacent ident interps glue); "k = `${\"AB\"}${c}`" {c:CD}→Some("ABCD") (quoted-literal inner bytes + ident); "k = `${missing}`" {a:x}→None (unresolved ref → no partial candidate). 65th landing.

## µ-features — ml_scorer/ml_features.rs ML feature-vector extraction (Iter 90)
- Module: cfg(feature="ml"). Turns a (candidate `text`, `context`) pair into a fixed `[f32; NUM_FEATURES]` vector for the MoE scorer. NUM_FEATURES=42 (pub(crate)): 4 length + 4 entropy + 4 char-class + 4 prefix + 4 context + 4 placeholder + 4 structure + 6 file-type one-hot + 3 extra (comment/assignment/test-file) + 1 decode-structure. compute_features_with_config(text, context, known_prefixes:&[String], secret_keywords, test_keywords, placeholder_keywords) -> [f32;42] is the ONE public entry — `pub fn`, re-exported `pub use ml_features::compute_features_with_config;` at ml_scorer.rs:24 (so integration tests reach it as keyhog_scanner::ml_scorer::compute_features_with_config directly; ALSO a testing.rs facade at :1827 + the dump_features example use it; it's the parity anchor for ml/parity_check.py so the Python port must stay byte-identical). compute_features_public(text,context) is `#[cfg(test)]`-only (zeros on empty, else forwards with empty keyword lists). Empty text → debug_assert (callers guarantee non-empty).
- Pipeline (compute_features_with_config): zero-init f; len=text.len(); ent=shannon_entropy(text_bytes); text_summary=summarize_text_bytes (ONE byte pass for has_upper/lower/digit/symbol + dot_count + dash_count, then unique_chars=unique_byte_count(bytes) — a SEPARATE pass, the canonical entropy owner, deliberately NOT folded). Then apply_* writers each own a contiguous feature range: length(0-3), entropy(4-7), char(8-11), prefix(12-15), context(16-19), placeholder(20-23), structure(24-27), file_type(32+idx), extra(38-40), decode_structure(41). f[28..32] stay 0 (padding). binary_feature(bool)->f32 = 1.0/0.0 (if/else, NOT f32::from — left as a named predicate). normalized_ratio(n,d)=0.0 if d==0 else (n/d).min(1.0). longest_known_prefix = max len of known_prefixes that text.starts_with, else 0 (LAW10 sentinel).
- Constants (all named, Tier-ish): MAX_NORMALIZED_TEXT_LENGTH=200.0, MEDIUM/LONG/VERY_LONG_LENGTH_THRESHOLD=20/40/100, MAX_NORMALIZED_ENTROPY=8.0, ML_LOW_ENTROPY_FEATURE_THRESHOLD=3.5 (f64; high/very-high reuse entropy crate's HIGH/VERY_HIGH, not private copies), MAX_PREFIX_LENGTH=10.0, OPENAI_PREFIX="sk-", AWS_ACCESS_KEY_PREFIX="AKIA", LOW_VARIETY_BYTE_THRESHOLD=3, MIN_LOW_VARIETY_LENGTH=5, MIN_HEX_PLACEHOLDER_LENGTH=10, MAX_UNIQUE_CHAR/DOT/DASH_NORMALIZATION=40/5/10, FILE_TYPE_OFFSET=32, file-type indices CONFIG=0/SOURCE=1/CI=2/INFRA=3/OTHER=4/BINARY=5, COMMENT_CONTEXT=38/ASSIGNMENT=39/TEST_FILE=40/DECODE_STRUCTURE=41. TEST_FILE_CONTEXT_FRAGMENTS=[test,mock,fixture,spec] (hoisted &[&[u8]] const — avoids 4 String allocs/call). Marker tables BINARY/CI/INFRA/SOURCE/CONFIG_MARKERS + SOURCE_EXTENSIONS + COMMENT_PREFIXES=["#","//","/*","--"] are file-type/context signals.
- infer_file_type priority: binary > ci > infra > source > config > other (first match wins; one-hot at FILE_TYPE_OFFSET+idx). is_*_context use ascii-CI substring (contains_any_ascii_case_insensitive_static via crate::ascii_ci::ci_find_nonempty) except is_source_context/is_infra_context/is_config_context also test a CASE-SENSITIVE `contains` (SOURCE_MARKERS / "from " / has_unquoted_equals). has_unquoted_equals: byte scan for `=` whose neighbors are neither `'` nor `"`. has_assignment_operator = has_unquoted_equals || contains(": "). unique_bigram_stats: [u64;1024] bitmap over the 65536 bigram space, returns (unique, len-1); <2 bytes → (0,0).
- µ-features-01 (LANDED Iter 90, byte-identical Vector-7 one-owner DEDUP): the comment-context check `COMMENT_PREFIXES.iter().any(|p| context.trim().starts_with(p))` was computed at TWO sites — apply_context_features for feature[19] AND apply_extra_features for feature[38] (COMMENT_CONTEXT_FEATURE_INDEX). Both features encode the SAME "match is inside a comment" signal and the model was trained on them as identical; two copies of the predicate are a silent drift hazard (one could gain a comment marker the other lacks, desyncing two trained-identical features). Hoisted to one `fn context_starts_with_comment_prefix(context) -> bool` called from both. Same expression, just named → byte-identical feature values, unobservable through compute_features_with_config / the parity port. Gap test via the REAL pub fn (no facade): tests/gap/ml_features_comment_context_single_owner.rs (2 tests, #![cfg(ml)], EXACT assert_eq on binary features): text "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE" (len 20) in "# …" context → f[15]=1.0 (AKIA) f[14]=0.0 (not sk-) f[1]=1.0 (len>=20) f[2]=0.0 (<40) f[19]=1.0 f[38]=1.0 AND f[19]==f[38]; "sk-abc123" in "openai_key = sk-abc123" → f[14]=1.0 (sk-) f[15]=0.0 f[19]=0.0 f[38]=0.0 AND f[19]==f[38] (the single-owner invariant: the two comment features always move together). 66th landing.

## µ-decstruct — decode_structure.rs decode-through verdict (Iter 92)
- Module: PORTABLE (no top cfg; `pub(crate) mod decode_structure` in lib.rs:85). keyhog's decode-through advantage: a generic high-entropy candidate is decoded once and turned into a verdict (binary asset / protobuf envelope vs credential) consumed by ML feature 41 (ml_features apply_decode_structure_feature → evidence(text).is_binary_payload()), confidence penalties, and the generic/entropy fallbacks. DecodeStructure{decodable:bool, decoded_len:usize, printable_ratio:f32, magic:Option<&'static str>, protobuf_wire:bool} (pub(crate), Copy/Default). DecodeStructure::is_binary_payload = magic.is_some() || (protobuf_wire && decoded_len>=8). DecodeEvidence wraps structure + decoded_is_base64_blob + decoded_is_hex_key_material + decoded_contains_nul_byte (cfg(any(feature="entropy",test))) + decoded_contains_placeholder; const accessors.
- evidence(candidate) [pub(crate)]: memoized by util_hash::hash_fast over DECODE_FACTS_CACHE thread_local (HashMap cap 256, util_hash::memoize_by_hash w/ DEFAULT_MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES). analyze(candidate)=evidence(.).structure(). compute_decode_facts: trim; bail Default if trimmed.len()<MIN_DECODE_LEN(=16); decode_candidate else Default; bail Default if decoded empty; printable = count of bytes in 32..127 or {9,10,13}; structure{decodable:true, decoded_len, printable_ratio=printable/len, magic=magic_format(bytes), protobuf_wire=parse_protobuf_wire(bytes)}. decoded_is_base64_blob = len>=32 && all is_standard_base64_byte; decoded_is_hex_key_material = len in {32,40,48} && all ascii_hexdigit. decodes_to_printable_text = decodable && len>=8 && printable_ratio>=0.85 && !is_binary_payload.
- decode_candidate(s): if contains `_` && is_underscore_hex_candidate → hex_decode.ok(); else base64_decode ok → Some; else if len>=16 && len even && is_plain_hex_candidate → hex_decode.ok(); else None (all LAW10 recall-preserving trial decodes). is_underscore_hex_candidate: hex_len (non-`_` bytes) >=16 && even && all bytes are `_`-or-hexdigit. magic_format: 28-entry SIGS table (png/jpeg/gif×2/gzip/bzip2/xz/zstd/zip×2/7z/rar/pdf/elf/mach-o×3/java-class/pe/sqlite/ogg/riff/wasm/zlib×4); `.find(|(sig,_)| b.starts_with(sig)).map(name)` — first-match wins, &'static str.
- is_random_base64_blob(value,min_len,max_len,min_diversity) [pub(crate)]: THE parameterized base64-blob skeleton. len in min..=max; standard_base64_shape(value) Some; reject if !has_padding && !length_multiple_of_four; admit = has_plus || has_slash || has_padding || (min_diversity!=0 && length_multiple_of_four && distinct_alnum>=min_diversity). looks_like_uniform_base64_blob(v)=is_random_base64_blob(v,44,600,32) (penalty path; suppression::shape::looks_like_standard_base64_blob shares it at 40..=80,div32). is_byte_distribution_base64_blob(value,min,max) [pub(crate)]: SEPARATE stricter skeleton for emit-drop paths; admit = (has_plus && has_slash) || (has_padding && (has_plus||has_slash)) — NO diversity admit, requires BOTH puncts; deliberately NOT folded into is_random_base64_blob (mutually-exclusive admit policies, MC-12).
- parse_protobuf_wire(data) [NOW pub(crate)]: const FIXED_WIRE_WIDTHS:[usize;8]=[0,8,0,0,0,4,0,0]. bail false if n<8. Loop: read_varint→tag; wire=tag&7, field_no=tag>>3 (false if 0); wire 0=varint (read another varint), wire 1|5=fixed (advance by FIXED_WIRE_WIDTHS[wire], bounds-checked), wire 2=len-delimited (read len varint, advance, bounds-checked), else false. fields++. Return i==n && fields>=3. read_varint: base-128 LE, &0x7F<<shift, stop on high-bit-clear, None if shift>63.
- µ-decstruct-01 (LANDED Iter 92, byte-identical Vector-7 one-owner DEDUP): the wire-type `1` (64-bit fixed) and `5` (32-bit fixed) match arms in parse_protobuf_wire had CHARACTER-IDENTICAL bodies — both `match i.checked_add(FIXED_WIRE_WIDTHS[wire as usize]) { Some(x) if x<=n => i=x, _ => return false }`. The per-width difference is ALREADY in the FIXED_WIRE_WIDTHS table (indexed by the runtime `wire`), so the two arms were a duplicated bounds-check body (drift hazard: a bounds fix in one arm could miss the other). Merged into one `1 | 5 => {…}` arm — byte-identical (same table lookup by runtime wire: 1→8, 5→4), unobservable through is_binary_payload / analyze / evidence. Widened parse_protobuf_wire to pub(crate) + added parse_protobuf_wire_for_test facade (testing.rs ROOT). Gap test tests/gap/decode_structure_protobuf_fixed_width_fields.rs (3 tests, EXACT bool assert): [0x09,0×8, 0x15,0×4, 0x18,0x01] (16B, field1/wire1 + field2/wire5 + field3/wire0, 3 fields, whole buffer) → true; [0x15,0,0,0] (wire5 declares 4B fixed but only 3 follow) → false (bounds advance fails closed); [0x09,0×8] (9B single wire1 field, fully consumed) → false (1 field < 3-field floor). 67th landing.

## µ-placeholder — placeholder_words.rs canonical placeholder vocabulary (Iter 94)
- Module: PORTABLE (no cfg). One canonical placeholder-word vocabulary (Tier-B rules/placeholder_words.toml, parsed once via LazyLock<Vec<PlaceholderWord>>) consumed by surface-form / decoded-form / doc-marker suppression so there's ONE list, not path-dependent copies. PlaceholderWord{lower:String, upper:String} (pub(crate)) with accessors lower()/upper()/lower_bytes()/is_example()(lower=="example"). PLACEHOLDER_WORDS LazyLock PANICS on invalid TOML ("refusing to run without placeholder suppression truth" — fail-closed, NOT a silent fallback). words()->&'static [PlaceholderWord]; example_word()=words().find(is_example).
- contains_placeholder_word(c)=…_with_entropy_hint(c, None). contains_placeholder_word_with_entropy_hint(c, hint): upper=c.to_ascii_uppercase(); words().any(|w| placeholder_word_suppresses(c, &upper, w.upper(), hint)). contains_non_example_placeholder_word_with_entropy_hint(c, upper, hint): same but filters out is_example first (takes a PRE-uppercased `upper` param — caller owns the alloc). bytes_contain_placeholder_word(bytes)=words().any(|w| ascii_ci::ci_find(bytes, w.lower_bytes())) — pure ci substring, no entropy gate. bytes_contain_entropy_placeholder_marker(bytes): hardcoded OR-chain of ci_find markers (your_/replace_me/change_me/insert_here/fake_/dummy_/mock_, secret_key&&len<20, AKIA-prefix && (EXAMPLE-suffix || "1234567890"), `<`/`>` present, or exact whole-buffer null/none/undefined/empty/default/secret/password) — Tier-B-violation candidate (hardcoded list) noted for a future migration, NOT this landing.
- placeholder_word_suppresses(credential, upper, token, hint) [NOW pub(crate)]: for each upper.match_indices(token): before=upper[..idx].chars().next_back(), after=upper[idx+token.len()..].chars().next(); left_boundary=before.is_none_or(|c| !c.is_alphanumeric()), right_boundary likewise. If neither boundary → false (continue scanning). If BOTH → true (always suppress). If exactly ONE → !looks_like_high_entropy_marker_collision(credential, hint). any() over all matches.
- looks_like_high_entropy_marker_collision(credential, hint): false if credential.len()<40 || !(contains('+')||contains('/')); entropy = hint.unwrap_or_else(|| entropy::shannon_entropy(credential.as_bytes())); return entropy >= HIGH_ENTROPY_MARKER_COLLISION_ENTROPY. parse_placeholder_words(raw)->Result<Vec<PlaceholderWord>,String>: with_capacity(words.len()); per word trim + non-empty + must-equal-lowercase + must-be-ascii-alphanumeric + BTreeSet dedup; err if final list empty.
- µ-placeholder-01 (LANDED Iter 94, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): the high-entropy collision floor was a bare `entropy >= 4.8` literal in looks_like_high_entropy_marker_collision (the single tuned threshold that decides whether a long +/-bearing credential is a REAL secret merely colliding with a placeholder substring vs an actual placeholder). Named it fn-local `const HIGH_ENTROPY_MARKER_COLLISION_ENTROPY: f64 = 4.8;` (single use, documents the bits/byte meaning). Same f64 comparison → unobservable through contains_placeholder_word / bytes_contain_placeholder_word. Widened placeholder_word_suppresses → pub(crate) (already used internally, no dead_code) + added placeholder_word_suppresses_for_test facade (testing.rs ROOT, uppercases the credential then calls through). Gap test tests/gap/placeholder_word_entropy_collision_threshold.rs (4 tests, EXACT bool assert): "foo example bar"/EXAMPLE/None → true (both-sided boundary always suppresses); "EXAMPLEKEY"/EXAMPLE/None → true (one-sided, len 10<40 so collision gate never engages); 47-char "EXAMPLEaB+/cD9z…"(×5, has +/)/EXAMPLE with hint Some(5.0) → suppress=false (5.0>=4.8 → real-secret collision, NOT suppressed); SAME credential with hint Some(4.0) → suppress=true (4.0<4.8 → still a placeholder). The 5.0-vs-4.0 pair pins the exact 4.8 cut the named const now spells. 68th landing.

## µ-caesar — decode/caesar.rs Caesar/ROT-N decoder (Iter 96)
- Module: cfg(feature="decode") (pub(crate) mod caesar in decode.rs:8). CaesarDecoder impl Decoder (name="caesar"): for each extracted candidate ≥ MIN_CAESAR_LEN(=16), emit decoded variants for the credential-shaped Caesar shifts. decode_chunk: bail Vec::new() if source_type contains "/caesar" (no self-recursion) OR is_source_code_path(path); compute credential_url_line_spans + private_key_material_spans; with_extracted_value_spans(chunk.data, |cands| …) — per candidate skip if candidate_inside_spans(url|privkey spans) OR len<16 OR !candidate_shape_invariant; else try_shift=matched_caesar_shifts(candidate); for shift in 1..=25 where try_shift[shift]: decoded=caesar_shift(candidate,shift); if looks_credential_shaped(&decoded) → push_decoded_text_chunk (NON-spliced, intentional — Caesar's value is the bare decoded candidate; splicing would multiply shifted-shape collisions).
- caesar_shift(input, shift:u8) -> String [pub(crate)]: String::with_capacity(input.len()); per char: 'A'..='Z' → base b'A', off=(ch-base+shift)%ALPHABET_LEN, (base+off) as char; 'a'..='z' → base b'a' same; else identity. Pure position-wise bijection (letters rotate, digits/punct fixed) — the soundness foundation for the rotated-prefix gate.
- ROTATED_PREFIX_AC LazyLock<Option<AhoCorasick>>: needles = for each confidence::KNOWN_PREFIXES P, for k in 1..=25: caesar_shift(P, 26-k) (= rot_{-k}(P)). SOUNDNESS: caesar_shift(c,k).contains(P) ⟺ c.contains(caesar_shift(P,26-k)), so "some shift of c contains a known prefix" ⟺ "c contains some needle" — exact, not a superset. AC build failure → warn_prefilter_disabled + None (LAW10 loud, matched_caesar_shifts falls back to all-25, recall-preserving). matched_caesar_shifts(candidate)->[bool;26] [pub(crate)]: Some(ac) → for m in ac.find_overlapping_iter(candidate): try_shift[(m.pattern().as_usize()%25)+1]=true (needle idx i → k=(i%25)+1; find_OVERLAPPING required since needles nest); None → all of 1..=25 true. [bool;26] is 25 shifts + index-0-unused (NOT alphabet size).
- candidate_shape_invariant(s)->bool [pub(crate)]: bytes.any(is_ascii_alphabetic) && has_digit_and_long_alnum_run(bytes) — the shift-INVARIANT half (digits are shift-identity, alnum-ness preserved), checked ONCE on the raw candidate to skip the 25× fan-out; true superset of per-shift looks_credential_shaped, recall-exact. has_digit_and_long_alnum_run(bytes) [private]: requires ≥1 ascii_digit AND a contiguous run ≥ MIN_ALNUM_RUN(=8) ascii_alphanumeric. looks_credential_shaped(s)->bool [pub(crate)]: has_digit_and_long_alnum_run(s) && KNOWN_PREFIXES.any(|p| s.contains(p)) — the prefix gate is the ONE thing a shift can newly satisfy, so it stays in the per-shift loop (NOT in candidate_shape_invariant). has_digit_and_long_alnum_run is shared VERBATIM by both gates (one owner, no drift).
- Path gates: source_path_matches(path, exts, filenames) — ZERO-alloc (ends_with_ignore_ascii_case for exts, path_basename_bytes + eq_ignore_ascii_case for filenames; the prior `.replace('\\',"/").to_ascii_lowercase()` was 2 heap allocs/chunk/decoder, Law-7 fixed). is_program_source_code_path = PROGRAM_SOURCE_CODE_EXTENSIONS(40+ exts)+SOURCE_CODE_FILENAMES(kconfig/makefile/cmakelists.txt). is_source_code_path = program OR CAESAR_TEXT_NOISE_EXTENSIONS(.md/.rst/.txt/.adoc) (kept SEPARATE so entropy doesn't inherit Caesar's broad "source"). line_has_credential_url(line): finds "://", scheme = ≥2 alpha (or +) bytes before it; userinfo = up to first / ? # ws; requires `:` before first `@`. credential_url_line_spans / private_key_block_spans (BEGIN…PRIVATE KEY…END) / encoded_private_key_payload_spans (≥128-char base64 run that decodes to a PEM private key) — all gate OUT candidates so Caesar doesn't out-resolve a plaintext URL/PEM.
- µ-caesar-01 (LANDED Iter 96, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): the Caesar letter-rotation modulus `26` was a bare literal at THREE genuinely-same-meaning sites — `caesar_shift(prefix, 26 - k)` (AC builder inverse shift) and the two `(ch - base + shift) % 26` (upper + lower rotation). Hoisted to module-level `const ALPHABET_LEN: u8 = 26;` at all three. CRITICAL Law-1 discrimination: the `[bool; 26]` shift table (matched_caesar_shifts) is sized `25 shifts + 1` — a COINCIDENTAL 26 with a DIFFERENT meaning — and was intentionally LEFT BARE (the const doc says so). Same u8 modulus → byte-identical rotation, unobservable through caesar_shift / decode_chunk. Gap test via a new caesar_shift_for_test facade (testing.rs ROOT, #[cfg(feature="decode")], calls crate::decode::caesar::caesar_shift — module pub(crate), fn pub(crate)): tests/gap/caesar_shift_rotation_exact.rs (5 tests, #![cfg(decode)], EXACT string assert): shift("ABC",1)="BCD"; shift("XYZ",3)="ABC" (Z-wrap); shift("Hello, World! 123",13)="Uryyb, Jbeyq! 123" (ROT13, digits/punct identity); shift(shift("hello",13),13)="hello" (ROT13 self-inverse); shift("BLJB",25)="AKIA" (the soundness-proof bijection example). 69th landing.

## µ-reverse — decode/reverse.rs reverse-string decoder (Iter 98)
- Module: cfg(feature="decode") (pub(crate) mod reverse in decode.rs:12). ReverseDecoder impl Decoder (name="reverse"): catches secrets reversed char-by-char to dodge byte-substring scans (e.g. `RNK1ESEMURKWESFEDBA-46AIKA` = `AKIA-64ABDEFSEWKRUMSEK1NR` reversed). Runs AFTER other decoders. decode_chunk: bail Vec::new() if source_type contains "/reverse" (reverse∘reverse=identity, no self-recursion); else with_extracted_value_spans → decode_candidate_refs_exact over candidates filtered by (value.len() >= MIN_REVERSE_LEN(=16) && !suppression::shape::looks_like_prefixed_hash_digest(value) && looks_reversible(value)), decode closure = Ok(reverse_str(s)).
- reverse_str(s) -> String [pub(crate)]: s.chars().rev().collect() — Unicode-SCALAR reversal (not byte; UTF-8-safe). REVERSED_KNOWN_PREFIXES LazyLock<Vec<String>>: confidence::KNOWN_PREFIXES filtered len>=3, each reverse_str'd. SOUNDNESS: reverse(candidate).contains(prefix) ⟺ candidate.contains(reverse(prefix)) (reverse is a position bijection) — so the gate avoids allocating a reversed copy for the common no-match case. The len>=3 filter deliberately drops the 2-char `0x` Ethereum prefix (random `0x` in ~1.6% of 80-char base64 routed spurious reversed blobs through; 4 FPs attributed; recall loss near zero since every 3+ char vendor prefix hf_/SG./eyJ/sk-/ghp_ still gates).
- looks_reversible(candidate) -> bool [pub(crate)]: TWO gates. (1) scan candidate.as_bytes() in REVERSED order accumulating a contiguous ascii_alphanumeric run; non-alnum resets run to 0; saw_long_run once run >= MIN_REVERSE_ALNUM_RUN(=12) → break; if !saw_long_run return false. (2) REVERSED_KNOWN_PREFIXES.any(|p| candidate.contains(p)) — the reversed text must carry a known provider prefix (stops monotonic `ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ`-style prose, which clears gate 1 but reverses to nothing meaningful, from emitting decoy chunks — Kimi-decode audit finding #4).
- µ-reverse-01 (LANDED Iter 98, byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const): the alnum-run threshold in looks_reversible was a bare `if run >= 12` literal — the same tuned-gate kind the sibling Caesar decoder already names `MIN_ALNUM_RUN=8`, and the doc already calls it "A 12+ ASCII alphanumeric run". Named it module-level `const MIN_REVERSE_ALNUM_RUN: usize = 12;` (single code site; the other `12` in the file is the doc comment). Same usize comparison → byte-identical admission, unobservable through looks_reversible / decode_chunk. Gap test via two new facades (reverse_str_for_test + looks_reversible_for_test in testing.rs ROOT, #[cfg(feature="decode")], call crate::decode::reverse::{reverse_str,looks_reversible} — module + fns pub(crate)): tests/gap/reverse_decoder_admission_gate.rs (4 tests, #![cfg(decode)], EXACT assert): reverse_str("ABCDEF")="FEDCBA"; reverse_str("héllo")="olléh" (Unicode-scalar, NOT byte — `é` survives); looks_reversible("AIKAABCDEFGH")=true (12-run + contains AIKA=reverse(AKIA)) vs looks_reversible("AIKAABCDEFG")=false (11-run < 12, fails gate 1 before the prefix check) — pins the EXACT 12 boundary; looks_reversible("ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ")=false (26-run clears gate 1 but no reversed-known-prefix → the documented Kimi-finding-#4 rejection). 70th landing.

## µ-sharedre — shared_regexes.rs shared assignment-detection regex (Iter 100)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/shared_regexes.rs (36 LOC, portable, regex is a BASE workspace dep at Cargo.toml:81 `regex = { workspace = true }` — NOT feature-gated, so ASSIGN_RE compiles under ci-lean). Module decl `pub(crate) mod shared_regexes;` at lib.rs:175.
- `pub(crate) static ASSIGN_RE: LazyLock<Option<Regex>>` (L19): compiles `(?i)([a-z0-9_-]{2,32})\s*[:=]\s*["'`]([a-zA-Z0-9/+=_-]{4,})["'`](?:;|,)?$`. On `Regex::new` Ok → `Some(re)`; on Err → calls `crate::prefilter_degrade::warn_prefilter_disabled("shared assignment regex (ASSIGN_RE)", &error)` then `None`. The Option<Regex> wrapper is a DELIBERATE LOUD-degrade (NOT Law-10 silent): warn_prefilter_disabled (prefilter_degrade.rs:29) is an unconditional `eprintln!` stating "PREFILTER DISABLED … recall is preserved but … slower … build-invariant violation; please report it" — the source is a compile-time-constant pattern so the Err arm is unreachable in practice; the wrapper exists so a future upstream `regex` validation-tightening degrades to "feature disabled + loud warning" rather than poisoning the LazyLock and panicking every worker.
- TWO OWNERS confirmed (the dedup target, kimi-dedup audit row #9): engine/scan_postprocess/fragments.rs:24 (`let Some(assign_re) = crate::shared_regexes::ASSIGN_RE.as_ref() else { … }` — cross-line fragment reassembly within one chunk) AND multiline/structural.rs:53 (`let Some(assign_re) = ASSIGN_RE.as_ref() else { … }`, imported at structural.rs:8 — the multi-line code-block preprocessor pass). Pre-consolidation the same regex source lived in BOTH files; single source now so the two scan paths can't drift silently.
- `pub(crate) fn warm_runtime_regexes()` (L33): `let _ = ASSIGN_RE.as_ref();` — forces eager LazyLock init (warm-up), carries its own `// LAW10:` annotation ("forces lazy-static/regex eager init (warm-up); not a fallback").
- PATTERN SEMANTICS (load-bearing for both scan paths): key class `[a-z0-9_-]{2,32}` with `(?i)` (so A-Z also admitted, capture preserves original case); separator `[:=]` (either `:` or `=`); delimiter any of `"` `'` backtick; value class `[a-zA-Z0-9/+=_-]{4,}` (base64-ish, ≥4 chars); end-anchored `$` with an optional single `;`/`,` tail via `(?:;|,)?`. NOTE structural.rs:360 documents a DELIBERATELY-LOOSER sibling path that admits single-char names (`a`,`b`) — ASSIGN_RE's `{2,32}` floor is NOT shared by that path (Law-1: don't couple the two key-length rules).
- LANDING: gap-test-ALONE (no byte-identical fix — the module is already single-source/deduped and 36 LOC clean; warn-degrade is correct). NEW facade testing.rs `pub fn assign_re_captures_for_test(line) -> Option<(String,String)>` returns the (key,value) groups or None (ASSIGN_RE is pub(crate), reachable crate-wide via `crate::shared_regexes::ASSIGN_RE`; integration test needs the pub-fn seam). Gap test shared_assign_regex_contract.rs (5 tests, 6 asserts) pins EXACT values: `api_key = "abcd1234"` → Some(("api_key","abcd1234")); `Token: 'S3cr3tV'` → Some(("Token","S3cr3tV")) (colon+singlequote+case-preserve); value `{4,}` floor (`kv="abcd"`→Some vs `kv="abc"`→None); key `{2,32}` floor (`a="abcd"`→None vs `ab="abcd"`→Some); `$` end-anchor (`… "abcd1234" trailing`→None) + optional `;`/`,` tail both admitted & excluded from the value group. Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs after scanner_src_files_exceed_standard_500_loc (alphabetical, sc<sh<si).

## µ-stripe — checksum/stripe.rs Stripe-key structural validator (Iter 102)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/checksum/stripe.rs (now ~38 LOC w/ the named consts; portable, NOT feature-gated — checksum mod is `pub mod checksum` at lib.rs:72, so validate_checksum/checksum_adjusted_confidence/ChecksumResult are all reachable from integration tests directly, NO facade needed). ci-lean compiles it.
- StripeTokenValidator (pub(crate) struct) impl ChecksumValidator::validate(&self, credential:&str) -> ChecksumResult: inline 6-prefix array [sk_live_, sk_test_, pk_live_, pk_test_, rk_live_, rk_test_]; `prefixes.iter().find_map(|p| credential.strip_prefix(p))` → None ⇒ ChecksumResult::NotApplicable; else payload = the post-prefix slice. payload.len() < MIN_STRIPE_PAYLOAD_LEN(=24) || > MAX_STRIPE_PAYLOAD_LEN(=128) ⇒ Invalid; !payload.chars().all(is_ascii_alphanumeric) ⇒ Invalid; else StructurallyValid. KEY DETECTION-TRUTH CONTRACT (doc L6-8): Stripe publishes NO checksum, so a valid shape is StructurallyValid NOT Valid — it must NOT receive the embedded-checksum confidence floor.
- ChecksumResult (checksum/mod.rs:24, `pub enum`, #[non_exhaustive], derives PartialEq/Eq/Copy): Valid | StructurallyValid | Invalid | NotApplicable. validate_checksum(credential) (pub fn mod.rs:60): iterate VALIDATORS (LazyLock<Vec<Box<dyn ChecksumValidator>>>, order: GithubClassic, GithubFineGrained, Npm, Slack, Pypi, Stripe, Gitlab); first NON-NotApplicable verdict wins; all NotApplicable ⇒ NotApplicable. (sk_/pk_/rk_ overlap NO other validator's prefix — github ghp_/github_pat_, npm npm_, pypi pypi-, slack xox, gitlab glpat- — so a Stripe shape routes unambiguously to StripeTokenValidator.)
- CONFIDENCE FLOW (checksum_adjusted_confidence, pub fn mod.rs:144 → confidence::policy::apply_checksum_confidence): Valid ⇒ floor at CHECKSUM_VALID_FLOOR(=0.9, mod.rs:103); StructurallyValid ⇒ confidence passes THROUGH unchanged (NO floor — this is the Stripe contract); Invalid ⇒ None (caller DROPS the match); NotApplicable ⇒ passthrough. So a Stripe `sk_live_…` shape at incoming 0.5 stays 0.5 (does NOT lift to 0.9 like a CRC-valid GitHub token would).
- LANDING (byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const, code↔doc coherence): the payload length window `payload.len() < 24 || payload.len() > 128` had TWO bare literals. Named module-level `const MIN_STRIPE_PAYLOAD_LEN: usize = 24;` (= the doc's "24+ alphanumeric chars") + `const MAX_STRIPE_PAYLOAD_LEN: usize = 128;` (sanity ceiling), each single-use, SAME usize values → byte-identical comparison, unobservable through validate(). One cohesive "name the payload window" change (like µ-caesar-01's ALPHABET_LEN). Declined coupling 24 to any detector-regex "24+" (cross-module Law-1 risk; kept contained).
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/stripe_token_checksum_contract.rs (7 tests, EXACT ChecksumResult + confidence values, through the REAL pub API): valid sk_live_+24-alnum → StructurallyValid (NOT Valid); checksum_adjusted_confidence(0.5, pk_live_+24) == Some(0.5) (THE no-floor contract — passthrough not lifted to 0.9); 24-floor (23→Invalid, 24→StructurallyValid); 128-ceiling (128→StructurallyValid, 129→Invalid); non-alnum payload (rk_live_+23a+`!`) → Invalid; all 6 prefixes → StructurallyValid; Invalid shape (sk_live_+23) → checksum_adjusted_confidence(0.9,…) == None (drop). Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs after static_intern_seed_source_types (alphabetical, st-a < st-r < structured).

- QUERY API (all pub(crate), all -> Result<_,String> threading the parse error): is_residual_weak_anchor(id) → rules.weak_anchor.contains(id) (HashSet O(1)); is_private_key_block_detector(id) → rules.private_key_block.contains(id); stripe_hot_confirmed_prefixes() → rules.stripe_hot_confirmed_prefix.as_slice() (&'static [String], order-preserving); validate() → classification_rules().map(|_| ()) (startup fail-closed entry). THESE FOUR had ZERO behavioral coverage — the module's own #[cfg(test)] tests (live_classification_rules_parse, parse_rejects_{duplicate_detector_ids,unknown_detector_ids,duplicate_prefixes}) exercise ONLY parse_classification_rules directly, never the cached query path. (The tests/unit/gates/detector_id_owner.rs grep hits are a SOURCE-SHAPE gate asserting the owner module *contains* these fn names, NOT behavioral.)
- VALIDATORS: validate_classification_ids(rule_name, ids, valid_ids) (private): per-id empty-trim check → "has an empty detector id"; local HashSet `seen` dup check → "lists detector '…' more than once"; then detector_catalog::validate_rule_detector_ids (catalog membership). validate_prefixes(rule_name, prefixes) (private): per-prefix empty → "has an empty prefix"; trim!=self → "has surrounding whitespace"; !is_ascii → "is not ASCII"; local HashSet dup → "lists prefix … more than once". Both share the `seen`-HashSet dup-detection IDIOM but interleave it single-pass with their OWN per-item checks (empty/whitespace/ascii vs catalog) → NOT a clean one-owner extract; splitting would change single-pass error precedence (Law-1/refactor risk) — DECLINED a dedup.
- LANDING: gap-test-ALONE (no clean byte-identical fix — no magic numbers, no stale doc; the only shared idiom is the dup-HashSet which can't be extracted byte-identically). NEW facades testing.rs (4 pub fn, forward verbatim to the pub(crate) query fns): detector_is_residual_weak_anchor_for_test, detector_is_private_key_block_for_test, detector_stripe_hot_confirmed_prefixes_for_test (maps the &'static [String] via <[String]>::to_vec → Vec<String>), detector_classification_validate_for_test. Gap test detector_classification_query_contract.rs (4 tests, EXACT values vs the LIVE bundled TOML): validate()==Ok(()); is_weak_anchor("flickr-api-key")==Ok(true) & ("datadog-api-key")==Ok(true) & ("ssh-private-key")==Ok(false) [cross-bucket: a private-key-block id is NOT a weak anchor] & ("definitely-not-a-classified-id")==Ok(false); is_pk_block for the 3 private-key ids ==Ok(true) & ("flickr-api-key")==Ok(false) [cross-bucket]; stripe_prefixes()==Ok(vec!["sk_live_","sk_test_","rk_live_","rk_test_"]) [EXACT ordered slice, Vec preserves TOML order]. ## µ-registry — decode/pipeline/registry.rs decoder registry + per-decoder profiler (Iter 106)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/decode/pipeline/registry.rs (235 LOC now; private `mod registry` at pipeline.rs:171; pipeline is private `mod pipeline` at decode/mod.rs:11; decode is `pub mod decode` at lib.rs:82). decode-feature path, ci-lean-verifiable.
- DECODERS static OnceLock<RwLock<Vec<Arc<dyn Decoder>>>> (parking_lot::RwLock); decoder_registry() (private) get_or_init(RwLock::new(default_decoders())). default_decoders() (private) -> Vec<Arc<dyn Decoder>> = EXACTLY 13 in ORDER: Base64("base64"), Hex("hex"), Url("url"), QuotedPrintable("quoted-printable"), HtmlNamedEntity("html-named-entity"), HtmlNumericEntity("html-numeric-entity"), OctalEscape("octal-escape"), MimeEncodedWord("mime-encoded-word"), Json("json"), UnicodeEscape("unicode-escape"), Z85("z85"), Reverse("reverse"), Caesar("caesar"). ORDER load-bearing: reverse+caesar run LAST (their µ-blocks document "runs after the other decoders") — the structural decoders claim a chunk first; the evasion decoders only fire on what's left. (The Html*/Octal/Unicode names are macro-generated by `simple_decoder!` in url.rs:182.)
- register_decoder(Box<dyn Decoder>) is `pub fn` (the public extension API) — dedup by name (tracing::warn! + ignore on duplicate), Arc::from(decoder), push to the global RwLock write. active_decoders() pub(super): non-test = decoder_registry().read().clone(); cfg(test) ALSO extends with THREAD_DECODERS (thread-local, for ScopedDecoderRegistration test isolation). register_thread_decoder/ScopedDecoderRegistration are #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) (scoped, Drop-removes by rposition name match).
- PROFILER (measurement-only, `keyhog scan --profile`): DECODER_NS + DECODER_PRODUCED are `[AtomicU64; MAX_PROFILED_DECODERS]` (was bare 16) — per-decoder ns + sub-chunks-emitted accumulators. record_decoder_run(idx, elapsed, produced) pub(super): early-return if idx >= MAX_PROFILED_DECODERS (so a decoder past slot 16 is silently un-profiled — the headroom over 13 is what keeps that from biting). decoder_profile_dump() pub(crate): swap-reset + sort desc by ms then by produced, eprintln the per-decoder time% + emitted% tables; bails if total==0 && prod_total==0. decoder_profile_reset() pub(crate). profile_enabled() pub(super) delegates engine::profile::enabled().
- LANDING (byte-identical Vector-6 magic→named-const, 7 sites, ONE meaning): the profiler slot count `16` was bare at SEVEN sites — `[AtomicU64; 16]` ×2 (DECODER_NS + DECODER_PRODUCED types), `[Z; 16]` ×2 (their array-repeat initializers), `decoder_index >= 16` (record_decoder_run guard), `names.len().min(16)` ×2 (decoder_profile_dump times + produced). All SAME usize 16, SAME meaning (the fixed profiler capacity). Named module-level `const MAX_PROFILED_DECODERS: usize = 16;` at all 7 → byte-identical (array sizing with a const usize is valid), unobservable through any public API. Law-1: the 13 default-decoder COUNT is a SEPARATE number (NOT coupled — 16 is capacity-with-headroom, 13 is the current set).
- TEST ENABLEMENT (mirrors the existing decoder_profile_dump re-export chain): new `pub(crate) fn default_decoder_names()` in registry.rs (maps default_decoders().name(), default_decoders stays private) → re-exported `pub(crate) use registry::{…, default_decoder_names}` in pipeline.rs:177 → `pub(crate) use pipeline::{…, default_decoder_names}` in decode/mod.rs:22 → testing.rs facade `pub fn default_decoder_names_for_test() -> Vec<&'static str>` (#[cfg(feature="decode")]).

The CLI owns the public exit-code table; these MIRROR it.

Value-identical across the u8(cli)/i32(scanner) boundary. The mirror is SOURCE-STRING-checked cross-crate by cli/tests/contract/exit_codes_single_owner.rs::scanner_require_gpu_hard_exit_matches_cli_exit_contract (asserts process_exit.rs CONTAINS `REQUIRE_GPU_UNMET_EXIT_CODE: i32 = 12` + `BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE_EXIT_CODE: i32 = 3` formatted from the CLI consts) AND that NO scanner file OUTSIDE process_exit.rs contains `process::exit(` (single-owner hard-exit contract).
- FNs (pub(crate), -> ! never-type): require_gpu_unmet(message: impl AsRef<str>) + backend_unavailable(message: impl AsRef<str>). Both eprintln!("keyhog: {}", message.as_ref()) then std::process::exit(CODE). NOT behaviorally runnable in-test (they terminate the process) — only the CONSTANTS are value-testable.
- LANDING (Vector-7 one-owner DEDUP, contained, unobservable): the two fns duplicated `eprintln!("keyhog: {}", message.as_ref()); std::process::exit(CODE)` verbatim. Extracted private `fn exit_with(code: i32, message: impl AsRef<str>) -> !` (the ONE owner of the `keyhog: <msg>` notice + exit shape); the two pub(crate) fns become thin wrappers `exit_with(NAMED_CONST, message)`. Behavior byte-identical (same prefix, same eprintln, same exit code), signatures unchanged → unobservable through the public API. SAFE vs all gates: the cli contract test's const-string check is unchanged (consts untouched); its `process::exit(` scan SKIPS process_exit.rs (helper_path continue) so the single remaining `std::process::exit(code)` inside the helper is fine; the inline-numeric-exit gate passes (arg is `code`, not a digit); process_exit_non_empty (>=20 bytes, no todo!) + no_unwrap_expect + no_inline_tests all still pass. Aligns with the module's single-owner ethos (the `keyhog: ` prefix now can't drift between the two exit paths).
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/process_exit_code_contract.rs (2 tests, EXACT values, via new testing.rs facade `pub fn process_exit_codes_for_test() -> (i32,i32)` reading the two pub(crate) consts): scanner_hard_exit_codes_match_documented_contract → (12, 3) EXACTLY (pins the COMPILED scanner-side values — complements the CLI's SOURCE-STRING check with a real value assertion); the_two_hard_exit_codes_are_distinct → 12 != 3 (the two hard-stop reasons must be distinguishable by exit code — an invariant NOT checked scanner-side before). Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs between prefix_trie_propagation_move_on_last and profile_relaxed_ordering_single_reference (alphabetical, proc < prof).

## µ-deadcadence — deadline.rs cadence gate + the hot-loop re-check cadence (Iter 110)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/deadline.rs (62→71 LOC; `mod deadline` portable, ci-lean-verifiable, no cfg gates). Owns the wall-clock-timeout machinery shared by every hot scan loop. FNs: expired(Option<Instant>)->bool (`deadline.is_some_and(|d| Instant::now() >= d)`); cadence_tick(iteration,cadence)->bool (`iteration > 0 && iteration.is_multiple_of(cadence)` — SINGLE owner of the "is this iteration a re-check boundary" gate, already pinned by deadline_cadence_tick_dedup.rs); expired_on_cadence(deadline,iteration,cadence)->bool = `cadence_tick(..) && expired(..)`; struct LoopDeadline{start:Instant,budget:Duration} with from_deadline(Option<Instant>)->Option<Self> (clamps negative remaining to Duration::ZERO) + expired(self)->bool (`budget.is_zero() || start.elapsed() >= budget`, the CHEAP monotonic variant that avoids a per-call Instant::now subtraction); loop_expired(Option<LoopDeadline>)->bool; loop_expired_on_cadence(..)= `cadence_tick(..) && loop_expired(..)`. All pub(crate), all #[inline] except the consts/struct.
- LANDING (Vector-6 magic→named-const + Vector-7 one-owner DEDUP, byte-identical, cross-file, unobservable): the re-check cadence `64` was a BARE LITERAL copied across FIVE hot-loop sites in FOUR files — phase2_anchor_scan.rs:94 (loop_expired_on_cadence(loop_deadline,iters,64)), extract.rs:178 + extract.rs:317 (loop_expired_on_cadence(loop_deadline,match_count,64)), phase2_generic.rs:140 (expired_on_cadence(deadline,line_iter,64)) + phase2_generic.rs:177 (expired_on_cadence(deadline,capture_iter,64)). All five mean the SAME thing: an inner scan loop bounded by MAX_INNER_LOOP_ITERS that re-checks the deadline once per 64 iterations to honor the timeout without an Instant::now() per iter. Introduced `pub(crate) const HOT_LOOP_DEADLINE_CADENCE: usize = 64;` in deadline.rs (the one owner, right after cadence_tick) and replaced all five literals with `crate::deadline::HOT_LOOP_DEADLINE_CADENCE`. Compiled value unchanged (64 everywhere) → byte-identical, no public-API change. Law-1 DISCRIMINATION: phase2_compiled_anchored.rs:278 uses a DELIBERATELY tighter `16` cadence — left as a distinct literal, NOT coupled (different meaning: that phase's per-candidate test loop wants finer deadline granularity).
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/hot_loop_deadline_cadence_single_owner.rs (3 tests, EXACT values, via new testing.rs facade `pub fn hot_loop_deadline_cadence_for_test() -> usize` reading the pub(crate) const, plus the existing expired_on_cadence_now_for_test / loop_expired_on_cadence_now_for_test which drive each wrapper with an already-reached `Instant::now()` deadline so the result IS the cadence gate): hot_loop_cadence_is_exactly_64 → == 64 (pins the COMPILED value); both_wrappers_tick_only_on_nonzero_multiples_of_the_shared_cadence → over iterations 0..=2*cadence+1, BOTH expired_on_cadence and loop_expired_on_cadence equal `iteration>0 && iteration%cadence==0` at EVERY iteration (proves both hot-loop wrapper families fire on the same boundary as the named const); the_two_boundaries_in_the_first_two_windows_are_64_and_128 → the firing iterations across two windows are EXACTLY vec![64,128]. Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs after homoglyph_expand_exact_and_reserved, before inline_gate (alphabetical: homoglyph < hot < inline).
