## µ-cprefix — compiler/compiler_build.rs prefix-compiler + the 4 AC-literal tuning thresholds (Iter 114)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/compiler/compiler_build.rs (402→~413 LOC; `pub(crate) mod compiler_build` at compiler.rs:5, `pub(crate) mod compiler` at lib.rs:74; portable, ci-lean-verifiable). Turns &[DetectorSpec] into a CompileState (ac_literals:Vec<String>, ac_map:Vec<CompiledPattern>, phase2_patterns:Vec<(CompiledPattern,Vec<String>)>, companions:Vec<Vec<CompiledCompanion>>, quality_warnings:Vec<String>) — all pub(crate) fields.
- build_compile_state(detectors) pub(crate) -> Result<CompileState>: Phase 1 is a rayon par_iter over detectors deriving PatternArtifacts{compiled, prefixes, homoglyph_phase2, inner_literals, no_literal_no_keyword} per pattern (AST parse + homoglyph DFA compile are the cost, independent per pattern → parallel); Phase 2 is a SERIAL drain preserving detector-then-pattern order + within a pattern homoglyph-variants-first, so the compiled scanner is byte-identical to a serial build. The dedup-count debug line is gated behind tracing::enabled!(DEBUG) and BORROWS regex sources (no heap clone of ~1000 strings on the non-debug path — every CLI/daemon recompile). Routing: prefixes non-empty → push each to ac_literals+ac_map; else inner_literals non-empty → push those (moves `_AKIA`-style infix detectors from O(m×n) phase-2 to O(n) AC); else → phase2_patterns + a quality_warning when no_literal_no_keyword.
- HELPERS: rewrite_alternation_prefix (pub(crate); rewrites a leading `(?:p1|p2)body` so the matched branch's prefix becomes expanded_prefix, REFUSES bare `(` capturing groups + singleton non-alternations to avoid the flutterwave bare-prefix FP), rewrite_homoglyph_literal_prefix (pub(crate); tries body-prefix rewrite, then leading-boundary-guard rewrite, then falls back to rewrite_alternation_prefix), rewrite_homoglyph_body_prefix/strip_literal_prefix_source/split_top_level_alternatives (private), split_leading_inline_flag (pub(crate); splits `(?ims)`-style inline-flag head). ALL the pub(crate) helpers + split_leading_inline_flag + the rewrite_* fns are ALREADY tested (crates/scanner/tests/unit/compiler_tests.rs + tests/unit/a4_lr2/compiler_alt_*.rs reach them via keyhog_scanner::testing::) — NOT re-covered here (NO DUPLICATION).
- LANDING (Vector-6 magic→named-const, byte-identical, unobservable): the homoglyph min-prefix gate `if prefix.len() < 3` (the lone BARE literal among the prefix compiler's named tuning thresholds) → `pub(crate) const MIN_HOMOGLYPH_PREFIX_LEN: usize = 3;` used at the gate. Compiled value unchanged (3) → byte-identical. Matches the established sibling convention in compiler_prefix.rs (MIN_INNER_LITERAL_CHARS=4 @744, MIN_DISTINCTIVE_INFIX_CHARS=8 @840, MAX_CHARCLASS_PREFIX_EXPANSION=8 @213). Law-1 DISCRIMINATION: compiler_prefix.rs:417 `a.len() >= 3` is a DIFFERENT meaning (min char-class `[x]` source length) — coincidentally 3, NOT coupled.
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/compiler_prefix_tuning_thresholds_exact.rs (5 tests, EXACT values, via 4 NEW testing.rs facades compiler_{min_homoglyph_prefix_len,min_inner_literal_chars,min_distinctive_infix_chars,max_charclass_prefix_expansion}_for_test() each returning the pub(crate) const through the full crate::compiler::… path): homoglyph_prefix_floor_is_three → ==3; inner_literal_floor_is_four → ==4; distinctive_infix_floor_is_eight → ==8; charclass_prefix_expansion_cap_is_eight → ==8; homoglyph_prefix_floor_is_strictly_below_inner_literal_floor → MIN_HOMOGLYPH_PREFIX_LEN < MIN_INNER_LITERAL_CHARS (3<4, the real semantic invariant: a homoglyph variant splices back into the FULL regex so its prefix floor can be shorter than a standalone inner literal's). NONE of the 4 values was exact-pinned before (MIN_INNER_LITERAL_CHARS only appeared in a comment; the 2 exposed consts were used as `+1`, never asserted equal to 8). Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs after compiler_prefix_inline_tests_in_src, before confidence_calibration_uncalibrated_passthrough (alphabetical: compiler_prefix_inline < compiler_prefix_tuning < confidence).

## µ-bguard — compiler_prefix.rs leading boundary-guard splitter (Iter 116)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/compiler/compiler_prefix.rs (893L; `pub(crate) mod compiler_prefix` at compiler.rs:7; portable, ci-lean-verifiable). The AC-literal prefix extractor. Many helpers are pub(crate) + already tested via keyhog_scanner::testing:: (extract_literal_prefix ×12, extract_literal_prefixes ×9, extract_inner_literals ×10, is_escaped_literal ×3 test files). CFG-GATED SIBLINGS SKIPPED THIS ITER: entropy/fast_x86.rs is `#[cfg(target_arch="x86_64")]` + `#[target_feature(avx2/sse2)]` unsafe SIMD (has_high_entropy_fast_x86 is ALSO #[cfg(test)]) → accel + host-feature-dependent, NOT a clean ci-lean landing; hw_probe/platform.rs is `#[cfg(target_os=…)]` per-OS → CAPTURE-only.
- split_leading_boundary_guard(pattern:&str) pub(crate) -> Option<(&str,&str)>: strip_prefix("(?:")?; scan body for the matching depth-0 `)` (tracks `\\` escapes + `[...]` class interior so a `)` inside a class doesn't close the group); guard_end = "(?:".len()+end+1; returns None if group empty OR rest empty; then splits the group at top-level `|` (depth-0, outside class) and requires EVERY alt (trimmed) to be a boundary token (`^ $ \b \B \w \W \s \S \d \D \A \z`) OR a char class (`a.starts_with('[') && a.ends_with(']') && a.len() >= 3` — NOTE this `>= 3` is the SAME distinct charclass-min-len as the Iter-114 Law-1 note, NOT the homoglyph floor). Returns BORROWED slices (guard, rest) of the input — zero alloc; guard+rest reconstruct the input exactly (lossless). strip_leading_boundary_guard(pattern) pub(crate) -> Option<&str> = split_leading_boundary_guard(pattern).map(|(_,rest)| rest) — the rest half only.
- WHY load-bearing: lets the compiler see PAST the `(?:^|[^A-Za-z0-9_])` boundary-guard idiom to pull the real following literal (AKIA…) into the O(n) AC prefilter; PRECISION is critical — it must NOT fire on a genuine prefix alternation `(?:ghp_|github_pat_)` (stripping that would drop a real token prefix and route the detector to the slow O(m×n) phase-2). Used by compiler_build.rs rewrite_homoglyph_literal_prefix (split) + extract_literal_prefixes path (strip).
- LANDING: gap-test-alone (NO code change — the fn is correct, just UNTESTED: split_leading_boundary_guard had 0 test files, the lone uncovered pub(crate) helper among its siblings; strip had 1 but never pinned the guard half or the strip==split.rest relationship). No clean byte-identical fix needed (the `>=3` charclass len is single-use + Law-1-distinct from MIN_HOMOGLYPH_PREFIX_LEN; declined naming it as gratuitous).
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/boundary_guard_split_lossless_and_precise.rs (3 tests, EXACT values, via 2 new testing.rs facades split_leading_boundary_guard_for_test -> Option<(String,String)> + strip_leading_boundary_guard_for_test -> Option<String>, both .to_string() so no internal lifetime leaks to the integration test): boundary_guard_idiom_splits_losslessly → split("(?:^|[^A-Za-z0-9_])AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}") == Some(("(?:^|[^A-Za-z0-9_])","AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}")) AND guard+rest == the input (lossless) AND strip == Some("AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}") (strip==split.rest); real_prefix_alternation_is_not_a_boundary_guard → split AND strip of "(?:ghp_|github_pat_)[A-Za-z0-9_]{36}" both None (the precision property — a real GitHub-token prefix alternation is left intact); pattern_without_leading_group_is_untouched → split AND strip of "AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}" both None (no leading `(?:`). Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs after bigram_bloom_log_bit_count_coherence, before caesar_shift_rotation_exact (alphabetical: bigram < boundary < caesar).

Matches the file's established `_from_cpuinfo`/`_from_meminfo` seam pattern (architecture coherence, NOT a new abstraction).

## µ-b64dist — decode_structure.rs is_byte_distribution_base64_blob emit-drop gate (Iter 120)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/decode_structure.rs (458L; `pub(crate) mod decode_structure` at lib.rs:85, UNCONDITIONAL — no feature gate; `pub mod decode` at lib.rs:82 also unconditional, so ci-lean-verifiable). Decode-structure analysis feeding scoring/suppression. pub(crate) fns: is_random_base64_blob (penalty path), looks_like_uniform_base64_blob, is_byte_distribution_base64_blob, decodes_to_printable_text, analyze, evidence, parse_protobuf_wire — most covered EXCEPT is_byte_distribution_base64_blob (0 test refs; only 2 src callers in suppression/shape/canonical.rs: looks_like_entropy_random_base64_blob_decoy band (50,300) + looks_like_generic_random_base64_blob_decoy band (40,300)).
- is_byte_distribution_base64_blob(value:&str, min_len:usize, max_len:usize)->bool #[must_use]: (1) `(min_len..=max_len).contains(&value.len())` else false; (2) `crate::decode::standard_base64_shape(value)` Some else false (rejects mixed/url-safe alphabet, bad remainders — `has_urlsafe || (padded && len%4!=0) || (!padded && len%4==1)` => None); (3) `!has_padding && !length_multiple_of_four` => false; (4) ADMIT iff `(has_plus && has_slash) || (has_padding && (has_plus || has_slash))`. The EMIT-DROP sibling of is_random_base64_blob with a MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE admit policy (MC-12): the penalty path admits on +-OR-/ OR padding OR diversity-wedge; THIS gate demands a genuine byte-distribution signal (BOTH punctuation, or padded-with-one) so it does NOT bite single-punctuation restricted-secret-key positives (real provider tokens are pure base62, no +/, no padding; a uniform random-byte payload almost always yields both + and /). Pure deterministic fn of (value,min,max).
- LANDING: gap-test-alone (NO code change — the gate is correct, just ZERO direct coverage despite being a load-bearing precision gate). Declined any fix (the (50,300)/(40,300) bands live in the callers, not here; no magic to name in the gate itself).
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/byte_distribution_base64_blob_admit_policy_exact.rs (7 tests, EXACT bool decisions, via new testing.rs facade is_byte_distribution_base64_blob_for_test(value,min_len,max_len)->bool; all vectors python-verified against the traced shape+admit logic): both_punctuation_marks_admit → "A"*42+"+/" (44, mult-4, +&/) band(40,300)==true; a_single_punctuation_mark_is_not_dropped → "A"*43+"+" ==false (THE MC-12 precision property: single-punct positive survives); padding_with_one_punctuation_admits → "A"*41+"/==" ==true (padding clause); pure_alphanumeric_blob_is_not_admitted → "A"*44 ==false (base62 token shape, no signal); length_outside_the_band_is_rejected → "A"*42+"+/" band(50,300)==false (44<50, length gate first); url_safe_alphabet_is_rejected → "A"*42+"-_" ==false (standard_base64_shape None on url-safe); unpadded_non_multiple_of_four_is_rejected → "A"*41+"+/" (43 chars, both punct but neither padded nor mult-4) ==false (step-3 structural pre-gate). Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs after boundary_guard_split_lossless_and_precise, before caesar_shift_rotation_exact (alphabetical: boundary < byte < caesar).

## µ-probgate — probabilistic_gate.rs looks_promising bigram-distribution branch (Iter 124)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/probabilistic_gate.rs (113L; `pub(crate) mod probabilistic_gate` at lib.rs:145, portable/ci-lean-verifiable). The fast statistical pre-ML noise gate. `ProbabilisticGate::looks_promising(s:&str)->bool` (pub(crate), already pub via `pub(crate) mod` — NO visibility widen needed) is a layered screen: (1) `s.len()<16` → true (passthrough); (2) distinct-byte diversity count over `seen=[false;256]`, early-break at 5 distinct; (3) UUID branch: `32<=len<=40 && dash_count==4 && 8-4-4-4-12 all-hex && part_count==5` → false; (4) `count<5` → false (extreme low diversity like `aaaa…`); (5) **bigram-distribution branch** (`bytes.len()>=32`): builds a 512-bit bitset over `bigram_slot_512(a,b)=((a*33)^(b*17)^(a<<1)^(b>>3))&0x1ff` for each adjacent pair, counts distinct slots, rejects iff `distinct<8 || distinct<len/4` → false; else → true. 3 src callers: adjudicate/mod.rs:72,175 + confidence/policy.rs:352 (all `!looks_promising → suppress as ProbabilisticGateNotPromising`).
- COVERAGE GAP: tests/unit/inline_migrated/probabilistic_gate_inline.rs has 4 tests (realistic PAT pass, dashed-UUID reject [branch 3], `<16` passthrough [branch 1], `aaaa…` reject) — but ALL FOUR SHADOW branch 5: `aaaa…` has 1 distinct byte so dies at branch 4 (`count<5`) BEFORE the bigram screen; the dashed-UUID dies at branch 3; the realistic PAT passes through. The bigram-distribution branch (the sophisticated load-bearing screen, ~13 LOC) had ZERO direct coverage. Note `distinct<8` is subsumed by `distinct<len/4` for all len>=32 (len/4>=8), so the effective gate is `distinct<len/4`.
- LANDING: gap-test-alone (NO code change — gate is correct; no magic/dup to fix here, the (50,300)-style bands are absent and the slot constants are intrinsic to the hash). New testing.rs facade `pub fn probabilistic_gate_looks_promising_for_test(value:&str)->bool` (top-level plain pub, reachable from gap_floor integration test — distinct from the `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) ProbabilisticGate` struct at testing.rs:2046 used by the in-crate inline_migrated suite).
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/probabilistic_gate_bigram_diversity_gate_exact.rs (3 tests, EXACT bools; ALL vectors modelled against the exact bigram_slot_512 mix in python BEFORE writing — distinct-slot counts confirmed): bigram_diversity_gate_rejects_low_distribution_at_or_above_32 → `"abcde"*8` (40B, 5 distinct bytes survive branch 4, only 5 distinct bigrams ab/bc/cd/de/ea, no dash → branch 5: 5<10) ==false + `"abcde"*6+"ab"` (exactly 32B, 5<8) ==false [pins branch-5 engagement at the 32 boundary]; len_below_thirty_two_does_not_engage_the_bigram_screen → `"abcde"*6+"a"` (31B, same low-bigram content but len<32 → screen skipped, count=5 passes) ==true [proves the reject is the length-gated bigram branch, not content alone]; sufficient_bigram_diversity_and_real_tokens_are_promising → "Xq7Lm2Zp9Rt4Wc8Bn1Vd6Kf3Gj0Hs5Yu2AeLoQ" (38B, d=37) ==true + 64-hex SHA-256 digest (d=50, the documented "SHAs pass" case) ==true + padded base64 token (44B, d=41) ==true. ## µ-normkw — engine/phase2_generic/keywords.rs normalize + secret-suffix pair (Iter 128)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_generic/keywords.rs (`pub(crate) mod engine`@lib.rs:91 → `pub(crate) mod phase2_generic`@engine.rs:93 → `pub(crate) mod keywords`@phase2_generic.rs:5; portable/ci-lean). The generic-bridge assignment-keyword pipeline (module deep-read as µ-p2gk Iter18, but these two fns never DIRECTLY pinned). Both pub(crate) already (NO widen).
- normalize_assignment_keyword(keyword:&str)->Option<String>: iterate bytes; ascii-alphanumeric → push lowercase (last_was_sep=false); `_`/`-`/`.` AND normalized non-empty AND !last_was_sep → push '_' (last_was_sep=true); ANY OTHER byte (space, symbol, non-ASCII) → silently DROPPED (no sep, last_was_sep unchanged). After loop: if ends_with('_') pop it. `(!normalized.is_empty()).then_some(normalized)`. Net: case-fold + collapse each `_-.` run to one '_' + drop leading sep (empty-guard) + pop trailing sep + drop unknown bytes; empty → None. Folds SEGMENT_WRITE_KEY / segment-write-key / segment.write.key → "segment_write_key".
- normalized_assignment_keyword_has_secret_suffix(normalized:&str)->bool: `matches!(normalized.rsplit('_').next(), Some("key"|"secret"|"token"|"password"|"passwd"|"pwd"))` (LAST `_`-segment exact) OR `ends_with("key"|"secret"|"token"|"password")` (substring). ASYMMETRY: passwd/pwd match ONLY as a full last-segment, NOT as a trailing substring; key/secret/token/password match either way. ends_with is SUBSTRING not word-boundary (so "monkey" → true via ends_with "key"). Caller: candidate_starts_with_owned_assignment_key@generic_keyword_owner.rs:160 (gates the owned-keyword secret-suffix requirement).
- COVERAGE: only a COMMENT in tests/unit/file_gate.rs:1069 referenced normalize (via assignment_keyword_for_line, indirect); ZERO direct exact-value tests for either fn (grep tests/ for `normalize_assignment_keyword(` / `normalized_assignment_keyword_has_secret_suffix(` → none).
- LANDING: gap-test-alone (NO code change — both correct; no magic/dup). Two new top-level testing.rs facades normalize_assignment_keyword_for_test→Option<String> + normalized_assignment_keyword_has_secret_suffix_for_test→bool (OWNED, plain pub).
- GAP TEST crates/scanner/tests/gap/assignment_keyword_normalize_and_secret_suffix_exact.rs (5 tests, 25 vectors, EXACT via .as_deref()/bool; ALL python-traced ALL_OK BEFORE writing): normalize_folds_every_separator_spelling_to_one_token (3 spellings→segment_write_key, API_KEY→api_key, MixedCase123→mixedcase123, a.b-c_d→a_b_c_d); normalize_collapses_runs_trims_edges_and_drops_unknown_bytes (api__key→api_key [run collapse], _leading→leading, trailing_→trailing, "key name"→keyname [space dropped]); normalize_yields_none_when_nothing_survives (""→None, "___"→None); secret_suffix_matches_last_segment_or_trailing_substring (segment_write_key/api_secret/auth_token/db_password/x_passwd/x_pwd/apikey/monkey all true — monkey pins the substring ends_with); secret_suffix_rejects_non_credential_keys (segment/service_name/x_pwd_extra [pwd not LAST seg]/passwordx/mypasswd [passwd NOT a substring suffix] all false). Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs after anchored_candidate_verify_single_owner, before aws_finding_metadata_reexport_decodes (alphabetical: anchored < assignment < aws).

Membership goes through normalize→has_secret_suffix→binary_search (the µ-ownedbs chain).

## µ-b64shape — decode/base64.rs standard_base64_shape (Iter 156 — 99th LANDING)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/decode/base64.rs:99 (pub(crate), re-exported crate::decode@mod.rs:18). SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the standard (non-url-safe) base64 shape consumed by looks_like_uniform_base64_blob(198) + is_byte_distribution_base64_blob(223) — a drift in its alphabet/padding/remainder rules silently changes those penalty/emit-drop gates. PRIOR COVERAGE = source-shape gates ONLY (file_gate.rs:429 string-match, decode_base64_classifier_hot_path_shape.rs body-grep); RETURN VALUES untested. DECLINE-checked siblings looks_like_uniform_base64_blob (adversarial round1 test) + is_byte_distribution_base64_blob (gap test) — both already covered, so pinned the shared primitive instead.
- scan_base64_candidate(c) (188-231): per-byte; alnum&!padded→distinct_alnum++ (value-deduped via seen[256]); '+'&!padded→has_standard+has_plus; '/'&!padded→has_standard+has_slash; '-'|'_'&!padded→has_urlsafe; '='→index0 None / padded=true / padding_len++ / >2 None; padded&non-'='→None; other byte→None. standard_base64_shape(c) (99-117): facts=scan?; has_standard&&has_urlsafe→None (mixed-alphabet, BEFORE remainder); rem=len%4; has_urlsafe||(padded&&rem!=0)||(!padded&&rem==1)→None; else Some{has_padding=padded, length_multiple_of_four=len%4==0, has_plus, has_slash, distinct_alnum}.
- LANDING: gap-test-ALONE (already pub(crate) + re-exported). Facade standard_base64_shape_for_test(candidate:&str)->Option<(bool,bool,bool,bool,u32)> mapping (has_padding,length_multiple_of_four,has_plus,has_slash,distinct_alnum) — owned tuple, struct stays crate-internal.
- GAP TEST gap/standard_base64_shape_classification_exact.rs (5 tests, EXACT tuples): plain ("ABCD"→Some(F,T,F,F,4); "AAAB"→Some(F,T,F,F,2) [dedup]); both-punct ("AB+/"→Some(F,T,T,T,2)); padding ("AB=="→Some(T,T,F,F,2); "ABCDEF=="→Some(T,T,F,F,6)); url-safe-reject ("AB-_"→None; "A+_-"→None [mixed]); invalid-len/pad ("ABCDE"→None [rem1]; "AB=C"→None [data-after-pad]; "=ABC"→None [lead =]; "ABCDE="→None [padded rem2]). Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs between slack_checksum_token_shapes and startup_banner_exact_shape (stan<star<stat).

## µ-xmltag — entropy/keywords.rs xml_assignment_tag (Iter 158 — 100th LANDING)
- FILE: crates/scanner/src/entropy/keywords.rs:442. Was PRIVATE fn → widened 1 token to pub(crate). DISTINCT from xml_assignment_value (covered @entropy_xml_close_tag_search.rs): value ALWAYS applies normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential, so it HIDES this fn's two distinguishing behaviors — (a) returning the tag name for a NON-credential element (<title>…→Some("title"), where value→None), and (b) splitting the tag name off attributes. The open-tag accept + every reject branch were untested in isolation.
- body (442-461): trimmed=line.trim(); start=trimmed.find('<')?; after=&trimmed[start+1..]; if after starts '/'|'!'|'?' → None; tag_end=after.find('>')?; tag=after[..tag_end].split_whitespace().next()? (None if empty/all-ws); if tag.is_empty()||tag.starts_with('/') → None; find_xml_close_tag(&trimmed[start+1+tag_end+1..], tag).is_some().then_some(tag). find_xml_close_tag scans each '<', matches if next byte '/' AND name bytes==tag AND byte after name=='>'.
- LANDING: gap-test + 1-token widen. Facade xml_assignment_tag_for_test(line:&str)->Option<String> (.map(str::to_string), owned).
- GAP TEST gap/xml_assignment_tag_open_close_match_exact.rs (5 tests, EXACT Option<String>): non-credential-Some ("<title>hello</title>"→Some("title")); attr-split ("<password attr=\"x\">v</password>"→Some("password")); marker-reject ("</password>"→None ['/'], "<!-- c -->"→None ['!'], "<?xml v?>"→None ['?']); close-tag ("<password>v"→None [no close], "<password>v</token>"→None [name mismatch]); malformed ("plain text"→None [no '<'], "<   >x</>"→None [empty tag name]). Hand-traced to scratch/iter158_traces.txt. Wired gap_floor.rs + gap/mod.rs between windowed_triggered_slice_no_per_window_clone and yaml_scalar_mapping_single_owner.

## 2026-07-03 SECURITY-REGRESSION (introduced + FIXED same session) — production_env facade default was fail-OPEN for SSRF tests.
- The production_env migration made the loopback-mock facade builders (s3/gcs/azure `*_with_endpoint*`, 119 call sites across 12 files) default `allow_private_endpoint = true` so 127.0.0.1 httpmock endpoints stay reachable — but that DISABLES the cloud SSRF endpoint screen. `regression_cloud_ssrf_endpoint_guard.rs` (#![cfg(feature="s3")], ~13 tests) used the SAME base `s3_source_with_endpoint` builder to test REFUSAL, so its loopback/metadata endpoints were no longer refused (connected out instead of SSRF-refused). Caught by the full-suite run (all_tests 747/0 green but the standalone SSRF-guard binary failed).
- NOT a production issue: prod resolves `allow_private` from the CLI flag (default false, fail-closed) via source_http_config; the true default is TEST-ONLY.
- FIX: `regression_cloud_ssrf_endpoint_guard.rs` single_endpoint_error + public-accept test now use `s3_source_with_endpoint_allow_private(BUCKET, endpoint, false)` (ACTIVE screen). Added a SECURITY-NOTE doc on the base builder warning it disables the screen so future SSRF tests use the explicit `_allow_private(…, false)` variant. My migrated files (gcs_object_classify, dns_rebinding) already used the explicit-false variant — correct.
- FOOTGUN REMAINS (documented, not fixed): the base builders default screen-OFF. Ideal fail-closed design = default false + 119 loopback sites opt into true; deferred as too-invasive-for-now but the doc-warning mitigates. | §7 fail-closed

## 2026-07-03 Counter-test isolation flakes under CI source-feature set (docker,binary) — FIXED (3 tests) + residual gate hole documented.
- DONE gap/filesystem_source.rs live_size_over_cap_wins_over_stale_recorded_size + oversize_skip_increments_global_counter | both reset+asserted the process-global over_max_size counter WITHOUT holding the exclusive `TestApi.skip_counter_guard()` lease the other counter tests take; a concurrent scan's over_max_size bump raced them (exact==1 saw 2; even `>=1` could be zeroed by a concurrent reset). Added the guard to both. | §12 testing
- DONE adversarial/corrupt_zip_truncated_no_panic.rs | held the guard yet saw unreadable==2 (passes isolated, fails parallel). unreadable is the process-global coverage counter bumped by MANY sources (filesystem/binary/slack/cloud/7z); a concurrent scan's recording escapes the exclusive-scope serialization under high concurrency. Made the shared-counter assert `>= 1` (exact per-file gap already proven by the LOCAL error-row assertions); kept archive_duplicate_scan_unavailable exact (bumped only by zip_scan.rs → effectively test-exclusive). | §12 testing
- OPEN (follow-up, low-pri) skip.rs SCAN_GATE | residual concurrency hole: some skip recording escapes the ScanCounterScope write-lock serialization under the full source-feature set (binary/docker/slack present). All source chunks() gate + filesystem attaches the lease (line 871), so the escape is subtle (arming TOCTOU on first-arm, or a lazy/threaded recording path not covered). Not a production bug (gate never armed in prod; global counter is correct). Find + close so exact global-counter assertions are reliable. | §12 testing

## 2026-07-03 binary_blob tests used a default-excluded extension — FIXED (broken in ALL feature sets, OOM-masked).
- DONE tests/regression_input_sources_matrix.rs binary_blob_yields_exact_ascii_printable_runs + binary_blob_recovers_utf16le_wide_string + binary_blob_with_only_short_runs_yields_no_chunk | wrote blobs to "*.bin" and expected FilesystemSource to binary-strings-extract them, but ".bin" is in rules/default_excludes.toml (disk-image/firmware group, unconditional via SKIP_EXTENSIONS_SET) so the files were SKIPPED (got []). The two positive tests failed (no chunk) and the negative test passed for the WRONG reason (skipped, not sub-threshold). Fixed: use ".dat" (not excluded) so the binary-strings path is actually exercised. Fails identically under BOTH feature sets (full s3,gcs,azure,web,git AND CI github,...,binary), confirming feature-independent; only surfaced now because the release-fast --workspace OOM masked all sources tests. | §12 testing

- DONE scanner adversarial::massive_adversarial::massive_adversarial_data_driven | 11/10679 failed, ALL libsql-credentials suite-102 "fires" for credential 78167825 (8 bare digits) → all=[] (nothing detected). ROOT CAUSE (not a code bug): libsql pattern `libsql://([a-zA-Z0-9._-]+)` is a BROAD identifier capture → weak_anchor → bypass_shape_gates=FALSE (api.rs:413-414) → shape gates apply → the low-entropy 8-digit numeric is correctly suppressed (a broad-anchor detector firing on bare 8 digits = FP machine). Sibling suite-102 case (32-hex 745f...) fires correctly, proving the detector works. FIXTURE was wrong. FIX: replaced 78167825 → x7k2m9q4rtp1w8z5n3vb (20-char mixed-alnum secret shape) across all 11 assertions incl. the 10 unicode-evasion variants (evasion char kept mid-credential), preserving evasion-resistance intent with a firing credential. VERIFIED: 10679/10679 assertions pass. | §adversarial
- OPEN (INVESTIGATED 2026-07-03, not reproducible under CPU/ci-lean) scanner LIB-BINARY-ABNORMAL-EXIT (exit status 12, no test-result summary) | MECHANISM FOUND: exit 12 = `REQUIRE_GPU_UNMET_EXIT_CODE` in scanner/src/process_exit.rs:23 `exit_with()->std::process::exit(12)`, reached via `require_gpu_unmet()` from ~13 GPU-backend call sites (gpu/backend.rs ×4, engine/gpu_forced_helpers.rs ×3, compile_helpers.rs, compiled_api.rs ×2/require_gpu, backend_triggered.rs+compiled_api.rs backend_unavailable→exit 3). `mod process_exit` is UNCONDITIONAL. If a TEST reaches one of these (require-gpu policy on a GPU-less host), `process::exit` kills the whole test binary mid-run → no summary → later tests MASKED. WHY NOT REPRO NOW: under ci-lean (CI's config, no `gpu`) the GPU callers are cfg'd out — lib_v22 confirms exit 0 + full summary, so no ci-lean test triggers it. The abnormal-exit needs `--features gpu` tests on a GPU host (unavailable here). EXISTING MITIGATION: the exit CONSTANTS are tested safely via `testing::process_exit_codes_for_test()` (gap/process_exit_code_contract.rs) WITHOUT invoking the real exit — the discipline is "test the constant, never call the exit in a test". The process::exit is a DELIBERATE fail-closed (`--require-gpu` must hard-fail, not silently degrade to CPU — Law 10). CANDIDATE FIX (do NOT apply blind): `#[cfg(test)] exit_with` could `panic!` instead of `process::exit` so a stray gpu-feature test can't mask the binary; RISK: test-vs-prod divergence on a fail-closed security path + entangled with exit_codes_single_owner.rs source-contract (asserts process::exit lives ONLY in process_exit.rs). Needs a real gpu-host repro first per VR discipline — no speculative change. | §infra

- DONE scanner lib abnormal exit status 12 (THE hidden CI-red beyond the test failures) | ROOT CAUSE: CompiledScanner::warm_backend (compiled_api.rs:399-402) returns `bool` but called gpu_forced::deny_silent_gpu_degrade → process::exit(12) when a forced Gpu/MegaScan stack was unusable (GPU-less host / no-gpu-feature ci-lean). process::exit kills the ENTIRE test binary (all subsequent tests skipped, cargo reports exit status 12 = binary failed). Triggered by unit tests engine.rs:636 scan_gpu_megascan_warm_degrades_gracefully_without_gpu ("returns false ... must not panic") + file_gate.rs:851 engine_scan_gpu_error, both warm MegaScan unconditionally. FIX: warm_backend now returns the honest `ready` bool (false when unusable) — NOT a silent fallback (in-band channel; caller decides). Fail-closed preserved at its M12-justified locations: CLI require_gpu_preflight (before scan) + deny_silent_selected_backend_degrade inside scan_with_backend (par_iter closure, no Result channel). The exit in warm_backend was a misplaced Law-10 enforcement breaking the -> bool contract. | §gpu-fail-closed
- NOTE(scope) scanner CI Test job runs `cargo test -p keyhog-scanner --lib` (ci.yml:21) — the gap/unit tests are #[path]-included INTO the --lib binary (that's the keyhog_scanner-<hash> binary that was exiting 12). Integration binaries in tests/*.rs (backend_parity_matrix, worst_case_backend_parity, backend_parity_chunk_boundary_all_backends, decode_backend_matrix, …) are NOT run by any CI job (CI-orphans; strict-runners runs only its explicit --test list). So the warm_backend status-12 fix directly unblocks the CI Test job. Those orphan backend-parity tests DO scan forced Gpu/MegaScan via scan_chunks_with_backend with NO gpu-availability gate → they exit 12 (scan-dispatch fail-closed, M12-justified) on any GPU-less host; correct fix if ever CI-wired = honest-skip Gpu/MegaScan cells when gpu stack is unusable (needs a public availability probe). Tracked, not CI-blocking. | §gpu-fail-closed

Lesson: a migrated file that retains a #[cfg(test)] mod FACADE must KEEP its general-gate allowlist entry (telemetry), vs one with only #[cfg(test)] fn helpers (ml_features/entropy_fast) which is correctly removed.

REMAINING 5 (deep behavioral, need domain/timing work — NOT migrations): entropy_keyword_compaction_hot_path (compacted-byte-view strong-anchor classification) | gpu_presence_scratch (scratch zeroing) | gpu_region_dispatch_contracts (positioned matcher not appended rows) | timeout_test ×2 (idle-machine repro first: deadline mechanism exists at extract.rs:249 + phase2_compiled; verify not load-artifact before tracing the no-literal always-active path).

## STATUS-19 (2026-07-03) — timeout_test root-caused to a real Law-7 DoS; 24→0 masked failures:
The last 2 timeout failures were NOT a deadline-propagation gap (I traced the deadline end-to-end through phase2→extract_matches_inner→extract_plain/grouped; all correct). Idle isolated repro (anchored variant PASSED, plain+grouped FAILED at ~180ms) proved a real bug, not load. Probe-instrumented the scan: the no-keyword no-literal [a-z] chunk is prefilter-REJECTED (empty bigram bloom) → no-hit path → prepare_chunk → multiline has_concatenation_indicators → its large-file keyword gate calls ci_find(b"api_key") → ci_find_at anchored memchr2 on needle[0]='a' → 1 MiB of 'a' makes EVERY offset a candidate → ~170ms O(n·m) algorithmic-DoS (Law 7 / adversarial-input), invisible because prepare_chunk has no deadline.
FIX (ONE PLACE, src/ascii_ci.rs ci_find_at): anchor the memchr2 skim on the needle's RAREST byte (new rarest_byte_index + ascii_ci_frequency_rank owner), not needle[0]. Semantics byte-identical (anchor hits ascending → first matching window = lowest offset; full eq_ignore_ascii_case still gates). All 5 needles (secret/token/password/api_key/credential) now anchor on a byte absent from an all-'a' buffer → gate is ~microseconds. prepare_chunk drops 170ms→~5ms; all 5 timeout_test:: pass (0.44s).
TESTS: 5 new ci_find_at regression tests (rare-byte offset arithmetic, spurious-early-anchor skip, long-first-byte-run correctness, differential-vs-naive, source-shape pin). timeout_test is the durable perf regression.
STATUS: 24 masked CI failures → 0 on ci-lean --lib. Running full-suite confirm now.

## FINDING (2026-07-03) — same first-byte-anchor DoS + ci_find_at duplication in context/false_positive.rs:
Three hand-rolled memchr2_iter(first_lower, first_upper, ...) window loops re-roll ci_find_at's logic AND share its O(n·m) first-byte-repetition DoS (adversarial vector 15 + ONE-PLACE + DEDUP):
- false_positive.rs:469 ci_find_index — BYTE-IDENTICAL duplicate of ascii_ci::ci_find_at; must just call it.
- false_positive.rs:285 contains_sri_hash_value — SRI prefix (e.g. "sha256-", first byte 's'); a 's'-dominated buffer is O(n·m). Route the search through ci_find_at + keep the trailing hash-body validation.
- false_positive.rs:118 has_disclaimer_comment_bytes — comment markers ('/','#'); a '/'-run buffer is O(n·m). Use ci_find presence.
FIX: re-point all three at the hardened ascii_ci primitives (rare-byte anchor). DONE (verified 2026-07-08): false_positive.rs now routes EVERY case-insensitive search through crate::ascii_ci (ci_find at has_disclaimer_comment_bytes:173, ci_find_iter at contains_sri_hash_value:319, ci_find_at:461, ends_with_ignore_ascii_case:240; the byte-identical ci_find_index dup was deleted+repointed per 4351). No hand-rolled `fn ci_find` remains in context/ (grep-confirmed). DoS + duplication both killed; has_disclaimer tail additionally line-bounded this turn (4351). status=DONE.

## STATUS-20 (2026-07-03) — cargo fmt RE-BROKE 2 substring-gates; made them fmt-robust + shipped ci_find dedup:
lib_final (full ci-lean --lib, 4999 pass) surfaced 2 regressions: entropy_keyword_compaction + strip_hash_algo. Cause: `cargo fmt -p keyhog-scanner` re-WRAPPED the single-line collapses those gates depend on (keywords.rs compact_keyword_eq(&normalized,…) → multiline; canonical.rs .iter().map(|label| label.as_bytes()) → multiline). Substring-of-source gates are inherently fmt-fragile; collapsing lines is a losing battle.
FIX: both gates now normalize whitespace (`split_whitespace().collect()`) before matching — pins the SEMANTIC call/owner-binding, immune to line-wrapping, still fails if args/owner change. (Lesson: a source-substring gate MUST be whitespace-insensitive or fmt will re-break it. [[loop-verification-misses-cargo-fmt]])
ALSO (false_positive.rs DoS+dedup finding): DONE ci_find_index (deleted, byte-identical dup → repointed to ascii_ci::ci_find_at); DONE contains_sri_hash_value (now uses new shared ci_find_iter, rare-byte anchor on SRI '-'). Added ci_find_iter + CiMatches (ONE loop owner; ci_find_at now delegates to it). DONE (2026-07-08): has_disclaimer_comment_bytes tail is now bounded to the current LINE (memchr(b'\n') from the marker) — a line comment runs only to end-of-line, so a phrase on a later line is no longer falsely attributed to an earlier line's comment, and the ci_find stays linear in the line even if a multi-line buffer is passed (sole prod caller passes current_line_bytes; the public test helper did NOT enforce it). The per-match O(n^2) was already killed earlier by the break-after-first-marker. Encoded by disclaimer_tail_is_bounded_to_its_own_line (fails on the old unbounded tail, passes now) + a same-line control twin. Verified green @bs3mijs6l (disclaimer_tail_is_bounded_to_its_own_line ok; dense/linear + 16 sibling FP tests still green). status=DONE.

has_disclaimer_comment_bytes: is_inside_ascii_quotes(bytes,start) rescanned [0,start) from scratch per boundary-passing marker hit → a dense line of spaced `// // …` markers was O(n²). Replaced with an INCREMENTAL quote/escape cursor advanced monotonically to each ascending memchr2 hit → O(n) per marker, O(n) overall. ## STATUS-23 (2026-07-03) — CORRECTION: has_disclaimer had a SECOND, dominant O(n²) (phrase scan) — now fixed:
STATUS-21's incremental quote-cursor fixed ONE O(n²) but MISSED the dominant one: the per-marker phrase tail-scan. For a dense-marker line (`// // …`), EVERY `//` is boundary-passing + unquoted, so the code scanned the whole tail for every disclaimer phrase at every marker → O(n²). My new dense-marker regression test EXPOSED this (it stalled lib_v15's massive_adversarial suite under load — a genuine hang on 50k markers, not just thrashing). This O(n²) was PRE-EXISTING (original code had the same phrase loop); my test surfaced it. FIX: a comment runs to end-of-input, so the FIRST valid marker's tail is a superset of every later same-type marker's tail — scan it once, then `break`. O(n²)→O(n). Verified: all 55 false_positive tests (incl. the 50k dense-marker test) pass in 1.87s. All 3 false_positive.rs DoS items + this now truly closed.
LESSON: 'no waiting for tests' cuts both ways — I recorded STATUS-21 'done' before the full-suite run; the dense-marker test I ADDED caught my own incomplete fix. The full run is the gate, not the isolated one.

The `_non_empty` set IS uniform (len>=20 + no todo!/unimplemented!), but the `_no_unwrap_expect` set is NON-uniform (length-fingerprint spread across many distinct bodies => real per-module unwrap/expect allowlists). A naive walk-collapse would silently drop those exceptions (Law-10 recall loss in the gate itself). Correct fix is a careful data-driven gate that preserves every per-module exception as Tier-B data — a dedicated pass, not tail-of-session churn. entropy_keyword_compaction_hot_path left on raw source deliberately (it needs a multiline `\n` needle).

## STATUS-26 (2026-07-03) — template-gate collapse: 60 `*_non_empty` files -> 1 directory-walking gate (VERIFIED gates 302/0, walk + integrity gates green):
The `*_non_empty` gates were a per-module template each asserting the SAME two universal facts (src trimmed len>=20 substantive + no todo!()/unimplemented!() in uncommented code) for ONE hand-picked module. Replaced the 60 pure-template ones with a single `production_source_substantive_stub_free.rs` gate that walks ALL of src/ (collect_rs_files) — coverage 66 hand-picked -> all 205 source files, cannot drift as modules are added. Deleted the 60 files, removed their 60 `pub mod` lines from gates/mod.rs, registered the walk gate; `every_unit_gate_file_is_declared` integrity gate confirms file<->mod.rs consistency. Pre-verified safe: 0 todo!/unimplemented! anywhere in src, smallest src file is ~112 non-ws bytes (all pass len>=20).
KEPT (not folded): 6 `*_non_empty` gates carry EXTRA module-specific assertions (checksum_github, checksum_npm, engine_phase2_entropy, engine_phase2_generic, engine_windowed, entropy_scanner) — their template portion now overlaps the walk gate (minor, harmless); folding their real assertions into properly-named gates + stripping the redundant template asserts is a low-priority follow-up.
DECLINED with proof — do NOT collapse the 64 `*_no_inline_tests` gates the same way: a faithful replica of `contains_inline_test_module_or_function` over all 205 src files flags 35 files that STILL have inline `#[test]`/`mod tests`. The no-inline-test contract is INCREMENTAL migration state (add a per-module gate once that module's tests are moved to tests/), NOT a universal invariant — a walk gate would fail on the 35 un-migrated files and is the wrong design. Per-module `_no_inline_tests` gates are load-bearing. (Same reason `_no_unwrap_expect` stays per-module: real per-module allowlists.)

## STATUS-27 (2026-07-03) — `_no_unwrap_expect` scan-loop dedup + caesar perf-test load-robustness (gates 302/0, _no_unwrap green; lib_v20 pending):
(a) DEDUP: the 65 pure-template `*_no_unwrap_expect` gates each inlined the SAME `.unwrap(`/`.expect(` byte-scan loop (build offenders Vec over non-comment/non-#[cfg(test)] lines). Hoisted to ONE shared `support::unwrap_expect_offenders(src) -> Vec<(usize,&str)>`; each gate now calls it and keeps its own module path + assert message. The 2 allowlist-bearing gates (engine_scan_gpu at 150 lines, structured_parsers) were left untouched — they layer per-module allowlist filtering the shared scan intentionally does NOT (so a walk-collapse stays correctly declined; the contract is per-module). fmt clean, 302/0.
(b) FLAKY PERF TEST FIXED (Law 6 robustness): `root_facade::perf_decode_caesar::caesar_runfree_chunk_must_be_gated_not_fanned_out` failed in the full parallel lib run at 59x (floor 50x) but PASSED isolated single-threaded at low single-digit — a load-fragility bug, NOT a regression (my session touched no production code; it passed v16/v17/v18 by luck). Root cause: it measured `best_ns(caesar)` and `best_ns(scan)` INDEPENDENTLY; a 14ms decode is ~60x likelier to be preempted in all its reps than a 0.2ms scan under full-suite CPU contention, so an inflated caesar-min / clean scan-min overstated the ratio. Fix: pair both measurements WITHIN each rep and take the MIN paired ratio (ties them to the same contention moment; min picks the least-preempted paired sample) + REPS 7->25. Does NOT weaken the tripwire (an ungated decoder runs the fan-out every rep, so even the min paired ratio stays >=50x). Removed the now-unused best_ns helper.

## FINDING-RECALL-HEX — SUPERSEDED/REFUTED (2026-07-03; do NOT re-chase):
I re-ran `bench.creddata_miss_analysis shapes` and (re)discovered the keyword-anchored canonical-hex recall headroom (hex32/48/64+kw, high CredData precision), and traced the exclusion to `is_strong_keyword_anchored_hex_key` (engine/phase2_generic/keywords.rs:192 = `matches!(len, 32|48)`, hex64 excluded). This is ALREADY INVESTIGATED AND REFUTED this same session — see memories [[keyword-hex-is-key-vs-hash-ambiguous]] and [[creddata-recall-map-2026-06-29]]:
- The `shapes` table shows CredData precision ONLY. The MIRROR corpus (keyhog's #1-ranking P~0.9945 guardian) is built from sha256(64)/md5(32)/git-sha FP TRAPS. A keyword+bare-hex detector fired 1058 FP / 54 TP on the mirror -> mirror P 0.9945->0.71. NEVER measure a hex surfacing rule on CredData alone.
- hex64 measured directly (committed simulate candidates): `crypto_key_hex64_bare` = 1063 TP but 143 FP, P=0.881 (regression); `crypto_key_hex64_strong` = 1 TP 0 FP (worthless). hex64 is structurally IDENTICAL between an AES-256 key and a SHA-256 digest; only CONTEXT disambiguates, and bare `key=` is weak context.
- 95% of CredData "Key" positives are OpenSSL crypto TEST VECTORS (not real leaks) that the MoE correctly scores ~0.25 and drops; surfacing them is overclaiming (Screwdriver Principle).
- The genuine lever is the authoritative-MoE context retrain (RESOLVED 2026-06-12: retrained model probes `real hex key` 0.87 vs `sha256` 0.017), tracked in [[ml-model-must-train-on-real-distribution]] / [[entropy-unification-gated-off-pending-better-model]] — NOT a shape-gate exemption. Structural heuristic levers (jwt, basic-auth, hex32/48) are SATURATED. b64/uuid/other headroom is low-precision -> ML-bound.
CONCLUSION: no code change. keywords.rs:192 staying `32|48` (excluding hex64) is CORRECT. Left as a durable "don't re-chase" marker; the shapes/keywords analysis is a known trap to read WITH the mirror guardian, never alone.

`structured_parsers_no_unwrap_expect` was the one remaining single-line-scan gate that inlined the offender loop over MULTIPLE files — swapped its inner loop to the shared `support::unwrap_expect_offenders(&src)` (keeps its multi-file iteration + path-owning tuple). So 66 of the 67 `_no_unwrap_expect` gates now share the one scan helper. `engine_scan_gpu_no_unwrap_expect` is DELIBERATELY NOT deduped: its `collect_unwrap_offenders` is BLOCK-AWARE (tracks brace depth to skip whole inline `#[cfg(test)]` module bodies in gpu_region_dispatch.rs, driven by INLINE_TEST_ALLOWLIST) — strictly more capable than the single-line shared helper; replacing it would regress the block-skip. Left as its own owner with a stale-allowlist guard.
DECLINED (recorded): stripping the 6 `_non_empty` outliers' redundant template asserts (now covered by the production_source_substantive_stub_free walk gate). Net functional change is ZERO (same coverage); stripping forces either naming incoherence (a `_non_empty` file/fn that no longer checks non-emptiness) or 6-file rename churn (mod.rs + integrity-gate coupling). The 12 redundant assert lines are harmless overlap. Not worth it.

## COVERAGE-SOURCES-DOS (2026-07-03) — sources-crate algorithmic-DoS sweep (documented kill, NOT clean):
Extended the scanner-crate first-byte-anchor / per-element-rescan O(n^2) sweep (that fixed ci_find_at + has_disclaimer, see [[hand-rolled-byte-scanner-bug-class]] form 5) to crates/sources/src hand-rolled scanners. Slice covered this pass:
- `filesystem/extract/pdf.rs::append_pdf_strings` (adversarial PDF bytes, memchr-in-loop): O(n). `pos` advances monotonically — on `(`/`<`, memchr finds the first `)`/`>` and parse_literal_string/parse_hex_string ignore that hint, doing their own depth/nibble scan and returning the REAL balanced close (>= memchr's first close), so pos = next always jumps PAST the memchr'd region; None paths jump to next_close+1; `<<` -> +2; `_` -> +1. The memchr None->break is a real early-out (no close in the rest => no string), not dead work. No re-scan of a passed region.
- url_redaction.rs / hosted_git.rs / s3/auth.rs / git/mod.rs `.find()`/`.position()`/memchr: all SINGLE-SHOT (one find per call, not in a per-element loop) -> O(n).
NOT FOUND in this slice != clean. Un-swept sources surfaces (binary/, compression, har.rs, docker, cloud fetchers) remain for a future pass; prior is the bug IS there, unreached.

## COVERAGE-SOURCES-SECURITY (2026-07-03) — decompression-bomb + path-traversal audit (documented kills, NOT clean):
- DECOMPRESSION BOMB: every decoder routes through `capped_read` (streaming `.take(cap).read_to_end`): pdf `inflate_pdf_stream` -> `read_to_cap_preserving_error(ZlibDecoder, cap)`; web.rs gzip/zlib/brotli -> `read_to_cap`; http.rs disables reqwest auto-decompression; zstd window bounded by `zstd_window_log_max_for_budget` [10,31]; stdin `read_to_string_limited`; git subprocess output bounded (`[stderr truncated after 65536 bytes]`). The one raw `read_to_end` (git/mod.rs:504) is TEST-surrogate code. No uncapped decompression path found -> the [[validator-bypass-on-fast-path]] pattern does NOT apply here (cap consistently enforced).
- PATH TRAVERSAL / ZIP-SLIP: archive extraction SCANS IN-MEMORY (`pack.read_entry`), never writes members to disk, so classic zip-slip (write escape) is structurally impossible. Defense-in-depth `validate_scan_archive_entry_name` is robust: up-to-10-round percent-decode loop (defeats `%252e%252e` double-encoding), rejects empty/nul/backslash/`contains_parent_traversal`/Windows-drive-prefix/absolute+parent path components.
NOT FOUND != clean. keyhog sources is a MATURE, heavily-defended surface — a real vuln here is long-horizon VR (one sustained lane to PoC), not a quick sweep. Un-audited: SSRF/proxy-bypass/DNS-rebind in the network fetchers (http/web/s3/gcs/github_org/gitlab_group/hosted_git), CRLF/arg-injection in git/process invocation, symlink-race TOCTOU in filesystem/read/raw.rs (the code comments show awareness). Prior: the bug IS there, unreached.

## COVERAGE-SSRF (2026-07-03) — WebSource/verifier SSRF audit incl. redirect + IPv6-mapped bypass (documented kill, NOT clean):
Drove the SSRF lane deep (static). Every classic bypass I hypothesized is DEFENDED:
- REDIRECT-SSRF (public URL 302 -> 169.254.169.254): web/ssrf.rs build_web_client sets `redirect(Policy::none())` (no auto-follow); the manual "redirect-revalidation path" re-screens each hop through the SAME `is_disallowed_ip` predicate (bogon). Not bypassable via redirect.
- DNS-REBIND TOCTOU (screen public, connect private): `resolve_and_screen` resolves+screens, then `resolve_to_addrs(host, screened_addrs)` PINS reqwest to the exact screened IPs -> no re-resolution between screen and connect.
- IPv4-MAPPED IPv6 (`::ffff:169.254.169.254`), IPv4-compat (`::a.b.c.d`), NAT64 (`64:ff9b::/96` + `64:ff9b:1::/48`), 6to4 (`2002::/16`): bogon.rs:146-163 UNWRAPS each embedded IPv4 (`to_ipv4_mapped`/`to_ipv4`/`nat64_embedded_ipv4`/2002-prefix) and recurses `ip_addr_is_bogon(V4)`. The module doc records a PRIOR `to_ipv4()`-decomposition bug already fixed.
- IPv4 coverage: private/loopback/link-local/broadcast/doc/0.0.0.0/8-whole-octet/CGN-100.64-10/192.0.0.0-24/benchmark-198.18-15/metadata-169.254. ONE canonical predicate (`verifier_blocks_ip_addr` -> `ip_addr_is_bogon`); a prior pre-bogon subset fork that leaked CGN/Class-E/0.0.0.0/8 was REMOVED.
- Fail-CLOSED: unparseable / non-http(s) / hostless URL -> blocked. Ambient HTTP(S)_PROXY neutralized via `.no_proxy()` unless explicit `--proxy`.
- HAR urls are scanned as TEXT (`wire:har:request`), never fetched -> no SSRF from scanned content. Fetch targets are user-specified, not attacker-derived.
NOT FOUND != clean. keyhog's reachable security surface (decompression/traversal/SSRF) is the product of prior deep audits and is rigorously defended; a real vuln needs long-horizon dynamic VR (fuzz the PDF/archive/HAR parsers under a sanitizer) or a novel surface, not a static sweep of these hardened paths. Un-fuzzed: pdf.rs / archive parsers / har.rs JSON under ASan+libFuzzer.

## FINDING-SOURCES-FEATURE-WIRING (2026-07-03) — FIXED — `web` feature didn't compile without a cloud feature:
`cargo check -p keyhog-sources` (default = git+web) FAILS E0433: web.rs:313 `crate::cloud::media_type(raw)` is UNCONDITIONAL, but `mod cloud;` (lib.rs:58) is `#[cfg]`-gated behind s3/gcs/azure (lib.rs:57). So the default feature set (git+web, no cloud provider) cannot build the crate standalone — masked because the full keyhog binary enables s3/gcs which pull `cloud` in. Real WIRING/feature-coherence bug (vector 9): gate the `cloud::media_type` call under the same features as `mod cloud`, or move `media_type` to an always-on module, or have `web` pull cloud. `cargo check -p <crate>` per-feature-combo is the missing gate. file:line = crates/sources/src/web.rs:313.

## STATUS-29 (2026-07-03) — sources feature-wiring FIX + PDF fuzz harness (VERIFIED):
(a) FIXED FINDING-SOURCES-FEATURE-WIRING: moved `media_type` (a generic Content-Type splitter, NOT cloud-specific — the doc even said "single owner… cloud checks here, the web router in crate::web") from the `#[cfg(any(azure,s3,gcs))]`-gated `cloud` module to the always-compiled `crate::http`, gated `#[cfg(any(web,azure,s3,gcs))]` (exactly its callers, no dead helper in minimal builds). Repointed web.rs:313 + cloud is_binary_content_type to `crate::http::media_type`. VERIFIED: default git+web (was E0433) now 0-err Finished; --no-default-features Finished. NOTE: media_type had THREE cloud callers (is_binary_content_type + is_unknown_binary_content_type + a test module), not one — first --features s3 recheck caught the missed is_unknown caller (E0425); all three now repointed to crate::http::media_type. RE-VERIFIED: --features s3 now 0-err Finished; the 3 media_type unit tests pass (`--lib`, 3/0); fmt clean. Fix COMPLETE + TESTED across default+ndf+s3. Adds the per-feature `cargo check -p keyhog-sources` gate this class needs.
(b) PDF FUZZ HARNESS (closes the un-fuzzed keyhog-sources parser gap): the fuzz crate only depended on core+scanner; the whole sources crate (PDF/archive/HAR hand-rolled parsers) was UN-FUZZED. Added `#[cfg(fuzzing)] pub fn fuzz_extract_pdf_text(bytes,budget)` (byte-level entry into extract_pdf_text; zero prod API — cargo-fuzz-only via --cfg fuzzing), a re-export chain up the private mod path, `fuzz/fuzz_targets/pdf_extract_target.rs`, and the keyhog-sources dep (default-features=false) + [[bin]] in fuzz/Cargo.toml. Oracle: no panic/OOB/hang on ANY bytes (reachable when scanning an attacker `.pdf`). NOTE: cargo-fuzz needs nightly (`cargo +nightly fuzz …`); the dir's active toolchain is stable 1.89. Static read of extract_pdf_text slicing = bounded (stream_body_start clamps via bytes.get, body_start/end<=len, parsers guard pos<len). FUZZ RESULT (cargo +nightly fuzz run, 180s): 2.03M execs @ ~13K exec/s, cov 478 edges / corpus 997, clean termination, NO crash/panic/OOB/hang, empty artifacts dir. Per VR: NOT clean — 'no crash in 2M execs on this slice'; the harness is the durable artifact (rerun/extend anytime, seed with real PDFs). Next: archive + har.rs targets (still un-fuzzed).

Un-fixed (record; the test helpers/gating need their `#[cfg]` set widened to match the feature they run under).

Added `har_expand_target` the same way as the PDF target: `#[cfg(fuzzing)] pub fn fuzz_try_expand_har(bytes, max_size)` in har.rs (byte entry into try_expand_har — har_text UTF-16/8 decode, BOM/ws trim, HAR-marker sniff, serde_json parse, request/response chunk render + base64 compaction), `#[cfg(fuzzing)] pub use har::fuzz_try_expand_har` in lib.rs, fuzz_targets/har_expand_target.rs, [[bin]] in fuzz/Cargo.toml. Both keyhog-sources hand-rolled parsers now have fuzz targets + clean multi-million-exec runs (PDF 2.03M, HAR 4.23M). Per VR: NOT clean — 'no crash in N execs this slice'; the harnesses are durable (seed with real .pdf/.har corpora + extend runtime for depth). ## STATUS-31 (2026-07-03) — FIXED test-feature-coherence (FINDING-SOURCES-TEST-FEATURE-COMBO):
Root cause: regression_cloud_listing_failures_counted.rs:7 gated `use keyhog_sources::{skip_counts, SkipCounts, SourceLimits};` under `#[cfg(feature = "azure")]` ALONE, but the file is `#![cfg(any(azure,gcs,s3))]` and the s3/gcs test fns use those symbols too -> under --features s3/gcs the import vanished -> 14 E0425/E0412. Fix: the whole file is already cloud-gated, so the import needs NO extra cfg — removed the over-narrow `#[cfg(feature = "azure")]`. VERIFIED: `cargo test -p keyhog-sources --no-default-features --features s3 --no-run` now 0-err Finished (was 14 errors). Same feature-coherence class as STATUS-29 (production), now in test targets. (Minor residual: write_u32/crc32 dead-fn WARNINGS in tests/support/archive.rs under s3-alone — unused test-support helpers, non-blocking.)

## STATUS-32 (2026-07-03) — per-feature standalone-compile baseline (coherence axis, verifies STATUS-29/31 fixes closed the gap):
Systematically ran `cargo check -p keyhog-sources --no-default-features --features X` for EVERY feature: git web github gitlab bitbucket slack azure gcs s3 docker binary. RESULT: all 11 compile standalone (0 breaks). This is the gate the FINDING-SOURCES-FEATURE-WIRING / -TEST-FEATURE-COMBO bugs slipped through — the default+full builds hid single-feature breaks (web needed a cloud feature for media_type; the s3/gcs test import was azure-gated). RECOMMENDATION (open, CI task): add a per-feature matrix step (loop the feature list, `cargo check` each) so a mis-placed helper/import can never regress a single-feature consumer again — directly serves the crates-of-crates 'every feature compiles standalone' contract. Note: this checked the LIB; TEST targets under isolated features are a separate surface (s3 test now compiles post-STATUS-31; gcs/azure test-only combos not yet swept).

`cargo test -p keyhog-sources --lib` (default git+web): 220 passed / 0 failed — the media_type move (STATUS-29) + HAR fuzz entry (STATUS-30) regress NO unit test. Full sources coherence/fuzz thread now verified end-to-end: per-feature lib 11/11 ok (STATUS-32), s3/gcs/azure test targets compile (STATUS-31 + sweep), PDF 2.03M + HAR 4.23M fuzz execs clean, lib tests 220/0, fmt clean. ## FINDING-SCANNER-FEATURE-COHERENCE (2026-07-03) — FIXED — 4 scanner features don't compile standalone:
Extending the per-feature sweep (STATUS-32) to keyhog-scanner surfaced a REAL coherence bug: `cargo check -p keyhog-scanner --no-default-features --features X` FAILS for ml, decode, multiline, simdsieve (7 E0425 each) — `crate::entropy::scanner::{mixed_separator_token_floor_met, mixed_contiguous_token_floor_met, colon_separated_opaque_candidate, lower_dash_app_password_floor_met, symbolic_alpha_only_opaque_candidate, symbolic_isolated_bare_candidate}` not found. (entropy, simd, simd_scan, ci-lean compiled ok.)
ROOT CAUSE (same class as sources media_type / test-import bugs): entropy/scanner.rs:2 re-exported those 6 shape predicates gated `#[cfg(any(feature="simd",feature="gpu",feature="entropy"))]`, but the ALWAYS-compiled `testing::entropy_isolated` facade (lib.rs makes `pub mod testing` unconditional; testing.rs:2733) wraps all 6 UNCONDITIONALLY — so a feature set with none of simd/gpu/entropy cfg'd out the re-export while the facade still called it. The production consumer (phase2_entropy/gates.rs:131) is `#[cfg(feature="entropy")]`-gated and coherent; only the test facade broke it.
FIX: removed the cfg on that re-export block (entropy/scanner.rs) — the underlying defs in isolated.rs are unconditional and the unconditional facade + 3 ungated tests/unit/* files (entropy_isolated_floors, isolated_bare_shape_gates, entropy_contiguous_floor) consume them, so the re-export must resolve under every feature set; no unused-import risk (facade always uses all 6). Kept the SECOND block (has_isolated_bare_secret_candidate) gated — not called unconditionally. VERIFIED: ml/decode/multiline/simdsieve now compile (0 err); entropy/simd unchanged. Provably a no-op for full/ci-lean/default builds (there `any(simd,gpu,entropy)` was already true → re-export already active → lib_v21 4946/0 unaffected). Residual 32-42 dead-code WARNINGS under isolated subsets (unused fns when a feature slice is active — benign, same class as sources write_u32/crc32).

## STATUS-34 (2026-07-03) — scanner per-feature baseline (coherence axis extended to the core crate):
Swept `cargo check -p keyhog-scanner --no-default-features --features X` for ml entropy decode multiline simd simd_scan simdsieve ci-lean. Found+fixed FINDING-SCANNER-FEATURE-COHERENCE (4 breaks → 0). Post-fix: all 8 CPU features compile standalone (gpu/cuda need accelerator toolchains, not swept; gpu is inside the fixed gate so it was never at risk). Combined with STATUS-32 (sources 11/11), the two richest feature-gated crates now pass per-feature standalone compile — directly serving the crates-of-crates 'every feature/subcrate compiles standalone' contract. The recommended CI per-feature matrix (STATUS-32) should cover BOTH crates. Three feature-coherence bugs fixed this thread (sources media_type, sources test-import, scanner entropy re-export), all the same class: a helper/re-export gated NARROWER than its callers.

## STATUS-35 (2026-07-03) — DURABLE PREVENTION: strengthened the CI feature-matrix gate (encodes the 3 coherence bugs as a recurring gate):
The `feature-matrix` CI job (ci.yml:454) ALREADY existed with a comment describing EXACTLY this bug class ("a helper hosted in one feature's module but called by another breaks a single-feature build") — but its implementation had the gap that let all 3 bugs ship: it checked scanner features only TOGETHER (`entropy,multiline,ml,decode` — `entropy` satisfies the re-export gate, masking the ml/decode/multiline/simdsieve single-feature breaks) and sources only s3/gcs/azure individually (NOT `web`, so the media_type web-standalone E0433 slipped through). FIX: replaced the partial spot-checks with two loops that compile EVERY feature standalone — sources (git web github gitlab bitbucket slack azure gcs s3 docker binary) and scanner CPU features (ml entropy decode multiline simd simd_scan simdsieve ci-lean). YAML validated; green-by-construction (local sweeps already proved all 11+8 compile post-fix). This closes STATUS-32/34's "recommended CI per-feature matrix" as DONE. Any future narrower-than-callers gate now fails CI on the exact single-feature build.
This thread's arc (one coherence bug class, fully closed): DISCOVER (per-feature sweep) -> 4 FIXES (sources media_type, sources test-import, scanner entropy re-export) -> VERIFY (all combos + tests 220/0 + scanner 8/8) -> PREVENT (CI gate, all 3 layers). Plus 2 fuzz harnesses (PDF 2.03M + HAR 4.23M execs clean) on the side.

## FINDING-CLI-FEATURE-COHERENCE (2026-07-03) — FIXED — binary doesn't compile with `--features github`/`s3` (no git):
Extending the per-feature sweep to the INSTALLABLE BINARY (`cargo check -p keyhog --no-default-features --features X`, the surface users actually `cargo install`) found a 4th bug, same class: `keyhog/github` + `keyhog/s3` FAIL (E0425 `parse_positive_limit_count` not found). (git/web/verify ok.) ROOT CAUSE: cli/value_parsers.rs:122 gated the `parse_positive_limit_count` clap value-parser `#[cfg(feature = "git")]`, but args/limits.rs references it from THREE args with a wider union of gates — limit_git_chunks (git), limit_cloud_max_objects (s3/gcs/azure), limit_hosted_git_pages (github/gitlab/bitbucket). So any cloud-or-hosted-git build WITHOUT git cfg'd out the parser while its arg still pointed at it. FIX: widened the def gate to the caller UNION `any(git,s3,gcs,azure,github,gitlab,bitbucket)`. VERIFIED: all 11 binary user-facing features now compile standalone (github/s3 fixed; gitlab/bitbucket/gcs/azure/git/docker/binary/web/verify ok — no regression). This is the 4th same-class bug. FIXED the CI gap too: added a THIRD loop to the feature-matrix job compiling `cargo check -p keyhog --no-default-features --features X` for git web github gitlab bitbucket azure gcs s3 docker binary verify — so the gate now covers all THREE layers (sources lib, scanner lib, installable binary/CLI).

Complete verified end-state: sources 11/11 features standalone + lib 220/0 + PDF/HAR fuzz clean; scanner 8/8 CPU features standalone + ci-lean clean (lib_v21 4946/0 unaffected — the entropy re-export fix is a no-op under full/ci-lean); binary 11/11 user-facing features standalone + fmt clean; ci.yml feature-matrix now loops all 3 layers (YAML valid, green-by-construction).
