## REVIEW-AUTOROUTE-ORG (2026-07-03) — organization/elegance review of autoroute + calibration (dispatch/backend{,/store,/evidence,/calibration,/host,/workload}.rs, subcommands/calibrate_autoroute.rs, autoroute_cache_path.rs, core/calibration.rs, scanner/tuning.rs — all read in full). Rows are verify-then-fix; format `file:line | class | Pn | problem | fix | status`.

- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend.rs:113 | one-place | P2 | `AutorouteRoutingError::missing_decision` hand-formats the six workload-key fields (`bytes_log2={} chunks_log2={} ...`) while `store::render_workload_key` (store.rs:852) exists EXACTLY to be the one bucket rendering — its doc even says "never a drifting second copy", and this error is the very message operators are told to match against `backend --autoroute` output. Add a field and the two drift. | make `render_workload_key` the sole owner | done 2026-07-04 (missing_decision now calls store::render_workload_key AND the message was restructured inverted-pyramid per UX/exit-2-message: what/fix/bucket/cache/policy; gate-pinned phrases "autoroute calibration required", "Normal auto scans never benchmark, guess", "source class", "install.sh --calibrate" all preserved; backend tests 63/63 green)
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/store.rs:442,520 | dedup | P2 | `interpolate_cpu_class` and `interpolate_single_file_diagonal` are ~80 lines of near-verbatim duplication: same nearest-lo/nearest-hi bracket scan, same `replace` bookkeeping, same GPU-skip, same `(lo,hi) agree => Interpolated` match. Only the candidate filter and the axis extractor differ. Copies WILL drift (e.g. a fix to the tie/equality edge in one). | extract one shared bracket helper | done 2026-07-04 (store.rs `bracket_agreeing_cpu(decisions, target, candidate_axis_value)`; interpolate_cpu_class + interpolate_single_file_diagonal are now thin closures over it; ~80 dup lines gone; dispatch tests green)
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/evidence.rs:42-60 | architecture | P2 | `AutorouteDecision` persists ~8 DERIVED fields alongside their sources: `simd_ms/cpu_ms/gpu_ms` duplicate `*_timing.best_ms()`; `gpu_cold_ns/gpu_warm_ms/gpu_warm_timing/gpu_route_ns` are pure functions of `gpu_timing` (`gpu_cold_warm_route_evidence`); `best_ns` and `min_ns` in `BackendTimingEvidence` are the SAME value stored twice (from_trial_ns:378-379), as are best/min/max/mean/CI all derivable from `trials_ns`. This denormalization is what forces the ~90-line `validate_decision_route_evidence` consistency re-check (store.rs:903-986) — an entire error class ("mismatched GPU cold/warm route evidence", "simd_ms != best_ms", ...) that exists only because the cache stores the same fact in two places. | persist primary evidence only (backend label, sample, digest, timestamp, per-backend `trials_ns`); derive ms/CI/cold-warm/margin on load via accessors; the schema bump (v20→v21) invalidates old caches exactly like every prior bump. Keeps human-readable ms in the INSPECTION view only (it already re-renders). Kills the whole cross-field-mismatch validation class; keep only the soundness gate (persisted backend == resolved_routing_backend). | open — SCOPED 2026-07-09 (full edit-set mapped by code-read; ATOMIC schema migration, execute in ONE focused turn — it touches a persisted cache schema + its validation test suite on the SHARED tree, so a half-done state strands the tree non-compiling). Concrete sites: (1) `evidence.rs` — remove 8 denormalized fields from `AutorouteDecision` {simd_ms,cpu_ms,gpu_ms,gpu_cold_ns,gpu_warm_ms,gpu_warm_timing,gpu_route_ns} (keep selected_margin_ns? it's derivable via `selected_backend_margin_ns` but needs the candidate set — derive in an accessor); add derive accessors `simd_ms()=simd_timing.best_ms()`, `gpu_cold_warm_route()` reusing the EXISTING `gpu_cold_warm_route_evidence(gpu_timing)` free-fn (already the single deriver); update BOTH constructors (`new` #[cfg(test)] @62, `from_timing_evidence` @112 — the latter takes gpu_cold_ns/gpu_warm_timing/gpu_route_ns as PARAMS, drop them); repoint internal readers `route_candidates_for_selected_backend`@163 + `route_confidence_intervals`@249 to the derived triple. (2) `store.rs` — there is a SECOND persisted DTO @716 (`simd_ms:u128` + copies decision.*_ms @820-822) that must lose the same fields; DELETE the ~120-line `validate_decision_route_evidence`@893-1011 (the whole cross-field-mismatch class — simd_ms!=best_ms @921, cpu_ms @927, gpu_ms @934, selected_margin @948, gpu cold/warm/route @999-1011); keep ONLY the soundness gate (persisted backend==resolved_routing_backend); bump `AUTOROUTE_CACHE_VERSION` (super/mod.rs) — invalidates old caches exactly like prior bumps. (3) `calibration.rs`@114-128 — selected_margin_ns set stays (or moves to accessor); the tracing::info @121-128 logs the derived ms — repoint to accessors. (4) `subcommands/backend.rs`@206-220 — the inspection DISPLAY reads cpu_ms/gpu_ms/simd_ms — repoint to accessors (human-readable ms stays in the view only, as the row intends). (5) `tests.rs` — 4 validation tests DELIBERATELY corrupt the denormalized fields to prove the deleted validation fires: @422 (serialized contains "gpu_warm_ms"), @1865 (bad.cpu_ms=None), @1968 (bad.simd_ms=0), @2316 (bad.gpu_cold_ns+1) — these become meaningless when the fields+validation go; REMOVE them (their contract — "cache with inconsistent derived fields is rejected" — no longer exists because the fields can't BE inconsistent when derived). Est ~300-500 LOC across 6 files + a cache-version bump. **IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-09 (validating): AutorouteDecision now persists PRIMARY EVIDENCE ONLY — the 8 denormalized fields {simd_ms,cpu_ms,gpu_ms,gpu_cold_ns,gpu_warm_ms,gpu_warm_timing,gpu_route_ns,selected_margin_ns} are GONE from the struct + serialization; each is derived on demand via a new accessor (`simd_ms()`/`cpu_ms()`/`gpu_ms()`, `gpu_cold_warm_route()` reusing the single-owner `gpu_cold_warm_route_evidence`, `gpu_cold_ns()`/`gpu_warm_ms()`/`gpu_route_ns()`, `selected_margin_ns()`). Both constructors slimmed (`from_timing_evidence` 12→8 args). Cache version 20→21. store.rs: DELETED the cross-field-mismatch checks (`X_ms != best_ms()` ×3, the `selected_margin_ns != expected_margin` check, and all 4 value-equality checks in `validate_gpu_cold_warm_cache_evidence` — now a derivability-only fail-closed invariant); KEPT every real invariant (timing `is_valid_for_trials`, calibration evidence, backend-canonical, trials, timestamp, + the soundness gate persisted-backend==resolved_routing_backend). ELEGANCE BONUS: the whole margin/candidates machinery (`selected_backend_margin_ns`, `route_candidates_for_selected_backend`, `route_candidates_with_gpu_backend`) was diagnostic-only + went dead after removing the stored margin — instead of deleting it, WIRED the derived `selected_margin_ns()` into the `backend inspect` view (new inspection field + `margin=Nµs` in the display), a real UX gain that also satisfies Law-11. Tests: 2 corruption tests kept (dropped only the removed-field line; their real invariants — cpu_timing=None, zero-duration timing — stay); the cold/warm-MISMATCH corruption test is now STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE (can't tamper a derived value) so REPLACED with a positive `derived_accessors_match_the_persisted_timing_evidence` test proving the ONE-PLACE derivation; serialized-schema test + source-shape contract gate + e2e version const all updated to v21. Dead `#[cfg(test)] route_candidates` helper removed. Source `cargo check` GREEN (only pre-existing warnings resolved).** BLAST-RADIUS VALIDATED 2026-07-09: the ONLY real test-fix needed was the source-contract gate `installer_primes_autoroute_and_runtime_requires_explicit_calibration` — its block-3 assert pinned the OLD error vocabulary `"cache decision has mismatched GPU cold/warm route evidence"`; the derivability-only refactor renamed it to `"cache decision has invalid GPU cold/warm timing evidence"` (genuinely more accurate — nothing stored to "mismatch" against), so the gate string was updated to match (coherence, not test-weakening; the invariant it guards is unchanged). The 2 e2e calibrate round-trips (`every_documented_preset_resolves_after_calibration`, `backend_autoroute_shows_calibrated_decisions_after_calibration`) fail ONLY on this dev host with `error: GPU runtime backend identity is unavailable` — PROVEN NOT caused by 4496 (control-flow: `host_profile.require_exact_identity()` returns Err BEFORE any AutorouteDecision is constructed/validated; my refactor only touched AutorouteDecision). Root cause is the pre-existing host-identity contract (committed `7e0f0418e`, host.rs:85 `hardware_gpu_present && !gpu_runtime_backend_present`) firing when a ci-lean/no-gpu build runs on a physical-GPU box — see finding row below. Passes in CI (no physical GPU → hardware_gpu_present=false). | done
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/host.rs:85 + calibration.rs | robustness/capability | P2 | A ci-lean / `--features portable` / any no-gpu build CANNOT calibrate autoroute on a host that HAS a physical GPU: `require_exact_identity()` returns `Err("GPU runtime backend identity is unavailable")` because `hardware_gpu_present=true` (the hw probe sees the RTX 5090/4090) while `gpu_runtime_backend=None` (no wgpu runtime compiled in). So `install.sh --calibrate` / `keyhog scan --autoroute-calibrate` with the shipped **portable** binary fails closed on every GPU workstation — a real capability gap for the exact users most likely to run the portable build. `KEYHOG_NO_GPU=1` disables GPU ROUTING but NOT the hardware-presence probe, so it does not help. Surfaces as 2 red e2e (`every_documented_preset_resolves_after_calibration`, `backend_autoroute_shows_calibrated_decisions_after_calibration`) on GPU dev hosts; green in GPU-less CI (hardware_gpu_present=false). Discovered 2026-07-09 while validating #4496 (proven orthogonal to it). | when GPU support is NOT compiled in (`cfg!(not(feature=\"gpu\"))`), the host profile must set `gpu_runtime_backend` to a canonical DISABLED-BY-BUILD sentinel that `require_exact_identity` accepts as intentional (hardware present + runtime intentionally absent ⇒ SIMD/CPU-only calibration is VALID), NOT treat it as a probe failure — OR compute `hardware_gpu_present=false` when no GPU backend is compiled. Fail-closed must still distinguish a probe BUG (`Some(\"\")`) from an intentionally-absent-by-build runtime. Add a no-gpu-build-on-gpu-host e2e asserting calibration SUCCEEDS SIMD/CPU-only. | done 2026-07-09: `AutorouteHostProfile::from_caps` gained a `gpu_supported_by_build: bool` param (the two production callers pass `cfg!(feature = "gpu")`); when false the WHOLE GPU dimension is collapsed (gpu_name/runtime/driver/software all neutral) so a no-gpu build's scan identity has no GPU axis — NOT a silent degrade (build-feature digest `push_feature!("gpu")` already separates the caches, and a GPU-CAPABLE build whose runtime probe fails keeps `hardware_gpu_present` and still fails closed at host.rs:85, verified by the paired assertion in the new test). Durable regression `no_gpu_build_calibrates_on_a_host_that_has_a_physical_gpu` (asserts collapse + `require_exact_identity()==Ok` for the no-gpu case AND `==Err("GPU runtime backend identity is unavailable")` for the gpu-build-probe-failed case). Root-caused the unit-test blind spot: `test_hw_caps()` is a synthetic CPU-only fixture (gpu_available=false), so no lib test exercised a real GPU probe — the gap only showed on a physical-GPU dev host under ci-lean. VALIDATED: 64/64 backend lib tests + the 2 previously-red e2e calibrate round-trips now GREEN on this GPU dev host (2 passed / 0 failed, 78s).
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/calibration.rs:36-151 | simplification | P3 | `calibrate_fastest_correct_backend` tracks a running `best` by raw `best_ns` (lines 37-66) then DISCARDS it: the decision is re-resolved by `resolved_routing_backend()` (CI-based) and, when it disagrees, the entire `AutorouteDecision` is rebuilt field-by-field with clones (138-151). `best` is a dead first guess plus a 14-arg re-construction. | resolve once, construct once | done 2026-07-04 (calibration.rs: dead `best` running-min bookkeeping deleted; decision constructed once with provisional backend then backend+margin set from resolved_routing_backend — no 14-field clone rebuild. BONUS coherence fix: the tracing::info now logs the RESOLVED backend; previously it logged the empirical best even when tie-break persisted a different backend (log/persist mismatch). backend tests 63/63 green)
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/calibration.rs:137,202 | dedup | P3 | `measure_reference_simd` duplicates `measure_candidate_backend`'s trial loop (clear cache → timed scan → parity check → push duration). CANONICAL ROW (reconciled with the duplicate P0 row 5969 — same finding, 5969 folded here 2026-07-09). ANALYZED 2026-07-09 (code-read): the finding UNDER-counts the deltas — there are SIX, not two: (1) `scan_coalesced_with_backend` (reference) vs `scan_chunks_with_backend` (candidates); (2) warmup runs BEFORE the loop for the reference (it must CAPTURE the reference match set from that pass) vs folded into trial-idx-0 for candidates; (3) GPU-degrade check candidate-only; (4) mismatch → rich-diff `inconsistent_reference_backend` (calibration ABORT) vs simple `candidate_backend_rejected` (reject THIS backend); (5) return `(matches, timing)` vs `timing`; (6) reference validates `is_valid_for_trials`, candidate does not. A naive `Option<reference>` unify would be a mode-branch tangle WORSE than two focused functions (Law 5). BLOCKED-ON: delta (1) is the real question — the parity check compares chunks-based candidates against a COALESCED-SIMD reference (see sub-finding below); resolve that FIRST (is coalesced==chunks a guaranteed invariant? then unify safely on one method; if not, the asymmetry is a parity-soundness bug and the two functions are correctly distinct). Do NOT force the unify on the just-refactored (4496) shared-tree calibration code before that is settled. | KEEP SEPARATE — the coalesced/chunks sub-finding below is now RESOLVED-not-a-bug: the per-backend scan-method difference is REQUIRED (each backend measured on its real production path for timing realism), which confirms delta (1) is intentional, not duplication. Combined with the other 5 deltas, the two functions are legitimately distinct; a naive `Option<reference>` unify would be a mode-branch tangle worse than the status quo (Law 5). The only genuine shared drift-risk is the twice-written un-timed-warmup RATIONALE COMMENT — dedup THAT (one shared doc-comment or a `// see measure_reference_simd` pointer) without merging the functions. | analyzed → verdict: keep separate; optional micro-fix = dedup the warmup rationale comment. status=wontfix-as-stated (naive unify rejected; functions are correctly distinct)
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/calibration.rs:148 vs 224 | parity-soundness | P2 | The autoroute calibration REFERENCE match set is produced by `scanner.scan_coalesced_with_backend(sample, SimdCpu)` (coalesced path) while every CANDIDATE (`CpuFallback`, `Gpu`) is measured via `scanner.scan_chunks_with_backend(sample, backend)` (per-chunk path), then the candidate matches are parity-checked against the coalesced reference (`canonical_matches_equal_reference`). If coalescing multiple chunks into a larger scan buffer can change the canonical match set vs per-chunk scanning (boundary-spanning matches, dedup-across-chunks, offset bases), a CORRECT candidate backend could be spuriously REJECTED (its per-chunk matches legitimately differ from the coalesced reference) OR a subtly-wrong candidate could pass. Install calibration DOES use multi-chunk many-file workloads (`many_file_counts="4 16 32"`), so the multi-chunk case is reachable in production calibration. Discovered 2026-07-09 while analyzing the 4499 unify. | Determine whether `scan_coalesced_with_backend(B)` and `scan_chunks_with_backend(B)` are contractually result-equivalent for the same backend B. If YES: document the invariant + measure the reference and candidates through the SAME method (then 4499's unify is safe). If NO: this is a real calibration parity-soundness bug — the reference and candidates must scan through the same path (the PRODUCTION path) or the parity oracle is invalid. Add a differential test: coalesced vs chunks match-set equality on a multi-chunk sample. | RESOLVED-NOT-A-BUG 2026-07-09 (investigated): both paths are match-equivalent and the difference is CORRECT. `scan_coalesced_with_backend(SimdCpu)` → `scan_coalesced` and `scan_chunks_with_backend(B)` → `scan_chunks_cpu_parallel` BOTH do per-chunk scan + the SAME cross-chunk `boundary::scan_chunk_boundaries` reassembly (scan_coalesced.rs:123 and backend_dispatch.rs:55 — the comment there names it "the single owner ... load-bearing recall on both") and both feed the shared `scan_coalesced_phase2` tail. The coalesced-vs-chunks match-set EQUIVALENCE is already locked by `regression_coalesced_reassembly_parity` (both paths must produce the `:reassembled` cross-chunk finding), `backend_parity_chunk_boundary_all_backends`, and `backend_crossover_sweep` — which is the FOUNDATIONAL autoroute contract (all backends produce identical findings; only speed differs). So the calibration parity check comparing chunks-candidates against the coalesced-SIMD reference is SOUND: a divergence is a REAL backend bug it correctly rejects, never a coalescing artifact. The method difference is REQUIRED for timing realism — each backend is measured on the production path a real scan uses for THAT backend (SIMD→coalesced, others→chunks). No test/fix needed. This also confirms 4499's two functions are legitimately distinct (keep separate). status=closed-not-a-bug
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/calibration.rs:217 | dedup | P3 | `calibration_match_identity_set` is a SECOND encoding of match identity (formatted string set) next to `evidence::canonical_match` (tuple) — same six fields, hand-formatted with per-byte hex. It exists only for the inconsistent-reference diff log. If `CanonicalMatch` gains/changes a field the debug diff silently disagrees with the equality it explains. | derive diff from canonical_matches | done 2026-07-04 (evidence.rs render_canonical_match destructures CanonicalMatch — one owner of identity fields; calibration_match_identity_set is now canonical_matches().map(render); byte-identical string format, existing evidence test green)
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/store.rs:218,749,1097 | dedup | P3 | THREE separate read→envelope-version-check→full-deserialize sequences over the same cache file: `load_autoroute_cache`, `inspect_autoroute_cache`, `read_mergeable_configs`. Each re-implements the version gate and JSON-error wording with slightly different messages. | one parse primitive | done 2026-07-04 (store.rs parse_autoroute_cache + CacheParseError{NotJson,Version,Payload}; load/inspect/read_mergeable all consume it with their own policies; all three error-message texts preserved verbatim; dispatch tests green)
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend.rs:482-542 | simplification | P4 | `load_persistent_autoroute_decisions` checks `path.exists()` twice (500 and 508) and carries a dead `_ => HashMap::new()` arm — the second match can only see the Some+exists case after the early return. Also `cache_load_error` is initialized `None` then conditionally set in a way an if-let chain expresses directly. | collapse prologue | done 2026-07-04 (let-else with .filter(exists); dead `_ =>` arm gone; behavior identical incl. host-identity early-return ordering)
- cli/orchestrator/dispatch/backend/store.rs:99-208 | hardcoded-list/coherence | P3 | `AutorouteBuildFeatures::current()` hand-maintains a mirror of the Cargo feature graph in four functions (`current_cli_features` lists 19 literals; `current_scanner_dependency_features` re-encodes which scanner features each CLI feature pulls in). Nothing locks it to Cargo.toml: add a feature (or change `ci-lean`'s scanner set) and the cache identity silently stops distinguishing builds it should. This is exactly the drift class the feature-coherence thread (STATUS-34/36) just closed elsewhere. | emit the resolved feature list from build.rs (`CARGO_FEATURE_*` env at compile time gives the CLI's own features for free; the scanner-dep mapping can be asserted by a gate test that parses Cargo.toml and compares) — at minimum add the gate test | open

Verdict on the non-CLI pieces reviewed: `core/calibration.rs` (Beta confidence store) and `scanner/tuning.rs` are CLEAN on this axis — single owner per value, the posterior-mean/observations one-place contract is explicit and honored, tuning's BoolOverride/resolve-snapshot pattern is uniform across all 14 knobs. `evidence.rs` `two_sided_95_student_t_critical` hardcoded t-table is fine (documented, conservative tail). `workload.rs` is tight. The interpolation/below-floor/diagonal soundness reasoning in store.rs is genuinely well-documented — the issue there is only the duplicated bracket-scan mechanics, not the design.

Reliable cheap triage: `grep 'test <name>' <green-suite log>` — name present in a 0-failed run = stale row; name ABSENT = check if the fn was renamed/removed (also stale) vs gated out of that feature set (unverified). This turn flipped 5 stale row-groups; 4 rows are genuinely open. A fuller sweep of the §OPEN block (lines ~63+) against a green full-suite log would likely drain more.

## T1 — Inline #[cfg(test)] blocks in src/ → move to tests/ (no_inline_tests_in_src contract; §OPEN 3978 floor-gate)

# ── CLASS REVIEW 2026-07-04 (applies to the still-`[ ]` DR-003..DR-040 below): the inline
#    `#[cfg(test)]` blocks DO still exist (spot-checked magic.rs/capped_read.rs/compression_limits.rs/
#    diff_parser.rs/sanitize.rs/web-ssrf.rs/gpu_region_dispatch.rs/mark_stats.rs(11)/fragments.rs — all
#    present, mixed clean/dirty). BUT this is NOT a CI-red backlog: the scanner engine files are ALREADY
#    ALLOWLISTED in `tests/gap/no_inline_tests_in_src.rs` with documented rationales (e.g. `engine/phase2/
#    mark_stats.rs` :228 "exposes a pub(crate) telemetry facade over thread-local mark-gate internals";
#    gpu_region_dispatch :26, phase2_gpu_dfa :31, windowed_support :195, scan_filters :135, engine/mod :223),
#    and the sources files are either uncovered or per-module-gated — so the gate PASSES and NONE are
#    unallowlisted violations (CI is green). VERDICT: LOW-VALUE style-migration, NOT a correctness/gate issue.
#    Migrating an allowlisted inline test OUT requires exposing its `pub(crate)` internals via a testing-module
#    re-export (Law-11 over-exposure) purely to satisfy a style preference — a NET-NEGATIVE trade for tests that
#    already test truth in the right place. Per-file resolution = KEEP allowlisted-with-rationale (the accepted
#    outcome), migrate ONLY a file whose tested items are already pub (no new exposure). Do NOT force the whole
#    series; do NOT edit the dirty ones into the concurrent refactor. Leaving rows `[ ]` as low-pri, not blocking.

## T2 — Hardcoded lists → Tier-B rules/*.toml (hardcoded lists BANNED). VERIFY not already migrated (entropy-floors, DECODER_SUFFIXES already done).
- [~] DR-050 core/safe_bin.rs:22/34 SYSTEM_BIN_DIRS — DEFENSIBLE-KEEP (verified 2026-07-03): SECURITY BOUNDARY (trusted exec dirs), deliberately compile-time + auditable; runtime extension already exists via set_extra_trusted_dirs (absolute-only). Tier-B TOML would make a security control user-droppable = weakening. Do NOT migrate; the hardcoded-lists ban targets DATA lists, not security boundaries.
- [~] OVER-SCOPED (verified 2026-07-04): `EXE_SUFFIXES` is a 4-item OS-gated const (`#[cfg(unix)] &[""]` / `#[cfg(windows)] &[".exe",".com",".bat",".cmd"]`, safe_bin.rs:43/46) feeding the trusted-binary SECURITY check. TOML-editing what counts as an executable is a security-adjacent vector, and the cfg-split is correct per-platform variance (not a duplication violation). Fixed OS-semantics set — stays compiled, not Tier-B.
- [~] OVER-SCOPED (verified 2026-07-04): `ALLOWED_FOR_VERIFY = &["python3","python","node"]` (spec.rs:388) is a SECURITY allowlist of executables keyhog SPAWNS for verification. Making it user-editable TOML violates "fail closed for security controls" (a TOML-write vector → arbitrary-engine spawn / RCE). It must stay compiled. (Note: the sibling `is_allowed_for_verify` uses a STRICTER variant-match that rejects even `Other("python3")`, so the two are not a mechanical duplicate — different intent, kept.)
- [~] DR-053 — DEFENSIBLE KEEP, not Tier-B (verified 2026-07-04): AKIA/ASIA are AWS-SPEC-FIXED key-ID prefixes (long-term/STS), a 2-element protocol constant — NOT community-extensible detection data (the Tier-B doctrine is for CVE rules/wordlists/sigs/grammars). Already a clean ONE-PLACE const feeding the compile-time-derived AWS_KEY_ID_LEN = PREFIX_LEN + BODY_LEN. TOML-ifying adds a loader for a fixed pair, breaks the derivation, and lets a bogus user prefix create FPs. Same class as Z85 alphabet / gzip magic (protocol constants, not Tier-B).
- [~] OVER-SCOPED (verified 2026-07-04): `CWE_HARDCODED_CREDENTIALS_ID = "CWE-798"` + `OWASP_AUTH_FAILURES_ID = "A07:2021"` (sarif.rs:60/61) are TWO fixed standard identifiers (immutable — CWE-798 IS the CWE for hardcoded credentials, A07:2021 IS the OWASP category), already documented as SINGLE OWNER consumed by both SARIF sites (result props + taxonomies) to prevent drift. Not a hardcoded community-data LIST; externalizing immutable standard IDs to TOML adds a parse-failure path and gains nothing. No migration warranted.
- [~] DR-055 OVER-SCOPED (verified 2026-07-04): extract.rs:444 is NOT a data list — it's a format-DISPATCH chain (`if ext=="pdf" → pdf::extract_pdf_chunks; else if "7z" → seven_zip::…; else if "rar" → rar::…; else if is_openpack_archive_ext(ext) → archive::extract_openpack`). Each branch routes to a DIFFERENT extractor FN, so the "list" is code dispatch, not extensible data (a new format needs a new extractor fn + handler, all code). The only sub-list is `archive::is_openpack_archive_ext` (openpack exts) — CONFIRMED already Tier-B (`OPENPACK_EXTS` at archive.rs:49 loads `rules/openpack-extensions.toml`). So NOTHING to migrate: the top-level dispatch KEEPS as code (like DR-058/059) and its only data sub-list is already externalized. Fully resolved..toml (§OPEN 123)
- [ ] DR-056 SCOPED — bench-gated consolidation (verified 2026-07-04): the inline chain in extract.rs:364 (`filename.contains(".min.") || .contains(".bundle.") || .ends_with(".chunk.js") || .ends_with(".min.js") || .ends_with(".bundle.js")`) bypasses the existing Tier-B `DEFAULT_EXCLUDES` owner (`filesystem/filter.rs` → `rules/default_excludes.toml`) — a real ONE-PLACE violation. BUT it's NOT a mechanical move: (1) `.min.js`/`.bundle.js` are REDUNDANT (subsumed by the `.min.`/`.bundle.` infixes) → essential set is `{.min., .bundle.}` infix + `{.chunk.js}` suffix; (2) the mechanism has `suffixes`/`filenames`/`filename_prefix_suffixes` but NO infix/contains field → `.min.`/`.bundle.` need a NEW `infixes` field (schema+parse+validate+match) added to `DefaultExcludeRules`; (3) the inline matches CASE-SENSITIVELY on the basename while the whole mechanism is CASE-INSENSITIVE (`ends_ci`) → consolidating flips CS→CI (broadens to `.MIN.js` etc.). FIX = add `infixes` field (CI, basename-matched) + move `.min./.bundle.` there + `.chunk.js` to `suffixes` + delete the inline gate; the CS→CI broadening is a defensible micro-improvement (minified regardless of case) but is a default-EXCLUDE (recall-affecting) change → CredData A/B before landing (expect ~0 impact; minified-case-variants ~absent in CredData).
  - **EXECUTED + A/B-VALIDATED + LANDED (2026-07-04)**: added the `infixes` field to `DefaultExcludeRules`+`DefaultExcludeSection`+`RuleListKind::Infix` (parse/validate: dot-bounded, no path seps / CI-substring match on basename in `is_default_excluded_bytes`); moved `.min.`/`.bundle.` → `default_excludes.toml` `infixes`, dropped the now-subsumed `.min.js`/`.min.css` suffixes, added `.chunk.js` suffix; deleted the inline gate in `extract.rs`. **CredData A/B (baseline=DR-330-tree scan, churn cancels): detection-NEUTRAL — F1 0.2942 IDENTICAL, tp4109/fp9907/fn9808 unchanged** (2 FPs shifted `entropy-api-key`↔`entropy-token`, net 0). Regression `default_excludes_drop_minified_and_bundled_files_by_infix` (walk-based, in `tests/default_excludes_flag.rs`) locks the behavior: 4 minified/bundled files excluded (incl. the deliberate CS→CI case `App.MIN.js`), 3 near-miss controls scanned (`app.js`/`bundler.ts`/`chunky.js`), skip-count asserted. NOTE1: an in-src `#[cfg(test)]` mod was CAUGHT by the `filesystem_no_inline_tests` ORG GATE (filesystem/*.rs forbids inline tests) → moved to the integration test (walk-based, like the sibling lockfile tests). NOTE2: `--features ci-lean` is scanner/keyhog-only — sources tests must run WITHOUT it ([[ci-lean-feature-is-scanner-only]]). GATES GREEN: sources `all_tests` **721 passed / 0 failed** (the `filesystem_no_inline_tests` org gate now passes — it was the 1 failure that caught the in-src mod), `default_excludes_flag` **17/17** | status=**LANDED-neutral (all gates green 2026-07-04)**oml
- [~] DR-057 MIS-TIERED (verified 2026-07-04): these are DEFAULT public-SaaS endpoint CONSTANTS (`gitlab_group.rs:12 DEFAULT_ENDPOINT = "https://gitlab.com"`, etc.) — the correct canonical defaults, not community-extensible DATA. A service base-URL is an operational knob → belongs in **Tier-A config (CLI/TOML `--gitlab-url` / base-url override), NOT a Tier-B `rules/*.toml` data file** (Tier-B is wordlists/rules/sigs). Override machinery already partly exists (s3 `endpoint`/`base_url`/`allow_private_endpoint`; `hosted_git.rs:269` operator-configured self-hosted clone origins). REAL sub-task = audit each connector (gitlab/bitbucket/github/gcs/s3/slack) for a self-hosted base-URL override and ADD a Tier-A one where missing (a CAPABILITY gap: can't scan self-hosted instances) — NOT relocate the defaults into `source-endpoints.toml`. Original ref: gitlab_group.rs:12/171, bitbucket_workspace.rs:13/200, github_org.rs:319, gcs.rs:380/434, s3/mod.rs:460/480, slack.rs:30/144/145
- [~] DR-058 OVER-SCOPED (verified 2026-07-04): `ENTROPY_DETECTOR_METADATA` (helpers.rs:12) is a FIXED `[(&str,&str,&str);4]` whose entries reference `detector_ids` Rust consts (`ENTROPY_GENERIC`…) and is INDEX-PARALLEL with `classify_entropy_detector_index` (:26), whose branches return the literal indices `0/1/2/3`. The table + classifier are compile-time-coupled by index; the ids are code symbols, not data; it is `#[cfg(entropy)]` + pre-interned into `entropy_metadata_by_index` for perf (PERF-locality_intern-1). Only 4 entries and NOT independently extensible (a 5th entropy detector needs a new `detector_ids` const + a new classify branch + intern slot — all code). Moving it to TOML would break the index-parallel invariant for zero Tier-B benefit → KEEP as const, exactly like the fixed-standard verdicts (DR-051/052/054/057/063a). Not a migration.
- [~] DR-059 OVER-SCOPED (verified 2026-07-04): `generic_keyword_prefilter_stem` is a COMPACTION FUNCTION, not a data list — it reduces each already-Tier-B `assignment_keywords()` entry to a coarse prefilter stem, and its own doc constrains every output stem to be "a recall-preserving SUBSTRING of one or more regex arms" with an `else { keyword }` fallback that keeps unknown keywords' exact spelling so a keyword-list expansion can't go invisible to the prefilter. The genuine DATA (the keyword vocabulary) is ALREADY Tier-B (`assignment_keywords` loads from a data file); the 8 stems here are compaction TARGETS bound by a code-level recall invariant, not user-extensible content — moving them to TOML would let a non-substring stem silently break prefilter recall. KEEP as code, like DR-058 (logic, not data). Not a migration.

The same-vocab-two-places DRIFT risk is closed; `git-sha:` stays local because colon-digest SUPPRESSION intentionally covers only docker/python/git-LFS digests, not commit refs. Behavior-preserving ⇒ verified by tests, not bench; combined `all_tests` with DR-330 = **3981 passed / 0 failed**. `b"git-sha:".as_slice()` chain compiled clean; both gates byte-identical.

Tier-B (user-editable) is WRONG here. At most a trivial named-const hoist for documentation; NOT a Tier-B data file.

- [~] DR-090 STALE (verified 2026-07-04): the canonical decode-depth owner is `core/config.rs` `ScanConfig::default { n: 10 }`, which the CLI maps from TOML `decode_depth` → `config.max_n`; `.keyhog.toml.example:69 decode_depth = 10` AGREES with it. The "4" was `runtime.rs::fused_depth_default(worker_threads)` — a thread-derived FUSED-batch depth, a DIFFERENT knob, not the decode depth. No divergence to reconcile.
- [!] DR-091 .keyhog.toml.example:13 min-severity None/Info vs "medium" — REAL DIVERGENCE, reconcile+one owner
  ↳ TRIAGE (verified 2026-07-04): the report filter is `orchestrator/postprocess.rs:288` `if let Some(min_severity) = filter.min_severity` — an `Option`, so the COMPILED default is `None` = report EVERYTHING (Info+). `.keyhog.toml.example:13 severity = "medium"` therefore does NOT match the default. This is a genuine coherence gap but the reconcile needs a PRODUCT DECISION, not a mechanical swap: either (a) the intended default IS report-all → change the example to `severity = "info"` / comment it out + note "default: report all", or (b) the intended default SHOULD be `medium` (quieter mass-scan default) → change the compiled default (`filter.min_severity` default) to Medium and keep the example. Both are one-owner reconciles; pick per product intent (is a noisy report-all or a quiet medium+ the right out-of-box behavior?). Kept [!].

- [ ] DR-150 DECOMPOSED + MOSTLY OVER-SCOPED (verified 2026-07-04): three separate sub-claims, none a clean mechanical dedup —
  - (a) **comment-prefixes "dup" = OVER-SCOPED (keep separate)**: the three marker lists are LEGITIMATELY DIVERGENT by consumer, not duplicates. `rules/ml-feature-markers.toml` `comment_prefixes` = `[# // /* --]` (4, ML signal, scanner/ml_scorer); `rules/inference-markers.toml` `comment_prefixes` = `[// # /* <!-- <# "* " */ "rem " REM]` (9, richest — HTML/PowerShell/batch/C-block-continuation, scanner/context); `rules/inline-comment-markers.toml` `markers` = `[// # -- /* <!--]` (5, CLI directive parse, cli/inline_suppression). They OVERLAP on `// # /*` but each has purpose-specific extras. Unifying to one owner is NOT behavior-preserving: the UNION over-broadens ml + inline-suppression (they'd match `rem `/`<#`), the INTERSECTION under-covers inference (loses `<!--`/`<#`/…) — inverse of the DR-063b case (there the shared subset was identical; here the sets genuinely differ). Each consumer correctly owns its set (Law 5 responsibility). NOT a dedup.
  - (b) **sk-proj-/sk-ant- under sk- = DO NOT REMOVE (removal REGRESSES, not neutral)** — verified via the consumer, not just the list: `KNOWN_PREFIXES` (confidence/prefixes.rs:26) feeds `known_prefix_body` (:96-101) which strips the **LONGEST** matching prefix (`min_by_key(body.len())`), and the resulting BODY feeds sequence/placeholder suppression (`context::inference`). Removing `sk-proj-`/`sk-ant-` is therefore NOT behavior-preserving: for `sk-proj-aaaaaaaa…` the strip would fall back to the short `sk-` → body `proj-aaaaaaaa…` (NOT an all-same run → NOT suppressed) instead of `sk-proj-`→ body `aaaaaaaa…` (all-same → suppressed). So the "redundant under sk-" premise is WRONG — the specific prefixes are load-bearing for correct body extraction; removing them would let `sk-proj-`/`sk-ant-` placeholders leak. KEEP them. (The confidence-FLOOR use IS subsumed by `sk-` — `known_prefix_confidence_floor` only needs ANY match — but the body-strip use is not, and the body-strip is the binding constraint.) The orthogonal question "is the broad 3-char `sk-` itself too FP-prone for the 0.8 floor?" was **EXECUTED as a same-tree CredData A/B (2026-07-04) and ANSWERED: KEEP `sk-` — it is LOAD-BEARING, not removable.** Removing `sk-` (keeping `sk-proj-`/`sk-ant-`) scored F1 +0.00010 (fp −6, tp/fn +0) on CredData — a tiny STRICT precision gain, all 6 dropped FPs NON-OpenAI (postmark −3, easypost −1, helicone −1, entropy-token −2). BUT the regression suite (blind-spot the corpus misses) proves removal REGRESSES real vendor recall: `regression_hexbody_vendor_prefix_floor.rs` asserts "deepseek `sk-<32hex>` control must keep reporting (no regression)" + "being on the list is what lets a vendor token surface"; classic OpenAI `sk-[48]` fixtures in `openai_html_comment_bug_62.rs`/`regression_env_file_source.rs`/`regression_hot_token_right_boundary.rs` + the `openai-api-key` self-validate fixture all depend on the `sk-` floor. DeepSeek keys ARE `sk-<hex>`. So the +0.0001 corpus gain is not worth regressing test-suite-protected DeepSeek/OpenAI recall — a textbook case of the regression suite catching what a corpus aggregate can't ([[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]]). REVERTED (git diff clean). Same body-strip + vendor-recall caution applies to (c). **DR-150 now FULLY RESOLVED: (a) keep-separate, (b) keep sk- + specifics (both load-bearing), (c) bench-judgment — nothing mechanical to do.**
  - (c) **.env.local/.production/.staging under .env**: same subsumption-judgment — depends on whether the sensitive-path match is prefix/contains and whether `.env` alone is the intended breadth. Bench-attributed, not mechanical.
  - VERDICT: (a) over-scoped keep-separate; (b)/(c) need bench attribution (a deliberate prefix-precision bench session), NOT a mechanical dedup. Original bundled claim: placeholder_words dup your_; audit.toml RUSTSEC dup → derive/consolidate

## T8 — Decode-abort Law-10 (one bad escape drops a valid prefix — recall loss) (§OPEN 66)
- [!] DR-190 scanner/decode/url.rs:611 mime_q_decode hex_val? aborts → treat bad as literal, continue. Same root cause + bench-gate as [[DR-191]] (raw always scanned → narrow impact; literal+continue adds corrupted-partial FP surface; needs CredData A/B). Churned decode/ zone.
- [!] DR-191 scanner/decode/unicode_escape.rs:27 \uXXXX + :37 \xXX abort → literal backslash, continue. ANALYSIS (2026-07-04): `unicode_escape_decode` returns `Err(())` on bad hex (the `?` at :27/:39), which is DELIBERATE per its doc ("skip the candidate rather than emit a corrupted decode"). Impact is NARROW, not total: `decode_chunk` (pipeline.rs:26-34) scans the RAW chunk ALWAYS and only ADDS decoded views (the `seen` set is seeded with the raw key), so an abort loses only the decoded view of THIS candidate — recall loss is limited to secrets whose VALUE is itself fully `\u`/`\x`-escaped AND that carry a bad escape earlier in the same extracted span. The proposed literal+continue emits a PARTIALLY-corrupted decode as a new scan candidate → adds FP surface. Net-positive is NOT obvious → this is a CredData differential-bench-gated detection change (recall gain in the narrow case vs precision cost), NOT a blind flip. Shared root cause with [[DR-190]]/[[DR-192]]/[[DR-193]]. decode/*.rs is the concurrent decode-rework's churned zone.
- [!] DR-192 scanner/decode/json.rs:134 \uXXXX + :139 unknown-escape abort → keep literal, continue. Same root cause + bench-gate as [[DR-191]] (raw always scanned → narrow impact; corrupted-partial FP surface; needs CredData A/B). NOTE: JSON `\uXXXX` is spec-mandatory, so a bad `\uXXXX` is malformed JSON — literal+continue on malformed input is even more FP-prone here. Churned decode/ zone.
- [!] DR-193 scanner/decode/url.rs:285,477 whole-candidate decode fails on one bad escape/entity → literal+continue (§OPEN 66). Same root cause + bench-gate as [[DR-191]] (raw always scanned → narrow impact; corrupted-partial FP surface; needs CredData A/B). Churned decode/ zone. The 4 (DR-190–193) should be resolved as ONE bench experiment: apply literal+continue to all decoders, A/B on CredData (recall Δ vs precision Δ), keep only if net-positive — never merge unbenched.
  ↳ DR-190–193 TRIAGE (verified 2026-07-04): all 4 abort patterns CONFIRMED STILL PRESENT — `mime_q_decode` `hex_val(..)?` (url.rs:603 body), `unicode_escape_decode` `take_hex_digits_indexed(..,4)?`/`..,2)?` (unicode_escape.rs:27/37), json `take_hex_digits(&mut chars,4)?` (json.rs:134), + 11 `?`/`Err`/`return None` whole-candidate aborts in url.rs. Class = a decoder `?`-propagates on ONE malformed escape and drops the ENTIRE decoded view (Law-10-adjacent: the recall loss is a secret that appears ONLY in the decoded form — e.g. `sec…` spelling "sec…" — where a single injected bad escape suppresses the decode; the RAW scan is unaffected, so severity is LOW–MEDIUM, a targeted evasion vector not a broad recall hole). Correct fix = emit the offending bytes as literal + keep decoding the remainder. DOUBLE-BLOCKED for solo execution: (1) all three files are ` M` inside the 973-file concurrent refactor — editing collides; (2) the change alters decode output → MUST be CredData differential-bench-validated (partial-decode can add FP-shaped tokens), not landed blind. Keep [!] (real-open); execute once the decode/ refactor settles + with a bench harness A/B.
- [x] DR-194 DONE (STALE, verified 2026-07-04 = §OPEN 94): api.rs:207-212 now routes through the single owner `super::decision::evasion_decoder_reason(source_type)` which covers BOTH `/caesar` AND `/reverse` (comment :207-210: "Both decoders share ONE owner so this gate can never diverge... was `/caesar`-only, silently leaking `/reverse` generic-fallback noise"). The evasion-decoder set has one definitional home; no /reverse leak.

## T9 — Dead-code removal / visibility demotion (verify zero non-test callers)
- [x] DR-212 (NEW 2026-07-04) LANDED + VERIFIED — the "multiline-only" hypothesis was WRONG. A ci-lean `--tests` build proves ALL 10 warnings point at ONE file: `crates/scanner/src/engine/segment_attribution.rs` (Segment/GlobalMatch/AttributedMatch/NormalizedSegment/SegmentAttributionError/map_offsets_to_segments/validate_segments/checked_end/3×new all "never used/constructed"). Root cause: it's a `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) mod` "parked primitive" that NOTHING references — `crates/scanner/src/testing.rs::segment_attribution` (a `pub mod`, ungated) carries a full SELF-CONTAINED DUPLICATE (its own Segment/GlobalMatch/AttributedMatch/NormalizedSegment/validate_segments/map_offsets_to_segments), and THAT copy is what external tests use (`tests/gap/gpu_ac_degenerate_triples_degrade.rs`, `tests/adversarial/segment_attribution_adversarial.rs`, asserted by `tests/testing_facade_compile_contract.rs:55`). So engine's copy is dead rot + a ONE-PLACE violation. EXECUTED FIX (2026-07-04) = the RE-EXPORT unification (my earlier "re-export impossible" note was WRONG — it works by widening, not by test-crate visibility): (1) `engine/segment_attribution.rs` items `pub(crate)`→`pub`; (2) `engine/mod.rs` drop the `#[cfg(test)]` gate → `pub(crate) mod segment_attribution;` (always compiled, `pub` items); (3) `testing.rs::segment_attribution` body (180 lines) REPLACED with `pub use crate::engine::segment_attribution::{map_offsets_to_segments, AttributedMatch, GlobalMatch, Segment, SegmentAttributionError};`. Now ONE owner (engine), testing is a thin re-export facade, external tests reach the SAME primitive (`pub use` widens the `pub` items from the `pub(crate)` module to the doc-hidden `testing` public surface). This DODGES the churned gates entirely: the 2 gate files still read `engine/segment_attribution.rs` by path (file kept, unwrap/inline-test-free → both gates still pass) so NO `tests/unit/gates/` edit needed — no collision with the concurrent refactor. Dead-code warnings gone (engine's items are now used via the re-export). Logic proven byte-identical (diffed map_offsets/validate_segments bodies). VERIFIED GREEN 2026-07-04: ci-lean lib build (bcs5x092x) exit 0 with ZERO segment_attribution warnings — the 10 dead-code warnings ELIMINATED (only residual is the unrelated pre-existing `GENERIC_PASSWORD`/DR-337) — + fmt clean (rustfmt collapsed the now-shorter `new()` signatures; scoped `rustfmt` on the 3 files, no crate-wide `cargo fmt` collision). Net: 180-line hand-copied duplicate → 6-line `pub use`, ONE canonical owner, 10 warnings gone, both segment_attribution gates still green (engine file kept). DONE.
- [x] DR-200 LANDED 2026-07-04: registry.rs is fully REMOVED (no file/dir, zero SourceRegistry/VerifierRegistry prod refs) but 3 gate tests still read `/src/registry.rs` (non-existent) → CI-RED. Deleted the 3 dead gate files (registry_no_inline_tests/no_unwrap_expect/non_empty) + their mod decls in gates/mod.rs. Exactly the failure DR-200 predicted.
- [~] DR-201 — ALREADY DONE (verified 2026-07-04): the exact fn `record_chunk_and_check_unchanged` no longer exists (renamed to `record_chunk_at_offset_and_check_unchanged`, which IS used as the public incremental-scan API). Stale finding.
- [~] DR-202 — TEST-ONLY FACADE, not a clean remove (verified 2026-07-04): apply_code_scanning_props is reached ONLY via keyhog_core::testing::CoreTestApi by 3 test files. If prod applies the props inline, it's a Law-11 test-only symbol — but removal requires rewiring those SARIF security-severity tests to the prod path. Moderate, not mechanical.
- [ ] DR-203 core/config.rs — ANALYSIS (2026-07-04): DISAMBIGUATED from the CLI path. The CLI config-file `max_file_size` IS wired+effective (`cli/config/schema.rs:73` → read `cli/config/scan.rs:546` → `apply_limits_section` → `resolved.max_file_size` → `sources.rs:201` `fs_source.with_max_file_size`), so a user's TOML limit is NOT silently dropped. DR-203 is only about the CORE-library `Config` struct fields (`config.rs` `max_file_size`/`dedup`), which carry EXPLICIT docs "retained for the canonical config surface but not carried into ScannerConfig" — the live/CLI path uses `ScanArgs`, so a keyhog-core *library* consumer setting `Config.max_file_size`/`.dedup` gets them ignored. `secret_filenames` has 0 non-test readers (genuinely dead). RESOLUTION is an architecture call on the public core `Config` surface: WIRE the core fields into `ScannerConfig` (so library users' values take effect — fixes a real library-consumer silent-ignore) vs REMOVE them (Law-3 compat change to public core API). Not a mechanical fix; needs the core-config-surface owner's decision. Churned core.
- [~] DR-204 REFRAMED (verified 2026-07-04): the `Credential` zeroizing-bytes struct (credential.rs:39) IS publicly exported (`api.rs:15 pub use ...Credential`) and is constructed within credential.rs's own `TryFrom` conversions (credential.rs:217-235), but has NO keyhog-internal (non-test) consumer outside credential.rs. So "delete" is WRONG — it's a legitimate public library-API primitive (zeroizing credential bytes, exactly what a keyhog-core library consumer wants). The REAL question is a security-wiring one: keyhog is a secret scanner, yet found-secret VALUES flow through the finding/verifier/reporter path as plain `String`, not this zeroizing `Credential` — so matched secrets linger un-zeroized in memory. Either (a) accept `Credential` as an external-consumer public primitive (Law-11 "used by external, not internal" carve-out) and keep, or (b) WIRE `Credential` through the finding path so matched secrets zeroize on drop (a real security-hardening architecture change, not a delete). Needs the security-architecture owner's call; NOT a mechanical delete. Churned core.
- [ ] DR-205 scanner/entropy/scanner.rs:69 find_entropy_secrets + :92 _with_threshold zero prod → pub(crate)/facade [REVIEWED 2026-07-04: CONFIRMED genuine — both are `pub fn` (scanner.rs:69,92) re-exported at `entropy/mod.rs:24` (`pub use scanner::{find_entropy_secrets, find_entropy_secrets_with_threshold}`), but the PRODUCTION scan path calls the `_with_canonical_lift_and_lines` variant (phase2_entropy.rs:109), so the two bare forms have ZERO in-crate production caller. Tightening to `pub(crate)` (or collapsing to one facade) is right IFF they aren't a deliberate public LIBRARY entry — an API-surface decision. BLOCKED: entropy/scanner.rs is dirty/M under the active entropy refactor; a visibility change there collides with in-flight structural edits. Do with that refactor, decide public-lib-vs-internal then.]
- [ ] DR-206 scanner/ml_scorer/ml_weights.rs:177 accessors zero non-local → private [REVIEWED 2026-07-04: CONFIRMED — `gate_weight()` (and siblings `expert_fc1_weight` etc.) are `pub(crate)` with ZERO caller outside ml_weights.rs (grep for non-local, non-def, non-test callers is empty — the forward pass reads the parsed struct directly, not via these accessors). Genuine Law-11 over-visibility → should be private or removed. BLOCKED: ml_weights.rs is dirty/M under the active ml refactor; folds naturally into that work. Verify they're truly unused (not reached via a macro/re-export) before deleting.]
- [~] DR-207 — NOT A CLEAN DEMOTION (verified 2026-07-04): find_base64_strings/z85_decode/find_hex_strings are `pub use`-exported decode primitives consumed by MANY tests/ integration tests via the PUBLIC path (regression_cpu_decoder_parity, regression_decode_primitive_bounds, gap/decode_pipeline_layers, regression_z85/hex_*); z85_decode also used in PROD (base64.rs:71). - [x] DR-208 LANDED 2026-07-04: is_gzip_magic + is_zlib_magic were pub(crate) but called ONLY intra-module (inflate.rs:48/53), zero external/test callers → demoted to private `fn`. inflate tests GREEN (2/0).
- [~] OVER-SCOPED / WORKING-AS-DESIGNED (verified 2026-07-04 — CORRECTS an earlier wrong "confirmed dead" note): `has_high_entropy_fast` and its `_x86`/`_neon`/`_scalar` variants are `#[cfg(test)]` DELIBERATELY. fast.rs:260-262 documents it: "NOT wired into any production scan path... it exists so the parity/differential tests can validate the early-exit against the exact reduction on each ISA." It is a TEST HARNESS for the distinct-byte-ceiling early-exit predicate (`log2(unique) < threshold ⇒ skip`), sound by construction (returns false only when a proven ceiling is below threshold, so it's parity with `shannon_entropy >= threshold` BY DESIGN). Not dead code to "wire", not something to "remove" (it guards the early-exit's cross-ISA soundness). The underlying `unique_byte_count` ceiling primitive IS used in production (plausibility.rs:333, ml_features.rs:499, penalties.rs:96, mod.rs:192). SLIVER of a real item: whether the validated early-exit is also applied as a short-circuit INSIDE the production `entropy_from_histogram` reduction — but since the histogram already yields distinct-count ~free, that perf win is marginal and would need a criterion bench to justify; NOT the "wire a dead SIMD path" the finding claimed. Original ref: fast.rs:221,254 + fast_neon.rs:37 + fast_x86.rs:57 (§OPEN 74)
- [~] DR-210 — HEADLINE WRONG (verified 2026-07-04): is_private_ip_addr_fast is NOT dead — it's a public COMPATIBILITY ALIAS delegating to the canonical is_private_ip_addr, exercised by dozens of tests (ssrf_guard, new_verifier_bogon_ssrf, outliers) AND guarded by verifier_safety_contracts ('must call the single classifier instead of owning a duplicate table'). Removing breaks the contract + tests. Sub-parts (bogon redundant loopback/unspec, ssrf 0X, cache dead while) need SEPARATE verification — not the claimed dead-fn removal.
- [x] DR-211 DONE (verified 2026-07-04): `DEFAULT_HTTPS_PORT: u16 = 443` is a single `pub(crate) const` owner at verifier lib.rs:227 ("ONE owner for the `.unwrap_or(443)` that was [inlined]"); a repo-wide grep for a bare `443` literal in `crates/verifier/src` (excluding the const def + tests) returns NOTHING — no duplicate.
