## T13 — Real bugs (verify + fix + regression test)
- [~] DR-213 RESOLVED (verified 2026-07-04): re-ran the full `cargo test -p keyhog-core` (release-fast, `--no-fail-fast`) → **2038 passed / 1 failed**. The lone remaining failure — `perf_algo_complexity::dedup_additional_locations_is_subquadratic_in_group_size` (a subquadratic-TIMING guard, `perf_algo_complexity.rs:158`) — was a LOAD-INDUCED false-fail (3 concurrent cargo builds were hammering the box; timing-ratio guards are contention-sensitive). Re-run ISOLATED/unloaded it PASSES (`1 passed; 0 failed`). So the ~13 baseline failures are GONE (concurrent refactor + the T1 inline-test migration landed them) and there is NO real functional break; CI itself runs core via `--lib` only (ci.yml:441), which never included most of those integration failures anyway. Original triage preserved below. --- ORIG: `cargo test -p keyhog-core` (full suite) was RED at baseline — ~13 gate/test failures in files I NEVER touched (every_embedded_detector_parses::shared_loader_returns_every_embedded_detector, regression_encoding_base64_padding leading_padding_is_rejected, merkle_persisted_cap_trims_in_place, registry_no_inline_tests/no_unwrap/non_empty, auto_fix_no_unwrap_expect, spec_validate_merkle, no_inline_tests_in_src on src/state_file.rs). PRE-EXISTING (not caused by DR-146 — its own rule_filter/allowlist/missing_file_returns_empty tests pass). AMBIGUOUS: some are known T1 inline-test violations (state_file.rs, registry), but every_embedded_detector_parses + base64_padding failing may be REAL functional breaks OR a full-suite CWD artifact (memory [[raw-test-binary-cwd-and-crate-source-reader]]: some gates need `cd crates/core` / read_crate_source; `cargo test -p keyhog-core` runs many binaries, some may read workspace-relative paths). CONCRETE failures w/ panic messages (from full `cargo test -p keyhog-core`):
  * every_embedded_detector_parses:107 — a loaded detector has EMPTY patterns (CWD-independent embedded loader → likely REAL corpus/parse regression OR the loader returns empty-shell specs). Related: toml_compat_missing_patterns_array_errors:75 'Missing patterns array must be rejected' — missing-patterns spec NOT rejected. RESOLVED 2026-07-04 — CONFIRMED STALE TESTS, not real bugs. The embedded corpus has 3 pattern-less detectors (generic-api-key, generic-secret, generic-keyword-secret) that carry NO regex BY DESIGN (documented in-file: 'fires in phase 2, gated by keywords + entropy_floor') and match via keywords — `is_generic_or_entropy_detector` confirms the category. FIXED every_embedded_detector_parses: assertion now `!patterns.is_empty() || !keywords.is_empty()` (a detector must match via pattern OR keyword; neither = corrupt) — a Law-6 TRUTH fix, code was always correct (scanner 4946/0). toml_compat_missing_patterns_array_errors: its test input (keywords, no patterns) is actually a VALID generic detector, so 'missing patterns → InvalidToml' tests an obsolete requirement — needs a validation-CONTRACT decision (require patterns-OR-keywords, reject only NEITHER) before fixing; left for follow-up. ORIGINALLY these two LOOKED like error-VARIANT drift, not real accept-bad-spec bugs: scanner is 4946/0 GREEN and scanning REQUIRES valid detectors, so bad/pattern-less specs ARE being rejected somewhere — the tests assert a SPECIFIC `Err(SpecError::InvalidToml)` variant + non-empty patterns, which a serde/validation refactor likely changed (rejected via a different variant / the embedded loader shape changed). CONFIRM by printing the actual `load_detectors_from_str` return variant + checking whether any embedded spec truly has empty patterns; fix = realign the test variant OR the validation. Treat as PROBABLY-STALE pending that check, not a live corpus break.
  * merkle_persisted_cap_trims_in_place:140 + html_report_summary:181 — SOURCE-SHAPE gates asserting specific code strings (.then_with(...path.cmp), canonical verification predicate) → broke on a refactor, likely stale-gate not real break.
  * github_annotation_escapes_workflow_command_injection:55 — output carries U+FFFD (per [[github-annotations]]/DR-310 sanitize_terminal neutralizes CR/LF to U+FFFD); test may be out of sync w/ that sanitization. VERIFY.
  * no_inline_tests_in_src (state_file.rs) + registry_no_inline_tests/no_unwrap/non_empty + auto_fix_no_unwrap_expect + spec_validate_merkle + base64_padding leading_padding_is_rejected + merkle_persisted_cap — the inline-test ones are known T1; the rest need per-test triage (stale-gate vs real).
  TRIAGE VERDICT: core-red is DOMINATED BY STALE TESTS + known-T1 inline-test violations, NOT real functional breaks (code is correct — scanner/sources/verifier all GREEN). Remaining to triage: merkle_persisted_cap_trims_in_place:140 + html_report_summary:181 (source-shape gates asserting exact code strings — broke on refactor, update to match); github_annotation_escapes:55 (U+FFFD sanitize sync per DR-310); base64_padding leading_padding_is_rejected (per DR-311 code correctly rejects — likely error-msg drift); no_inline_tests/registry/auto_fix gates = known T1. FIX = realign each stale gate to the current (correct) code.
  FIXED 4 stale gates 2026-07-04 (all GREEN, code was always correct): (1) every_embedded_detector_parses → patterns-OR-keywords invariant (3 generic detectors pattern-less by design); (2) leading_padding_is_rejected → correct msg 'padding = with no preceding data' (leading = is idx-0 case, not 'data after padding'); (3) merkle_persisted_cap_trims_in_place → eviction-sort tie-breakers now read a.0.path/a.0.chunk_offset (tuple refactor); (4) html_report_summary → notChecked source var renamed findings→allFindings.
  REMAINING (need judgment, NOT mechanical): github_annotation_escapes (expects %0A but code neutralizes newline→U+FFFD per [[github-annotations]]/DR-310 — a SECURITY test, verify exact U+FFFD output before updating); auto_fix_no_unwrap_expect (flags the DR-303 .expect at auto_fix.rs:476 — a DEFENSIBLE validated-invariant; needs Law-2-waiver-vs-refactor decision); toml_compat_missing_patterns + spec_validate_merkle::missing_patterns_field_is_parse_error — BOTH assert an OBSOLETE contract (patterns required at PARSE time). spec.rs:75 is `#[serde(default)] pub patterns` (proven), so missing patterns parses OK (empty); a regex-kind no-patterns detector is rejected LATER by validate_patterns_present quality gate, and phase2-generic keyword detectors are valid pattern-less. The test's own comment ('patterns is NOT #[serde(default)]') is factually FALSE. REWRITE both to assert the REAL contract (regex-kind no-patterns → quality-gate reject; generic/keyword → valid) — needs the exact validate contract to avoid asserting wrong behavior; no_inline_tests_in_src(state_file.rs) = known-T1 inline-test migration. [registry_* gates DELETED via DR-200.]
  MORE FIXED 2026-07-04: spec_hash_ignores → rewritten as spec_hash_ignores_description_but_binds_client_safe (client_safe is now DELIBERATELY hashed per merkle_spec_hash cs:; description still ignored — Law-6 truth). CORE RED PROGRESS: 13 → 4 remaining.
  MORE FIXED 2026-07-04 (all GREEN): 3× regression_org_split_merkle (cache_temp_file_prefix / merkle_tmp_hygiene / merkle_storage_split) — the tmp-file create+sweep logic was DEDUP'd into shared `state_file::{write_atomically(path,prefix,bytes), sweep_stale_tmp_siblings(parent,prefixes,cutoff)}` (a ONE-PLACE win: merkle AND calibration both delegate). Rewrote all 3 gates to verify the safety contract at its NEW shared owner (write_atomically `.prefix(prefix)`; sweeper `!prefixes.iter().any(|p| name_str.starts_with(p))` + explicit read-dir Err log) + the delegation — non-weakening (13/13 in that file green). PLUS state_file.rs T1 no_inline_tests_in_src: migrated the 2 read_capped inline tests to tests/unit/state_file_read_capped.rs via a new CoreTestApi::read_capped shim (pub(crate) fn not reachable from integration tests otherwise); deleted the inline mod.
  MORE FIXED 2026-07-04 (all GREEN): github_annotation_escapes — scan-derived fields (file_path, detector_name) are neutralized by `sanitize_terminal` (control chars→U+FFFD, replace_controls escape.rs:21) BEFORE `escape_property` %-escapes; the test asserted the old pre-sanitize `%0A`. Updated the 2 stale assertions to the actual U+FFFD (stronger: obliterates the newline; the `lines().count()==1` injection-safety assertion still holds) while KEEPING verification's `%0A` (it uses escape_command_data, not sanitize_terminal) — documented the two-layer defense. PLUS both obsolete-contract tests (missing_patterns_field_is_parse_error, toml_compat_missing_patterns) — rewrote to the REAL contract via public `keyhog_core::validate_detector`: omitting patterns PARSES (serde-default) and a default(Regex)-kind no-patterns detector is a `QualityIssue::Error("no patterns defined")` at validation time (load_detectors_from_str only parses; validate_patterns_present enforces recall-safety). Both green.
  CORE RED NOW 13 → 0 (FULLY DRAINED). SEVERITY_FROM_STR (MINE — user confirmed, now RESOLVED): the untracked `regression_severity_from_str.rs` + modified spec.rs are my own TDD. 2 tests were red (non_string_number wanted 'invalid type'; unknown_variant wanted the `client_safe` alias hidden from the error). FIXED: replaced Severity's `#[derive(Deserialize)]` with a hand-written `impl Deserialize` — `deserialize_str` + a visitor that (a) exact-matches the 6 canonical kebab forms AND accepts the `client_safe` snake alias on input, (b) errors via `unknown_variant(other, SEVERITY_CANONICAL_WIRE_FORMS)` which lists ONLY canonical forms (alias hidden), (c) lets non-string inputs fall to serde's `invalid type` diagnostic. Serialize stays derived (kebab round-trip). SAFE: no binary/non-self-describing serde path for Severity exists (every load is serde_json/toml). All severity tests green. PLUS a 2nd stale patterns-required test fixed: regression_spec_loader_failclosed::every_embedded_detector_has_nonempty_id_and_patterns → renamed _and_a_matcher, assertion now `patterns.is_empty() && keywords.is_empty()` (3 generic detectors are keyword-only by design; DR-213 class). FULL-CORE GREEN 2324/0 verified (reconcile_core_v7). Severity change is a core type used cross-crate → rechecking scanner/verifier/sources next.

## RESEARCH-BETTERLEAKS (2026-07-03, re-appended 2026-07-04 after a concurrent-writer clobber) — competitor deep-read (github.com/betterleaks/betterleaks @f629149, Go ~45k LOC, gitleaks successor by the original author). Engine read in full: detect/detect.go, internal/exprruntime/*, config/betterleaks.toml (331 rules), codec/, sources/. Context: 06-21 fullfield leaderboard — betterleaks F1 0.530 leads, keyhog 0.307 rank 2/6; keyhog WINS mirror (0.92) and beats noseyparker/titus/kingfisher/trufflehog. betterleaks' edge is BOTH P (0.73 vs 0.33) and R (0.42 vs 0.29). Rows:

- STEAL/tokenEfficiency | detection-truth | P1 | betterleaks' "rare not random" FP filter: BPE-tokenize candidate (tiktoken cl100k_base); chars-per-token >= 2.5 (2.1 short-string variant) OR >=5-char NLTK dictionary-word hit => human-language => suppress (bindings_filter.go:134). This is the DICTIONARY-side complement of keyhog's bigram randomness discriminator (KH-L-0413, the +38%-recall lever): the bigram model separates random-vs-English on letter adjacency; BPE token efficiency captures SUBWORD structure (camelCase identifiers, concatenated words) that bigram scoring misreads. Their P=0.73 vs our 0.33 on CredData is substantially this filter — it holds precision on the generic pool where our entropy-token FPs are 94% (fp 4463/tp 291). Evaluate as (a) MoE input feature, (b) precision gate on the entropy-*/generic floor path — the FP pool KH-L-0405/0406 proved is NOT heuristic-tunable may be BPE-separable | bench-gated A/B both corpora | open
- STEAL/generic-stopwords | detection-truth | P2 | their generic-api-key rule ships a decade-curated stopword corpus + match-context FP-shape regex alternation (key_board/mon-key/csrf_token/keyvault/turkey/...), distilled from gitleaks' world-scale FP reports. Diff against placeholder_words.rs + fake-sequences.toml; import the delta as Tier-B with negative twins | one-time corpus diff | open
- STEAL/required-rules | capability | P2 | multi-part rules: primary + `required` co-occurring rules within line/column proximity, declared per-rule in TOML. This is the generic keyword bridge GENERALIZED and data-driven — a surfacing mechanism for the 42.5% anchorless CredData pool (the pool the ledger says needs a new surfacing path; the id+secret pair pattern is exactly aws-id↔aws-secret adjacency) | design Tier-B `requires = [{detector, within_lines, within_cols}]` on DetectorSpec; bench-gated | open
- STEAL/expr-filters | generalization | P2 | rules embed expr-lang predicate filters in TOML (entropy(x)<=t || failsTokenEfficiency(x) || matchesAny(...)) — Tier-B as a bounded predicate language, strictly more expressive than static lists, cached compiled programs. Relevant to A3 (suppression thresholds → TOML): instead of scalar knobs, a bounded expr per detector | assess bounded deterministic eval on the suppression path | open
- STEAL/inline-validation | capability | P2 | 110/331 rules carry a `validate` expr (live HTTP introspect, worker pool + response cache + valid/invalid/unknown taxonomy + env allowlist) as TOML DATA. keyhog has 344 detectors with [detector.verify] — coverage comparable — but compare EXPRESSIVENESS: their expr can branch on status+body; audit whether keyhog's verify spec schema covers the same introspection patterns | schema-gap audit verifier spec vs their 110 validators | open
- THREAT/assessment | research | P3 | betterleaks weaknesses vs keyhog (our moat): no SIMD/GPU (re2 via wazero WASM + AC prefilter; per-rule FindAllStringIndex over full fragment per decode pass; full lowercase copy per fragment); fail-OPEN filter/prefilter eval errors (warn+continue — our Law-10 class); log.Fatal in library constructor; Detector struct mid-v2 deprecated-field graveyard; ~12k test lines vs our differential/proptest/e2e depth; codec is flat segment decode (b64/hex/percent/unicode/ascii), no structured JSON/JWT recursion. BUT they are 3.1s vs our 73s on CredData — their simple engine is 23x FASTER on the real corpus (our §-speed lane) | context row | intentional
- BENCH/pin-freshness | testing | P3 | betterleaks IS in the leaderboard already (06-21). Gap: repo moves fast (clone @f629149 = 2026-07-03; rules + token-efficiency thresholds evolve); verify benchmarks' betterleaks version pin vs latest release and re-run leaderboard on bumps so the 0.530 target tracks reality | check pin, re-run on bump | open

## IDEAS-SPEED+UX+INSTALL (2026-07-04, user-requested brainstorm; grounded in the 06-21 profile: extract=85.6%/8.02M runs, run-count-bound; noseyparker single-pass = the 66x) — rows:

- SPEED/som-window-extraction | perf | P1 | Refinement of the known single-pass lever (front2 memory lever #1): compile all confirmed patterns into ONE Hyperscan DB with HS_FLAG_SOM_LEFTMOST; the single pass yields (pattern, start, end) directly, then run each pattern's full Rust regex ONLY on a ±window around its own SOM hits (captures/validation). Converts O(confirmed_patterns × chunk) → O(chunk + hits × window). Guard: memory warns naive per-pattern anchoring can be WORSE when candidates are dense (base64-trigraph fires everywhere) — so make whole-chunk-vs-SOM-window a MEASURED per-bucket decision through the EXISTING autoroute machinery (decode-density bucket already in WorkloadKey) instead of a heuristic | prototype behind tuning override + differential parity gate + CredData wall oracle | open — SCOPED 2026-07-09 (code-read): this is NOT a from-scratch redesign; the anchor-localization FOUNDATION already exists as `ConfirmedAnchorIndex` (scan_postprocess/confirmed_anchor.rs) — it builds a shared-anchor AC over each confirmed pattern's REQUIRED-PREFIX LITERAL and verifies those `eligible` patterns anchored at candidate positions instead of whole-chunk (phase2_compiled_anchored.rs anchored loop). The gap this row closes is exactly the `else { continue }` at confirmed_anchor.rs:70-72: patterns with NO required-prefix literal (`required_prefix_literals_with_cap` = None) are NOT eligible → they take the whole-chunk `Phase2WholeChunk` fallback (phase2_compiled_anchored.rs:270). So 4786 = LOCALIZE THE PREFIXLESS CONFIRMED PATTERNS via HS SOM_LEFTMOST (a start-of-match position source the prefix-AC can't provide for them), mirroring what ConfirmedAnchorIndex does for prefix patterns. PREREQUISITE — VERIFIED SATISFIED 2026-07-09 (CPU/SIMD HS path): the `hyperscan` 0.3.2 crate the scanner's `simd` feature already links exposes `HS_FLAG_SOM_LEFTMOST` (hyperscan-sys hyperscan.rs:33 = 256) AND its block-scan callback signature is `|id, from, to, flags|` where `from` IS the leftmost start-of-match offset (hyperscan crate lib.rs doctest). The scanner's `HsScanner` (crate::simd::backend) simply does not compile a SOM-enabled DB today (it uses HS only as a trigger prefilter + Rust-regex confirmed validation). So the CPU HS path can adopt SOM with NO upstream vyre dependency (vyre is the GPU megakernel, a separate path — the GPU-side SOM analogue is a distinct follow-up). Integration point: build a SOM-enabled HS block DB over the PREFIXLESS confirmed `ac_map` patterns (those where `required_prefix_literals_with_cap`=None, confirmed_anchor.rs:70-72), then for each SOM hit `(pattern_id, from, to)` run that pattern's Rust regex ONLY on `data[from.saturating_sub(W)..(to+W).min(len)]` instead of the whole chunk. **FEASIBILITY BLOCKER discovered 2026-07-09 (de-risking) — the naive "HS DB with SOM_LEFTMOST" approach is UNDERMINED: the scanner ALREADY tried and rejected SOM_LEFTMOST — `simd/backend.rs:431` comment: "No SOM_LEFTMOST - it causes 'Pattern too large' on complex regexes; match positions are extracted by the regex crate." The prefixless confirmed patterns (no simple literal prefix) are precisely the COMPLEX regexes that blow up HS compilation with SOM enabled, so a SOM DB over them would fail to compile for exactly its target patterns → win evaporates. THE FEASIBLE PATH instead (mirrors what already works): extend the `ConfirmedAnchorIndex` machinery to localize on ANY internal literal substring the pattern requires (not just the leading `required_prefix_literals`) — an AC over each prefixless pattern's mandatory internal literal(s), verifying the Rust regex only in a window around internal-literal hits. This reuses the proven AC-anchor + windowed-verify design (no HS-SOM, no Pattern-too-large risk) and naturally handles the "no required literal at all" residue (those genuinely keep whole-chunk, loudly counted). Options to still try HS-SOM as a secondary lane: `HS_MODE_SOM_HORIZON_SMALL` bounds SOM tracking distance (may dodge Pattern-too-large) — measure which prefixless patterns compile under it; SOM-localize that subset, AC-localize the rest, whole-chunk the residue. NEXT STEP is a data pass: how many confirmed `ac_map` patterns are prefixless, and of those how many have a usable mandatory INTERNAL literal (AC-localizable) vs none (irreducibly whole-chunk)? That count decides the ceiling of this lever. **DATA-PASS LANDED 2026-07-09**: `keyhog_scanner::testing::confirmed_pattern_localization_distribution()` compiles the embedded corpus and partitions the confirmed `ac_map` into `(prefix_anchored, prefixless_with_internal_literal, whole_chunk_residue)` using the SAME predicates the scanner uses (`phase2_required_prefix_literals_for_test` + `compiler_prefix::regex_has_required_literal_run` at `MIN_INNER_LITERAL_CHARS`); the durable test `som_window_localization_ceiling_over_embedded_corpus` (now a standalone CI-wired binary `tests/som_window_localization_ceiling.rs`, `pub mod`-included in all_tests.rs — NOT the `unit/root_facade/confirmed_pattern_profile.rs` shard, which cannot link as an integration binary because its `_mirror` sibling calls the crate-private `confirmed_profile_dump`; that shard stays an #[ignore] lib-measurement orphan tracked under DR-334) prints the distribution + locks that `prefixless_with_internal_literal > 0` (the lever is non-moot). Run: `cargo test -p keyhog-scanner --test som_window_localization_ceiling --features ci-lean -- --nocapture`. **MEASURED 2026-07-09 (embedded corpus, 923 detectors): prefix_anchored=2283 (70.1%) · prefixless_internal_literal=255 (7.8%) · whole_chunk_residue=718 (22.1%) · total=3256.** DECISION = **REDIRECT the internal-literal sub-lever (low ceiling), keep the SINGLE-PASS lever.** Rationale: of the 973 confirmed patterns NOT already prefix-localized, only 255 (26%) carry a required internal literal an AC could anchor on; 718 (74%) have NO required literal at all → irreducibly whole-chunk. CRUCIALLY the 255 addressable patterns are STRUCTURALLY the sparse/cheap ones (a required internal literal means the pattern only confirms where that literal occurs = low run-count), while the 8M-run cost driver (base64-trigraph alternations `(?:NzA|NzE|…)`, homoglyph char-classes — front2 memory) has NO single required literal (an alternation of distinct 2-char strings yields no common literal run) so it lands in the 718 residue that internal-literal-AC CANNOT localize. So the internal-literal extension would localize the patterns that are already cheap and MISS the ones that are expensive — near-backwards from where the cost is. (COUNT-based, not run-count-weighted; a fully rigorous cost weighting needs the `confirmed_pattern_profile_mirror` per-pattern run-count measurement, but the structural argument — addressable set is the sparse patterns, cost is in the literal-less dense residue — already decides the direction.) THEREFORE: do NOT build `ConfirmedSomIndex`/internal-literal-AC as a standalone lever. The residue (718 literal-less dense patterns) is addressable ONLY by the SINGLE-PASS MULTI-PATTERN confirmed extraction (front2 memory lever #1) — combine all confirmed patterns into ONE pass so cost is O(chunk) once instead of O(patterns×chunk) — which is the same architecture as SPEED/vyre-region-batch-cpu (row 4791). CONSOLIDATE: 4786 and 4791 are ONE lever (single-pass multi-pattern phase-2), not two; build that, not the internal-literal partial. HS-SOM_LEFTMOST is ALSO out for the residue (they are exactly the complex regexes that error "Pattern too large", simd/backend.rs:431). The ceiling lock stays green as a drift guard on the distribution. | open — DE-RISKED + MEASURED 2026-07-09: internal-literal sub-lever REDIRECTED (low ceiling); residue → fold into single-pass multi-pattern (4791)
- SPEED/shared-trigraph-scan | perf | P1 | REFUTED-AS-STATED (2026-07-04, corpus grep): the trigraph alternation is NOT byte-identical across detectors — `NzA|...` lives in ONE detector (discord-bot-token.toml keywords+patterns); other vendors have distinct per-detector alternations. The 8M-run cost is per-pattern whole-chunk confirmed extraction (patterns whose trigram keywords occur in ~all base64 => confirmed on ~every chunk). No shared-prefix memoization to hoist; the lever IS the SOM-window single-pass row above | fold into SPEED/som-window-extraction | done (refuted/merged)
- SPEED/incremental-merkle-default | perf | P2 | massive REPEAT-scan speedup orthogonal to the 66x: core/merkle_index exists — wire content-hash skip into the DEFAULT scan path (persisted per-tree index; unchanged files skip phase-2 entirely). CI/pre-commit re-scans become ~O(changed files). Law-10: skip only on proven hash match, loud stat line ("N files unchanged, skipped via index") | wire + e2e + honest reporting | open
- SPEED/vyre-region-batch-cpu | perf | P1 | **THE CONSOLIDATED SINGLE-PASS LEVER (4786 folded in here 2026-07-09).** the GPU megakernel already does multi-pattern presence-per-region batching (vyre scan_presence_by_region); port the same batching shape to the CPU confirmed pass so CPU phase-2 stops being per-pattern. This is the ONE architecture that addresses the 718 literal-less dense residue the 4786 data pass proved is the real cost driver (base64-trigraph/homoglyph alternations with no anchorable literal → cannot be AC-localized → only a single combined pass over the chunk removes the O(patterns×chunk) blowup). Design: one combined multi-pattern pass yields positions, then attach captures/validators only for patterns that actually hit; the whole-chunk-vs-batched decision routed through autoroute's decode-density bucket; behind a ScannerTuning route override, off by default until the differential parity gate (regression_coalesced_reassembly_parity + backend_parity_* + CredData wall oracle) PROVES recall-neutrality. | **BUILD COLLISION-BLOCKED 2026-07-09** — NOT size-deferred: the entire scanner phase-2 zone is foreign-uncommitted (git status: ALL of phase2_*.rs, confirmed_extract.rs, confirmed_anchor.rs, extract.rs, scan_postprocess/* dirty — a 1379-file in-flight foreign refactor). Editing those files now would corrupt the foreign work AND mine (shared-tree policy). This lever's build MUST wait until that foreign phase-2 refactor commits and the tree quiesces; a future turn should re-check `git status crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2*` and start the build only when it is clean. Until then this is the top perf lever ON HOLD, not open-for-editing. | on-hold (collision) — consolidated single-pass architecture; 4786 internal-literal sub-lever redirected here
- TEST/core-security-primitive-property-tier | testing | P2 | initiative (SINGLE OWNER for the core security-primitive property tier — the credential-specific row below is folded in here): add the missing PROPERTY tier (proptest 10k+, arbitrary inputs) over core's security/leak-guard/correctness primitives that had only EXAMPLE coverage. All three files are in `crates/core/tests/property/` (registered in property/mod.rs → auto CI-wired via core all_tests' `pub mod property;`, so NONE are DR-334 orphans) and use ONLY the STABLE PUBLIC API so they survive the foreign scanner refactor. **ALL THREE LANDED + VERIFIED GREEN 2026-07-09:** (A) `credential_contract.rs` (14: 12 proptest@10k + 2 named — incl `CredentialHash` serde: arbitrary-[u8;32] hex round-trip + fail-closed-on-bad-length/non-hex, draining backlog finding.rs:493) — `Credential`/`SensitiveString`/`CredentialHash`: Eq⟺byte/str biconditional (constant-time-compare correctness half), Hash⟺Eq consistency via HashSet dedup (the Arc-interning invariant), Ord=raw-lexicographic + antisymmetry + Eq-consistency, Clone preserves Eq+Hash, serde `deserialize(serialize)==c` over arbitrary bytes (BOTH text+b64 branches — durable guard vs the kimi-wave2 §Critical round-trip corruption class) + explicit `b64:`-looking-text-value regression, Debug/Display exact length-only redaction + the Debug-redacts/Display-exposes asymmetry. (B) `redaction_contract.rs` (7: 6 proptest@10k + 1 boundary) — `redact` leak-guard: short(≤8)→exact `****`, long output length-BOUNDED ≤11 chars regardless of secret size, secret MIDDLE never appears (formula-independent sentinel-elision), ASCII exact prefix+`...`+suffix reconstruction, arbitrary-UTF-8 no-panic + char-boundary safety (the ASCII-fast-path vs chars()-path seam), determinism + boundary examples. (C) `dedup_contract.rs` (5 proptest@10k) — `dedup_matches`: None=1:1 lossless passthrough, Credential collapses to one-per-distinct-(detector,credential) with the output key-set EQUAL to the input key-set (no recall loss / no fabrication — the `DEDUP_LOST_SINGLETON` contract), input-ORDER-independence (sorted output, the SARIF-fingerprint/baseline invariant), highest-confidence-winner (group confidence = max over group), File-scope-never-merges-below-Credential. (D) `raw_match_ordering_contract.rs` (6 proptest@10k) — `RawMatch::Ord` (the sort + capped-heap eviction order that makes the per-chunk finding set REPRODUCIBLE, `push_match`): total-PREORDER laws (reflexive, antisymmetric, transitive), the documented key PRIORITY (confidence-desc dominates, then severity-desc), and sort determinism/idempotence/permutation-stability. INVESTIGATION RESULT (recorded so it's not re-chased): `RawMatch::Ord` is a total PREORDER NOT consistent with its field-wise `Eq` (it keys only on confidence/severity/detector_id/credential/offset/line, ignoring detector_name/service/entropy/etc. → cmp==Equal does NOT imply Eq) — but this is SOUND, not a bug: `grep` proved `RawMatch` is NEVER a `BTreeMap`/`BTreeSet` key anywhere, so Ord is used purely for ordering where a total preorder is correct. The suite therefore deliberately does NOT assert Ord⟺Eq (Law 6 — that would enshrine a misunderstanding). (Minor doc nit, NOT fixed — finding.rs is foreign-dirty: the finding.rs:110 "downstream code relies on for HashMap/BTreeMap keys" comment is misleading since RawMatch is used as neither.) (E) `allowlist_glob_contract.rs` (6 proptest@10k + 1 hand-verified examples) — the hand-rolled PATH-GLOB matcher behind `Allowlist::is_path_ignored` (`allowlist/glob.rs` segment-NFA: `**`=any-run-incl-zero, `*`=within-segment star, whole-path anchored, `dir/`→`dir/**`, path normalized first). SECURITY matcher — over-match silently SUPPRESSES a real finding. Properties: determinism, empty-allowlist-ignores-nothing, ANY-of-globs MONOTONIC union, `**` matches every path (incl empty), pure-literal is whole-path-anchored (matches exact, NOT a longer path NOR a proper prefix), path-normalization equivalence (`./` + `\`-sep). Driven via public `Allowlist::default()` + the public `ignored_paths` field (is_path_ignored rebuilds its index on direct mutation) — no crate-internal path. INVESTIGATION: the `pub(super)` NFA (`glob_match_segments`/`segment_match_units`) is reached through the public method; the semantics examples are each hand-traced against the automaton so they lock EXACT behavior (Law 6). (F) `cross_detector_dedup_contract.rs` (3 proptest@10k + 1 concrete) — `dedup_cross_detector`, the RECALL-SAFETY stage (collapses two detectors on the same credential+file to one winner; losers demoted to `cross_detector.*` companions, never dropped — the `DEDUP_LOST_SINGLETON` guard's whole reason). Properties over the REAL `dedup_matches → dedup_cross_detector` pipeline (so the private-module `DedupedMatch` is constructed the production way, not named): output has EXACTLY ONE finding per distinct `(credential_hash, file)` group AND the output group-set EQUALS the input group-set (no finding lost, none fabricated — the recall biconditional); every demoted loser survives as a `cross_detector.*` companion on its winner (companion count == group size − 1 — no evidence dropped); grouping is input-order-independent; + a concrete two-detector-collapse case (higher-confidence det wins, loser → companion). Total 43 tests, ~38 proptests@10k ≈ 380k property iterations. VERIFIED: credential 12/12, redaction 7/7, dedup 5/5, ordering 6/6, allowlist-glob 7/7, cross-detector 4/4 green; full core `--test all_tests` = 2088+ pass 0 fail. | done 2026-07-09 (all six suites landed + green)- TEST/flaky-perf-dedup-subquadratic | testing | P3 | `crates/core/tests/perf_algo_complexity.rs:158` `dedup_additional_locations_is_subquadratic_in_group_size` is a LOAD-SENSITIVE flaky timing test: asserts the dedup(2N)/dedup(N) doubling ratio < 3.0x (best-of-5), but under concurrent CPU load (e.g. a parallel cargo build) the ratio inflates (observed 3.36x: 9.831ms→33.051ms) and it FAILS; run isolated on an idle box it PASSES instantly (0.15s). It is NOT a real regression — the dedup algorithm is already O(K) via the per-group `seen_locations` IndexSet (dedup.rs:224-228 `insert_new_location_identity`). TWO fixes: (1) the panic message's "ROOT CAUSE" text is STALE — it still cites the REMOVED O(K^2) `additional_locations.iter().any(...)` scan and says "No cap exists / FIX: track seen locations in a per-group HashSet", but that HashSet fix ALREADY LANDED; update the message to reflect current code. (2) the 3.0x threshold is too tight for a wall-clock ratio on a shared/loaded runner — widen to ~4x, OR switch the assertion from wall-time to an operation COUNT (comparisons performed) so it measures COMPLEXITY not throughput (the honest fix: a subquadratic-COMPLEXITY test should count ops, not race the clock). NOT mine and NOT dedup-source-mine (dedup.rs is foreign-dirty); recorded as a test-quality finding. | open — flaky (load-sensitive) + stale panic message; make it count ops not wall-time
- SPEED/gpu-bench-lane | perf | P3 | the leaderboard runs backend=simd only; on GPU hosts the megakernel is the real speed story but is UNMEASURED on CredData. Add a gpu lane to the leaderboard (5090 host) so the GPU speed claim is a measured number, not vibes. (Front-2 "win without GPU" stays the primary lane) | bench lane + pin | open

- UX/warn-dedup-summary | dogfood/UX | P2 | the 5227-identical-WARN class (jupyter parse spam, fixed once) needs the GENERAL fix: a rate-limited warn dedup in the reporter (first occurrence loud + end-of-scan "warning X repeated N times" summary), so the next noisy class can't flood. Law-10-safe: count is preserved and reported | reporter-level dedup | done 2026-07-04 (new crates/cli/src/log_dedup.rs: WarnRepeatLimit tracing Filter — first 3 occurrences per WARN callsite shown, rest counted; WarnDedupSummaryGuard Drop prints "warning at file:line repeated N more times" at run end (Law-10: hidden from stream, never from operator); ERROR never suppressed. The shareable-artifact gap vs commercial scanners | new reporter behind format flag | open
- DOCS/calibrate-coherence | coherence | P2 | TWO real doc bugs in docs/src/reference/autoroute-calibration.md: (1) "The installer runs a visible calibration phase for you: ./install.sh --calibrate" MISREADS — DEFAULT ./install.sh calibrates (do_install → prime_autoroute_cache, install FAILS if calibration fails, install.sh:1226); --calibrate is the standalone RE-RUN mode (install.sh:17). Doc should say: plain install calibrates automatically and fails closed; --calibrate re-runs it later. (2) "keyhog calibrate-autoroute (same work, no reinstall)" OVERSTATES — the subcommand does ONLY the core stdin+filesystem ladder; git/docker/web probes are installer-only (calibrate_autoroute.rs header says exactly this). Doc must state the delta or an operator who only ran the subcommand gets exit-2 on git-source scans and doesn't know why | fix both paragraphs | done 2026-07-04 (docs/src/reference/autoroute-calibration.md Calibrate section rewritten: leads with "a normal install calibrates automatically" + fail-closed install; calibrate-autoroute scoped honestly to the core stdin+filesystem ladder with the git/docker/web installer-only delta called out and the exit-2 remedy named)
- INSTALL/rust-orchestration | robustness/architecture | P1 | the structural robustness play: keep moving install logic OUT of the 2630-line sh + 1926-line ps1 (two divergent implementations of everything — the sed -i/worktree/.zfunc/PATH-normalize bug rows are all shell-duplication artifacts) INTO the binary, following the calibrate-autoroute precedent (its header documents the pattern). Candidates: PATH wiring + completions install + doctor-gate + uninstall manifest → `keyhog self install/update/uninstall`; shell keeps only bootstrap (fetch, checksum, exec). Every migrated phase = one testable Rust impl instead of two shell dialects | migrate one phase at a time, e2e per phase | open
- INSTALL/hardening-batch | robustness | P2 | concrete gaps beyond the 5 open install rows: (a) idempotency e2e (install twice → zero dup PATH entries/completions); (b) concurrent-install lock file; (c) disk-space + writable-dir preflight before download; (d) DONE-ALREADY (verified 2026-07-04: doctor.rs:21-28,116+ has canonicalized PATH-shadow detection); (e) non-TTY/CI mode contract (no prompts, machine-readable phase lines); (f) shell-matrix CI for install.sh (dash/bash/zsh/busybox ash); (g) interrupted-install rollback e2e (kill -INT mid-download and mid-calibration → tree left clean; calibration already traps, download path unverified) | one e2e per item in integration-smoke | open

## T14 — CONSOLIDATE detection into the detector (2026-07-04, user's #1 repeated demand: "detection lives ONLY in the detector; nothing in multiple places")
Read every duplication site before editing. JUDGMENT LOG (Flash-hunter false positives ruled out):
- NOT a violation: the 9× `16 * 1024 * 1024` (state_file CALIBRATION_CACHE/RULE_CONFIG, spec/load DETECTOR_TOML, encoding MAX_STANDARD_BASE64, decode base64 MAX_BASE64/Z85, inflate MAX_INFLATE, sources raw-read/docker) are SEMANTICALLY DISTINCT limits that coincidentally equal 16 MiB — coalescing to one const would wrongly COUPLE unrelated caps that must be free to diverge. ONE-PLACE = one owner per CONCEPT, not per value. Left as-is by design.
- NOT a violation: `detector_ids.rs` is already the single owner of detector-id strings + prefix-derived family predicates, guarded by `detector_id_corpus_guard` (every const must name a real embedded detector) + `is_private_key_block_detector` already delegates to `detector_classification`. Clean.
- [x] DR-320 (DONE 2026-07-04) libsql-credentials contract had AUTO-SYNTHESIZED numeric-noise positives (`libsql://78167825`, cred `78167825`, reason literally "synthesized body satisfying detector's primary regex") that fire **0 matches on EVERY path** (direct + base64/hex/url decode-through) — correctly suppressed as pure-numeric noise. A fake Law-6 test (passes the regex, never fires) + the "autogenerated tests" the user banned. FIXED: replaced with REAL Turso credentials verified to fire on direct AND decode-through — `libsql://myapp-db-a1b2c3d4.turso.io` (cred=host) + `SQLD_AUTH_TOKEN=aB3dEf6gH9jK2mN5pQ8rS1tU4vW7xY0z`; negatives now genuine suppression (placeholder + EXAMPLE marker), evasion=firing host. contracts_runner (7/7) + adversarial_explosion_runner decode-through (2/2) GREEN. This closes the last scanner-red from the prior session.
- [x] DR-321 (DONE 2026-07-04) checksum prefixes duplicated: `checksum/{github,gitlab,npm,slack,stripe}.rs` each hardcoded its own bare prefix literal (`ghp_`, `github_pat_`, `glpat-`/`glcbt-`/`glrt-`, `npm_`, `pypi-`, `xoxb-`/`xoxp-`, `sk_*`/`pk_*`/`rk_*`) that ALSO lives in the detector TOML pattern — same detection signal in ≥2 places, free to drift. FIXED (prefix-literal single-ownership, NOT the full spec-driven-validator rearchitecture which stays a separate larger task): new `checksum/prefixes.rs` is the ONE owner of every family prefix const; all 5 validators now strip via `super::prefixes::*` (no bare literals left in the validator bodies); guard test `checksum_prefixes_are_backed_by_their_detector` binds each prefix to its authoritative `detectors/<id>.toml` (detector = source of truth) AND cross-checks the binding covers the real single-owner set via the `checksum_prefixes()` accessor, so a new family can't slip past unbound. Stripe `pk_*` publishable prefixes deliberately validator-only (public, no detector). Prefix→detector binding lives in the TEST (detector-id strings belong only to `detector_ids.rs` in src, per the `detector_id_owner` gate). CONSOLIDATION SIDE-EFFECTS (3 gates the new module tripped, all fixed same push): (a) `file_gate_matrix_lists_every_scanner_src_module` — registered `prefixes.rs` in `tests/FILE_GATE_MATRIX.toml` (error+adversarial=true, guard-test IS its coverage, matching detector_ids.rs/assignment_keywords.rs convention); (b) `detector_ids_module_owns_scanner_detector_identity` — moved the detector-id binding out of src into the test; (c) guard rewritten to read prefixes from the accessor + bind in-test. NOTE the checksum-fabricated-fixture invariant [[checksum-wiring-invalidates-fabricated-token-fixtures]] is unaffected (validators unchanged behaviorally; only the literal source moved).
- [x] DR-322 (DONE 2026-07-04 — guard, not table-rewrite) `simdsieve_prefilter.rs` `define_hot_pattern_tables!` hardcodes 12 hot-pattern prefix-byte literals (`b"ghp_"`, `b"sk-proj-"`, `b"AKIA"`/`b"ASIA"`, `b"SG."`, `b"xoxb-"`/`b"xoxp-"`, `b"sq0csp-"`, `b"sk_live_"`/`b"sk_test_"`/`b"rk_live_"`/`b"rk_test_"`) that duplicate detector TOML patterns. The detector IDs in the table are ALREADY single-owned (`crate::detector_ids::*`); the remaining duplication is the PREFIX BYTES. Table is RECALL-LOAD-BEARING [[simd-trigger-union-recall-load-bearing]] — a full derive-from-metadata rewrite risks recall + needs the SIMD-trigger-union + backend_parity gates, so NOT attempted. Instead: guard `hot_pattern_prefixes_are_backed_by_their_detector` (in `tests/regression_hot_pattern_process_parity.rs`, an existing `#![cfg(feature="simdsieve")]` file — NOT inline in src, which the `no_inline_tests_in_src` + `simdsieve_prefilter_no_inline_tests` gates forbid) binds every prefix byte to its authoritative `detectors/<id>.toml` via a new `testing::simdsieve_hot_pattern_bindings()` accessor (zips `HOT_PATTERNS`×`HOT_PATTERN_DETECTOR_IDS`; the ids are already `detector_ids` consts so no id literals leak into src). Asserts the prefix appears verbatim in the pattern — SAME detector-is-source-of-truth pattern as the checksum-prefix guard (DR-321), ZERO change to the recall-load-bearing table. All 12 bindings pre-verified present. Closes the silent-drift hole (a prefilter prefix diverging from its detector = an invisible SIMD-path recall hole) without touching the trigger set. (First attempt put the guard inline in src — tripped the two no-inline-test gates; moved to tests/.)
- [x] DR-323 (RESOLVED-INTENTIONAL 2026-07-04) `ml_scorer/ml_features.rs:51` `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_PREFIX="AKIA"` + `:50 OPENAI_PREFIX="sk-"` vs `core/aws.rs:36 AWS_KEY_ID_PREFIXES=["AKIA","ASIA"]` and the aws/openai detector TOMLs. ANALYSIS: `"AKIA"`/`"ASIA"`/`"sk-"` are AWS/OpenAI's OWN public identifier prefixes (stable vendor constants, not keyhog detection vocab that drifts), consumed by THREE independent concerns that merely share the literal — (1) DETECTION = the `aws-access-key`/`openai-api-key` detector TOML patterns (Tier-B, the source of truth per "detection lives only in the detector"); (2) verifier account-decode = `core/aws.rs` (private `mod aws`; the const is verifier enrichment); (3) an ML FEATURE = the `ml_features` `starts_with(prefix)` model input, whose exact string is part of the trained model's input contract [[ml-model-must-train-on-real-distribution]] and CANNOT change source/value without a distribution shift (note `ml_features` uses the BROAD `"sk-"`, deliberately NOT the detector's `"sk-proj-"` — a different feature by design). Forcing a shared Rust const would (a) require adding public API to a currently-private core module, (b) create core↔scanner coupling for a vendor constant, and (c) risk the ML feature contract — net-negative. Verdict: NOT one concept duplicated but three concerns sharing a vendor literal; each names it locally. Left independent BY DESIGN (documented here so it isn't re-flagged). Tried the core-side rename (`AWS_LONG_TERM_KEY_PREFIX`) — removed zero duplication (the string still appears once), pure churn, reverted.
- [~] DR-324 (BENCH-GATED — not a duplication bug) `entropy/keywords.rs` `CREDENTIAL_COMPACT_KEYWORDS` + `KEY_MATERIAL_COMPACT_KEYWORDS`. Re-examined: these are NOT duplicated — `KEY_MATERIAL_COMPACT_KEYWORDS` is documented as the canonical owner shared by BOTH the compact-credential membership check AND `entropy::scanner::KEY_MATERIAL_ANCHORS` (single-owned WITHIN entropy). So the ONE-PLACE issue here is already satisfied. TWO genuine-but-gated items remain, NEITHER a quick safe fix: (A) Tier-B migration — moving the vocab from inline `const` arrays to `rules/` data files is an ARCHITECTURAL aspiration ("detection vocab as data"), behavior-preserving only if the data file transcribes the lists exactly; the entropy compact-credential check gates entropy findings, so any transcription slip is a silent recall/precision shift → needs CredData A/B. (B) cross-subsystem VOCAB DIVERGENCE — `KEY_MATERIAL_COMPACT_KEYWORDS` compact forms (privatekey/signingkey/encryptionkey/secretkey/sessionkey) that the GENERIC_RE `assignment_keywords` bridge does NOT capture; unifying widens the generic bridge's FP surface → explicitly bench-gated (CredData A/B). The `masterkey` sub-divergence from this cluster is now CLOSED by DR-326 (added to generic-api-key.toml). Both A and B require the paid CredData A/B loop [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]] [[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]] — cannot be validated in this env; forcing them blind violates the recall-change discipline. Left tracked, not force-closed | status=bench-gated

## T14 — scanner-red drain (2026-07-04) — the full `cargo test -p keyhog-scanner --features ci-lean` red surface
- [x] DR-325 (DONE) 3 test-wiring manifest gates RED (`gap_mod_covers_every_gap_rs_except_mod`, `a3_multiline_unit_files_are_declared`, `every_scanner_test_module_file_is_wired`) — 3 VALID migrated tests on disk had lost their `mod` declaration in the refactor (invisible coverage loss): `gap/context_cfg_test_attribute_marks_test_code`, `unit/a3_multiline/{inline_array_quoted_key_not_spliced,template_reference_brace_inside_string_literal}`. Verified their symbols still exist, wired all 3 back into their mod.rs. All 3 gates GREEN (verified in reconcile). Restored coverage, not a gate weakening.
- [x] DR-327 (DONE) `contract::every_detector_has_at_least_one_pattern` RED — stale patterns-only twin of the core DR-213 gate (3 generic detectors are keyword-only by design). Re-pointed at the single-owner `is_generic_or_entropy_detector` predicate (same as the correct truth-matrix twin); tests the gated `load_detectors` population. GREEN.
- [x] DR-326 (FIX APPLIED) `masterkey_hex32_surfaces` RED — real recall regression, root-caused via repro (`master_key`=hex32 surfaces, `masterkey`/`masterKey` = 0 matches; `apikey`/`secret_key`/`secretkey` surface). ARCHITECTURE: the runtime capture vocab `ASSIGNMENT_KEYWORDS` is DERIVED from the DETECTOR keywords (`derive_assignment_keywords` unions every `service=generic kind=phase2-generic` detector's keywords → `separator_spellings`, which for `master_key` emits only the 3 separator forms, NO compact). `apikey`/`accesskey`/`secretkey` capture because `generic-api-key.toml` LITERALLY lists those compact forms; `master_key` had no compact twin. (My first stab edited `RECALL_CRITICAL` — a `#[cfg(test)]` recall-floor const, NOT the runtime vocab — reverted; it would have broken the floor test.) REAL FIX = detector-is-source-of-truth: added compact `"masterkey"` to `generic-api-key.toml` keywords (consistent with the existing apikey/accesskey/secretkey compact twins). derive_assignment_keywords now emits it → GENERIC_RE captures → `is_strong_keyword_anchored_hex_key` surfaces the hex32. Verifying; recommend CredData A/B (low risk — strong anchor, compact twin of an existing keyword).
- [x] DR-328 (DONE 2026-07-04 — corrected fix) `decoded_real_secret_text_and_canonical_hex_keys_still_surface` RED. Two tests are in genuine tension at `decoded_benign_text_reason(base64)`: the md5/sha1 UNIT test (`canonical.rs`) calls it DIRECTLY with no keyword and requires a base64-wrapped md5(hex32)/sha1(hex40) → `Some("decoded_bare_hash_digest")` (suppress); the canonical-keys INTEGRATION test requires a base64 AES-128/192/256 KEY under `api-key:`/`token:` to SURFACE. Both decode to hex32/40/48 — the FUNCTION (which sees only the base64, no keyword) CANNOT tell md5 from an AES key by length. My first stab (`&& !matches!(len,32|40|48)` in `decoded_benign_text_reason`) satisfied the integration test but BROKE the md5/sha1 unit test (surfaced hashes) — wrong level. REVERTED. CORRECT FIX = discriminate at the CALLER (`engine/phase2_generic_shape.rs:326`), which HAS the anchor context: it already computes `allow_decoded_hex_key_material = allow_encoded_text_secret && decode_evidence.decoded_is_hex_key_material()` (the latter = `matches!(decoded_len,32|40|48)`) and already exempts the `decoded_placeholder` reason with it — I extended that SAME exemption to `decoded_bare_hash_digest`. Net: `decoded_benign_text_reason` is UNCHANGED (md5/sha1 unit test green); a decoded hex key under a STRONG keyword anchor (allow_encoded_text_secret=true) surfaces (integration test green); a bare md5/sha1 with no strong anchor keeps allow_decoded_hex_key_material=false and stays suppressed (precision held). This mirrors the DIRECT path's `is_strong_keyword_anchored_hex_key` — ONE key-vs-hash policy across both paths, reusing the existing anchor mechanism (no new logic). Still recommend a CredData A/B on the decode-through FP surface to confirm no real-world regression.
- [x] DR-329 (DONE 2026-07-04) PEM `-----BEGIN` marker (private-key detection signal, in the `private-key`/`ssh-private-key`/`github-app-private-key` detector patterns) was hand-rolled as a bare `starts_with("-----BEGIN")` in TWO scanner-logic sites with NO canonical owner: `suppression/decision.rs` (carve-out so a PEM body is not masking-pattern suppressed → detector would silently miss real OPENSSH keys) and `entropy/plausibility.rs` (marks the value plausible). FIXED (behavior-preserving): single-owner `pub(crate) const PEM_BEGIN_MARKER: &str = "-----BEGIN"` + `pub(crate) fn is_pem_block(value)` in `credential_shapes.rs` (its module doc: "Detector-owned credential shape rules"); both sites now call `credential_shapes::is_pem_block(...)`; `testing::pem_begin_marker()` accessor + guard `pem_begin_marker_is_backed_by_the_private_key_detector` binds the const to `private-key.toml` (same detector-is-source-of-truth pattern as DR-321/322). No new module (avoids FILE_GATE_MATRIX churn). CI-COVERAGE FIX: the guard was FIRST put in `tests/pem_private_key_recall_64.rs`, but that file is a CI-ORPHAN (autotests-discovered only; NOT `#[path]`-included in `all_tests` and NOT named by any workflow `--test` — CI runs only `--test <runner>` subsets + `--test all_tests` + `--lib`, never a bare `cargo test -p keyhog-scanner`), so the guard would NEVER run in CI. MOVED to `tests/regression_github_app_pem_boundary.rs` (a `regression_*` file `#[path]`-included in `all_tests`, which ci.yml:229 runs via `--test all_tests`). Verified: guard passes (`pem_begin_marker_is_backed_by_the_private_key_detector ... ok`). All 4 marker/prefix guards now CI-covered: DR-321 in the lib (`--lib`), DR-322/329/330 in `all_tests` regression files (`--test all_tests`).
- [ ] DR-330 JWT-shape: canonical `crate::jwt::looks_like_jwt` (jwt.rs:148, validates 3 base64url segments) EXISTS, but three sites hand-roll a WEAKER `starts_with("eyJ")` approximation instead: `entropy/plausibility.rs:112` (`starts_with("eyJ") && matches('.').count()==2`), `:216` (prefix-only), `suppression/shape/canonical.rs:72` (`is_jwt_like`). Same-concept divergence — the hand-rolled forms accept non-JWT `eyJ…` strings the canonical predicate would reject, so the JWT-shape decision differs by call site. UNIFY to `looks_like_jwt`, BUT this TIGHTENS the shape test on the entropy plausibility + canonical-suppression paths (fewer false JWT matches → behavior change on those gates) → BENCH-GATED (CredData A/B) like DR-324. Encode now, validate before landing. SAFE SUB-PART — DONE 2026-07-04: the bare `"eyJ"` header LITERAL is now single-owned as `jwt::JWT_BASE64_HEADER_PREFIX` + `has_jwt_header_prefix()`; the 3 sites (`plausibility.rs:112` [keeps its `&& matches('.').count()==2`], `plausibility.rs:216`, `canonical.rs:72`) call the predicate while KEEPING their own extra conditions so behavior is byte-identical; `testing::jwt_header_prefix()` accessor + guard `jwt_header_marker_is_backed_by_the_jwt_detector` (in `tests/regression_jwt_header_field_order_recall.rs`) binds the const to `jwt-token.toml`. The FULL `looks_like_jwt` shape unification (tightens the plausibility gate → behavior change) REMAINS bench-gated. PRECISION (verified 2026-07-04): `looks_like_jwt` (jwt.rs:148) is currently `#[cfg(test)]`-gated (test-only), so the full unification ALSO requires un-gating it to a production fn first (its `jwt_segments` helper is already non-test). Concrete plan when benched: (1) drop `#[cfg(test)]` on `looks_like_jwt`; (2) at `plausibility.rs:137` replace `has_jwt_header_prefix(v) && v.matches('.').count()==2` with `looks_like_jwt(v)` (base64url-validates each of the 3 segments vs bare dot-count), at `:232` and `canonical.rs:72` decide prefix-only-vs-full per gate; (3) CredData A/B — the tightening flips `eyJ…`-prefixed non-JWT base64 blobs from rejected→scored on the plausibility path (precision/recall tradeoff), so measure per-detector before landing.
  - **EXECUTED + A/B-VALIDATED + LANDED (2026-07-04)**: did the full-unification at the PRIMARY site the row flagged. (1) dropped `#[cfg(test)]` on `jwt::looks_like_jwt` → production fn (0 non-test callers before; now 1 production caller, no Law-11 issue); (2) `plausibility.rs:137` `has_jwt_header_prefix(v) && v.matches('.').count()==2` → `has_jwt_header_prefix(v) && crate::jwt::looks_like_jwt(v)` — the **recall-safe** form (keeps the `eyJ` anchor + ADDS real 3-base64url-segment validation ⇒ new set is a strict SUBSET of old ⇒ scores a superset ⇒ recall cannot decrease; avoids the bare-`looks_like_jwt` broadening that would drop the `eyJ` requirement and reject any `x.y.z` base64url). (3) **CredData A/B** (same-tree baseline vs change, churn cancels): **detection-NEUTRAL — overall F1 0.2942 IDENTICAL, tp4109/fp9907/fn9808 unchanged**; only 6 entropy candidates re-labeled `entropy-token`↔`entropy-api-key` (net tp/fp cancel exactly). Pure ONE-PLACE win (canonical predicate replaces the hand-rolled eyJ+dot-count) at zero detection cost; full scanner `all_tests` = **3981 passed / 0 failed** (combined with DR-063b). REMAINING (optional, lower-value): `plausibility.rs:232` (prefix-only broad reject) + `canonical.rs:72` (`is_jwt_like` suppression) serve different purposes — leave unless a future A/B shows a win | status=primary-site-LANDED-neutral, 2 secondary sites optional
- [x] DR-333 (DONE — re-confirmed 2026-07-04: the described `(?`/`^`/`ssh-`/`$ANSIBLE_VAULT`/`age1`/`vault:`/`ENC[`/… vocab lives in `matches_universal_rejection`, now externalized to `rules/universal-rejection-prefixes.toml`; `is_known_non_secret`'s ONLY residual inline literals are the single `data:image/` scheme + the `Ag`+length COMPOUND — both correctly kept in code, per the file's own doc, since a one-element toml is over-engineering and the compound isn't a pure prefix. Nothing left to migrate.) `entropy/plausibility.rs:108-120` `is_known_non_secret` hardcodes a ~15-entry structured/encrypted-secret PREFIX vocabulary inline (`(?`, `^`, `ssh-`, `ecdsa-`, `eyJ`, `$ANSIBLE_VAULT`, `ENC[`, `-----BEGIN`, `Ag`+len, `age1`, `vault:`, `AQI`, `CiQ`, …) as a chain of `value.starts_with("…")` — a hardcoded detection-adjacent list, banned per Tier-B ("hardcoded lists are banned"). Several entries duplicate owned markers (`-----BEGIN` = DR-329 `PEM_BEGIN_MARKER`; `eyJ` = DR-330 jwt marker; `age1`/`vault:`/`ENC[` are sops/vault/ansible structured-secret markers). FIX: move the list to a Tier-B `rules/` data file (e.g. `structured_secret_prefixes.toml`) parsed once (mirror `secret_prefixes.rs`'s loader+legacy-parity-guard pattern), and reference the already-owned markers for the overlapping entries. Behaviour-preserving only if transcribed exactly → the plausibility gate feeds entropy scoring, so CredData A/B before landing | status=**MIGRATED+VERIFIED (2026-07-04)**: the 19 pure-`starts_with` prefix terms (`/`, `./`, `../`, `${`, `{{`, `${{`, `(?`, `^`, `ssh-`, `ecdsa-`, `$ANSIBLE_VAULT`, `ENC[`, `age1`, `vault:`, `AQI`, `CiQ`, ```` ``` ````, `---`, `===`) moved to the single Tier-B owner **`rules/universal-rejection-prefixes.toml`** behind `UNIVERSAL_REJECTION_PREFIXES` (LazyLock, fail-closed). The overlapping OWNED markers were NEVER raw prefixes here — the fn already delegates to `is_pem_block()` (PEM) + `has_jwt_header_prefix()` (JWT 3-part), and those + `Ag`+len + Windows-drive-path + `contains("://")` correctly STAY inline (compound, not pure-prefix) → no duplication introduced. Transcribed EXACTLY (byte-for-byte, order-independent `.any(starts_with)` == the former `||` chain) so behavior is unchanged ⇒ no bench needed for the externalization itself. `cargo check` exit 0; behavior-preservation via the exact `gap/entropy_universal_rejection_prefix_gate_exact` test. (Future CONTENT additions to the file remain CredData-A/B-gated, as noted.)
- [ ] DR-332 (ARCHITECTURE — the unified prefix-vocab vision, the ROOT of the scattered-prefix cluster DR-321/322) the SAME vendor secret-prefix vocab (`ghp_`, `sk_live_`, `glpat-`, `npm_`, `xoxb-`, `sk-proj-`, …) is maintained in FOUR independent places, each for a different consumer: (1) the detector TOML patterns = DETECTION source of truth; (2) `checksum/prefixes.rs` = validator self-selection (now bound to detector by the DR-321 guard); (3) `simdsieve_prefilter.rs` HOT_PATTERNS = SIMD prefilter trigger (now bound by the DR-322 guard); (4) `rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml` = split-secret reassembly gate (Tier-B, byte-for-byte legacy-parity guarded, but NOT bound to any detector — a team can add a prefix matching no detector, or a detector prefix can be absent here, silently). The guards (DR-321/322) PREVENT DRIFT but each subsystem still MAINTAINS its own copy. TRUE ONE-PLACE = the detector TOMLs are the sole source and (2)(3)(4) DERIVE their prefix sets from detector metadata (literal-anchor extraction at build/load) rather than hand-maintained lists. This is the categorical fix for "detection lives ONLY in the detector" applied to prefixes. LARGE + recall-sensitive (simd is recall-load-bearing [[simd-trigger-union-recall-load-bearing]]; multiline affects split-secret recall) → needs a design pass + backend_parity + SIMD-trigger-union + CredData bench before landing. FEASIBILITY (established 2026-07-04): the prefix is NOT hidden in the regex — every prefix-anchored detector ALREADY declares it in its `keywords` field (`github-classic-pat: keywords=["ghp_"]`, `gitlab-personal-access-token: ["glpat-","gitlab"]`, `npm-access-token: ["npm_"]`, `aws-access-key: ["AKIA","ASIA"]`, `slack-bot-token: ["xoxb","slack"]`). So the single source ALREADY EXISTS (detector `keywords`), and derivation is the SAME mechanism as `derive_assignment_keywords` (which builds the GENERIC_RE vocab from detector keywords). The one wrinkle: a detector's keywords mix the PREFIX token (`glpat-`, `AKIA`) with a SERVICE word (`gitlab`, `slack`) — the derivation must pick the prefix (heuristic: separator-terminated / non-dictionary token, or an explicit `checksum_prefix`/`hot_prefix` detector-spec field added to disambiguate cleanly). Cleanest end-state: add an optional typed field to the detector spec so the prefix is DECLARED (not heuristically extracted), and checksum/simd/multiline consume it. NEXT-BIG-CONSOLIDATION; DR-321/322 guards + DR-329/330 markers are the incremental down-payment (they PREVENT drift; this ELIMINATES the copies). VERIFIED-CURRENT (2026-07-10, grep of live tree): HOT_PATTERNS = {AKIA, ASIA, ghp_, rk_live_, rk_test_, SG., sk_live_, sk-proj-, sk_test_, sq0csp-, xoxb-, xoxp-}; the child rows 6194/6195/6196/6197/6215/6216/6221 are ACCURATE-not-stale — `github_pat_`, `glpat-`, `npm_`, `sq0atp-`, `sk-ant-`, `hf_`, `pypi-` (and per 6199/6203/6228 `gloas-`, `AIza`, `glrt-`, `glcbt-`) are genuinely ABSENT from HOT_PATTERNS. BLOCKED-ON-FOREIGN: the per-prefix and derive fixes all require editing `secret_prefixes.rs` (LEGACY const + count), `checksum/prefixes.rs`, and `detector_ids.rs`, and ALL THREE are foreign-dirty (active multi-agent uncommitted churn — a same-file edit now risks colliding with and clobbering that work, banned). SCOPE-DECISION: the individual "add HOT_PATTERNS entry" fixes (6194-6228) are NET-NEGATIVE as standalone edits — each grows location-(3) while (2)(4)(5) still drift, i.e. MORE hand-maintained duplication, the opposite of this row's goal; they must land as a BATCH atop the derive step (single detector-keyword source → checksum/simd/multiline consumers), not piecemeal. This head row owns the cluster status; do not resolve 6194-6228 individually | status=open-architecture
- [ ] DR-334 (CI-ORPHAN test files — real coverage hole, surfaced while placing the DR-329 guard) CI never runs a bare `cargo test -p keyhog-scanner` (all invocations are `--test <runner>` subsets, `--test all_tests`, or `--lib`). So a top-level `tests/*.rs` file runs in CI ONLY if it is (a) `#[path]`-included in `all_tests.rs`, (b) named by a workflow `--test <stem>`, or (c) inline in the lib. `recall_locks_wired.py` enforces this ONLY for `regression_*.rs` files. NON-`regression_*` top-level test files that are autotests-discovered but none of (a)/(b)/(c) are SILENT CI-ORPHANS — they pass locally (`cargo test -p keyhog-scanner` autotests) but NEVER run in CI, so the regressions they guard can ship. CONFIRMED orphan: `pem_private_key_recall_64.rs` (a real PEM-recall regression lock for task #64 — its `-----BEGIN` confidence-floor / PEM-body-entropy-bypass assertions have NOT been running in CI). FIX: (1) extend `recall_locks_wired.py` to cover EVERY top-level `tests/*.rs` (not just `regression_*`), failing on any autotests-only orphan; (2) wire the orphans found (start: `pem_private_key_recall_64.rs` → `#[path]` into `all_tests.rs`, adjusting its `mod support;` to the nested form the other all_tests entries use). Audit needed: grep every top-level `tests/*.rs` not in all_tests / not a `--test` target to size the orphan set.
  - AUDIT DONE (2026-07-04): (1) the originally-confirmed orphan `pem_private_key_recall_64.rs` is NOW WIRED into `all_tests.rs` (2 refs) — resolved. (2) A grep-audit of all 262 scanner top-level test files vs all_tests `#[path]`/`mod` entries (207) + workflow `--test` targets (89) surfaced 5 candidates, but 1 (`regression_keyword_bridge`) is a FALSE POSITIVE (actually wired, all_tests=2 — the grep regex missed its entry) → **grep is not a sound oracle for this gate; a real check needs the build graph, not text**. (3) Of the 4 remaining, THREE are UNTRACKED (`homoglyph_normalize_consistency`, `ml_model_arch_wgsl_parity`, `regression_prefilter_trigger_union`) = another agent's uncommitted work — NOT shippable CI-orphans (CI checks out tracked files only; this is ALSO why `recall_locks_wired.py`'s `git ls-files` correctly doesn't flag the untracked `regression_prefilter_trigger_union` — it will be gate-checked once committed). (4) The ONE tracked candidate `entropy_placeholder_marker_truth_table.rs` is a top-level `#[path]`-WRAPPER around `unit/root_facade/entropy_placeholder_marker_truth_table.rs` (registered in `unit/root_facade/mod.rs`); whether its underlying test runs in CI depends on the intricate all_tests↔unit inclusion path (all_tests has NO bare `mod unit;`), so the durable gate-extension MUST be wrapper-aware (a wrapper of an already-covered unit test is not an orphan) — a text-grep extension would false-positive every such wrapper. NET: no confirmed shippable tracked orphan remains right now; the real deliverable is a BUILD-GRAPH-based (not grep) wrapper-aware wiring gate | status=scoped, no live orphan

  CONTEXT (found while sweeping): this is a KNOWN, in-progress effort — `scripts/gates/tests_wired.py` already generalizes `recall_locks_wired.py` to ALL top-level tests of `ENFORCED_CRATES = ["verifier","core","sources"]`; the SCANNER is not yet enforced (it carries the orphan backlog). `recall_locks_wired.py` (scanner `regression_*` only, run via `run_all.sh`) currently FAILS with **66** orphaned `regression_*.rs` (verified all 66 are TRUE orphans — 0 wired via `pub mod` elsewhere).
  SWEEP ATTEMPT 2026-07-04 (bulk wire → REVERTED, proves it needs incremental triage): wired 65 `regression_*` orphans into `all_tests.rs` and ran `--test all_tests`. They COMPILE clean (no API rot) but running them exposed exactly why they were never wired: (a) ROT — ≥4 tests fail asserting behavior the code drifted from while the test never ran: `regression_backend_trigger_parity` ×3 (CPU-vs-CPU SimdCpu/CpuFallback trigger parity + absolute-offset contract — either stale AWS-checksum fixture `AKIAQYLPMN5HFIQR7XYA` now dropped per [[checksum-wiring-invalidates-fabricated-token-fixtures]], or a REAL collector divergence — must diff the two backends' finding sets), `regression_decode_url_plus::trailing_bare_percent_after_valid_escape_emits_no_url_layer` ×1; (b) RUNAWAY — the all_tests binary hit **11 GB RSS / 70+ min CPU** (normal all_tests is ~12 min / <2 GB), so ≥1 orphan has a pathological memory/time test — prime suspects the depth/DoS/corpus ones (`regression_structured_depth_dos`, `regression_reverse_decode_depth`, `regression_nested_base64`, `regression_recall_cloud_providers/vcs_saas`); if a depth test is unbounded that is a REAL DoS regression the orphan catches (security-critical) — needs isolated investigation. DoS-REGRESSION RULED OUT (probed 2026-07-04): `regression_structured_depth_dos` (26 tests/0.01s), `regression_reverse_decode_depth` (13/2.5s), `regression_nested_base64` (15/4.6s) all PASS FAST individually — so the 11 GB is CUMULATIVE memory from 65 modules each building a ~919-detector `CompiledScanner` in ONE binary (volume → RAM pressure → swap thrash → the 70-min CPU), NOT a single pathological test. Implication: the orphans are individually fine; the sweep issue is (a) the 4 rot failures and (b) don't cram all 65 into one all_tests binary — split, or ensure tests share a scanner. REVERTED the bulk wire (`all_tests.rs` back to the bv3d754cu-green baseline; the 4-fixes+4-guards work stays verified). CONCLUSION: the sweep MUST be incremental — wire in small batches, and for each failing lock fix to current truth (Law 6/9, never delete/weaken), bound or move-to-nightly the heavy/DoS tests. This is a multi-session triage, NOT a bulk edit; bulk-wiring hangs CI, which is precisely why 66 were left orphaned. One of the 66 (`regression_entropy_floor_per_family.rs`) is git-tracked but DELETED in the worktree (status `D`, another agent's uncommitted deletion) — a ghost the gate lists but can't be `#[path]`'d; dropped it → 65 wired. PHASING (corrected — BUCKET A can NOT be allowlisted: verified perf/gpu/oom run in ZERO workflows, so "run elsewhere" is false): (1) DONE-ish: 65 `regression_*` correctness orphans → all_tests (CI-safe); (2) NEXT: 61 non-`regression_*` CORRECTNESS orphans (confidence_floor_*, detector_capture_group_*, phase2_*, structured_*_contract, unit_* aggregators, …) → all_tests, with care for the `unit_*`/`backend_parity_*` ones (may double-include a subdir or need GPU); (3) the ~20 perf/gpu/oom/target_spec orphans are NOT fast-CI-safe (GPU absent, timing/memory) and run NOWHERE — they need a DEDICATED nightly/perf/gpu workflow step (design decision), not an allowlist lie; (4) THEN add `scanner` to `tests_wired.py` ENFORCED_CRATES + populate `ALLOWED["scanner"]` only with the (3) files once their nightly step exists. Also core still has orphans (`tests_wired.py` flags `regression_severity_*`, `regression_spec_loader_failclosed`) — separate crate sweep.
  ROT TRIAGE 2026-07-04 (both fixed to current truth, Law 6/9 — never weakened): (1) `regression_decode_url_plus::trailing_bare_percent_after_valid_escape_emits_no_url_layer` — STALE CONTRACT: asserted the OLD all-or-nothing behavior (a truncated trailing `%` aborts the whole candidate → 0 url layers), but `decode/url.rs:264-266,293-298` was DELIBERATELY changed to BEST-EFFORT (a `%` without two hex digits is a literal byte, so `%41%` → `A%` recovers the real secret instead of discarding the candidate; `url_decode` refuses only when NO valid `%XX` exists). Rewrote → `..._decodes_valid_keeps_literal_percent` asserting the EXACT bytes `token = "A%"` (stronger). VERIFIED 12/12. (2) `regression_backend_trigger_parity` ×3 — STALE FIXTURE, one root cause: `GHP_TOKEN = ghp_aBcD1234EFgh5678ijklMNop9012qrSTuvWX` fabricated with a BAD CRC32 → shipped `GithubClassicPatValidator` silently drops it → `github-classic-pat` = 0 findings. Replaced with canonical valid `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` (= CI-passing `regression_github_pat_boundary::GHP_VALID`). VERIFIED 14/14. NOTE the AKIA fixture is FINE (the aws-only parity test passes) — the earlier "AKIA dropped" guess was wrong; it was always github. SYSTEMIC (DR-335 candidate): `worst_case_backend_parity.rs` pastes the SAME bad `ghp_aBcD…` and will fail identically when wired — every orphan using a vendor-prefixed token must be checksum-audited during the sweep; and `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` is copy-pasted as a per-file const in ~6+ files → a shared `tests/support` canonical-valid-token module would dedup it. | status=sweeping

  BATCH 2 (BUCKET P) IN PROGRESS: 16/17 verified green; wiring the 13 clean + keyword_bridge (rot-fixed) once reverify confirms. ROT FIX #4 — `regression_keyword_bridge` (5 tests) STALE IDENTITY: the generic `KEY=value` bridge's stamped identity was migrated `generic-password` → `generic-secret` (phase2_generic.rs:440-442 stamps `GENERIC_SECRET` / name `Generic Secret (Key=Value)` / service `generic`; `detector_ids::GENERIC_PASSWORD` is now DEAD/unused — the build even warns on it), but the test file was left HALF-migrated + self-contradictory (`const BRIDGE_DETECTOR_ID="generic-password"` at line 48 vs a helper doc at line 110 already saying "generic-secret id"). Reconciled the const + name + module/const docs to the real bridge stamp. BUT this fix is only PARTIAL (bridge-accurate) — it dropped keyword_bridge from 5→3 failures and revealed the REAL depth: the test conflates TWO identities under one `BRIDGE_DETECTOR_ID` const. Empirically, `password=<value>` and `client_password=<value>` route to the DEDICATED `generic-password` DETECTOR (id "generic-password", its own high-precision pattern fires), while synonyms `pwd`/`passwd`/`token`/`secret`/etc. fall through to the `generic-secret` BRIDGE — so the every-keyword-bridges-to-one-identity test model is simply WRONG against current code. keyword_bridge is EXCLUDED from wiring (NOT green); completing it needs the full keyword→id routing map (run each keyword) AND resolves a possible DETECTION INCONSISTENCY: `password` vs its synonym `pwd`/`passwd` routing to DIFFERENT detector ids is either an intended precision split (generic-password.toml owns the exact `password` shape) or an incoherence — a detector-DESIGN review, not a test massage (Law 6/9: assert correct behavior, don't enshrine a bug). My const=generic-secret + doc edits STAY as a better starting point than the original self-contradiction. SIDE-FINDING (DR-337): `generic-password.toml` is NOT dead after all — it DOES fire for exact `password=`; but `detector_ids::GENERIC_PASSWORD` const IS unused (bridge stopped using it) — either wire the const to the password path or drop it (build warns).

GATE IMPACT: `recall_locks_wired.py` flagged orphans 66 → **3**: (1) `entropy_floor_per_family` = the deleted ghost (another agent's uncommitted worktree deletion; git-tracked so gate lists it — resolves when that deletion is committed, NOT mine to stage), (2) `ml_scorer_features` (DR-338), (3) `unicode_hardening_strip` (DR-340). keyword_bridge + prefilter_trigger_union are NOT gate-flagged (recall-lock criteria), so leaving them unwired keeps the gate at 3. DEEP FINDING #4 — DR-340 `regression_unicode_hardening_strip::is_evasion_char_invisible_operators_gap_now_closed` (excluded): asserts the invisible-operator gap (U+2061-2064) is closed on the strip path but it fails — ROOT CAUSE FOUND (reverted my partial fix, kept excluded): the test is STALE against a GREATLY-EXPANDED `is_zero_width` — it now covers the WHOLE `U+2060..=206F` invisible block (not just 2060-2064) PLUS NBSP `U+00A0` PLUS the space separators `U+2000-200A/2028/2029/202F/205F/3000/1680/0085` (the unicode-completion work #71-74). So THREE assertions are stale: the loop (needs 2060-206F), the boundary (2065→ must be a codepoint past 206F like U+2070), and the NBSP line-200 assert (`!is_evasion_char(00A0)` — but NBSP is now IN is_zero_width). MECHANICAL part is easy (loop→206F, boundary→2070); but the NBSP/space-separator assertions raise a DESIGN question that is NOT mine to guess: NBSP and U+2000-3000 are visible-width SEPARATORS, not zero-width — putting them in `is_zero_width` (vs a separate `is_separator`) is either intended aggressive evasion-stripping or a ONE-PLACE mis-categorization that could over-normalize legitimate spaces. Differential-bench-gated (CredData precision, not just mirror). RESOLVED as a stale-test fix: [[unicode-evasion-hand-rolled-sets-incomplete]] confirms the separator-hardening (#73) + the whole #71-74 completion WERE landed AND precision-bounded/differential-bench-validated — so the expansion is INTENDED, and asserting it is correct Law-6 truth (not enshrining a bug). Rewrote all 4 drifted assertions: loop→`2060..=206F`, boundary→U+2070 (first cp past 206F in neither set), NBSP(00A0)+MMSP(205F)→now flagged (they're in the expanded separator set). VERIFYING then WIRING (would drop recall_locks orphans 3→2). Minor DR (detection-owner, non-blocking): `is_zero_width` NAME now covers visible-width separators — consider renaming/splitting to `is_zero_width_or_separator` for ONE-PLACE clarity.
  GATE STATE 2026-07-04: `scripts/gates/run_all.sh` (the `audit-gates` CI job) runs BOTH recall_locks_wired.py (scanner) AND tests_wired.py (verifier/core/sources). tests_wired = GREEN (fixed this session, 63→0). recall_locks_wired = still RED, now at 3→2 orphans after DR-340: (1) `ml_scorer_features` (DR-338 — CANNOT fix without enshrining a model-quality bug; needs a real model fix or a justified `--test` allowlist), (2) `entropy_floor_per_family` ghost — the gate tracks the COMMITTED tree (line 52), so on CI's clean checkout the file EXISTS + is unwired = a real orphan; blocked on the other agent committing their worktree deletion (status D) OR restoring+wiring it. Neither is mine to force. So audit-gates stays red until DR-338 + the ghost clear — both external to this sweep; my `fmt` drift (sort-order + trailing blank) is FIXED (cargo fmt applied, --check clean). DR-339 REFINED: datadog recall on the DEFAULT path is INTACT (wired `contracts/datadog-api-key.toml` has the identical `DD_API_KEY=7b3e5d8c…` positive + `detector_id="datadog-api-key"` and passes in CI via contracts_runner); prefilter_trigger_union's failure is specific to the explicit `scan_chunks_with_backend(CpuFallback)` path. ESCALATION: this may be MORE than a test issue — per [[simd-trigger-union-recall-load-bearing]] datadog-api-key is one of ~49 NO-LITERAL detectors (twilio-auth-token, datadog, …) that fire only once a regex/HS confirms (no standalone AC-literal prefix). The trigger-union fix @3ccad545 covered SimdCpu/GPU; if the pure-scalar `CpuFallback` path (which is the FALLBACK chosen on HS-less hosts by `select_backend`) does NOT union those triggers, then on any host without Hyperscan the scanner SILENTLY loses ~49 detectors — a Law-10 silent recall fallback, the exact bug class the user bans. MUST confirm: get the exact `count_detector(scalar,"datadog-api-key")` (0 vs nonzero) and trace whether `ScanBackend::CpuFallback` runs the full regexset (datadog fires) or an AC-literal-only prefilter (datadog dropped). If dropped, the FIX is to union the no-literal triggers on the CpuFallback scalar path too (not just SimdCpu/GPU) — a real detection-engine recall fix, HIGH priority. contracts_runner only proves the DEFAULT path fires datadog, not CpuFallback. **DR-339 RESOLVED (empirical, 2026-07-04 — the earlier "~49-detector Law-10 SILENT recall loss" escalation was WRONG for datadog):** added a diagnostic to the test + ran it — on `CpuFallback` the value `7b3e5d8c1a9f4e2b6c8d3a5e9f1b7c4d` **IS surfaced, loudly, as TWO findings: `entropy-api-key` + `generic-secret`** (the generic KEY=value bridge fires on `DD_API_KEY=` since it carries the `api_key` credential keyword, and the entropy fallback fires on the 32-hex body). So the SECRET IS NOT LOST — there is NO silent recall loss; the miss is that the value is not ATTRIBUTED to the named `datadog-api-key` detector on the pure-scalar path. Root: datadog-api-key is a no-AC-literal detector whose trigger is HS/regex-confirmed; on `CpuFallback` (no HS) it isn't activated, but its keyword-assignment form is ALWAYS also caught by the generic bridge + entropy net, so recall is preserved and the fallback is LOUD (a real finding), not silent — Law-10 compliant. Correct severity = **MEDIUM detector-ATTRIBUTION gap on HS-less hosts** (generic-secret/entropy instead of the precise `datadog-api-key`), not a recall emergency. Proper fix (real but non-urgent): register no-literal named detectors' keywords into the CpuFallback scalar phase2 keyword-AC so they attribute precisely — needs per-detector differential-bench (some of the ~49 may have non-KEY=value forms the bridge does NOT catch → those WOULD be real recall gaps; datadog is not one). Test `datadog_hs_only_detector_fires_on_simd_cpu` stays EXCLUDED as a legit attribution-expectation finding (its parity twin `datadog_hs_only_parity_cpu_vs_simd` PASSES). FINAL TALLY (this session): **78 orphans wired + CI-covered** (batches 24/16/16/22), 4 shallow rot fixed to truth (decode_url_plus, url_qp_html, backend_trigger_parity, +partial keyword_bridge), 4 deep findings precisely encoded (DR-336 keyword routing, DR-338 ml score, DR-339 datadog CpuFallback backend, DR-340 unicode invisible-op). Orphan gate 66→3.   DR-341 (gate-correctness bug, FIXED 2026-07-04) — `scripts/gates/tests_wired.py::runs_all_targets` scanned workflow YAML **line-by-line**, so a multi-line `cargo test -p <pkg> \`⏎`--test a \`⏎`--test b` (exactly the shape of ci.yml:97 "Strict runner gate") was misread as an ALL-TARGETS run (its `--test` flags sit on continuation lines) → the gate would vacuously report **0 orphans** for that package. Harmless for the currently-enforced crates (verifier/core = wired; sources' all-targets is a genuine single-line step at ci.yml:283), but a LANDMINE: the instant `scanner` joins ENFORCED_CRATES it would pass with ZERO real checking, hiding every orphan. FIX: added `logical_command_lines()` folding `\`-continuations into one logical command before the narrowing-flag check + a self-test case; self-test PASS, enforced gate still GREEN (192 wired). With the fix, `runs_all_targets(keyhog-scanner)` correctly flips True→False and the REAL scanner orphan count surfaces: **80**.

  DR-342 (backend_crossover_sweep rot, found during pure-verify, EXCLUDE-not-wire): `scan_coalesced_large_chunk_windows_instead_of_capping` (line 436) fails its SANITY assert `windowed_n > CAP(32)` — a 4 MiB tiled `benign_payload` no longer yields >32 findings on the windowed reference scan (the scanner got more precise on benign text → fewer FPs), so the windowing-vs-capping gate can't run. NOT a scanner bug: fix = seed the 4 MiB fixture with >32 REAL secrets (the assert message even says "raise the payload size if this trips") so `scan_coalesced` windowing (per-window cap → ~4×) is distinguishable from whole-chunk capping (~CAP). Test-fixture density fix, MEDIUM.
