  ⚠️ NEW FINDING (DR-345, 2026-07-04) — DEEPER ORPHAN CLASS below the top-level sweep: `tests_wired.py`/`recall_locks_wired.py` only enumerate TOP-LEVEL `tests/*.rs`; SUBDIRECTORY test files (`tests/gap/*.rs`, `tests/adversarial/*.rs`) run only when a top-level aggregator `#[path]`-includes them. `tests/gap/mod.rs` DECLARES **173** `pub mod` but the running aggregator `gap_floor.rs` (wired via `all_tests.rs:361`) `#[path]`-wires only **159** — and `gap/mod.rs` itself is included by NO target (only READ as a file by the `integration/a8_harness/gap_mod_wires_a3_slice_gates.rs` gate). So ~14 `gap/*.rs` are CI-orphans, CONFIRMED including `gpu_ac_degenerate_triples_degrade` (declared `gap/mod.rs:72`, absent from `gap_floor`, no other `#[path]`) and `tests/adversarial/segment_attribution_adversarial.rs` (declared `adversarial/mod.rs:35`, no top-level includer of `adversarial/mod.rs`) — i.e. the ONLY behavioral tests of the `segment_attribution` primitive (DR-212) never run in CI. Note: DR-212's fix is still fully verified WITHOUT this test (compile-clean + byte-identical logic prove behavior); this finding is about restoring the primitive's behavioral coverage.
    ✅ PARTIAL EXECUTED (2026-07-04): WIRED `tests/adversarial/segment_attribution_adversarial.rs` (14 PURE behavioral `map_offsets_to_segments` edge-case tests, zero source-greps) into `all_tests.rs` via `#[path]` → **14 passed / 0 failed** in-suite → this ALSO behaviorally CONFIRMS the DR-212 re-export (the re-exported primitive handles every edge case identically) AND closes 1 of the subdir orphans. `gpu_ac_degenerate` deliberately NOT wired: it bundles 1 behavioral test with 3 brittle source-grep gates over GPU engine files (drift-fail risk under the concurrent refactor). EXACT `gap/mod.rs`-decls-minus-`gap_floor`-#[path] diff = **14** files, classified: (a) 3 whole-file `#![cfg(feature="gpu")]` (`gpu_forced_backend_no_silent_degrade`, `gpu_require_policy_fails_not_skips`, …) → compile EMPTY under ci-lean, belong in a `--features gpu` build not all_tests; (b) 1 decode-behavior test `decode_pipeline_layers` (asserts base64-floor-12/hex-floor-16/z85 etc. "derived by reading `src/decode/*`") → the `decode/*` files are ` M` in the concurrent refactor, so wiring risks drift-fails — hold until that settles; (c) 10 brittle source-grep gates (`gpu_ac_degenerate`=10 greps, `gpu_moe_readback_bounded`=6, `gpu_tests_fail_not_skip`=4, `no_inline_tests_in_src`=4, `no_inline_tests_in_a3_slice`=3, `degraded_batch_paths…`/`gpu_no_hit…`/`gpu_per_chunk…`/`gpu_phase2_empty…`/`gpu_phase2_unions…`/`gpu_shader_sigmoid…`=2 each) → drift-prone. So `segment_attribution_adversarial` (adversarial/, stable, pure behavioral) was the ONE cleanly-wireable orphan and it's DONE. The RIGHT fix for the rest = a floor-gate diffing each `<dir>/mod.rs` decl-set vs the aggregator `#[path]` set (blocked by the concurrent `gap/` rework — the 173-vs-159 count is a moving target while they edit it; author the gate once it settles). Confirmed the existing `a8_harness/gap_mod_wires_a3_slice_gates.rs` gate does NOT cover this (it only asserts 5 specific decls are PRESENT in gap/mod.rs, not that gap/mod.rs⊆gap_floor) — so a floor-gate is non-duplicative.

## UX-LOGIC-2026-07-04 (colored-CLI premium pass + logic flaws)
- crates/core/src/report/text.rs confidence bar/percent | correctness/robustness/elegance | P3 | FIXED | The box `Confidence:` line clamped the BAR fill (`filled.min(BAR_WIDTH)`) but NOT the percent (`(confidence*100) as u32`) — asymmetric. `confidence` is a PUBLIC VerifiedFinding field, so a library-constructed finding with confidence 1.5 renders a full bar but "150%", and NaN renders "NaN%"/garbage (clamp doesn't sanitize NaN; scanner's finalize_confidence guards the internal path but not the public API). Fixed by deriving BOTH bar and percent from ONE sanitized value (`display_conf = is_finite ? clamp(0,1) : 0.0`), NaN→0 matching finalize_confidence. Added 2 regressions in regression_report_text_format.rs (over-range→100%/full ■■■■■■, NaN→0%/empty □□□□□□); 4/4 text_confidence tests green.

(a) suppressions.md's generate-command `jq -r '.credential_hash' >> .keyhogignore` appended bare hex with NO `hash:` prefix → those lines become PATH globs (never match a credential); fixed to `jq -r '"hash:" + .credential_hash'`. (b) precommit.md showed `hash:sha256:abc123...` — the `sha256:` prefix is baseline-file-only (baseline.rs:24); after `strip_prefix("hash:")` it leaves `sha256:abc...` which FAILS parse_sha256_hex → rejected as invalid. WIRED correctly: request.rs screens BOTH the URL string AND post-DNS resolved addrs, DNS-PINS the accepted IP into the TCP connect (defeats rebinding), screens even behind a proxy, SSRF-before-HTTPS to avoid topology-leak. DISCIPLINE NOTE: I saw a v4/v6 multicast "asymmetry" in bogon.rs and started adding v4 `is_multicast()`/`>=240` — then found the TWO-LAYER design is INTENTIONAL: bogon is the fleet-wide table that deliberately leaves v4 multicast/Class-E to consumers (ssrf.rs:66-68 comment), and `verifier_blocks_ip_addr` (ssrf.rs:75) ALREADY adds `is_multicast()||octets[0]>=240` on top — the verifier already blocks them (ssrf_guard.rs:138-153 assert exactly this). - crates/cli/src/subcommands/doctor.rs on_path check | correctness/UX | P2 | FIXED | `keyhog doctor`'s "on PATH" health line did a raw `d == dir` string compare against each PATH entry, so a trailing-slash/symlinked/`.`-relative entry (`~/.local/bin/` vs `~/.local/bin`) gave a FALSE "on PATH: no" even when correctly installed. Inconsistent WITHIN the same file: the shadow check 30 lines below already canonicalizes, and the installer's `Test-PathContainsDir` normalizes trailing separators. Extracted pure `dir_is_on_path(dir, pathvar)` canonicalizing both sides via the existing helper; added doctor/tests.rs (trailing-slash-matches + absent-reports-false, real tempdirs). 4/4 green.
- KH-GAP-004 + white-box #[path] conflict for the two new test modules | test-org | P2 | FIXED | Initially added inline `#[cfg(test)] mod` blocks in style.rs + doctor.rs — both violate `no_inline_tests_in_src`. doctor.rs → sibling `subcommands/doctor/tests.rs` (`mod tests;`), fine. style.rs could NOT use a sibling: `tests/unit/style.rs` white-box-includes it via `#[path="../../src/style.rs"]`, so ANY `mod tests;` inside style.rs resolves to a nonexistent `src/tests.rs` and breaks the `all_tests` binary (E0583). Put the style unit test (no_color_from_env matrix) into that existing white-box `tests/unit/style.rs` instead; left an explanatory no-inline-block comment in style.rs. Lesson: a src module that is `#[path]`-included by an integration test cannot declare its own `mod`.

## DEEP-REVIEW-2026-07-04 (installers + my recent changes, live-dogfooded on windows-thinkpad PS 5.1 / Win11)
Method: static footgun scan + AST parse + LIVE runtime dogfood of the read-only `-Diagnose` path on the ThinkPad (the parser-can't-catch layer). Findings:

- install.ps1:1879 | correctness/UX | P2 | FIXED+LIVE-VERIFIED | `Say "  Version: $((Get-CurrentVersion) -or '(does not run)')"` — PowerShell `-or` is a BOOLEAN operator, so the version STRING was coerced to $true and `-Diagnose` printed "Version: True" for every install. Neither the AST parser nor eye-review caught it; the live dogfood did. Fix: explicit `$version = Get-CurrentVersion; if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($version)) { $version='(does not run)' }`. Re-ran `-Diagnose` live → now prints "Version: KeyHog v0.5.40"; PARSE-OK. Confirmed one-off (grepped all `-or '`/`-or "`/`-or $` — every other -or is a legit boolean; install.sh side correctly uses `${ver:-(does not run)}`, no parity bug).
- install.sh:676 + install.ps1:456 | verify-UX/security | P3 | open | verify_release_signature / Verify-ReleaseSignature conflate a NETWORK-FETCH FAILURE for the .minisig with "no signature published" (download_asset catch → allow_unverified_install "No .minisig signature was published"). Default fails CLOSED (safe — refuses without --insecure), so NOT a bypass on a normal run. But two real defects: (1) misleading message — a transient CDN drop reports "not published"; (2) under --insecure a transient drop SILENTLY skips verification of a signature that IS published. Fix needs a network-error-vs-404 distinction (curl exit codes / IWR StatusCode) so a network failure is retried-or-fatal while a genuine 404 is the only "not published". Low sev (--insecure is explicit opt-in). Same class in Verify-Checksum.
- crates/cli/src/log_dedup.rs | UX/Law-10-edge | P4 | open | WarnDedupSummaryGuard prints the "warning repeated N more times" summary on Drop; `std::process::exit` (the Ctrl-C interrupt path, lib.rs:259) SKIPS Drop, so on a hard-interrupted scan the aggregate summary is lost (the first-3 warnings still printed live, only the tail count is skipped). Normal completion returns ExitCode through cli_main's scope → guard drops → summary prints (verified the exit paths). Acceptable for an interrupted partial run; note it or flush the summary in the interrupt handler.
- install.sh / install.ps1 footgun scan | audit | — | CLEAN | all `rm -rf` use quoted bare vars (no trailing path component → empty var is at worst a no-op, never `rm -rf /x`); no eval; no curl|sh in the logic (only in doc comments); mktemp used throughout; download retry (5x backoff) present both sides; signature+checksum verify gate on the TEMP file BEFORE placement (correct order, fail-closed default); binary backup-before-overwrite with restore-on-failure. Well-hardened.
- install.ps1 version/update logic | verify | SOUND (live) | exact-tag equality (`$existingTag -eq $Script:Tag`) not semver (intentional); `-Diagnose` live showed correct "up to date" for v0.5.40==latest; Get-VersionTagFromText regex extracts the tag correctly. Test-PathContainsDir also re-validated LIVE on the real install path ("PASS ... is on PATH").
- docs em/en-dash sweep | docs/polish | DONE | Removed every em/en/horizontal-bar dash from all shipped docs (docs/src/**, README.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, CHANGELOG.md). En-dash numeric ranges → hyphen; em-dash prose → colon/semicolon/comma/parens by context (no blind hyphen swap). Verified 0 remaining via `grep -rlE '—|–|―'`. BACKLOG.md/LEDGER.md excluded (internal ledgers, row separators). Re-verified output-formats.md "nine values" claim against OutputFormat enum (scan.rs:34, 9 variants) — accurate.

## §SPEED — THE HEADLINE (2026-07-07, profiled): phase2 always-active RegexSet = 97% of scan
**GOAL (user-ordered): GPU OOM faster than Hyperscan over 8MB; fix recall on ALL paths; beat everything.**
Profiled 8MB dense corpus, --backend simd, --profile:
- SCAN 111808ms CPU-sum (~15.5s wall). **phase2:prefilter = 108857ms = 97.4%** ("populate: always-active RegexSet prefilter").
- phase1(trigger)=27.7ms(0.0%), preprocess=362ms, entropy=1154ms(1.0%), ml=30ms, decode=0. Phase-1 is a rounding error — GPU-ing phase1 CANNOT help; phase2 dominates.
- Worst always-active patterns (RegexSet, NOT Hyperscan-compilable): `\b([a-z0-9]{6}\.[a-z0-9]{16})\b` 45ms/run; homoglyph URL `[nпνｎ][aаαａ][tтτｔ][sѕｓ]://..@` 25ms; homoglyph PEM `-----[BВΒＢ][EЕΕＥ]G[IІΙＩ][NΝＮ] RSA…` ~9ms×several.
- ROOT CAUSE: per-detector homoglyph-variant regexes (each ASCII char → `[BВΒＢ]` unicode class) explode the NFA; Hyperscan rejects them → slow RegexSet fallback run always-active on every chunk's full bytes.
- GPU==SIMD at 8MB (16.5 vs 15.5s) / 32MB (37 vs 36.6s): GPU currently NOT faster (memory: "GPU region-presence didn't beat CPU/SIMD through 64MiB on 5090").
**THE FIX (unifies speed+recall): move the always-active anchorless RegexSet (homoglyph-variant + generic patterns) onto a GPU megakernel DFA.**
- Speed: GPU DFA runs char-class patterns at memory bandwidth; CPU RegexSet is 108s → target ms. This is HOW GPU beats Hyperscan >8MB (HS can't even compile these).
- Recall: GPU running the always-active set = parity with simd on anchorless detectors → kills the --backend gpu DIVERGES gap (gpu 4435 vs simd 5155). Update the LAW10 contract test gpu_region_presence_anchorless_gap_surfaced.rs from "divergence surfaced" → "parity asserted" once closed (Law 9: strengthen, not weaken).
- Infra exists: engine/phase2_gpu_dfa.rs (Phase2GpuDfaCatalog, prefixless_always_active_candidates, build_phase2_gpu_admission_workload). vyre src: /media/.../libs/performance/matching/vyre (build_regex_dfa_unanchored + scan_presence_by_region).
- CPU-side interim win (helps all backends): the always-active RegexSet should be a single lazy-DFA / literal-prefiltered, not a per-pattern RegexSet scan of full bytes.

### §SPEED refined (2026-07-07): sparse vs dense + GPU-DFA state + the megakernel plan
MEASURED 8MB, RTX 5090, release binary:
| input | simd(HS) | gpu | note |
|---|---|---|---|
| dense (concat CredData, secret-laden) | 15.5s | 16.5s | phase2 prefilter=97%; no-candidate gate never skips (every chunk has a detector-prefix literal) |
| sparse (normal source-like text) | 1.71s | 2.58s | gate skips → fast; GPU ADDS ~0.9s overhead, loses |
KEY: GPU is currently SLOWER than HS at 8MB on BOTH densities (pipeline warmup/dispatch overhead, and it only replaces phase-1 which is 0.0% of cost). The "OOM faster than HS >8MB" win is specifically the HIGH-DENSITY large input (leak dumps / base64 blobs / secret-laden config) where HS's ~2700-pattern always-active RegexSet can't be gated (15s+) and a bandwidth-bound GPU DFA wins.
GPU phase-2 DFA STATE (engine/phase2_gpu_dfa.rs): it is an ADMISSION accelerator ONLY, covering ≤64 patterns (TARGET_SHARD_PATTERNS=16 × MAX_SHARDS=4) out of ~2700 always-active. A GPU hit only says "run phase-2 on this chunk"; it does NOT mark WHICH patterns hit, so the CPU RegexSet prefilter (the 108s) still runs in full. self-reports "GPU speed evidence is incomplete."
THE MEGAKERNEL PLAN (unifies OOM-speed + recall-parity; the user-ordered core):
1. Expand the GPU DFA catalog from a 64-pattern admission gate → a full ~2700-pattern MARKING kernel: compile ALL always-active patterns into GPU DFA shards, run at bandwidth, read back a per-pattern match bitmap (the same set the CPU RegexSet produces). Raise MAX_SHARDS/TARGET_SHARD_PATTERNS; ~170 shards @16 patterns on 32GB 5090 is feasible.
2. Wire that bitmap as the phase-2 active set so it REPLACES the CPU always-active RegexSet (kills the 108s). Extraction stays CPU (one owner) but only on marked patterns.
3. Kill the GPU dispatch/warmup overhead (~0.9s) so GPU wins even at 8MB (persistent pipeline/pinned buffers; vyre megakernel-batch).
4. Recall parity falls out: GPU now covers the anchorless always-active set == simd → close the --backend gpu DIVERGES gap; flip LAW10 test gpu_region_presence_anchorless_gap_surfaced.rs to assert parity (Law 9 strengthen).
vyre src of truth: /media/mukund-thiru/SanthData/Santh/libs/performance/matching/vyre (build_regex_dfa_unanchored + scan_presence_by_region). Interim CPU win (all backends): make the always-active marking one combined lazy-DFA with a stronger no-candidate gate (current gate never skips real source because detector-prefix literals like key/token/secret are ubiquitous — the gate literal set is too common to be selective).

### §SPEED — MEGAKERNEL DE-RISKED (2026-07-07): GPU already emits per-pattern matches; marking is PLUMBING
KEY DISCOVERY (engine/phase2_gpu_dfa/shard.rs::scan_admission_into): the GPU regex-DFA ALREADY computes per-pattern match triples `{pattern_id, start, end}` (scratch.matches), and `pattern_id` maps to `self.phase2_indices[pattern_id]` = the real phase-2 detector index. The admission path DISCARDS this — it collapses every match to `admitted[region]=true`. So the GPU kernel already does the hard part (multi-pattern DFA at bandwidth, reporting WHICH pattern hit WHERE).
=> Converting the GPU DFA from an admission gate → a MARKING engine that replaces the CPU always-active RegexSet (the 97%/108s cost) is THREE tractable steps, NOT a kernel rewrite:
  1. MARKING WIRING: in scan_admission_into (and its shard loop), instead of only `admitted[region]=true`, record the marked phase-2 set per region: `scratch.mark(phase2_indices[m.pattern_id])`. Return a per-region active-pattern set, not just a bool. Then feed it into scan_coalesced_phase2 as the active set so the CPU RegexSet prefilter is SKIPPED for covered patterns. (Recall parity: covered patterns are marked by GPU byte-for-byte; uncovered still fall to CPU — LOUD, never silent.)
  2. COVERAGE EXPANSION: raise PHASE2_GPU_DFA_MAX_SHARDS (4) / TARGET_SHARD_PATTERNS (16) so the catalog covers ALL ~2700 always-active patterns, not 64. Bounded by MAX_STATES=16384/shard + 32GB 5090. ~170 shards @16. Drives uncovered_patterns→0 so the CPU RegexSet never runs on the hot path.
  3. DISPATCH OVERHEAD: the ~0.9s per-scan GPU cost is pipeline warmup + buffer alloc (--no-daemon pays it every scan). Persist the pipeline/pinned buffers (daemon amortizes; vyre megakernel-batch already batches). Verify GPU-via-daemon steady-state beats simd.
VERIFY EACH: (a) GPU-vs-simd findings PARITY on creddata (identical finding set) after marking wiring — proves recall-complete; (b) dense-8MB GPU time drops from 16.5s toward bandwidth (target OOM<HS's 15s); (c) contracts_runner green on --backend gpu; (d) flip LAW10 gpu_region_presence_anchorless_gap_surfaced.rs from "divergence surfaced" to "parity asserted" (Law 9 strengthen). shard.rs match triples own the byte-widths in vyre_libs::scan::dispatch_io — no ABI dup needed.

### §SPEED — step-1 marking wiring: EXACT seam map (turnkey, 2026-07-07)
- SOURCE of per-pattern hits: engine/phase2_gpu_dfa/shard.rs::scan_admission_into — loop over `scratch.matches` (each `{pattern_id,start,end}`); today it only sets `admitted[region]=true`. ADD: build a per-region marked set `scratch.mark(self.phase2_indices[m.pattern_id])` and thread it out.
- CARRIER: engine/phase2_gpu_dfa/workload.rs::Phase2GpuDfaAdmission { admitted, complete, matches_seen } — ADD `marked: Vec<RegionActiveSet>` (per-region). expand_phase2_gpu_admission maps subset→full_len (mirror the admitted mapping).
- CONSUMER/seam: engine/gpu_region_dispatch.rs:530 `self.scan_coalesced_phase2_with_admission(..., admission.admitted.as_slice())`. When `admission.complete` for a region (GPU covered ALL always-active patterns), pass the GPU `marked[region]` set as the phase-2 active set and SKIP the CPU always-active RegexSet (phase2_prefilter::mark_matches). Uncovered/`!complete` region → CPU mark_matches (LOUD, never silent — Law 10). This is the line that kills the 97%/108s cost.
- PARITY GATE (add): on a corpus, assert GPU `marked[region]` == CPU `mark_matches` active set byte-for-byte (findings-identical). Pin before flipping default. Then coverage-expand (MAX_SHARDS/TARGET_SHARD_PATTERNS) until uncovered_patterns→0 so `complete` holds for all regions.
- FIXED THIS TURN (unblocks gpu test gate): crates/scanner/tests/unit/gates/gpu_shader_arch_consts_match_model_arch.rs used `crate::ml_scorer`/`crate::gpu` (integration test → wrong crate root); changed to `keyhog_scanner::` (latent, gpu-feature-gated, dormant until built with --features gpu).

### §SPEED — step-1a LANDED (2026-07-07): GPU marking plumbing (inert, additive, green)
DONE: GPU regex-DFA now PRODUCES the per-region marked phase-2 pattern set (was discarded).
- shard.rs::scan_admission_into: new `marked: Option<&mut [Vec<usize>]>` param; per match pushes `phase2_indices[m.pattern_id]` into `marked[region]` alongside `admitted[region]=true`.
- phase2_gpu_dfa.rs::scan_admission_with_scratch: accumulates `marked` across shards, dedups per region, returns it.
- workload.rs::Phase2GpuDfaAdmission gains `marked: Vec<Vec<usize>>`; expand_phase2_gpu_admission remaps subset→full (same mapping as admitted). All 5 construction sites updated; 2 expand tests strengthened to assert the remap.
- INERT: `marked` is computed + carried but NOT yet consumed → zero behavior/recall change. Verified via build.
### §SPEED — step-1b NEXT (consumer wiring + parity gate): the recall-critical substitution
The active-set marking runs DEEP, not at the admission seam: scan_coalesced.rs::scan_coalesced_phase2_with_admission (line 325) → per-chunk scan_windowed_with_triggered → phase-2 extraction → Phase2AlwaysActivePrefilter::mark_matches. `phase2_admission: Option<&[bool]>` only ADMITS no-trigger chunks (positive-only), it does NOT mark.
PLAN: (1) thread `phase2_gpu_marked: Option<&[Vec<usize>]>` + a `gpu_marking_complete: bool` through scan_coalesced_phase2_with_admission → the per-chunk path → extraction. (2) At the always-active mark site, when `gpu_marking_complete` for that region, seed the active `ActivePatternsScratch` from the GPU `marked[region]` set and SKIP `mark_matches` (the 97%/108s cost); when NOT complete, run CPU mark_matches (LOUD, Law 10 — never silent). (3) PARITY GATE (mandatory before default-on): a test that runs both paths on a corpus and asserts the GPU-marked active set == CPU mark_matches active set, region-by-region, byte-for-byte. (4) then coverage-expand MAX_SHARDS/TARGET_SHARD_PATTERNS so uncovered_patterns→0 → `complete` holds → CPU RegexSet never runs on the hot path. Consumer caller at gpu_region_dispatch.rs:530 already has `admission.marked` available to pass.

### §SPEED — step-1b SUBSTITUTION SITE pinned (turnkey)
Exact site: engine/phase2_compiled.rs:401 `Some(prefilter) => prefilter.mark_matches(&self.phase2_patterns, text, &mut scratch, ...)` — this fills the per-chunk `ActivePatternsScratch` (phase2.rs:115, `scratch.mark(index)` adds a phase-2 index). SUBSTITUTION: when GPU marking is complete for this chunk, replace the mark_matches call with `for &idx in gpu_marks[region] { scratch.mark(idx); }` and skip mark_matches (the 97%/108s always-active RegexSet). Thread `gpu_marks/gpu_complete` from scan_coalesced_phase2_with_admission (scan_coalesced.rs:325) → the per-chunk closure (line 340-364, has chunk_index to index gpu_marks) → the fn at phase2_compiled.rs:368. NOTE the trigger-anchored patterns (phase-1 Hyperscan/GPU-literal triggers) still mark via the existing `triggered` path; GPU marks REPLACE only the ALWAYS-ACTIVE prefilter set. Parity gate asserts the union (triggered ∪ gpu-always-active) == (triggered ∪ cpu-mark_matches) per chunk.

`cargo test --features gpu --lib -- replayed_gpu_dfa phase2_gpu_admission` → 11 passed, 0 failed. Marking logic proven by deterministic DFA replay (no GPU device): marked==[[0],[0],[]] / [[],[0]]; subset→full remap asserted. Step-1a is inert (no behavior change) + correct.

GPU marks thread EXACTLY like confirmed_anchor_literal_matches (an existing per-chunk GPU-hint param):
- scan_coalesced.rs:325 scan_coalesced_phase2_with_admission: add `phase2_gpu_marks: Option<&[Vec<usize>]>` + `gpu_marks_complete: bool` (per-chunk, like confirmed_anchor_literal_matches: Option<&[Vec<(u32,u32)>]>).
- → backend_triggered.rs:7 scan_prepared_with_triggered: add `Option<&[usize]>` (single-chunk slice).
- → windowed.rs:65 scan_windowed_with_triggered: same.
- → phase2_compiled.rs:365 populate_active_phase2 (the active-set builder; mark_matches at ~401): when gpu_marks_complete, `for &idx in gpu_marks { scratch.mark(idx) }` and SKIP mark_matches; else CPU mark_matches (LOUD).
- CALLER gpu_region_dispatch.rs:530 passes admission.marked + admission.complete; non-admission wrapper scan_coalesced_phase2 (line 275) passes None/false.
- ACCEPTANCE GATE (write FIRST, additive/safe): unit test builds Phase2AlwaysActivePrefilter + Phase2GpuDfaCatalog from the SAME small pattern set, runs both on a text, asserts GPU marked set == CPU mark_matches active set (for covered patterns). Only after green: wire the substitution. Then coverage-expand MAX_SHARDS so uncovered_patterns=0.

### §DETECTION — stopword whole-token fix LANDED (2026-07-07): recovers the migration's +35 FN
ROOT CAUSE: per-detector `stopwords` suppression used `contains_ignore_ascii_case` (SUBSTRING) at suppression/api.rs:755 → a real secret embedding a stopword's letters mid-run (`attestationKey` ⊃ `test`, `bypassword` ⊃ `password`) was silently suppressed → the +35 CredData FN the migration introduced.
FIX: new ONE-PLACE predicate `keyhog_core::contains_token_ignore_ascii_case` (core/src/ascii_ci.rs) — matches the stopword only at a TOKEN boundary (string edge, non-alnum, or digit↔letter transition; NO camelCase hinge, recall-first so real camelCase-named secrets aren't suppressed). `test123456`/`Password1`/`your_key_here`/`key_test`/`9test` still suppressed; `attestationKey`/`contestant`/`bypassword` no longer are. 3 unit tests in core (token_ci_tests). suppression/api.rs:755 now calls it.
VALIDATION PENDING: creddata A/B (same-HEAD, this change only) must confirm FN drops ~35 with FP flat. Bench is the arbiter [[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]]. THEN (separate bench) add per-detector allowlist_values for the clean FP wins (12345678/123456790/apikeyvaluehere&name/tonrconsumerkey — BACKLOG FP map lines ~112-125).

### §DETECTION — stopword whole-token A/B RESULT + sequential-placeholder fix (2026-07-07)
STOPWORD WHOLE-TOKEN BENCH (isolated, ci-lean/CPU, vs POSTMIG): tp 5245→5245, fp 8181→8187 (+6), fn 9856→9856 (UNCHANGED), f1 0.3677→0.3676. HYPOTHESIS REFUTED: the migration's +35 FN did NOT come from substring stopword matching (whole-token recovered ZERO FN). Net −6 FP (tiny). KEPT anyway: whole-token is the correct semantics + the safe FOUNDATION for aggressive stopword expansion (substring matching would cause real FN, blocking expansion). The 6 FP will be recovered by shape gates + expanded stopwords.
SEQUENTIAL-PLACEHOLDER FIX (landed, benching): is_sequential_placeholder (context/placeholder.rs) only caught all-same-byte + repeated-pair bodies; extended it with a MONOTONIC-RUN check (ascending/descending adjacent ASCII steps > 9/10 ratio) reusing count_adjacent_byte_steps + sequential_step_threshold (ONE PLACE). Catches `12345678`/`abcdefgh`/`87654321` generalizably (NO hardcoded corpus literals — Vector 6). Wired: entropy path → is_known_example_credential (adjudicate/entropy.rs:145) → is_sequential_placeholder. Targets the entropy-token flood (4128 FP = 50% of all FP; `12345678` was a chunk of it). 6 unit tests (core token_ci + scanner sequential_placeholder_tests). Does NOT catch `123456790` (6/8 sequential, below the strict 9/10 ratio — left for allowlist; not worth perturbing the proven hex ratio).
FP STRUCTURE (creddata, why precision is hard): entropy-token 4128 + entropy-api-key 2080 = 6208 of 8181 FP (76%). These are high-shannon-entropy non-secrets; generalizable shape gates (sequential/keyboard-walk) chip at the edges, but the BULK needs the entropy ML model authoritative [[entropy-unification-gated-off-pending-better-model]] or entropy_high↑ (recall-gated per-detector tuning). Sequential is one clean chip; next levers: keyboard-walk gate (needs Tier-B layout), per-detector entropy_high A/B.

### §DETECTION — pure-FP detectors (creddata, 0 TP — verified genuine, not label artifact)
- aws-secrets-manager-arn: 33 FP / 0 TP. The detector's OWN header comment says "an ARN is an identifier, not a credential." An ARN is public (in every CloudFormation template) → it can NEVER be a TP on a secret benchmark → every match is FP by construction. FIX (architectural, not a toml tweak): reclassify ARN matches as an INFORMATIONAL/inventory finding class that does not count toward secret precision, OR gate it off the default secret profile. Do NOT just delete (loses inventory capability). Clean −33 FP, 0 recall loss once reclassified.
- google-cloud-storage-hmac-key: 78 FP / 0 TP. Has THREE patterns incl. a bare `[a-zA-Z0-9+/]{40,}` (matches ANY 40+ char base64) — the over-match source. BUT that pattern is load-bearing: the real GCS HMAC SECRET *is* a 40-char base64, so deleting it loses real-world secret recall. FIX: require a stronger per-pattern context anchor (hmac_secret/secret key= adjacency) on the bare-base64 pattern instead of firing near any GOOG/HMAC keyword. Recall-sensitive (real GCS secrets) → bench-gated on a corpus that actually contains GCS HMAC secrets (CredData has none → can't validate recall here). Left tracked.
- CONFIRMED via fp_hist: entropy-token/entropy-api-key FP and TP overlap in the top length buckets (TP peaks bucket19=128 alongside FP=1168) → NO length/entropy threshold separates them; the 76% entropy flood needs the context-aware ML model authoritative, not a shape gate. [[entropy-unification-gated-off-pending-better-model]] [[ml-model-must-train-on-real-distribution]]

### §DETECTION — CREDDATA GAP fully diagnosed (2026-07-07): the path to beating betterleaks
CURRENT (ci-lean/CPU, this session, stopword+sequential landed): tp 5245, fp 8168, fn 9856, P 0.391, R 0.347, F1 0.368. betterleaks ~0.53 F1 (P 0.86). Gap = PRECISION (keyhog P 0.39 vs 0.86), and precision = the entropy flood.
FP ANATOMY: entropy-token 4116 fp/263 tp (P 0.06) + entropy-api-key 2067 fp/580 tp (P 0.22) = 6183 of 8168 fp = 76%. fp_hist proves FP and TP OVERLAP in the top length buckets → no shape/length/entropy threshold separates them without killing TP. Heuristic surfacing is SATURATED [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]].
THE ONE VIABLE LEVER (entropy-* only): entropy-token/entropy-api-key are MODEL-AUTHORITATIVE (final = ml_conf directly; entropy_ml_authoritative defaults ON, verified scanner.rs:153) — so RETRAINING the MoE to score CredData's hard-negative high-entropy strings LOW directly lowers their confidence below threshold. This is architecturally viable for entropy-* (NOT for generic-*, where heuristic is a floor the model can only raise). Pipeline: harvest current entropy-token/entropy-api-key FP as hard negatives → blend with the real+synthetic corpus → retrain (ml/harvest_corpus.py + train_classifier --real-corpus, file-grouped split) → validate held-out recall doesn't regress → A/B creddata. [[ml-model-must-train-on-real-distribution]]. This is the major creddata task; heuristic chips (sequential −13fp, info-filter −33fp) are marginal beside it.
CLEAN NON-MODEL WINS remaining (small): (1) aws-secrets-manager-arn severity=info, 33fp/0tp — bench counts info as secret FP; exclude info-severity from secret precision in the scorer OR pass --min-severity low in the bench keyhog adapter (benchmarks/bench/scanners/keyhog.py:214) [fair: info≠secret prediction]. (2) google-cloud-storage-hmac-key bare-base64 pattern anchor-tighten (recall-gated, needs a corpus with real GCS secrets).
LANDED THIS SESSION (validated, recall-safe): stopword whole-token predicate (foundation) + sequential-placeholder monotonic-run gate (−13 fp, 0 tp loss). Multi-axis: detection precision + GPU marking step-1a + 9 new tests.

### §DETECTION — MoE retrain-on-creddata MEASURED (2026-07-07): close, blocked on tail-class recall
Ran `CORPORA=creddata KEYHOG_BIN=<ci-lean> ml/retrain_loop.sh` (measure-only, no ship). Harvest: 32788 records / 4010 files (real pos 8823, neg 23965). Retrain blended synthetic(23040)+real, file-grouped held-out.
SCRATCH MODEL held-out: synthetic-val F1 0.959 (vs shipped 0.964); REAL held-out F1 0.794 / P 0.737 / R 0.862 / recall@0.40-floor 0.873. PROBES ALL CORRECT: ghp PAT 0.985, real hex key 0.857, anchored 0.975 (want high); md5 0.023, sha256 0.018, placeholder 0.015, base64-PNG 0.006, bare token 0.042, prose 0.029 (want low). Per-detector held-out: entropy-token P0.59/R0.68, entropy-api-key P0.29/R0.74, generic-keyword-secret P0.91/R0.94, generic-secret P0.88, generic-password P0.90 — the model DISCRIMINATES well (bench P0.06 on entropy-token reflects full-corpus class imbalance, NOT model failure).
GATE REFUSED `--write`: per-class real held-out recall floor 0.5 failed on ~12 TINY classes (n_pos 1-4 each): Auth:Token:UUID, Azure Access Token:JSON Web Token, Google Multi(4), Password:Token(3), UUID:Key(2), Auth:BASE64-PEM, Auth:Password, google-oauth-client-secret(4), npmrc-auth-token, kubernetes-bootstrap-token, postmark-webhook-credentials — each hit 0 recall. The gate correctly prevents a tail class silently dying (Law: aggregate must not hide a failed tail).
PATH TO SHIP (the creddata precision endgame; multi-iteration ML, not one-shot): (1) class-weight or oversample the failing tail classes in train_classifier so their few positives clear the 0.40 floor WITHOUT re-flooding FP; (2) OR calibrate the per-class floor for n_pos<5 classes (a single-positive class at floor 0.5 is a harsh, noisy gate — but relaxing it risks the exact silent-tail-death the gate guards). (3) then `--write --verify` runs the full mirror+creddata bench vs the replaced-model baseline + contract-recall gate → only ships if creddata FP drops AND no recall regression. This is THE lever to beat betterleaks on creddata; heuristic chips (sequential/stopword) are marginal beside a shipped better model. Harvest artifact: ml/data/real_corpus.jsonl (32788 rows, reusable for the next iteration).

### §DETECTION — homefield-betterleaks measured (2026-07-07): my fixes NEUTRAL, same betterleaks gap
Fixed binary (stopword+sequential) on homefield-betterleaks: tp51 fp59 fn65 P0.464 R0.440 F10.451 — IDENTICAL to baseline (that corpus has no sequential/substring-stopword FPs). betterleaks F1 0.607 (P0.773 R0.500). keyhog loses on the SAME structural gap as creddata: precision (P0.46 vs 0.77) AND recall (0.44 vs 0.50). CONFIRMS cross-bench: heuristics saturated; beating betterleaks on BOTH losing benches needs the MoE retrain (scoring-bound, tail-class-gate-blocked) + more surfacing — the same large lever, not per-bench heuristics.

### §DETECTION — homefield-betterleaks is a COVERAGE gap (addressable, unlike creddata's model gap)
Per-detector analysis of the homefield-betterleaks bench (my binary). The recall gap here is MISSING VENDOR DETECTORS (betterleaks' 331 tight rules have them, keyhog lacks them → 0 TP), NOT the entropy/model problem:
RECALL (add these detectors — Tier-B .toml each; formats need per-vendor research vs gitleaks/betterleaks rules, NOT guessed):
- gitea-access-token (fn4/tp0) — gitea PAT, 40-hex sha1
- greptile-api-key (fn3/tp0)
- etsy-access-token (fn3/tp0)
- slack-legacy-token (fn2/tp0), slack-legacy-bot-token (fn2/tp0), slack-config-access-token (fn2/tp2 partial)
- weights-and-biases-api-key (fn2/tp0) — 40-hex
- sumologic-access-token (fn5/tp1)
Each needs: exact regex + keywords (Tier-B), positive/negative/adversarial tests, verify config, mirror+homefield bench (must not regress). betterleaks has all of these — port the FORMAT (not the code).
PRECISION (tighten):
- google-api-key: 16 FP/0 TP. The DRIVE pattern `(?:DRIVE|GOOGLE_DRIVE)[._-]?API[._-]?KEY[=:\s"']+([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{30,})` captures ANY 30+ char value after DRIVE_API_KEY= instead of requiring the canonical `AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}` like every other pattern in the detector. Google Drive keys ARE AIza-format → tighten the capture to `AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}` (recall-safe, kills the 16-FP over-match). Bench-gate mirror+homefield.
- entropy-token 22fp/5tp, huggingface-user-token 4fp/0tp, posthog-api-key 2fp/0tp — same entropy-flood + over-match classes as creddata.
NOTE: this is why keyhog loses homefield-betterleaks on BOTH axes — precision (entropy+google over-match) AND recall (8 missing vendor detectors). The recall half is concrete detector work; the precision half is the shared entropy lever.

detectors/google-api-key.toml:42 DRIVE pattern capture `([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{30,})` → `(AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35})` — now consistent with the detector's other 8 patterns (all require the canonical AIza prefix). Google API/Drive keys are universally AIza-prefixed, so recall-safe; kills the 16 FP/0 TP homefield-betterleaks over-match. status=fix-pending-bench (compile-embedded → needs rebuild + mirror/homefield/creddata A/B to confirm no TP loss + the −16 FP). ### §DETECTION — sequential-placeholder RE-SCOPED (2026-07-07): universal wiring broke 15 vendor contracts
CAUGHT BY contracts_runner (the 839-contract recall oracle, 19min): the monotonic-run arm I first added INSIDE is_sequential_placeholder fired via the UNIVERSAL is_known_example_credential — which STRONG vendor detectors (socure/openai/square/marketo/segment/npmrc/lunacy/google-forms + 15 total) also call. Their contract fixtures use the literal alphabet as filler (`sdk_key="abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwx123456"`) → my gate suppressed them → "scale MISSED 0 surfaced" → oracle RED (2 test fns, 6+ detectors). LESSON: is_known_example_credential is the UNIVERSAL per-candidate example check; a shape gate added there hits vendor-anchored matches whose KEYWORD is the evidence and whose value shape must not be second-guessed.
FIX (scoped, correct): extracted the monotonic logic to `context::is_monotonic_sequence_placeholder` and wired it ONLY into the ENTROPY shape path (adjudicate/entropy.rs, `sequential_run` stage) — where value-shape IS the evidence (entropy-token/entropy-api-key/generic-keyword-secret, exactly the −13 FP source). is_sequential_placeholder reverted to its original (all-same + repeated-pair). New scoping-proof test asserts is_known_example_credential does NOT suppress the alphabet (vendor-safe) while is_monotonic_sequence_placeholder does. Verifying: contracts GREEN + creddata −13 FP holds (entropy path unchanged in effect).

### §DETECTION — homefield-betterleaks gap RE-ANALYZED (2026-07-07, corrected)
The earlier "8 missing vendor detectors" list was PARTLY WRONG — verified keyhog's actual coverage against betterleaks' manifest.jsonl (317 rows, its own ground truth):
- greptile: GENUINE gap (0 detectors) → ADDED greptile-api-key.toml (in-flight verify bxbg3ural).
- slack-config-access-token / slack-legacy: ALREADY COVERED — keyhog has slack-config-token.toml (xoxe.xoxp-/xoxe.xoxb-) + slack-app/bot/user. NOT a gap; per-detector NAME differs from betterleaks but location-match counts it TP.
- wandb (weights-and-biases): ALREADY HAVE wandb-api-key.toml, BUT regex requires EXACTLY `[a-f0-9]{40}`; betterleaks example `wandb_api_key = 872ab943740b34157041da2529fb160d` is 32-hex → MISSED. FIX (next batch): widen capture to `([a-f0-9]{40}|[a-f0-9]{32})` (keyword-anchored so FP-safe) + add 32-hex contract positive. status=ready.
- gitea: 0 detectors, PRECISION-HARD — token is bare 40-hex (`GITEA_TOKEN=<sha1>`) which collides with git commit SHAs (`gitea/gitea@<40hex>` is a NEGATIVE in the same corpus). Needs careful negative handling (exclude `@`/commit context). status=deferred-needs-precision-design (NOT a mechanical add).
- etsy: 0 detectors — manifest positives are base64 blobs / source-code noise, no clean keystring format extractable. status=deferred-unclear-format.
- sumologic: 0 detectors — manifest examples are ALL placeholders (`{SumoAccessKey}`, `<SUMOLOGIC ACCESS KEY>`, `$2`) or a false "sumo" substring hit (`sumOfExposures`). No real positive to anchor. status=deferred-placeholder-only.
NET: the cleanly-addressable homefield recall was greptile (added) + wandb-widen (ready). google-api-key tighten (−16 FP) is the precision lever. gitea/etsy/sumologic are NOT mechanical adds.

detectors/wandb-api-key.toml capture `([a-f0-9]{40})` → `([a-f0-9]{40}|[a-f0-9]{32})` (keyword-anchored WANDB_API_KEY, FP-safe; regex-verified 32+40 match, no-keyword rejected; existing contract negatives are non-hex placeholders → no collision). Folded into the in-flight bxbg3ural bench (step-3 release build picks it up). PENDING next run: add a 32-hex positive to crates/scanner/tests/contracts/wandb-api-key.toml to test-lock it (deferred now because contract tomls are runtime-read → editing during the in-flight contracts run would false-fail against the old 40-only binary).

### §DETECTION — CredData retrain endgame: gate precisely characterized (2026-07-07)
The big CredData precision lever is the model-authoritative entropy-* MoE (entropy-token 4116 FP P=0.06 + entropy-api-key 2067 FP = 76% of 8168 CredData FP). Retrain is BLOCKED by `per_class_gate_error` (ml/train_classifier.py:829): defaults `--min-real-class-support=1` + `--min-real-class-recall=0.50` → EVERY held-out class with ≥1 positive must clear 0.50 recall@0.40. Real corpus = 32788 records / 160 classes, long tail (dozens of classes n_pos 1-4). A class with 1 hard held-out positive scoring <0.40 = recall 0.0 → REFUSE. support=1 is statistically meaningless for n_pos<~5 (one datapoint) — the gate is unshippable-by-construction on the tail. KEY INSIGHT: the retrain's CredData value is PRECISION (score entropy non-secrets lower); the gate demands RECALL. Class-weighting/oversampling raises recall but ADDS FP → WRONG direction for the precision goal. The correct precision lever is HARVESTING the entropy-* FP cases as NEGATIVES (harvest_corpus.py) → retrain → model scores them lower — but that needs the fresh CredData FP value/location dump (gated on the in-flight bench) + the binary. The regression check (baseline per-class) already guards real regressions independent of the absolute floor. DECISION NEEDED (user-owned, do not silently relax): either (a) raise min-real-class-support to a statistically-meaningful ~5-10 keeping the regression guard, or (b) accept the retrain stays measure-only until harvest-negatives improve tail precision. status=blocked-needs-fp-harvest+policy.

### §TESTING (#177) — placeholder suppression adversarial suite (2026-07-07)
context/placeholder.rs: added mod placeholder_suppression_adversarial_tests — 10 tests / 37 assertions across the previously thin-covered pure predicates: is_empty_input_hash (4 canonical empty-digests + case-fold + 4 near-miss negatives incl truncation/embed), is_hex_sequential_placeholder (ascending/descending/wrapping/upper + 4 negatives incl 15-char gate + non-hex), is_sequential_placeholder (all-same + period-2 + non-alnum pair; period-3 & <8 negatives), sequential_step_threshold (5 exact ratio locks), is_known_example_credential (all 6 arms + 3 real-secret survivors). ALL 37 assertions pre-verified against a faithful Python replica of the exact Rust logic (0 mismatches) before landing — validated at the next test build (safe from the in-flight release-build bench; test code not compiled by --release ci-lean build).

### §DETECTION — CredData precision ceiling CORRECTED (2026-07-07, strategy pivot)
REFUTED my own prior "harvest FP negatives → retrain" plan: ml/harvest_corpus.py ALREADY classifies every non-overlapping finding on a known file as a NEGATIVE (label=0) attributed to the file category (classify_finding L116; "hard negatives keyhog currently fires on" L151). The cached real_corpus.jsonl (32788 recs) ALREADY contains the entropy FP cases as negatives. The model trains on them and STILL can't separate — because entropy-token/entropy-api-key FP and TP are FEATURE-INDISTINGUISHABLE (fp_hist overlap in top buckets; value-shape features alone cannot tell a real 32-char base64 secret from a random 32-char base64 non-secret). So the CredData precision ceiling is NOT a data/retrain problem — it is a FEATURE-ARCHITECTURE problem: the MoE needs CONTEXT features (surrounding line semantics, assignment target, file role) not just the value bytes. That is a major arc (new feature extractor + serve-path parity + retrain), the real long-term CredData lever. Heuristic surfacing + sequential/example gates are SATURATED (−19 FP is the marginal ceiling of value-shape heuristics). Aligns with memory [[base64-protobuf-precision-is-inseparable-from-recall]] + [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]].

### §DETECTION — HONEST bench results, all changes (2026-07-07, correct binary)
Binary /mnt/FlareTraining/santh-archive/cargo-target/release/keyhog (17:44, ci-lean). Earlier "fp=0" runs were a KEYHOG_BIN path bug (global cargo target-dir = /mnt/FlareTraining/santh-archive/cargo-target, NOT keyhog/target/). REAL numbers:
- HOMEFIELD-betterleaks: fp=59 tp=55 fn=61 P=0.4825 R=0.4741 F1=0.4783.
  * greptile-api-key: 3 TP / 0 FP — REAL WIN (new detector works).
  * wandb-api-key: 1 TP / 0 FP — REAL WIN (32-hex widen works).
  * google-api-key: STILL 0 TP / 16 FP — my DRIVE tighten was the WRONG pattern. The 16 FP are from the BARE `AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}` pattern (line 18, no anchor). 15/16 are RANDOM AIzaSy… strings = betterleaks' CURATED negatives (its google rule allowlists/curates them; shape-INDISTINGUISHABLE from real keys — same ceiling as CredData). Only 1 (`AIzaSyabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567`) is a shape-catchable placeholder, and catching it via monotonic gate re-opens the vendor-contract conflict (contracts use alphabet filler as POSITIVES). Near-unwinnable on betterleaks' home turf.
- CREDDATA: fp=8181 tp=5245 fn=9856 F1=0.3677 = POSTMIG EXACTLY (zero delta). The sequential-scoped fix delivers ~0 CredData benefit: the earlier "−13 FP" was COLLATERAL of the OVER-BROAD universal is_known_example_credential placement (which broke 15 vendor contracts). Scoping it to entropy-only (to fix contracts) also scoped away the FP win. There is a REAL tension: monotonic suppression helps precision but a monotonic value is a legit vendor-contract POSITIVE — can't satisfy both by shape.
NET durable wins: greptile + wandb (+4 homefield TP). The precision plays (sequential/google) hit shape-indistinguishability ceilings. KEPT anyway (correct + tested + no regression): sequential-scoped (correct monotonic gate, adversarial-tested), google DRIVE tighten (real Drive keys ARE AIza), stopword revert (removed +6 FP).
OPEN (winnable): SES_PASS=dzdvnffvqp contract RED — generic-keyword-secret doesn't fire on bare `*_PASS=` (migration added the `pass` prefilter stem but not detector-keyword firing). Real *_PASS= recall gap; FIX = complete the wiring.

### §DETECTION — SES_PASS / *_PASS= recall FIX (2026-07-07, completes migration wiring)
ROOT CAUSE (via --dogfood): `SES_PASS=dzdvnffvqp` suppressed as `entropy_keyword_free_too_short` — the entropy path treated it KEYWORD-FREE (high entropy bar) because `normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential` (entropy/keywords.rs:360) matched the last `_`-segment against {key,secret,token,password,passwd,pwd} but NOT `pass`. Same value with `DB_PASSWORD=` fires (password recognized). My DET-0 migration added the `pass` PREFILTER stem (assignment_keywords::PASS_STEM) + `keyword_is_credential_anchor`, but MISSED this entropy-path suffix set — incomplete wiring. FIX: added `"pass"` to the `_`-separated-suffix match (keywords.rs:364). Boundary-safe by construction: the `_`-suffix requirement is the left boundary → `bypass`/`compass`/`encompass` (no `_`) and `ci_bypass` (suffix `bypass`) all excluded; only `<name>_PASS=` promoted. Locked with gap/normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential_exact.rs::a_pass_suffix_is_a_credential_but_bypass_and_compass_are_not (7 assertions). Verifying (bufr0bmwx): dogfood SES_PASS surfaces + contracts GREEN + creddata (+TP from real *_PASS=) + mirror (fp=975 guard: pass-widen must not flood). This is a WINNABLE real recall fix (dominant CredData *_PASS= env pattern), unlike the shape-indistinguishable google/entropy ceilings.

### §DETECTION — SES_PASS fix is TWO parts + ONE-PLACE unification (2026-07-07)
Fix #1 alone (credential-SURFACE suffix in normalized_assignment_keyword_is_credential) made `SES_PASS=` keyword-ANCHORED but a 2.7-entropy value still dropped: dogfood proved `SES_PASS=<20char>` + `MY_PASS=<20char>` now fire (real *_PASS= recall recovered), `FIRST_PASS=true` correctly stays suppressed — but the exact fixture (10-char entropy-2.7 `dzdvnffvqp`) still failed because BOTH entropy classifiers (`classify_entropy_detector_index` index-bucket [helpers.rs] AND `classify_keyword_to_detector_id` detector-id [scanner.rs]) routed `SES_PASS` to the API-Key/default tier (HIGH floor) since they only checked `password`/`pwd` — `DB_PASSWORD=<same value>` fires because it hits the Password tier (LOW floor). Fix #2: added ONE shared boundary-safe predicate `entropy::keywords::keyword_is_password_family` (last separator-segment == `pass`, so `bypass`/`compass`/`encompass`/`*_PASSING` excluded) and routed BOTH classifiers through it — a ONE-PLACE unification (the two sibling classifiers can no longer drift on which keys are passwords). Tests: gap/…_exact.rs `password_family_routes_pass_stem_but_not_bypass_or_passing` (12 assertions, Python-verified 0 mismatch) + the credential-surface `a_pass_suffix…` test. Verifying (bj7imd3b9 quick dogfood → then full contracts+creddata+mirror). Precision guard: mirror POSTMIG fp=975 — the Password-tier routing must not flood; the last-segment boundary + value gates (FIRST_PASS=true suppressed) bound the risk.

### §DETECTION — SES_PASS root cause was the OWNING-DETECTOR find (fix #3, the real one)
Fixes #1 (entropy credential-surface) + #2 (entropy classify ONE PLACE) recovered *_PASS= with DECENT-entropy values (dogfood: MY_PASS=<20char> fires) and confirmed boundary safety (BYPASS/COMPASS/FIRST_PASS=true all correctly 0), but the exact fixture (10-char entropy-2.7) still failed. TRUE root cause: phase2_generic.rs:334 owning-detector find matches the boundary-validated keyword token (`pass`, from the existing generic_bridge_keyword_boundary_rejected guard) against generic phase2 detector keyword lists — NO detector had `pass`, so it fell back to generic-secret (HIGH floor) → `generic_entropy_below_floor` on 2.7. `DB_PASSWORD` owns via generic-keyword-secret (keyword `password`, LOW floor 1.5) → surfaces. The DET-0 PASS_STEM design added `pass` to the assignment_keywords PREFILTER only (admits the candidate) but deliberately NOT as a detector keyword — leaving it OWNERLESS. FIX #3: added `pass` to generic-keyword-secret.keywords (a shapeless phase2-generic detector with NO regex anchor, so the design's regex-pollution concern doesn't apply). Now `pass`-keyed candidates are owned by generic-keyword-secret at its low floor. Boundary-safe (the bridge guard already rejects bypass/compass; value gates suppress FIRST_PASS=true). merkle_spec_hash test is mechanism-only (no golden); `pass` already in RECALL_CRITICAL superset → derived set unchanged. FOLLOWUP (ONE PLACE): assignment_keywords::PASS_STEM is now redundant (derived from the detector) — remove it + its test after verifying. Verifying bwn420h8c dogfood → full contracts+creddata+mirror.

### §DETECTION — SES_PASS fix COMMITS creddata-precision to DET-1 (ML feature), not the floor-raise (2026-07-07)
IMPORTANT interaction: the SES_PASS fix makes generic-keyword-secret OWN `*_PASS=` candidates at its LOW floor (entropy_floor 1.5) so a 2.7-entropy password surfaces. But the ledger's crude creddata-precision lever (raise generic-keyword-secret entropy_low 3.0→4.5 to kill the entropy-token 4465-FP flood) would RE-SUPPRESS SES_PASS=dzdvnffvqp (2.7) → break the contract. The entropy-token flood AND *_PASS= recall both route through generic-keyword-secret, so a whole-detector floor-raise can't separate them. CONCLUSION: creddata precision MUST come from DET-1 (the 43rd ML feature: service-vocab keyword vs generic key — separates real secrets from generic-keyword IDs by SIGNAL, not floor; ships F1 0.457 w/o mirror regression per the plan), NOT the crude floor-raise. The floor-raise lever is now OFF the table (conflicts with the *_PASS= recall win). This is the right call — DET-1 was already the identified "clean win"; the floor-raise was the cruder alternative. dzdvnffvqp surfacing at 2.7 is exactly the kind of weak-but-real password the ML model (not a blunt floor) should adjudicate.

### §SPEED — GPU step-2 coverage limit SCOPED (2026-07-07): it is NOT a constant-bump
The 64-pattern cap (phase2_gpu_dfa.rs:43-49) = TARGET_SHARD_PATTERNS(16) × MAX_SHARDS(4), and it is DFA-STATE-BOUNDED, not arbitrary: each shard combines 16 regex patterns into ONE GPU DFA capped at MAX_STATES=16_384; combining more patterns/shard EXPLODES the DFA state count. So covering the ~2700 phase-2 patterns is a real scaling problem, NOT a MAX_SHARDS bump:
- More patterns/shard (16→N) → DFA state explosion past 16_384 → won't fit GPU.
- More shards (4→~169 for 2700) → 169 separate GPU DFA passes → dispatch overhead (the step-3 concern) likely exceeds the CPU-RegexSet savings, AND first-use DFA compilation cost (the candidates.rs comment's reason for the bound).
Candidate selection (candidates.rs prioritized_phase2_gpu_dfa_candidates) already gives the 64 slots to the highest detector-BREADTH patterns; the rest stay CPU-authoritative → `complete` never holds → step-1b (GPU-marks-substitute-CPU) is permanently inert at 64 coverage. IMPLICATION: "GPU OOM-faster than Hyperscan >8MB" via the DFA megakernel needs a DIFFERENT approach than per-pattern DFA sharding — candidates: (a) vyre literal-set region-presence (GPU AC-style, scales to all literal-anchored patterns = the majority) as the primary GPU prefilter + DFA only for the literal-free minority; (b) a single large NFA/bit-parallel GPU automaton instead of many small DFAs. This is a research arc, GPU-bench-gated on the RTX 5090. NEXT: benchmark current --backend simd-regex (Hyperscan) vs gpu-mega-scan vs gpu-region-presence on the 8.4MB corpus to measure the real gap + which GPU path is closest.

### §SPEED — GPU >8MB BLOCKER FOUND + FIXED (2026-07-07): output-buffer overflow → CPU degrade
ROOT CAUSE of "GPU not OOM-faster than Hyperscan >8MB": the GPU positioned-literal path NEVER RAN on >8MB. On the 8.4MB dense corpus vyre's GpuLiteralSet found 122020 matches but the output buffer capped at 65_536 (GPU_POSITIONED_LITERAL_MAX_MATCHES) → vyre fails CLOSED (Law-10 correct, "exceeds the output-buffer cap; decoding would silently drop 56484") → the whole scan degraded to CPU. So GPU speed evidence on the target size class was UNMEASURABLE — it degraded before running.
THE DEEPER BUG: gpu_region_dispatch.rs HAS adaptive window-halving (split_positioned_window) meant to page dense windows down until each fits the cap — but it was wired ONLY to the `matches.len() >= cap` SUCCESS path (line ~682). vyre signals overflow via `Err(BackendError)`, not a capped-success list, so the Err branch (line ~666) degraded to CPU and the halving was DEAD for the real overflow signal.
FIX (landed, gpu-bench-gated, verifying bjhyhf0aj):
1. Raised GPU_POSITIONED_LITERAL_MAX_MATCHES 65_536 → 1<<20 (1M). Scratch-reused buffer, only actual count read back → one-time ~16MB device alloc (nothing on 32GB 5090), ZERO per-dispatch cost. Makes the realistic >8MB..~70MB range fit ONE dispatch (122k << 1M) instead of overflowing.
2. Err branch now detects the fail-closed overflow (is_gpu_output_buffer_overflow, matches vyre's own tested "output-buffer cap" substring) and HALVES+retries on GPU (the same paging the success path does) instead of degrading — so >1M-match inputs (dense >70MB) also stay on GPU. Only a min-splittable-floor window or a genuine device error degrades loudly.
3. Contract test overflow_detection_tests: overflow→pager, device-error→degrade; locks the vyre wording so a drift fails loudly not silently.
NOTE: this is DISTINCT from the region-presence anchorless-divergence gap (task #181) — that's a RECALL gap (GPU literal-presence can't reproduce SIMD's ~49 no-literal detectors); this was a SPEED/robustness blocker (GPU couldn't run at all on dense >8MB). Both must be closed for "GPU beats Hyperscan >8MB with full recall". NEXT after bench: measure GPU-region-presence vs simd wall time on 8.4MB now that GPU actually runs; if GPU faster → the anchorless-recall gap (#181) is the remaining blocker to a shippable GPU-default.

### §SPEED — GPU overflow fix VALIDATED + the real bottleneck (2026-07-07)
FIX CONFIRMED: after the cap raise (65_536→1<<20) + Err-branch halving, gpu-region-presence RUNS on the 8.4MB corpus — stderr shows ONLY the anchorless DIVERGES notice, NO "output-buffer cap"/degrade. Overflow-detection unit test green. Was: degrade-to-CPU on every dense >8MB scan.
BENCH (median-of-5, 8.4MB, RTX 5090): simd/Hyperscan 3.950s · gpu-region-presence 2.043s (**1.93× faster than Hyperscan**) · cpu 1.572s (fastest). All 8070 findings (parity held on this corpus).
INSIGHT — why GPU isn't "oom" (10×) faster yet: this corpus is candidate-DENSE (8070 findings / 122k literal candidates) so entropy+ML+decode POST-PROCESSING dominates, NOT the phase-2 regex prefilter (the only thing GPU accelerates). cpu beating gpu confirms the prefilter is not the bottleneck here. The size class where GPU should crush Hyperscan is PREFILTER-BOUND: many bytes, FEW candidates (prefilter scans everything, post-processing is cheap). NEXT: measure simd vs gpu on a sparse >8MB corpus to isolate the prefilter advantage; that's the honest "GPU vs Hyperscan" number. Also: bench script bug fixed — it flagged the anchorless DIVERGES notice as "degrade"; DIVERGES is a recall notice, not a fallback.

### §SPEED — THE bottleneck is the always-active RegexSet, NOT Hyperscan (2026-07-07, --profile proof)
`--profile` on 16MB sparse (simd/default features, so Hyperscan IS compiled): phase2:prefilter = **94.7% of scan** (15.6s CPU-summed), and the mark split is `[hs=0 (0.0%) regexset=18 (100.0%)]` — "populate: always-active RegexSet prefilter=15610.2 ms". So the dominant cost is the ALWAYS-ACTIVE (anchorless, no-literal) patterns running on the Rust `regex` RegexSet, NOT Hyperscan. Hyperscan handles the literal-anchored patterns (fast, cheap here); the anchorless set is routed to RegexSet BY DESIGN and dominates. (Matches the known "[[fb:prefilter homoglyph]]" + HsFallback "always-active prefilter is a 2.4x whole-scan pessimization" notes.)
CONSEQUENCE for the GPU goal: gpu-region-presence accelerates LITERALS (not the bottleneck) → it can't win on prefilter-bound input (measured: 16MB sparse gpu-region-presence 1.80s SLOWER than simd 1.35s — GPU dispatch/transfer overhead with nothing to offload). The GPU DFA path (phase2_gpu_dfa) DOES target the always-active set — but covers only 64 patterns. So "GPU oom-faster than Hyperscan >8MB" reduces to: **accelerate the always-active anchorless prefilter** (currently RegexSet @94.7%). Levers, in order of likely win: (1) can the anchorless patterns run on HYPERSCAN instead of RegexSet? Hyperscan is built for multi-pattern anchorless scan and is ~10-50× faster than RegexSet — if they're HS-compilable this is a huge CPU-only win before any GPU. (2) GPU-DFA the anchorless set (expand coverage past 64, the real step-2). (3) shrink the always-active set. INVESTIGATE NEXT: count of always-active patterns + why they route to RegexSet not HS (HS-incompatible constructs? or just unwired?).

### §SPEED — 2791/2807 phase-2 patterns are always-active RegexSet (Hyperscan UNUSED on the hot path) — the real lever (2026-07-07)
Measured (debug trace): total phase-2 = 2807, **always_active = 2791 (99.4%)**, phase2-anchor eligible = 1. So ~all phase-2 regex detectors run through the always-active RegexSet — the 94.7%-of-scan bottleneck — and the mark split proves Hyperscan is NOT applied there (`hs=0 regexset=100%`). Hyperscan (`simd` feature = dep:hyperscan) IS compiled and handles the confirmed-anchor LITERAL patterns, but the huge anchorless set falls to Rust RegexSet (~1 MB/s vs HS's GB/s). Split rule (gpu_artifacts.rs:98): always-active = pattern has NO keyword ≥4 chars → keywordless/homoglyph/regex-only detectors, which is nearly all of them.
THE LEVER (CPU-only, likely bigger than any GPU change): route the HS-COMPATIBLE always-active patterns through Hyperscan instead of RegexSet. HS is purpose-built for thousands of anchorless patterns in one automaton. Existing machinery hints it's partially there: `append_hyperscan_unsupported_patterns` / `reroute_hyperscan_unsupported_patterns` (gpu_artifacts.rs) + HsFallbackEngine (the memory's "always-active prefilter = 2.4x whole-scan pessimization / 270x redundant work" note = this exact component, only partially fixed). INVESTIGATE: does an HS path for the always-active set exist but sit disabled/bugged, or was it never wired? Homoglyph unicode-class variants may be the HS-incompatible remainder that must stay on RegexSet (keep those; route the rest). This subsumes the GPU-DFA step-2 (GPU would then race HS, not RegexSet). Recall parity gate + criterion before/after mandatory.

### §SPEED — ★ THE WIN: HS_PREFILTER_MAX_LEN_DEFAULT=4096 caps Hyperscan off large chunks; HS is 1.3–11.5× FASTER there (2026-07-07)
The always-active prefilter (94.7% of scan) has a Hyperscan path (Phase2HsEngine) that is GATED to chunks ≤ 4096 bytes (HS_PREFILTER_MAX_LEN_DEFAULT, scanner_config.rs:38). >8MB scans chunk at ~900KB → HS never engages → the slow RegexSet runs. The gate comment (phase2_prefilter.rs:587) claims "HS's unicode automaton loses to the folded/truncated RegexSet on large chunks" — MEASURED FALSE on the current binary (RTX 5090, `--config [tuning].hs_prefilter_max_len=huge` forces HS on large chunks, confirmed `hs=18 (100%)`):
- 16MB ASCII sparse (prefilter-bound): 1.349s → 0.703s = **1.92×**; prefilter CPU 15942ms → 4569ms.
- 8.4MB ASCII dense (8070 findings): 4.157s → 3.236s = **1.28×**; findings hash IDENTICAL (81b90af4) — recall parity holds.
- 8MB NON-ASCII homoglyph (54% non-ascii bytes): 7.642s → 0.666s = **11.5×** — the RegexSet ASCII-fold can't apply so it runs the full unicode automaton at 7.6s while HS does 0.666s. This is the order-of-magnitude ("oom") win, and it's the exact HOMOGLYPH-EVASION surface (unicode-heavy input) — CPU-only, no GPU.
FIX: raise HS_PREFILTER_MAX_LEN_DEFAULT (the size-dispatch is obsolete — HS wins at every tested size; it already wins on small chunks). Keep RegexSet as the fallback for HS-incompatible `dropped` patterns + build failure. VALIDATE: phase2 prefilter parity tests + contracts_runner + creddata/mirror bench (detection MUST be unchanged) + criterion. This SUBSUMES the GPU-DFA step-2 for the anchorless set — HS on CPU already beats RegexSet by 1.3–11.5×; GPU would need to beat HS, a far higher bar. The GPU overflow fix (earlier) stays valid for the literal path; this HS-cap fix is the bigger, simpler lever for the user's ">8MB faster" goal.

### §SPEED — ★ CORRECTION (SUPERSEDES the "★ THE WIN" block above): HS-on-large is a RECALL DIVERGENCE, cap reverted to 4096 (2026-07-07)
The HS-cap lift is NOT a clean parity speedup. Validated (deterministic, 2 identical creddata runs): lifting HS_PREFILTER_MAX_LEN_DEFAULT 4096→∞ changed creddata fp 8185→5488 (−2697), tp 5258→5198 (−60), F1 0.3684→0.4031. Mirror UNCHANGED (small files, HS already engaged). The delta is ENTIRELY in the anchorless always-active detectors (entropy-token −1767fp/−23tp, entropy-api-key −684/−8, generic-keyword-secret −156/−21, generic-secret −84/−8); zero vendor detectors moved. bench score.py matches TP by VALUE (containment), so the −60 TP are 60 REAL ground-truth secrets no longer surfaced — a genuine recall loss on large files (Law 6/10 silent-weaker-path). The +0.035 F1 is a SIDE EFFECT of the entropy detectors being FP-heavy (drops 45 FP per 1 TP), NOT a principled precision decision — and it is INSEPARABLE from the recall loss (both come from HS under-marking; fixing HS to be recall-identical would restore BOTH the 60 TP and the 2697 FP → no F1 change, just speed). So the achievable CLEAN win is pure SPEED (HS recall-identical on large chunks), not the F1 bump.
REVERTED: HS_PREFILTER_MAX_LEN_DEFAULT back to 4096 (default behavior unchanged, no recall loss); the `[tuning] hs_prefilter_max_len` knob stays for opt-in. Comments in scanner_config.rs + phase2_prefilter.rs updated to the true reason (recall divergence, not the old false "HS slower on large" claim).
ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS (the fix that unlocks the pure-speed win): HS builds with SINGLEMATCH (phase2_hs.rs:28, "fires once instead of storming the callback") → it reports ONE position per pattern per chunk, i.e. presence only. If the entropy/generic EXTRACTION is driven by the marked match POSITIONS (not a whole-chunk rescan), then on a large chunk RegexSet supplies ALL positions while HS supplies one → HS extracts fewer candidates → −FP −TP. FIX DIRECTION: confirm whether always-active marking is presence-only (binary) or position-driven; if position-driven, HS must report all positions for those detectors (drop SINGLEMATCH for them, or whole-chunk-rescan on HS-mark) so it is recall-identical. Then lift the cap for the pure 1.3–11.5× speed win. Until then: 4096 stays. NOTE: the GPU >8MB overflow fix (earlier this session) is INDEPENDENT and stays landed+valid.

### §SPEED — non-ASCII HS 11.5× lever: ROOT-CAUSE MECHANISM + fix path (2026-07-07)
WHY the is_ascii gate keeps RegexSet on large non-ASCII (the −60 CredData TP when HS ran there):
`Phase2HsEngine::mark` scans `match_text.as_bytes()` with `utf8:false` (BYTE mode) — chosen because HS_FLAG_UTF8 rejects the homoglyph classes. But the host RegexSet matches in Rust regex UNICODE mode. For the generic/entropy patterns (broad `.`/`\w`/`\p{...}` + length-bounded quantifiers) these DIVERGE on non-ASCII: byte-mode `.`/`\w` match single BYTES not codepoints, and `{m,n}` counts bytes not chars — so HS UNDER-marks those patterns on multi-byte content → the pattern is never extracted → real secret lost. (Confirmed empirically: the −60 TP were "only entropy/generic detectors"; deterministic.)
FIX PATH (unlocks HS on ALL chunks incl. non-ASCII = the 11.5× win, recall-safe):
1. Extend `hs_prefilter_requires_host_regex` (or a new byte-mode-safety predicate) to detect byte-mode-UNSAFE patterns — those whose match set differs between byte and unicode semantics (contain unescaped `.`, `\w`/`\W`/`\d`/`\s` with unicode flag, `\p{...}`, or any non-ASCII literal inside a bounded quantifier). Route them to the existing `dropped` set (host regex per-chunk, recall-identical, Law-10-clean).
2. With the divergent patterns on the host path, HS byte-mode becomes recall-IDENTICAL to RegexSet on non-ASCII too → the is_ascii clause in `hs_prefilter_engages` can drop (engage HS on every chunk regardless of ascii-ness).
3. VERIFY: HS-on-all vs RegexSet must be CredData/mirror byte-parity (the exact gate that caught the naive cap-lift), THEN measure the non-ASCII >8MB speedup.
BLOCKED ON: per-pattern divergence analysis needs the binary (run HS-on vs HS-off per non-ASCII CredData file to enumerate the exact under-marked pattern ids) — cannot start while the lock is held. This is the highest-value remaining SPEED unit.

### §SPEED — non-ASCII HS fix path: VIABILITY CONFIRMED + host-RegexSet requirement (2026-07-07, pure analysis)
Static scan of all 1878 detector regexes: **96.2% (1807) are byte-mode-SAFE** (ASCII classes `[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{n}` etc. — HS byte-mode matches them IDENTICALLY on non-ASCII); **only 3.8% (71) are byte-mode-DIVERGENT, ALL `dot-quant`** (unescaped `.` + quantifier `.{n}`/`.*`/`.+`/`.?` — byte-mode `.` = one byte, unicode `.` = one codepoint, so `.{20}` diverges on multi-byte content). Filter further to always-active (anchorless) → ≤71, likely far fewer.
=> FIX IS VIABLE (small divergent set) BUT with a REQUIRED refinement: the current `dropped` host path is a per-pattern `is_match` LOOP (fine for the few ^/$ patterns today). Adding ~71 there as a loop = 71 separate chunk passes → dominates + erases the win. MUST combine the host-path patterns into ONE `regex::RegexSet` (single pass). Then HS-on-non-ASCII = 1 HS pass (≈2720 pats) + 1 small-RegexSet pass (≈71 pats) vs today's 1 RegexSet pass (2791 pats); rough model ≈ 4.5× faster on non-ASCII >8MB (conservative vs the 11.5× raw-HS upper bound).
CONCRETE NEXT: (a) add byte-mode-safety predicate (unescaped `.`+quantifier, `\w`/`\W`, `\p{}`) → route matches to a NEW combined-RegexSet host member on Phase2HsEngine (replace the `dropped` Vec<(usize,LazyRegex)> loop with a RegexSet + id map); (b) drop the is_ascii clause in `hs_prefilter_engages`; (c) bench-verify CredData/mirror BYTE-PARITY (the gate that caught the naive cap-lift), then measure non-ASCII >8MB. Est. ~150-250 LOC in phase2_hs.rs + phase2_prefilter.rs. Highest-value remaining SPEED unit; no binary needed to START the predicate+RegexSet refactor (only to final-verify).

### §SPEED — non-ASCII HS 11.5× lever: IMPLEMENTED, verification running (2026-07-07)
Code complete (verification gate bzzndv8rx: build + CredData/mirror byte-parity + non-ASCII 8MB speedup + findings-parity):
- **phase2_hs.rs**: new `is_byte_mode_divergent(src)` predicate (routes unescaped `.`, `\w\W\d\D\s\S\b\B`, `\p{}\P{}`; escape/class-tracked like `hs_prefilter_requires_host_regex`) + `DivergentHost { set: RegexSet, phase2_ids }`. `build` routes divergent always-active patterns into ONE unicode host RegexSet (`(?i)` inlined per case flag, 64MB size limit, per-pattern `dropped` fallback on set-compile failure). `mark`/`any_match` add the single divergent RegexSet pass. + `byte_mode_divergence_tests` (safe-vs-divergent classification lock).
- **phase2_prefilter.rs**: `hs_prefilter_engages` drops the `is_ascii` clause → `fallback_hs && chunk_len <= max_len` (max_len now a safety valve). Call site + `hs_gate_tests` rewritten.
- **scanner_config.rs**: `HS_PREFILTER_MAX_LEN_DEFAULT` 4096 → `usize::MAX` (HS now engages on every chunk; recall preserved by DivergentHost).
MECHANISM: HS byte-mode is findings-IDENTICAL to the unicode RegexSet for ASCII-class patterns even on non-ASCII content (ASCII bytes are single-byte), so only the ~12.5% divergent patterns needed the unicode host set. This unblocks HS on all chunks = the large-chunk speedup on non-ASCII too. GATED BY CredData/mirror byte-parity (8185/5258, 975/2768) — the same gate that caught the naive cap-lift's −60 TP.

### §SPEED — non-ASCII HS fix: CAUGHT + FIXED a ~10× perf regression (2026-07-07)
FIRST implementation (divergent patterns REMOVED from HS DB → routed to a unicode host RegexSet that ran on EVERY chunk) was RECALL-correct but a CATASTROPHIC Law-7 pessimization: a creddata scan burned 3 HOURS of CPU in 17min wall (vs ~1-2min baseline) — the 235-pattern dot-heavy RegexSet ran unanchored on every ASCII chunk (where byte-mode HS was already exact). Same class as the HsFallbackEngine 270× redundant-work violation in CLAUDE.md ("recall-preserving but slow is STILL a bug").
FIX: divergent patterns STAY in the byte-mode HS DB (exact on ASCII) AND are compiled into the host RegexSet, which now runs ONLY on `!match_text.is_ascii()` chunks (mark/any_match gated). ASCII chunks → HS only (fast, unchanged); non-ASCII chunks → HS + the 235-pattern RegexSet (bounded, rare, still far cheaper than the 2791-pattern portable set). LESSON: any always-active supplemental pass MUST be gated to the chunks that actually need it — an unconditional extra whole-chunk scan is a whole-scan multiplier. Verify (bcvam86xa) now has a 180s wall cap so a perf regression fails loud instead of hanging.

### §SPEED — non-ASCII HS lever: CLOSED as a DEAD END (CPU-proven, reverted) (2026-07-07)
Implemented the DivergentHost approach (divergent `.`/`\w`/`\s` patterns → unicode host RegexSet supplementing byte-mode HS on non-ASCII) and it was RECALL-CORRECT (non-ASCII findings parity 2==2, mirror 975/2768 exact). But a contention-independent CPU-time measurement killed it: on a 2 MiB non-ASCII corpus HS-on used **51.9s CPU vs the RegexSet's 18.8s (2.75× SLOWER)**. Root reason: HS byte-mode ≈ the RegexSet's cost on non-ASCII, and the divergent set is dot-heavy so it CANNOT be prefix-AC-gated the way the portable batches are → pure added overhead. The recall-required work dominates non-ASCII either way, so the gated portable RegexSet is strictly faster. FULLY REVERTED to the proven `is_ascii` gate (HS on ASCII any size = the real 2× >8MB win; RegexSet on large non-ASCII = recall-safe AND faster). The "11.5× non-ASCII HS win" documented earlier was an artifact of HS UNDER-marking (dropping recall), not a real win. DO NOT re-attempt HS-on-non-ASCII without a fundamentally cheaper divergent path. Dead-end rationale is now inline in scanner_config.rs + phase2_prefilter.rs (ONE PLACE). KEPT from this batch: detector recall adds (gitea, gitlab-rrt, sourcegraph keyword-form, assemblyai {24,32} widen) — independent of the reverted perf change.

### §DETECTION — CredData FP attribution (evidence for #184, from rv-creddata.json 2026-07-07)
Per-detector FP on CredData (total 8185 FP). The F1 gap to betterleaks (0.37 vs 0.53) is a PRECISION problem concentrated in a few detectors:
- **entropy-token: fp=4120 tp=263 P=0.06** — #1 drag (50% of all FP alone).
- **entropy-api-key: fp=2073 tp=575 P=0.22** — #2. Together these two = 75% of all CredData FP.
- generic-keyword-secret fp=530 P=0.62; generic-secret fp=252 P=0.43; nats-credentials fp=179 P=0.25; entropy-password fp=165 P=0.57.
- PURE-FP (0 TP, 0 recall risk on CredData): google-cloud-storage-hmac-key fp=78 (unanchored `GOOG[0-9A-Z]{37,40}` firing in DECODED content — 0 in raw files), aws-secrets-manager-arn fp=33 (flags ARNs, which are IDENTIFIERS not secrets per its own doc — arguably shouldn't fire).
MODELLED F1 levers (from these counts): disabling entropy-token alone → P 0.39→0.55, F1 0.368→~0.414 (loses 263 real TP — a recall/precision tradeoff, NOT a free win). Real fix = entropy PRECISION not disabling: the entropy→MoE ML routing is env-gated OFF (KEYHOG_ENTROPY_ML_AUTHORITATIVE) because pure authority regressed recall on the OLD model; the model was since retrained on the real distribution (recall 0→0.76 per memory), so an A/B of the authoritative flag on the NEW model is the highest-value CredData experiment — needs a STABLE bench env (currently hostile: multi-session earlyoom killing scans). tp=0 detectors are safe micro-wins (−111 FP, ~+0.002 F1) but the entropy floods are the only path to actually beating betterleaks on CredData.

### §DETECTION — entropy_ml_authoritative CORRECTION + ready A/B (2026-07-07)
CORRECTION to the "entropy unification gated off" memory: `entropy_ml_authoritative` now DEFAULTS ON (core/config.rs:128 `default_entropy_ml_authoritative()->true`). So the CredData entropy-token P=0.06 (4120 FP) flood happens WITH the MoE model active. The flag gates `allow_canonical_lift` — the MoE RELEASES entropy candidates the shape-heuristics would block (higher recall + higher FP). So the A/B is: does turning it OFF (`--no-entropy-ml-scoring`, CLI-only, no env fallback) trade enough FP for a net F1 gain? Modelled ceiling if it removed entropy-token's FP without its TP: F1 0.368→~0.41; real answer needs the run.
READY-TO-RUN (blocked only on a stable bench env — currently hostile: sessions 1635/e987 + earlyoom killing scans; had to kill a 3hr runaway from the reverted perf bug): direct keyhog A/B, both scored by bench/score.py against corpora/creddata/CredData/meta/*.csv —
  keyhog scan CredData/data --format json --show-secrets --no-suppress-test-fixtures --no-config --backend simd --no-daemon --no-gpu [--no-entropy-ml-scoring] --output {on,off}.json
Needs a ~40-line harness (bench has score() at score.py:264 but no CLI). DET-1 (43rd ML feature service-vocab-vs-generic + retrain, projected F1 0.457) is the higher-ceiling CredData lever but requires INPUT_DIM 42→43 + a full model retrain (can't land edit-only — the model rejects a 43rd input without retraining).

- clerk-frontend-api-key ← drop clerk-api-key  (`pk_(?:live|test)_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}`; BOTH are the PUBLISHABLE/client_safe key — clerk-api-key is MISNAMED, it is NOT the secret `sk_`; the real sk_ secret is already caught via Stripe's shared `sk_live_` prefix)

### §DETECTION — entropy A/B DEFINITIVE (2026-07-07, env-clear, scored by bench/score.py)
RESOLVED. entropy_ml_authoritative ON (default) vs OFF on CredData:
- ON : fp=8185 tp=5258 F1=0.3684  (== baseline, harness validated)
- OFF: fp=19478 tp=5248 F1=0.2635  (fp +11293, tp -10, F1 -0.1049)

Turning it off lets shape-heuristics flood 19k FP. The flag is OPTIMAL; do NOT flip it. The 8185 FP are what SURVIVE the best model → the ONLY CredData precision lever left is a BETTER MODEL or a post-model precision filter: the betterleaks "rare-not-random" BPE chars-per-token filter (§line 4734) is now the #1 #184 lever. status=A/B-experiment CLOSED (negative), BPE-filter PROMOTED to primary.

### §DETECTION — BPE "rare-not-random" filter VALIDATED offline (2026-07-07) — #184 PRIMARY LEVER
Offline prototype on ab_on.json (real CredData scan) scored by bench/score.py, filter = suppress entropy-* findings whose tiktoken cl100k_base chars-per-token > T:
- BASELINE: fp=8185 tp=5258 F1=0.3684
- T=2.0: fp=4168 tp=5149 F1=0.4217   T=2.2 (PEAK): fp=4260 tp=5203 P=0.550 R=0.345 F1=0.4236 (+0.0552, +15%)   T=2.5: fp=4481 tp=5225 F1=0.4213
WHY it works: entropy-token FP are WORD-LIKE structured identifiers (`PInvoke.User32.WindowMessage.WM_SYSCOLORCHANGE` = 3.1 chars/tok, XML/HTML fragments, dotted API paths) — they compress into few common BPE subwords; real secrets (`ghp_a8Xk…` = 1.18 chars/tok) tokenize into many random short tokens. At T=2.2 the suppressed FP are entropy-token 3082 + entropy-api-key 778 + entropy-password 120. Recall cost = -55 TP (word-like real secrets) for -3925 FP.
This closes 1/3 of the gap to betterleaks (0.53) with ONE filter — betterleaks' OWN technique, so fair. status=OFFLINE-VALIDATED, needs Rust impl (tiktoken cl100k_base BPE + chars/tok gate on the entropy post-score path) + full bench + mirror-recall check.

### §SPEED — GPU >8MB crossover MEASURED (2026-07-07, env-clear, RTX 5090, ci-lean+gpu build)
Fresh-process CLI scans, wall median, warmup+measured:
- 16 MiB (cache-affected corpus): simd 0.69s (23 MiB/s) vs gpu 1.14s → GPU 0.61x (SIMD wins)
- 31 MiB UNIQUE base64 content: simd 2.32s vs gpu 2.84s → GPU 0.82x (SIMD wins)
- GPU init overhead ≈ 0.44s/scan (tiny-file gpu 0.97s vs simd 0.53s) — paid EVERY one-shot process.
FINDING: "GPU faster >8MB" is NOT met for one-shot CLI scans at 16–32 MiB. The GPU SCAN KERNEL is ~AT-PARITY with AVX-512 Hyperscan (gpu 2.84 − 0.44 init ≈ 2.40 ≈ simd 2.32), but per-process CUDA context init (~0.44s) makes the TOTAL slower. GPU total-win needs either (a) files >> 32 MiB to amortize init, (b) the DAEMON (persistent GPU context — the real fix), or (c) a genuinely faster kernel. PLUS `--backend gpu` has the loud anchorless-recall gap (doctor warns; entropy/generic + HS-only vendor under-fire). So GPU is not a CLI win at 8–32 MiB today. NOTE: earlier memory "1.93x dense >8MB" was a different measurement (dense content / kernel-only / daemon), not the one-shot CLI path. status=open — the daemon-amortized path + the >>32MiB crossover point are the next measurements; the init cost is the real lever (#183 reframed: amortize GPU init, don't just make the kernel faster).
PARITY CONFIRMED: keyhog scan JSON is a top-level LIST (not {findings:...}); simd 32 == gpu 32 findings on the 16MiB AKIA corpus — GPU is slower but recall-EQUAL on literal-anchored content (the anchorless gap only bites no-literal detectors, as doctor warns).
