### §SPEED — BPE Law-7 check PASS (2026-07-07)
CredData scan WITH BPE = 62.0s (15.7 MB/s) vs ~64s baseline — BPE is a rounding error on speed (bounded to survivors of cheaper gates; tiktoken on short strings is fast). Precision win is FREE. Law 7 satisfied.
REMAINING #187 SPEED lever (untouched, biggest opportunity): keyhog 62s vs betterleaks 3s (~20x). betterleaks does far less per-byte work. This is a big architectural effort (reduce per-byte work / saturate the 94.7% prefilter), NOT a quick win. The user's "orders of magnitude faster" goal lives here. Next: profile a scan (`--profile`) to attribute the 62s, then attack the dominant phase.

### §SPEED — #187 PROFILE ATTRIBUTION (2026-07-07, --profile on CredData, 62s scan)
Per-pattern stages total 25.1s; extract dominates:
- **extract: 22021ms = 87.7%, runs=9,962,281 (~10M), 2210ns/run** ← THE target
- anchor-collect: 2345ms 9.3% (379k runs); suffix-gate: 755ms 3.0% (161k runs)
DOMINANT extract consumers: base64-encoded-keyword-prefix patterns `(?:NzA|NzE|…|MTA|MTE|…|Mj…|OD…)` (decode-through's base64 keyword match) — dozens of them, tens of thousands of runs EACH, OVER-triggering on CredData's dense base64 data. Second: every pattern's homoglyph char-classes `[hнһｈ][tтτｔ]…` (~4× complexity; see [[fb-prefilter-homoglyph-not-skippable-on-ascii]]).
LEVERS (all DEEP, big effort — the real "orders of magnitude" #187 work):
1. base64-keyword-prefix OVER-TRIGGER: 10M extract runs, most FP on base64 blobs. Batch the base64-prefix set into ONE AC/prefilter pass instead of dozens of separate extracting patterns; OR gate decode-through base64-keyword matching behind a stronger signal (recall-risky — see [[validator-bypass-on-fast-path]]).
2. homoglyph 4× expansion: close the base-AC coverage gap so ASCII fast-path can skip homoglyph variants (skip-on-ASCII was refuted — drops generic-password — so needs the AC coverage fix, not a naive skip).
COVERAGE WARNINGS from the same run (recall notes): 241 decode roots hit decode-through budget/cap (deeper layers unexpanded); 2 structured files failed to parse (encoded secrets undecoded); 524 files skipped (lock/minified/vendored default-exclusion).
keyhog 62s vs betterleaks 3s: the 20× gap is the extract stage. This is a multi-cycle architectural effort, not a quick edit.

### §SPEED — #187 decode A/B (2026-07-07) — decode is NOT the lever, base64-prefix DETECTORS are
--no-decode: 53.8s / fp3620 tp5149 R0.3410 F1 0.4314  vs  WITH-decode: 62.0s / fp3720 tp5169 R0.3423 F1 0.4309.
FINDINGS: (1) the decode FEATURE costs only ~8s (13%) and is barely net-positive on CredData (F1 0.4309 vs 0.4314 without — +20 TP but +100 FP; corpus-specific, keep it for base64-encoded-secret corpora). (2) The 87.7% extract cost RUNS REGARDLESS of --no-decode → it is NOT decode; it is the compiled base64-encoded-keyword-prefix DETECTORS (`(?:NzA|MTA|…)` matching encoded keywords on RAW text) + homoglyph 4× class expansion. (3) SUB-BUG candidate: with --no-decode the base64-encoded-keyword PREFILTER patterns (which exist to trigger decoding) still run — wasted work in no-decode mode; gating them off under --no-decode is a clean ~8s no-decode-mode win.
#187 REAL LEVER (default mode) = the extract-stage pattern cost: batch the base64-prefix set into ONE AC pass + close the homoglyph base-AC coverage gap so ASCII can skip variants. DEEP architectural, recall-risky, multi-cycle. NOT churnable in single edits — needs a dedicated scoped push with A/B benching per change.

### §SPEED — #187 ROOT CAUSE ISOLATED: discord-bot-token over-trigger (2026-07-07)
detectors/discord-bot-token.toml has 90 base64-numeric-prefix keywords (MTA,MTE…OTk = base64 of digit-pairs 10-99). These 3-char literals appear constantly in ANY base64 blob → prefilter triggers ~10M times → each runs the full token-shape extract regex which fails (no `.<6-8>.<27-38>` structure). MEASURED VALUE vs COST: CredData = **0 findings** but the dominant share of the 87.7% extract / ~10M runs; mirror = 21 findings (must preserve). So on real corpora it is ~30% scan cost for ZERO yield, yet cannot be disabled (mirror + bare production tokens need it).
FIX (recall-SAFE, the highest-leverage single #187 change): a cheap per-match DOTTED-SHAPE PRE-GATE — after a base64-prefix trigger, memchr for `.` within ~21-33 bytes AND a second `.` after, BEFORE running the extract regex. Base64 blobs (no dots) skip extract; the 21 mirror tokens (which HAVE the dotted shape) still run the full regex → recall-identical. The existing suffix_gate.rs gates by required SUFFIX LITERAL and CANNOT express "2 dots at offsets" — so this needs a NEW per-pattern shape-gate type (hot-path code in engine/extract.rs or a pre-extract confirmed-pass gate), A/B benched per change (mirror 21 discord TP must stay; CredData wall-time must drop). status=SPECIFIED, ready for a dedicated hot-path push.

### §ARCH/CONFIG — per-detector entropy-threshold migration COMPLETED (2026-07-07) — user: "complete full migration"
The blocked per-detector entropy migration (documented by the orphan `entropy/gates.rs` stub + the stale PER-DETECTOR-MIGRATION-BLOCKED comment atop keywords.rs) is now DONE. Goal: entropy thresholds/gate floors resolve per-detector from `DetectorSpec` (Two-tier TOML config), not blanket module constants.
- DetectorSpec already carries all 6 override fields (spec.rs: entropy_low/high/very_high, mixed_alnum_floor, keyword_free_min_len, min_len — all Option).
- scanner.rs path: was ALREADY per-detector (min_len, keyword_free_min_len, entropy_low/high/very_high resolved via `spec.and_then(..).unwrap_or(CONST)`).
- NEW WIRING (this session): threaded the classified detector id through the extraction path — `extract_candidates` / `extract_candidates_with_rejections` / `_internal` take `detector_id: Option<&'static str>`, set on the `PlausibilityContext`; the 3 scanner.rs call sites derive it via `classify_keyword_to_detector_id(&context.keyword)`. `plausibility.rs::passes_secret_strength_checks` now resolves `entropy_high` + `mixed_alnum_floor` per-detector via `get_spec(context)` (was raw `HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD` / `MIXED_ALNUM_TOKEN_THRESHOLD`). This made the previously-DEAD `DETECTORS`/`get_spec` live and removed the dead `with_detector_id` builder (Law 11).
- Correctly LEFT as constants (documented, no detector to resolve OR fixed semantics): isolated.rs (isolated bare tokens = unattributed, no detector), ml_features.rs (model feature boundary fixed at training), confidence/ (global scoring policy), engine keyword_free_threshold (path-sensitivity pre-gate).
- Deleted the untracked orphan `entropy/gates.rs` + the stale blocked-migration comment. BEHAVIOR-PRESERVING: no detector TOML sets the override fields yet → all resolve to the module constants → identical recall/precision (verify: entropy tests + bench must be unchanged). A detector .toml can now tune its own entropy floors.
status=done pending build+bench green.

### §DETECT/#184 — per-detector FP attribution on CredData (2026-07-08, real matcher, no re-scan)
Corrected stale memory ("API 1335FP/1TP" is WRONG). Actual top FP drivers on CredData (mig scan, ci-lean/CPU):
- entropy-api-key 1347FP/760TP (medEnt 4.79) — HIGH-entropy FPs, only 151<4.0 → NOT threshold-tunable, needs ML/context. Dominant lever.
- entropy-token 1090FP/260TP (medEnt 4.62) — same, 13<4.0. Needs ML/context.
- generic-keyword-secret 242FP/994TP (medEnt 3.62, 230<4.0) — low-entropy FP cluster, floor-tunable IF TPs higher (verify TP entropy first).
- nats-credentials 179FP/115TP; postgresql-connection-string 77FP/39TP; kubernetes-bootstrap-token 73FP/14TP.
PURE-FP precision bugs (0 TP on CredData → safe to tighten, but must not regress real-world/mirror recall):
- **google-cloud-storage-hmac-key 78FP/0TP** — FIXED (2026-07-08): root cause was keyhog compiling every regex case_insensitive(true), so `GOOG[0-9A-Z]{37,40}` also matched lowercase → CamelCase `GoogleAnalyticsConnectorProfileCredentials` (case-folded GOOG keyword + 38 letters) false-fired. Fix = `(?-i)` on pattern 1 + scoped `(?-i:…)` on pattern 2's GOOG body (prefix stays CI for HMAC_ACCESS_KEY env recall), same guard as aws-access-key. VERIFIED: CredData fp 3720→3651 (-69), TP 5169 UNCHANGED, F1 0.4309→0.4322; mirror IDENTICAL. Contract positives (uppercase GOOG5I75…) still match. status=done
- **rabbitmq-management-credentials 41FP/23TP** — captures the bare word `password` as a credential. ROOT CAUSE (shared): `password` IS an exact placeholder marker (Tier-B TOML, pinned by placeholder_words.rs test const LEGACY_ENTROPY_EXACT: null/none/undefined/empty/default/secret/password), but `bytes_contain_entropy_placeholder_marker` suppression runs on the ENTROPY/generic path (phase2_generic_shape.rs) only — NAMED/vendor detectors (rabbitmq, etc.) bypass it, so a bare placeholder value slips through. FIX = a UNIVERSAL exact-placeholder-value gate applied to ALL findings (named+generic) pre-emit, not just the generic path. Affects every named detector that can capture `password`/`secret`/`default`/etc. class=shared-cause-suppression-bypass sev=high status=open
- **aws-secrets-manager-arn 33FP/0TP** — flags ARN identifiers (`arn:aws:secretsmanager:…:secret:…`), which are resource ids not secrets; CredData scores FP. class=precision-policy sev=med status=open-REScoped-2026-07-09: read the detector — it is ALREADY `severity = "info"` with `Verify: none` and header comments stating "an ARN is an identifier, not a credential", so "demote-to-informational" is a NO-OP (already done). The real question is a SCORING/POLICY one, not a regex fix: (A) the CredData bench counts severity=info identifier surfacing as a positive finding, so every ARN is an F1 "FP" — the principled fix is for the bench (and default `scan` findings gate) to treat `severity=info` identifier detectors as an inventory/informational lane, NOT a credential claim counted in precision; OR (B) gate the detector to fire only when a colocated secret VALUE is present (but an ARN standing alone is the common real case, so this mostly means dropping it from the default set). NEITHER is a safe unilateral edit: (A) is a bench-scoring + product decision (do users want ARN inventory in default scans?), (B) is recall-coupled and needs `bench analyze` A/B. Leaving as a policy decision for the owner; NOT a detector-regex bug.
Note: entropy-* FP dominance (2437 combined) confirms heuristic surfacing SATURATED — the real #184 lever is model-authoritative ML/context on the entropy path, not per-detector threshold tuning. The migration this session ENABLES per-detector entropy config but the big FP drivers are high-entropy (immune to floor bumps).

### §DETECT/#184 — universal exact-placeholder gate for named detectors (2026-07-08) — DONE
Fixed the shared-cause named-detector placeholder bypass (rabbitmq `password` FP class). New `placeholder_words::is_exact_entropy_placeholder(bytes)` (ONE owner, extracted from `bytes_contain_entropy_placeholder_marker` Category 5) wired as a Tier-A universal gate in `suppress_named_detector_finding_stage` (StageId::ShapeGate("exact_placeholder_value")): any NAMED-detector value that IS EXACTLY a Tier-B exact placeholder (null/none/undefined/empty/default/secret/password) is dropped — whole-value only, so `mysecretkey123` survives. Test: `suppression::api::tests::exact_placeholder_value_is_suppressed_for_named_detectors`.
VERIFIED: CredData fp 3651→3610 (-41), TP 5169 UNCHANGED; mirror IDENTICAL. LOGICALLY contract-safe: the gate fires ONLY when the WHOLE credential is exactly one of 7 placeholder words, and grep proved NO contract positive credential is any of them → cannot suppress a contract positive. Unit test passes. (Full contracts_runner in debug was pathologically slow/20min+; folding a release contract run into the #187 build.)
COMBINED this session (gcs-hmac + this): CredData fp 3720→3610 (-110), F1 0.4309→0.4329 (+0.0020), recall flat, mirror flat. Both pure precision wins with zero recall cost.

### §DETECT/#184 — entropy-floor tuning is DEAD for the low-entropy FP clusters (2026-07-08, negative result)
Analyzed TP-vs-FP entropy band distributions (rab_cd.json, real matcher):
- generic-keyword-secret 994TP/242FP: TP bands <3.0=492, 3.0-3.5=304, 3.5-4.0=119, >=4.5=55 → 915/994 TP are <4.0. FP bands 3.5-4.0=169, 3.0-3.5=42. TP & FP OVERLAP in the low bands; ANY floor bump kills 100s of real low-entropy keyword-anchored secrets. NOT entropy-tunable.
- nats-credentials 115TP/179FP: TP 3.5-4.0=71 vs FP 3.5-4.0=76 — same overlap. NOT entropy-tunable.
CONCLUSION: the remaining big CredData FP drivers (entropy-api-key/token high-entropy 2437FP + these low-entropy keyword clusters) are all INSEPARABLE in entropy space from real secrets. #184 precision past ~0.43 requires model-authoritative ML/context (the entropy MoE on the keyword+generic paths), NOT threshold tuning. The safe threshold/pattern wins are the pure-FP over-broad detectors (gcs-hmac DONE, aws-arn=info-by-design) + placeholder bypasses (rabbitmq DONE). Those are now drained; the residue is an ML task.

### §DETECT/#184 — more 0-TP over-broad detectors on CredData (2026-07-08)
Beyond gcs-hmac (fixed) + aws-arn (info-by-design): detectors with 0 TP and >=8 FP (safe to tighten, verify mirror flat):
- **ipinfo-api-token 12FP/0TP** — matches CamelCase identifiers `globalInfo`, `setAttributesOfItemAtPath` (keyword `info`/`ipinfo` case-folds into `...Info`, pattern captures the word). SAME case-insensitive bug class as gcs-hmac → `(?-i)` or require the real `ipinfo_` token shape. status=open
- **aws-codecommit-credentials 12FP/0TP** — pattern 2 (`git-codecommit\.…/v1/repos/…`, no capture) flags the PUBLIC clone URL as `critical`. BUT the contract `aws-codecommit-credentials.toml` DELIBERATELY asserts this URL as a positive (`credential = git-codecommit.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/repos/my-repo`) — so removing pattern 2 breaks a contract that encodes intent. Same class as aws-arn: is a public resource URL a 'finding'? Correctness argues NO (no secret material; CredData+betterleaks don't flag it) but it's a PRODUCT/POLICY decision (flag public identifiers as info-disclosure vs not) — NOT a unilateral mechanical fix. status=decision-pending(user)
- **putty-private-key 10FP/0TP** — captures PuTTY key headers of unencrypted EXAMPLE keys (`Encryption: none`, Comment php/dsa). Likely example/placeholder keys → example-marker suppression, not an over-broad regex per se. status=open (investigate)
- **huggingface-user-token 8FP/0TP** — `hf_` + word-like/Caesar-shifted bodies (`hf_tljqsfdpsetupif…`) — decode-artifact or word-like body; needs entropy/word-like gate on the hf_ body. status=open
Total safe residue ≈ 42 FP (ipinfo+codecommit+putty+hf). All 0-TP so zero CredData recall risk; each needs a mirror-flat check.

### §PERF/#187 — discord-bot-token base64-prefix over-trigger (2026-07-08, precise scoping)
CONFIRMED: discord-bot-token has 77 keywords = {discord, DISCORD} + 75 three-char base64 prefixes (MTA/MTE/…/OTk = base64 of Discord-snowflake leading digit-pairs 10–99). These 3-char strings occur constantly inside ANY base64 blob, so on base64-heavy corpora (CredData) they trigger the strict 3-dot token regex `(prefix)[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,25}\.[...]{6,8}\.[...]{27,38}` ~10M times → ~30% of scan cost for 0 findings (real tokens are rare + need the full 3-dot shape).
FIX (hot-path, needs focused perf work + recall proof): a cheap literal-dot pre-gate before the discord regex runs — a genuine discord token ALWAYS has exactly 2 `.`; a triggered window with <2 dots in range can NOT match, so `memchr(b'.', window)` skips it in O(1) vs running the regex. Generalizable: any detector whose primary regex requires a mandatory literal char can gate on a cheap byte-presence pre-scan. Preserves recall (real tokens have the dots). MUST bench: CredData scan-time (expect large drop) + recall flat (mirror + discord contract). Location: phase2 keyword-trigger → regex-eval path (engine/phase2*). status=open (substantial; do with criterion before/after, not a rushed edit)

### §PERF/#187 — discord dot pre-gate IMPLEMENTED (2026-07-08) — data-driven, recall-safe
Implemented the cheap literal pre-gate as a Tier-B detector field (no compiler/CompiledPattern plumbing — `extract_anchored` already holds `&DetectorSpec` via `self.detectors[entry.detector_index]`):
- `DetectorSpec.verify_required_bytes: Option<String>` (spec.rs) — mandatory literals that must appear within a window of a candidate for the anchored regex to match.
- `phase2_anchor_scan.rs::extract_anchored`: precompute the bytes once; per candidate position, `memchr` the `VERIFY_REQUIRED_WINDOW`(=256)-byte window and `continue` (skip the DFA verify) if any mandatory byte is absent. Module const `VERIFY_REQUIRED_WINDOW`.
- `discord-bot-token.toml`: `verify_required_bytes = "."`. All 3 discord patterns have 2 unconditional `\.`; max contract token = 61 « 256, dots within ~28 bytes of the anchor → RECALL-SAFE (absence of `.` proves no match). CredData has 0 discord findings so recall cannot change; mirror flat check + discord contract are the recall proofs.
REVERTED — DEAD END (2026-07-08, honest A/B). Same-machine A/B (min-of-3, window=128): pre-gate ON 62500ms vs OFF 60498ms → ON is 3.3% SLOWER (and OFF ran second = warmer cache, so the true delta is even less favorable). ROOT CAUSE the approach fails: the anchored DFA already FAILS FAST on dotless base64 (patterns 1+2 need the `.` after prefix+{20,25}≈28 chars, so the DFA aborts at ~28), while a memchr pre-gate scans the FULL window (must be >=75 for pattern-3's discord-anchored dot) per position — strictly MORE work than the fast-failing DFA. Recall was flawless (fp/tp/mirror identical) but there is no perf win to bank, so per Law 7/11 the added hot-loop branch + Tier-B field is net-negative and reverted (spec field, phase2_anchor_scan pre-gate, discord TOML line, regression_discord_dot_pregate.rs all removed). The memory's "discord 30% / 10M runs" is NOT the DFA-verify cost in ci-lean/CPU — it's the AC keyword-TRIGGER (scanning base64 for the 75 prefix literals) + candidate collection. The real #187 lever is reducing those triggers, which is recall-risky (the prefixes catch BARE discord tokens with no `discord` keyword). LESSON: a memchr pre-gate only wins when the required byte is EARLIER than where the DFA fast-fails; here it isn't. status=reverted-deadend

### §DETECT/#184-adjacent — case-fold over-broad bug CLASS: 22 detectors (2026-07-08, INTROSPECTION/shared-cause)
gcs-hmac's root bug (uppercase-only `[0-9A-Z]{n}` class compiled case_insensitive(true) → also matches lowercase, so CamelCase words false-fire) is a CLASS. Hunt found 22 detectors with an uppercase-only `[A-Z0-9]{>=12}` class and NO `(?-i)`:
4everland-api-token, aws-govcloud-access-key, aws-ses-smtp-credentials, ceph-rados-gateway-credentials, digitalocean-spaces-credentials, dynatrace-api-token, filebase-api-key, huawei-cloud-api-credentials, linode-object-storage-access-key, minio-presigned-credentials, newrelic-user-api-key, octopus-deploy-api-key, scaleway-api-key, slack-config-token, storj-api-credentials, vultr-api-key, wasabi-access-key (+ gcs-hmac FIXED).
FIX = scope `(?-i:…)` to the uppercase-only class (real keys ARE uppercase — VERIFIED for wasabi `D1R7E3FCOQ5SBACMK1F2`, linode `KPQXRMSNTBVWYZ4Q7M28`, newrelic `NRAK-2EYOINJ…`, digitalocean `7DUURP7HR…`; keep the surrounding keyword prefix case-insensitive for env-var recall). PER-DETECTOR VERIFICATION REQUIRED before each edit: confirm the contract positive credential is uppercase-only (mixed-case key ⇒ the class is a DIFFERENT bug, widen to `[A-Za-z0-9]` instead of adding `(?-i)`). CAUTION: some detectors (scaleway) ALSO have a UUID/mixed pattern — scope only the uppercase-key pattern. NOTE: none of these 22 fired FPs on CredData (they're keyword-anchored, unlike gcs-hmac's common-word `GOOG`→`Google` fold) → this is real-world/other-corpus precision-hardening, NOT a CredData #184 lever. Batch-fix + per-detector contract recall check. status=open

### §ONE-PLACE/#185 — canonical detector_spec_by_id: 3 duplicate id→spec maps → 1 (2026-07-08, DONE)
Found 3 independent `LazyLock<HashMap<String, DetectorSpec>>` statics — entropy/scanner.rs `DETECTORS`, entropy/plausibility.rs `DETECTORS`, adjudicate/mod.rs `DETECTOR_SPECS` — each calling `load_embedded_detectors_or_fail()` and rebuilding an IDENTICAL id→spec map. That parsed the whole compiled-in corpus 3x at startup (Law 7) and put the same lookup in 3 places (ONE-PLACE violation). FIX: one canonical `keyhog_core::detector_spec_by_id(id) -> Option<&'static DetectorSpec>` (lib.rs, next to the loader, ONE fail-closed LazyLock), all 3 consumers repointed (scanner get_spec, plausibility get_spec, adjudicate's 3 call-sites min_confidence/entropy-floor/min_len). Removed now-dead imports (scanner LazyLock; adjudicate HashMap+LazyLock+DetectorSpec). Behavior-IDENTICAL by construction (same `.map(|s|(s.id.clone(),s)).collect()`, same by-id lookup). Compiles clean (no errors/unused), all 4 files fmt-clean. Validating via ci-lean lib tests. status=done-pending-test

### §182-note — stripe hot-confirmed fast-path is per-detector code (gray-area, LOW priority) (2026-07-08)
`compile.rs:375` + `confirmed_extract.rs:265` special-case `== STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` for a checksum-confirmed "hot direct-emit" optimization (prefixes already in detector_classification::stripe_hot_confirmed_prefixes). This is a per-detector ALGORITHM/optimization, not a hardcoded detection VALUE (thresholds/patterns/floors — those the migration moved to TOML), so NOT a clear DET-0 violation. Generalizing to a `checksum_confirmed` DetectorSpec flag is only worth it IF another detector becomes hot-confirmable (currently stripe-only → YAGNI). Verified via audit: no `detector_id == "literal"` special-casing anywhere in scanner logic; bundled_detector_ids + id→spec map are single-owner. status=open-lowpri

## §GPU-VYRE — megakernel levers L1–L7 (maps to F2 parity + F3 perf; several live in vyre @ libs/performance/matching/vyre — fix there, publish, flow back; probe on RTX 5090/4090, Law 8)
- [ ] **L1 Positions everywhere, not presence.** Main dispatch returns per-chunk PRESENCE bits only; CPU tail re-walks admitted chunks with full regexes. Positioned candidates exist for only 2 literal families. Validation-window machinery ALREADY built (`validation_window_range` + `ac_match_upper_bounds` + `validate_detector_match` in gpu_region_batch.rs). Extend positioned output to ALL detector trigger literals so phase 2 validates small windows instead of whole chunks. Attacks the dominant cost.
- [ ] **L2 vyre literal-set under-fire bug (PREREQUISITE; lives in vyre).** GPU region-presence under-fires; `(chunk,detector)` pairs recovered only by `gpu_recall_floor` (gpu_region_dispatch.rs:318-322). Caps how many buckets GPU can win (autoroute requires proven parity). NARROWED: presence-write + hit→region mapping in vyre-libs/src/scan/classic_ac/bounded_ranges/prefilter/suffix3.rs (`*_presence_by_region_program_ext` ABI ~L421-481). RULED OUT: suffix1/2/3 prefilters (over-approximate only) AND the region-attribution binary search (rounds correctly). Under-fire is in the DFA-walk replay emit or the byte-class mask BUILD — needs a GPU differential (`literal_set_presence_and_positions_gpu.rs` vs `_reference.rs`) on a reproducing literal set; capture a failing (literal-set, haystack) case first. Dedicated RTX session.
- [ ] **L3 Phase-2 GPU DFA coverage + persistence.** Admission catalog covers ≤64 of ~2,700 always-active patterns (`PHASE2_GPU_DFA_MAX_SHARDS = 4×16`, 16,384-state cap), lazily compiled ON the scan path. Compile at install/calibration time instead (persist via gpu_cache.rs alongside autoroute decisions); cap can then rise on big-VRAM hosts. Each covered pattern turns a GPU miss into a trusted skip of CPU no-hit admission work. NOTE (2026-07-07 scoping): NOT a constant-bump — see §SPEED "GPU step-2 coverage limit SCOPED".
- [ ] **L4 Host-side batch build.** `build_region_presence_batch` copies + ASCII-lowercases the ENTIRE corpus single-threaded, then zeroes on drop — extra full pass + second resident corpus copy (likely much of the 3.5 GB vs 1.8 GB RSS gap). Fix: rayon-parallelize the fold, OR (better, vyre lever) case-insensitive literal matching IN the kernel so the host fold disappears.
- [ ] **L5 Pipelining.** Route is one synchronous mega-sequence (coalesce → presence → positioned → DFA → readback → CPU tail). Sub-batch with overlapped upload/compute/readback. Related: positioned-literal overflow is a serial halving loop against a fixed match buffer; a device-side match-count prepass or dynamically sized buffers (vyre API) removes the retry ladder. (Buffer since raised to 1<<20 — see §SPEED GPU >8MB fix.)
- [x] **L6 The 4 GiB cliff — DONE.** `scan_coalesced_gpu_region_presence` splits chunks into region-boundary sub-batches ≤ `REGION_PRESENCE_BATCH_BYTE_LIMIT` (one owner, gpu_region_batch.rs) instead of degrading to CPU. Recall-identical by construction; testable `subbatch_ranges_by_len` + 4 pins.
- [ ] **L7 MegaScan is a label, not an engine.** `--backend mega-scan` collapses onto region presence (rule_pipeline.rs regex-NFA route removed); only VRAM-adaptive input sizing survives. If vyre's megakernel/segmentation API can fuse literal presence + positioned matches + DFA admission into ONE launch, that replaces today's 3 dispatch families (~50–300 µs fixed cost each).

### §184-HOMEFIELD — full characterization of betterleaks's home corpus (2026-07-08, offline from bpe_homefield-betterleaks.json Jul7)
Homefield ("homefield-betterleaks") is betterleaks's OWN modern AI/API-key corpus (anthropic/openai/cursor/deepseek/mistral/vercel/xai/etc). keyhog standing: **F1 0.4935 (P 0.4957 R 0.4914), fp=58 fn=59 tp=57** — a BALANCED gap (both axes ~equal). This is the least-explored #184 bench and the roadmap below is per-category (robust to the in-flight scanner-src refactor — all levers are detector-TOML/gate, not engine).

**PRECISION (fp>0, tp=0 — checksum-less named PREFIX detectors matching EXAMPLE keys):**
- gcp-api-key (=google-api-key detector) 16 FP/0 TP — `AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}`, no checksum → matches the contract's own `AIza9X3kQp7…`-style examples. TOP precision lever.
- huggingface(-access-token) 6 FP/0 TP (`hf_[a-zA-Z0-9]{34,}`), vercel-* 8 FP/0 TP (integration/personal/app-access/app-refresh 2 each), xai 2/0, flyio 2/0, weights-and-biases-v1 2/0, posthog-personal/project 3/0, mistral/nvidia/ollama/replicate/stability-ai/togetherai/vercel-ai-gateway 1 each.
- ROOT CLASS = SAME as the rabbitmq placeholder bypass I fixed: NAMED detectors bypass example/context suppression. FIX = extend named-detector suppression to catch doc/example/well-formed-but-fake keys (needs the FP VALUES from a fresh homefield scan to design precise gates; checksum-less prefix detectors fundamentally can't self-distinguish real vs example).

**RECALL (fn>0, tp=0 — real secrets keyhog MISSES):**
- MISSING detectors (clean ADD): sumologic-access-token (6 FN — biggest single recall lever), etsy-access-token (3), cisco-meraki-api-key (1).
- COVERAGE gaps (detector exists, wrong/old format): planetscale-id (2 FN; have planetscale-api-token-v2/-service-token not the -id form), databricks-api-token (1 FN; have databricks-token).
- github-* (app-token 2, fine-grained-pat 1, oauth 1, pat 1, refresh-token 1 = 6 FN) + gitlab-* (pat, pat-routable, ptt, runner-auth-routable = 4 FN) + slack-legacy-* (bot/token/workspace = 5 FN) + slack-config-access-token (3) + slack-user-token (2) + curl-auth-user (2): keyhog HAS these core detectors → HYPOTHESIS: homefield uses FABRICATED tokens with invalid checksums, and keyhog's (correct) checksum validation REJECTS them → FN, while betterleaks likely doesn't validate → counts them. If true, this is a bench-vs-real TRADEOFF (checksum validation is correct for real leaks, costs recall on fabricated-token benches). CONFIRM by checking if the homefield github/gitlab/slack FNs are checksum-invalid (needs the FN values from a scan). If confirmed, options: (a) accept as correct, (b) a bench-mode that reports checksum-invalid-but-shaped as low-confidence.

NEXT (needs a stable tree/build — currently blocked by the parallel refactor): fresh homefield scan with per-finding values → confirm the example-FP + fabricated-checksum-FN hypotheses → add sumologic/etsy/meraki detectors + planetscale-id/databricks-api-token forms + named-detector example suppression. status=open-analyzed

### §184-CREDDATA + UNIFIED 3-bench diagnosis (2026-07-08, offline from bpe_*.json Jul7)
CredData: F1 0.4309 (P 0.5815 R 0.3423), tp=5169 fp=3720 **fn=9932** (recall-DOMINATED). Top levers (FP and FN in the SAME generic categories = inseparable by shape):
- Key: fp=616 **fn=3727** tp=75 (recall 0.02) — #1 lever by far.
- UUID: fp=495 **fn=2289** tp=5 (recall 0.00) — same shape is real secret 2289x AND non-secret 495x.
- Password fp=523 fn=1288 tp=1141 (0.47); Secret fp=590 fn=788 (0.37); Token fp=118 fn=284 (0.51).
- Pure recall misses: AWS-S3-Bucket 92 FN/0 TP (missed entirely), JWK recall 0.01, AWS-Client-ID 108 FN, Token:UUID 92, Salt 75, Nonce 74.
CONFIRMS memory: generic path is the drag; heuristic surfacing SATURATED; Key/UUID/Password/Secret recall needs model-authoritative ML+CONTEXT (a model that says "this UUID on THIS line IS a secret" — shape can't, proven by FP=FN coexistence).

**UNIFIED: the 3 benches need DIFFERENT levers (strategic ranking for #184):**
1. HOMEFIELD (F1 0.4935) = MOST TRACTABLE WITHOUT ML. Dominated by (a) MISSING modern-service detectors (sumologic 6FN, etsy 3, cisco-meraki 1; planetscale-id/databricks-api-token FORM gaps) and (b) named checksum-less prefix detectors matching EXAMPLE keys (gcp 16FP, hf 6, vercel 8, xai 2 — all 0 TP). Both are DATA/GATE fixes (add TOMLs + extend named-detector example suppression, same class as the rabbitmq bypass fix). Fabricated-checksum-FN hypothesis explains github/gitlab/slack misses.
2. MIRROR (F1 0.821) = MoE PRECISION on canonical shapes. 975 FP = 100% canonical high-entropy non-secrets (lorem 483, uuid 256, base64-protobuf 64, sha1 60, k8s-uid 59, git-sha 51) that the ML recall-LIFT releases and the MoE fails to reject. Model quality.
3. CREDDATA (F1 0.4309) = MoE RECALL+context on generic Key/UUID/Password/Secret. Model quality + context. Biggest+hardest.
=> Fastest "beat betterleaks on MORE benches": drive HOMEFIELD with detector coverage + example suppression (no ML); CredData+mirror need the ML/MoE track. status=open-analyzed

### §185/migration-elegance — resolution pattern inlined 16× (2026-07-08, self-audit)
Audit of the per-detector entropy migration's elegance: docs EXCELLENT (spec.rs per-field + architecture-law block 112-120), one-owner consts CLEAN (entropy/mod.rs, no production literal re-def), detector_spec_by_id unification EXEMPLARY (borrows the one embedded_detector_specs Vec). ONE GAP: `spec.and_then(|s| s.entropy_X).unwrap_or(DEFAULT_X)` is inlined 16× across entropy/plausibility.rs, entropy/scanner.rs, adjudicate/mod.rs, engine/phase2_generic_shape.rs — the field↔default PAIRING is duplicated (latent mispairing risk: nothing stops a site pairing entropy_high with LOW_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD). FIX = 5 resolver helpers in entropy/mod.rs (resolve_entropy_high/_low/_very_high/_mixed_alnum/_keyword_free_min_len), each pairing the field with its correct default ONCE; repoint all 16 sites. Blocked on collision: all 4 target files are in the user's active parallel refactor. status=open-blocked-on-user-tree

### §185-AUDIT — comprehensive scanner elegance/doc audit (2026-07-08, read-only, answers "are they ALL elegant?")
Evidence-based, whole-scanner (not just my refactors). HONEST verdict: my session's refactors are ~90% clean; the BROADER scanner carries real elegance/doc debt:
1. RESOLUTION INLINING (my migration + pre-existing): 17 sites, 7 fields — `.and_then(|s| s.FIELD).unwrap_or(DEFAULT)` for min_len (×5), entropy_high (×4), keyword_free_min_len (×2), entropy_very_high (×2), entropy_low (×2), mixed_alnum_floor (×1), min_confidence (×1). FIX = ~7 resolver helpers pairing each field↔default ONCE. (Blocked: files in user's active refactor.)
2. entropy_floors.rs family_floor HARDCODES per-family entropy floors INLINE in a match: `id==GENERIC_API_KEY => 3.5`, `GENERIC_API_KEY && len<=40 => 2.8`, `GENERIC_SECRET && len<=24 => 2.8`, etc. — DET-0/#182 smell (migration moved the fixed thresholds to TOML but NOT these length-conditional generic-family floors, which duplicate DEFAULT_FLOOR=3.5's value inline). FIX = per-family floor table in Tier-B TOML, or at minimum reference DEFAULT_FLOOR not a bare 3.5.
3. DOCS: scanner crate does NOT enforce `#![warn(missing_docs)]` (core DOES). 158 of 406 truly-`pub` (public-API) fns (38%) have NO doc comment. FIX = add the lint to scanner lib.rs + document the public surface (large, incremental). => answer to "everything documented?" is NO for the scanner public API.
CAVEAT (audit honesty): NOT every repeated float is a violation — `5.1`/`8.1` etc. are FORMAT specifiers (`{pct:>5.1}`), many are test literals or legitimately-varying calibration coefficients. A magic-number sweep needs per-value triage, not blanket naming.
status=open (this IS the #185 scope, now evidence-scoped)

### §185-AUDIT CORRECTION (2026-07-08, after investigation)
Finding #1 (resolution inlining ×17) is DOWNGRADED after reading the actual defaults: the sites do NOT share one (field,default) pairing — `entropy_high` resolves to HIGH_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD in 3 sites but to DEFAULT_GENERIC_ENTROPY_THRESHOLD at adjudicate:279 (INTENTIONAL+DOCUMENTED, adjudicate:245-246: tied to ScanConfig::default().entropy_threshold to prevent drift — a PRIOR one-place fix), and `entropy_very_high` to a runtime `keyword_free_threshold` param at scanner:202. So a blanket helper-per-field would be WRONG (erases the module-const vs config-default distinction). Only ~7 constant-pairing sites (entropy_low→LOW ×2, keyword_free_min_len→KEYWORD_FREE_MIN_LEN ×2, entropy_high→HIGH ×3) are OPTIONALLY consolidatable — a minor readability nit, NOT a ONE-PLACE violation (each fallback is explicit+readable at its site). VERDICT: the migration's resolution is largely SOUND. Real remaining #185 debt is only: (2) entropy_floors family_floor length-conditional inline table (data-drive candidate, needs decision) + (3) scanner 38% undoc public API + no missing_docs lint. status=corrected

### §185-AUDIT FINAL VERDICT (2026-07-08, all 3 flags investigated)
All 3 mechanical-audit "debt" flags DISSOLVED or shrank on investigation — the codebase's ONE-PLACE discipline is genuinely STRONG:
1. Resolution inlining ×17 → NOT a violation: defaults legitimately vary by context; the one apparent divergence (entropy_high→DEFAULT_GENERIC at adjudicate) is INTENTIONAL+DOCUMENTED (config-tied to prevent drift, a prior one-place fix).
2. entropy_floors family-floor hardcoding → NOT a gap: already Tier-B-migrated (EntropyFloorTable::from_specs loads from detector TOMLs); the hardcoded match is `legacy_base_floor`, a DOCUMENTED parity-TEST proving the migration changed no floor.
3. "38% undoc public API" → INFLATED: of 146, 103 are testing.rs scaffolding + 12 are trait-impls (inherit docs). REAL gap = ~43 non-test public fns (hw_probe 15, engine 11, decode 6, scanner_config 5, telemetry 4, gpu 2) — modest, incremental, mostly internal-visibility helpers.
METHODOLOGY LESSON: grep-based elegance audits produce false positives (can't tell intentional-context-variance / legacy-parity-tests / test-scaffolding from real violations). Each flag MUST be investigated before it's a task. NET: this session's refactors (entropy migration, detector_spec_by_id unification) are elegant+documented; the codebase already uses config-tied consts, Tier-B migration+parity tests, and delegating test facades. Only genuine remaining #185 item: doc-pass on ~43 internal pub fns + optionally add scanner `#![warn(missing_docs)]` (both in user's active scanner-src, deferred to avoid collision). status=RESOLVED-mostly-clean

### FINDING ROOT-CAUSED (2026-07-08) — scan panic = ML INPUT_DIM 42->43, weights.bin not retrained
EXACT diagnosis (read-only, no edits):
- weights.bin: COMMITTED/unmodified, 47136 bytes = 11784 f32. Solving TOTAL_F32_COUNT(INPUT_DIM) = 198*INPUT_DIM + 3468 (= EXPERTS_OFF + EXPERT_COUNT*EXPERT_PARAM_COUNT with EXPERT_COUNT=6, FC 32/16/1) => 11784 <=> INPUT_DIM=42. So weights.bin was trained/exported at INPUT_DIM=42.
- model_arch.rs: NEW untracked file, INPUT_DIM=43 => TOTAL_F32_COUNT = 264 + 6*1953 = 11982. Delta 11982-11784 = 198 = one input feature (192 expert fan-in over 6 experts *32 fc1 + 6 gate).
- ROOT CAUSE: user's active ML refactor ADDED a 43rd input feature (ml_features.rs modified; model_arch.rs/service_vocab.rs new; ml_features_bigram_stats/ml_features_extraction tests in flight) but weights.bin was NOT retrained/re-exported at 43 features.
- ONLY correct fix: retrain the 6-expert MoE with the 43-feature input and re-embed weights.bin (11982 f32) via the ml/ training+export pipeline. Reverting INPUT_DIM->42 would undo the intended feature; fabricating an 11982-length buffer would be a garbage model. => NOT mechanically fixable; owned by the user's ML-feature work. I did not touch ml_weights.rs/model_arch.rs/weights.bin (active WIP + needs retraining, not a code edit).
- UNBLOCKS: all e2e detection #177/#184 (scan path returns) the moment weights.bin is retrained at 43 features.
status=root-caused-awaiting-weights-retrain

### UNBLOCK (2026-07-08) — deep detection lane REACHABLE without the `ml` feature
`ml = []` is a SEPARABLE feature; every ml_scorer/ml_weights call site is `#[cfg(feature="ml")]`. Building the scanner WITHOUT `ml` (features = entropy,decode,multiline,simdsieve,simd) never invokes the broken weights.bin OnceLock → the scan path WORKS. Confirmed: a compile→scan of `AKIAQYLPMN5HFIQR7BBB` recovers it exactly; prose yields nothing; CPU==SIMD parity holds; a PEM key body is recovered. So END-TO-END detection-truth #177/#184 IS testable NOW for named/structural/entropy detectors (the bulk of detection) — the ML re-scoring layer (a FP-refinement on top) is the only part gated by the broken weights. Tests run under the no-ml feature set; they also hold under `ml` once weights.bin is retrained at 43 features. This reopens the deep #177 lane. status=lane-open

### FINDING (2026-07-08) — GitHub token checksum validation may be inconsistent across prefixes
Observed via detection diagnostics: a FABRICATED ghp_ token (invalid CRC32 checksum) is correctly REJECTED, but fabricated gho_/ghr_/ghs_ tokens FIRE. GitHub uses the same base62-CRC32 checksum scheme for ghp_/gho_/ghr_/ghs_/ghu_. If the ghp_ detector checksum-validates but gho_/ghr_/ghs_ do not, that's a precision inconsistency (fabricated gho_/ghr_/ghs_ → FP) and a ONE-PLACE violation (checksum predicate applied to one prefix, not its siblings). VERIFY: grep the github detector spec(s) for the checksum validator wiring across prefixes; unify so all GitHub token prefixes share the same checksum gate (or confirm a deliberate per-prefix policy). DONE (verified 2026-07-08): fixed this session at the CHECKSUM layer (not the detector TOML) — GITHUB_OAUTH_FAMILY_PREFIXES=[gho_,ghu_,ghs_,ghr_] (checksum/prefixes.rs:33) now chain into the SAME classic base62-CRC32 gate as ghp_ (github.rs:78), so fabricated gho_/ghr_/ghs_/ghu_ are now REJECTED exactly like fabricated ghp_. Locked by github_oauth_family_shares_classic_checksum + checksum_prefixes_are_backed_by_their_detector (unit/checksum.rs). file=crates/scanner/src/checksum/{github.rs,prefixes.rs} | class=precision/one-place | severity=MED | status=DONE

### FINDINGS (2026-07-08) — detection edge-case behaviors (via e2e diagnostics, no-ml)
1. LONG-LINE RECALL GAP: an AWS key buried in a 1020-char line ("x"*500 + AKIA... + "y"*500) is NOT detected (scan returns empty) even though the AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} substring is intact. Secrets in very long lines / minified-bundle lines may be silently missed. class=recall | severity=HIGH | verify: is there a max-line/token-length gate that skips long runs? if so it's a recall hole for minified JS/bundles. status=RESOLVED (2026-07-09): the DELIMITED mid-line case (filler·500 + secret + filler·500, the reported shape) is now GATED green by line_length_runner::credential_sufficient_secrets_survive_embedded_mid_line (699/699). The literal NO-SPACE abutting-alnum case (xxxAKIA<16>xxx) is CORRECT word-boundary non-match (a 20-char alnum substring inside a longer token is not a key), not a recall bug
2. NO-BOUNDARY CAPTURE POLLUTION: "prefixAKIAQYLPMN5HFIQR7BBBsuffix" is detected but the reported credential is the WHOLE run "prefix...suffix", not the clean 20-char key. Hurts precision + dedup (same key + different surroundings → different reported values). class=precision/capture | severity=MED | verify capture-group vs token-span extraction. status=open
3. NESTED-ENCODING: double-base64 (base64(base64(AKIA))) is NOT recovered — decode is single-level. class=recall(evasion) | severity=LOW | likely intentional depth bound; confirm + document the decode-depth policy. status=open
(All 3 found by the no-ml e2e detection loop; not edited — detection logic is user WIP.)

### FINDING (2026-07-08) — finding offset is in normalized-space, not raw-input space (unicode)
Corpus finding-invariants sweep: for a unicode-whitespace-heavy input, detector 123formbuilder-api-key reported location.offset=42, which points into the UNICODE-NORMALIZED text, not the raw input where the clean credential actually sits (near the end). unicode_hardening normalizes/strips whitespace+homoglyphs before matching and offsets are relative to the normalized buffer. For a file scanner, an offset that doesn't map back to the ORIGINAL byte position misreports the leak site in unicode-heavy files (jump-to-location lands wrong). class=reporter/location-accuracy | severity=MED | triage: does keyhog map offsets back to raw space, or is normalized-space the documented contract? If normalized-space, that's a location-accuracy bug for real files. ASCII inputs are unaffected (no normalization). status=open

The test SURFACED 2 real things: (1) FINDING offset-in-normalized-space (recorded), (2) the complete Severity domain has ClientSafe (publishable keys) — my initial 5-set was incomplete. SESSION: 126 test fns / 21 files; two corpus-wide backbone tests now guard 866 (fire) + 829 (well-formed) of 923 detectors; 9 findings logged. status=done

### FINDING (2026-07-08) — CPU vs SIMD backend DIVERGENCE on unicode-normalized inputs
Corpus per-detector backend-parity sweep: CpuFallback and SimdCpu produce DIFFERENT findings for the same input on 4 detectors, ALL ascii=false (unicode whitespace/control/homoglyph heavy). Cases: elasticsearch-basic-auth (CPU→entropy-token, SIMD→elasticsearch-basic-auth: attribution differs), mongodb-connection-string & ssh2-private-key (SIMD fires, CPU MISSES: recall differs), splunk-victorops-api-key (CPU→nothing, SIMD→powerschool-api-credentials: mis-attribution). Clean-ASCII inputs: ALL 862+ detectors agree (parity holds). So the split is in the unicode-hardening/normalization path being applied differently per backend → recall+attribution depend on which backend runs, for unicode-heavy files. class=backend-parity/recall | severity=MED-HIGH (recall must not depend on backend; memory: SIMD-trigger-union is recall-load-bearing) | test: detector_corpus_backend_parity asserts strict ASCII parity + counts/surfaces unicode divergences. status=open

THREE corpus-wide backbone tests now guard all 923 detectors across 3 axes: (1) fire-rate 866/920, (2) finding well-formedness 907/829, (3) backend parity 879. ### FINDING (2026-07-08) — 3 specific detectors silent on valid tokens → generic mis-attribution
Triage of the 54 corpus-ratchet non-firing detectors: 41 ungeneratable (proptest subset limit, not bugs), 6 unicode-normalization artifacts, 1 correct checksum-suppress (github-pat-fine-grained), 1 context-dependent-but-works (asana-pat fires with keyword ctx), 2 npm probe-quirk. REAL CANDIDATES (3): telegram-bot-token (rx `[0-9]{8,10}:[A-Za-z0-9_-]{35}`, ex `36969501:Y3v_-X128qWyqrf_g__n_s-O--j-_m6-2GY`), twilio-api-key (rx `SK[a-f0-9]{32}`, ex `SKf77dea48db85fef690ffcbfc3fc3a4e6`), sanity-api-token (rx `sk[a-zA-Z0-9]{8,16}[a-f0-9]{24}`) — each matches its OWN regex but the SPECIFIC detector never fires (bare OR with keyword ctx, and on BOTH CpuFallback+SimdCpu). The value IS caught, but only by generic-secret/entropy-* → WRONG service attribution (a twilio key reported as generic-secret). class=recall/attribution | severity=MED-HIGH | root-cause candidates: prefilter/AC-trigger drops the short 2-char literal (SK/sk) before regex eval; OR dedup lets generic supersede specific; OR compiled regex adds a boundary the spec regex lacks. Triage each in the detector's compiled trigger + dedup path (user WIP). status=open

### CORRECTION (2026-07-08) — of the 3 candidate gaps, 2 CONFIRMED + 1 false alarm
Encoded as detection_gaps_documented.rs and reproduced: telegram-bot-token + twilio-api-key are CONFIRMED silent (empty result — specific detector never fires on its own valid token; #[ignore] regression tests that flip live on fix). sanity-api-token is NOT a gap — it fires with the correct `sanity_token =` keyword (earlier probe used a mismatched keyword); kept as a LIVE green guard. Net: 2 confirmed recall/attribution gaps (telegram-bot-token, twilio-api-key), root-cause candidates = short 2-char literal (SK/[0-9]:) prefilter/AC-trigger miss OR dedup. status=telegram+twilio open, sanity closed

### §185 HUNT (2026-07-08) — hot-path ONE-PLACE hygiene verified clean (no violation)

No ONE-PLACE violation in these hot paths; predicates (looks_like_*) are each distinct logic. status=verified-clean (this slice)

### §185 HUNT v2 (2026-07-08) — same-named consts triaged: ZERO divergent-value duplicates
All 5 same-named consts are NOT violations: DEFAULT_ENDPOINT (gitlab.com vs api.bitbucket.org — semantically-distinct module-local defaults for different sources, not a shared contract); MAX_CHARCLASS_PREFIX_EXPANSION / MAX_RESIDENT_FINDINGS / MIN_CAESAR_LEN / MIN_DISTINCTIVE_INFIX_CHARS all = ONE canonical owner + a testing.rs re-export alias (`= crate::…::X` / `= super::X`), the correct single-source-of-truth pattern. No two-definitions-different-values latent bug. §185 hot-path + const hygiene is clean in the scanner/core/sources/cli slices searched; residual §185 work (if any) lives in the per-detector .toml layer that §182 (user WIP) is actively migrating. status=verified-clean

### CORRECTION (2026-07-08) — my "detector recall bugs" were partly a BACKEND/test-methodology error
Re-verified all 4 candidate bugs against CpuFallback + SimdCpu. RESULTS:
- mongodb-connection-string: FIRES on SimdCpu (mongodb-connection-string + generic-password) but NOT on CpuFallback (generic-password only) for the SAME plain ASCII URI `mongodb://admin:pw@cluster0.example.com:27017/db`. => NOT a detector bug; a REAL CpuFallback-vs-SimdCpu recall DIVERGENCE on ASCII. This ALSO exposes a GAP in my detector_corpus_backend_parity test: it asserted CPU==SIMD on all ASCII and passed, because its regex-generated examples never covered this real-URI case. The parity test needs real-corpus cases, not only generated ones.
- twilio-api-key: has `required=true` companion (TWILIO_API_SECRET within 3 lines) + a spec comment noting SK is 2 chars (below the 4-char AC-prefilter floor) so it relies on keyword triggers. Silent even WITH companion+keyword on BOTH Cpu and Simd. => DOWNGRADE from "confirmed bug"; it is precision-by-design + a possible keyword-trigger wiring issue, needs deeper triage, NOT a simple recall miss. My original bare-SK test was wrong (missing required companion).
- telegram-bot-token, jwt-token: silent on BOTH Cpu and Simd even with keyword/valid shape (jwt has a clean `eyJ` literal anchor + matches its regex `eyJ...\.eyJ...\.` yet fires on neither). => remain real CANDIDATES but backend-independent; need triage against the default/auto backend + the ml pipeline.
- MegaScan backend: ABORTS (process exit 12) under `--features entropy,decode,multiline,simdsieve,simd` (no gpu) — likely requires gpu feature; a backend that panics instead of failing-closed is a robustness finding.
METHODOLOGY FIX: my ~1000 detection tests use ScanBackend::CpuFallback — the WEAKER fallback path that skips the HS trigger union (~49 no-literal detectors fire only via HS). CpuFallback is fine for detectors that DO fire on it (the 866 per-detector set self-selected those), but detection-TRUTH assertions should prefer SimdCpu/auto to avoid understating recall. Re-basing candidate.
NET: 2 clean confirmed items remain (entropy-token semver FP; offset-in-normalized-space); 1 reclassified as backend divergence (mongodb); 1 downgraded (twilio); 2 still-candidate (telegram, jwt). Integrity: over-claimed "6 confirmed bugs" -> corrected to 2 confirmed + 1 backend-divergence + 3 candidates.
status=corrected

### FINDING (2026-07-08, root-caused) — CpuFallback AC-literal extraction mishandles optional group in mongodb regex
mongodb-connection-string regex is `mongodb(?:\+srv)?://...`. On CpuFallback: fires on `mongodb+srv://...` but NOT plain `mongodb://...`. On SimdCpu (Hyperscan): fires on BOTH. postgres/mysql/redis connection-strings (plain `scheme://` literal, no optional infix) fire fine on CpuFallback. => ROOT CAUSE: CpuFallback's AC-literal prefilter derives a trigger literal from `mongodb(?:\+srv)?://` that effectively requires the `+srv` infix (the optional group is folded into the literal as present), so plain `mongodb://` never triggers the regex on the literal path. SimdCpu runs the full HS regex so catches both. This is a SILENT-FALLBACK recall loss (Law 10): a user on the CpuFallback path silently misses every plain mongodb:// connection string. FIX options: (a) fix the literal extractor to emit BOTH `mongodb://` and `mongodb+srv://` for an optional-infix group; (b) split the detector into two patterns (`mongodb://…` and `mongodb\+srv://…`) so each yields a clean literal. Test: detection_gaps_documented::cpufallback_matches_simd_on_mongodb_uri (#[ignore], reproduces). Likely affects OTHER regexes with an optional infix before the anchor literal — audit `\?`/`(?:…)?` before `://` or key separators. status=open-actionable

### REFINEMENT (2026-07-08) — mongodb CpuFallback divergence mechanism is NOT literal-extraction
Correcting the prior root-cause over-claim: mongodb-connection-string has keywords=["mongodb","mongo"], and the input contains "mongodb", so the KEYWORD trigger should engage the detector on BOTH backends regardless of any AC-literal. Yet CpuFallback still doesn't fire on plain mongodb:// while SimdCpu does. => the divergence is in REGEX EXECUTION, not triggering: the CpuFallback CPU-regex path and the SimdCpu Hyperscan path disagree on whether `mongodb(?:\+srv)?://[^:@\s"']+:[^@\s"']+@host\.host` matches a plain `mongodb://...` URI (both MUST, since the optional group matches empty). Candidate mechanisms (need scanner-internals): (1) CpuFallback builds a DFA/regex form that mis-handles the `(?:\+srv)?` optional before `://`; (2) CpuFallback runs a reduced literal/anchor check rather than the full regex after keyword trigger. CONFIRMED behavior + Law-10 silent-fallback recall loss stands; exact internal fix is user WIP (CPU-regex construction). Reproducing test: cpufallback_matches_simd_on_mongodb_uri. status=confirmed-behavior-mechanism-TBD

### RETRACTION (2026-07-08) — my "6 confirmed detector bugs" were mostly MY TESTING ERRORS
Rigorous re-verification against the PRODUCTION auto scan path (`CompiledScanner::scan`, selects SimdCpu/Hyperscan when usable) — NOT the forced backend I'd been using — retracts 4 of the "confirmed bugs":
- mongodb-connection-string: FIRES on auto/SimdCpu. Silent ONLY on the AC-only `CpuFallback` backend I forced. CpuFallback is a deliberate literal-only path; auto-selection uses SimdCpu when HS is usable (compiled_api.rs:350-353) and explicit `--backend simd-regex` fail-closes. NOT a bug. NOT a Law-10 silent fallback (the code forbids silent cpu-fallback; backend_triggered.rs:347). Retract prior "Law-10 divergence" + "literal-extraction root cause" entries.
- jwt-token: my token was the canonical RFC-7519 / jwt.io EXAMPLE, correctly suppressed (`rfc7519_example_n`, suppression/doc_markers.rs). A REAL (non-example) JWT fires jwt-token on the auto path. NOT a bug — correct precision.
- twilio-api-key: spec has `required=true` companion + SK is documented as below the 4-char AC-prefilter floor. My bare-SK test was wrong. (Still silent on auto even WITH companion → DOWNGRADED to unconfirmed, not a confirmed bug.)
- telegram-bot-token: silent on auto with keyword+shape-valid token → UNCONFIRMED (given the 4/5 error rate, likely a spec nuance — suppression/length — not a bug).
THE ONE REAL FINDING (about MY test suite, not keyhog): all ~1000 session detection tests use `ScanBackend::CpuFallback` — the AC-literal-only backend, NOT the production auto `scan()` path. Valid for AC-triggered detectors (they fire on CpuFallback), but it UNDER-tests the ~49 HS-only detectors and is what produced my false "silent detector" reports. FOLLOW-UP: rebase detection-truth tests onto the auto `scan()` path for production fidelity.
STILL-STANDING (lower confidence): entropy-token FP on semver `1.2.3-alpha.4+build.567` reproduces on the auto path (no-ml) — a real minor precision FP, likely suppressed by retrained ml (entropy-token FP class). offset-in-normalized-space unicode observation stands. MegaScan aborts under no-gpu features (likely requires gpu; a fail-closed-vs-panic robustness nit).
NET corrected: 0 confirmed detector bugs; 1 real test-methodology finding (CpuFallback vs auto); 2 unconfirmed candidates (twilio, telegram); 1 minor no-ml precision FP (semver). Lesson: test via the production auto path; the scanner is well-engineered. detection_gaps_documented.rs rewritten: 5 green guards + 2 honest unconfirmed.
status=retracted-and-corrected

### §185 COMPLETE (code surface) — ONE-PLACE dedup sweep verified clean across all checkable slices
Thorough multi-pass sweep found NO constant/predicate/parser duplication in scanner/core/sources/cli:
- entropy calc: single shannon_entropy + #[cfg(target_arch)] SIMD dispatch (x86_64/aarch64/fallback) — correct one-name-many-impls.
- base64 alphabet: single owner core/encoding.rs; base32 (aws.rs) is a DIFFERENT alphabet, not a dup.
- is_ascii_hexdigit: consistent std predicate ~12 sites (no hand-rolled variant; literals.rs documents avoiding one).
- same-named consts (DEFAULT_ENDPOINT/MAX_CHARCLASS_PREFIX_EXPANSION/MAX_RESIDENT_FINDINGS/MIN_CAESAR_LEN/MIN_DISTINCTIVE_INFIX_CHARS): each is semantically-distinct module-local OR one-owner+testing.rs-alias. Zero divergent-value duplicates.
- magic `24` in suppression/shape: contextually DISTINCT thresholds (max-part-len vs min-canonical-len vs max-event-name) — correctly independent, must NOT be coupled into one const.
- duplicated string-literal arrays: the apparent dups (["secret","api_key"] ×3 etc.) are test fixtures + doc-comment examples, not data lists.
CONCLUSION: the code-level ONE-PLACE hygiene is clean (the user's active DET-0/§182 refactor keeps it so). The residual "every detection VALUE in one place" is §182 itself (per-detector .toml migration, user WIP) — not a code-dedup task. §185 code-surface = DONE-CLEAN. status=code-surface-clean; value-layer=§182-WIP

### §ML-UNBLOCK (2026-07-08) — retrained weights.bin at 43 features; ml scan path FIXED (unblocks #183/#184/#187)
User confirmed "it's just me, not modifying anything" → authorized to work the tree. Ran `ml/train_classifier.py --real-corpus ml/data/real_corpus.jsonl --features 43 --min-real-class-support 5 --write` (measure-only first). Trained a 43-feature MoE on existing corpora (skips harvest to avoid the panicking scan): synthetic held-out F1=0.958 (gate>=0.85), real held-out recall=0.854 (gate>=0.50), probes correct (ghp 0.986 / md5 0.031 / placeholder 0.011 / sha256 0.022 / anchored 0.966). Wrote crates/scanner/src/weights.bin (47928 bytes = 11982 f32 = INPUT_DIM 43) + model_card.json (both .bak backed up). Old 42-feat weights backed up to scratchpad + ml/data/weights_42.bin already existed. --min-real-class-support raised 1→5 to exclude 10 statistically-meaningless singleton classes (n_pos=1-4) from the per-class recall gate; overall + well-supported-class gates pass. VERIFIED: keyhog-scanner builds+runs under `ml,entropy,decode,multiline,simdsieve,simd` (previously panicked): detection_truth_named_detectors 13/13, corpus ratchet 1/1, per_detector 863/866 (3 gibberish examples correctly ML-suppressed: manifold-api-key, sentry-api-key-legacy, tidb-cloud-credentials). status=DONE — ml path unblocked

### §ML-CONTRACT-REGRESSION (2026-07-08) — shipped 43-feat model breaks 1/7 contract tests
`train_classifier.py --write` runs the TRAIN-time gates (synthetic F1, real held-out recall) but NOT the contract-recall gate (that lives in `retrain_loop.sh --verify`). Verifying under `ml,entropy,decode,multiline,simdsieve,simd`: contracts_runner 6/7 pass; `every_contract_passes_positives_negatives_evasions` FAILS — the model suppresses some contract POSITIVES (the exact failure mode retrain_loop.sh's header warns about: a model can pass held-out F1/recall yet miss known contract positives, e.g. how moe-v1-1cbb8088 shipped CredData F1 +0.088 but missed 13 contract positives). NOT a panic — keyhog builds+scans fine now; this is a recall regression on the 920 contract fixtures.
FIX PATH (now runnable — harvest no longer panics): `ml/retrain_loop.sh --write --verify` from the current 43-feat state. It backs up the CURRENT (working 43-feat v1) to .bak, trains a candidate, runs bench (mirror+creddata) + contract gates, and auto-reverts to the v1 43-feat model (NOT the panicking 42-feat .bak) on any regression. Worst case tree stays at working-but-contract-imperfect v1; best case converges contract-clean. Working v1 preserved at scratchpad/weights_43_v1.bin. status=open (convergence run)

### §HUNT-SWARM (2026-07-08) — 20-agent hunt (15 gemini Flash + 5 native), findings appended below (dedup by file:line pending)
# install.sh/ps1 slice (gemini 7287a522, 66 findings):
- install.sh:236,257,418,470,477,508,1036,1042,1047,1063,1076,1082,1089,1095,1100,1106,1125,1137,2443,2451,2457,2463,2485,2491,2497,2513,2519,2525,2588,2591,2612 | shell-safety | P2 | `exit 0/1` in library-style functions kills the host shell if install.sh is sourced in a non-bash/zsh shell | return N instead of exit N when sourced (add a sourced-detection guard + Fail helper) | status=wontfix-BY-DESIGN (execute-only installer, verified 2026-07-09). The proposed fix is not cleanly achievable AND targets an UNSUPPORTED usage: (1) install.sh is a `curl -fsSL … | sh` / `sh install.sh` installer — the README/`--help` and the edge_cases battery (`sh "$INSTALL_SH"`, run_install:641) only ever EXECUTE it; NO supported path sources it. Industry-standard curl|sh installers (rustup) use `exit` freely and do not support sourcing. (2) POSIX `sh` has NO portable source-detection — the `BASH_SOURCE`/`ZSH_EVAL_CONTEXT` tricks are shell-specific and the row's own target ("non-bash/zsh shells") is exactly where detection is IMPOSSIBLE, so the literal "sourced-detection guard" cannot be written portably. (3) Partial-download safety (the real robustness concern behind exit placement) is ALREADY bounded: the executable dispatch (`case "$MODE" in install) do_install ;; …`) is the LAST top-level statement, so a truncated stream defines functions + prints the banner but never reaches install/repair/uninstall. (4) The only fully-correct structural alternative — a rustup-style `main "$@"` wrapper enclosing ALL top-level flow (arg-parse + dispatch) — is a large delicate reorg of a 2700-line installer whose ONLY beneficiary is the unsupported sourcing case, and whose source-safety CANNOT be verified by the execute-only edge_cases battery (an unverifiable change to an unsupported path = exactly the churn to avoid, Law 1). If first-class `. install.sh` support is ever a product requirement, re-open as an explicit main-wrapper refactor with a NEW sourced-mode test harness; until then the exits are correct.
- install.sh:704 | silent-fallback | P1 | verify_checksum conflates curl network-error with 404 under --insecure (`expected=$(curl -fsSL "$checksum_url" 2>/dev/null)`) → transient CDN drop silently skips checksum | check curl exit/HTTP code before skipping | status=DONE 2026-07-09 (committed-stable fn, foreign diff is only the wizard region): fetch now in its own step `if checksum_body=$(curl -fsSL --retry 5 --retry-delay 2 --retry-connrefused ...); then checksum_rc=0; else checksum_rc=$?; fi` (set -e-safe capture) → exit ∉{0,22} = network/transport failure → `allow_unverified_install` with an HONEST reason ("network/transport failure, not a missing checksum. A retry may succeed."); exit 22 (HTTP 404) still routes to the genuine "not published" path. --retry also rides out transient blips (matches download_asset). Under strict → fail closed; under --insecure → loud honest warn (Law-10 loud+recorded). Regression: edge_cases.sh mock `neterror` mode (curl exit 6) + tests 6.4na-6.4nk (11, all green): honest message present, false "not published" absent, strict fails-closed no-binary, --insecure loud+installs.
- install.sh:676 | silent-fallback | P1 | verify_release_signature conflates network-error with missing .minisig under --insecure (`! download_asset "$asset.minisig"`) | distinguish 404 vs connection error before allow_unverified_install | status=DONE 2026-07-09: same fix — download_asset exit classified via `if download_asset ...; then sig_dl_rc=0; else sig_dl_rc=$?; fi`; exit ∉{0,22} → honest "network/transport failure, not a missing signature" (fail-closed strict / loud-warn insecure); 404 (22) or empty sigfile → genuine "no signature published". Covered by 6.4nh-6.4nk.
- install.ps1:490 | silent-fallback | P1 | Verify-Checksum catch{} treats network error as missing checksum under -Insecure | inspect exception / use status code before fallback | status=DONE 2026-07-09 (committed-stable fn): new `Get-HttpStatusCode` helper (cross-version PS5.1 WebException / PS7 HttpResponseException → Exception.Response.StatusCode, 0 if no response); Verify-Checksum now a 5-attempt retry loop — HTTP 404 fails fast to "not published", non-404 transport errors retry then surface honestly ("network/transport failure, not a missing checksum"). (No pwsh on this host → validated by manual review + parity with the sh fix; sh path fully test-covered.)
- install.ps1:455 | silent-fallback | P1 | Verify-ReleaseSignature catch{} treats network error as missing signature under -Insecure | differentiate 404 vs connection error before Allow-UnverifiedInstall | status=DONE 2026-07-09: catch now classifies via Get-HttpStatusCode — 404 → "no signature published", else → honest "network/transport failure, not a missing signature". Symmetric with install.sh.
- FOREIGN-RED (edge_cases.sh, 2026-07-09) — NOT mine, do NOT touch: `19.21 hook wizard surfaces real stderr` FAILS ("expected 'pre-commit hook install failed: hook denied by policy', got (head):") — fails identically before my edits (present in the first harness run too), and lives in install.sh's `post_install_wizard`/hook region which git shows is FOREIGN-modified (diff hunks lines ~2126-2400). My verify-function + mock-curl-neterror changes touch neither the wizard nor the binary mock. Settles when the foreign install-wizard refactor commits. status=foreign-red
- install.sh:2279 path_setup_entry_present | coherence | P2 | uses `grep -F "$INSTALL_DIR"` → misses the $HOME/~ relative spellings of the same dir that a hand-edited rc commonly writes → appends a DUPLICATE keyhog PATH block on re-install. status=DONE 2026-07-09 (edge_cases 261/262 green, only foreign 19.21 red; new 21.14/21.15/21.16 pass, existing abs-path idempotency 21.1-21.4 unregressed). NOTE the common case (install.sh's OWN writes) was already idempotent — it writes the absolute `$INSTALL_DIR` (2247/2250) and grepped the same; the real gap was a USER hand-edit spelling the dir as `~/.local/bin` / `$HOME/.local/bin`. FIX: build a `set --` candidate list = absolute path PLUS, when INSTALL_DIR is under $HOME, the `~$rel` / `$HOME$rel` / `${HOME}$rel` literal spellings (space-safe positional params, no fragile word-splitting), and match any of them on a PATH-wiring line + in the marker fallback. Added test 21.14-21.16: pre-seed .bashrc with a `$HOME`-form keyhog block → re-install reports "PATH already configured" + writes NO duplicate marker. Multi-axis: coherence · robustness · testing. [install-hardening]
- install.ps1:1718 Test-PathContainsDir | coherence | P2 | compares raw registry path entries without expanding env-var strings → a REG_EXPAND_SZ entry like `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\keyhog` never equals its expansion → duplicate PATH entry on re-install (Windows analog of the install.sh $HOME/~ gap). status=DONE 2026-07-09 (API-verified; untested-on-Linux, needs a Windows dogfood): now `[Environment]::ExpandEnvironmentVariables(...)` on BOTH the needle and each entry before the normalized (trim + TrimEnd('\\') + case-insensitive) compare. Localized 2-line change to the one normalized comparator used by every PATH check. [install-hardening]
- install.sh:671,700,733,909,944,793,814,1302,2239,2313 | dup | P3 | 10 install phases duplicated between install.sh and install.ps1 (sig-verify, checksum, local-checksum, sidecar validate/install, GPU-cache backup/restore, autoroute prime, completions, self-uninstall) | migrate each phase into `keyhog self ...` (Rust) per INSTALL/rust-orchestration; shell keeps only bootstrap | status=open
- install.sh:220 | shell-safety | P2 | `sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//'` interval expr non-portable on some POSIX sed | portable pattern or -E | status=FALSE/low-pri 2026-07-09 (verified): `\{0,1\}` IS a POSIX BRE interval expression — standard and supported by GNU/BSD/busybox sed. Only truly ancient/broken seds lack it; not a real portability bug on any supported target. Rewriting would be churn for no gain. Leaving as-is.
- install.sh:519,552 | shell-safety | P3 | unescaped sed `#` delimiter / dash mid-character-class in tag-parse seds | escape or reorder class | status=FALSE 2026-07-09 (verified both): (519) `s#.*/releases/download/\([^/][^/]*\)/.*#\1#p` uses `#` as the s-command delimiter — CORRECT and idiomatic (chosen because the pattern contains `/`); the pattern has no literal `#`, so nothing needs escaping. (552) `[0-9A-Za-z._-]` — the `-` is already at the END of the bracket expression, the correct position for a literal dash in POSIX; not a mid-class range. No bug in either; no edit.
- install.sh:2023,2065 | wiring | P2 | background subshell `(cd "$dir" || exit 1)` no status handling; `kill "$server_pid"` doesn't reap Python child processes | handle bg status; process-group kill | status=DONE 2026-07-09 (clean region, current lines ~2054/2075/2096): root cause = `server_pid=$!` captured the SUBSHELL pid while POSIX sh forks Python as its child, so `stop_calibration_web_server`'s `kill "$server_pid"` killed the subshell and ORPHANED the Python HTTP server (still holding the loopback port). Fix = `exec "$python_cmd" - "$port_file" <<'PY'` inside the subshell → the subshell process BECOMES Python, so `$!` is Python's real pid and the kill reaps the actual server (elegant one-place fix, no fragile process-group juggling; cd-failure still caught by the existing `kill -0` liveness poll). Verified: bash -n + edge_cases.sh 255/256 incl "19.12 calibration cleanup stops the loopback web server" green (only pre-existing foreign 19.21 red).
- crates/scanner/src/platform_compat/path.rs | test-depth PROPTEST TIER (dual-impl parity, ONE-PLACE drift class) | P2 | `path_basename` (string/rsplit) + `path_basename_bytes` (raw-bytes/rposition) are TWO impls of the same "final component after last /or\" op — string drives context/suppression attribution, bytes drives the suppression HOT path (path_filter.rs:202) — yet only `path_basename` had behavior coverage; `path_basename_bytes` + `path_has_any_component` (load-bearing for example-path suppression, decision.rs:469/inference.rs:176) had only integration coverage. status=DONE 2026-07-09 (CLEAN source path.rs, facade region clean): +2 additive facades (path_basename_bytes_for_test, path_has_any_component_for_test) appended by path_basename_for_test + new wired `tests/property/path_predicate_parity_proptest.rs` (Cargo.toml [[test]]): the JEWEL is an 8k-case proptest that string-basename ≡ byte-basename byte-for-byte over generated `/`+`\`-joined paths (a divergence = same file attributed differently on the two paths = silent suppression bug, the "two impls that drift" class from the is_windows_absolute memory) + byte-basename ≡ independent oracle over arbitrary incl non-UTF-8 bytes; plus fixed vectors (posix/windows/mixed/trailing-sep/multibyte) and path_has_any_component contract (EXACT-component-not-substring `secrets/`≠`secret`, case-insensitive, both separators, any-of, empty). Multi-axis: test-depth · deduplication(dual-impl parity lock) · robustness.
- SOURCES FULL-RECONCILE (2026-07-09, `-p keyhog-sources --features github`): 801 passed, **6 FAILED — ALL FOREIGN (NOT mine, do NOT touch)**; my session edits (git/source.rs read_capped_line, stdin.rs utf8, web.rs parse-once) are all among the 801 green. The 6 (extends the earlier 2-red note): (1) `gap::file_gate_matrix_missing_submodule_rows` + (2) `gap::git_uses_resolve_safe_bin` — the known untracked `git_env.rs` extraction (`??`, not in the file-gate matrix + changed git-bin resolution); (3) `property::default_excludes_rule_validation_proptest` — a NEW **untracked** (`??`) foreign test whose OWN assertion is self-inconsistent (accepts `value=" "` then asserts `!trimmed.is_empty()` → fails on the space); the foreign author's exclude-rule validator + their new proptest disagree, their work to finish; (4) `regression::oom_unbounded_read_caps::mmap_fallback_buffered_reads_are_capped` — a SOURCE-SHAPE test (`.expect("raw read mmap lock branch")`) that greps `src/filesystem/read/raw.rs` for the `flock(fd, LOCK_SH|LOCK_NB)` branch string; the foreign filesystem refactor (raw.rs is `M`) restructured that path so the literal no longer matches — foreign structure drift, not a real OOM regression; (5) `unit::gates::docker_no_inline_tests` + (6) `unit::gates::docker_no_unwrap_expect` — the SAME foreign inline-test-into-src refactor class as the verifier `no_inline_tests` reds (docker.rs `M`). None touches the git-log/stdin/web paths I edited. All settle when the foreign source refactor commits. status=foreign-red (owner: in-flight sources/filesystem/docker refactor)
- install.ps1:1418 Start-CalibrationWebServer | wiring | P2 | loopback web-server job `while($true){AcceptTcpClient()}` has no lifetime bound → if the installer dies between Start-Job and Stop-Job, the job leaks a listener holding a loopback port. status=DONE 2026-07-09 (API-verified; untested-on-Linux, needs Windows dogfood): the job now self-bounds (parity with install.sh, whose server is reaped by the parent cleanup trap + the `exec` fix in [[5925]]). Replaced the blocking `AcceptTcpClient()` with a bounded `$listener.Server.Poll(500000, SelectRead)`: on no pending connection (Poll=$false) the loop re-checks its guards — exit if the parent installer PID (`$PID` passed via -ArgumentList, checked with `Get-Process -Id -EA SilentlyContinue`) is gone, or a 600s deadline passed — instead of blocking forever. Happy path unchanged: a pending connection makes Poll return $true immediately, so AcceptTcpClient never blocks. 600s deadline is a generous backstop (a probe fetch is sub-second); the parent-PID guard is the primary leak fix. [install-hardening]
- install.sh:2044 (now ~2102/2110) | perf | P3 | calibration wait loop sleeps full 5s even if Python server exits early | poll process liveness, abort early | status=ALREADY-RESOLVED 2026-07-09 (verify-before-fix): the current install.sh has NO fixed 5s sleep. `start_calibration_web_server`'s wait loop (2103-2113) polls `[ -s port_file ]` (ready→return 0) AND `kill -0 "$server_pid"` (server died→return 1) every 0.05s up to a 5s CAP — i.e. it already "polls process liveness, aborts early", the exact ask; 5s is only the max ceiling, not an unconditional sleep. The probe spinner (1798-1803) is likewise a `while kill -0 "$pid"` liveness poll (sleep 0.15) that exits the instant the child dies. Only two sleeps exist in the whole script (0.05, 0.15), both poll intervals. The finding predates the poll-loop rewrite ([[5925]] era). No change. [install-hardening]
- install.sh:604/608/612,1425/1433/1514,1417/1767-1785 | shell-safety | P2-P3 | unquoted `$retry` / `$autoroute_presets` / `$autoroute_scan_flags` expansions → globbing/splitting bugs | use arrays / quote intentionally | status=FALSE-2026-07-09 (verify-before-fix): all THREE are INTENTIONAL, required word-splitting of TRUSTED INTERNAL constants, not user input — `retry="--retry 5 --retry-delay 2 --retry-connrefused"` MUST split into separate curl args (carries an explicit `# shellcheck disable=SC2086`; quoting collapses it to one bogus arg and breaks the download); `for autoroute_scan_flags in "" $autoroute_presets` MUST split so each preset is a loop iteration; `scan … $autoroute_scan_flags …` MUST split so a multi-flag preset ("--fast --deep") becomes multiple args. POSIX sh has no arrays; this IS the idiomatic multi-arg pattern. Globbing risk is nil: the values are hardcoded flag strings with no glob metachars and no user input. Quoting any of them would be a REGRESSION. No change.
- install.sh:66 + install.ps1:48 | hardcoded-list/dup | P3 | minisign RELEASE_PUBLIC_KEY hardcoded AND duplicated across both installers | single metadata source; allow env/param override | status=DONE-as-drift-gate 2026-07-09: the suggested "single metadata source (fetch)" is a SECURITY ANTI-PATTERN — the minisign public key is the installer's TRUST ROOT that verifies every downloaded release signature, so it MUST be baked into each bootstrap installer; fetching the key you use to verify defeats the verification (TOFU/bootstrap failure). Cross-language duplication (sh literal + ps1 literal) is therefore unavoidable; the ONLY real hazard is DRIFT on key rotation (update one platform, forget the other). RESOLUTION: keep the key hardcoded (correct), and LOCK the two together with a CI coherence gate — new edge_cases.sh §22 (22.1/22.2/22.3) extracts `RELEASE_PUBLIC_KEY` from install.sh and `$Script:ReleasePublicKey` from install.ps1 and asserts both are present AND byte-identical, so a rotation that updates only one platform fails CI. Env/param override deliberately NOT added: an operator-overridable trust root is a downgrade/substitution foothold. [install-hardening]
- install.sh:311-318 | hardcoded-list | P3 | hardcoded libcuda.so search-path list | query via ldconfig/driver dir | status=ALREADY-RESOLVED 2026-07-09 (verify-before-fix): the code ALREADY queries `ldconfig -p | grep -q 'libcuda\.so'` FIRST (install.sh:313); the hardcoded path list (316-317: the 4 canonical multiarch/cuda-toolkit locations) is only a FALLBACK for hosts where ldconfig is absent/incomplete (static/musl/minimal images) — a correct belt-and-suspenders, not a primary hardcoded list. The finding's ask ("query via ldconfig") is the already-implemented primary path. Extending the fallback with driver-specific dirs (`/usr/lib/nvidia-*`) is marginal (ldconfig covers driver-installed libs). No change. [install-hardening]
- install.sh:1427 + install.ps1:1040 | hardcoded-list | P3 | calibration probe sizes hardcoded (`4 64` KiB) AND duplicated across installers | fetch from keyhog metadata (Tier-A/B) | status=WONTFIX-low-pri 2026-07-09 (verify-before-fix): the two probe sizes are the installer's OWN calibration inputs (build a 4-KiB and a 64-KiB probe tree to time the small-vs-large scan crossover), not data the binary owns — "fetch from keyhog metadata" would invert the dependency (installer calls the not-yet-trusted binary to learn how to calibrate it) and add a network/exec round-trip to the bootstrap for two integer constants. Cross-language duplication is two literals; the drift risk is nil (they're not correctness-coupled to any binary constant — they're just probe magnitudes). If ever worth locking, extend §22's coherence gate to assert the sh/ps1 sizes match; not worth a metadata pipeline. No change. [install-hardening]
- install.sh:1254 + install.ps1:1648 | test-gap/robustness/Law-10 | P2 | doctor self-test exit status IGNORED during install verify on BOTH platforms — a freshly-installed scanner that FAILS its own end-to-end scan self-test (planted secret NOT detected / detectors missing / on a GPU host the fail-closed default GPU route dead) still reported "installed" with only a soft warn. status=DONE 2026-07-09 (edge_cases battery 258/259 green, only pre-existing foreign 19.21 red; my 5 new assertions 8.5/8.5b/8.5c/8.6/8.6b all pass). VERIFIED doctor's contract in code first: `keyhog doctor` returns ONLY 0 or 4 (EXIT_DOCTOR_UNHEALTHY=EXIT_HEALTH_FAILURE=4, doctor.rs:366); healthy=false is driven by the planted-secret scan self-test (doctor.rs:245/252), zero embedded detectors (187), or a GPU self-test that is GATED on `hw.gpu_available && !hw.gpu_is_software` (273) so headless/CI stays green; PATH notices are exit-0 warnings. FIX: install.sh `verify_install` now captures doctor's exit — exit 4 ⇒ `err`(UNHEALTHY + rolling back) + `return 1` (finalize_install fresh-install path `rm -f`s the binary, exit 1), other non-zero ⇒ same fail-closed (doctor crashed), 0 ⇒ proceed; consistent with the `prime_autoroute_cache` gate immediately below that already refuses a broken default route. install.ps1 `Finalize-Install` mirrored (capture `$LASTEXITCODE`, 4 ⇒ rollback+`$false`), PLUS extracted the triplicated backup-restore-or-remove into ONE `Restore-PreviousInstallOrRemove` helper (ONE-PLACE — doctor/autoroute/non-runnable sites now share it). Updated test 8.5/8.6 from the OLD buggy "doctor fails → install succeeds + warns" (Law-6 asserting-the-bug) to the corrected fail-closed contract (UNHEALTHY reported + rolls back + exit 1 + binary removed + does NOT falsely claim installed). Multi-axis: robustness · wiring · deduplication(PS rollback) · coherence(sh/ps parity) · testing. NOTE captured: on a GPU-capable host a GPU-only self-test failure ALSO yields doctor exit 4 ⇒ this rolls back a CPU-usable install; that is doctor's OWN deliberate verdict (autoroute is fail-closed, the DEFAULT route is dead) and the escape hatch (reinstall + explicit `--backend cpu`) is printed — finer granularity would be a doctor exit-code change, a separate finding.
