crates/scanner/src/entropy/avx512.rs:59,61 | coherence+arch | P3 | target_feature misses avx512dq; calculate_shannon_entropy still scalar-counts | add probed features; AVX-512 gather counting | status=done RECONCILED working-as-designed (both sub-claims are non-bugs, and the suggested fix contradicts a documented decision): (1) "still scalar-counts" is DELIBERATE — the docstring (avx512.rs:45-48) states the path "is now bit-identical to the scalar path: the previous vectorized polynomial-log2 reduction diverged from the exact reference by ~5e-3 bits/byte for no measurable speedup (the 256-bin loop is a negligible fraction of the work), so soundness wins." The body delegates to the shared histogram_8way (memory-bound counting, no AVX-512 win) + exact entropy_from_histogram. The proposed "AVX-512 gather counting" is exactly the inexact reduction that was REMOVED for numerical soundness — re-adding it would regress correctness. (2) "target_feature misses avx512dq" is NOT a safety bug: the body uses ZERO avx512dq instructions (it is scalar), so enabling only avx512f+avx512bw is correct/minimal for what it emits. The production dispatch (entropy/fast.rs) gates on f+bw+dq, a strict SUPERSET of the function's f+bw requirement — so the safety precondition is always satisfied (never UB). The only effect is that an exotic f+bw-without-dq CPU uses a lower tier that yields the BIT-IDENTICAL value (the path == scalar), so zero recall/perf/correctness impact. Optional pure-cosmetic cleanup: relax the dispatch's dq requirement to match the code's actual f+bw need — but entropy/fast.rs is foreign-dirty and the change is behaviorally a no-op. No functional action warranted.

# ── decode slice (gemini fa3415bb) ──
crates/scanner/src/decode/mod.rs:42,46,50,54,58,94,110,142 | wiring+dup+test-gap | P2-P3 | 4 decoders use `Result<String,()>` unit-error; extracted_value_strings_for_test not #[cfg(test)]; percent-escape + backslash-escape validation duplicated vs url.rs/unicode_escape.rs/json.rs | descriptive error type; gate test fn; shared escape validators | status=open
crates/scanner/src/decode/base64.rs:20,29,38,71,73,269,280,328 | silent-fallback+over-broad | P1-P2 | base64/z85 trial decode + inflate + UTF-8 errors silently discarded; MAX_BASE64_INPUT_LEN/MAX_Z85_INPUT_LEN hardcoded 16MB; ambiguous classification never tries url-safe fallback | loud/recorded decode failures; configurable limits; url-safe fallback | status=open
crates/scanner/src/decode/base64.rs:109,113,129,139,180,306,342,356 | dup+perf+hardcoded | P2-P3 | is_base64_candidate_byte vs is_standard_base64_byte dup; subslice + Cow allocs; redundant O(N) rescans; z85_val hardcoded match (use LUT); unchecked u64 mul | dedup predicates; zero-alloc; checked mul; LUT | status=PARTIALLY-INVALID-VERIFIED (2026-07-09): the two headline sub-items are FALSE POSITIVES. (a) "unchecked u64 mul" (base64.rs:342 `value = value*85 + z85_val(byte)`) is PROVABLY bounded — the loop is `bytes.chunks_exact(5)` so at most 5 z85 digits, each z85_val < 85 → max value = 85^5-1 ≈ 4.437e9 < 2^33 << u64::MAX; `value*85` cannot overflow u64. Line 344 then correctly rejects `value > u32::MAX` (85^5-1 > u32::MAX so some chunks are legitimately refused). NOT a DoS/overflow. `checked_mul` would be dead defensiveness. (b) "is_base64_candidate_byte vs is_standard_base64_byte dup" is NOT a dup — candidate = alnum + `+/=-_` (includes URL-safe `-_`), standard = alnum + `+/=` (excludes URL-safe); they encode DIFFERENT alphabets by design (candidate-detection vs strict-standard-shape). Merging would erase the standard-vs-urlsafe distinction (a correctness regression). DO NOT dedup. || REMAINING (real but micro perf, unverified-impact): subslice/Cow allocs + redundant O(N) rescans in scan_base64_candidate; z85_val range-match → 256-byte LUT (marginal — the range match already compiles to a tight branch tree; LUT trades a cache line for it). status=open-for-perf-micro-only (the overflow + dup framings refuted). Consistent with the session-wide finding: HUNT dup/perf/silent-fallback items are heavily false-positive on this hardened tree.

# ── cli/orchestrator slice (gemini 88835d25) ──
crates/cli/src/orchestrator/dispatch.rs:160 + fused.rs:285 | silent-fallback | P0 | chosen_backend=Gpu can internally degrade to CPU with NO loud line (both dispatch + fused paths); GPU_SCANNED_CHUNKS still increments (dispatch.rs:228) | compare gpu_degrade_count before/after each batch, emit loud degrade warning, only count real-GPU chunks (Law 10) | status=DONE-with-CORRECTION (2026-07-09). VERIFY-BEFORE-FIX corrected the P0 HEADLINE: the "NO loud line" claim is INACCURATE — the GPU region-presence dispatch path (gpu_region_dispatch.rs, reached via `scan_chunks_with_backend(batch, Gpu)`) ALREADY degrades LOUDLY: `deny_silent_gpu_degrade`/`_with_reason` (gpu_forced_helpers.rs) hard-fails under --require-gpu (exit 12) and otherwise emits a ONE-SHOT `eprintln!` (RUNTIME_DEGRADE_WARNED OnceLock), and `record_gpu_degrade` bumps `gpu_degrade_count` on every degrade event. So the Law-10 loudness is NOT the bug. The REAL residual (the finding's own second clause) is TELEMETRY COUNT ACCURACY: `GPU_SCANNED_CHUNKS` was incremented from the router's CHOICE (`is_gpu_backend(chosen_backend)`), not real GPU execution — dispatch.rs counted after the arm on chosen-GPU, fused.rs counted BEFORE the dispatch unconditionally — so a chosen-GPU batch that degrades mid-dispatch overstated GPU acceleration in the completion summary. FIX (exactly the row's prescription): added lightweight `pub fn gpu_degrade_count(&self) -> u64` on CompiledScanner (one relaxed atomic load — runtime_status() carries it but recomputes digests, too heavy per-batch); BOTH producer paths now snapshot the counter before the GPU arm and count the batch as GPU-scanned ONLY if it was routed to GPU AND `gpu_degrade_count` did not increase while dispatching it (a degrade => those chunks ran CPU/SIMD). fused.rs's increment moved from BEFORE the dispatch (unconditional) to AFTER, gated on the same snapshot. VERIFIED: `cargo check -p keyhog` RC=0, 0 errors. Gate safety confirmed by inspection: orchestrator_dispatch_scanner_worker (checks scan_sources body + SimdCpu arms — untouched), autoroute_install_calibration_contract (scanner gpu_forced fetch_add + digest — unrelated), file_gate (oversized-chunk/drain-panic — unrelated) do NOT pin the GPU-count placement. Did NOT run cli all_tests (heavy + carries known foreign reds generate_contracts/cli_surface_snapshot). | [gpu-cpu-parity]

crates/cli/src/orchestrator/dispatch.rs:37,274,307,334,362,389,403,577 | silent-fallback+dup+perf | P0-P2 | oversized coalesced chunk skipped with warning not fail; ticker join failure discarded; produce_sources loop dup vs fused.rs; source.chunks() errors logged+continue (masks missing coverage); default mtime/size on None -> wrong cache hits; mem::take realloc; scanner-thread panic -> generic error; forget() called on ALL findings regardless of verified/suppressed | fail-closed on oversize; propagate errors; shared iterator; uncacheable on missing meta; reuse buffer; distinct exit code | status=MOSTLY-INVALID-VERIFIED (2026-07-09): traced the full scan-coverage + cache + exit-code chain; the P0 "silent-fallback masks coverage" HEADLINE is a FALSE POSITIVE — this path is exemplary Law-10 (loud + fail-closed), not silent. Per sub-item: (1) OVERSIZED chunk skip (dispatch.rs:329-332) — record_oversized_coalesced_chunk_skip prints "WARNING: ... it was NOT scanned for secrets" + path AND increments counts.over_max_size → source_coverage_incomplete() (run.rs:476-502) → run.rs:438-443 returns EXIT_SOURCE_FAILED "Not reporting clean: some requested bytes were not scanned". LOUD + FAIL-CLOSED, not "warning not fail". (2) source.chunks() Err (dispatch.rs:333-337) — tracing::warn + record_source_error()→SOURCE_ERRORS→source_coverage_incomplete()→same EXIT_SOURCE_FAILED; a TOTAL failure additionally → record_failed_source()→FAILED_SOURCES→run.rs:285-293 EXIT_SOURCE_FAILED. Partial-coverage is DELIBERATELY not total-fail (comment run.rs:281-284) but still trips coverage-incomplete. NOT silent. (3) mtime/size None→0 default (dispatch.rs:362-363) — NOT a wrong-cache-hit: record_borrowed_key_at_offset_with_metadata (merkle_index.rs) gates unchanged on `previous.hash == entry.hash` (CONTENT HASH ONLY); mtime/size are stored but NOT in the unchanged decision, so 0/0 defaults can't force a false skip; they feed only the cross-run racy guard where 0 biases toward RE-SCAN (fail-safe, storage.rs:86-89). RECALL-SAFE, already LAW10-commented. (4) scanner-thread panic (dispatch.rs:403 area) — surfaced via SCANNER_PANICKED→EXIT_SCANNER_PANIC (run.rs:432), not a generic error. (5) forget()-on-all-findings (dispatch.rs:577-581) — it's idx.forget() (merkle cache EVICTION, not mem::forget), and forgetting on RAW matches (pre-suppression) is the CORRECT conservative choice (doc 571-576): never cache-skip a secret-bearing file, else an allowlist/suppression change wouldn't re-surface it. "Fixing" to final-findings-only would INTRODUCE a silent-miss (Law-10 regression). DO NOT. || GENUINELY OPEN (minor, non-silent): produce_sources loop dup vs fused.rs (real ORGANIZATION/dedup — a shared chunk-iterator helper); ticker join failure discarded (minor); mem::take is idiomatic (send-then-clear), NOT a realloc bug. status=DEDUP-DONE (2026-07-09): the one genuinely-open sub-item (produce_sources loop dup vs fused.rs) FIXED. The two producer loops had BYTE-IDENTICAL per-source fail-closed bookkeeping — the oversized arm (record_oversized_coalesced_chunk_skip), the Err arm (record_source_error + src_errored + warn), and the `src_chunks==0 && src_errored → record_failed_source` finalize — a real drift risk (a change to the ceiling or receipts in one loop could silently under-account coverage in the other). Extracted ONE owner in dispatch.rs: `enum ClassifiedSourceChunk {Scan(Chunk), Skip}` + `classify_source_chunk(chunk_result, &mut src_chunks, &mut src_errored) -> ClassifiedSourceChunk` (owns the oversized+error bookkeeping) + `finalize_source_outcome(src_chunks, src_errored)` (owns the total-failure receipt). Both loops now `let Scan(c) = classify(...) else { continue }` and differ ONLY in the scannable-chunk handler (coalesced: record_unchanged_chunk/push_chunk/should_flush; fused: batch.push/send-on-full) + call finalize_source_outcome. behavior-preserving (break 'sources preserved in both). fused.rs reaches them via super::. RECONCILE GREEN @bi6a6kivk: -p keyhog --lib 134 passed + lane10_silent_fallback_surfacing 9 passed (the coverage-surfacing/fail-closed contract — directly validates the classifier/finalizer preserve accounting) + regression_cli_exit_codes 14 passed / 0 failed. (unused-import `Command` warnings are pre-existing foreign-git-refactor noise, not mine.) LESSON: dispatch.rs coverage handling is a MODEL of loud+fail-closed; do not let a hunt "fix" regress it.
- 2026-07-09 E2E-DETECTION-HEALTH-CONFIRMED: with weights.bin re-synced (5727 resolved), ran a representative detection_truth suite through the restored public scan API (CompiledScanner::compile(embedded_detector_specs) → scan_chunks_with_backend CpuFallback, ci-lean): detection_truth_{cloud_providers, connection_strings, multiline_pem, decode_formats, detector_identity, evasion_categories, precision_negatives, named_detectors} = 2+6+5+5+8+9+13+8 = 56 passed / 0 failed. The scan path (structural + entropy + ML-scored) is HEALTHY end-to-end. The earlier "no e2e detection test can be GREEN" (5730) is OBSOLETE — 18 detection_truth files exist and pass. #177/#184 deep-detection is unblocked (only the stale golden VALUE remains, user-owned).

crates/core/src/spec/load.rs:392 | coherence | P1->RESOLVED-BY-ANALYSIS (2026-07-08) | load_detectors_from_str parses TOML but does NOT call validate_detector. Caller-impact analysis: ~77 callers, only ONE non-test (the CoreTestApi facade in core/src/testing.rs itself) — ALL production detector loads go through the GATED paths (load_detectors -> assemble_detector_load -> should_reject_detector -> validate_detector, and load_embedded_detectors_or_fail). The string path is a test-only PARSE primitive and tests deliberately invoke validate_detector SEPARATELY (new_core_allowlist_spec, spec_validate_merkle test both as distinct ops). So there is NO production bypass; wiring the gate into the parse primitive would conflate two intentional operations and break the ~77 parse-only test callers. If runtime dynamic-injection ever becomes a real prod feature, IT must call validate_detector at its call site (not baked into the parse primitive). NOT a code change. | status=DONE-analysis
crates/core/src/spec/load.rs:214 | coherence | P1 | assemble_detector_load did not check for DUPLICATE detector IDs (two files sharing an id silently shadow — the loser's patterns/companions never fire, attribution ambiguous). Verified no current dupes (923 files/923 ids) so it can't break the live corpus. Encoded by dir_with_duplicate_detector_ids_is_gate_rejected_naming_the_id (gate-on rejects naming 'demo'; gate-off keeps both). Verified green @bqu46jx19 (dir_with_duplicate_detector_ids_is_gate_rejected_naming_the_id ok; 16 pass). | status=DONE

crates/core/src/spec.rs:433,542,595 | hardcoded-list+dup | P2 | ALLOWED_FOR_VERIFY script engines + SEVERITY_CANONICAL_WIRE_FORMS hardcoded; ORDERED severity array duplicates the wire forms | Tier-B; single severity source | status=PARTIAL-DONE (2026-07-09): the SEVERITY single-source dedup DONE. The 6 (variant, wire-form) pairs were enumerated in 5+ places (SEVERITY_CANONICAL_WIRE_FORMS array, visit_str deserialize match, from_filter_label match, ORDERED array, as_str match) — a 7th severity needed 5+ edits that could drift. FIX: made `Severity::as_str` a `pub const fn` (THE single wire-form owner); `SEVERITY_CANONICAL_WIRE_FORMS` now const-derives `[&str; Severity::ORDERED.len()]` from ORDERED+as_str at compile time (auto-grows with the enum, can't drift, omits the private client_safe alias); `visit_str` and `from_filter_label` now SCAN `ORDERED` comparing `as_str` (keeping the explicit `client_safe` back-compat alias + their distinct exact-vs-lenient normalization) instead of re-listing the 6 arms. ORDERED + enum decl order (which derives Ord → severity_lte suppression ranking) UNTOUCHED. Comprehensive existing suite validates: regression_severity_from_str (13 tests: exact case-sensitive no-trim deserialize, alias, unknown-variant advertises canonical set not alias, non-string invalid-type, sequence parse) + regression_severity_filter_label (trim+lowercase label parse via RuleSuppressor). Const-eval of the derived SEVERITY_CANONICAL_WIRE_FORMS compiles clean. || REMAINING (deliberately NOT done): ALLOWED_FOR_VERIFY = &["python3","python","node"] (spec.rs:585) is a FAIL-CLOSED SECURITY allowlist (which script engines a detector TOML may execute for verification) — making it user-extensible Tier-B would let a malicious/rogue rules file add arbitrary interpreters (RCE surface). The "Tier-B" fix suggestion is WRONG for this one; it should stay hardcoded (or be an OPT-IN, audited config with a loud warning), NOT free Tier-B data. status=open-for-ALLOWED_FOR_VERIFY-decision (do NOT reflex-Tier-B a security allowlist).

crates/core/src/dedup.rs:158,291,438 | perf+coherence | P2 | sequential sort of all raw matches; is_decoder_alias_pair can't collapse nested decodes; None confidence treated as 0.0 not detector/global default | parallel sort; fix nested collapse; resolve None to default | status=PARTIALLY-VERIFIED (2026-07-09): (c) "None confidence → 0.0" (dedup.rs:435-446) is DOCUMENTED-DELIBERATE, not a bug — it's a winner-selection sort default within a TOTAL order (confidence→severity→detector_id→credential→hash→offset); the inline comment is explicit ("a deterministic ordering choice, not a swallowed value; the tiebreaks keep the order TOTAL so no finding is dropped"). It does NOT drop the None-confidence match — it just isn't chosen as PRIMARY (its locations still merge). "Resolve None to detector/global default" would change WHICH duplicate is primary (recall/attribution-affecting) with no correctness gain — INVALID as a fix. (b) "is_decoder_alias_pair can't collapse nested decodes" (dedup.rs:287) — REAL edge: two decoder-locations (base64(hex(secret))) both return is_decoder_location=true → the `==` guard returns false → not collapsed → possible dup. BUT fixing risks OVER-merging two genuinely-distinct secrets at nearby offsets; needs a concrete failing repro + over-merge analysis before touching (recall/precision-sensitive dedup). status=open-needs-repro-for-nested-decode-only. (a) parallel sort — perf micro, unmeasured; core has no rayon dep on this path (would need one). Deprioritized.
crates/core/src/credential.rs:279 | coherence | P3 | SensitiveString uses non-constant-time eq while Credential uses constant-time | constant-time compare for SensitiveString | IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-09 (validating): extracted the timing-safe comparison into ONE owner `pub(crate) fn constant_time_bytes_eq(a, b)` (was hand-rolled inline only in `Credential::eq`); both `Credential::eq` and `SensitiveString::eq` now route through it, so secret material can no longer leak bytes through branch timing in the SensitiveString path (it wraps Zeroizing secret strings). ONE-PLACE: the constant-time logic now has a single home instead of one impl being timing-safe and its twin not. 3 new inline tests (`constant_time_eq_tests`) assert helper correctness across empty/len-mismatch/first-byte/512-byte-equal-prefix-differing-suffix + both types' eq behavioral correctness. VALIDATED: `cargo test -p keyhog-core` 3/3 green. status=done. **CORRECTION 2026-07-09**: those inline tests VIOLATED the core `credential_no_inline_tests` + `no_inline_tests_in_src` gates (credential.rs is contractually inline-test-free) AND used `.unwrap()` (tripping `credential_no_unwrap_expect`) — the original "3/3 green" was a FILTERED run that never executed the gate binary, so the regression shipped latent and only surfaced on a full `--test all_tests` run 2026-07-09. FIXED: inline module DELETED, its coverage fully SUBSUMED + strengthened by the new `property/credential_contract.rs` (10k-proptest `prop_credential_eq_iff_bytes_eq` / `prop_sensitive_eq_iff_str_eq` cover the same equal-prefix/differing-suffix/len-mismatch/empty cases over arbitrary inputs); credential.rs now carries only a paraphrased pointer comment (the gate is a naive `!src.contains("#[cfg(test)]")` substring check, so the comment must AVOID the literal attribute text). Full core `--test all_tests` GREEN after the fix (2070+ pass; the lone straggler is the flaky perf timing test, see TEST/flaky-perf-dedup-subquadratic).
crates/core/src/dedup.rs:551 | coherence/security | P4 | `DedupKeyRef::equivalent` compares credential SECRET material via non-constant-time `&str == &str` (`self.credential == key.1.as_str()`), inconsistent with the `constant_time_bytes_eq` now used by `Credential::eq` + `SensitiveString::eq` (see 5999). Surfaced by the 5999 dedup audit. Two nuances that make it distinct from 5999: (a) it's a `HashMap` `Equivalent` impl, so `==` only runs AFTER a hash MATCH (`credential.hash` gates it), heavily masking the timing channel; (b) it's a HOT dedup-probe path where constant-time O(len) removes the `==` early-exit — a small but real cost on every collision probe. Threat model is weak: the scanner already holds the plaintext it is deduping. | DECIDE deliberately (do NOT reflex-fix): either route through `constant_time_bytes_eq` for full coherence with 5999 (accept the marginal post-hash-collision cost), OR document why hash-gated dedup equivalence is exempt. Measure the dedup-probe cost first. | status=open

# ── install.ps1 + CI slice (gemini 5865fae0) ──
install.ps1:233,285,295,1453,1460,1469,1483,1495,1500,1506,1511,1515,1520,1539,1549,1780,1786,1792,1815,1821,1827,1844,1850,1856,1917,1927,1930 | shell-safety | P2 | 27 exit-1 sites kill an interactive host | Fail helper (throw/return) + top-level try/catch preserving -File exit codes | status=CONFIRMED-REAL-blocked-on-pwsh (2026-07-09). VERIFY-BEFORE-FIX confirms this IS a genuine bug (UNLIKE the sh twin [[5914]] which is wontfix): install.ps1's PRIMARY documented install method is `iwr …/install.ps1 -useb | iex` (README.md:188, install.ps1:4) — `iex` evaluates the script IN THE CALLER'S SESSION (not a subshell like sh's `curl|sh`), so EVERY `exit 1` on a failure path (checksum mismatch, missing GPU sidecar, sig-verify fail, …) TERMINATES THE USER'S INTERACTIVE POWERSHELL SESSION — a real, bad-UX bug on the recommended Windows path. CORRECT FIX (the row's): a `Fail` helper that `throw`s (a terminating error `iex` propagates WITHOUT killing the session — user sees the message, session survives) instead of `exit`, plus a single top-level `try { … } catch { … }` that, when run via `-File keyhog-install.ps1`, maps the throw to the proper `$LASTEXITCODE`/exit code (`$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'` at :46 already makes non-terminating errors terminate). BLOCKER (real, environment): implementing a 27-site exit→throw refactor of the PRIMARY Windows installer safely REQUIRES a `pwsh` host to verify the `iex`-vs-`-File` terminating-error semantics — there is NO pwsh on this Linux dev host, and shipping an unverified breaking change to the installer violates the robustness contract (a feature isn't real until the shipped path works). NEXT: implement on a Windows/pwsh host (or add a pwsh CI job) with a sourced/iex-mode test that asserts a failure path does NOT kill the host session AND `-File` mode still returns the right exit code; optionally lock the shape via a static assertion in regression_install_static_analysis_gate.rs. Do NOT blind-edit the 27 sites without that verification.

install.ps1:453,485,673 | silent-fallback | P1 | Download-Asset/Invoke-RestMethod catch{} conflates network error with 404 (signature, checksum, sidecar) under -Insecure | check HTTP status before fallback | status=DONE (2026-07-09, Law 10). BOTH installers fixed to classify HTTP-404 vs transport failure before deciding "asset not published": (ps1) Get-HttpStatusCode helper (install.ps1:449) used at Verify-ReleaseSignature (479), Verify-Checksum (524), and Download-VerifiedGpuLiteralSidecar (~716) — a 404 says "not published / rebuild the workflow", any other transport error says "network/transport error $code ... a retry may succeed"; (sh) install.sh sidecar download (903-) now captures download_asset's exit code — curl exit 22 (HTTP >=400 = real 404) => "No GPU literal artifact sidecar was published ... rebuild the release workflow", any other non-zero => "network/transport failure, not a missing asset. A retry may succeed" — both still FAIL CLOSED (exit 1, no binary written). sh verify_release_signature/verify_checksum already carried the same exit-code classification (prior session). TESTED: edge_cases 6.4f (genuine 404 => "not published") + NEW 6.4f-net..net6 (curl exit 6 network error => honest transport message, does NOT claim "was published", does NOT say "rebuild the workflow", still exits 1, no binary) — all green (270/271; the one fail is pre-existing foreign 19.21 hook-wizard, not this). ps1 path API-reasoned (no pwsh on this host).

FIXED by extracting a buffer-reusing primitive `mark_gpu_presence_into(&mut [u64], &[u32])` (marks GPU presence bits + prefix-propagation straight into an existing bitmap) and calling it on the already-allocated `triggered` — eliminating one `Vec<u64>` allocation per GPU-union chunk. `triggered_patterns_from_gpu_presence` REIMPLEMENTED via the primitive (API + its 4 callers incl gpu_region_dispatch:378 + the 4 gpu_presence_bit_partition.rs tests preserved, behavior byte-identical — the old union `zip(gpu.iter()) *slot|=*bits` and the new `mark_gpu_presence_into(&mut triggered, &presence)` OR the SAME marked bits into the SAME-length buffer). VERIFICATION STATUS (honest): the refactor COMPILES CLEAN under ci-lean — the whole keyhog-scanner lib + its 5294-test unit binary built with ZERO errors including this change. BUT the dedicated `gpu_presence_bit_partition` regression tests are GPU-GATED (they also exercise `phase2_keyword_hints_from_gpu_presence`, which is `#[cfg(feature="gpu")]`) and therefore did NOT run under ci-lean (0 matched) — so they are NOT yet executed against this change; a `--features gpu` run was then executed on this 5090 host (target dir `/mnt/FlareTraining/santh-archive/cargo-target`) and the 4 gpu_presence_bit_partition tests GENUINELY RAN AND PASSED: `test result: ok. 4 passed; 0 failed` RC=0 (all 4 named tests visible with "ok": appended_gpu_presence_bits_become_phase2_keyword_hints_only, appended_gpu_presence_anchor_bits_are_absence_proofs_only, positioned_confirmed_anchor_bits_are_not_presence_bits, positioned_generic_keyword_bits_are_not_presence_bits). So the refactor is now REALLY verified behavior-preserving (not just compile-clean). [Process note: an earlier ledger draft of this row claimed "4/4 green ci-lean" off an RC=0 run that had actually matched 0 tests — the tests are gpu-gated and were filtered out; corrected to this genuine gpu-run result. RC-illusion caught: RC=0 with `0 passed` means the filter matched nothing, NOT that tests passed.] THOROUGH VERIFICATION (2026-07-10): beyond the 4 targeted partition tests, ran the FULL `--features gpu` keyhog-scanner lib suite on the 5090 → `5331 passed; 4 failed` (234s). The 4 failures are EXACTLY the pre-existing foreign [reconcile-red] regressions (challenging_ac_prefilter / entropy_run_admits / fragment_cache / decoded_reverse_placeholder), NOT introduced by this change; the 5331 passing INCLUDES all gpu-gated tests that exercise the GPU-union path this fix touches (55 more tests than the ci-lean run). So the `mark_gpu_presence_into` extraction is verified regression-free across the entire gpu surface. NOTE: those same 4 regressions fail IDENTICALLY under ci-lean (5276/4) AND gpu (5331/4) — they are FEATURE-INDEPENDENT foreign regressions, not gpu- or ci-lean-specific. REMAINING sub-items (still open, some foreign-blocked): (1) the DUP — GPU+SIMD both run the full CPU AC pass (collect_triggered_patterns_cpu) as the union base; deduping that is architectural, needs care (the CPU AC IS the recall-load-bearing union base per [[simd-trigger-union-recall-load-bearing]], so it can't just be dropped). (2) new_trigger_bitmap's per-chunk `Vec<u64>` on ALL backend paths (the CPU-base bitmap itself) — pooling it needs a thread-local/ScanState scratch threaded through callers (the bigger reuse-bitmaps change). (3) the SIMD error-fallback over-marks all patterns (:327 loop) — a rare degrade path. status=open (1 of ~3 sub-items done)
crates/scanner/src/engine/backend_triggered.rs:429,450,464 | perf | P3 | div/mod on every iteration in phase2_keyword_hints/always_anchor/mark_triggered_pattern | bitwise shifts | status=INVALID-noop (2026-07-10 code-verified): every operand is UNSIGNED (`literal_idx`/`pattern_idx`/`propagated_index` are `usize`, presence words `u32`) and every divisor is a compile-time-CONSTANT power of two (`/32 %32` @431/452, `/64 %64` @465-473). LLVM already lowers constant-power-of-two unsigned div/mod to `shr`/`and` — hand-writing `>>6`/`&63` emits BYTE-IDENTICAL machine code while DESTROYING readability (`literal_idx/32`,`%32` self-document "which 32-bit presence word, which bit"). (Manual shifts would only differ for SIGNED operands, where `/2ⁿ` needs round-toward-zero fixups — not the case here.) No change; the "optimization" is a compiler-guaranteed no-op and a Law-5/readability regression. NOTE: the REAL perf lever on this file is the sibling 6074 (per-chunk `Vec<u64>` bitmap allocation on the hot scan path), not these already-optimal index computations.

crates/sources/src/git/source.rs:283 | perf | P2 | std::io::BufReader::new(log_stdout).lines() reads log lines without an upper bound on line length | replace with read_capped_line to bound memory usage | status=done 2026-07-09: `GitCommitEnumerator` field `log_lines: Lines<BufReader<ChildStdout>>` → `log_reader: BufReader<ChildStdout>` + reusable `log_line_buf: Vec<u8>`; `next_id` now pulls via `super::read_capped_line(.., GIT_PLUMBING_LINE_BYTES=4096)` — bounds each commit-id line so a hostile repo emitting a multi-GB single line (no `\n`) can't OOM (a real object-id line is ~40-64 bytes). Over-cap line → LOUD `git_output_line_truncated_error` (Law-10 fail-closed), never a silent truncated-id scan — matches the sibling tag_messages + fsck plumbing readers (ONE-PLACE: same helper+cap+pattern). Removed now-unused `use std::io::BufRead`. Enumeration semantics preserved (final newline-less line still yielded; parse_git_object_id_line uses split_whitespace). Verified GREEN: regression_git_commit_scan (14) + regression_git_metadata_scan (16) — both directly exercise `GitCommitEnumerator::next_id` (the changed path). (A combined 5-target run timed OUT under CONTENTION when I foolishly ran it concurrently with a second target-dir build — the blob_drops git-subprocess tests starved; re-running commit_scan+metadata ALONE = instant GREEN, confirming the timeout was resource contention, not my change. LESSON: don't run two subprocess-heavy source builds concurrently even on isolated target dirs.)
crates/sources/src/filesystem/extract.rs:813 | silent-fallback | P2 | None => return in file content reading failure silently swallows read errors | propagate SourceError instead of returning silently | status=false 2026-07-09 (STALE — already fixed): the `_ =>` arm (extract.rs:852) does NOT silently swallow — it `emit(Err(SourceError::Other("failed to scan filesystem file '{path}': primary read path refused the file; file was not scanned")))`, surfacing a LOUD SourceError to the chunk-emit callback → coverage-incomplete → EXIT_SOURCE_FAILED (fail-closed, Law-10 compliant). The described "None => return silently" fix is exactly what the current code already does. Verified by reading extract.rs:807-860.

crates/sources/src/git/diff_parser.rs:145 | perf | P3 | unify diff parser processes lines with redundant allocations of string and byte buffers | reuse buffer allocations across lines parsed | status=done-PARTIAL+corrected (2026-07-10): the row's premise was OVERSTATED — the per-content-line HOT path is ALREADY zero-alloc (`parse_line` returns `UnifiedDiffEvent<'a>` borrowing the input slice: `AddedLine(&'a [u8])`, Metadata/Other/HunkStart carry no owned buffers; the `UnifiedDiffParser` struct holds only `in_hunk: bool`, no reusable buffers). Allocations occur ONLY on `+++ ` FILE-HEADER lines (once per file in a diff) and malformed-line error excerpts (rare) — not "across lines". FIXED the one genuine reducible alloc: `normalize_git_relative_path` was heap-allocating a `String` PER path component (`part.to_string()`) into a `Vec<String>` before `join`; switched to `Vec<&str>` borrowing from the input `&str`, so an N-component header path now does 1 `join` allocation instead of N+1. Covered by the existing `path_sanitizer_normalizes_without_allowing_escape` test (`./a/../b.txt`→`b.txt`, `../secret.txt`→None). Remaining per-header allocs (`from_utf8_lossy`, `replace('\\','/')`, `unescape_quoted_git_path_body` Vec<u8>) are once-per-file and semantically needed (lossy decode / separator normalization / octal-unescape) — not hot-path, not worth further churn. Net: hot path was already optimal; closed the only per-component wasted alloc. SECURITY TEST-DEPTH ADDED (2026-07-10): `git diff` output is attacker-controlled (a malicious repo), yet diff_parser.rs had only 6 example tests + zero adversarial fuzzing. Added 3 proptests (4000 cases each, 12k total, GREEN 9/0 RC=0) to the in-module suite: (1) `parse_line_is_total_on_arbitrary_diff_bytes` — the stateful parser never panics on any byte sequence across any state (`@@` hunk math, `+++`/quoted-path branch, octal unescape); (2) `sanitize_path_bytes_never_yields_an_escaping_path` — SECURITY INVARIANT proven over 4000 crafted inputs: any `Some(path)` is never absolute, never retains a `..` traversal component, never empty (a crafted `+++ b/../../etc/passwd` sanitizes to `None`, not an escaping write/scan path); (3) `unescape_quoted_git_path_body_is_total` — the octal/backslash unescaper's index arithmetic is panic-free on arbitrary bytes. Positive result: the sanitizer is robust against malicious diff output (Review Vector 12, adversarial on a security surface).

### §FIX-LANDED (2026-07-08, mid hunt-swarm) — drained from the fresh pool + local-reviewed:
- crates/sources/src/git/{mod,diff,history,source,tag_messages}.rs | git-env isolation | DONE: added ONE-PLACE `git_command()` helper (hermetic env: GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/SYSTEM -> null device, GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0), repointed all 9 spawn sites off bare `Command::new(git_bin()?)`. Neutralizes host gpgsign/credential.helper/hooksPath prompt-hangs (the latent CI-hang class). + regression test `git_command_sets_hermetic_environment` asserting the exact 3-var env set. keyhog-sources lib build+test GREEN (exit 0).
- crates/scanner/src/ascii_ci.rs | dead-code | DONE: removed unused `compact_ci_contains` (Law 11; 0 refs repo-wide).
- crates/scanner/src/ml_scorer/ml_weights.rs | coherence | DONE: rewrote stale 42->43 doc block; points INPUT_DIM->model_arch.rs and feature layout->ml_features.rs as ONE-PLACE owners.
- LOCAL-REVIEW CORRECTION: gemini 2da9af17 flagged watch.rs:519 `scan_file_surviving_detector_ids` as dead — FALSE POSITIVE (it is a live test helper, 4 call sites in watch/tests.rs). Kept. Confirms the no-unreviewed-agent-output law.

### §HUNT-SWARM-NATIVE (2026-07-08) — 5 native subagents, 0 findings ()

### §HUNT-SWARM-NATIVE (2026-07-08) — 5 native subagents, 245 findings (prefix-vocab-arch:41, perf-always-active-pool:50, betterleaks-steal:64, gpu-cpu-parity:42, test-depth-gaps:48)
rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml:49 | Orphaned prefix — no backing detector | P1 | `xoxa-`, `xoxr-`, `xoxs-` (lines 49-51) are in the multiline gate and in `rules/known-prefixes.toml` (lines 18-20) but NO detector TOML contains these as a keyword or regex. The multiline reassembly gate fires on them and `known_prefix_confidence_floor` lifts any candidate starting with them to 0.8 — but AC/regex never matches, so the candidate is ultimately dropped. Result: the gate wastes compute and the 0.8 floor is never actually applied because no finding is emitted. | Either add a `slack-legacy-workspace-token.toml` detector covering `xoxa-`/`xoxr-`/`xoxs-` regexes (gitleaks-style), or remove these three entries from both `multiline_secret_prefixes.toml` and `known-prefixes.toml` so the gate accurately reflects backed prefixes. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]
rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml:64 | Orphaned prefix — no backing detector | P1 | `HRKU-` at line 64 is in multiline and in `known-prefixes.toml` (line 46) but the sole Heroku detector (`detectors/heroku-api-key.toml`) uses UUID format with keyword-context anchors — its regex `[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-…` never starts with `HRKU-`. No detector produces a finding that starts with `HRKU-`. crates/scanner/tests/unit/checksum.rs:186 | Factually wrong comment causing unchecked pk_ prefix binding | P3 | The comment at line 186 states `DELIBERATE OMISSION — Stripe pk_live_/pk_test_: the validator structurally recognises publishable keys, but NO detector surfaces them (a publishable key is PUBLIC, not a secret)`. This is factually wrong: both `detectors/clerk-api-key.toml` and `detectors/clerk-frontend-api-key.toml` emit findings for `pk_(?:live/test)_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}` — Clerk publishable keys, which ARE NOT Stripe publishable keys. The test at line 209 skips all `pk_` prefixes, so no binding test verifies that `pk_live_`/`pk_test_` in `STRIPE_KEY_PREFIXES` (checksum/prefixes.rs:46) corresponds to the correct detector. A Clerk `pk_live_` token goes through the Stripe structural validator (gets `StructurallyValid`), and two duplicate Clerk detectors exist with no cross-check. | Correct the comment to accurately state that Clerk detectors DO surface `pk_live_`/`pk_test_`. Add binding rows for `("pk_live_", "clerk-frontend-api-key")` and `("pk_test_", "clerk-frontend-api-key")` and add a negative test asserting the Stripe validator returns `StructurallyValid` (not `Valid`) for Clerk-format tokens. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]
crates/scanner/tests/detector_primary_regex_dedup_ratchet.rs:43 | Duplicate detector pair — identical regex, neither consolidated | P3 | `clerk-api-key` and `clerk-frontend-api-key` share the byte-identical primary regex `pk_(?:live/test)_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}` (confirmed by the ratchet test at line 43 and per-detector regression tests). The ratchet comment names `clerk-api-key` as the survivor-to-be-removed but neither detector has been dropped. Two detector ids fire on every Clerk token; SARIF/JSON output contains two identical findings; keyhog baseline files that suppress one id still see the other. | Remove `detectors/clerk-api-key.toml`. Add a `# Renamed from: clerk-api-key` migration note to `clerk-frontend-api-key.toml`. Update the dedup ratchet test to remove this pair from `KNOWN_DUP_PAIRS`. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]
crates/scanner/tests/detector_primary_regex_dedup_ratchet.rs:51 | Duplicate detector pair — identical regex for HuggingFace tokens | P3 | `huggingface-org-token` and `huggingface-user-token` share the byte-identical primary regex `hf_[a-zA-Z0-9]{34,}` (ratchet line 51). The `hf_` prefix is tracked as a single entry in `known-prefixes.toml` and `multiline_secret_prefixes.toml` — the org/user distinction is not encodable in the prefix shape. Every HuggingFace token produces two findings. This duplication inflates FP counts in bench and causes double-reporting in SARIF output. | Consolidate into a single `huggingface-token` detector. Remove one toml. Update the ratchet's `KNOWN_DUP_PAIRS` accordingly. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]

The entry misleads `known_prefix_confidence_floor` into applying the 0.8 floor to any text starting with `vercel_` even if no Vercel detector fires. | Replace the bare `"vercel_"` entry with the precise `"vercel_v2_"` prefix that actually appears in a detector regex, or remove the entry if the context-anchored detector makes a prefix floor redundant. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]

rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml:1 | No SIMD coverage for SendGrid in multiline — split-secret false negative | P3 | `SG.` is in `HOT_PATTERNS` (`simdsieve_prefilter.rs:84`) and `rules/known-prefixes.toml` (line 25) but absent from `rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml`. crates/scanner/src/secret_prefixes.rs:78 | Hardcoded shadow copy of Tier-B file — blocks single-step prefix additions | P3 | The `LEGACY` const (lines 78–104) inside the test module is a byte-identical, hand-maintained copy of the 25 entries in `rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml`, paired with a hard count assertion at line 121 (`assert_eq!(multiline_secret_prefixes().len(), 25)`). Adding any new prefix to the Tier-B TOML requires two additional edits in Rust — updating `LEGACY` and the count — otherwise the tests fail. This means the TOML is effectively not Tier-B: it has a compile-time shadow that must track it in lockstep. The file also notes `LEGACY` was the old inline array — the inline copy was meant to be deleted after migration, not kept as the new gate. | Replace the `loaded_reproduces_the_legacy_list_exactly` test with a test that checks a PROPERTY (no duplicates, all ASCII, minimum count >= prior baseline) rather than exact equality against a hardcoded shadow. Drop the `LEGACY` const. Keep the `prefix_count_is_exactly_twenty_five` ratchet only if new-prefix additions must be intentional — but rename it to `prefix_count_is_at_least_N` so it can grow without code change. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]
crates/scanner/src/checksum/prefixes.rs:1 | Architecture — five independent hand-maintained prefix vocabularies with no derive step | P3 | The secret-prefix vocabulary is split across five independently maintained locations: (1) detector TOMLs under `detectors/`, (2) `crates/scanner/src/checksum/prefixes.rs`, (3) `HOT_PATTERNS` in `simdsieve_prefilter.rs`, (4) `rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml`, (5) `rules/known-prefixes.toml`. The only cross-location binding test is `checksum_prefixes_are_backed_by_their_detector` in `tests/unit/checksum.rs`, which covers only location 2 vs 1 for checksum families. There is no test or derive step that ensures a prefix newly added to a detector TOML is propagated to locations 3, 4, and 5. Every drift finding in this audit traces to this structural gap. | Introduce a build-time or test-time derive step that reads all detector TOMLs, extracts their distinctive literal prefixes (from the first N chars of each regex or an explicit `prefix = ` field), and asserts they appear in known-prefixes.toml. Alternatively, make known-prefixes.toml the single source of truth and have the checksum module, HOT_PATTERNS slot builder, and multiline gate all reference it at startup, failing closed if a prefix is missing. | status=SUBSUMED-by-DR-332 (2026-07-10): this row describes the SAME "N independently hand-maintained prefix vocabularies with no derive step" architecture as the DR-332 head row (see the `open-architecture` row above, id DR-332). Per the ONE-PLAN rule these must not be two competing plans for one body of work — DR-332 is the single owner: it carries the feasibility analysis (derive from detector `keywords`, same mechanism as `derive_assignment_keywords`), the verified-current absent-prefix list, the foreign-blocked status, and the scope-decision (batch the per-prefix adds atop the derive step, never piecemeal). The only detail this row adds that DR-332 should absorb: the cross-location binding tests that DO exist today are `checksum_prefixes_are_backed_by_their_detector` (location 2↔1, DR-321) and `hot_patterns_backed_by_their_detector` (location 3↔detector, DR-322) — but locations 4 and 5 (`rules/multiline_secret_prefixes.toml` and `rules/known-prefixes.toml`) have NO detector-binding guard, so a detector prefix can be silently absent from the split-secret reassembly gate or the known-prefixes floor, which is the structural root of the drift children (6194-6228). Track under DR-332; do not action 6219 separately. | [prefix-vocab-arch]
detectors/slack-app-token.toml:16 | Per-detector min_confidence workaround masking a missing known-prefix entry | P4 | `slack-app-token.toml` at line 16 explicitly sets `min_confidence = 0.5` with the comment: 'xapp- is a distinctive 4-segment vendor prefix, but it is NOT in the scanner's KNOWN_PREFIXES floor list'. This is a per-detector patch for a vocabulary gap. Identical workarounds appear in `slack-config-token.toml` line 20 for `xoxe-`. These workarounds diverge from the standard floor mechanism and are invisible to callers examining KNOWN_PREFIXES. | Fix the root cause: add `xapp-` and `xoxe-` to `rules/known-prefixes.toml`. Then remove the `min_confidence` overrides from both TOML files so the standard floor path is the only floor path. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]
rules/known-prefixes.toml:1 | No HOT_PATTERNS slot for high-frequency PyPI prefix | P4 | `pypi-` is in `rules/known-prefixes.toml` (line 28), `checksum/prefixes.rs` (line 36 as `PYPI_API_TOKEN`), and `detectors/pypi-api-token.toml`, but absent from `HOT_PATTERNS`. PyPI macaroons are long (100+ chars) and structurally distinctive. The SIMD prefilter would accelerate them significantly given the token length. | Add a `pypi-` entry to the `define_hot_pattern_tables!` macro in `simdsieve_prefilter.rs`. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]
crates/scanner/src/checksum/prefixes.rs:45 | STRIPE_KEY_PREFIXES includes pk_ which also matches Clerk — cross-service validator confusion | P4 | `STRIPE_KEY_PREFIXES` at line 45-47 includes `"pk_live_"` and `"pk_test_"`. The `StripeTokenValidator` (stripe.rs) dispatches on these prefixes. Both Clerk detectors (`clerk-api-key.toml`, `clerk-frontend-api-key.toml`) emit `pk_live_`/`pk_test_` findings. A Clerk token like `pk_live_ABC…32chars` therefore goes through the Stripe validator and comes back `StructurallyValid` even though it is a Clerk token, not a Stripe publishable key. While `StructurallyValid` is currently neutral, the `STRIPE_KEY_PREFIXES` const name misleads future readers into thinking all `pk_live_` tokens are Stripe. | Rename the array to something service-agnostic (e.g. `STRIPE_SHAPED_KEY_PREFIXES`) and add a comment explaining that `pk_live_`/`pk_test_` are shared between Stripe (publishable) and Clerk (publishable) token shapes. Add a test asserting a Clerk-format `pk_live_` token returns `StructurallyValid`, not `Invalid`, from the validator. | status=open [prefix-vocab-arch]
detectors/gitlab-package-registry-token.toml:44 | Duplicate detector pair — same primary regex for gldt- shape | P4 | Both `gitlab-deploy-token.toml` and `gitlab-package-registry-token.toml` share the primary regex `gldt-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{20,}` (tracked in the ratchet at `detector_primary_regex_dedup_ratchet.rs:47`). `gldt-` is absent from `checksum/prefixes.rs`, HOT_PATTERNS, `known-prefixes.toml`, and `multiline_secret_prefixes.toml`. rules/known-prefixes.toml:23 | Overly broad catch-all prefix masking missing specific entries | P4 | `sk-` at line 23 is a 3-character catch-all that matches every OpenAI variant (`sk-proj-`, `sk-svcacct-`, `sk-admin-`), the legacy `sk-` form, and any other `sk-` prefixed credential. Because `known_prefix_body` uses longest-match, the catch-all is shadowed correctly for known longer prefixes, but any new `sk-XYZ-` form from any vendor will silently receive the 0.8 floor without being explicitly listed. This is an implicit-enrollment side effect that can lift FP strings incorrectly. | Remove or restrict `"sk-"` to only cover documented OpenAI legacy keys. detectors/huggingface-user-token.toml:1 | Duplicate detector pair — same primary regex for hf_ shape | P4 | `huggingface-user-token.toml` and `huggingface-org-token.toml` share byte-identical primary regex `hf_[a-zA-Z0-9]{34,}` (ratchet line 51). crates/scanner/src/simdsieve_prefilter.rs:54 | No SIMD HOT_PATTERNS slot for GitLab PATs or runner tokens | P4 | Across the GitLab family: `glpat-` (personal access token) is in checksum/prefixes.rs and multiline but not HOT_PATTERNS. `glrt-` (runner token) is in checksum/prefixes.rs and known-prefixes but not HOT_PATTERNS or multiline. `glcbt-` (CI build token) is in checksum/prefixes.rs and known-prefixes but not HOT_PATTERNS or multiline. `gloas-`, `glsoat-`, `gldt-` have no entry in checksum, HOT_PATTERNS, multiline, or known-prefixes. crates/scanner/src/checksum/prefixes.rs:56 | all_checksum_prefixes() used in binding test but does not cover non-checksum high-value prefixes | P5 | `all_checksum_prefixes()` at line 56 gathers only the families with bespoke CRC validators. This means a value that passed GENERIC_SECRET's entropy floor could still be rejected if the owning detector's `entropy_high` is higher (a detector calibrated for higher-entropy credentials). (2) If the owning detector has a LOWER floor, values that fail GENERIC_SECRET but pass the owning floor should be lifted. The current code implements both behaviors but the `else` branch at 416 double-gates case (1), potentially rejecting values the GENERIC_SECRET gate would pass. Add a contract test that verifies a value with entropy = GENERIC_SECRET_FLOOR + epsilon is emitted when the owning detector has floor < GENERIC_SECRET_FLOOR. | status=open [perf-always-active-pool]

crates/scanner/src/engine/scan_filters.rs:128 | Performance / is_entropy_candidate_byte 15-way Branch in Hot Scan | P2 | `is_entropy_candidate_byte(b)` at line 145 uses a `matches!` macro over 15 byte values. Docs: configuration.md knobs table row. core tests 34/34 green, scanner+cli check green. Default path byte-identical (serde default = old const) so recall-neutral, no bench needed.) CORRECTED PATH (if betterleaks TokenEfficiency parity on named detectors is still wanted): apply BPE ONLY to the narrow subset of named detectors that are NOT `is_service_anchored`/`is_structural_password_slot` AND whose captures are free-form (the loose hex/base64-fallback patterns the finding cites) — i.e. gate it behind the SAME `!bypass_shape_gates` + non-anchored predicate the Tier-B randomness floor already uses, NOT a blanket `!is_generic_or_entropy`. That is a much narrower, bench-gated change (mirror + CredData per-detector attribution, must show ZERO named-detector TP loss). CRUCIALLY the row's own suggested fix IS ALREADY SHIPPED for the case that matters: the structural-password-slot detectors (url-credentials/sql-password/cli-password-flag) are STRONG-anchor ⇒ `apply_tier_b=false` ⇒ they SKIP the Tier-B randomness floor entirely, so the dominant SHORT all-lowercase/`i8cr1w!`-style random passwords the MIN_ALPHA floor would decline DO surface via those detectors (see the 2026-06-29 strong-anchor family work in [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]]: `is_confident_dictionary_word` gate + `$VAR`/`changeme` placeholder gates carry precision instead of the length floor). So `xk3p1`/`i8cr1w!` under a genuine password context ALREADY surface. RESIDUAL (genuinely open, small): short random passwords in a GENERIC `password=` assignment with NO strong structural anchor still hit the floor — but that's the precision-sensitive generic path where lowering the floor re-floods (the KH-L-0413 ceiling measured +3554 FP for the unconditional lift). The `^[a-zA-Z_.-]+$` all-alpha reject port is a complementary idea but needs a CredData A/B (generic path is FP-heavy). NOT a clean bug — a deliberate precision/recall boundary that is already relaxed exactly where it's safe (strong anchors). | [betterleaks-steal]
crates/scanner/src/suppression/token_randomness.rs:91 | Bigram model covers only ASCII a-z; non-ASCII passwords always fail-safe (stay suppressed) | P3 | `for &byte in bytes` at line 91 only increments `alpha` for `byte.is_ascii_alphabetic()` and only scores bigrams over `b'a'..=b'z'`. BUT the row's "short-term" fix (flip non-ASCII-short to random) is PRECISION-UNSAFE: a real Cyrillic/Greek/accented DICTIONARY word or natural-language identifier would then be scored "random" → surfaced as a secret → FP flood on i18n source, the mirror image of the ASCII fail-safe's whole purpose. Also, like 6287, the case that matters (a genuine non-ASCII PASSWORD) is ALREADY covered by the strong-anchor structural-password-slot detectors (url-credentials/sql/cli) which skip the Tier-B randomness floor entirely — so a non-ASCII password under `://user:пароль@` or `IDENTIFIED BY 'κωδικός'` surfaces regardless of the ASCII-only bigram model. RESIDUAL: non-ASCII random passwords in a GENERIC assignment with no strong anchor. The SOUND fix is NOT the flip — it is a Unicode-aware randomness model (NFD-normalize accented chars to ASCII base for bigram scoring, OR a real BPE/tiktoken path for non-ASCII-dominant values), which is genuine ML work ([[ml-model-must-train-on-real-distribution]]), bench-gated on both corpora, NOT a one-line short-term flip. Downgrade the "short-term flip" suggestion — it trades a narrow recall gap for a broad i18n precision regression. | [betterleaks-steal]

crates/core/src/spec.rs:502 | body_contains / body_not_contains / json_path verify fields implemented but zero detectors use them | P3 | `SuccessSpec` has `body_contains: Option<String>`, `body_not_contains: Option<String>`, and `json_path: Option<String>` (lines 502-511). The verifier implements them in `crates/verifier/src/verify/response.rs:112-131`. But `rg -l 'body_contains\/body_not_contains\/json_path' detectors/` returns 0 files — 345 detectors with verify blocks use only `status: 200`. betterleaks uses allowlist Regexes on response body to confirm credentials via pattern matching (e.g., `active: true` in Stripe response). Start with detectors that currently have false-positive verify results due to status-only checks. | status=open [betterleaks-steal] — PARTIAL 2026-07-09: the IMPLEMENTATION half is now TRUTH-PINNED. New `regression_success_spec_body_json_matcher.rs` (9 GREEN) closes the "wired but unexercised → silent drift ships unnoticed" risk on `evaluate_success`'s body/json gates: body_contains/body_not_contains substring semantics (incl the `deactivated`-lacks-`active` subtlety — a wrong example the test itself caught pre-merge), json_path presence+non-null (null/miss→false, `0` is non-null→true), json_path+equals exact contract-string compare (String→raw, Bool→"true"/"false", Number→decimal), invalid-JSON→hard ResponseContractError (not silent-pass), full AND-combination of all gates, empty-spec→unconditional, + a str::contains differential sweep. REMAINING (the actual [betterleaks-steal] data task, bench-sensitive, unchanged): POPULATE body_contains/json_path in high-FP detector TOMLs so verification confirms via response body, not just 200-status — a detection-tuning change needing verify-corpus validation, distinct from the now-proven engine.
crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_generic.rs:21 | GENERIC_RE_ASSIGNMENT_TAIL optional suffix list missing 'id', 'num', 'data' patterns | P3 | The ASSIGNMENT_TAIL regex `(?:[._-]?(?:key/base/value/val/string/str/enc/raw/b64)){0,2}` captures optional suffixes between a keyword and the `=`. REFINE THE FIX two ways: (1) SPLIT the candidates by FP risk, do NOT add them as one blob — `hash`/`hex`/`iv`/`salt` are crypto-material contexts (secret-adjacent, likely recall-positive at low FP cost) while `id`/`num`/`data` are generic non-credential suffixes (`request_id`/`trace_id`/`item_num`/`config_data` — high FP risk on the FP-heavy generic path, only partly mitigated by the UUID shape gate); bench the two groups SEPARATELY so a good crypto-suffix add isn't blocked by a bad generic-suffix add. (2) The row says "mirror corpus" but mirror ≠ CredData — mirror won't show the FP cost that dominates this decision ([[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]]: generic path is `API` 1335 FP/1 TP); the gate MUST run CredData with `bench analyze` per-detector FP attribution ([[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]]), not just mirror recall. Sibling of the other GENERIC_RE-broadening rows (6319 vendor-suffix, 6333 charset) — same precision-sensitive bench discipline. | [betterleaks-steal]

benchmarks/reports/category-recall.md:24 | JWK/JWKS category: 0.025 recall, 77 FNs, no dedicated detector | P3 | `JWK` category has 2 TP / 77 FN with recall 0.025; no competitor is listed as best, meaning all fail. detectors/generic-api-key.toml:19 | betterleaks line-level allowlist regex (RegexTarget='line') has no keyhog equivalent | P3 | betterleaks supports `RegexTarget: "line"` in allowlists, meaning the allowlist regex matches against the ENTIRE source line rather than just the captured secret value. This means `mykey=value` (no separator) is not captured by the vendor arm. Only `my_key`, `my-key`, `my.key` are captured. In practice, compound identifiers without a separator like `apikey` are handled by the explicit keyword list, but single-word compound forms like `authkey` or `userkey` that are NOT in the explicit list are missed. | Make the last separator optional: `r"[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:[._-][a-z0-9]+){0,2}[._-]?(?:key/secret/token)"` and add a minimum length constraint on the prefix (>=3 chars) to avoid false triggers on single-char prefixes. | status=open [betterleaks-steal]
crates/scanner/src/suppression/token_randomness.rs:62 | RANDOM_LOGPROB_THRESHOLD = -6.85 hardcoded with no per-detector override | P3 | The `RANDOM_LOGPROB_THRESHOLD = -6.85` is a single `const` across all detector paths. betterleaks' entropy threshold is configurable per rule (`Entropy: 3.5` in generic.go:18). A single keyhog threshold must fit all detectors — but a low-entropy password detector (like `generic-keyword-secret`) and a high-entropy API key detector (like `generic-api-key`) have fundamentally different randomness distributions, so one threshold cannot be optimal for both. | Add `bigram_random_threshold: Option<f32>` to `DetectorSpec` and thread it through the `TokenRandomness` API; `None` uses the existing const default. This enables per-detector calibration alongside the existing per-detector `entropy_floor` field. | status=open-SPECULATIVE-no-current-need (2026-07-10): the mechanism ask (add an `Option<f32>` Tier-A override defaulting to the const) is architecturally reasonable and mirrors the existing per-detector `entropy_floor`. BUT the premise "one threshold cannot be optimal for both" is UNVALIDATED for the bigram model specifically: −6.85 separates English-word-structure (mean bigram logprob) from random-adjacency structure — a LANGUAGE property, not an entropy-magnitude property — so unlike an entropy FLOOR (which genuinely differs between a low-entropy password and a high-entropy API key), the English-vs-random bigram boundary is roughly detector-INDEPENDENT. Per [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]] the −6.85 threshold was tuned to a MEASURED both-axes win (CredData R 0.181→0.250 AND P 0.600→0.665, mirror P held) and NO detector has since been shown to need a different value. So this is a speculative Tier-A capability with no demonstrated demand — do NOT thread the plumbing until a bench shows a specific detector wants a different bigram threshold (adding an unused Option per detector is dead Tier-A surface, Review Vector 11/Law 2 concern). Keep as a documented future knob; act only when a per-detector A/B proves a divergent optimum. | [betterleaks-steal]

crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_generic.rs:439 | BPE gate on generic path is #[cfg(feature='entropy')] — disabled on ci-lean builds | P3 | The BPE gate at line 438 is wrapped in `#[cfg(feature = "entropy")]`. Confirmed: the real ci-lean TEST build (ci.yml:538) uses `--features ml,entropy,decode,multiline,simdsieve,simd` — entropy IN — so the BPE gate (phase2_generic.rs:438, `#[cfg(feature="entropy")]`) IS active in every real build. (The `for f in … ci-lean` loop at ci.yml:516 is a per-feature standalone COMPILE check, not the test config.) RESIDUAL: a deliberate no-entropy minimal build compiles out the BPE gate — but that's EXPECTED feature-opt-out (the whole entropy/tiktoken subsystem, including any BPE threshold plumbing, compiles out together; there is NO "BPE threshold configured but silently ignored" path because they're gated as a unit), NOT a silent Law-10 degrade. The `compile_error!`/`warn!` nicety is defensible only IF a BPE-threshold config field can be SET independently of the entropy feature (verify: if the field itself is `#[cfg(feature="entropy")]`, there's nothing to warn about). P5 cosmetic, not a precision bug. | [betterleaks-steal]

crates/scanner/src/suppression/token_randomness.rs:74 | MIN_DISTINCT_LETTERS=3 fails for 6-char passwords with 2 repeated letters followed by symbols | P4 | `MIN_DISTINCT_LETTERS = 3` — a value needs at least 3 distinct ASCII letters to get a `random` verdict. A symbol-bearing token like `aa$#bb!` (7 chars, not a `<>` template, not a confident English word) PASSES all three and surfaces via url-credentials REGARDLESS of MIN_DISTINCT_LETTERS — so the claimed FN does not occur for the named detectors. (Sibling of 6287/6288: same "the strong-anchor family already bypasses this floor" pattern.) RESIDUAL to CONFIRM (api.rs is foreign-dirty — read-only checked the design intent, not line-traced the exact gate order): verify by grep that `is_service_anchored`/`apply_tier_b=false` detectors do not reach `has_low_letter_diversity`; if confirmed, this row is a non-issue for its named detectors and should close. P4, precision-deliberate on the generic path. | [betterleaks-steal]

benchmarks/reports/recall-gap.md:44 | Token:UUID category: 0.456 recall vs betterleaks 0.923 — UUID-bodied token assignments missed | P3 | `Token:UUID` category has 92 FNs, recall 0.456 vs betterleaks 0.923. A `TOKEN_LIST=<uuid>` env assignment falls into the `UuidV4OrSubstring` suppression (gates.rs:212-217): the whole-value UUID gate is only released when `canonical_lift && value_is_exact_uuid`. The `canonical_lift` requires `same_line_credential_assignment && canonical_shape_lift_allowed` which requires a key-material anchor. `TOKEN` is in the entropy compact keywords but not in `KEY_MATERIAL_ANCHORS` in entropy/scanner.rs. | Add `"token"` to the `key_material` list in `rules/credential-keywords.toml` (it is already in `compact` but not in `key_material`). This promotes a `token = <uuid>` assignment to key-material status, activating the canonical lift. | status=open [betterleaks-steal]
detectors/generic-api-key.toml:19 | betterleaks all-alpha allowlist (`^[a-zA-Z_.-]+$`) — keyhog has no equivalent drop for all-alphabetic values | P2 | betterleaks generic.go line 44-50 has a first allowlist entry: `Regexes: []*regexp.Regexp{regexp.MustCompile("^[a-zA-Z_.-]+$")}` — any value that is ENTIRELY alphabetic letters, underscores, dots, or dashes is suppressed (it cannot be a secret since secrets need digits or special chars). This is a cheap, high-precision FP filter. keyhog's `PureIdentifier` gate in `phase2_generic_shape.rs:188-203` covers all-alphanumeric-plus-underscore but not all-alphabetic-plus-dots-dashes. The `looks_like_pure_identifier` check at `shape/mod.rs` may not cover dot-separated multi-segment values. | Add an early gate in `generic_value_shape_rejected` that rejects values matching `^[a-zA-Z_.-]+$` (all letters, underscores, dots, dashes — no digits, no special chars). crates/scanner/src/suppression/shape/prose.rs | English-prose suppression checks are keyword-anchor-gated but betterleaks' all-alpha filter is unconditional | P3 | keyhog's `looks_like_english_prose` gate in `phase2_entropy/gates.rs:109` is only applied when `!keyword_is_credential_anchor(&entropy_match.keyword)`. This combines betterleaks' all-alpha filter with keyhog's stronger BPE discrimination. | status=open [betterleaks-steal]
crates/scanner/src/suppression/api.rs:784 | stopwords matching is case-insensitive substring but betterleaks' ContainsStopWord is also stemming-aware | P4 | `contains_ignore_ascii_case(credential, word)` at line 788 checks if the credential CONTAINS the stopword as a case-insensitive substring. betterleaks' `ContainsStopWord` in its config also handles stemmed forms (e.g. `"analysis"` / `"analysi"` — note the truncated forms in stopwords.go like `"analysi"`, `"librarie"`, `"progres"`, `"classe"`) to match both singular and plural. keyhog's stopword check would miss `"analyses"` but catch `"analysis"` if `"analysis"` were in the list. | When populating the stopword list from betterleaks' corpus, preserve the truncated stem forms (e.g. `"analysi"`, `"librarie"`, `"clas"`) rather than converting to full English words. crates/scanner/src/suppression/token_randomness.rs:83 | Bigram model scores ALL alphabetic runs even if they are in base64 encoding (a-f only = hex FP risk) | P4 | `is_confident_dictionary_word` at line 193-206 includes a `has_non_hex_letter` guard to prevent hex keys from being misread as English. A pure base64 value like `abcdefabcdef12` (chars a-f only) would score poorly as English (low bigram probability for sequences like `ab`, `cd`, `ef`) and incorrectly be treated as a random token, lifting the identifier gate. In practice this is suppressed by the base64-blob shape gate earlier, but the interaction is subtle. | Add the `has_non_hex_letter` guard to `is_random_token` as well, mirroring `is_confident_dictionary_word:199`. crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_generic.rs:30 | GENERIC_RE_VENDOR_SUFFIX_ARM max 2 intermediate segments misses 3-part service identifiers | P4 | The vendor suffix arm `[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:[._-][a-z0-9]+){0,2}[._-](?:key/secret/token)` allows at most 2 intermediate segments. All three BROADEN the same regex on the GENERIC path, which [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]] identifies as the FP-heavy lever (`API` 1335 FP / 1 TP, `Key` FP 2537) — so the "FPs shouldn't increase" hope is exactly the thing a bench must PROVE, not assume (Review Vector 1: measure first). VERIFIED 2026-07-10: the regex is still `{0,2}` (unchanged). DISCIPLINE: evaluate 6300+6319 TOGETHER under ONE CredData A/B (`bench analyze` per-detector FP attribution, not just mirror aggregate — see [[bench-isolation-stale-baseline-trap]]); land the pair + 6341's test as one bench-gated change, never piecemeal. Do not resolve 6300/6341 independently of this head.

crates/scanner/src/adjudicate/entropy.rs:149 | Entropy fallback placeholder gate skips UUID shape check before calling example-suppression path | P4 | `entropy_fallback_example_suppression_stage` at line 149 checks `is_known_example_credential(value)` first. benchmarks/reports/recall-gap.md:41 | Secret:Key category 0.312 recall vs Titus 0.812 — structural gap in key-bearing secret detector | P3 | `Secret:Key` shows keyhog at recall 0.312 (10 TP / 22 FN) vs Titus 0.812. crates/scanner/src/confidence/penalties.rs | Confidence penalties don't account for BPE-borderline values (chars/token near threshold) | P4 | The confidence penalty system in `confidence/penalties.rs` applies entropy-based, shape-based, and placeholder-based penalties but has no BPE-proximity penalty. A low BPE penalty could signal borderline word-like findings. | Add a BPE proximity penalty: if `chars_per_token(value) > 1.8 && chars_per_token(value) <= ENTROPY_BPE_MAX_CHARS_PER_TOKEN`, apply a small confidence penalty (e.g., `-0.05` per 0.1 above 1.8). This surfaces borderline cases with lower confidence rather than suppressing them entirely. | status=open [betterleaks-steal]

benchmarks/reports/recall-gap.md:29 | BASE64 Private Key: 0 recall, 14 FNs, betterleaks catches some | P3 | `BASE64 Private Key` category has 0 TP / 14 FN. crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_generic.rs:21 | GENERIC_RE_ASSIGNMENT_TAIL value charset excludes `!`, `@`, `#`, `$`, `%`, `^`, `&`, `*` — limits password recall | P3 | The ASSIGNMENT_TAIL value capture group `([a-zA-Z0-9/+=_.:!@#$%^&*-]{8,128})` includes `!@#$%^&*` — these special chars ARE in the charset. But `/`, `\`, `` ` ``, `~`, `?`, `;`, `(`, `)`, `[`, `]`, `{`, `}` are NOT included. Genuinely absent: `\` `` ` `` `~` `?` `;` `()[]{}`. So there is NO code change worth making — the value class is already broad, and further broadening the GENERIC (FP-heavy: [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]]) path's value class is PRECISION-NEGATIVE without an entropy-compensated bench. crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_generic.rs:67 | generic_keyword_alternation: regex ordering puts shorter alternatives first due to vec sort | P4 | At line 50, keywords are sorted `longest-first` before joining into the alternation. But betterleaks uses RE2 (`go-re2`) which is not alternation-order-sensitive. Rust's `regex` crate uses leftmost-first semantics: if `auth_token` and `auth` are both in the alternation, and a line contains `auth_token=<val>`, the longer `auth_token` wins only if it's listed before `auth`. DET-RED-ml-saturation-2026-07-09: crates/scanner/tests/regression_ml_scorer_features.rs:446 | detection-truth/model-quality | P2 | `high_entropy_token_scores_above_obvious_placeholder` FAILS ("strong 0 must outrank placeholder 0") in BOTH ci-lean AND --features gpu — NOT caused by my DET-1 coherence edits (the test is untouched; I only fixed comments + ADDED a feature-42 test). ROOT-CAUSED 2026-07-09: `ml_score` -> `ml_scorer::score` -> `forward_pass` -> `sigmoid(logit)`; the DET-1-retrained model emits a logit so negative that sigmoid saturates to EXACTLY 0.0 for BOTH a high-entropy 32-char token AND an all-'x' placeholder when the context is empty OR a GENERIC `api_key = <value>` assignment. This is CONSISTENT WITH DET-1's DESIGN (generic-keyword+opaque-value = identifier => reject/~0; only a SERVICE-named / known-prefix context is credited — the exact split DET-1 shipped). DECISIVE DIAGNOSTICS: (a) the model is CORRECTLY LOADED — `ml_weights::model()` FAILS CLOSED BY `panic!` on any weights-width mismatch (ml_weights.rs:71); no panic occurred, so weights.bin is the valid 43-feature width (this is NOT a silent weights-load failure). (b) the model is NOT all-zero — the DET-1 landing measured CredData F1 0.4816 / P 0.7688 on the shipped binary, which REQUIRES score>threshold for real TPs in crediting contexts. So the test encodes a PRE-DET-1 assumption ("raw entropy alone ranks a token above a placeholder") the retrained model deliberately rejects. Two candidate fixes were tried + REVERTED (empty-ctx, then `api_key=` ctx — both still 0/0 because api_key is a GENERIC role word), proving discrimination needs a SERVICE/prefix crediting context I must not trial-fit blind. RESOLUTION (detection-owner call, like tree-red #1/#2, BENCH-GATED — do NOT reflex-weaken, Law 6/9): either (i) re-express the ranking under a genuinely-crediting context (service-named e.g. "stripe_api_key = <token>" so feature 42 fires, or a builtin-prefixed token so feature 14/15 fires) and assert strong>placeholder there — VERIFY it discriminates before landing; OR (ii) if the model saturates REAL credentials to 0 too broadly in generic contexts, that is a MODEL over-saturation regression owned by DET-2/retrain (measure the CredData recall delta first). Test left at the foreign baseline (still red) pending that decision. | status=open-detection-truth [gpu-cpu-parity]

crates/scanner/src/gpu/backend.rs:380 | TRUST_GATE_PERMANENT_CACHE | P2 | MOE_NUMERIC_TRUST: OnceLock<bool> permanently caches the GPU parity verdict after the first probe. A driver that passes the initial probe then starts producing divergent scores (thermal throttle, wgpu context loss + recover, memory pressure) continues to be 'trusted' for the entire process lifetime with no re-probe and no operator notice. The OnceLock cannot be invalidated or re-probed. | Add a per-batch finite + range sanity check (all output scores in [0,1]) alongside the existing NaN guard. When a score unexpectedly exceeds GPU_MOE_PARITY_TOLERANCE from the expected distribution, clear trust by resetting to CPU for that batch and emit a one-shot eprintln. Alternatively, use an AtomicBool with a counter-based re-probe every N batches (e.g. every 10k calls) to catch post-probe drift. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]
crates/scanner/src/gpu.rs:219-224 | LAW10_VIOLATION | P2 | The GPU score-count mismatch arm (Some(scores) where scores.len() != candidates.len()) emits only tracing::warn! and falls to score_features_on_cpu(). There is no eprintln! and no call to moe_runtime_degrade(), so when the GPU dispatch returns a malformed batch the silent CPU fallback violates Law-10: the operator cannot observe this degradation and the throughput collapse from GPU to CPU is invisible. | Call moe_runtime_degrade(format!("score count mismatch: gpu returned {} scores for {} candidates", scores.len(), candidates.len())) before score_features_on_cpu(). Under --require-gpu this exits with code 12; otherwise it emits a one-shot eprintln. This matches the existing pattern for dispatch failures (None arm). | status=DONE (2026-07-09, Law-10 + ONE-PLACE + gpu-cpu-parity). UNDERSTOOD-FIRST: this arm is UNREACHABLE via the real backend — `backend::batch_score_features` OWNS the length invariant (backend.rs:542 `batch_size = features.len()`; :695 `if scores.len() != batch_size` => it ALREADY calls moe_runtime_degrade("score count mismatch") + returns None), and the caller builds `features` one-per-candidate, so a `Some` whose len != candidates cannot occur. The old bare `tracing::warn!`-then-CPU was thus (a) a silent fallback IF ever reached (Law-10) and (b) a ONE-PLACE duplicate of the backend's own mismatch degrade with a SECOND hand-rolled warn. FIX: widened `moe_runtime_degrade` fn -> `pub(super)` (backend.rs:399) so it is the SINGLE degrade owner, then the caller arm now `debug_assert_eq!`s the invariant (documents unreachability in dev) + routes through `backend::moe_runtime_degrade(&format!("caller-side score count mismatch: backend returned {} scores for {} candidates", ...))` before the CPU rescue — so a future backend contract break fails LOUD via the SAME owner (hard-fail under --require-gpu via require_gpu_unmet -> exit 12, one-shot eprintln otherwise) instead of a divergent silent path. Also documented the `None` arm (backend already degraded it loudly; below-threshold None impossible here since this branch is large-batch-only). VERIFIED: `cargo check -p keyhog-scanner --features gpu` RC=0, 0 errors (the lone dead_code warning is pre-existing foreign bpe.rs:39 ENTROPY_BPE_MAX_CHARS_PER_TOKEN under the gpu cfg, untouched by this change). Both files were foreign-dirty; edits are surgical/additive (visibility widen + arm rewrite), no foreign work destroyed. GATE RECONCILED: the source-shape gate `ml_score_cardinality` (unit/gates) pinned the OLD bare-`tracing::warn!` mismatch message; updated it to assert the IMPROVED shape (`backend::moe_runtime_degrade(` + "caller-side score count mismatch") — a STRONGER Law-10 contract, not a weakening — and re-verified GREEN. gpu_moe_degrade_contract.rs still green (its `.split("fn moe_runtime_degrade(")` still matches the now-`pub(super)` fn; body/latches unchanged). | [gpu-cpu-parity]
crates/scanner/src/compiler/compiler_compile.rs:77-78 | LAW10_VIOLATION | P2 | When build_gpu_literals encounters an empty literal it emits tracing::warn! and returns None, silently disabling the entire GPU literal scan. The calling code in compile.rs receives None and routes every scan to CPU/SIMD, but there is no eprintln! — the operator running a GPU build with --backend gpu sees no indication that the GPU literal matcher was discarded because a detector produced an empty literal string. | Add an eprintln! alongside the tracing::warn! at line 77, mirroring the report_gpu_matcher_unavailable pattern in gpu_lazy_helpers.rs. The message should name the literal set type ('GPU literal set' vs 'GPU positioned literal set') and instruct the user to check their detector definition for empty AC literals. | status=DONE (2026-07-09, Law-10). `build_gpu_literal_rows` (compiler_compile.rs) now emits a one-shot `eprintln!` (guarded by a new `GPU_LITERAL_EMPTY_WARNED` OnceLock, mirroring report_gpu_matcher_unavailable) alongside the `tracing::warn!` when a detector produces an empty literal: the message names the `{label}` (GPU literal set / GPU positioned literal set), states the entire GPU literal matcher was discarded and scans route to CPU/SIMD, tells the operator to check their detector definitions for an empty AC literal, and notes --require-gpu for mandatory acceleration. The `tracing::warn!` still fires every time for logs; the eprintln is once/process to avoid spam. Compiled clean under --features gpu (RC=0). | [gpu-cpu-parity]

crates/scanner/src/gpu/backend.rs:752 | MISSING_CROSS_PLATFORM_TEST | P3 | GPU_MOE_PARITY_TOLERANCE = 0.01 but there is no documented analysis or test pinning this tolerance against observed Metal/Vulkan/DX12 softmax f32 exp() precision variance across the production GPU fleet. The gate logits fed to WGSL softmax can be arbitrary f32 values; on some drivers exp() precision degrades for large logit magnitudes, which could push the max_abs_diff above 0.01 on a correct but imprecise GPU, causing the trust gate to permanently fail closed for that host. | Add a criterion-backed measurement that dispatches the parity probe on each CI GPU host (RTX 5090, RTX 4090, Metal) and records the actual max_abs_diff. The vyre API signature includes region_base as a shard offset for splitting large literal sets across multiple GPU dispatches. No test exercises any non-zero region_base value to prove that keyhog's single-dispatch strategy produces recall-identical results to a multi-shard strategy, and there is no assertion that the literal set is always small enough to fit in a single dispatch. | Add a test that constructs a GpuLiteralSet with enough patterns to require at least two shards (region_base=0 and region_base=N/2), runs both shards, OR-combines the presence bitmaps, and asserts the result equals a single full-set dispatch. For a multi-sub-batch coalesced scan (REGION_PRESENCE_BATCH_BYTE_LIMIT triggers sub-batch splitting) the AC program is rebuilt from scratch for each sub-batch, paying the full compilation cost repeatedly on what is a hot code path. | Add a cached_program field to ScanDispatchScratch (or a separate PresenceScratch struct) and call build_or_reuse_program() that rebuilds only when the pattern set changes, matching LiteralSetScanScratch.cached_program semantics. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]
