LiteralSetScanScratch has a cached_prefilter field that avoids this rebuild; the ScanDispatchScratch path used by keyhog's GPU region-presence hot loop pays the full prefilter rebuild cost per sub-batch. | Extend the program cache fix (above) to also cache the prefilter tables. The cache key is the LiteralSet's pattern fingerprint; a pattern-set change invalidates both the program and the prefilter together. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]
crates/scanner/src/gpu/gpu_shader.rs:44 | SIGMOID_SAT_ROUNDING | P3 | The WGSL header interpolates SIGMOID_SAT with format string {sigmoid_sat:.1}, which truncates SIGMOID_SATURATION to exactly one decimal place. Currently SIGMOID_SATURATION = 6.0 so the value rounds identically. If saturation is ever retuned to a non-integer value such as 6.25 or 5.75, the GPU clamp will be rounded to 6.3 or 5.8 while the CPU clamp uses the exact float, producing a systematic GPU/CPU divergence near the saturation boundary with no compile error or test failure. | Use an exact format string {:?} or {:.6} to write all significant digits, or require that SIGMOID_SATURATION is always an integer by adding a static_assert in build.rs that SIGMOID_SATURATION.fract() == 0.0. Add a test to wgsl_body_sizes_arrays_and_clamps_from_named_consts that parses the emitted SIGMOID_SAT f32 value and asserts it equals model_arch::SIGMOID_SATURATION exactly. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]
crates/scanner/src/engine/backend_triggered.rs:384-386 | IMPLICIT_ORDERING_CONTRACT | P3 | gpu_presence_literal_count() returns ac_map.len() + phase2_keyword_count + phase2_always_anchor_literal_count, implicitly assuming that the GPU literal set compiled by build_gpu_literals chains ac_literals -> phase2_keywords -> phase2_always_anchor_literals in exactly this order. The three decode functions (triggered_patterns_from_gpu_presence, phase2_keyword_hints_from_gpu_presence, phase2_always_anchor_present_from_gpu_presence) all rely on this ordering to compute correct bit indices. There is no compile-time or test-time assertion that enforces the chain order in build_gpu_literals matches the decode functions' index arithmetic. | Add an integration test that builds a CompiledScanner with known detectors having ac_map N entries, M phase2_keywords, and K always_anchor literals; calls gpu_presence_literal_count(); asserts the value equals N+M+K; then manually sets specific bits in a synthetic presence bitmap and asserts each decode function returns the expected pattern/keyword/anchor rather than a shifted one. This pins the ordering contract so a reorder in build_gpu_literals fails loudly. | status=MOSTLY-COVERED, narrow gpu-gated residual (2026-07-10 deep-read). The DECODE side is already comprehensively pinned by tests/unit/root_facade/gpu_presence_bit_partition.rs (4 tests, cfg(gpu)): keyword-region bit → keyword_hints ONLY (triggered empty, anchor false); anchor-region bit → anchor true ONLY; and bits BEYOND the presence range (confirmed-anchor, generic-keyword literals) → stray_tail_bits, never decoded — with `gpu_presence_literal_count() == N+M+K` asserted as a precondition. The BUILD side order is not a hidden assumption but a LITERAL single-source `.chain()` in build_gpu_literals (compiler_compile.rs:36-42: `ac_literals.iter().chain(phase2_keywords).chain(phase2_always_anchor_literals)`) — self-documenting ac→kw→anchor. RESIDUAL (narrow): (1) gpu_presence_bit_partition.rs never sets a BASE-region bit (idx < ac_map.len()) to positively confirm triggered_patterns fires the right pattern while kw/anchor stay empty — only kw/anchor bits are spot-checked; (2) no automated lock that build_gpu_literals' emitted ORDER equals the decode layout. (2) is HARD to test post-compile: CompiledScanner retains only phase2_keyword_count / phase2_always_anchor_literal_count (usize), NOT the keyword/anchor STRINGS (consumed at the compile call site), so a test cannot re-drive build_gpu_literals from a scanner to compare order; it would need a compile-site assertion or retaining the lists. (1) is a small add but the full partition assertion needs the cfg(gpu) decode fns (phase2_keyword_hints/always_anchor_present are #[cfg(feature=gpu)]) → requires a --features gpu build on the shared gpu target dir (not ci-lean). Belongs to the gpu-feature lane (needs --features gpu on the shared gpu target dir); not run here to avoid a heavy shared-target build for a single spot-check. The load-bearing contract (decode region isolation) is NOT unguarded. [gpu-cpu-parity]

The region_base != 0 shard path in vyre's WGSL kernel (which adjusts the pattern-presence bit offset by region_base * presence_bitmap_words) is never exercised by any ground-truth assertion, leaving that code path unvalidated. | Add a gpu_only test that takes the randomized case fixture, splits the literal set in half (region_base=0 and region_base=N/2), dispatches both shards, OR-combines presence bitmaps, and asserts the combined result equals the full single-dispatch result. This validates both the shard offset arithmetic and that keyhog's single-shard strategy is equivalent. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]
crates/scanner/src/gpu/backend.rs:824-845 | TIMEOUT_DISCARDED | P3 | gpu_moe_numerically_trustworthy(readback_timeout) passes readback_timeout into gpu_moe_parity_max_divergence only on the first OnceLock fill. All subsequent calls return the cached bool without ever using the readback_timeout argument. batch_score_features callers believe they are setting a per-parity-probe timeout, but after the first call the value is silently ignored. A caller with a shorter timeout (e.g. a latency-sensitive scan path) cannot override the timeout set by the first caller (typically the self-test or the first batch). | Document in the function signature and caller doc that readback_timeout is effective only on the first call per process (after which the OnceLock caches the result). Remove the readback_timeout parameter from gpu_moe_numerically_trustworthy itself and instead have it read from a process-level configured timeout (e.g. ScannerTuningConfig::GPU_MOE_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT) so the one-shot semantics are explicit and no caller is misled. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]
crates/scanner/src/gpu/self_test.rs:22 | SELF_TEST_PERMANENT_CACHE | P3 | GPU_SELF_TEST_CACHE: OnceLock permanently caches the GPU self-test result. If the GPU driver passes the parity probe at startup and then thermal-throttles or crashes and recovers, keyhog doctor and any subsequent gpu_self_test() calls still return HEALTHY for the rest of the process lifetime. This mirrors the MOE_NUMERIC_TRUST permanent-cache issue: there is no mechanism to detect post-probe hardware divergence in the running scan. | Add a lightweight live-check path: gpu_self_test() returns the cached result for 'adapter present + parity passed' but also exposes a force-recheck variant gpu_self_test_force() that clears the cache and re-runs the probe. Wire force-recheck to the --self-test flag and to any operator-visible health-check CLI subcommand so a support scenario can trigger a fresh probe. | status=open-DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY-P4 (2026-07-10 code-verified): the permanent `OnceLock<Result<GpuSelfTest,String>>` cache (self_test.rs:22) is REAL but its blast radius is much smaller than the row implies — it does NOT affect scan CORRECTNESS. The row's own premise ("no mechanism to detect post-probe hardware divergence IN THE RUNNING SCAN") is FALSE: the SCAN path re-checks GPU MoE parity PER-BATCH and fails closed to CPU on divergence, independently of this cache — the code comment at self_test.rs:58-59 states exactly this ("the scan path enforces `batch_score_features` fails closed to CPU on the same divergence, so doctor and the scanner never disagree"). So a stale-cached self-test can only make the DIAGNOSTIC (`keyhog backend --self-test` / doctor) report a stale verdict in a LONG-LIVED process (daemon/watch); a one-shot CLI process is too short-lived for staleness to matter (one call). Also the stale-FAIL direction (transient startup failure cached as Err → GPU never re-probed for the process) is the more real annoyance than stale-OK. So: NOT a [gpu-cpu-parity] scan bug (scanning is already fail-closed per-batch); it is a diagnostic-accuracy nit for daemons. The proposed `gpu_self_test_force()` (clear cache + re-probe, wired to an operator health-check) is a reasonable small robustness add for daemon operators, but P4 not P3, and re-probe cost (a real GPU dispatch) means it must be operator-triggered, never automatic. Reclassify diagnostic-robustness, not detection-parity. | [gpu-cpu-parity]

Companion test: floor=true + simd_prefilter present returns Ok. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]

A third concurrent batch size that straddles a different page boundary or wgpu internal alignment could expose residual races that the two-size test does not cover. | Add a third concurrent batch size of GPU_BATCH_THRESHOLD/2 (would fail to reach GPU but exercise the CPU path concurrently) and a fourth at 3*GPU_BATCH_THRESHOLD/2 (falls to GPU with odd alignment). crates/scanner/src/engine/gpu_region_dispatch.rs:45 | FREE_LITERAL_NO_VYRE_ANCHOR | P3 | GPU_POSITIONED_LITERAL_MAX_MATCHES: u32 = 1 << 20 (1M matches) is defined as a free keyhog literal. Vyre's own output-buffer cap is a separate internal constant (exposed only via the 'output-buffer cap' error string). If vyre's internal cap changes (e.g. is increased to 2M), keyhog's pager will never trigger on realistic inputs and the is_gpu_output_buffer_overflow branch effectively becomes dead code — silently changing adaptive pager behavior. | Document in the comment at line 45 that this value must be <= vyre's internal output buffer cap. Add an assertion or at least a note in the gpu_pager_recovers_from_output_buffer_overflow test that the trigger threshold is being set AT vyre's actual cap so the pager fires on real overflows not on artificial underflows. | status=open [gpu-cpu-parity]

A profiler reading MOE_SCORE_NS cannot distinguish these regimes; the throughput attribution is misleading for any performance analysis of GPU ML acceleration. | Add a separate MOE_FUSED_NS counter for the small-batch path and rename the large-batch MOE_SCORE_NS bucket to make clear it measures only the GPU dispatch phase. A vyre refactor that changes the message (e.g. to 'exceeds output buffer capacity') would cause the pager to silently degrade every overflow to CPU instead of splitting and retrying — a performance regression with no test failure at the point of change. | Replace the string-based check with a structured error type in vyre's API that distinguishes overflow errors from other dispatch errors, then match on the variant rather than the message substring. ## RECONCILE 2026-07-09 — test-depth-gaps cluster (6364-6386) audited, mostly STALE (same pattern as HUNT + orphan clusters)
Verified each "Law-6 shape-only assertion" finding against the CURRENT test file — largely already fixed by a prior pass. FIXED this session (all GREEN via all_tests): scan_sarif_has_runs (^above), scan_git_diff_head_worktree_json_shape (severity `.is_some()`→`=="critical"`), explain_aws_access_key (loose `||"aws"`→exact id + "critical" severity pin), scan_dedup_none_reports_all_occurrences (`len()>=2`→asserts both aws-access-key AND a github detector by id, so a dup-of-one-credential can't pass). Verified ALREADY-DONE/STALE (no edit): scan_jsonl_format_valid_objects (pins detector_id==aws-access-key), scan_format_sarif_clean_exit_zero (version + results-empty), scan_format_sarif_has_schema (version + $schema), reliability/output_format.rs (version + $schema + clean-results-empty @:123). GENUINELY OPEN (real, NOT shape-nitpicks): 6368 e2e_explain.rs github loose-OR — BLOCKED (that file is the CI-ORPHAN of 6402; fixing an assertion in a non-CI flaky file is low-value until de-orphaned); 6369 e2e_watch.rs --quiet — FIXED (2026-07-09): the byte-length proxy passed VACUOUSLY (both outputs empty — 300ms < debug ~900-detector compile, so the banner never printed before kill). Replaced with a deterministic stderr-reader-thread poll (spawn_watch_streaming + stderr_contains_within): noisy MUST emit the "watching:" banner, --quiet MUST suppress it; the quiet window is tied to the OBSERVED noisy banner latency + margin so it's robust across fast/slow machines (Law-6 truth + Law-7 kills the fixed-sleep). GREEN standalone 7.28s. NOTE: e2e_watch.rs is itself a CI-ORPHAN (not in all_tests, same class as 6402) — assertion now truthful regardless.; 6373(P1) exit-11 scanner-panic never asserted at PROCESS level (real missing-coverage, needs panic-injection e2e); 6377/6378/6383/6385/6386 proptest/coverage ADDITIONS (some GPU/foreign-blocked). NET: ~70% of the cluster's rows are already-truthful; 4 fixed here.

crates/cli/tests/gap/main_maps_scanner_panic_to_system_exit.rs:10 | Missing coverage axis: exit-11 never asserted at process level | P1 | The test at line 10-11 asserts `EXIT_SCANNER_PANIC == 11` (a constant check) and that the string `SCANNER_PANICKED` appears in `lib.rs` source. This is a source-shape check (Law 6 violation), not an e2e proof that the binary actually exits 11 when the scanner panics. There is no test in the suite that drives `keyhog scan` to produce a scanner panic and asserts `process.exit_code() == 11`. | Add an e2e test that injects a poisoned detector (or uses a test-only panic hook) and asserts the binary exits with code 11. | status=open-VERIFIED-accurate (2026-07-10, grep): the gap is REAL — the only exit-11 coverage is a constant-check (`tests/unit/orchestrator/orchestrator_exit_constants_exported.rs:8` + `tests/gap/main_maps_scanner_panic_to_system_exit.rs:8` assert `EXIT_SCANNER_PANIC==11`) plus a SOURCE-SHAPE check (`main_maps_scanner_panic_to_system_exit.rs:10-11` reads `lib.rs` and asserts it `.contains("SCANNER_PANICKED")`/`"EXIT_SCANNER_PANIC"`) — exactly the Law-6 shape violation this row names; NO test drives a real panic through the process. MECHANISM-BLOCKED: there is NO panic-injection hook anywhere (grep for `KEYHOG_*PANIC`/`force_panic`/`inject.*panic` = 0 hits). The real route is `cli/src/lib.rs:70` `SCANNER_PANICKED: AtomicBool` set at :98 by the panic hook, mapped to exit 11 at :356-369 — it only fires on a genuinely caught scanner panic, and the scanner is hardened against panics (see the compiler/simdsieve totality proptests — adversarial input does NOT panic), so no input can drive exit-11 from outside. The fix therefore requires adding a deliberate test-only panic path (cfg(test-hook) env like `KEYHOG_TEST_FORCE_SCANNER_PANIC`) into `cli/src/lib.rs` — which is FOREIGN-DIRTY — AND a design decision on whether a gated panic-backdoor is acceptable in the shipped binary (Law 2 tension). Not a clean standalone landing; needs that hook first. Accurate open P1, mechanism-scoped. | [test-depth-gaps]

crates/scanner/tests/property/scanner_fuzz.rs:140 | max_shrink_iters too low for adversarial fuzz finding minimization | P4 | `max_shrink_iters: 256` for the 10k-case panic-safety block. A panic triggered on a 16 KiB random input shrinks very poorly at 256 iterations, typically leaving a multi-KB minimal case. This masks the root cause and makes the CI failure unintelligible. The comment justifies this with `"a panic on a 12 KiB random input shrinks to … a 12 KiB random input, basically"` — but that is an argument for a better strategy (structured byte generation), not for capping shrink. | Use a structured strategy that generates shorter inputs by construction (e.g., `0..4096` as max length) and raise `max_shrink_iters: 10_000` so real minimal cases are found. | status=open [test-depth-gaps]

crates/verifier/tests/regression_ssrf_screen_matrix.rs:1 | Missing coverage axis: SSRF proptest for arbitrary IP space | P1 | The SSRF regression suite (`regression_ssrf_screen_matrix.rs`, `new_verifier_bogon_ssrf.rs`) uses fixed known-answer vectors. There is no proptest covering arbitrary IPv4/IPv6 addresses to ensure `is_private_ip_addr` never classifies a private/loopback/link-local address as public. A predicate edit that shifts an octet boundary (e.g. `10.0.0.0/8` check uses `>=` vs `>`) would only be caught by the fixed vectors that happen to land on the boundary. | DONE (2026-07-08): ssrf_ip_screen_proptest.rs added (aggregated via property/mod.rs pub mod) — fast/full differential over arbitrary IPv4+IPv6 (100k samples each), all dangerous ranges blocked (10/8,172.16/12,192.168/16,127/8,169.254/16,224/4,240/4), public NOT blocked, integer/hex/octal/inet_aton-short encodings blocked via is_private_url, never-panic/fail-closed. 7 tests green @bb522wos0. | status=DONE [test-depth-gaps]
crates/verifier/tests/regression_verify_poll_bounds.rs:1 | Missing coverage axis: rate-limiter proptest | P2 | The rate-limiter is tested with only fixed boundary cases (zero rate, NaN rate, negative rate, concurrent updates). There is no proptest over arbitrary `(rate, n_requests, delay_ms)` tuples to verify the token-bucket invariant: `n_requests_allowed ≤ rate * elapsed_seconds + initial_budget` for any combination. A drift in the token refill calculation would only be caught by the fixed cases that happen to trigger it. | FIXED (2026-07-08): rate_limiter_interval_proptest drives arbitrary f64 (200k full-range bit-pattern draws — NaN/±inf/subnormal/huge) through new()+set_default_rps() asserting the interval is never zero (= never infinite rate). The finding's "token-bucket invariant" is a wrong mental model: the limiter is INTERVAL-based (per-service last_request+interval), not a token bucket; the real security invariant is the zero-interval guard, which this locks. | status=DONE
crates/cli/tests/e2e_explain.rs:18 | Missing coverage axis: explain machine-parseable format not tested | P3 | `explain aws-access-key` and `explain github-pat` are tested with text-format stdout only. The `explain` subcommand emits a machine-parseable JSON/TOML format (according to the detector corpus shape). There is no test asserting `keyhog explain aws-access-key --json` or a structured output path works end-to-end and contains correct fields. | Add an e2e test: `keyhog explain aws-access-key --format json` (or the applicable flag), parse the output as JSON, and assert `obj["id"] == "aws-access-key"`, `obj["service"] == "aws"`, and `obj["severity"] == "critical"`. | status=INVALID-AS-WRITTEN → reframed CAPABILITY (2026-07-10 verified): the row's premise is FALSE — `explain` has NO machine-parseable format. `crates/cli/src/args/explain.rs` `ExplainArgs` carries ONLY `{detector_id: String, detectors: PathBuf}`; there is no `--format`/`--json` flag, so `keyhog explain <id> --format json` is an unknown-arg error, and there is no JSON path to e2e-test. So this is NOT a test-depth gap (nothing to cover). It IS a legitimate but OPTIONAL capability gap (Review Vector 3): if scripting/agent consumers want structured explain output, ADD `explain --format {text,json}` to ExplainArgs + a json emitter (id/service/severity/patterns/verify), THEN the e2e test this row describes becomes real. Reclassify: not [test-depth-gaps]; a small capability item, land the flag first. | [capability]
crates/cli/tests/e2e/scan_git_staged_format_sarif_has_schema.rs:64 | Missing negative twin: no test for git-staged with planted secret emitting SARIF result | P3 | The test `scan_git_staged_format_sarif_has_schema` only tests a clean staged file (no secrets) and asserts the `$schema` URL. There is no companion test that stages a file with a planted secret and asserts `runs[0].results` contains an entry with the correct `ruleId` and location. The positive-truth / negative-twin pair is incomplete. | Add a companion test that stages a file containing a planted AWS key, runs `keyhog scan --git-staged --format sarif`, and asserts `runs[0]["results"][0]["ruleId"] == "aws-access-key"` and `location.physicalLocation.artifactLocation` matches the staged file path. | status=open-LOW-VALUE (2026-07-10 verified): the two ORTHOGONAL halves are already covered — (a) git-staged FINDS a planted secret: `crates/cli/tests/e2e_binary.rs::git_staged_scan_finds_only_staged_secret` stages a planted credential, runs `--git-staged`, and asserts only the staged secret surfaces (JSON); (b) SARIF emits the correct `ruleId` for a planted secret: `crates/cli/tests/regression_cli_sarif_output_file.rs` asserts `runs[0].results[…].ruleId` == the planted detector id + level/fingerprint. Source (git-staged) and format (SARIF) are ORTHOGONAL in the reporter — the SARIF emitter serializes findings identically regardless of which source produced them — so the specific git-staged×SARIF×planted triple is belt-and-suspenders, not a real coverage hole. Keep as an easy add if desired, but it is not a genuine gap; downgraded from P3. | [test-depth-gaps]
crates/scanner/tests/adversarial/round1_decoded_is_base64_blob_property.rs:26 | Missing adversarial axis: no negative twin for decode-through false-positive suppression | P3 | The `round1_decoded_is_base64_blob_property` test (1k cases, sub-contract) only asserts positive: that the decoded blob is recognised as base64. The suppression path is untested by property. | Add a negative proptest: generate random 32-byte sequences with low entropy (uniform bytes from a 4-character alphabet) and assert the scanner emits zero findings for them. | status=mostly-STALE (2026-07-09): the UNIT-level negative twin ALREADY EXISTS — round1_decoded_is_base64_blob_property.rs:51-69 `random_secret_bytes_are_not_flagged` (1k cases) asserts random non-alphabet bytes are NOT flagged as a base64 blob (the suppression's negative direction). The finding's REMAINING ask is a broader END-TO-END property (drive the full scanner on a low-entropy uniform-4-char-alphabet blob, assert zero findings) — a scanner-level test distinct from this unit predicate. LOW-pri: the entropy-floor + BPE gates already suppress low-entropy uniform blobs (covered by high_precision/entropy contract tests); an e2e proptest would add belt-and-suspenders but needs CompiledScanner setup in a ci-lean-wired file. Not the shape-gap the row implied. | status=open (residual e2e only)
crates/scanner/tests/adversarial/top10_twilio_auth_token_true_positive_must_fire.rs:1 | Missing criterion perf floor for twilio/datadog HS-only-trigger detectors | P3 | The `top10` true-positive suite tests scan correctness but there is no criterion benchmark that measures per-file scan throughput for twilio-auth-token and datadog-api-key — the two HS-only-trigger detectors whose recall is entirely Hyperscan-dependent. - crates/cli/src/subcommands/detectors/brace_rewrite.rs:92,137,65,79 | correctness/utf8-corruption | HIGH | rewrite_braces + rewrite_braces_in_string_literals iterate BYTES and do `byte as char` (Latin-1 reinterpretation), so any non-ASCII UTF-8 byte in a `[detector.verify*]` string literal is corrupted into mojibake by `keyhog detectors --fix-template-braces` (e.g. body="héllo" -> "hÃ©llo"). fix_single_brace_in_verify_blocks itself is safe (&str push_str). ZERO tests on all 3 fns. FIX: iterate chars (Vec<char>), push actual char; identifier charset is ASCII so slice-extend stays correct. + regression test (ASCII rewrite unchanged, non-ASCII preserved, already-doubled {{x}} untouched, regex-quantifier {4,6} in verify-string untouched by rewrite_braces since digits aren't an identifier start). ## HUNT: clean sources files (foreign refactor does NOT own these — editable)
- crates/sources/src/hosted_git/sanitize.rs:20 | correctness/SECRET-LEAK (redaction) | HIGH | the git-clone-stderr credential redactor's URL-userinfo regex `([a-z][a-z0-9+\-.]*://)([^/@\s]+)@` stopped the userinfo capture at the FIRST `@`, so a git basic-auth password carrying a literal `@` (`https://alice:pa@ss@github.com/…` — git surfaces raw stderr even though RFC 3986 says `@` should be %40-encoded) redacted only `alice:pa@` → LEAKED the `ss@github.com` tail into keyhog's error output. Same first-`@`-vs-last-`@` leak class that url_redaction.rs fixes with `rfind`. Reached in prod at hosted_git.rs:854 + :1029 (github/gitlab/bitbucket org-clone failure messages). FIX: `[^/@\s]+` → `[^/\s]+` — greedy to the LAST `@` before the path `/`/whitespace (the `/`-exclusion still confines it to the authority so a path `@` is never a spurious userinfo boundary); mirrors url_redaction's rfind semantics; over-redaction only on malformed pathless-with-query URLs and it fails CLOSED (hides more, never leaks). +3 inline regression tests (embedded-@-with-path, embedded-@-without-path, @-in-path-not-a-boundary); existing 4 redaction tests unchanged → no regression. Verified `--lib --features github` 8 passed/0 failed @bhsbgzpp5. ONE-PLACE SMELL (folded into [[DR-019]]): the regex is hand-COPIED into the inline test's `live_patterns()` — I updated BOTH copies in sync, but the real fix is DR-019 exposing the compiled prod patterns via facade so the test can't test a different regex than production. status=DONE

NO property sweep proving the security no-leak invariant. ADDED 5 web-gated proptests (4k cases each): (1) any userinfo `user:<secret>@` collapses to EXACTLY `***@host/path` (secret charset spans the rich authority set incl. embedded `@` — the rfind boundary — but excludes the `/?#&`/space terminators so the expected output is deterministic), (2) bare `<secret>@` userinfo same, (3) every one of the 16 SENSITIVE_QUERY_KEYS masks its value to `key=***` while benign `&page=2` survives (value-scoped, not whole-query wipe), (4) LITERAL no-leak: an UPPERCASE-disjoint secret in BOTH userinfo+query → `!output.contains(secret)` (disjointness makes it sound), (5) no over-redaction: benign-key + no-userinfo URL returned byte-for-byte. - crates/scanner/src/compiler/compiler_prefix.rs:65 | recall-edge (AC trigger miss) | P4 | the alternation-branch non-capturing-group strip in `extract_literal_prefixes` handles `?:`/`?i:`/`?m:`/`?s:`/`?im:`/`?is:`/`?ms:` but NOT the 3-flag `?ims:` form — so `(?ims:pk|sk)\.…` keeps `?ims:pk` as the first branch, `extract_literal_prefix` bails on the leading `?`, the whole alternation clears (partial-coverage guard), and the detector carries NO AC literal-prefix anchor (falls to the keyword phase-2 lane). CONSERVATIVE miss (no wrong extraction, no panic — a recall edge only), and `(?ims:` scoped-alternation detector regexes are rare. FIX = add the `?ims:`/`?isu:`/… general N-flag strip (share the `strip_group_prefix` owner which ALSO lacks `?ims:`) — but it is a RECALL-affecting routing change → bench-gated (CredData A/B, expect ~0 since no current detector uses this exact shape; verify none regress). status=open [test-depth-gaps]

Replaced the inline copy with a call to the single owner `strip_group_prefix(&pattern[1..end])` — provably identical (same forms, same order-independent result; verified char-by-char incl the `?im:` vs `?i:` prefix-overlap case). Now the recognised-flag set has ONE definitional home, so the pending `?ims:` extension is a one-place change both routing paths pick up together. Verified: 9 compiler-prefix tests green under ci-lean @bl1ws6syz. **DETECTION-SAFE — EXONERATED 2026-07-09 on TWO independent grounds** (contracts_runner re-run surfaced 2 failures + 7 detectors "raw finding count = 0" — but they are FOREIGN, see the FOREIGN-RED note below, NOT this dedup): (1) byte-identical — `strip_group_prefix` recognises the same flag-form set as the old inline chain; (2) NEVER EXECUTED for the failing detectors — the alternation branch is gated `if pattern.starts_with('(') && pattern.contains('|')` (compiler_prefix.rs:37) and all 4 CLEAN failing github detectors are literal-prefix patterns (`gho_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}`, `ghr_…`, `ghs_…`, `ghu_…`) that do NOT start with `(`, so my code path is not reached for them at all. status=**DONE-verified**. - crates/scanner/src/decode/caesar.rs:578,583 | correctness/robustness (latent u8-overflow panic on public facade) | P3 | `caesar_shift` computes `(ch as u8 - base + shift) % ALPHABET_LEN` — the u8 add happens BEFORE the `% 26`, so a letter at offset 25 (`z`/`Z`) with `shift >= 231` overflows (25+231=256 > u8::MAX) → **debug panic** ("attempt to add with overflow"). The public test facade `caesar_shift_for_test`/`testing::caesar_shift` accept ANY `u8`, so it is reachable from a caller passing a raw shift. NOT production-reachable: every in-crate caller passes `shift ∈ 1..=25` (caesar.rs:78 `ALPHABET_LEN - k`, k∈1..=25; caesar.rs:469 shift from `(i%25)+1`). The doc says "modulo ALPHABET_LEN", so the value is meant to be shift-invariant mod 26 — the impl only honours that for `shift <= 230`. FIX (trivial, total, byte-identical for all current callers): reduce first — `let shift = shift % ALPHABET_LEN;` at the top of `caesar_shift`, then `ch - base + shift` is at most 25+25=50, no overflow, correct rotation for ANY u8. BLOCKED 2026-07-09: caesar.rs is FOREIGN-DIRTY (` M`, in-flight decode refactor) — cannot edit. Guarded meanwhile by bounding the new proptests to the non-overflowing range (documented, ref this row). status=open (blocked-on foreign-decode-refactor-commit; 1-line fix ready) [robustness]

- crates/scanner/src/generic_keyword_owner.rs:keyword_span_owned_by_named_detector | correctness/robustness (latent mid-codepoint slice panic) | P3 | the bounds guard is `if keyword_start > keyword_end || keyword_end > line.len() { return false }` — it checks LENGTH but NOT char boundaries, then slices `&line[keyword_start..keyword_end]`. A mid-codepoint span on a multibyte line panics (`span_owned(&["x"], "é", 0, 1)` → "byte index 1 is not a char boundary"). NOT production-reachable: the pub(crate) fn is called with regex/AC-derived offsets that bound an ASCII assignment key (char-aligned), and the expansion loop stops at `is_assignment_key_byte` which rejects ≥0x80 bytes so it never splits a multibyte char. But the fn (and its `_for_test` facade) has an unguarded char-boundary precondition. FIX (fail-safe, total): add `|| !line.is_char_boundary(keyword_start) || !line.is_char_boundary(keyword_end)` to the guard → return false (not owned) on a mis-aligned span. NB proptest surfaced a real subtlety: `decodes_to_printable_text` also requires `!is_binary_payload()`, and `is_binary_payload = magic.is_some() || (protobuf_wire && len>=8)` — a RANDOM alnum payload sometimes parses as protobuf-wire → flagged binary → rejected. Fixed by building the payload to start with `'s'` (0x73, wire type 3 → `parse_protobuf_wire` rejects at tag 0), keeping it readable text. Pinned the REAL decode-gate contract. [test-depth-gaps]

- crates/scanner/tests/gap/detector_precision_decoys.rs:+55 | test-depth PROPTEST TIER (is_random_token fail-safe floors) **DONE 2026-07-09 @baehq0xyk (73 passed total = 62 fixed + 11 property)** | P1 | +3 proptests over `is_random_token` (suppression/token_randomness.rs — CLEAN source; the randomness gate that LIFTS identifier-suppression, so a false `true` = an FP). Asserts the three model-INDEPENDENT fail-safe floors (soundness-over-reach, precision-safe): <MIN_ALPHA(6) alphabetic chars → None → never random (any digit padding); pairs==0 (letters never adjacent, glued by a digit) → None → never random despite ≥6 letters; <MIN_DISTINCT_LETTERS(3) distinct letters (pure run / ≤2-letter alternation) → never random regardless of bigram score. Deliberately does NOT sweep the reverse "random letters ⇒ random" (model-dependent). Consumes the existing `testing::entropy_isolated::is_random_token` export. - HUNT-entropy crates/scanner/src/entropy/avx512.rs:61 | MISSING TEST COVERAGE | low | calculate_shannon_entropy lacks safety tests for disabled target features | fix: add target-gated safety unit tests | status=done RECONCILED covered-at-the-right-level: you CANNOT unit-test "calling the unsafe avx512 fn on hardware without avx512" — that is UB by definition, not an assertable behavior. The correct safety contract is "the SAFE dispatch never routes to the avx512 path unless the CPU supports it", and that IS tested: tests/unit/entropy_fast_x86_tier.rs::tier_resolves_once_to_a_stable_known_variant asserts the cpuid-resolved tier is a supported variant (Avx512/Avx2/Scalar) and stable, and ::dispatched_tier_matches_scalar_reduction_to_ulps asserts whichever tier THIS CPU selects reduces bit-for-bit (to ULPs) against the scalar reference — so on an avx512 host the avx512 path is exercised and proven equal to scalar, and on a non-avx512 host it is provably never selected. The unsafe fn's precondition (f+bw present) is enforced by the resolver and validated here. Row predates entropy_fast_x86_tier.rs.

- HUNT-struct crates/scanner/src/structured/parsers/line.rs:114 | PERF | low | Redundant full-buffer compute_line_offsets scan performed twice per preprocess | fix: calculate line offsets once and thread them through | status=wontfix-net-negative CONFIRMED-REAL but the fix is a worse trade than the problem: the two scans are line.rs:114 (line-number ATTRIBUTION inside the parser, only for parsers using resolve_line_number_options) and structured/mod.rs:235 (offset SYNTHESIS after parse), both on the same `text`, separated by the parse→synthesize boundary. "Calculate once and thread through" means changing the 9-parser dispatch signature (mod.rs:190-199: parse_json/yaml/hcl/toml/env/docker_compose/tfstate/jupyter/line) to return `(pairs, Option<Vec<usize>>)` or accept a `&[usize]` — over-broad (hcl/others don't compute offsets at all; only line.rs-path parsers do) for the gain of ONE `compute_line_offsets` call, which is a single SIMD memchr_iter newline pass over a config-sized buffer (a rounding error). Adding cross-parser API coupling to save one memchr scan violates keep-it-simple with no measurable benefit. Revisit ONLY if the structured path is ever shown hot in a profile.

- HUNT-struct crates/scanner/src/compiler/compiler_build.rs:277 | SILENT FALLBACKS | medium | rewrite_alternation_prefix tracks paren depth incorrectly when escaped or in classes | fix: skip escaped parens and classes during depth scan | status=done FIXED as a ONE-PLACE unification: the leading-group close-finder (compiler_build.rs:277) was a NAIVE `(`/`)` counter (its own comment dismissed class parens as "rare in real detectors") while the SIBLING scanner in the SAME file, split_top_level_alternatives (:370), was already fully escape+class-aware (`escaped`/`in_class` flags). Two paren scanners, divergent discipline. Rewrote the close-finder to the SAME char_indices + escaped + in_class discipline. CONCRETE bug it fixes (traced by hand): `rewrite_alternation_prefix("(?:a|b\\)c)x","a","[a]")` — the naive scanner stopped at the escaped `\)` and spliced the MALFORMED, unbalanced `[a]c)x`; corrected scanner finds the real close and yields `[a]x`. Also `[)]`/`[(]` classed parens (naive mis-located the close INSIDE the class → declined rewrite, lost SIMD-prefix acceleration → recall). Common path (no escape/class) byte-identical to before. Tests: new tests/unit/a4_lr2/compiler_alt_escaped_class.rs (4 cases: escaped-close-paren, class-close-paren, class-open-paren, plain non-regression control) via the existing keyhog_scanner::testing::rewrite_alternation_prefix facade, mounted in the CLEAN a4_lr2/mod.rs. VERIFIED GREEN: keyhog-scanner --lib compiler_alt 11 passed / 0 failed, RC=0 ci-lean (my 4 new + existing 6 compiler_alt unchanged — no common-path regression). Broader compiler group also green (94 passed). FUZZ-HARDENED (2026-07-10): added tests/unit/a4_lr2/compiler_alt_rewrite_regex_validity_proptest.rs — a 3000-case proptest sweeping adversarial escaped-paren (`\(` `\)`), classed-paren (`[()]`), and range-class filler between the matched prefix and the group close, asserting a valid base regex NEVER rewrites to a malformed one (Some ⟹ regex::Regex::new(output).is_ok()). GREEN: 1 passed / 0 failed, RC=0 — the corrected close-finder emits well-formed output on all 3000 cases (the pre-fix naive scanner would fail the `\)` cases with the unbalanced splice). PRODUCTION-ENTRY HARDENED (2026-07-10): added tests/unit/a4_lr2/compiler_alt_rewrite_regex_validity_proptest's companion `compiler_homoglyph_rewrite_regex_validity_proptest.rs` (3000 cases) that fuzzes the REAL compiler entry `rewrite_homoglyph_literal_prefix` — which routes through BOTH its literal-head path (strip_literal_prefix_source / rewrite_homoglyph_body_prefix) AND the alternation fallback — over both a literal-head base (`gh<filler>[a-z]*`) and an alternation-head base (`(?:gh<filler>|other)tail`), asserting valid-in ⟹ valid-or-declined-out. GREEN 1/0 RC=0. BUG-CLASS SWEPT CLOSED (2026-07-10): audited every sibling regex-surgery helper in compiler_build.rs for the same escape/class-blindness — `strip_literal_prefix_source` (:361) is genuinely escape-AWARE (matches `\x`-escaped prefix chars via is_escaped_literal), `rewrite_homoglyph_body_prefix` (:345) is sound, `split_top_level_alternatives` (:383) already carries the escaped/in_class/depth flags (the reference the fix was mirrored on), and `split_leading_inline_flag` (:411) is pure flag-head parsing — so the naive-paren-counter defect existed ONLY in rewrite_alternation_prefix; the family is now uniformly escape/class-correct. RECONCILE (2026-07-10): re-ran the whole compiler lane post-foreign-churn — `unit::a4_lr2::compiler` 14 passed / 0 failed RC=0 — confirming all three new files + the 8 pre-existing compiler_alt tests still green (rewrite_alternation_prefix / rewrite_homoglyph_literal_prefix symbols intact; the "rename migration" I briefly suspected was a TRANSIENT tool-output-channel artifact in one window (some grep/rg results momentarily rendered identifiers as `ln`/`n`), NOT a real code change and NOT an rg-specific behavior — a controlled `rg`-vs-`grep`-vs-Read A/B confirmed all three now agree on the full identifier names; the file always read `hot_pattern_index_at` via Read.

OOB offset + BIDIRECTIONAL prefix oracle (Some(i)⟹prefix present at offset; None-in-range⟹no prefix present) + in-range index + first-match tie-break; (3) `a4_lr2/compiler_split_flag_partition_proptest.rs` (2 props ×4000) — `split_leading_inline_flag` PARTITION (flag+body==input always, the contract callers reassemble on) + totality on multi-byte tails + flag-shape; (4) `suppression_decode_b64_roundtrip_proptest.rs` (3 props ×4000) — `try_decode_b64_to_utf8` totality on arbitrary candidate text (crafted-input DoS surface, [[decode-structured-hotpath-dos-audit]]) + the len<8 floor + standard-base64-of-utf8 ROUNDTRIP soundness (decodes back to the exact plaintext) + non-UTF-8 (0xFF run) ⟹ None; base64 inputs generated at runtime by a local RFC-4648 encoder so no encoded literal enters the self-scanned source. Pairs with the 6703 compiler-alternation fuzz work; together the whole compiler_build regex-surgery family, the simdsieve slot resolver, and the suppression base64 peek are now adversarially fuzzed (~31k cases this batch). REVERTED the line.rs inline module (Law 9: fix the violation, never weaken the gate); line.rs is back to its original test-less state; both gates re-verified GREEN (5 passed / 0 failed RC=0). So line.rs coverage is NOT landed. Net DURABLE test additions this batch: 7 proptests / ~43k cases — 4 scanner (in dedicated tests/unit + tests/unit/a4_lr2 files, gate-compliant) + 3 diff_parser security (in the keyhog-SOURCES crate's PRE-EXISTING inline mod, which that crate permits; the scanner gates don't apply there), all GREEN. (2) Started an inline proptest module in `crates/core/src/report/escape.rs` whose naive `escape_cdata` invariant `!contains("]]>")` FALSELY failed on correct code (the canonical `]]]]><![CDATA[>` escape intentionally emits `]]>`); discovered `tests/regression_report_escape_invariants.rs` ALREADY proptests all 5 escapers with the CORRECT round-trip invariant → REVERTED the duplicate (ONE-PLACE/Law-9), left only a pointer comment. Lesson saved to memory [[tests-live-in-dedicated-regression-files-not-src]]: grep the dedicated tests trees before writing any keyhog test. (A THIRD self-correction — the line.rs gate violation above — is recorded inline; it was caught by the full reconcile and reverted, reinforcing that same memory.) FINAL RECONCILE STATE (honest): sources 225 lib + core both RC=0 GREEN; the scanner full ci-lean lib-unit run that INCLUDED my transient line.rs inline module showed 5276 passed / 6 FAILED — 4 foreign-caused [reconcile-red] regressions + my 2 self-inflicted no-inline-tests gate failures; after reverting line.rs, both gates re-verified GREEN and the scanner suite is back to 5276+ pass / exactly 4 foreign-red (re-verified still red 20min later, NOT mine — the backend_triggered/hcl/compiler code fixes + the 4 gate-compliant scanner proptests all coexist cleanly). | status=done
- [x] HARDENING (2026-07-10) crates/sources/src/capped_read.rs — added `read_to_cap_is_exact_prefix_and_truncation_flag_matches` proptest (4000 cases) to the pre-existing inline `mod tests` (the SOURCES crate permits inline test modules; verified GREEN `test result: ok. 31 passed; 0 failed; … 0.71s` under ci-lean `target-test`). `capped_read` is the DoS chokepoint bounding the DECODED output of every streaming decompressor (PDF FlateDecode, gzip/zstd members) and the byte cap on all cloud/web/git/stdin reads; it had 40 EXAMPLE tests but — verified by grep (`grep -rln read_to_cap | xargs grep -l proptest` → EMPTY) — NO property test fuzzed the core invariant across the input space. The new proptest generalizes all 40 point-checks into one contract over arbitrary (payload 0..2048B, cap 0..3000, hint Option<0..1M>): (1) kept length == min(input_len, cap); (2) `truncated` IFF input strictly exceeded cap; (3) kept bytes are the EXACT `input[..len]` prefix (value-integrity the scanner relies on — never reordered/corrupted); (4) NEW cross-variant equivalence no example test checked — `read_to_cap` and `read_to_cap_preserving_error` agree byte-for-byte on the clean path and the preserving variant fabricates no error for an infallible reader (Law 10: no silent divergence between the two public entry points). Non-duplicative (Testing-Contract property coverage on a security-critical primitive), collision-safe (file was CLEAN). | status=done

It had 6 hand-picked boundary examples; the new test is a COMPLETE-DOMAIN differential over all 256 bytes against an independent std oracle (`(byte as char).to_digit(16)` — lossless for 0..=255, exact same contract), which subsumes the point examples and locks the three ranges (`0-9`/`a-f`/`A-F`) against any future off-by-one edit (Law 6: truth via independent oracle over the whole input space). Byte-domain primitive so immune to the [[hashkit-hex-decode-multibyte-silent-accept]] char-vs-byte class. Collision-safe (file CLEAN). | status=done

It has THREE layered XXE defenses (a case-insensitive `<!DOCTYPE`/`<!ENTITY` pre-screen via `cloud::contains_forbidden_xml_markup`, a DocType-event reject, and a `PredefinedEntityResolver` that blocks custom-entity expansion), and the DEFENSES were verified UNTESTED: the integration tests (regression_s3_key_listing / regression_s3_listing_pagination) feed only VALID XML through a mock server (happy path only), and `internal_contracts.rs` references `parse_s3_listing` only as a source-shape WIRING string, never behaviorally. New tests: (1) happy-path parse asserting keys/sizes/is_truncated/next_continuation_token; (2) serde-default pagination fields on a terminal page (is_truncated=false, no token — not an error); (3) THE security prize — 4 hostile payloads (DOCTYPE, lowercase-`<!doctype` evasion, classic external-entity `<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">` XXE, bare `<!ENTITY>`) each rejected with "DTD/entity declarations", failing closed so an XXE-defense regression cannot land silently. Deterministic (the pre-screen is a simple case-insensitive string check). - [~] HARDENING + ONE-PLACE (2026-07-10) crates/sources/src/process_excerpt.rs — TWO axes on the always-compiled shared bounded-stderr capture `drain_stderr_excerpt` (used by every child-process integration: ghidra/docker/…). (A) ONE-PLACE/COHERENCE FIX: the truncation marker hardcoded the literal `65536` while the cap is `const STDERR_EXCERPT_BYTES = 64*1024` — a magic number that would silently LIE to the operator if the cap changed (Review Vector 10 coherence). Changed the marker to derive the byte count from the const (`format!("…{STDERR_EXCERPT_BYTES}…")`). (B) TESTING: the fn's BEHAVIOR was verified UNTESTED (the only tests-tree references are source-shape/owner GATES — `process_stderr_excerpt_owner.rs`, `hosted_git_security_contract.rs` — checking the string appears / has one owner, never behavior). Added inline `mod tests` (6 tests): under-cap verbatim (no marker), EXACTLY-cap boundary kept whole (no marker — keep==read on the last chunk), over-cap keeps the first 64K verbatim + appends the marker, non-UTF-8 lossy decode (U+FFFD, no panic), EINTR-retry (Interrupted is retried not aborted), and hard-error → "stderr unavailable: …". Assertions derive the marker from the const so they're cap-value-agnostic. **SELF-CORRECTION #7: fix (A) REVERTED** — the marker-derive-from-const change tripped `process_stderr_excerpt_owner.rs:18`, a gate that PINS the exact literal `"[stderr truncated after 65536 bytes]"` in the owner file as a contract. Per Law 9 (satisfy the gate, don't edit a gate to green my own code) I restored the literal (with a comment explaining it's gate-pinned). Fix (B), the 6 behavioral tests, STAND and stay green (their `expected_marker()` = `format!("…{STDERR_EXCERPT_BYTES}…")` evaluates to the same literal since the const IS 65536). Not feature-gated → tests verified under default `--lib` (6 passed) + owner gate re-verified green after revert. | status=done (tests landed; marker ONE-PLACE fix reverted → task below)

- [~] SECURITY-TEST (2026-07-10) crates/cli/tests/unit/orchestrator/{allowlist_root_real_extensionless_file_uses_parent,allowlist_root_real_dotted_directory_stays_itself}.rs (2 NEW files via the existing `CliTestApi::allowlist_root_for_test` facade — CLEAN files, collision-free) — closed a genuine real-FS BRANCH gap in `cli/src/orchestrator/allowlist.rs::allowlist_root`, the function that decides WHICH directory's `.keyhogignore` loads for a scan target (a wrong root silently suppresses real findings or misapplies policy — Law-10-adjacent recall risk). The 3 existing `allowlist_root_*` tests all use NON-existent paths, so they exercise only the shape HEURISTIC (extension→file, no-extension→dir, no-parent→"."); the real-FS `is_dir()`/`is_file()` branches (lines 78-87) were UNTESTED in the cases where FS-reality DISAGREES with the heuristic — exactly where those checks are load-bearing: (1) a real EXTENSIONLESS file (`Dockerfile`/`Makefile`/`LICENSE`) must anchor at its PARENT via `is_file()`, but the heuristic alone (`(no-ext, Some(parent)) => dir`) would wrongly anchor inside the file's own path → `keyhog scan ./Dockerfile` would load `.keyhogignore` from `./Dockerfile/`; (2) a real DOTTED directory (`my.config`, `repo.git`) must anchor at ITSELF via `is_dir()`, but the heuristic (`(has_ext, Some(parent)) => parent`) would wrongly anchor at its parent, loading a sibling tree's allowlist. Both tests create REAL tempdir FS entries (tempfile) so the FS probe genuinely fires. Registered alphabetically in unit/orchestrator/mod.rs (clean). VERIFIED: `cargo test -p keyhog --test all_tests` → `unit::orchestrator::allowlist_root_real_dotted_directory_stays_itself ... ok` + `...real_extensionless_file_uses_parent ... ok` (`test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed`). NOTE the sibling `load_allowlist`/`load_rule_suppressor` fail-closed (malformed `.keyhogignore(.toml)` → bail, never silent-ignore) is already guarded by the SOURCE-SHAPE gate `allowlist_policy_fail_closed_contract` (asserts the "refusing to scan with silently ignored" strings + absence of the `unwrap_or_else`/"ignoring rules" fallback) — a behavioral test would need a new facade for the pub(crate) fns for marginal gain over the shape gate, so NOT added (anti-make-work). | status=done

NOTE: verifier IS in `tests_wired.py::ENFORCED_CRATES` (test-file WIRING is green, 192 files) — these no-inline gates are a SEPARATE contract (src must carry no inline tests) and are the foreign reds. | foreign verifier lane owner: re-home the inline tests + fix the unwrap. | IN PROGRESS (2026-07-10 — DOING the migration per the stop-hook, tree stable-dirty/no live writer so safe): **2 of the ~6 inline-test files re-homed** so far — (1) `verify/request.rs` `pinned_key_tests` (3 tests) → `tests/unit/pinned_client_key.rs` via `testing::{canonical_pinned_addrs, pinned_keys_equal_for_test}` pub-fn wrappers (learned: `pub use` of a `pub(crate)` item is E0364 — must use `pub fn` wrappers/trait methods; caught by the verify); (2) `oob/session.rs` `poller_degradation_tests` (3 tests incl. an async `wait_for_timeout_honours_degraded_flag`) → `tests/unit/oob_poller_degradation.rs` via 3 `testing` pub-fn wrappers (`oob_poller_is_degraded`/`oob_elapsed_verdict`/`oob_degraded_error_threshold`, backed by a `pub(crate) use` in oob/mod.rs) + a new `VerifierTestApi::oob_session_set_degraded_for_test` accessor (ungated `set_degraded_for_test` from `#[cfg(test)]`→always-compiled pub(crate) seam) + the EXISTING `interactsh_client_for_test`/`oob_session_for_test` seams. Deleting session.rs's inline module (which held `.expect()` scaffolding calls) should ALSO clear `oob_session_no_unwrap_expect` (same mechanism as the docker unwrap gate). REMAINING (4 files, same pattern): `src/cache.rs:481`, `src/sigv4.rs:208`, `src/verify/credential.rs:714`, `src/verify/response.rs:275` — each still carries an inline `#[cfg(test)]`, keeping the GLOBAL `no_inline_tests_in_verifier_src` + their per-file gates red. Verifying the request+session pattern (b35b1wrye) before re-homing the 4. | ALL 6 RE-HOMED (2026-07-10, full verifier inline-test migration DONE, final reconcile in flight): request+session VERIFIED GREEN (b35b1wrye: `verify_request_no_inline_tests`+`oob_session_no_inline_tests`+all 6 re-homed tests `... ok`, 8 passed). Then the remaining 4: (3) `src/cache.rs` `evict_tests` — DELETED as REDUNDANT (its oldest-evicted/count-zero-noop/count≥len-clears contract is already covered byte-for-byte by `tests/unit/cache_eviction_primitive.rs` via the `evict_oldest_dashmap_survivors_for_test` facade; re-homing would DUPLICATE — ONE-PLACE; and it used `dashmap::DashMap` directly which the project deliberately keeps out of the test crate); (4) `src/sigv4.rs` `uri_encode_tests` (5 tests) → `tests/unit/sigv4_uri_encode.rs` via `testing::{aws_uri_encode, canonical_query_string}` wrappers; (5) `src/verify/credential.rs` `oob_verdict_tests` (4 tests) → `tests/unit/credential_oob_verdict.rs` via `testing::oob_combined_verdict`; (6) `src/verify/response.rs` `body_capacity_tests` → `tests/unit/response_body_capacity.rs` via `testing::{body_capacity_hint, MAX_RESPONSE_BODY_BYTES}`. Each bumped its private fn to `pub(crate)` + added a `pub fn` testing wrapper (NOT `pub use` — E0364 on pub(crate)); the 3 test bodies were extracted VERBATIM by a brace-counting script (swap `use super::`→`use keyhog_verifier::testing::`, dedent) so ZERO transcription risk. All 6 inline `#[cfg(test)]` now gone from verifier src ⇒ the GLOBAL `no_inline_tests_in_verifier_src` + all 5 per-file gates + `oob_session_no_unwrap_expect` (the `.expect()` scaffolding left with the deleted session module) should ALL clear. Final `keyhog-verifier` reconcile (bij6zh71u) confirms. | status=DONE + VERIFIED (2026-07-10): FULL `keyhog-verifier` reconcile (default features, bij6zh71u) = **1992 passed / 0 FAILED**, and ALL 7 target gates `... ok` — `gap::no_inline_tests_in_src::no_inline_tests_in_verifier_src` (GLOBAL) + `unit::gates::{cache,oob_session,verify_credential,verify_request,verify_response}_no_inline_tests` + `oob_session_no_unwrap_expect` (the `.expect()` scaffolding cleared with the deleted session module, as predicted). The ~11 new `testing` facade wrappers + 5 re-homed test files (pinned_client_key, oob_poller_degradation incl. the async fail-closed test, sigv4_uri_encode, credential_oob_verdict, response_body_capacity) integrate with ZERO regression across the full 1992-test suite. - HUNT-struct crates/scanner/src/structured/parsers/json.rs:434 | MISSING BOUNDS | medium | static JUPYTER_TEXT_OUTPUT_MIME_TYPES panics if bundled rules file is invalid | fix: log warning and use safe default list instead of panic | status=done RECONCILED not-a-bug + suggested-fix-is-WRONG: the data is `include_str!("rules/jupyter-mime-types.toml")` — EMBEDDED at compile time, so no runtime/attacker/user input can reach the parse (the "MISSING BOUNDS" runtime-crash framing is a misread). The panic is a DELIBERATE, DOCUMENTED Law-10 fail-closed: the source comment (json.rs:428-431) says verbatim "A panic here indicates a build-time defect in the bundled file, not a runtime hostile-input risk — fail-closed (Law 10), naming the file." A corrupt embedded file cannot occur in a shipped binary (it was valid at build). The proposed fix ("use a safe default list instead of panic") would be a NET-NEGATIVE Law-10 VIOLATION: silently substituting a reduced mime-type list on a bundled-file defect is exactly the silent recall-degrading fallback Law-10 bans. Keep the loud fail-closed.

The panic is a Law-10 fail-closed on a build-time-constant asset. Suggested "fallback to built-in list" is a WRONG net-negative: it would silently degrade the assignment-name-class set on a bundled-file defect (silent recall fallback, Law-10 banned). Keep fail-closed.

- HUNT-struct crates/scanner/src/structured/parsers/hcl.rs:292 | SILENT FALLBACKS | high | strip_hcl_line_comment cuts off the entire line at /*, dropping values after inline block comments | fix: correctly parse and skip inline block comments instead of truncating | status=done FIXED by ONE-PLACE unification (not a patch to the buggy body): strip_hcl_line_comment was a SECOND hand-rolled HCL comment parser that shared #/// /quote logic with the stateful owner strip_hcl_comments but diverged on /* (returned &line[..open], truncating the line and dropping trailing assignments). Deleted the divergent body; it is now a thin single-line driver over the ONE owner (`strip_hcl_comments(line, &mut false)`), returning String (correct inline-block stripping deletes interior bytes, unexpressible as a sub-slice). Call sites parse_hcl_default/parse_hcl_assignment rebound the String; brace_delta unchanged. NOTE: the bug was LATENT — every current caller receives input already stripped upstream by strip_hcl_comments, so no live recall loss existed; the defect was the ONE-PLACE divergence (two comment grammars, one wrong) + a trap for any future raw-input caller. Widened both fns to pub(crate) (matching parse_hcl) and added 5 tests to structured_hcl_parser_contract.rs: differential ONE-PLACE lock (single-line driver == owner over 11 inputs), inline-block-preserves-trailing-code, #/// -still-strip + not-in-strings, and 2 parse_hcl operator-path recall tests (value before inline block comment; assignment after multi-line block comment). VERIFIED GREEN: structured_hcl_parser_contract 15 passed / 0 failed (was 10), RC=0 ci-lean. FOLLOW-UP HARDENING (2026-07-10): added 2 more to the same file — parse_hcl_keeps_value_before_trailing_line_comment (#/// trailing-comment recall) and a 3000-case robustness proptest parse_hcl_is_total_and_deterministic_on_structural_fuzz (adversarial interleavings of #, //, /* */, quotes, escapes, braces, heredoc markers → parse_hcl must never panic, always yield 1-based line numbers, and be deterministic) — fuzzes the now-single comment grammar for panic-freedom. VERIFIED GREEN: structured_hcl_parser_contract 17 passed / 0 failed, RC=0 ci-lean — the 3000-case fuzz found NO panic, confirming the unification introduced no robustness regression.

CODE-VERIFIED 2026-07-10 (both claims accurate). BLOCKED: decode/base64.rs is FOREIGN-DIRTY — land in that lane. status=open [MERGED-6718+6719: do as ONE table, not two separate fixes]

- HUNT-decode crates/scanner/src/decode/hex.rs:86 | robustness(DoS)+perf | P2 | hex_decode underscore-path allocates a cleaned String from the FULL input BEFORE the MAX_HEX_INPUT_LEN(32MB) cap is checked → unbounded alloc | fix: bound the collect | status=open-VERIFIED-P2 [MERGED 6721+6902: SAME hex.rs:86 underscore-path allocation, 6902's DoS framing is the important one, 6721 is its perf facet] — CODE-VERIFIED 2026-07-10: `hex_decode` (hex.rs:79) fast path (no `_`) correctly checks `input.len() > MAX_HEX_INPUT_LEN` BEFORE decoding, but the underscore path (:86) does `let cleaned: String = input.chars().filter(|c| *c != '_').collect();` FIRST and checks `cleaned.len() > MAX_HEX_INPUT_LEN` AFTER — so a large underscore-laden input (e.g. `a_a_a_…`, `input.len()` ≫ 32MB) allocates a String up to `input.len()` bytes BEFORE the cap rejects it. The 32MB cap exists precisely to bound this and is defeated on the underscore path (alloc-before-guard, Law 7 + DoS). CONCRETE FIX (addresses both the DoS AND 6721's throwaway/String→bytes perf ask in one change): collect into a `Vec<u8>` capped DURING the scan — `let mut cleaned = Vec::with_capacity(input.len().min(MAX_HEX_INPUT_LEN + 1)); for b in input.bytes() { if b==b'_' {continue;} if cleaned.len() > MAX_HEX_INPUT_LEN {return Err(());} cleaned.push(b); }` — bounds the allocation to ≤MAX+1 regardless of input size (hex is ASCII so byte-filtering `_` is correct; non-ASCII bytes fail decode anyway). BLOCKED: decode/hex.rs is FOREIGN-DIRTY — land in that lane. NOTE the upstream candidate span may already bound input length, but hex_decode's own cap must still guard its own allocation (defense in depth; [[decode-structured-hotpath-dos-audit]]).

- HUNT-decode crates/scanner/src/decode/util.rs:32 | MISSING BOUNDS | medium | take_hex_digits lacks count bounds check risking u32 overflow | fix: assert or reject count > 8 | status=done RECONCILED not-a-bug (claim misreads a defined bit-shift as overflow): `value = (value << 4) | digit` is a left-shift by the CONSTANT 4 — in Rust `<<` only panics when the shift AMOUNT >= bit width (>=32), never for a constant 4, in debug OR release. For count>8 it would silently DROP high bits (not panic), but that path is unreachable: ALL call sites pass count = 2 (`\xNN`) or 4 (`\uNNNN`) — verified every caller (json/unicode_escape/resolve_escaped_codepoint), max 16 bits into a 32-bit accumulator (u32 holds 8 hex digits). `count` is a fixed per-escape-type literal, never attacker-controlled. No overflow, no panic, no bounds bug. Reachable behavior IS tested: tests/unit/a3_decode/take_hex_digits_empty_iterator.rs (short-iterator → Err) + unit/decode.rs (valid packing). A defensive `debug_assert!(count <= 8)` would document the contract but guards nothing reachable.
- HUNT-decode crates/scanner/src/decode/url.rs:428 | MISSING BOUNDS | low | html_named_entity_decode truncates entity names at 10 chars | fix: increase limit to 32 | status=done RECONCILED not-a-bug: the `entity.len() > 10` break is a DoS accumulation bound (stop scanning a malformed `&veryLongGarbage…` that is not a real entity), directly parallel to the DOCUMENTED MAX_NUMERIC_ENTITY_DIGITS=10 bound immediately below it (url.rs:446-451). It can NEVER truncate a real decode: HTML_NAMED_ENTITIES loads from rules/html-named-entities.toml (url.rs:371) which holds 8 entities whose LONGEST key is 5 chars (`quot;`/`nbsp;`/`apos;`) — every key sits comfortably under the cap, so the `;` terminator always breaks first for a valid entity. No entity is lost. "Increase limit to 32" would only widen the DoS window with zero recall benefit unless/until a >10-char key is added to the Tier-B file (at which point the cap should track the file's max, not a magic 32). Correct as-is for the current data.

- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/verify/credential.rs:659 | SILENT FALLBACKS | High | OOBOptional policy returns http_only_result directly, ignoring successful OOB observations. | fix: Return Live if observed is true, else fallback to http_only_result. | status=intentional 2026-07-09 (design decision, explicitly CONTRACTED — NOT a reflex fix): `oob_combined_verdict`'s `OobOptional => http_only_result` arm is DELIBERATELY tested by `oob_optional_always_passes_through_http` (credential.rs:798), which loops `observed ∈ {false,true}` specifically to assert the observation is IGNORED under OobOptional. So the current behavior is an intentional conservative-HTTP-authoritative choice ("OOB is optional/best-effort telemetry, recorded in metadata `oob_observed` but not verdict-flipping — avoids a false-Live from a spurious callback"), the opposite of the finding's proposal. The finding's alternative (observed⇒Live, consistent with OobOnly) is ALSO defensible, but flipping it would REWRITE a deliberate contract test (Law 6/9: failing/changed contract tests are findings, not license to weaken). This is a genuine product tradeoff (conservative vs OOB-can-upgrade) that is the MAINTAINER's to decide, not a silent-bug fix. Also NOTE: zero shipped detector TOMLs set any `oob` policy (all `oob:null`), so the arm is inert in practice regardless. status=needs-maintainer-decision (do NOT reflex-flip a contracted verdict).
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/oob/session.rs:505 | SILENT FALLBACKS | High | Persistent OOB poll failures are not propagated to waiters, leading to silent timeouts and incorrect Dead verdicts. | fix: Track consecutive errors and trigger session abort/disable on threshold. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): added a `degraded: AtomicBool` on OobSession set by the poller once `poller_is_degraded(consecutive_errors)` (>= OOB_DEGRADED_ERROR_THRESHOLD=3) and cleared on the next successful poll. `wait_for`'s two timeout returns now call `elapsed_verdict(degraded)` → returns `Disabled("OOB collector unreachable … inconclusive, not a dead secret")` (verifier fails closed) instead of a false `NotObserved` when the channel is down (Law 10). Pure decisions `poller_is_degraded`/`elapsed_verdict` are single-owner + unit-tested (threshold boundary + verdict mapping, 2/2 green). NOTE: full poller→flag→Disabled integration needs a failing-client seam (InteractshClient is concrete); pure-fn tests + wiring review cover it.

- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/verify/response.rs:123 | PERF | Medium | Parses the JSON response body multiple times in evaluate_success and extract_metadata. | fix: Parse once and pass the parsed Value reference. | status=open (VERIFIED real 2026-07-09, deferred as a contained multi-file refactor): a single response body is `serde_json::from_str`-parsed up to 3× per verification — evaluate_success (when spec.json_path set, response.rs:123), body_indicates_error (:155), extract_metadata (:238). Parse-once needs threading `Option<&serde_json::Value>` (parsed once at the call site) through all THREE signatures + their 3 call sites (verify/credential.rs, verify/multi_step.rs, verify/mod.rs) — all FOREIGN-DIRTY. Best done as ONE focused unit when those files settle (or with per-call-site local parse+share), not rushed. NOT a false positive; scoped and left open.
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/verify/aws.rs:108 | SILENT FALLBACKS | Low | Missing region companion returns generic invalid AWS region error instead of missing-companion resolution error. | fix: Validate region companion presence in companions map before resolving. | status=open
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/verify/credential.rs:475 | SILENT FALLBACKS | High | verify_credential returns early for AwsV4 self-constructing probes, bypassing custom headers, success conditions, and OOB. | fix: Unify AwsV4 probe path with standard verification pipeline. | status=false 2026-07-09 (STALE — the paths are ALREADY unified; line drifted). The AwsV4 branch (credential.rs:403-430) does NOT return early or bypass the pipeline. `is_self_constructing_auth` diverges ONLY for URL handling — AwsV4 self-constructs its STS endpoint in `build_aws_probe`, so it uses a placeholder URL and SKIPS the `url_template` resolve/SSRF-screen (correct: it has no url_template; comment 398-402 "Non-AwsV4 auth still resolves + SSRF-screens"). It then CONVERGES with every other auth at :486+ through the SAME `validate_header_body_templates` + `apply_header_body_templates` (custom headers, :509), request execution, success evaluation, and OOB. The cited fix ("unify the paths") describes the CURRENT state. The finding's line:475 now points at a `credential` arg inside the shared `build_request_for_step` call, not any early-return (heavy refactor drift). Completes the HUNT-verifier triage: 6706=intentional-contracted, 6714=stale-already-unified.

- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/verify/credential.rs:607 | PERF | Medium | OOB verifier unconditionally blocks on wait_for even if the HTTP response already failed and policy requires both to succeed. | fix: Short-circuit OOB wait if HTTP fails under OobAndHttp. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): extracted the policy truth-table into a pure `oob_combined_verdict(policy, http_only_result, http_live, observed)` (ONE testable owner), then added a short-circuit in combine_oob: under OobAndHttp with !http_live the verdict is provably http_only_result regardless of `observed` (the !http_live arm), so we skip the up-to-30s wait_for and return immediately, recording oob_observed=false + oob_skipped=http-failed-under-oob-and-http. Verdict-preserving (only skips a positive observation for a credential whose HTTP already failed under OobAndHttp — doomed either way). NEW inline tests oob_verdict_tests (4): the short-circuit invariant (OobAndHttp+!http_live ignores observed) + full OobAndHttp/OobOnly/OobOptional truth table. Dirty file, additive. NOTE: sibling finding credential.rs:659 (OobOptional ignores successful OOB — should it return Live if observed?) is a SEPARATE product-semantics question left open; my change does not alter OobOptional behavior.
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/cache.rs:392 | PERF | Medium | evict_oldest_dashmap_entries collects and sorts entire DashMap, causing large allocations and CPU overhead on cap hit. | fix: Use select_nth_unstable to find oldest count entries without full sorting. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): replaced the O(n log n) full `sort_unstable_by_key` with `select_nth_unstable_by_key(count, ..)` + truncate — O(n) partition of just the oldest `count` (the only ones removed) on every cap-hit. Behavior-preserving (same eviction set; boundary ties arbitrary in both). Guarded `count==0` (no-op) and `count>=len` (remove all, avoids the select_nth index panic). NEW inline tests cache::evict_tests (2): exact oldest-count evicted + newest retained; count=0 noop + count>=len clears without panic. Dirty file, additive perf-only edit.
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/rate_limit.rs:110 | PERF | High | Service rate limit is halved on 429 but never recovers or increases back to default when requests succeed. | fix: Implement AIMD or reset rate limit after a period of successful requests. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): CONFIRMED REAL (wording imprecise — it was a HARD-SET, not a halving) + FIXED with proper AIMD. Root cause: on HTTP 429 both verify/multi_step.rs:149 and verify/credential.rs:563 called `update_limit(service, 0.5)` — a hard SET to 0.5 rps (2s interval) that pinned the service to that pace for the whole process lifetime and NEVER recovered, even after thousands of successes (Law 7 throughput bug; the global record_error/record_success counter self-recovers but the per-service override did not). FIX (7 edit sites, rate_limit.rs + credential.rs + multi_step.rs + new test): (1) ServiceLimit gained `base_interval` (the configured target/floor), set at all construction sites (wait lazy-create, update_limit); (2) new `penalize_service(service)` = MULTIPLICATIVE DECREASE (interval *= 2 via checked_mul, capped at max(RATE_LIMIT_MAX_INTERVAL=8s, base) — never faster than base, never overflows/panics; seeds a backed-off slot if absent); (3) new `reward_service(service)` = ADDITIVE INCREASE (interval -= base/2 per success, saturating_sub floored at base_interval; no-op if absent/at base); (4) new `service_interval(service)` introspection accessor; (5) the two 429 sites now call penalize_service (sync, no await); (6) record_rate_limit_feedback gained a `service` param and calls reward_service on the Live/Dead/Revoked success arm (one unified feedback funnel; retry_loop threads &spec.service through — 3 callers updated + 5 test-helper calls); (7) update_limit kept (async) as the clean per-service CONFIG setter (sets base_interval=interval), no longer the backoff mechanism (its only prior callers were the 2 429 sites; still pinned by new_verifier_allowlist_cache::rate_limiter_update_limit_sets_per_service_override). All AIMD consts hoisted as single named owners (RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER/MAX_INTERVAL/RECOVERY_STEP_DIVISOR, Tier-A-tunable-later). Added tests/unit/rate_limit_aimd_recovery.rs (4 tokio tests, EXACT-Duration assertions: 429 doubles 100→200→400ms, success recovers -50ms/step floored at base after 6, cap saturates at 8s over 40 penalties without overflow + recovers off the ceiling, penalize-unknown seeds 2x-default slot, reward-unknown is no-op). rate_limit is `pub mod` so the test hits RateLimiter directly — no facade plumbing. Global-backpressure test rate_limit_feedback (record_error/record_success counter) UNTOUCHED, stays green. Verified against pinning tests before editing (429-classification tests assert RateLimited/transient — unchanged; update_limit override test — kept). | RECONCILE GREEN @b8bgy8wk8: -p keyhog-verifier --test all_tests + --test new_verifier_allowlist_cache (filter rate_limit) = 46 passed + 8 passed / 0 failed (incl 4 new AIMD tests + feedback + per-service-override + typical-intervals + nan-clamp). Lib compiled clean across both binaries (retry_loop/record_rate_limit_feedback signature changes + rate_limit.rs AIMD).
- HUNT-verifier NEXT-CANDIDATE rate_limit.rs:134 | reserve_service_slot reserves a future slot (rolls last_request forward) BEFORE the tokio::time::sleep; if the wait future is cancelled/dropped mid-sleep the reservation is not reverted → later callers wait for a slot that will never be used (artificial delay). REAL in principle but low practical impact (verify futures run to completion in the retry loop, rarely cancelled mid-wait); a drop-guard that reverts the reservation adds complexity. status=open (evaluate after AIMD reconcile).
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/rate_limit.rs:134 | PERF | Medium | reserve_service_slot reserves future slots that are never released if request is cancelled, causing artificial delays. | fix: Return a guard that can revert reservation on request cancellation. | status=open
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/oob/session.rs:528 | PERF | Low | Persistent poll errors skip the GC step in spawn_poller due to continue statement, leaking memory. | fix: Move the GC check to a place where it always runs regardless of poll success/failure. | status=DONE (2026-07-09, fixed alongside :505): moved the `next_gc` GC block to the TOP of the poller loop (right after the shutdown check) so it runs every iteration including error iterations that `continue`. Previously the error arm's `continue` jumped past the post-poll GC, so `observations` grew unbounded for the full duration of a collector outage — exactly when the poller couldn't drain it. Verified by the oob compile + poller_degradation tests.

- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/verify/request.rs:274 | PERF | High | PinnedClientKey includes exact IP list, causing new reqwest Client creation whenever DNS returns round-robin IPs in a different order. | fix: Sort/normalize IP list or key cache only by host. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): added `canonical_pinned_addrs()` (sort_unstable) and keyed PINNED_CLIENT_CACHE on the CANONICAL (sorted) address set, so a DNS round-robin reorder of the same A/AAAA records is a cache HIT instead of rebuilding a fresh reqwest Client (TLS+pool) every request. `build_pinned_client` still receives the original order (the pin is a set; connection-attempt order unchanged).
