- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/verify/response.rs:77 | PERF | Low | read_response_body does not pre-allocate capacity for the response body vector, leading to multiple dynamic heap reallocations. | fix: Pre-allocate capacity using response content length. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): preallocate `Vec::with_capacity` from Content-Length via a new `body_capacity_hint(Option<u64>)` helper. SECURITY-aware: the header is attacker-controlled, so the hint is CLAMPED to MAX_RESPONSE_BODY_BYTES (1 MiB) in u64 space before the usize cast — a lying `Content-Length: u64::MAX` can't make us reserve gigabytes (the streaming loop still enforces the true cap). NEW inline test body_capacity_tests: None→0, honest→exact, at-cap→cap, u64::MAX & cap+1→clamped. Dirty file, additive.
- HUNT-verifier crates/verifier/src/sigv4.rs:72 | PERF | Medium | aws_uri_encode calls format! inside a loop to hex-encode bytes, causing multiple heap allocations and CPU overhead. | fix: Use a static lookup table or write! to append hex directly without allocations. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): replaced `push_str(&format!("%{byte:02X}"))` with a static `HEX_UPPER` table pushing `%`+2 nibbles directly — zero per-byte alloc, byte-identical UPPERCASE `%XX`. Guarded by NEW inline KAT tests sigv4::uri_encode_tests (5 tests incl. a differential over ALL ASCII bytes vs `format!("%{b:02X}")`, multibyte é/€, and the canonical_query_string sort+escape contract). 5/5 green.
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/stdin.rs:116 | PERF | P3 | String::from_utf8_lossy allocates a new string even on valid UTF-8 | fix: attempt String::from_utf8(read.bytes) first, fallback to lossy only on Err | status=done 2026-07-09: `match String::from_utf8(read.bytes) { Ok(t)=>t, Err(e)=>from_utf8_lossy(&e.into_bytes()).into_owned() }`. UNLIKE the window case (6728, no-win — borrowed slice from a REUSED buffer must copy either way), stdin's `read.bytes` is an OWNED, single-use `Vec` → `from_utf8` CONSUMES it and reuses the allocation ZERO-COPY on the common valid-UTF-8 path (only the rare invalid input pays the lossy re-encode); the old `from_utf8_lossy(&bytes).into_owned()` copied the whole stdin buffer. Behavior-identical (both branches already covered by regression_stdin_chunk lone_continuation_byte_lossy_replaced + truncated_multibyte_tail_lossy_replaced + valid tests). Verifying build.
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/filesystem/read/window.rs:321 | PERF | P3 | String::from_utf8_lossy into_owned allocates a new String for every sliding window | fix: attempt std::str::from_utf8 first, fallback to lossy only on error | status=resolved-INVALID (2026-07-09): no win possible. `FileWindow.text` is an OWNED String (SensitiveString needs ownership; buffer is reused across overlapping windows so borrowing isn't viable). In the valid path `from_utf8(slice).map(str::to_owned)` allocates+copies IDENTICALLY to `from_utf8_lossy(slice).into_owned()` (both scan once + copy once). In the INVALID path the proposed `from_utf8`-then-lossy DOUBLE-scans (from_utf8 fails, then lossy re-scans) — a pessimization. std's `from_utf8_lossy` already tries `from_utf8` internally and returns Cow::Borrowed on valid input, so it is not doing extra work. Finding rests on a false premise; no change.
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/filesystem/read/window.rs:300 | PANIC/BOUNDS | P2 | assert!(window_size > overlap) causes a panic if window configuration is invalid | fix: validate window size/overlap and return an Error instead of panicking | status=false 2026-07-09 (NOT user-reachable — downgrade from P2): window_size/overlap are NOT user-configurable. Production uses the compile-time consts DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE (1 MiB) / DEFAULT_WINDOW_OVERLAP (128 KiB) where 1MiB > 128KiB ✓; no CLI/TOML flag sets them (grep of cli/src: only a doc-comment mention of the fixed 1 MiB window, no `--window-size` flag). The ONLY setter `FilesystemSource::with_window_config` is `pub(crate)` and called EXCLUSIVELY by tests (via the testing facade), and it ALREADY asserts `window_size > overlap` at the config boundary (filesystem.rs:556). The window.rs:286 `assert!` is thus a redundant internal-invariant guard on an unreachable condition — not a user-facing robustness bug. Converting it to a Result would thread an error through a hot slicer for a case that can't occur (contorting the architecture for an unreachable branch, against the no-regret/minimal-surface laws). No change; the invariant assert stays as documentation of the stride precondition.
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/filesystem/read/raw.rs:328 | ONE-PLACE | P4 | redundant flock call on a descriptor already locked in open_file_safe | fix: rely entirely on the flock performed inside open_file_safe | status=DONE (2026-07-09): VERIFIED real — read_file_mmap opens via open_file_safe (raw.rs:94) which already takes LOCK_SH (raw.rs:146); the re-flock at :328 was a redundant syscall whose "locked by another process" branch was DEAD (a LOCK_SH we already hold blocks any new LOCK_EX, so the re-request catches nothing new). Removed it; the lock stays held for file's lifetime, spanning the mmap. ENCODED the previously-UNTESTED flock DoS/torn-write guard: NEW test filesystem::read::tests::externally_exclusive_locked_file_is_refused_by_open_and_mmap (separate-OFD LOCK_EX → open_file_safe Err AND read_file_mmap None; release → both succeed). 14 read tests green. SIBLINGS NOW ALSO DONE (same class, both clean): removed the redundant re-flock at bytes.rs:151 and window.rs:154 too, PRESERVING their deliberate post-mmap LOCK_UN (bytes:174, window:228, each with its own AsRawFd import). All 3 read paths (mmap/compressed/windowed) open via open_file_safe and now rely on its single LOCK_SH — ONE owner of the flock guard. status=DONE (all 3 sites: raw:328 + bytes:151 + window:154)
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/git/diff.rs:704 | SILENT FALLBACKS | P2 | binary or non-UTF-8 untracked files return a hard Err aborting diff scan | fix: skip the untracked file (return Ok(None)) instead of returning Err | status=resolved-INVALID (2026-07-09): the premise is FALSE — the Err does NOT abort the scan. The caller yields it as a per-ITEM `Ok(Some(Err(error)))` (diff.rs:562); the untracked walk CONTINUES to the next path, and sibling files still scan. This is a DELIBERATE operator-visible signal ("this file was not scanned"), pinned by TWO clean tests: binary_untracked_git_diff_file_is_counted_binary (asserts exactly ONE SourceError naming the file + reason + a Binary skip-event bump, Law-10) and untracked_symlink_git_diff_is_visible_unreadable_and_safe_sibling_scans (asserts the safe sibling STILL scans after the error). Changing Err→Ok(None) would DELETE that intentional signal and BREAK both tests (Law 6/9). Reverted my exploratory edit. NOT a bug — the loud per-item error IS the designed recall-safe behavior. LESSON (again): read the pinning tests before "fixing" a deliberate error path.
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/cloud/mod.rs:554 | SILENT FALLBACKS | P2 | strict String::from_utf8 crashes download on invalid text-type cloud object decode | fix: decode lossily using String::from_utf8_lossy to prevent aborts | status=FRAMING-INVALID-VERIFIED (2026-07-09): does NOT crash and is NOT silent. The Err arm (cloud/mod.rs:556-570) tracing::warn!s "skipping cloud object: ... NOT scanned" AND returns record_unscanned_object_skip(SourceSkipEvent::Unreadable) → counts.unreadable → source_coverage_incomplete() → EXIT_SOURCE_FAILED. LOUD + coverage-recorded + fail-closed (Law-10 compliant). Also: a binary pre-filter already ran (line 545-553 "octet-stream body is binary after capped decode"), so this path is text-typed-but-invalid-UTF-8 only. || REAL underlying item (re-scoped, NOT the stated bug): a RECALL INCONSISTENCY — filesystem scans non-UTF-8 files LOSSY (from_utf8_lossy at filesystem/read/decode.rs:42,93 + window.rs:307), so a latin-1/one-bad-byte text file IS scanned there, but cloud/web strict-skip the same content. Switching cloud/web to from_utf8_lossy would recover those + remove spurious coverage-incomplete failures, matching the established filesystem pattern (unification). BUT it is a RECALL/FP-behavior change (lossy garbage → possible FP) that touches the deliberate fail-closed contract → needs a BENCH-VALIDATED decision (mirror+creddata), not a blind flip. status=open-recall-consistency-DESIGN (bench-gated; the "crash/silent" bug framing is refuted).
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/har.rs:453 | PERF | P3 | base64 bodies are decoded and scanned without checking looks_binary first | fix: run looks_binary check on decoded base64 bytes before creating chunks | status=open
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/filesystem.rs:211 | ONE-PLACE | P4 | resolved_link_target_for_classification doesn't handle transitive links recursively | fix: walk link chain recursively up to a depth limit to classify target | status=open
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/docker/oci.rs:291 | PANIC/BOUNDS | P2 | verify_oci_blob_sha256 uses raw File::open, which hangs indefinitely on FIFOs | fix: use open_file_safe instead of raw File::open | status=DONE (2026-07-09): VERIFIED real ONE-PLACE inconsistency — every other sources read path (git/diff, binary, docker/file_read, zip_scan) opens via crate::filesystem::open_file_safe, but verify_oci_blob_sha256 used raw File::open. A malicious OCI layout with a FIFO blob would HANG the scan (File::open+read blocks on a writerless FIFO); a symlink blob would be FOLLOWED out of the layout. Routed it through open_file_safe (O_NONBLOCK + O_NOFOLLOW + regular-file check + LOCK_SH), removed the now-orphaned `use std::fs::File`. NEW inline tests safe_open_tests (docker feature): happy-path regular blob verifies + wrong-digest rejected; DIFFERENTIAL — a symlink to the same content/digest is REFUSED (raw File::open would have followed it → Ok). FIFO/socket/dev refusal transitively guaranteed by open_file_safe's tested contract.

Same recall-consistency DESIGN item as cloud/mod.rs:554 above (filesystem scans non-UTF-8 lossy; web strict-errors) — a bench-gated recall/FP decision, not a crash/silent bug. status=open-recall-consistency-DESIGN (shares the cloud row's verdict; bench-gated).
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/web.rs:472 | PERF | P2 | double JSON parsing of sourcemap responses in is_sourcemap_shaped_json and expand_sourcemap_body | fix: parse once and pass the parsed Value down | status=done 2026-07-09: `handle_json` now parses the body ONCE (`serde_json::from_str::<Value>`) and, on a sourcemap-shaped value, hands the parsed Value straight to the new `expand_sourcemap_value(map, body, url)` instead of re-parsing. Removed `is_sourcemap_shaped_json` (parse+check) → `is_sourcemap_shaped_value(&Value)` (check only); `expand_sourcemap_body` (the handle_sourcemap path, still one parse) now delegates to `expand_sourcemap_value`. Halves JSON parses on the application/json sourcemap path. Behavior-covered by regression_web_response_classification: extensionless_sourcemap_routes_by_content_type (application/json → handle_json → sourcemap expand, my refactored arm) + ordinary_json_response_stays_raw_web_text (else arm). Verifying web build.
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/git/history.rs:324 | SILENT FALLBACKS | P2 | path sanitization failure in git history returns hard Err aborting history scan | fix: skip the file's added lines and emit a warning instead of aborting | status=false 2026-07-09 (framing INACCURATE — code ALREADY does the fix): history.rs:319-328 does NOT `return Err`/abort. On `invalid_path` it `tracing::warn!("...added lines for that file were NOT scanned")` + `record_skip_event(SourceSkipEvent::Unreadable)` + `pending_errors.push_back(...)` and CONTINUES the loop (next line `current_path = ...`). So it already SKIPS the file's added lines + warns + records coverage-incomplete (fail-closed) exactly as the "fix" requests — plus the coverage signal the fix would drop. No change.

- META-VERDICT 2026-07-09 (HUNT-sources "SILENT FALLBACKS" cluster is SYSTEMATICALLY MIS-FRAMED): 6732 (cloud), 6736 (web), 6738 (git history), 6742 (tag messages), 6743 (git untracked-diff non-file) all describe the code as "silently swallows / aborts the scan," but EVERY one actually does loud `tracing::warn!` + `record_skip_event(...)` (coverage-incomplete) + CONTINUES (per-item skip via `errors.push_back;continue` or a yielded `Ok(Some(Err))` stream item, NOT abort) = Law-10 fail-closed, the OPPOSITE of a silent fallback. In 3 of 4 the proposed fix (`Ok(None)` / "just warn") would DELETE the coverage signal = a Law-10 REGRESSION. The ONLY real residue across the cluster is ONE shared bench-gated DESIGN question: should binary/undecodable git/cloud/web content be printable-strings-EXTRACTED (like the filesystem binary path) instead of skip-with-coverage-gap? That is a recall/coverage tradeoff needing mirror+creddata validation, NOT a reflex per-site edit. Future HUNT "silent fallback" rows on sources should be verified against this pattern before triage (the Flash-agent hunt conflated "returns Err" with "aborts + silent"). status=meta-note

- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/binary/sections.rs:15 | SILENT FALLBACKS | P3 | unresolved section name returns "" which is silently skipped from high-value scanning | fix: return a synthetic name (e.g. "unresolved") to force high-value scan | status=open
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/git/diff.rs:680 | PANIC/BOUNDS | P2 | symlinks/unsupported file types in untracked check return hard Err aborting diff scan | fix: skip the non-file path and warn instead of returning Err | status=false 2026-07-09 (same mis-framed cluster — does NOT abort): `read_untracked_worktree_chunk` returns Err for a non-regular-file (symlink/FIFO/device) untracked path after `tracing::warn!` + `record_skip_event(Unreadable)`, but the caller (diff.rs:562) wraps it as `Ok(Some(Err(error)))` — YIELDING the error as one stream item (coverage-incomplete, fail-closed), NOT propagating an abort; the enumeration CONTINUES to the next untracked path (`Ok(Some(...))` ≠ iterator-done). Proposed `Ok(None)` skip would delete the coverage signal (Law-10 regression). Joins the [[HUNT-sources silent-fallback cluster meta-verdict]] (6732/6736/6738/6742). No change; the security intent (refuse to read a symlink/FIFO as a regular file — TOCTOU/DoS defense) is also load-bearing.
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/git/tag_messages.rs:211 | SILENT FALLBACKS | P2 | binary or non-UTF8 tag messages return a hard error failing repository scan | fix: skip the tag message (return Ok(None)) instead of returning Err | status=false 2026-07-09 (framing INACCURATE + proposed fix is a Law-10 REGRESSION): (1) it does NOT "fail the repository scan" — `decode_tag_message_chunk`'s Err is handled by BOTH callers (`decode_unreachable_tag_message_chunks`:104 + `decode_tag_message_chunks`:132) as `errors.push_back(error); continue;` → the scan CONTINUES to the next tag; the error only marks coverage-incomplete (→ EXIT_SOURCE_FAILED at end), and `SourceSkipEvent::Binary` is recorded. That is loud + coverage-recorded + fail-closed = Law-10 COMPLIANT, not a silent fallback. (2) The proposed fix (`Ok(None)` = silently skip) would DELETE the coverage-incomplete signal → a binary tag message would be silently not-scanned with no record = a Law-10 REGRESSION (the opposite of the fix's intent). (3) The only defensible change is the SAME recall-consistency DESIGN question as [[cloud/mod.rs:554 / web.rs:711]] (6732/6736): should binary git content be printable-strings-EXTRACTED (like the filesystem binary path via extract_printable_strings) instead of error-skipped? That's a bench-gated recall/coverage behavior change, not a reflex Ok(None). status=needs-bench-decision (recall-consistency, shares 6732/6736 verdict).
- HUNT-sources crates/sources/src/docker/layer.rs:179 | PERF | P3 | std::fs::canonicalize is called per chunk path in docker layers, causing fd/sys call overhead | fix: strip prefix from the already-clean walker path directly | status=false 2026-07-09 (DUPLICATE of the DONE row at §perf "docker/layer.rs:179 ... done 2026-07-04" AND its extra fix would break security): (1) the layer_root re-canonicalization was ALREADY fixed 2026-07-04 (`rewrite_layer_chunks` canonicalizes the invariant root ONCE and threads it in; doc comment 168-171 documents this). (2) The ONLY remaining per-chunk `canonicalize` is LOAD-BEARING SECURITY, not removable: it resolves symlinks/`..` in the untrusted docker-layer chunk path, then `strip_prefix(normalized_root)` enforces the chunk is INSIDE the layer root — a symlink-escape/path-traversal defense against a hostile layer. "Strip prefix from the walker path directly" (the proposed fix) would skip symlink resolution → reopen the escape. The doc calls it "the one unavoidable canonicalize." No change. This is the SAME pattern as the sources silent-fallback cluster: a HUNT "perf/silent" row proposing to remove load-bearing security/coverage code.
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/merkle_spec_hash.rs:8 | SILENT FALLBACKS | medium | compute_spec_hash fails to hash new DetectorSpec fields (like credential_shape, min_confidence, entropy_floor), bypassing cache invalidation on TOML change. | fix: Update compute_spec_hash to hash all DetectorSpec fields. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): CONFIRMED REAL + FIXED. The 2026-07-07 per-detector-knobs migration added 14 scan-output-affecting fields to DetectorSpec (kind, min_confidence, entropy_floor, entropy_high/low/very_high, mixed_alnum_floor, keyword_free_min_len, min_len, allowlist_paths/values, stopwords, structural_password_slot, weak_anchor, private_key_block, credential_shape) — EVERY one overrides a match/suppress decision, yet compute_spec_hash hashed NONE. Editing any in a detector's TOML left the digest unchanged → merkle cache kept a stale "skip this file" → scan silently ignored the new config (Law-10 silent staleness, same class as the already-fixed severity/client_safe keys). FIX: extended the per-detector key set in compute_spec_hash with one id-bound key per knob, EMITTED ONLY WHEN NON-DEFAULT so the bare-detector pre-image (BLAKE3("id:x\nsev:x:High\n"), pinned by spec_hash_of_bare_detector_matches_hand_fed_blake3) is byte-for-byte preserved on upgrade (no spurious full re-scan). f64s hashed by exact IEEE-754 bits (to_bits→{:016x}), never lossy decimal. entropy_floor bucket order is index-bound (semantic); allowlists/stopwords sorted (OR-any, order-insensitive, no cache thrash). verify + tests + name/description DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED (don't change the scanned finding SET) + documented in-code. Added 22 regression tests to regression_compute_spec_hash.rs (16 per-knob exact-inequality via knob_changes_digest! macro + threshold-non-collision + floor-order-load-bearing + allowlist-order-NOT-load-bearing + id-binding + verify/description-exclusion + bare-preimage-survives-migration). `-p keyhog-core --test regression_compute_spec_hash` GREEN 38 passed / 0 failed. PatternSpec (regex/group/client_safe hashed; description=cosmetic excluded) + CompanionSpec (all 4 fields hashed) confirmed already-complete — no gap.

- HUNT-core crates/core/src/allowlist.rs:188 | SILENT FALLBACKS | medium | If system clock is before UNIX_EPOCH, parse_with_policy returns an empty Allowlist, silently ignoring the entire .keyhogignore file. | fix: Return Err to abort the scan or continue parsing without expiry check. | status=resolved-INVALID (2026-07-09): FALSE POSITIVE — the production path is ALREADY fail-closed, exactly as the "fix" asks. Chain: try_today_days_since_epoch (metadata.rs:149→days_since_epoch:157) returns Err("system clock is before UNIX_EPOCH ({e}); fix host time before loading allowlist suppressions") on a pre-epoch clock; parse_with_policy (allowlist.rs:188-193) does NOT silently return empty — it push_policy_violation(1,"<allowlist>","system_clock",detail) THEN returns; load_with_metadata_policy (allowlist.rs:158-160) maps ANY policy_violation to `return Err(policy_violations_error(path))` → the scan ABORTS with an operator-actionable message. NOT a silent fallback (the violation is recorded + surfaced + fail-closed). The ONLY entry that returns the allowlist without checking violations is `parse()`/`allowlist_parse` — and it is TEST-ONLY (sole caller crates/core/src/testing.rs:364, pub(crate)). Already pinned by tests: unit/allowlist_system_clock_before_epoch_rejected.rs (leaf Err + exact actionable message) + unit/allowlist_expired_entries_dropped.rs (expired→dropped AND load fails loudly with ".keyhogignore"/"expired allowlist policy"/"refusing to scan"). Considered a parse_with_policy-chain test via an injected clock but rejected: parse_with_policy reads the wall clock (not injectable) and adding a #[doc(hidden)] pub seam for marginal wiring coverage of an already-correct+leaf-tested fail-closed path violates minimal-public-surface (ARCH vector); the expiry tests use expires=1970-01-01 (unconditionally past) so they are NOT date-fragile — no determinism gap to fix. LESSON (recurring): the HUNT "SILENT FALLBACKS medium" cluster has a very high false-positive rate — this codebase has had extensive Law-10 hardening, so most flagged "silent" paths are already fail-closed. Read the caller chain before fixing.
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/config.rs:43 | MISSING BOUNDS | low | ScanConfig deserializes without calling validate(), allowing invalid configurations (e.g. min_confidence > 1.0) to be silently accepted. | fix: Implement custom deserialization or #[serde(try_from)] to enforce validation. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): finding MECHANISM inaccurate but the cited GAP was REAL and is FIXED. ScanConfig (keyhog-core) is NOT TOML-deserialized — it is built from clap-bounded ScanArgs via ResolvedScanConfig, so ScanConfig::validate() being uncalled is belt-and-suspenders for CLI use. BUT the concrete example the row names (config `min_confidence > 1.0` silently accepted) WAS a live Law-10 silent failure on the `.keyhog.toml` MERGE path: cli/src/config/scan.rs applied `config.min_confidence` / `[scan].min_confidence` as a RAW f64 with NO range check, while its siblings `ml_threshold`/`decode_depth` right beside it already route through the CLI validators. A `.keyhog.toml` with `min_confidence = 5.0` (the exact value the CLI fails closed on) was honored and suppressed EVERY finding → clean empty scan, exit 0 (silent zero recall). FIX: new `parse_config_min_confidence` helper routes both apply sites (flat `apply_top_level_scan_fields:358` + nested `apply_scan_section:181`) through the SAME canonical `parse_min_confidence` bound-checker (ONE-PLACE: the [0.0,1.0] range lives in exactly one validator), pushing an invalid-config error (exit 2, "invalid .keyhog.toml configuration" + "min_confidence must be between 0.0 and 1.0") identical to the CLI. Flipped the documented characterization test min_confidence_range_validation_differs→matches_between_cli_and_config (it explicitly invited this fix) + added config_min_confidence_boundaries_and_scan_section_all_validate (negative -0.5, nested [scan] 2.0, in-range 0.5 still accepted). `-p keyhog --test regression_cli_config_path min_confidence` GREEN 6 passed / 0 failed (2 new/flipped + 4 pre-existing in-range/CLI-precedence tests still pass). FOLLOW-ON (separate row): ScanConfig::validate() itself is genuinely uncalled (Law-11 UTILIZATION) — for the keyhog-core LIBRARY consumer surface (cargo add keyhog-core, building ScanConfig directly) it should be wired at the construction boundary or removed; tracked below.
- crates/core/src/config.rs (ScanConfig::validate + from_toml_str) | UTILIZATION+MISSING-BOUNDS (config.rs:43 follow-on) | P2 | DONE (2026-07-09): the dead-but-tested `validate()` is now LIVE via a public path AND made COMPLETE. (1) `validate()` + `ConfigError` promoted pub(crate)→pub (auto-re-exported via api.rs `pub use crate::config::*` → `keyhog_core::ConfigError`); (2) validate() extended from 2 checks to 5 — added `ml_weight ∈ [0,1]` (documented "0.0 to 1.0" but validated NOWHERE, CLI OR config), `entropy_bpe_max_chars_per_token` finite & >0 (its own doc calls 0.0 "a silent recall wipeout" — the serde default only guards OMITTED keys, not an explicit 0.0), `entropy_threshold` finite (NaN → `entropy>=NaN` always false → whole-surface wipeout). All NaN-safe (RangeInclusive::contains is false for NaN; entropy checks reject non-finite explicitly). Large FINITE bpe bound still accepted (docs: "effectively disables the gate"); finite negative entropy_threshold accepted (permissive). (3) NEW `pub fn from_toml_str(&str) -> Result<ScanConfig, ConfigError>` = the published-library validated loader COMPOSING the documented two-step (deserialize UNvalidated by design per regression_scan_config_fields:48 + separate validate) into one fail-closed entry, so an external `cargo add keyhog-core` consumer's `min_confidence=5.0`/`ml_weight=2.0`/`bpe=0.0` is rejected exactly as the CLI rejects it instead of silently zeroing recall. New ConfigError::{InvalidMlWeight,InvalidBpeBound,NonFiniteEntropyThreshold,Parse(String)} (Parse keeps toml's error as String so the public type doesn't leak the toml version — Law-5 modularity). Deserialize semantics UNCHANGED (no try_from) so the "deserialize does not validate" test contract holds. Facade `scan_config_validate` (Result<(),String> via .to_string()) still compiles. +19 exact-variant regression tests (regression_scan_config_from_toml_str.rs): round-trip, in-range loads, each out-of-range field → its EXACT ConfigError variant+value, large-finite-bpe/negative-entropy accepted, malformed/unknown-field/empty → Parse, direct validate() on hand-built config, inclusive boundaries. GREEN: regression_scan_config_from_toml_str 19 + regression_scan_config_fields + _toml_merge + _precedence all pass (68 total, 0 failed) — no regression to the deserialize contract. status=DONE
- crates/cli/src/config/scan.rs:549 + crates/cli/src/value_parsers.rs (ml_weight) | MISSING BOUNDS (silent) | P2 | ml_weight is documented [0.0,1.0] but validated on NEITHER CLI surface: config `apply_top_level_scan_fields:549` applies `config.ml_weight` as a raw f64 with no check, and there is NO `parse_ml_weight` value_parser on the CLI `--ml-weight` arg (unlike --min-confidence/--ml-threshold). So `--ml-weight 5.0` or `ml_weight = 5.0` in .keyhog.toml is silently accepted and distorts every confidence blend. Core `ScanConfig::validate()` now rejects it (library layer, DONE above), but the CLI/config boundary should reject it too (fail-closed at input, matching min_confidence). fix: add `parse_ml_weight` (mirror parse_min_confidence) as the CLI value_parser + a `parse_config_ml_weight` at both apply sites (flat 549 + [scan] mirror if present); flip/add a characterization test like the min_confidence one. | status=DONE (2026-07-09): FIXED on BOTH surfaces + unified. (1) Extracted shared `parse_unit_interval(s, key_label, example)` in value_parsers.rs — ONE definitional home for the [0,1] parse+bound+non-finite handling that `parse_min_confidence` AND the new `parse_ml_weight` both delegate to (the two were structural twins; key_label names the bare key so ONE message serves CLI flag + config key). parse_min_confidence refactored to delegate — BYTE-IDENTICAL (pinned test min_confidence_out_of_range_keeps_pinned_wording GREEN 29 passed + property NaN/Inf/invariant 22 passed). (2) Wired `value_parser = parse_ml_weight` on the `--ml-weight` arg (args/scan.rs:887 — was `#[arg(long, value_name="WEIGHT")]` with NO parser). (3) config path: `parse_config_ml_weight` at the single apply site (scan.rs, flat `config.ml_weight` — there is NO [scan].ml_weight mirror, schema.rs:81 is top-level only). (4) test ml_weight_range_validation_matches_between_cli_and_config (CLI 5.0→exit2, config 5.0→exit2 "invalid .keyhog.toml"+"ml_weight must be between 0.0 and 1.0", in-range 0.75 loads). regression_cli_config_path 15 + value_parser_fix_guidance 29 + property 22 + all_tests::unit::value_parsers 54-filtered GREEN, 0 failed. parse_ml_threshold LEFT AS-IS (its finite-first message style deliberately differs; not force-merged). status=DONE
- crates/cli/src/config/scan.rs (threads) + VALIDATION-PARITY AUDIT of every .keyhog.toml numeric knob | MISSING BOUNDS (silent) + intra-config inconsistency | P2 | DONE (2026-07-09): systematic audit of "does the config apply path enforce the SAME bound the CLI value_parser does?" across ALL config numerics. Result: 3 of 4 suspected gaps were NOT gaps, 1 was REAL. REAL: `threads` — CLI `--threads` binds parse_positive_thread_count (rejects 0, "--threads must be >= 1"), the sibling config `reader_threads`/`fused_batch`/`fused_depth`/`per_chunk_timeout_ms`/`min_secret_len` all have inline `==0` config guards, but `threads` was applied RAW on BOTH apply sites (flat `config.threads` + nested `[scan].threads`) — so `.keyhog.toml` `threads = 0` was silently accepted while `--threads 0` AND `reader_threads = 0` were rejected (intra-config inconsistency + CLI divergence). FIXED: added the `==0` guard to both sites mirroring reader_threads exactly ("- threads = 0" / "- [scan].threads = 0: use a positive integer"). +test config_threads_zero_is_rejected_matching_reader_threads_and_cli (CLI 0→exit2, flat 0→exit2, [scan] 0→exit2, in-range 2 loads). NOT-GAPS (audited, no change — recorded so the audit is explicit): `timeout`(Option<u64>), `rate`(Option<usize>), `max_commits`(Option<usize>) are ALL bare `#[arg(long)]` with NO CLI value_parser — unvalidated on BOTH surfaces, so there is no parity gap to close by mirroring the CLI; adding validation would be SPECULATIVE (0-semantics unclear: 0 may mean no-timeout/unlimited/auto). `verify_rate`(f64, default 5.0) is a SEPARATE field that DOES use parse_verify_rate and is not the config `rate`. byte-sizes/decode_depth/ml_threshold/min_confidence/ml_weight all already route through parse_config_* (verified). So the config-numeric validation surface is now COMPLETE for every knob that has a canonical CLI validator. FOLLOW-ON (optional, low-pri): if timeout/rate/max_commits 0-semantics are ever defined as invalid, add matching CLI value_parsers FIRST (ONE-PLACE) then route config through them. GREEN: regression_cli_config_path 16 passed / 0 failed (was 15; +1 new test, no regression). status=DONE

Extracted generic `fn parse_positive_int<T: FromStr+Default+PartialEq>(s, accepted, constraint, example)` (Default==0 is the zero test; covers usize AND u64) — ONE home for the parse+zero-check; the 6 wrappers are now one-liners supplying only their per-flag wording+example. BYTE-IDENTICAL: every pinned message (--threads/--min-secret-len/--request-timeout-secs/"value must be >= 1"/"millisecond timeout must be >= 1"/"limit count must be >= 1") + the "not a valid integer" unparseable prefix preserved. regression_value_parser_fix_guidance 29 + property 22 + all_tests::unit::value_parsers 54 GREEN, 0 failed. parse_decode_depth ([1,limit] range) + parse_byte_size (byte-size grammar) + parse_verify_rate (finite+cap) + parse_ml_threshold (finite-first) LEFT separate (genuinely different logic). status=DONE

- HUNT-core crates/core/src/allowlist/metadata.rs:76 | PERF | low | metadata_tokens collects tokens into a Vec<&str> on each line, causing small allocations for every allowlist entry. | fix: Refactor metadata_tokens to return a lazy Iterator over split tokens instead of Vec. | status=open
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/spec/load.rs:142 | MISSING BOUNDS | medium | discover_detector_tomls returns a hard error if read_dir fails, ignoring the enforce_gate = false option. | fix: Return an empty vector or log a warning if directory reading fails with enforce_gate = false. | status=resolved-INVALID (2026-07-09): FALSE POSITIVE — the fail-closed hard-error IS correct. `read_dir` FAILURE (missing dir / permission denied) is distinct from "dir exists but empty"; surfacing SpecError::ReadFile is the right fail-closed posture. The finding's suggested "return empty vector" would be a textbook Law-10 SILENT FALLBACK: the scan would proceed with ZERO detectors and silently find nothing, hiding the real config error. enforce_gate governs the "too few detectors" quality gate, NOT whether an unreadable directory is tolerable. "Fixing" this would introduce the exact silent-recall-loss bug the tree's fail-closed doctrine forbids.
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/allowlist/metadata.rs:25 | MISSING BOUNDS | low | parse_inline_metadata splits on = but does not check if value has unbalanced quotes or invalid formatting. | fix: Add checks to ensure keys and values are correctly quoted and structured. | status=resolved-INVALID (2026-07-09): FALSE POSITIVE — already handled. metadata.rs:10-13 records `parsed.unterminated_quote` (unbalanced quote) into `malformed_tokens`; :19-22 records a token missing `=`; `assign_unique_field` records duplicate keys (Law 10 fail-closed). metadata_tokens tracks quote/escape state and reports `unterminated_quote`. Malformed tokens fail the allowlist load closed rather than silently misparsing. The robustness the finding asks for is present.
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/dedup.rs:669 | PERF | low | merge_companions allocates and sorts a Vec of companions on every duplicate match merge, creating heap pressure on large scans. | fix: Skip Vec allocation and sorting if the incoming map is empty or matches existing. | status=resolved-STALE (2026-07-09): ALREADY IMPLEMENTED — dedup.rs:661-665 has exactly the suggested guard (`if incoming.is_empty() { return; }` with comment "Most duplicate matches carry no companions; skip the Vec alloc + sort in that hot-loop-common case"). The finding predates that fix. No change needed.

- HUNT-core crates/core/src/merkle_index/storage.rs:435 | PERF | low | oldest_eviction_keys clones selected CacheKey candidates (PathBuf) during eviction cleanup, causing unnecessary allocations. | fix: Use references or extract indices to defer path cloning until removal. | status=open
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/spec/load.rs:188 | PERF | low | assemble_detector_load sequentially sorts the entire Vec of loaded DetectorSpecs by ID, causing a sync bottleneck after parallel parsing. | fix: Use rayon parallel sort to sort the loaded detectors concurrently. | status=open
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/spec/validate/regex_complexity.rs:70 | PERF | low | collect_regex_stats allocates a new RegexWalkFrame Vec stack for every regex pattern during spec validation. | fix: Reuse a thread-local vector for AST complexity traversal stack. | status=wontfix-infeasible-as-stated The suggested "thread-local vector" CANNOT compile: RegexWalkFrame<'a> holds `ast: &'a Ast` (regex_complexity.rs:63-64), and a `thread_local!` static requires a `'static` type — a borrow-carrying frame is not 'static, so it cannot be stored in a thread-local. A workaround (thread-local Vec of raw `*const Ast` + unsafe deref, or index-based flattening) trades memory-safety for eliminating ~1 small Vec alloc PER PATTERN at DETECTOR-LOAD time (a one-time, ~1000-pattern, µs-scale startup cost — not a scan-hot path). Unsafe pointer bookkeeping for a negligible one-time startup allocation is a bad trade (Law-1 no-regrets, keep-it-simple). The stack is already fully drained each call (`while let Some = stack.pop()`) so there is no leak; the alloc is the only cost and it is immaterial. Not worth doing.
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/spec/validate.rs:621 | PERF | low | ast_captures_len allocates a fresh Vec stack to find the maximum capture group index on every pattern spec validation. | fix: Reuse a preallocated/thread-local stack or rewrite using recursion. | status=open
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/spec/validate.rs:688 | PERF | low | ast_literal_runs allocates new frame and result vectors for every regex pattern literal runs analysis. | fix: Reuse preallocated/thread-local vectors for literal run stats traversal. | status=open
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/spec/validate.rs:845 | PERF | low | pure_character_class_ast allocates new frame and result vectors for every regex pattern pure character class check. | fix: Reuse preallocated/thread-local vectors for character class checks. | status=open
- HUNT-core crates/core/src/merkle_index/storage.rs:268 | SILENT FALLBACKS | low | When written_at_ns is 0, racy_floor becomes 0, so no entries are dropped, violating the documented fail-safe behavior. | fix: Explicitly handle written_at_ns == 0 by dropping all entries. | status=resolved-INVALID (2026-07-09): FALSE POSITIVE — the finding has the logic BACKWARDS. storage.rs:295 is `if entry.mtime_ns >= racy_floor { drop }` and `mtime_ns: u64` (line 41), so with written_at_ns==0 → second_floor(0)==0 → `u64 >= 0` is ALWAYS true → EVERY entry is dropped → full cold re-scan. That IS the documented fail-safe (comment lines 265-267), and it is EXACTLY what the finding's suggested "fix" (drop all when 0) asks for — already implemented. No change; "fixing" it would be a no-op or a regression.

- HUNT-misc crates/scanner/src/fragment_cache.rs:180 | PANIC/BOUNDS | Medium | (f1.line as isize - f2.line as isize).abs() can panic/overflow under large line numbers | fix: use f1.line.abs_diff(f2.line) < 100 | status=done ALREADY-FIXED: line 183 now reads `f1.line.abs_diff(f2.line) < 100` — the EXACT fix this row suggested. The signed-cast `.abs()` is gone; the code comment (lines 180-182) explicitly cites the isize::MIN overflow this row warned about. No cast, no panic path. Verified in-tree (foreign-clean on this fn).
- HUNT-misc crates/scanner/src/anchored_regex.rs:44 | SILENT FALLBACKS | Medium | CRLF flag mismatch between anchored verifier .crlf(self.case_insensitive) and base regex compile .crlf(true) | fix: always use .crlf(true) | status=resolved-INVALID (2026-07-09): NOT a bug. The base is compiled on TWO branches (LazyRegex::get, types.rs:460-464): ci detector→shared_regex ci(true)+crlf(true); non-ci→Regex::new ci(false)+crlf(false). The anchored verifier's `.crlf(self.case_insensitive)` deliberately mirrors both. "Always crlf(true)" would BREAK case-sensitive-detector parity on CRLF input (crlf(true) excludes `\r` from the dot). Pinned by parity test anchored_regex.rs::fail_closed_tests::anchored_crlf_and_case_flags_mirror_the_two_branch_base_compile.
- HUNT-misc crates/scanner/src/hw_probe/select.rs:446 | ONE-PLACE | Low | "auto" backend override is in BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES but unhandled in parse_backend_str | fix: parse and handle "auto" explicitly | status=done RECONCILED correct-by-design: parse_backend_str returns Option<ScanBackend> where None == "no forced backend, use autoroute" and Some(x) == forced route. "auto" correctly maps to None (via `_ => None`) — that IS handling it (autoroute is the None semantics, not a missing case). The None-ambiguity between "auto" and an invalid string is unreachable in production: clap's PossibleValuesParser (args/scan.rs) validates against BACKEND_OVERRIDE_VALUES BEFORE parse_backend_str ever sees the string, so only listed values arrive. An explicit `"auto" => None` arm would be cosmetically clearer but is behaviorally identical; the fn is hot-foreign (active megascan-alias refactor at lines 452-458) so no collision-risk edit warranted. Not a bug.

- HUNT-misc crates/scanner/src/prefix_trie.rs:36 | PERF | Low | collect_propagation recursively allocates subtree_indices and descendant_indices vectors on every node | fix: pass a mutable reference to a pre-allocated vector | status=wontfix-accurate-LOW (2026-07-10 code-verified): `build_propagation_table`/`collect_propagation` runs ONCE at scanner CONSTRUCTION (compiler setup), NOT per-scan — a one-time O(trie-nodes) cost, not a hot-path allocation. The code ALREADY optimizes the common case (line 62-68 comment: the last/only pattern per node MOVES `descendant_indices`, so a single-pattern-per-node trie does zero clones). The proposed fix (shared pre-allocated buffer) is NON-TRIVIAL here because the recursion RETURNS each node's `subtree_indices` to its parent (which folds it into its own accumulation) — a scratch-buffer-with-offset-ranges scheme would be needed, easy to get wrong, for zero measurable one-time-setup gain. The residual double-copy (each child subtree materialized into both `descendant` and `subtree`) is intrinsic to the return-to-parent + assign-to-propagation dual use. Not worth the refactor risk on a mature, correct, once-per-build function. If ever hot (it isn't), memoize at a higher level rather than micro-opt the recursion.
- HUNT-misc crates/scanner/src/alphabet_filter.rs:184 | PERF | Low | Calls is_x86_feature_detected!("avx2") on every single screen execution rather than caching it | fix: use cached flag from hardware probe | status=done RECONCILED not-a-bug: `std::is_x86_feature_detected!` caches internally (populates a static on first call, then a relaxed atomic load + predictable branch) — it does NOT re-run CPUID per call. Inline-per-call is the DELIBERATE repo-wide idiom, documented at entropy/fast.rs:26 ("each a branch plus an atomic load") and used identically in ascii_ci.rs, simd/backend.rs, hw_probe/mod.rs. Caching a bool only in screen() would break ONE-PLACE consistency (idiom split across 6+ sites) for a rounding-error gain on a predictable, cache-hot branch — Law-7 threshold not met. Distinct from from_bytes (line 19), where the dispatch was removed because all 3 SIMD bodies did IDENTICAL scalar work; screen_avx2 does genuinely different vector work so its dispatch is justified.
- HUNT-misc crates/scanner/src/alphabet_filter.rs:177 | SILENT FALLBACKS | Low | screen() silently falls back to scalar search when AVX2 is missing, bypassing central autoroute selection | fix: route fallback check through central autoroute | status=done RECONCILED not-a-violation (Law-10): the scalar path is RESULT-IDENTICAL to screen_avx2 (asserted by the prefilter-robustness differential proptest the fn doc cites, lines 196-198), taken ONLY when AVX2 is physically absent on the CPU — there is NO faster path being silently skipped (unlike the HsFallbackEngine 270x case where a faster path existed and was pessimized). Result-identical + no-faster-path-available = correct per-host SIMD dispatch, not a recall-losing or slow degrade. Law-10 targets silent WEAKER paths (recall loss) and slow allowed-fallbacks; neither applies. Routing through a "central autoroute" would add indirection with zero behavioral change.

- HUNT-misc crates/scanner/src/types.rs:233 | PANIC/BOUNDS | Medium | source_offset_from_mapping returns source.len().saturating_sub(1) which is out of bounds if source is empty | fix: return 0 or guard empty | status=done RECONCILED not-a-bug (full data-flow traced): (1) EMPTY source: `saturating_sub(1)` returns 0, not out-of-bounds — and this branch is only reached when a match was located in `source`, contradictory for an empty source (unreachable in practice). (2) The return value is a NUMERIC offset, never sliced here: it flows source_offset_from_mapping → source_offset_for_match → build_raw_match, where it is used ONLY as `location.offset = offset + base_offset` (postprocess/mod.rs:83) — a reported byte-position, not an index. (3) The ONE in-function slice is `source_line_at(source, original_start_offset)` (line 220), an EARLIER branch, and source_line_at already floor_char_boundary-snaps (types.rs:251) — the panic the existing test `source_offset_for_match_for_test` (testing.rs:2375) guards. (4) `len-1` is the LAST IN-BOUNDS byte index (out-of-bounds is `>= len`), deliberately clamped to stay indexable; downstream context slicing on the reported offset is is_char_boundary/floor_char_boundary-guarded (scan_loop.rs:51-52, context_window.rs:79). No panic path exists.

- VERIFIED-FINDING 2026-07-09 (from HUNT-misc, deep-verified by parent): crates/scanner/src/anchored_regex.rs:44 | correctness / ONE-PLACE regex-flag convention | HIGH | The anchored verifier (doc: "whole-chunk-equivalent left context") compiles its wrapper with `.crlf(self.case_insensitive)` and `.case_insensitive(self.case_insensitive)`, but the BASE detector regex path `shared_regex_compile` (compiler/compiler_compile.rs:292) uses `.crlf(true)` + `.case_insensitive(true)` UNCONDITIONALLY, and compile_companion (:339) uses `.crlf(true)`, while phase2_prefilter.rs:450/463 use `.crlf(case_insensitive)`. So a CASE-SENSITIVE detector's anchored verifier gets crlf(false) while its base got crlf(true) → `$`/`^`/`.` behave differently around `\r\n` → the anchored fast-path can DISAGREE with the whole-chunk match on CRLF input (recall/precision divergence on the exact path meant to be equivalent). fix: unify ALL regex-builder call sites through ONE owner that pins crlf(true) + the correct case-sensitivity convention (base uses ci(true) always, implying case sensitivity is inline-flag-driven — the anchored ci(self.ci) may ALSO be wrong); REQUIRES a differential test (anchored.get() vs base regex on a case-sensitive detector over CRLF input) before changing, since the subsystem is mid phase-2 refactor. status=RESOLVED-INVALID (2026-07-09, differential test WRITTEN & GREEN, my prior deep-verify was WRONG): the "base uses crlf(true)/ci(true) UNCONDITIONALLY" premise is false. `shared_regex_compile` is only ONE of the base's two branches — `LazyRegex::get` (types.rs:460-464) routes ci detectors to `shared_regex` (ci(true)+crlf(true)) and case-SENSITIVE detectors to plain `Regex::new` (ci(false)+crlf(false) defaults). The anchored verifier's `.crlf(self.case_insensitive)`+`.case_insensitive(self.case_insensitive)` mirrors BOTH branches exactly, so it AGREES with the base for every detector kind. phase2_prefilter.rs:450/463 do the same correct mirror. There is NO divergence; "unify to crlf(true)" would have INTRODUCED one (case-sensitive verifier crlf(true)≠base crlf(false)). The required differential test now exists and PROVES agreement: anchored_regex.rs::fail_closed_tests::anchored_crlf_and_case_flags_mirror_the_two_branch_base_compile (ci & non-ci verifiers vs their base branches on `A\rB`). LESSON: read the FULL compile-dispatch (LazyRegex::get), not just one branch, before calling a flag mismatch a bug. | class=correctness | sev=HIGH

- crates/scanner/src/fragment_cache.rs:180,253 | correctness+elegance | **FIXED 2026-07-09 (parent, clean-file)** | replaced `(f1.line as isize - f2.line as isize).abs() < 100` (BOTH occurrences) with `f1.line.abs_diff(f2.line) < 100` — usize abs-diff, no signed cast / no `abs()`-on-`isize::MIN` panic, self-documenting. - crates/scanner/src/fragment_cache.rs:169-192,242-265 | ONE-PLACE dedup | MEDIUM | the two reassembly passes duplicate the ENTIRE `if cluster.len()>=2 { for i { for j!=i { near pair-gate → join } } }` nested-loop block near-verbatim, differing only in the pushed type (`String` vs `ReassembledCandidate{value,...}`). fix: extract one `enumerate_near_fragment_pairs(cluster, |f1,f2| …)` helper taking a push closure (or returning an iterator of near (i,j) pairs) so the pair-enumeration + same-path + abs_diff<100 gate lives in ONE owner; both callers map the joined String to their own output type. **DONE 2026-07-09 @bl9yzls2h — extracted `record_and_collect<T>(fragment, make)` generic owner (record/dedup/evict/pair-gate in ONE place); `record_and_reassemble` + `record_and_reassemble_stamped` are now thin wrappers (a make-closure + their own canonical sort). 96 fragment tests green, behavior preserved.** status=done | class=dedup | sev=MEDIUM

- HUNT-robust DECODE-OVERALLOC-CLUSTER (caesar/hex/json/url/util/reverse/extractor/splice, ~13 rows) | over-allocation | TRIAGE 2026-07-09 | the EXPANSION decoders that actually multiply output ARE already capped: base64 MAX_BASE64_INPUT_LEN 16MB (:276), hex MAX_HEX_INPUT_LEN 32MB (:80/:87), z85 (:334), inflate MAX_INFLATE_BYTES 16MB; pipeline caps MAX_DECODED_TOTAL_BYTES 64MB + MAX_DECODED_CHUNKS_PER_ROOT 1000. The remaining decoders (caesar/json/reverse/url/util) run on SHAPE-GATED extracted candidates bounded by chunk size (1-3x, not unbounded), and caesar 25x was ALREADY collapsed to 1-3 aligned shifts via matched_caesar_shifts. So none is a true unbounded-OOM; worst case is a chunk-sized transient alloc. Files are FOREIGN-DIRTY. LOW priority: a single candidate-length guard at the decode entry would be the elegant ONE-PLACE hardening if ever warranted, but marginal safety is low given the expansion decoders are already capped. Not force-fixing dirty low-value code; revisit if a genuinely unbounded path (input not chunk-bounded) is proven. status=assessed-low-priority [robustness]

## META-VERDICT 2026-07-09 — HUNT-* cluster is systematically STALE/FALSE-POSITIVE (verify before fixing)
This session verified ~16 HUNT-* findings end-to-end against source + pinning tests. The pattern: HUNT-{sources,verifier,core,scanner,cli} findings were filed BEFORE the tree's extensive Law-10 + testing hardening and are now largely obsolete. Do NOT blind-fix — VERIFY first (read the caller chain + pinning tests); most are already correct:
- "SILENT FALLBACKS" sub-cluster: ~90% FALSE POSITIVE. Flagged paths are almost always LOUD + coverage-recorded + fail-closed (EXIT_SOURCE_FAILED via source_coverage_incomplete, or a per-item operator-visible Err). Verified: allowlist clock-before-epoch, git/diff.rs:704, dispatch.rs oversized/error, cloud/mod.rs:554 + web.rs:711 (record_unscanned_object_skip / SourceError → coverage-incomplete), mtime/size None→0 (content-hash-gated, cannot false-skip). Fixing several would REGRESS fail-closed into the imagined bug.
- "test-gap / zero unit tests" sub-cluster: STALE. Comprehensive external tests exist (no-inline policy moved them to tests/): token_randomness→22, suppression decision→6/api→7/shape→12/path_filter→3 files, 18 detection_truth_*.rs, severity→2 files/29 tests.
- "dup / perf" sub-cluster: mostly FALSE POSITIVE. Verified: is_base64_candidate_byte vs is_standard_base64_byte (different alphabets), z85 unchecked-mul (bounded 85^5<<u64::MAX), dedup None-conf→0.0 (documented total-order tiebreak), LazyLock panics (include_bytes!-embedded → correct fail-fast; graceful=Law-10 regression).
- REAL items exist but are GATED: user active ML refactor (weights/golden), bench-sensitive recall tuning (cloud/web lossy consistency, confidence floor), intricate edges needing a repro (nested-decode dedup). Need the owner or a bench run, not a blind edit.
ACTIONABLE: spend agents VERIFYING HUNT rows against source+tests (flip to resolved-INVALID with proof), NOT auto-fixing. Real fix-yield is in STRUCTURAL findings (missing coverage, clear behavioral bugs, clear DUPLICATION) — this session landed 4: merkle spec-hash knobs, AIMD rate-limiter, severity ONE-PLACE, dispatch/fused classifier dedup.
ADDENDUM 2026-07-09 (HUNT-core sub-cluster, 4 more verified): confirms the same pattern. FALSE POSITIVE / already-correct: (6766) merkle_index/storage.rs:268 racy_floor — finding had the logic BACKWARDS (`mtime_ns: u64 >= racy_floor` with floor=0 drops ALL entries = the documented fail-safe, not "no entries dropped"); (6756) spec/load.rs discover_detector_tomls read_dir hard-error is CORRECT fail-closed (the suggested "return empty" = a Law-10 silent-fallback scanning ZERO detectors); (6757) allowlist/metadata.rs unbalanced-quote — already detected via `unterminated_quote`→malformed_tokens (+ missing-`=` + dup-key, all fail-closed). ALREADY-IMPLEMENTED: (6758) dedup.rs:661 merge_companions `if incoming.is_empty() { return }` early-return is present with the exact comment. REMAINING HUNT-core = the PERF micro-alloc sub-cluster (glob segment_match/glob_match_segments Vec<char>/Vec<bool>, merkle storage PathBuf/CacheKey clones over up-to-8M entries, spec/validate AST-walk frame Vecs, metadata_tokens Vec<&str>): genuinely BENCH-GATED — the allocation exists (not FP) but is low-severity and can be neither confirmed a bottleneck nor fixed-with-proof without a criterion bench + a realistic large corpus; several also require an Arc<Path>/Arc<str> migration touching many call sites. Leave open as bench-gated-perf; do NOT blind-refactor.

## RECONCILE#16 (2026-07-09) — suppression/shape + adjudicate proptest sweep (15 GREEN suites, ~200k proptest execs) + 2 Law-10 NaN fixes
Continuation of the end-to-end drain, pivoting to the genuinely-uncovered CLEAN surface: the `crates/scanner/src/suppression/shape/**` + `adjudicate/**` precision predicates (FP-suppression gates that decide which candidates to drop). All reached through additive `for_test` facade wrappers over `pub(crate)` internals (ZERO net public surface; the fns were already re-exported at `suppression::shape` or widened visibility-only). Each suite = example truth-table + property tiers @4000 cases. Every suite fmt-clean + GREEN.

**Law-10 CODE FIXES (detection-truth axis, not just tests):**
1. **entropy_fallback_confidence NaN-sanitize (6690)** — `f64::min` IGNORES NaN, so `0.55.min(NaN)==0.55` laundered a broken entropy into a mid-tier confidence. Added `debug_assert!(!is_nan)` + collapse-to-0.0 (loud-in-debug, fail-closed-in-release). Bench-neutral (shannon_entropy is bounded [0,8], never NaN on real input).
2. **apply_named_detector_anchor_floor NaN-sanitize (6691)** — `NaN.max(FLOOR)==FLOOR` manufactured the anchor floor from garbage + leaked NaN downstream to poison every `>=` gate. Same debug-loud/release-fail-closed sanitize.
   Both pinned by `regression_confidence_nan_sanitize.rs` (legit-values-unchanged + debug should_panic + release fail-closed halves). GREEN 4.

**NEW PROPTEST SUITES (all under `crates/scanner/tests/property/`, registered as `[[test]]`):**
- `entropy_simd_scalar_parity_proptest` (7, **Law-8 gpu/cpu parity**) — scalar-vs-{simd,avx2,avx512} Shannon-entropy parity; avx2+avx512 tiers RAN FOR REAL on this box (5957 DONE).
- `checksum_primitives_proptest` (6) — CRC-32 known vectors + base62 whole-u32 round-trip (load-bearing for every token validator's precision).
- `hex_digest_fragment_proptest` (10) + `generic_keyword_boundary_proptest` (10) + `structured_dotted_token_proptest` (8) + `bare_auth_value_allowed_proptest` (6) — the adjudicate FP-suppression cluster (5950: 3 of 6 predicates now pinned; camelCase-hinge `myPass`⟹boundary vs `bypass`⟹reject; JWT/Discord dotted-token allowlist).
- `hex_uuid_shape_proptest` (7) — is_uuid_v4_shape (any RFC-4122 version, uniform-case) + looks_like_bare_hex_digest (32/40/48/56/64/72/128 widths).
- `masking_run_and_template_proptest` (8) — pins the deliberate DASH DIVERGENCE: has_three_or_more counts dash runs, has_n_or_more EXCLUDES them + bracketed `{…}`/`<…>`/`${…}` 80-byte cap.
- `aws_iam_arn_shape_proptest` — full-ARN vs trimmed-ARN mutual-exclusivity on the `arn:` prefix + resource-target requirement.
- `path_uri_shape_proptest` — scheme-URI colon-position (3..=15) + `/`-segment + config-filename-suffix gates.
- `masked_placeholder_proptest` (9) — trailing-ellipsis / xxx-star-prefix masks + repeated-block tiling detector.
- `serial_and_prefixed_hash_proptest` (8) — 5×5 dash serial key (29-char layout) + hash-algo-labelled digest (label required, substring+case-insensitive, canonical body length).
- `english_prose_shape_proptest` (6) — prose heuristic (all-lowercase>=16 or >=2 alpha words w/ a lowercase word); recall-critical pin: ANY digit ⟹ never prose (a secret is never swallowed as prose).
- `service_hex_and_truncated_uuid_proptest` (6) — pins the LENGTH-SET DIVERGENCE: is_canonical_service_hex_key {32/40/48/64} is a STRICT SUBSET of bare-hex-digest {32/40/48/56/64/72/128} (56/72/128 = digest but NOT service key) + truncated-uuid-v4 (34-char 6-4-4-4-12, v4 marker at 12, variant at 17).
- `identifier_and_markup_shape_proptest` (10) — program-identifier (snake/camelCase), kebab-config-key, `[sources.IDENT]` reference-selector, percent-encoded XSS markup, HTML event-handler `onEvent=` fragment (widened `mod public`/`mod source`→pub(crate), visibility-only).
- `regression_s3_numeric_param_validation` (4, FEATURE-GATED `#![cfg(feature="s3")]`) — **5008 DONE**: `optional_usize_source_param` (s3/gcs/azure max_objects) rejects non-numeric/negative with a named SourceError, accepts valid/omitted; run via `--features s3`. Directly closes a stop-hook "feature-gated, one-step-actionable" item.
- Sources: `contract/magic_byte_signatures.rs` + `property/magic_byte_signatures_proptest.rs` (BMP/PE/bzip2/PDF/ZIP/pickle/wasm classifiers, 4987+4989-4994 DONE) + `contract/default_excludes_rule_validation.rs` + proptest (filter.rs normalize/validate per-kind, 5009-5010 DONE) — via the pre-existing `sources/src/testing_facade.rs` (the earlier "no facade / would collide" annotation was WRONG; facade existed).

**~18 additive facade wrappers** added to `scanner/src/testing.rs` + `sources/src/testing_facade.rs` — all thin `pub fn X_for_test` delegations to `pub(crate)` internals (ONE-PLACE: no logic duplicated, no public surface widened beyond the test seam). `mod generic`/`is_hex_digest_fragment`/`bare_auth_value_allowed`/filter internals widened visibility-only.

**INFRA/CRUFT FINDINGS (new, append-only):**
- crates/scanner/src/model_card.json.bak | cruft | P3 | an UNTRACKED (`??`) `.bak` backup of `model_card.json` sits in `src/` (scattered-file rot: a stale model-card snapshot committed nowhere, shadowing the live `model_card.json`). NOT deleted this turn — it is in the user's active ML-model area [[ml-model-must-train-on-real-distribution]] and a concurrent agent is editing the tree (BACKLOG.md changed under me mid-turn), so a foreign scratch file must not be destroyed. FIX (user/ML-refactor): `rm` it (the live `model_card.json` is the source of truth) or add `*.json.bak` to `.gitignore`+clean. status=open
- crates/scanner/tests/all_tests.rs (aggregate) | test-infra | P3 | the full `--test all_tests` ci-lean build LINKS a ~200-600MB single binary; on a ~2GB-free NFS-backed box it OOM-killed at ~20min (empty log, exit 1) — a whole-suite reconcile is not reliably runnable as ONE binary here. FIX: split all_tests into sharded `[[test]]` groups (per-subsystem binaries) so link memory + wall-time stay bounded, matching the already-sharded `gap_*` + `property/*` [[test]] pattern; meanwhile per-`[[test]]` incremental builds (~1-2min) are the reliable verify path. status=open

**TURN TALLY:** 18 GREEN scanner/sources test suites + the s3 feature-gated target (~120 test fns, ~280k proptest case-executions) + 2 Law-10 NaN code fixes; DONE flips: 4987, 4989-4994, 5008, 5009-5010, 5957, 6690, 6691; STALE-with-proof: 5956 (dead entropy fns already gone), 6040 (model-card API is USED, not dead); 5950 partial (3/6 predicates); 4993 annotated-open (zstd wrapper one-step). ~30 additive `for_test` facade wrappers over pub(crate) internals; visibility-only widenings (`mod generic`/`public`/`source`→pub(crate); is_hex_digest_fragment/bare_auth/filter internals) — ZERO net public surface, confirmed by gap_floor=1262/0. Multi-axis: test-depth · detection-truth(Law-10 NaN) · gpu/cpu-parity(Law-8 SIMD) · organization/dedup(ONE-PLACE facades; pinned the dash-divergence, arn-split, service-vs-digest length-set-subset) · robustness(no-panic proptest tiers) · utilization(dead-code stale-flips). SUPPRESSION/SHAPE CLEAN SURFACE now essentially SATURATED (canonical/path/prose/public/source self-contained predicates all pinned); remainder routes through the FOREIGN decode/randomness/BPE modules. Every touched file fmt-clean; all mine GREEN. Reconcile via `--test gap_floor` = **1262/0** (the `--test all_tests` aggregate OOMs, see infra finding).

## RECONCILE#17 (2026-07-09) — decode DoS-hardening + prefix-scan → Aho-Corasick perf batch (4 code fixes + 3 GREEN adversarial suites)
Continuation of the end-to-end drain, pivoting to the decode subsystem's DoS/perf surface via the DOGFOOD→ENCODE→FIX pipeline (adversarial tests FIRST, then the code fix that proves/hardens the bound). Multi-axis: **perf** (2 AC swaps) · **robustness/DoS** (2 bomb/bounds suites) · **detection-truth** (empty-inflate→None) · **test-depth** (3 suites) · **organization/ONE-PLACE** (both prefix automata now derive from the single KNOWN_PREFIXES source).

**CODE FIXES (perf + correctness, not just tests):**
1. **caesar `contains_known_prefix` → `PLAIN_PREFIX_AC` (4964, P3 DONE)** — the per-shift gate (run on up to 25 decoded variants of every surviving candidate, on the decoder emitting ~84% of decode sub-chunks) was `O(prefixes × |variant|)` `str::contains` fan; now one linear `AhoCorasick::is_match`. New static mirrors the sibling `ROTATED_PREFIX_AC` (same fail-closed panic; both built from the ONE `KNOWN_PREFIXES` source). Proven behavior-neutral.
2. **reverse `REVERSED_KNOWN_PREFIXES` (Vec) → `REVERSED_PREFIX_AC` (6671 + 5972-half DONE)** — identical naive-scan in `looks_reversible`, same AC treatment (reverse is a bijection ⇒ soundness unchanged). `decode_borrowed` gate delimiter re-pointed.
3. **inflate empty-output → None (NEW, found by the bomb suite)** — a degenerate zlib stored block (`78 9c 00 00 00`) inflated to "" yet returned `Some("")`, making the caller emit a useless empty rescan sub-chunk. Now returns `None` (consistent with the non-container/malformed/non-UTF-8 paths; recall-neutral — empty text holds no credential). Doc updated.
4. **inflate `out` preallocate (4969, P4 DONE)** — `Vec::with_capacity(bytes.len())`, skips the initial doubling reallocs, bomb-safe.

**NEW ADVERSARIAL SUITES (crates/scanner/tests/property/, `#![cfg(feature="decode")]`, registered `[[test]]`):**
- `decode_dos_bounds_proptest` (9) — feeds 2 MiB + crafted pathological inputs to the leaf decoders (caesar/reverse/QP/MIME/octal); asserts no-panic + output bounded by input length. PROVES the leaf decoders are linear (fails LOUDLY — OOM/hang — if any were quadratic). Drains the "huge candidate → OOM" concern at the leaf.
- `inflate_bomb_bound_proptest` (9) — builds REAL 256 MiB gzip/zlib decompression bombs (via new `gzip_compress_for_test`/`zlib_compress_for_test` facades; the test binary can't link flate2 directly so the facade owns bomb construction) and proves `try_inflate_to_text` caps at `MAX_INFLATE_BYTES` (16 MiB) — no OOM/hang — plus happy-path round-trip + malformed→None + the empty→None fix. `MAX_INFLATE_BYTES` widened pub(crate) + `inflate_output_cap_for_test` facade so the test asserts the REAL constant (ONE-PLACE), not a hardcoded copy.
- `caesar_prefix_ac_equivalence_proptest` (5) — pins `PLAIN_PREFIX_AC.is_match == any(|p| s.contains(p))` on 6k arbitrary + 6k credential-alphabet + embed-real-prefix tiers (Law 6: the optimization changes cost, never behavior).

**Facades added** (scanner/src/testing.rs, all thin `pub fn X_for_test`): `try_inflate_to_text_for_test`, `inflate_output_cap_for_test`, `gzip_compress_for_test`, `zlib_compress_for_test`. ZERO net public surface.

**FOREIGN-TREE NOTE:** caesar.rs + inflate.rs are in the active foreign decode-refactor set (BACKLOG line 14); my edits are STRICTLY ADDITIVE (new statics, a small return-path change, a capacity hint, one const visibility widen) — nothing foreign removed/reverted — and build GREEN in isolation, per shared-tree policy. reverse.rs is NOT in the foreign set.

**TURN TALLY:** 4 code fixes (2 perf AC-swaps, 1 correctness empty→None, 1 alloc) + 3 GREEN adversarial suites (23 test fns, ~26k proptest execs) + 4 facades. DONE flips: 4964, 4969, 6671; 5972 → partial (AC half done). gap_floor GREEN **1262/0** — decode-perf batch caused ZERO collateral.

## RECONCILE#18 (2026-07-09) — sources/verifier robustness+perf drain (3 clean fixes + 3 verify-before-fix verdicts), all GREEN
Continuation, pivoting to the CLEAN (non-foreign, non-scanner-saturated) sources/verifier crates via the backlog's open HUNT-sources/HUNT-verifier rows. Multi-axis: **robustness/DoS** (git-log line cap) · **perf** (stdin zero-copy, web single-parse) · **Law-10/organization** (verified-stale cleanups). Every fix reuses an existing ONE-PLACE primitive; every "fix" verified against the code FIRST (3 of 6 turned out to be stale/intentional — verify-before-fix prevented 3 wrong edits).

**CODE FIXES (all verified GREEN):**
1. **git-log unbounded line → capped reader (6086, P2 DONE)** — `GitCommitEnumerator` `Lines<BufReader>` → `BufReader` + `read_capped_line(.., GIT_PLUMBING_LINE_BYTES=4096)`; a hostile repo's multi-GB single log line can no longer OOM (over-cap → LOUD `git_output_line_truncated_error`, Law-10 fail-closed, matching the sibling tag_messages/fsck plumbing readers — ONE-PLACE). Removed now-dead `use std::io::BufRead`. GREEN: commit_scan 14 + metadata_scan 16.
2. **stdin `from_utf8_lossy` → zero-copy `from_utf8` (6727, P3 DONE)** — stdin's OWNED single-use `Vec` lets `from_utf8` consume+reuse the allocation zero-copy on the valid path (lossy only on invalid). UNLIKE the window case (6728, borrowed reused-buffer = no win). GREEN: stdin_chunk 15 + stdin_source 4 (both lossy branches already covered).
3. **web sourcemap double-parse → single parse (6737, P2 DONE)** — `handle_json` parses the JSON body ONCE and hands the parsed Value to new `expand_sourcemap_value(map, body, url)`; `is_sourcemap_shaped_json`(parse+check) → `is_sourcemap_shaped_value(&Value)`(check only). Halves parses on the application/json sourcemap path. GREEN: web_response_classification 15 (both handle_json arms covered).

**VERIFY-BEFORE-FIX (3 findings resolved WITHOUT a code change — the code was already correct/the framing was wrong):**
- **6087 (extract.rs:813 "silent read-error swallow") → STALE**: the `_ =>` arm already `emit(Err(SourceError::Other("...file was not scanned")))` — loud, coverage-incomplete, fail-closed. The proposed fix is what the code already does.
- **6729 (window.rs:300 "panic on bad window config") → NOT USER-REACHABLE (downgrade P2→false)**: window params are compile-time consts (no CLI flag); the only setter is test-only `pub(crate)` and already asserts at the config boundary. The window.rs assert is redundant internal-invariant defense on an unreachable input; converting to Result would contort a hot slicer for nothing.
- **6706 (credential.rs OobOptional "discards OOB observation") → INTENTIONAL/CONTRACTED**: `oob_optional_always_passes_through_http` (credential.rs:798) deliberately loops observed∈{false,true} to assert OOB is ignored — a conservative-HTTP-authoritative design choice, not a bug. Flipping it (finding's proposal) would rewrite a contract test (Law 6/9). Zero detectors set any oob policy, so the arm is inert regardless. Maintainer-decision, not reflex-fix.

**TURN TALLY:** 3 code fixes (1 DoS bound, 2 perf) all GREEN + 3 verify-before-fix verdicts (2 stale, 1 intentional). DONE flips: 6086, 6727, 6737; verdicts: 6087=false, 6729=false, 6706=needs-maintainer. Each fix reused an existing primitive (read_capped_line / from_utf8 / a shared parse), no new duplication.
**+ VERIFIER TEST-DEPTH (2 GREEN suites, 16 fns, previously-untested live code):** `regression_success_spec_body_json_matcher` (9) pins `evaluate_success`'s body_contains/body_not_contains/json_path(+equals) gates (see 6245 annotation); `regression_verify_metadata_extraction` (7) pins `extract_metadata` (JSON-pointer response-metadata extraction — hit/miss/non-JSON→empty/type-mapping/root-pointer), which had ZERO test coverage. New facade `extract_metadata_for_test` added to `VerifierTestApi` (additive test seam). The success-matcher test itself CAUGHT a wrong example pre-merge (`deactivated` does not contain the substring `active`), proving the assertions are real (Law 6).

---

## DEEP DIVE — external audit (2026-07-09, Devin/GLM-5.2)

Project: keyhog v0.5.40, 5 crates, 5879 .rs files, 17,967 test fns.
Auditor: independent pass over structure, tests, GPU/Hyperscan crossover, architecture, and backlog hygiene.

### RATING

| Dimension | Score | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| **Detection coverage** | 8/10 | 900+ detectors, homoglyph-aware, decode pipeline, ML MoE scorer. Gap: creddata F1 0.37 vs betterleaks ~0.60 (precision flood from generic/entropy). |
| **Performance** | 7/10 | F3 homoglyph-skip landed (-19% wall). GPU beats Hyperscan 4.5x at 8 MiB on RTX 5090. But routing threshold is 128 MiB (not 8 MiB), and phase-2 CPU tail dominates. |
| **Architecture** | 8/10 | Clean 5-crate workspace, per-detector spec consolidation (DET-0), one-place facades. Deduction: engine/ has 40+ files, some coupling between phase2 variants. |
| **Test depth** | 9/10 | 17,967 tests, adversarial + property + gap + regression + contract. Proptest at scale. Deduction: per_detector_regression.rs (867 tests, 1 file) OOM'd at 19 GB. |
| **GPU innovation** | 7/10 | Vyre integration, region-presence batched dispatch, GPU regex-DFA admission. Deduction: megakernel fusion (phase-2 on GPU) is still pending — the biggest single perf win. |
| **Supply chain** | 8/10 | Exact version pins, minisign-verify for self-update, SHA-256 model verification. |
| **Backlog hygiene** | 5/10 | 6944-line BACKLOG.md is the single source of truth (good) but is nearly unreadable — multi-paragraph inline essays, session logs mixed with tasks, 778 open items. Needs a triage pass. |
| **Overall** | 7.4/10 | Strong engineering, real GPU work, deep test suite. Held back by backlog debt, the 8 MiB routing gap, and the phase-2 CPU bottleneck. |

### TEST COUNT ASSESSMENT

17,967 tests is NOT too many for a 900+ detector security scanner with GPU/SIMD/CPU backends, decode pipelines, git/web/cloud sources, and ML scoring. The test-to-source ratio is ~3:1 (17,967 tests / 5,879 .rs files), which is healthy for a security tool where false negatives cost real money.

**The real problem is test INFRASTRUCTURE, not test count:**
1. `per_detector_regression.rs` (867 tests in 1 file, 5055 lines) — the file that OOM'd at 19 GB. Each test calls `SCANNER.scan()` on the full detector catalog. The `LazyLock<CompiledScanner>` compiles once, but 867 scan calls on the full corpus accumulate match buffers. FIX: shard into per-detector-family files (matching the `gap_*` pattern), or use a test iterator instead of 867 separate `#[test]` fns.
2. `all_tests.rs` aggregate binary OOMs at link time (already in backlog as infra finding).
3. The 31 files with 50+ tests are fine individually; the issue is linking them all into one binary.

### NEW BACKLOG ITEMS (append-only, not fluff)

#### §SPEED — GPU 8 MiB crossover

- `crates/scanner/src/hw_probe/thresholds.rs:39` | routing | P2 | **GPU_MIN_BYTES_HIGH_TIER is 128 MiB but RTX 5090 benchmark proves GPU wins at 8 MiB (ratio 0.22, 4.5x faster).** The 2026-06-19 crossover sweep showed no GPU win through 64 MiB, but the 2026-06-20 perf trace (one day later, post split-positioned-matcher fix) shows GPU winning at 1/8/64 MiB. The threshold is stale — it predates the positioned-literal matcher split that landed 2026-06-20. FIX: lower `GPU_MIN_BYTES_HIGH_TIER` from 128 MiB to 8 MiB (the measured crossover point), gated on a fresh `backend_crossover_sweep` run that confirms the 2026-06-20 trace. Keep the 128 MiB floor as a `GPU_MIN_BYTES_HIGH_TIER_CONSERVATIVE` fallback for autoroute calibration hosts where the sweep hasn't run. status=open-needs-bench (the value change requires a fresh backend_crossover_sweep on the 5090 to confirm the 8 MiB crossover — a GPU-feature bench on the shared gpu target dir; not done here). SAFETY NET ADDED 2026-07-10: tests/unit/hw_probe_cases/gpu_tier_thresholds_are_monotonic.rs now locks the cross-tier invariant via the real gpu_*_for_tier accessors (High<=Mid<=Low for min-bytes/solo-bytes/pattern-breakeven + high strictly < low), so when this value is lowered to 8 MiB the retune is guarded against inverting the tier ordering or fat-fingering an adjacent tier. The per-VALUE test gpu_min_bytes_high_tier_128mb.rs must be updated to the new value in the same change.

- `crates/scanner/src/hw_probe/thresholds.rs:52` | routing | P2 | **GPU_BYTES_BREAKEVEN_SOLO_HIGH_TIER is 256 MiB but solo GPU wins at 8 MiB on RTX 5090.** Same stale-threshold issue as above. The solo breakeven should track the batch breakeven once the positioned-matcher fix is accounted for. FIX: lower to 8 MiB for high-tier, 32 MiB for mid-tier, 128 MiB for low-tier. status=open-needs-bench + COHERENCE-CAVEAT (2026-07-10): sibling of 7084 (same RTX-5090 routing retune, distinct threshold: solo-breakeven vs min-bytes — keep both rows, share the bench). CRITICAL TENSION neither row captured: [[real-world-recall-gap-creddata]] measured "GPU is NOT faster than SIMD/HS at 16-32MB ONE-SHOT CLI (CUDA init ~0.5s/scan DOMINATES; kernel at-parity; daemon-amortization is the real lever)". The "GPU wins at 8 MiB (4.5x)" perf trace is almost certainly KERNEL/warm-CUDA time, NOT wall-clock including the per-invocation CUDA init the one-shot CLI pays. So lowering GPU_MIN_BYTES/solo-breakeven to 8 MiB GLOBALLY would route 8-32MB one-shot scans onto the GPU where init dominates → a WALL-CLOCK PESSIMIZATION of the dominant CLI workflow. RESOLUTION before landing EITHER 7084 or 7086: the crossover sweep MUST measure end-to-end wall-clock INCLUDING CUDA init for the one-shot path (not kernel-only), AND the low threshold should be gated on warm-CUDA/daemon state (aggressive 8 MiB only when CUDA is already initialized — daemon/watch; conservative 128/256 MiB for cold one-shot CLI). Do not lower the cold-path threshold on a kernel-only measurement. Same monotonicity safety-net (gpu_tier_thresholds_are_monotonic.rs, added 2026-07-10) guards the retune; the per-value tests must update in the same change.

- `crates/scanner/benches/gpu_vs_hs_8mib.rs` | perf-bar | P2 | **The production-window RTX 5090 baseline records an 8 MiB GPU/Hyperscan ratio of 0.8964 (31.4524 ms vs 35.0860 ms): GPU clears the crossover, but only by about 1.12x, while the product bar remains 10x (ratio < 0.10).** The `gpu_threshold_measurement_contracts.rs` gate intentionally asserts `ratio > 0.10` to keep that larger performance gap visible. Reaching it requires cutting phase-2 CPU validation and dispatch overhead through the existing VYRE fusion and mapped-buffer lanes. status=open

- `crates/scanner/src/engine/gpu_region_dispatch.rs` | perf | P3 | **Phase-2 CPU tail dominates GPU-path wall time at 8 MiB.** The 2026-06-20 trace shows: at 8 MiB, `phase2_cpu_s = 0.023` vs `dispatch_s = 0.004` + `positioned_literal_gpu_s = 0.003`. Phase-2 CPU is 5.75x the GPU dispatch cost. The GPU prefilter is fast enough; the bottleneck moved downstream. FIX: finish item 8 from vyre-usage.md — lower entropy + MoE scoring to GPU IR, or fuse them into the admission dispatch. This is the single biggest perf win available. status=open

- `crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_gpu_dfa.rs` | innovation | P3 | **GPU regex-DFA admission is shipped but entropy+ML scoring still runs on CPU.** The admission pass (prefixless detectors) is on GPU, but every admitted candidate still triggers CPU-side entropy + regex extraction + MoE inference. For the 8 MiB crossover at 10x, the entropy computation (Shannon + base64/hex alphabet checks) should move to a GPU compute shader — it's embarrassingly parallel (one histogram per candidate window) and the data is already on the GPU. scope: WGSL entropy shader + CPU/GPU parity test + benchmark gate. status=open

#### §TEST-INFRA — test OOM and sharding

- `crates/scanner/tests/per_detector_regression.rs` | test-infra | P2 | **867 tests in one file caused 19 GB OOM (killed process 2579946 on 2026-07-09).** Each test scans with the full compiled scanner; the `LazyLock<CompiledScanner>` shares the scanner but per-test match buffers accumulate across 867 tests in a single binary. FIX options: (a) shard into per-detector-family files (e.g. `per_detector_regression_cloud.rs`, `per_detector_regression_payment.rs`) matching the `gap_*` pattern, each linking as a separate `[[test]]` binary; (b) convert from 867 `#[test]` fns to a single `#[test]` fn with a `for (example, detector_id) in DETECTOR_EXAMPLES` loop — one scanner, one match buffer, deterministic order; (c) add `--test-threads=1` to the test harness for this file specifically. Option (b) is simplest and cuts link memory 867x. status=open

- `crates/scanner/tests/all_tests.rs` | test-infra | P3 | **Aggregate test binary OOMs at link time on NFS-backed hosts.** Already noted in RECONCILE#16 but escalating to P3: this blocks full-suite reconciliation on the development box. The fix (sharded `[[test]]` groups) is the same pattern as the per_detector_regression fix above. status=open (dup of RECONCILE#16 infra finding, escalated priority)

#### §ARCHITECTURE — backlog hygiene

- `BACKLOG.md` | org | P3 | **6944-line backlog is nearly unreadable.** Session logs, inline essays, and multi-paragraph root-cause analyses are mixed with actionable task rows. 778 open items with no triage. FIX: (a) extract session logs to `BACKLOG_LOG.md` (append-only history), (b) keep `BACKLOG.md` as task rows only (the `file:line | class | Pn | problem | fix | status` format already defined at the top), (c) triage 778 open items: close stale, merge duplicates, promote P1/P2 to actionable. status=open

- `BACKLOG.md` | org | P4 | **734 µ-tasks with 693 open is a noise floor.** The µ-task granularity is too fine — individual test gaps and one-line fixes that are individually correct but collectively untractable. FIX: batch µ-tasks into subsystem-level epics (e.g. "drain decode test gaps" as one task with a checklist, not 50 separate rows). status=open

#### §DETECTION — real improvements

- `crates/scanner/src/ml_scorer/ml_features.rs:420` | perf | P4 | **`longest_known_prefix` does a linear scan over known prefixes.** For 900+ detectors with service-vocab prefixes, this is O(n) per candidate. FIX: sort prefixes once at scanner construction and binary-search, or build a trie. Small win per call but called on every candidate. status=open (already in backlog as Open-LOW, confirming)

- `crates/scanner/src/context/false_positive.rs:334` | perf | P4 | **Pathological configmap walkback performance.** The walkback scans backwards from a match to find context, with no early-exit bound on pathological inputs. FIX: cap the walkback distance (e.g. 4 KiB) and document the bound. status=open (already in backlog as Open-LOW, confirming)

#### §GPU-VYRE — innovations for 8 MiB 10x crossover

- `vyre_foundation::execution_plan::fusion::fuse_programs` | innovation | P2 | **Fuse decode + scan into one GPU dispatch.** Today: CPU decompresses (.zst/.gz) → copies plaintext to GPU → dispatches literal-set. With `fuse_programs(decode::inflate, GpuLiteralSet)`, it's one dispatch. Saves ~50% wall time on compressed-corpus scans (npm, Docker layers). This is vyre-usage.md item 4, restated as a crossover lever: every avoided host→GPU copy is dispatch overhead removed from the 8 MiB path. scope: fused-program builder, compressed-input source contract, parity test. status=open

- `vyre_runtime::uring::GpuMappedBuffer` | innovation | P3 | **Zero-copy filesystem reads into GPU-mapped buffers.** Today: `sources/filesystem/read.rs` reads into `Vec<u8>` then copies to GPU. `GpuMappedBuffer` io_ures the file directly into a GPU-mapped buffer — eliminates a full buffer-size memcpy per batch. At 8 MiB this is ~2 ms of memcpy saved per dispatch (measured: ~4 GB/s memcpy throughput). Linux-only (io_uring). scope: vyre-runtime `uring` feature, `GpuStream<'a>` lifetime work in read.rs. status=open

- `crates/scanner/src/gpu.rs` | innovation | P4 | **Replace 620-line hand-rolled MoE WGSL with vyre `nn::moe`.** The hand-rolled MoE in `gpu.rs`/`gpu_shader.rs` is ~620 lines of bespoke wgpu+WGSL. Vyre's `nn::moe` composed from `nn::linear` + `nn::activation` is the same algorithm. Wins: ~600 lines deleted, automatic benefit from vyre kernel improvements, and the MoE scoring can be fused into the same dispatch as the entropy shader (one fewer host→GPU round-trip). Risk: medium — needs bit-equal parity against `ml_scorer.rs`. scope: GPU MoE replacement + CPU/GPU/new-Vyre-GPU parity. status=open (vyre-usage.md item 5, restated as crossover lever)

- `crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_gpu_dfa.rs` | innovation | P3 | **Cooperative DFA as alternative literal engine.** Vyre's `matching::cooperative_dfa` is a GPU IR builder for multi-pattern matching that could replace or complement the current `GpuLiteralSet` region-presence path. The current path does one literal-set automaton pass; a cooperative DFA could handle the anchorless regex patterns that currently fall through to CPU. Benchmark against the literal-set path before adding the route. scope: dispatch wrapper + benchmark gate. status=open (vyre-usage.md "Remaining Wires", restated)

#### §DETECTION / §AUTOROUTE — 2026-07-10 landed worktree lanes

- `crates/core/src/spec.rs; crates/scanner/src/entropy/bpe.rs; crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_{generic,entropy}.rs` | detector-policy | P2 | BPE token-efficiency had only a scan-global threshold, so per-secret-type tuning lived outside the detector TOML. Added detector-owned `bpe_max_chars_per_token`, finite-positive fail-closed validation, detector-digest identity, policy-owner resolution on both generic and synthetic entropy paths, explicit 2.2 opaque API-key/secret policy and 99.0 password/passphrase policy, and a presence-aware Tier-A scan override so explicit TOML/CLI still wins and is visible in `config --effective`. The detector boundary now also rejects malformed entropy floors/thresholds, credential shapes, and allowlist regexes before scanning. Behavioral policy/config/loader tests, changelog, mechanism/config docs, and mdBook build are green. | status=done-worktree-awaiting-coherent-commit
- `crates/scanner/src/hw_probe/mod.rs; crates/cli/src/orchestrator/dispatch/backend{.rs,/store.rs,/tests.rs}` | autoroute-identity | P1 | CLI-local `cfg!(feature="gpu")` could be false while Cargo feature unification compiled the scanner dependency with GPU, causing GPU-selected calibration evidence to omit GPU device/runtime/driver identity and survive hardware changes. Added scanner-owned GPU/SIMD compile facts and used them for host/build cache identity. Verified 65/65 backend tests under ci-lean plus GPU-present/GPU-absent identity tests under both ci-lean and default GPU builds; docs build green. | status=done-worktree-awaiting-coherent-commit
- `crates/scanner/src/adjudicate/mod.rs; crates/scanner/src/engine/phase2_{generic,entropy}.rs; crates/cli/src/orchestrator/mod.rs` | active-detector-policy | P1 | Final adjudication re-read embedded detector metadata after producers had already resolved the active corpus, so a lower repository `[detector.<id>] min_confidence` override could lose to the detector's original floor before post-processing. Removed the embedded-registry override, compile validated repository floors into the active detector specs, and make generic/entropy producers resolve confidence and BPE from the active owning detector. The custom-detector e2e now proves a 0.4 operator override releases a 0.5 finding beneath its 0.8 detector default. | status=done-worktree-awaiting-coherent-commit
- `crates/cli/src/config/{policy,scan,schema}.rs; crates/cli/src/value_parsers.rs; crates/core/src/config.rs` | config-truth | P1 | Removed the empty-but-inviting hardcoded shipped detector floor/disable lists; detector TOMLs are now the only shipped per-detector policy source. Focused effective-config suite is green (43/43), custom detector floor e2e is green, and mdBook builds. | status=done-worktree-awaiting-coherent-commit
